histogram.name: Accessibility.Android.AnimationsEnabled
histogram.obsolete: Removed 11/2018 because a need was discovered for a non-boolean value. Superseded by Accessibility.Android.AnimationsEnabled2.
histogram.summary: Tracks whether animations are enabled on Android (e.g. if the animator duration scale is non-zero.) The purpose is to inform the design of the prefers-reduced-motion media feature; see http://crbug.com/722548. This is logged once, 45 seconds after startup.

histogram.name: Accessibility.Android.AnimationsEnabled2
histogram.summary:
Tracks whether animations are enabled on Android (e.g. if the animator duration scale is non-zero.) The purpose is to inform the design of the prefers-reduced-motion media feature; see http://crbug.com/722548. This is logged once, 45 seconds after startup.
This replaced Accessibility.Android.AnimationsEnabled because the older histogram did not capture the default (e.g. no flag) case.

histogram.name: Accessibility.Android.ScreenReader
histogram.summary: Tracks whether a screen reader is enabled on Android (e.g. Talkback). This is checked once, 45 seconds after startup. Note: prefer Accessibility.Android.ScreenReader.EveryReport when querying for unique users, as it is logged more frequently.

histogram.name: Accessibility.Android.TabSwitcherPreferenceEnabled
histogram.summary: Tracks whether the accessibility tab switcher is enabled when an accessibility service (e.g. TalkBack or Switch Access) is enabled that would typically cause the accessibility tab switcher to be used. Recorded when the activity is resumed or accessibility services are turned on.

histogram.name: Accessibility.AndroidServiceInfo
histogram.summary: Tracks flags and capabilities of enabled accessibility services. Recorded every time an Android web content view first creates a virtual view hierarchy, indicating that some accessibility service is running. The number of emits of this histogram won't be as useful as the unique user counts. The primary purpose of this is to guide optimization by determining what capabilities of accessibility services are widespread and which ones are relatively rare.

histogram.name: Accessibility.ATK-APIs
histogram.summary: Tracks usage of ATK APIs on Linux Desktop. Recorded when ATK APIs that were supposed to trigger AX mode enabled is called. The feature to turn on AX mode through ATK APIs is disabled for now; see http://crbug.com/1086506.

histogram.name: Accessibility.CaptionSettingsLoadedFromPrefs
histogram.summary: Whether custom caption settings were loaded from the user preferences. Recorded when WebKit preferences are overridden.

histogram.name: Accessibility.CaptionSettingsLoadedFromSystemSettings
histogram.summary: Whether custom caption settings were loaded from the system preferences. Recorded when WebKit preferences are overridden.

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosAlwaysShowA11yMenu
histogram.summary: Whether the Chrome OS Accessibility Menu is set to be shown regardless of the state of a11y features.(logged once 45 secs after startup).

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosAutoclick
histogram.summary: Whether the Chrome OS Autoclick feature is on (logged once 45 secs after startup).

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosAutoclick.TrayMenu.ChangeAction
histogram.summary: Recorded when a user has picked a new autoclick action type from the bubble menu.

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosAutoclickDelay
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2020 as it is no longer needed for analysis.
histogram.summary: If the user has enabled Autoclick, this is the delay set by the user for autoclicks to occur, in milliseconds, at startup and when changed.

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosCaretHighlight
histogram.summary: Whether the Chrome OS caret highlighting is on (logged once 45 secs after startup).

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosChromeVoxAfterSwitchAccess
histogram.summary: When ChromeVox is enabled, true if Switch Access was previously enabled.

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosChromeVoxNext
histogram.summary: Set when a Chrome OS user has spoken feedback enabled, recorded once each time ChromeVox starts up. True if the user has enabled "ChromeVox Next", a new ChromeVox experience that became the default in Chrome 56 (but can be toggled on or off by the user), false otherwise.

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosCursorHighlight
histogram.summary: Whether the Chrome OS cursor highlighting is on (logged once 45 secs after startup).

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosDictation
histogram.summary: Whether the ChromeOS dictation feature is enabled (logged once 45 secs after startup).

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosDictation.ToggleDictationMethod
histogram.summary: Records how users invoke Dictation.

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosDockedMagnifier
histogram.summary: Whether the Chrome OS Docked Magnifier feature is on (logged once 45 secs after startup).

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosFocusHighlight
histogram.summary: Whether the Chrome OS focus highlighting is on (logged once 45 secs after startup).

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosHighContrast
histogram.summary: Whether the Chrome OS High Contrast mode feature is on (logged once 45 secs after startup).

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosLargeCursor
histogram.summary: Whether the Chrome OS Large Cursor feature is on (logged once 45 secs after startup).

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosLargeCursorSize
histogram.summary: Cursor size of the Chrome OS Large Cursor (logged once 45 secs after startup).

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosScreenMagnifier
histogram.summary: Whether the Chrome OS Screen Magnifier feature is on (logged once 45 secs after startup).

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosSelectToSpeak
histogram.summary: Whether the Chrome OS select-to-speak is on (logged once 45 secs after startup).

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosSelectToSpeak.BackgroundShading
histogram.summary: Whether Select-to-Speak had background shading enabled when activated.

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosSelectToSpeak.SpeechPitch
histogram.obsolete: Removed 7/2018 in Issue 866550 in favor of using global Text-to-Speech settings for speech pitch.
histogram.summary: The speech pitch setting when Select-to-Speak was activated. It is stored as a sparse histogram with values (100 * speech_pitch). For example, a speech pitch of 1.0 (default) will be seen as 100.

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosSelectToSpeak.SpeechRate
histogram.obsolete: Removed 7/2018 in Issue 866550 in favor of using global Text-to-Speech settings for speech rate.
histogram.summary: The speech rate setting when Select-to-Speak was activated. It is stored as a sparse histogram with values (100 * speech_rate). For example, a speech rate of 1.0 (default) will be seen as 100.

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosSelectToSpeak.StartSpeechMethod
histogram.summary: A user may activate Select-to-Speak by holding down 'search' and clicking or dragging a region with the mouse, or by highlighting an area and using search + s to read just the highlighted area. Track the methods here.

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosSelectToSpeak.StateChangeEvent
histogram.summary: A user has tapped a button in the tray to change Select-to-Speak's state. The tap was interpreted by Select-to-Speak as a request to start selection, to cancel speech, or to cancel selection, depending on Select-to-Speak's internal state when the tap occured. This tracks when the button was tapped and the event that it generated.

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosSelectToSpeak.WordHighlighting
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2020 as it is no longer needed for analysis.
histogram.summary: Whether Select-to-Speak had per-word highlighting enabled when activated.

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosShelfNavigationButtonsInTabletModeChanged.OOBE
histogram.summary: The value for shelf navigation buttons setting set by the user during OOBE. The metric will be reported after the setting has been stable for 10 seconds, or the OOBE screen in which the value can be set is closed (if the setting changes multiple times in quick succession, only the final value will be reported).

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosShelfNavigationButtonsInTabletModeChanged.OsSettings
histogram.summary: The value for shelf navigation buttons setting set by the user in Chrome OS Settings page. The metric will be reported after the setting change has been stable for 10 seconds, or the settings window in which the value is set gets closed (if the setting changes multiple times in quick succession, only the final value will be reported).

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosSpokenFeedback
histogram.summary: Whether the Chrome OS Spoken Feedback feature is on (logged once 45 secs after startup). Note: prefer Accessibility.CrosSpokenFeedback.EveryReport when querying for unique users, as it is logged more frequently.

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosStickyKeys
histogram.summary: Whether the Chrome OS Sticky Keys feature is on (logged once 45 secs after startup).

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosSwitchAccess
histogram.summary: Whether the Chrome OS switch access is on (logged once 45 secs after startup).

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosSwitchAccessAfterChromeVox
histogram.summary: When Switch Access is enabled, true if ChromeVox was previously enabled.

histogram.name: Accessibility.CrosVirtualKeyboard
histogram.summary: Whether the Chrome OS Virtual Keyboard feature is on (logged once 45 secs after startup).

histogram.name: Accessibility.ImageLabels
histogram.summary: Whether the Chrome accessibility image label setting is on (logged once 45 secs after startup).

histogram.name: Accessibility.ImageLabels.ContextMenuOption
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2020 because we don't need to keep tracking this.
histogram.summary: When a user selects the Chrome accessibility image labels option in the context menu, whether they are trying to enable or disable the feature, or enable it for a single use. Recorded when the user selects the option from the context menu. The feature may not have been enabled if they do not confirm on the modal dialog that appears after.

histogram.name: Accessibility.ImageLabels.FromSettings.ToggleSetting
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2020 because we don't need to keep tracking this.
histogram.summary: When a user selects the Chrome accessibility image labels option in chrome://settings, whether they are trying to enable or disable the feature. Recorded when the user activates the toggle from the settings page. The feature may not have been enabled if they do not confirm on the modal dialog that appears after.

histogram.name: Accessibility.ImageLabels.ModalDialogAccepted
histogram.summary: Whether a user accepts or does not accept a modal dialog enabling the image labels option. If the user does not accept it this does not track whether they closed it with the negative button, the close button, or keyboard escape.

histogram.name: Accessibility.ImageLabels.PageLanguage
histogram.summary: The language of a web page where image labels were requested, as detected by Chrome (not necessarily what language the page claims to be in).

histogram.name: Accessibility.ImageLabels.RequestLanguage
histogram.summary: The language that was requested for image descriptions, based on the page language, the user's accept languages and top languages, and the server languages.

histogram.name: Accessibility.InvertedColors
histogram.summary: Whether Windows system settings show that high-contrast mode is enabled and the user has selected a light-on-dark color scheme (logged once 45 secs after startup). This causes Chrome to prompt the user with a bubble to optionally install a High Contrast extension and theme.

histogram.name: Accessibility.iOS.NewLargerTextCategory
histogram.summary: This metric is related to the Preferred Content Size chosen by the user. It is only recorded once per session. This is hit if the chosen category is not listed in the map defined in /ios/chrome/browser/ui/util/dynamic_type_util.mm. In that case, we should update the code by adding an entry for the new category in that map. This is logged when the helper to returning the multiplier associated with the current preferred content size is called.

histogram.name: Accessibility.LanguageDetection.CountDetectionAttempted
histogram.summary: The number of nodes on this page for which language detection was attempted.

histogram.name: Accessibility.LanguageDetection.CountLabelled
histogram.summary: The number of nodes on this page for which a detected language was successfully assigned.

histogram.name: Accessibility.LanguageDetection.LangsPerPage
histogram.summary: The number of unique languages which were detected to be the most probable language for at least one node on the page, regardless of whether a detected language was assigned for that node.

histogram.name: Accessibility.LanguageDetection.PercentageLabelledWithTop
histogram.summary: Of the nodes which had a detected language assigned, the percentage for which the assigned language was the language detected as having the highest probability.

histogram.name: Accessibility.LanguageDetection.PercentageLanguageDetected
histogram.summary: Of the nodes for which language detection was attempted, the percentage for which a language was successfully assigned.

histogram.name: Accessibility.LanguageDetection.PercentageOverridden
histogram.summary: Of the nodes for which a detected language was successfully assigned, the percentage where the assigned language differs from the author-provided language.

histogram.name: Accessibility.LiveCaptions
histogram.summary: Whether Live Captions are enabled. This is logged once, 45 seconds after startup.

histogram.name: Accessibility.LiveCaptions.Session
histogram.summary: Logged when there's a change in the lifetime of a Live Captions audio stream: When a session started and captions began arriving from the service, when a session ended because the audio stream finished, or when the session ended because a user clicked the close button on the caption bubble.

histogram.name: Accessibility.LiveCaptions.ToggleEnabled
histogram.summary: Records when a user enables or disables the Live Caption feature from chrome://settings.

histogram.name: Accessibility.Mac.DifferentiateWithoutColor
histogram.summary: Whether the "differentiate without color" Mac system setting is enabled. This is logged once, 45 seconds after startup.

histogram.name: Accessibility.Mac.FullKeyboardAccessEnabled
histogram.summary: Whether the "full keyboard access" Mac system setting is enabled. This is logged once, 45 seconds after startup.

histogram.name: Accessibility.Mac.IncreaseContrast
histogram.summary: Whether the "increase contrast" Mac system setting is enabled. This is logged once, 45 seconds after startup.

histogram.name: Accessibility.Mac.ReduceMotion
histogram.summary: Tracks whether the accessibilityDisplayShouldReduceMotion system property is enabled. The purpose is to inform the design of the prefers-reduced-motion media feature; see http://crbug.com/722548. This is logged once, 45 seconds after startup.

histogram.name: Accessibility.Mac.ReduceTransparency
histogram.summary: Whether the "reduce transparency" Mac system setting is enabled. This is logged once, 45 seconds after startup.

histogram.name: Accessibility.Mac.ScreenReader
histogram.summary: Tracks whether a screen reader is enabled on Mac (e.g. VoiceOver). This is logged once, 45 seconds after startup. Note: prefer Accessibility.Mac.ScreenReader.EveryReport when querying for unique users, as it is logged more frequently.

histogram.name: Accessibility.ManuallyEnabled
histogram.summary: Whether Chrome has enabled accessibility support because the user passed the --force-renderer-accessibility flag on the command-line (logged once 45 secs after startup).

histogram.name: Accessibility.ModeFlag
histogram.summary: Whether individual accessibility mode flags are set. Tracked when each mode flag is flipped from false to true. These flags are flipped automatically when software communicates with Chrome via accessibility APIs. Flags can only be flipped off by advanced users or for debugging using chrome://accessibility/ - and that isn't tracked in this histogram.

histogram.name: Accessibility.OOBEStartupSoundDelay
histogram.summary: Delay between login_prompt_visible and Chrome OS OOBE startup sound playback. Depends on sound subsystem initialization time.

histogram.name: Accessibility.ScreenReader.Image.MinSize
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2020 as this is stable and doesn't need to be tracked closely.
histogram.summary: For every image on a page with screen reader accessibility mode enabled, this histogram will be used to understand minimum dimension of the image, i.e. either the width or height in px.

histogram.name: Accessibility.ScreenReader.Image.SizeRatio
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2020 as this is stable and doesn't need to be tracked closely.
histogram.summary: For every image on a page with screen reader accessibility mode enabled, this histogram will be used to understand the size ratio of the image. This is the smaller dimension divided by the larger dimension.

histogram.name: Accessibility.Shortcuts.CrosDockedMagnifier
histogram.summary: Whether the Chrome OS Docked Magnifier shortcut is enabled. It's being emitted when the Docked Magnifier shortcut is triggered.

histogram.name: Accessibility.Shortcuts.CrosHighContrast
histogram.summary: Whether the Chrome OS High Contrast mode shortcut is enabled. It's being emitted when the High Contrast shortcut is triggered.

histogram.name: Accessibility.Shortcuts.CrosScreenMagnifier
histogram.summary: Whether the Chrome OS Full-screen Magnifier mode shortcut is enabled. It's being emitted when the Full-screen Magnifier shortcut is triggered.

histogram.name: Accessibility.Shortcuts.CrosSpokenFeedback
histogram.summary: Whether the Chrome OS Spoken Feedback shortcut is enabled. It's being emitted when the Spoken Feedback shortcut is triggered.

histogram.name: Accessibility.State
histogram.obsolete: Removed 12/2016 in Issue 672205 with the addition of Accessibility.ModeFlag.
histogram.summary: Whether Chrome has enabled accessibility support because it detects supported assistive technology running, or due to being manually enabled via a command-line flag (logged once 45 secs after startup).

histogram.name: Accessibility.VTTContainsStyleBlock
histogram.summary: Whether a VTT file contains an embedded style block. Recorded when a VTT file is parsed.

histogram.name: Accessibility.Win.AnimationsEnabled
histogram.summary: Tracks whether the SPI_GETCLIENTAREAANIMATION system property is enabled. The purpose is to inform the design of the prefers-reduced-motion media feature; see http://crbug.com/722548. This is logged once, 45 seconds after startup.

histogram.name: Accessibility.WinAPIs
histogram.summary: Tracks usage of all public Windows accessibility APIs.

histogram.name: Accessibility.WinAPIs.GetPropertyValue
histogram.summary: Tracks properties requested via UI Automation GetPropertyValue().

histogram.name: Accessibility.WinAudioDescription
histogram.summary: Whether Windows system settings show that audio descriptions are enabled (logged once 45 secs after startup).

histogram.name: Accessibility.WinHighContrastTheme
histogram.summary: Tracks which Windows high contrast theme is set in system settings (logged once 45 secs after startup). AccessibilityHighContrastColorScheme::None indicates that high contrast mode is not enabled.

histogram.name: Accessibility.WinJAWS
histogram.summary: Whether the third-party JAWS screen reader is running (logged once 45 secs after startup). Note: prefer Accessibility.WinJAWS.EveryReport when querying for unique users, as it is logged more frequently.

histogram.name: Accessibility.WinNVDA
histogram.summary: Whether the third-party NVDA screen reader is running (logged once 45 secs after startup). Note: prefer Accessibility.WinNVDA.EveryReport when querying for unique users, as it is logged more frequently.

histogram.name: Accessibility.WinSAToGo
histogram.obsolete: System Access To Go is a discontinued product.
histogram.summary: Whether the third-party System Access To Go screen reader is running (logged once 45 secs after startup).

histogram.name: Accessibility.WinScreenReader
histogram.summary: Whether Windows system settings show that a screen reader is running (logged once 45 secs after startup). Note that this does not necessarily mean that Chrome has detected a supported screen reader and has enabled its accessibility mode.

histogram.name: Accessibility.WinScreenReader2
histogram.summary: Whether the accessibility mode flag shows that a screen reader is running (logged once 45 secs after startup). In this case, Chrome has detected accessibility calls that would normally only occur from a screen reader. See also the more specific metrics such as Accessibility.WinJAWS/WinNVDA. Note: prefer Accessibility.WinScreenReader2.EveryReport when querying for unique users, as it is logged more frequently.

histogram.name: Accessibility.WinStickyKeys
histogram.summary: Whether Windows system settings show that Sticky Keys are enabled.

histogram.name: Accessibility.WinSupernova
histogram.summary: Whether the third-party Supernova screen reader is running (logged once 45 secs after startup). Note: prefer Accessibility.Supernova.EveryReport when querying for unique users, as it is logged more frequently.

histogram.name: Accessibility.WinZoomText
histogram.summary: Whether the third-party ZoomText screen magnifier is running. Note: prefer Accessibility.WinZoomText.EveryReport when querying for unique users, as it is logged more frequently.

histogram.name: AccountManager.AccountAdditionSource
histogram.summary: Records the source of in-session account addition (or re-authentication) triggers on Chrome OS. This is recorded whenever an in-session account addition (or re-authentication) is triggered - either explicitely by the user, or automatically by Gaia (for accounts requiring immediate re-authentication).

histogram.name: AccountManager.EduCoexistence.CreateRaptResult
histogram.summary: The result of creating a Reauth Proof Token (RAPT) for the parent in secondary EDU account addition flow. Recorded after parent enters their password and child access token is fetched.

histogram.name: AccountManager.EduCoexistence.FetchAccessTokenResult
histogram.summary: The result of fetching an access token for the child with |accounts.reauth| scope in secondary EDU account addition flow. Recorded after parent enters their password.

histogram.name: AccountManager.EduCoexistence.FetchParentsListResult
histogram.summary: The result of fetching a list of parents in secondary EDU account addition flow. Recorded when user opens 'Add EDU account' flow.

histogram.name: AccountManager.EduCoexistence.FlowResult
histogram.summary: Records the last completed step in the EDU account addition flow. Allows to track the drop rate by screen.

histogram.name: AccountManager.InitializationTime
histogram.summary: Measures the time taken to fully initialize Chrome OS Account Manager. This is recorded once per user session, at login time.

histogram.name: AccountManager.LegacySetPrimaryAccountAndUpdateAccountInfo
histogram.summary: Tracks the usage of the legacy Primary Account setting flow vs the new flow through Account Manager. This is recorded only once per session, at login time.

histogram.name: AccountManager.ManageAccountsServiceType
histogram.summary: Tracks the service type received with the mirror response header.

histogram.name: AccountManager.Migrations.Result
histogram.summary: Tracks the final result of migrating accounts to Chrome OS Account Manager. This is recorded only once per session, at login time.

histogram.name: AccountManager.Migrations.StepResult
histogram.summary: Tracks the result of individual migration steps of migrating accounts to Chrome OS Account Manager. This is recorded only once per session, at login time.

histogram.name: AccountManager.MirrorReauthenticationRequest
histogram.summary: Whether user has received a Mirror re-authentication request for account with invalid cookies. Recorded when we open a re-authentication dialog for the invalid account. See https://crbug.com/1012649 for details.

histogram.name: AccountManager.NumAccounts
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of accounts stored in Chrome OS Account Manager by a given user. This is recorded only once per session, at login time.

histogram.name: AccountManager.TokenLoadStatus
histogram.summary: Tracks the status of token loads from disk by Chrome OS Account Manager, for a given user. This is recorded only once per session, at login time.

histogram.name: ActivityTracker.Collect.AnalyzerCreationError
histogram.summary: The analyzer creation error code. Logged each time analyzer creation fails, at most once per processed stability debug file.

histogram.name: ActivityTracker.Collect.InitStatus
histogram.summary: Status of the initialization to collect stability debug files. Logged once, during the initialization of the stability debug file collection.

histogram.name: ActivityTracker.Collect.StabilityFileCount
histogram.summary: Number of files found during stability file collection. Logged each time the stability file collection proceeds (at most once per launch).

histogram.name: ActivityTracker.Collect.Status
histogram.summary: Status for the collection of a stability debug file. Logged each time a debug file collection attempt is made.

histogram.name: ActivityTracker.Collect.SystemSessionAnalysisStatus
histogram.summary: Status for the analysis of the system session state. Logged each time a debug file is collected.

histogram.name: ActivityTracker.Collect.TotalTime
histogram.summary: Time spent collecting stability debug information. Logged each time a collection is performed.

histogram.name: ActivityTracker.Collect.UncleanShutdownCount
histogram.summary: Number of unclean shutdowns, as derived from the stability instrumentation. Logged each time stability file collection is performed.

histogram.name: ActivityTracker.Collect.UncleanSystemCount
histogram.summary: Number of unclean shutdowns that can potentially be attributed to system instability. This should be smaller or equal to UncleanShutdownCount. Logged each time stability file collection is performed.

histogram.name: ActivityTracker.Collect.WriteDumpStatus
histogram.summary: Status of the minidump writing. Logged each time a writing a minidump is attempted.

histogram.name: ActivityTracker.CollectCrash.Event
histogram.summary: Events that occur during crash collection of the debug file. Logged each time an event of interest occurs during crash debug file collection.

histogram.name: ActivityTracker.CollectCrash.OpenForDeleteSuccess
histogram.summary: Success of the attempt to open the debug file for deletion. Logged each time a debug file is opened for deletion during collection from the crash handler.

histogram.name: ActivityTracker.CollectCrash.Status
histogram.summary: Status for the collection of a stability debug file. Logged each time a debug file collection attempt is made from the crash handler.

histogram.name: ActivityTracker.Record.Event
histogram.summary: Events pertaining to recording to the debug file. Logged each time an event of interest occurs wrt debug file recording.

histogram.name: ActivityTracker.Record.InitStatus
histogram.summary: Status of internal activity tracking initialization. Logged once, during the activity tracking initialization.

histogram.name: ActivityTracker.Record.SetupTime
histogram.obsolete: Setup time is <=10ms for 90% with 0.1% taking >500ms. Removed 2019/06.
histogram.summary: Time spent setting up the stability debugging instrumentation. Logged once, during setup of the stability debugging instrumentation.

histogram.name: ActivityTracker.ThreadTrackers.Count
histogram.obsolete: Gaussian distribution around 22 with 0.1% being >=100. Removed 2019/06.
histogram.summary: The number of threads being tracked for activities such as waiting for a lock/event, a thread to join, or a task to run. Analysis of this data can reveal why a thread will not exit. This value is updated every time a new thread gets an activity tracked for the first time so will show one count for every permanent thread but multiple counts for a thread than exits and is replaced.

histogram.name: ActivityTracker.ThreadTrackers.MemLimitTrackerCount
histogram.summary: The limit on the number of thread trackers that could be allocated from the persistent memory segment. Trackers beyond this number were allocated from the heap and thus were not available for analysis. This value is updated on every "memory full" failure.

histogram.name: AddSupervisionDialog.Enrollment
histogram.summary: Records the number of users who go through the Add Supervision process to convert regular accounts to Geller accounts. Initiated count is incremented when Add Supervision dialog is opened. Completed count is incremented when supervision is successfully enrolled. Signed out count is incremented when the user clicks "Sign out" after enrollment at the end of the process in the dialog. Closed count is incremented when the Add Supervision dialog is closed prior to enrollment, excluding sign out. Switched accounts count is incremented when the user clicks "Sign out" within the dialog to swich accounts before enrollment completion.

histogram.name: AddSupervisionDialog.EnrollmentCompletedUserTime
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time in milliseconds for users to successfully enroll in supervision.

histogram.name: AddSupervisionDialog.EnrollmentNotCompletedUserTime
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time in milliseconds for users to exit the Add Supervision dialog without enrollment. This can happen if the user closes the Add Supervision dialog or signs out to switch accounts.

histogram.name: AddSupervisionDialog.SignoutCompletedUserTime
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time in milliseconds for users to sign out after enrolling in supervision. Elapsed time is measured starting from when the Add Supervision dialog was first opened.

histogram.name: Ads.Features.AdResourceIsIsolated
histogram.summary: For a given ad request, logs information related to whether it is isolated from the top-level context. Logged per ad subresource request.

histogram.name: Ads.Features.ResourceIsSecure
histogram.summary: For a given request, logs information related to whether it is marked as an ad, and whether it is secure (e.g. https). Logged per subresource request.

histogram.name: Ads.Media.BytesReceived
histogram.obsolete: Removed 10/2019 in issue 1000058; no longer needed.
histogram.summary: Total number of bytes buffered over the lifetime of a WebMediaPlayer inside of an adframe. Suffixed by type of playback.

histogram.name: Ads.Media.Duration
histogram.summary: Duration in milliseconds of HTML5 media (when known). Only recorded for media within ad subframes. This is recorded every time the duration changes for a video, and can be recorded multiple times for a single video (the overall duration may not be known when loading starts). Because the duration will generally increase as more video gets buffered, this histogram may skew low.

histogram.name: Ads.Media.LoadType
histogram.summary: Load type of HTML5 media in ad subframes such as URL, MediaSource and MediaStream.

histogram.name: Ads.ResourceUsage.Size.Cache
histogram.summary: For a given resource fetched from the disk cache, logs the encoded body length of the resource, even if the resource request was canceled or incomplete. Recorded when the resource request is complete, or when the page is destroyed/navigated for incomplete resources.

histogram.name: Ads.ResourceUsage.Size.Cache2
histogram.summary: For a given resource fetched from the disk cache or blink memory cache, logs the encoded body length of the resource, even if the resource request was canceled or incomplete. Recorded when the resource request is complete, or when the page is destroyed/navigated for incomplete resources. The sum of samples across a page load may differ substatially from the true sum due to per-sample rounding. These are not intended to be compared to page level histograms.

histogram.name: Ads.ResourceUsage.Size.Mainframe.AdResource
histogram.summary: For a given ad resource in the main frame, logs the network bytes received for the resource, even if the resource request was canceled or incomplete.

histogram.name: Ads.ResourceUsage.Size.Mainframe.VanillaResource
histogram.summary: For a given non-ad resource in the main frame, logs the network bytes received for the resource, even if the resource request was canceled or incomplete.

histogram.name: Ads.ResourceUsage.Size.Mime
histogram.obsolete: Removed 10/2018. Replaced with Ads.ResourceUsage.Size.Cache.Mime and Ads.ResourceUsage.Size.Network.Mime.
histogram.summary: Size of resources tagged as ads, identified by the response header mime type. This includes resources that did not complete loading. Recorded when the resource request is completed. For incomplete requests, recorded when the page is destroyed/navigated.

histogram.name: Ads.ResourceUsage.Size.Network
histogram.summary: For a given resource fetched from the network, logs the network bytes used to load the resource (including headers), even if the resource request was canceled or incomplete. Recorded when the resource request is complete, or when the page is destroyed/navigated for incomplete resources. The sum of samples across a page load may differ substatially from the true sum due to per-sample rounding. These are not intended to be compared to page level histograms.

histogram.name: Ads.ResourceUsage.Size.Subframe.AdResource
histogram.summary: For a given ad resource in a subframe, logs the network bytes received for the resource, even if the resource request was canceled or incomplete.

histogram.name: Ads.ResourceUsage.Size.Subframe.VanillaResource
histogram.summary: For a given non-ad resource in a subframe, logs the network bytes received for the resource, even if the resource request was canceled or incomplete.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.Clicked.AreaRank
histogram.summary: The rank of the clicked anchor element in terms of area. This histogram is recorded when the anchor element is clicked.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.Clicked.ClickIntervals
histogram.summary: The interval between consecutive clicks of anchor elements. This histogram is recorded when an anchor element is clicked except the first click in the current document.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.Clicked.DurationLoadToFirstClick
histogram.summary: The duration between page load and the first click of an anchor element. If the first click happens before page load, then the sample is recorded in bucket 0. This histogram is recorded when an anchor element is clicked for the first time in the current document.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.Clicked.HrefEngagementScore
histogram.summary: The target link (href) engagement score of an anchor element. The score is retrieved from the site engagement service. Currently all scores reported are set to 0. This will soon change. This histogram is recorded when the anchor element is clicked.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.Clicked.HrefEngagementScorePositive
histogram.summary: The site engagement score of the target link (href) of an anchor element. The score is retrieved from the site engagement service. This histogram is recorded when the anchor element is clicked, and the score is larger than 0.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.Clicked.NavigationScore
histogram.summary: The calculated navigation score of the target link (href) of an anchor element. The score is retrieved from the site engagement service. This histogram is recorded when the anchor element is clicked and the score has already been calculated when the document is loaded.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.Clicked.NavigationScoreRank
histogram.summary: The rank of the navigation score of the target link (href) of an anchor element. This histogram is recorded when the anchor element is clicked and the score has already been calculated when the document is loaded.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.Clicked.OnDSE.SameHost
histogram.summary: True if the target link of the anchor element has the same host as the root document. Recorded only on default search engine pages.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.Clicked.OnNonDSE.SameHost
histogram.summary: True if the target link of the anchor element has the same host as the root document. Recorded only on pages whose URL do not match the default search engine.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.Clicked.RatioContainsImage_ContainsImage
histogram.summary: The ratio times 100 between the number of anchor elements that contains images and the total number of anchor elements. This histogram is recorded when the anchor element is clicked and it contains images.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.Clicked.RatioContainsImage_NoImage
histogram.summary: The ratio times 100 between the number of anchor elements that contains images and the total number of anchor elements. This histogram is recorded when the anchor element is clicked and it does not contain images.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.Clicked.RatioInIframe_InIframe
histogram.summary: The ratio times 100 between the number of anchor elements that is inside an iframe and the total number of anchor elements. This histogram is recorded when the anchor element is clicked and it is inside an iframe.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.Clicked.RatioInIframe_NotInIframe
histogram.summary: The ratio times 100 between the number of anchor elements that is inside an iframe and the total number of anchor elements. This histogram is recorded when the anchor element is clicked and it is not inside an iframe.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.Clicked.RatioRootHeight
histogram.summary: The ratio between the height of the root document and the height of the viewport times 100. This histogram is recorded when the anchor element is clicked.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.Clicked.RatioSameHost_DiffHost
histogram.summary: The ratio times 100 between the number of anchor elements whose href have the same host as the document and the total number of anchor elements. This histogram is recorded when the anchor element is clicked and href of the anchor element has a different host than the document.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.Clicked.RatioSameHost_SameHost
histogram.summary: The ratio times 100 between the number of anchor elements whose href have the same host as the document and the total number of anchor elements. This histogram is recorded when the anchor element is clicked and href of the anchor element has the same host as the document.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.Clicked.RatioUrlIncremented_NotIncremented
histogram.summary: The ratio times 100 between the number of anchor elements whose href is incremented by one from the url of the document and the total number of anchor elements. This histogram is recorded when the anchor element is clicked and its href is not incremented by one from the url of the document.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.Clicked.RatioUrlIncremented_UrlIncremented
histogram.summary: The ratio times 100 between the number of anchor elements whose href is incremented by one from the url of the document and the total number of anchor elements. This histogram is recorded when the anchor element is clicked and its href is incremented by one from the url of the document.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.ContainsImage
histogram.summary: True if the anchor element contains an image element, false if it is not.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.DocumentEngagementScore
histogram.summary: The site engagement score of the document URL. The score is retrieved from the site engagement service.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.HrefEngagementScore2
histogram.summary: The site engagement score of the target link (href) of an anchor element. The score is retrieved from the site engagement service.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.HrefEngagementScoreExternal
histogram.summary: The site engagement score of the target link (href) of an anchor element. The score is retrieved from the site engagement service. This histogram is recorded when href is an external link.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.IsAdFrameElement
histogram.summary: True if the anchor element was inside an iframe tagged as an ad iframe.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.IsInIFrame
histogram.summary: True if the anchor element is within an iframe, false if it is not.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.IsSameHost
histogram.summary: True if the target link of the anchor element has the same host as the root document.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.IsUrlIncrementedByOne
histogram.summary: True if the target URL of the anchor element and the URL of the root document only differ by one number, and the number in the target URL equals the number in the the URL of the root document plus one.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.RatioArea
histogram.summary: The ratio times 100 between the clickable region area of an anchor element, and the viewport area.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.RatioDistanceCenterToVisibleTop
histogram.summary: The ratio times 100 between the distance from the center of the clickable region of an anchor element to the top edge of the visible region, and the viewport height.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.RatioDistanceRootBottom
histogram.summary: The ratio times 100 between the distance from the bottom of the clickable region of an anchor element to the bottom edge of the root frame, and the viewport height.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.RatioDistanceRootTop
histogram.summary: The ratio times 100 between the distance from the top of the clickable region of an anchor element to the top edge of the root frame, and the viewport height.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.RatioDistanceTopToVisibleTop
histogram.summary: The ratio times 100 between the distance from the top of the clickable region of an anchor element to the top edge of the visible region, and the viewport height.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.RatioVisibleArea
histogram.summary: The ratio times 100 between the visible clickable region area of an anchor element, and the viewport area.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.Visible.HighestNavigationScore
histogram.summary: The highest navigation score of the anchor elements sent to the browser process on a page load. This histogram is recorded when the webpage is loaded. Normalized to a value between 0.0 and 100.0.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.Visible.NumberOfAnchorElements
histogram.summary: The number of anchor element metrics sent to the browser process on a page load. This histogram is recorded when the webpage is loaded.

histogram.name: AnchorElementMetrics.Visible.NumberOfAnchorElementsAfterMerge
histogram.summary: The number of anchor element metrics sent to the browser process on a page load. Anchor elements having the same href are merged and counted as 1. This histogram is recorded when the webpage is loaded.

histogram.name: Android.Activity.ChromeTabbedActivity.StopReason
histogram.summary: What caused ChromeTabbedActivity#onStop() to be called, which indicates that Chrome is sent to the background.

histogram.name: Android.Activity.ChromeTabbedActivity.SystemBackAction
histogram.summary: What happened when the user hit the system back button in ChromeTabbedActivity.

histogram.name: Android.AppNotificationStatus
histogram.summary: Records whether notifications are enabled for Chrome, as the Android app, every time metrics are logged.

histogram.name: Android.ArmFpu
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019-01: New releases of Chrome+Android+ARM rely on NEON support.
histogram.summary: Reports the FPU capabilities of the Android ARM device. This is recorded once per browser session during startup.

histogram.name: Android.AutofillAssistant.DropOutReason
histogram.summary: Reports the drop out reason of an Autofill Assistant script. It is recorded when Autofill Assistant is shut down or terminated. The AA_START value is used as a baseline and counts the number of attempted starts of Autofill Assistant flows. The sum of all drop out reasons should be the same as the number of attempted starts (i.e. AA_START).

histogram.name: Android.AutofillAssistant.FeatureModuleInstallation
histogram.summary: Reports the installation status of the DFM, e.g. installation deferred, foreground installation succeeded etc. The metric cannot currently distinguish whether the deferred installation succeeded or not.

histogram.name: Android.AutofillAssistant.OnBoarding
histogram.summary: Reports the basic user interactions that can happen in regard on boarding. It is recorded on Autofill Assistant startup and during the on boarding.

histogram.name: Android.AutofillAssistant.PaymentRequest.AutofillChanged
histogram.summary: Reports whether autofill information was changed during an autofill assistant payment request, or not. This is recorded every time the user sees the autofill assistant payment request.

histogram.name: Android.AutofillAssistant.PaymentRequest.FirstNameOnly
histogram.summary: Reports whether the user selected a profile which does not contain separate first and last name. This is recorded when the user confirms the payment request screen in autofill assistant provided that 'name' was one of the requested fields.

histogram.name: Android.AutofillAssistant.PaymentRequest.MandatoryPostalCode
histogram.summary: Reports on whether a billing postal code was required and whether the user ultimately succeeded or not. Also reports if the user had to change their profile or if the default profile already contained a billing postal code. This histogram is reported either when the user taps the 'continue' button of the autofill assistant payment request (and thus succeeds) or when autofill assistant closes during the action (i.e., the user aborted).

histogram.name: Android.AutofillAssistant.PaymentRequest.Prefilled
histogram.summary: Reports whether the autofill assistant payment request was successfully completed (i.e., the continue button was tapped) or not, and whether the payment request was initially pre-filled completely or not. This is recorded every time the user sees the autofill assistant payment request.

histogram.name: Android.BackgroundTaskScheduler.ExactTaskCreated
histogram.summary: Records that an exact task has been created. Recorded when the task is scheduled.

histogram.name: Android.BackgroundTaskScheduler.MigrationToProto
histogram.summary: Records the stored tasks migrated to the Protocol Buffer data format. The metric is recorded when the migration to the new format is being done. The migration is done at a restart / OS upgrade, if tasks stored in the old format are found.

histogram.name: Android.BackgroundTaskScheduler.TaskCanceled
histogram.summary: Records that a specific background task has been canceled.

histogram.name: Android.BackgroundTaskScheduler.TaskCreated.WithExpiration
histogram.summary: Records that a specific task has been created with an expiration deadline. Recorded when the task is scheduled.

histogram.name: Android.BackgroundTaskScheduler.TaskCreated.WithoutExpiration
histogram.summary: Records that a specific task has been created without the expiration feature activated. Recorded when the task is scheduled.

histogram.name: Android.BackgroundTaskScheduler.TaskExpired
histogram.summary: Records that a specific background task has expired. Recorded before the task is to be started.

histogram.name: Android.BackgroundTaskScheduler.TaskLoadedNative
histogram.summary: Records that a specific background task has loaded native.

histogram.name: Android.BackgroundTaskScheduler.TaskLoadedNative.FullBrowser
histogram.summary: Records that a specific background task has loaded native in Full Browser Mode.

histogram.name: Android.BackgroundTaskScheduler.TaskLoadedNative.ReducedMode
histogram.summary: Records that a specific background task has loaded native in Reduced Mode.

histogram.name: Android.BackgroundTaskScheduler.TaskScheduled.Failure
histogram.summary: Records that a specific background task has failed to be scheduled.

histogram.name: Android.BackgroundTaskScheduler.TaskScheduled.Success
histogram.summary: Records that a specific background task has been successfully scheduled.

histogram.name: Android.BackgroundTaskScheduler.TaskStarted
histogram.summary: Records that a specific background task has been started.

histogram.name: Android.BackgroundTaskScheduler.TaskStopped
histogram.summary: Records that a specific background task has been stopped by Background Task Scheduler before it was able to complete itself.

histogram.name: Android.ChildProcessLauncher.OnServiceConnectedTime
histogram.obsolete: Removed 08/2017. Made decision in issue 736066 with already acquired data. So this is no longer needed.
histogram.summary: Measure time from bindService call to onServiceConnected. This is part of launching child services on Android that's under Android's control. Recorded in the first onServiceConnected of a connection.

histogram.name: Android.ChildProcessLauncher.OnServiceConnectedTimedOut
histogram.obsolete: Removed 08/2017. Made decision in issue 736066 with already acquired data. So this is no longer needed.
histogram.summary: Boolean histogram that records whether bindServiced timed out. Timeout is recorded in a delayed task, and success is recorded in onServiceConnected callback.

histogram.name: Android.ChromeActivity.Type
histogram.summary: Recorded at the same time as "CustomTabs.Visible", this metric should eventually replace that one. This additionally distinguishes between Trusted Web Activities, WebAPKs and Webapps.

histogram.name: Android.ChromeBrowserProvider.CallerHasPermission
histogram.summary: Android: Count of requests to Chrome browser which are granted to other apps to read/write user's bookmarks and history. Only records cases when the app has permission according to Android's permission manager.

histogram.name: Android.ChromeBrowserProvider.SignaturePassed
histogram.summary: Android: Count of requests to Chrome browser which are granted to other apps to read/write user's bookmarks and history. Only records cases where the app is a system app or signed by Google.

histogram.name: Android.ChromeHome.DurationOpen
histogram.summary: The duration the Chrome Home bottom sheet was open.

histogram.name: Android.ChromeHome.OpenReason
histogram.summary: The reason the bottom sheet was opened.

histogram.name: Android.ChromeHome.OpenSheetVelocity.Fail
histogram.obsolete: Removed 03/2018. Chrome Home is being deprecated.
histogram.summary: Records the reciprocal of the velocity of a swipe that did not result in the bottom sheet opening. This value is recorded in microseconds per dp traveled and is only recorded if the "chrome-home-swipe-logic" experiment is set to "velocity".

histogram.name: Android.ChromeHome.OpenSheetVelocity.Navigation
histogram.obsolete: Removed 03/2018. Chrome Home is being deprecated.
histogram.summary: Records the reciprocal of the velocity of a swipe that resulted in the bottom sheet opening and the user navigating to a URL or a different sheet content. This value is recorded in microseconds per dp traveled.

histogram.name: Android.ChromeHome.OpenSheetVelocity.NoNavigation
histogram.obsolete: Removed 03/2018. Chrome Home is being deprecated.
histogram.summary: Records the reciprocal of the velocity of a swipe that resulted in the bottom sheet opening but the user taking no action (i.e. the next action is the sheet closing). This value is recorded in microseconds per dp traveled.

histogram.name: Android.ChromeHome.OpenSheetVelocity.Success
histogram.obsolete: Removed 03/2018. Chrome Home is being deprecated.
histogram.summary: Records the reciprocal velocity of a swipe that resulted in the bottom sheet opening. This value is recorded in microseconds per dp traveled and is only recorded if the "chrome-home-swipe-logic" experiment is set to "velocity".

histogram.name: Android.ChromeHome.Promo.Result.Menu
histogram.obsolete: Removed 01/2018 with the removal of the first version of Chrome Home.
histogram.summary: The result of showing the Chrome Home promo when launched from the overflow menu. This action can only be performed if Chrome Home is enabled.

histogram.name: Android.ChromeHome.Promo.Result.NTP
histogram.obsolete: Removed 01/2018 with the removal of the first version of Chrome Home.
histogram.summary: The result of showing the Chrome Home promo when launched from the NTP. This action can only be performed if Chrome Home is disabled.

histogram.name: Android.ChromeHome.Promo.Result.Startup
histogram.obsolete: Removed 01/2018 with the removal of the first version of Chrome Home.
histogram.summary: The result of showing the Chrome Home promo when launched from startup. This action can only be performed if Chrome Home is disabled.

histogram.name: Android.ChromeHome.Promo.ShowReason
histogram.obsolete: Removed 01/2018 with the removal of the first version of Chrome Home.
histogram.summary: The reason the Chrome Home promo was shown.

histogram.name: Android.ChromeHome.Survey.DownloadResponseCode
histogram.summary: The response code of the completed survey download request.

histogram.name: Android.ChromeHome.Survey.InfoBarClosingState
histogram.summary: If the infobar was visible when it was closed and if it was closed directly or not.

histogram.name: Android.ChromeHome.Survey.ShowSurvey
histogram.summary: Whether or not the survey was successfully shown after its download.

histogram.name: Android.ChromeHome.Survey.SurveyFilteringResults
histogram.summary: The result of the survey filtering process. Each enum represents a different filter that caught the user. This is recorded on cold starts when we check if a user qualifies for a survey.

histogram.name: Android.ChromeHome.TimeBetweenCloseAndNextOpen
histogram.summary: The time between the last time the Chrome Home bottom sheet was closed and the next time it was opened.

histogram.name: Android.ChromeHome.TimeToFirstOpen
histogram.summary: The time between app creation and the first open of the Chrome Home bottom sheet for this run of Chrome.

histogram.name: Android.ChromeHome.UserPreference.Enabled
histogram.summary: Records whether or not the user preference for Chrome Home is set to enabled. This is recorded whenever the browser is restarted and the state of Chrome Home is first checked. This metric is only recorded if the user's preference is set.

histogram.name: Android.CompressedResources.ExtractionBlockingTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time Chrome spends blocked on resource extraction. Generally, extraction is completed in the background but in certain cases a main-thread getResources() call may block on resource extraction.

histogram.name: Android.CompressedResources.ExtractionStatus
histogram.summary: Describes the result of Android resource extraction, and is recorded once per browser start as part of deferred startup (not at time of extraction).

histogram.name: Android.CompressedResources.ExtractionTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time spent on resource extraction, whether or not extraction was performed in the background or foreground.

histogram.name: Android.ContactsPicker.AddressHasDerivedField
histogram.summary: Measures whether the address selected from the contacts picker has derived fields. Derived fields refer to the individual attributes of a full address, such as city, country, region, or postcode.

histogram.name: Android.ContactsPicker.ContactCount
histogram.summary: Measures the total number of contacts shown in the contacts picker. Measured once when the Contacts Picker dialog is dismissed.

histogram.name: Android.ContactsPicker.DialogAction
histogram.summary: Records which action the user takes in the ContactsPickerDialog. Measured once when the Contacts Picker dialog is dismissed.

histogram.name: Android.ContactsPicker.PropertiesRequested
histogram.summary: The contact properties requested by the website (names, emails, telephone numbers). Measured once when the Contacts Picker dialog is dismissed.

histogram.name: Android.ContactsPicker.SelectCount
histogram.summary: Measures the total number of contacts selected in the contacts picker. Measured once when the Contacts Picker dialog is dismissed.

histogram.name: Android.ContactsPicker.SelectPercentage
histogram.summary: Measures the percentage of contacts selected in the contacts picker. Measured once when the Contacts Picker dialog is dismissed.

histogram.name: Android.CustomFeedback.Category
histogram.obsolete: Removed 12/2018. CustomFeedback never shipped to 100% and is no longer planned for launch.
histogram.summary: Recorded when the user selects a category button when in the Custom Feedback UI.

histogram.name: Android.CustomFeedback.CategoryDetails
histogram.obsolete: Removed 12/2018. CustomFeedback never shipped to 100% and is no longer planned for launch.
histogram.summary: Recorded when a user selects an option related to their problem within the Custom Feedback UI.

histogram.name: Android.DarkTheme.DarkSearchRequested
histogram.summary: Records if Chrome altered a google homepage/search request to include the dark theme. Recorded only when navigating to google or google search. Histogram total count will be the total number of requests to the google hompage or google search.

histogram.name: Android.DarkTheme.EnabledReason
histogram.summary: Records the reason why dark theme is enabled when dark theme is visible to user.

histogram.name: Android.DarkTheme.EnabledState
histogram.summary: Records whether dark theme is enabled or not on cold start and when the enabled state changes.

histogram.name: Android.DarkTheme.Preference.State
histogram.summary: Records user theme preference of system default, light or dark theme on cold start and when the theme preference changes.

histogram.name: Android.DeviceSize.LargestDisplaySize
histogram.summary: Records the largest display dimension in dp during deferred startup. The display size is not affected by Android N multi-window mode. Clamped at 200dp to 1200dp.

histogram.name: Android.DeviceSize.SmallestDisplaySize
histogram.summary: Records the smallest display dimension in dp during deferred startup. The display size is not affected by Android N multi-window mode. Clamped at 0 to 1000dp.

histogram.name: Android.DirectAction.Perform
histogram.summary: Counts calls to Activity.onPerformDirectAction made by the system on behalf of the assist app and which action was performed, if any. Available starting with Android Q.

histogram.name: Android.Download.InfoBar.CloseButtonClicked
histogram.summary: Records the state of the download infobar when the user taps on the infobar's close button.

histogram.name: Android.Download.InfoBar.Shown
histogram.summary: Records various types of messages representing the state of the downloads as they are shown on the download infobar.

histogram.name: Android.Download.Rename.Dialog.Action
histogram.summary: Records various actions taken by the user on the rename dialog in download home.

histogram.name: Android.Download.Rename.Result
histogram.summary: Records various error codes when users try to rename a download in a download home, recorded when backend rename callback is compeleted.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.Cancel
histogram.summary: Recorded when a download is cancelled from Java.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.Chips.Enabled
histogram.summary: Records the number of chips being shown on download home. Recorded during initialization and also during any list change.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.Filter
histogram.summary: Recorded when the user selects a filter from the Chrome download manager navigation drawer.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.ForegroundServiceLifecycle
histogram.summary: Records instances where the foreground undergoes a lifecycle change (when the foreground starts, changes pinned notification, or stops).

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.InitialCount
histogram.summary: The number of non-incognito download items displayed when the download UI is initialized.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.InitialCount.Viewed
histogram.summary: The number of non-incognito download items displayed that are already viewed by the user at any time, recorded when the download UI is initialized.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.Item.OpenFailed
histogram.summary: Recorded when a download fails to open.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.Item.OpenSucceeded
histogram.summary: Recorded when a download is opened.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.List.Section.Menu.Images.Action
histogram.obsolete: Removed October 2019. The feature was dropped during implementation review.
histogram.summary: Recorded when a menu action is taken on the images section.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.List.View.Action
histogram.summary: The count of Download Home list entry actions taken.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.Menu.Action
histogram.summary: The count of Download Home top level menu actions taken.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.Menu.Delete.SelectedCount
histogram.summary: The number of items selected when performing a multi-delete action.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.Menu.Share.SelectedCount
histogram.summary: The number of items selected when performing a multi-share action.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.NotificationInteraction
histogram.summary: Records instances where a user interacts with a download notification (ie. clicks on, pauses, resumes, cancels).

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.NotificationLaunch
histogram.obsolete: Removed July 2019. The relaunch rarely happens.
histogram.summary: Records instances when a notification is being launched for the first time or relaunched due to the need to dissociate the notification from the foreground (only on API less than 24).

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.NotificationsCount.ForegroundDisabled
histogram.summary: Records the number of notifications that are already existing (presumably displayed) when a new notification is being launched to help understand the frequency of multiple downloads with downloads as a foreground service disabled.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.NotificationsCount.ForegroundEnabled
histogram.summary: Records the number of notifications that are already existing (presumably displayed) when a new notification is being launched to help understand the frequency of multiple downloads with downloads as a foreground service enabled.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.OpenSource.Audio
histogram.summary: Records how users open audio download files on Android.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.OpenSource.Other
histogram.summary: Records the entry point for users opening downloaded files on Android, that are not audio nor video.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.OpenSource.Video
histogram.summary: Records how users open video download files on Android.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.OtherExtensions.InitialCount
histogram.summary: The extension type for non-incognito download items that match the "other" filter type. Recorded when the download UI is initialized.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.OtherExtensions.OpenFailed
histogram.summary: The extension type for downloads that match the "other" filter type. Recorded when a download fails to open.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.OtherExtensions.OpenSucceeded
histogram.summary: The extension type for downloads that match the "other" filter type. Recorded when a download is opened.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.OtherExtensions.Share
histogram.summary: The extension type for downloads that match the "other" filter type. Recorded when downloads are shared through the download manager.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.ServiceStopped.DownloadForeground
histogram.summary: Records instances of DownloadForegroundService stops. The number of expected stops (stopped) and unexpected stops (task removed, low memory, etc) can be compared to get a sense of how often the service crashes to see how it handles restarts. For example, the number of times the service is stopped because of low memory can be compared to the number of times the service has been destroyed to see how frequently it occurs. Or, the number of start sticky's will indicate how often the service is able to restart due to an unexpected stop.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.ServiceStopped.DownloadNotification
histogram.summary: Records instances of DownloadNotificationService stops. The number of expected stops (stopped) and unexpected stops (task removed, low memory, etc) can be compared to get a sense of how often the service crashes to see how it handles restarts. For example, the number of times the service is stopped because of low memory can be compared to the number of times the service has been destroyed to see how frequently it occurs. Or, the number of start sticky's will indicate how often the service is able to restart due to an unexpected stop.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.Share.Count
histogram.summary: The number of downloads shared at the same time.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.Share.FileTypes
histogram.summary: Recorded when downloads are shared through the download manager. A recorded value of "All" indicates that multiple types of downloads were shared.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.ShowStorageInfo
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2016 with code removal of download home V1 to follow.
histogram.summary: Recorded when the user clicks the info button on download home to toggle the storage info. The state recorded is after the visibility is toggled.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.SpaceUsed
histogram.summary: The percentage of total storage downloads consume.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.Thumbnail.MaxRequiredStretch
histogram.summary: Records the required stretch of the smaller dimension before displaying an image on download home. Measured in percentage.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.ViewRetentionTime.Audio
histogram.summary: The duration between when an audio file is downloaded and when the file is opened, only recorded when the user opens the file in download home UI.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadManager.ViewRetentionTime.Video
histogram.summary: The duration between when a video file is downloaded and when the file is opened, only recorded when the user opens the file in download home UI.

histogram.name: Android.DownloadPage.OpenSource
histogram.summary: Records the entry point for users opening the download page on Android.

histogram.name: Android.FeatureModules.AvailabilityStatus
histogram.summary: Availability status for each dynamic feature module. Reported once per Chrome start. This metric's purpose is to measure a feature module's install penetration over time.

histogram.name: Android.FeatureModules.CachedAwakeInstallDuration
histogram.summary: Duration of successful installs for each dynamic feature module. Only contains install durations for modules first requested *before* Chrome started last and may therefore be installed from cache. Omits deep sleep time.

histogram.name: Android.FeatureModules.CachedInstallDuration
histogram.summary: Duration of successful installs for each dynamic feature module. Only contains install durations for modules first requested *before* Chrome started last and may therefore be installed from cache.

histogram.name: Android.FeatureModules.InstallingStatus
histogram.summary: Install status counter for each dynamic feature module. Recorded during on-demand and deferred installs.

histogram.name: Android.FeatureModules.InstallStatus
histogram.summary: Install status for each dynamic feature module. Recorded after the installation has finished.

histogram.name: Android.FeatureModules.StartupTime
histogram.summary: Length of time during startup used by module framework code. This is our metric to improve and used to guard against regressions.

histogram.name: Android.FeatureModules.UncachedAwakeInstallDuration
histogram.summary: Duration of successful installs for each dynamic feature module. Only contains install durations of modules first requested *after* Chrome started last and are therefore unlikely to be installed from cache. Omits deep sleep time.

histogram.name: Android.FeatureModules.UncachedInstallDuration
histogram.summary: Duration of successful installs for each dynamic feature module. Only contains install durations of modules first requested *after* Chrome started last and are therefore unlikely to be installed from cache.

histogram.name: Android.HistoryPage.OpenSelected
histogram.summary: The number of selected items the user opened in new tabs from the native Android history page.

histogram.name: Android.HistoryPage.RemoveSelected
histogram.summary: The number of selected items the user removed from the native Android history page.

histogram.name: Android.InstantApps.ApiCallDuration2
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time spent in the getInstantAppIntent() API call.

histogram.name: Android.InstantApps.ApiCallDurationWithApp
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time spent in the getInstantAppIntent() API call when the API was able to find an Instant App for the URL. This is recorded for external intents coming into Chrome only and measures the delay before we dispatch the intent to Instant Apps.

histogram.name: Android.InstantApps.ApiCallDurationWithoutApp
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time spent in the getInstantAppIntent() API call when the API was not able to find an Instant App for the URL. This is recorded for external intents coming into Chrome that get checked for their Instant Apps eligibility, but end up being loaded in Chrome. It's critical to keep this latency low for our startup performance.

histogram.name: Android.InstantApps.CallSource
histogram.summary: For intents to load a web page in browser from Instant Apps, records where the request to load came from.

histogram.name: Android.InstantApps.DirectInstantAppsIntent
histogram.summary: Records events that occur when a user clicks on an intent:// URL that is dispatched to instant apps. The intent:// URLs to the Instant Apps are explicitly disabled unless they are launched from Google SERP, but we record other attempts as well.

histogram.name: Android.InstantApps.EligiblePageLoaded
histogram.summary: Records whether any given successful page load (for http and https schemes only) could have been handled by an Instant App.

histogram.name: Android.InstantApps.FallbackDuration
histogram.summary: Measures the time from when we first received an eligible intent for Instant Apps to the time we processed it in the case where the Instant Apps activity had to invoke a fallback intent. This happens only on Android N and older.

histogram.name: Android.InstantApps.HandleIntentDuration
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time spent triaging an incoming event to decide whether it needs to be routed to Instant Apps. This is time from ChromeLauncherActivity.onCreate() call to the time either startActivity() is called or the request proceeds. This is a superset of Android.InstantApps.ApiCallDurationWith[out]App.

histogram.name: Android.InstantApps.ShouldShowBanner
histogram.summary: Logs the reasons the banner is shown or hidden on an instant apps eligible pages. Triggered every time asynchronous Instant Apps API indicates the currently loaded page is instant apps eligible. Does not include Instant Apps eligible pages loaded directly through the omnibox.

histogram.name: Android.Intent.LaunchExternalAppFormSubmitHasUserGesture
histogram.summary: When an external application was launched as a result of a form submit, this tracks whether there was a user gesture associated with the submit.

histogram.name: Android.Intent.OverrideUrlLoadingIntentAction
histogram.summary: When a site navigates to a URL that can be parsed as an Intent, record the action of that Intent.

histogram.name: Android.IntentHeaders
histogram.summary: Records the usage of the Browser.EXTRA_HEADERS field for Intents that Chrome receives, breaking down by type of header and by whether the launching app was first or third party.

histogram.name: Android.IntentNonSafelistedHeaderNames
histogram.summary: Records hashes of header names used in the Browser.EXTRA_HEADERS fields for Intents that Chrome receives that aren't on the CORS header safelist. This can be used to match against known types of headers to measure their frequencies.

histogram.name: Android.IsLastSharedAppInfoRetrieved
histogram.summary: Signifies whether the last shared app information is retrieved successfully or not. This is logged when configuring the direct sharing menu item.

histogram.name: Android.KernelVersion
histogram.summary: Reports the kernel major and minor version from the utsname.release field. The low 16 bits of each version are combined into a 32-bit integer. The range is from [0x00020006,0x0004000a] to track kernel versions 2.6 through 4.10.

histogram.name: Android.Language.UiIsSystemLanguage
histogram.summary: Reports whether the Chrome UI language is the same as the Android system lanaguage. Recorded once per top-level ChromeActivty start.

histogram.name: Android.Language.WrongLanguageAfterResume
histogram.summary: Reports whether Chrome was started in a language other than the system language but we support the system language. That can happen if the user changes the system language and the required language split cannot be installed in time.

histogram.name: Android.MainActivity.ExplicitMainViewIntentDispatched.OnCreate
histogram.summary: Whether VIEW intent sent explicitly to .Main activity was dispatched by onCreate().

histogram.name: Android.MainActivity.ExplicitMainViewIntentDispatched.OnNewIntent
histogram.summary: Whether VIEW intent sent explicitly to .Main activity was dispatched by onNewIntent().

histogram.name: Android.MainActivity.UndispatchedExplicitMainViewIntentSource
histogram.summary: Sources (ExternalAppId) of undispatched VIEW intents sent explicitly to .Main activity alias.

histogram.name: Android.ManageSpace.ActionTaken
histogram.summary: Recorded when the user presses a button in the 'Manage Space' screen for Chrome. Note: the 'Clear App Data' option is likely incorrect, as the result of that button disables UMA recording (as we factory reset the app).

histogram.name: Android.ManageSpace.ClearUnimportantTime
histogram.summary: Records the time it takes to delete all unimportant sites when the user initiates the 'Clear Unimportant Sites' action in the 'Manage Space' screen for Chrome.

histogram.name: Android.ManageSpace.TotalDiskUsageMB
histogram.summary: Recorded when the user opens the 'Manage Space' screen for Chrome, after the disk usage is calculated. The disk usage value represents the accumulated disk usage by websites.

histogram.name: Android.ManageSpace.UnimportantDiskUsageMB
histogram.summary: Recorded when the user opens the 'Manage Space' screen for Chrome, after the disk usage is calculated. The disk usage value represents the accumulated disk usage by websites that are not considered 'important'.

histogram.name: Android.MemoryPressureMonitor.GetMyMemoryState.Failed.Time
histogram.summary:
The duration of each failed ActivityManager.getMyMemoryState() call made by MemoryPressureMonitor.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Android.MemoryPressureMonitor.GetMyMemoryState.Succeeded.Time
histogram.summary:
The duration of each successful ActivityManager.getMyMemoryState() call made by MemoryPressureMonitor.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Android.MemoryPressureNotification
histogram.summary: Memory pressure notifications sent by Android through ComponentCallbacks2. This metric was added 04/2018; for prior data for the browser process see MemoryAndroid.NotificationBackground and MemoryAndroid.NotificationForeground.

histogram.name: Android.ModalDialog.SecurityFilteredTouchResult
histogram.summary: Records the action taken on a security sensitive touch event in modal dialog. As of M84 it is used for site premission dialogs.

histogram.name: Android.ModerateBindingCount
histogram.summary: The number of moderate bindings which were kept while Chrome process is in the foreground. This is logged right before Chrome process goes into the background.

histogram.name: Android.MultiInstanceMigration.FailedToRenameMetadataFile
histogram.summary: Renaming the old tab metadata file failed during multi-instance migration. Only true is recorded.

histogram.name: Android.MultiInstanceMigration.NewMetadataFileExists
histogram.summary: The new tab metadata file already existed when multi-instance migration was attempted. Only true is recorded.

histogram.name: Android.MultiWindowMode.Active
histogram.summary: Records on every metrics upload whether the activity is running in Android N multi-window mode or not.

histogram.name: Android.MultiWindowMode.IsTabletScreenWidthBelow600
histogram.summary: Records whether the screen width is below 600dp when the activity is in Android N multi-window mode. True if the screen width is less than 600dp and false if is greater than or equal to 600dp.

histogram.name: Android.MultiWindowMode.ScreenHeight
histogram.summary: Records the screen height in dp when the activity is in Android N multi-window mode. Clamped at 200dp to 1200dp.

histogram.name: Android.MultiWindowMode.ScreenWidth
histogram.summary: Records the screen width in dp when the activity is in Android N multi-window mode. Clamped at 200dp to 1200dp.

histogram.name: Android.MultiWindowMode.TabletScreenWidth
histogram.summary: Records the screen width in dp when the activity is in Android N multi-window mode and the width is below 600dp.

histogram.name: Android.NativeBackgroundTask.TaskFinished
histogram.summary: Records that a specific background task has finished cleanly (i.e. no unexpected exits because of chrome crash or OOM). This includes tasks that have been stopped due to timeout.

histogram.name: Android.NativeBackgroundTask.TaskFinished.FullBrowser
histogram.summary: Records that a specific native background task started in Full Browser Mode has finished cleanly. See Android.NativeBackgroundTask.TaskFinished for more details.

histogram.name: Android.NativeBackgroundTask.TaskFinished.ReducedMode
histogram.summary: Records that a specific native background task started in Reduced Mode has finished cleanly. It is possible that Full Browser has launched while the task was running. See Android.NativeBackgroundTask.TaskFinished for more details.

histogram.name: Android.NativeBackgroundTask.TaskStarted
