histogram.summary: Records that a specific native background task has been started. This does not consider tasks that are marked as DONE or RESCHEDULED before a call to start native is made (regardless of whether native is already loaded).

histogram.name: Android.NativeBackgroundTask.TaskStarted.FullBrowser
histogram.summary: Records that a specific native background task has been started in Full Browser Mode. See Android.NativeBackgroundTask.TaskStarted for more details.

histogram.name: Android.NativeBackgroundTask.TaskStarted.ReducedMode
histogram.summary: Records that a specific native background task has been started in Reduced Mode. See Android.NativeBackgroundTask.TaskStarted for more details.

histogram.name: Android.NativeLibraryPreloader.Result.Browser
histogram.summary: The return value of NativeLibraryPreloader.loadLibrary() in browser process, is recorded once per browser process start.

histogram.name: Android.NativeLibraryPreloader.Result.Renderer
histogram.summary: The return value of NativeLibraryPreloader.loadLibrary() in renderer process, is recorded once per renderer process start.

histogram.name: Android.NativeStartupBridge.LoadFullBrowser
histogram.summary: The number of times full-browser mode is requested to be loaded from native code (running in reduced mode).

histogram.name: Android.NTP.Impression
histogram.summary: Counts impressions of the NTP on Android. It also counts potential impressions. A potential impression is a place where it would make sense to open the NTP instead of the current view. E.g. the no-tab view is counted as a potential impression since this view could be replaced with the NTP. For better comparison regular impressions are also counted. The no-tab view counting happens right after the user closed the last tab and the regular impression counting happens at construction time of the NTP (=whenever a new tab is opened).

histogram.name: Android.OfflineItems.StateAtCancel
histogram.summary: State of a download or offline page request at user-initiated cancel.

histogram.name: Android.Omnibox.InvalidMatch
histogram.summary: Recorded every time AutocompleteController.java interacts with autocomplete_controller_android.cc via an index reference to an item. Indicates how often the match referenced by Java is different from the match referenced by C++ at that position.

histogram.name: Android.Omnibox.SuggestionList.LayoutTime
histogram.summary:
Records time spent on positioning all suggestions on screen. This histogram is recorded every time a new suggestion list is received.
Values recorded here should generally aim to be small, likely below 2ms range. Higher values are an indicator of a performance problem.
Layout operation should take place just once when suggestion view is shown for the first time.
This metric is reported for all users and uses 100microsecond granularity.
When comparing Durations measured here, also compare Total Counts for a correct indication of how frequently views are laid out and how the overall time spent performing layout operation changed.
This histogram is related to Android.Omnibox.SuggestionView.Reused, Android.Omnibox.SuggestionList.MeasureTime, Android.Omnibox.SuggestionView.CreateTime.

histogram.name: Android.Omnibox.SuggestionList.MeasureTime
histogram.summary:
Records time spent on measuring all suggestions ahead of positioning them on screen. This histogram is recorded every time a new suggestion list is received.
Values recorded here should generally aim to be small, likely below 2ms range. Higher values are an indicator of a performance problem.
Measure operation should take place just once when suggestion view is shown for the first time.
This metric is reported for all users and uses 100microsecond granularity.
When comparing Durations measured here, also compare Total Counts for a correct indication of how frequently views are measured and how the overall time spent measuring views changed.
This histogram is related to Android.Omnibox.SuggestionView.Reused, Android.Omnibox.SuggestionList.LayoutTime, Android.Omnibox.SuggestionView.CreateTime.

histogram.name: Android.Omnibox.SuggestionView.CreateTime
histogram.summary:
Records time spent creating a single suggestion.
Values recorded here should generally aim to be small, below 1ms range. Higher values may be an indicator of a performance problem.
This metric is reported for all users and uses 100microsecond granularity.
When comparing Durations measured here, also compare Total Counts for a correct indication of how frequently views are created and how the overall time spent creating views changed.
This histogram is related to Android.Omnibox.SuggestionList.LayoutTime, Android.Omnibox.SuggestionList.MeasureTime, Android.Omnibox.SuggestionView.Reused.

histogram.name: Android.Omnibox.SuggestionView.Reused
histogram.summary:
Records number of times a suggestion was successfully reused.
Values recorded here tell how efficiently View reuse algorithms operate and how frequently these algorithms have managed to prevent unnecessary view creation. Higher Reuse counts indicate better performance.
When analysing this metric, consider checking also the Total Counts of Android.Omnibox.SuggestionView.CreateTime metric for a better information regarding how view creations and re-uses have changed:
* If this metric increased while the Total Count of the CreateTime decreased, the change has positive effect.
* If this metric increased (or decreased) and there is a similar change in Total Counts of CreateTime, then the effect is neutral.
* If this metric decreased, while the Total Count increased, then the change has a negative effect (fewer reuses, more time spent creating views)
This histogram is related to Android.Omnibox.SuggestionList.LayoutTime, Android.Omnibox.SuggestionList.MeasureTime, Android.Omnibox.SuggestionView.CreateTime.

histogram.name: Android.OmniboxFocusReason
histogram.summary: Records how the omnibox was focused.

histogram.name: Android.PackageStats.CacheSize
histogram.summary: Records the size of the user's cache at startup on Android O and above.

histogram.name: Android.PackageStats.CodeSize
histogram.summary: At startup, records the space on disk taken by Chrome application including APK resources, compiled libraries, and extracted native code. Only recorded for Android O and above.

histogram.name: Android.PackageStats.DataSize
histogram.summary: Records the size of the user's data directory at startup on Android O and above. Does not include cache size.

histogram.name: Android.Permissions.ReadStorage
histogram.obsolete: Removed from the code in July 2019. See https://crbug.com/975701.
histogram.summary: At startup, records whether the Chrome application has been granted the read storage permission on Android.

histogram.name: Android.PhotoPicker.BitmapScalerTask
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time the BitmapScaler spent scaling a bitmap.

histogram.name: Android.PhotoPicker.CacheHits
histogram.summary: Measures the total number of images served from the high-res cache during the lifetime of the dialog.

histogram.name: Android.PhotoPicker.DecodeRequests
histogram.summary: Measures the total number of images sent for decoding during the lifetime of the dialog.

histogram.name: Android.PhotoPicker.DecoderHostFailureOutOfMemory
histogram.summary: The percentage of out-of-memory decoding failures, per batch of photos (not videos) decoded. Intended to help identify if there are devices where decoding fails 100% of the time. Be wary at looking at other numbers in the histogram, as a value emitted at, say, 50% may represent one failure in a two-item decoding or 50 failures in a hundred-item decoding. These are qualitatively different experiences for a user.

histogram.name: Android.PhotoPicker.DecoderHostVideoFileError
histogram.summary: The percentage of file error decoding failures, per batch of videos (not photos) decoded. Intended to help identify if there are devices where decoding fails 100% of the time. Be wary at looking at other numbers in the histogram, as a value emitted at, say, 50% may represent one failure in a two-item decoding or 50 failures in a hundred-item decoding. These are qualitatively different experiences for a user.

histogram.name: Android.PhotoPicker.DecoderHostVideoIoError
histogram.summary: The percentage of IO error decoding failures, per batch of videos (not photos) decoded. Intended to help identify if there are devices where decoding fails 100% of the time. Be wary at looking at other numbers in the histogram, as a value emitted at, say, 50% may represent one failure in a two-item decoding or 50 failures in a hundred-item decoding. These are qualitatively different experiences for a user.

histogram.name: Android.PhotoPicker.DecoderHostVideoRuntimeError
histogram.summary: The percentage of runtime error decoding failures, per batch of videos (not photos) decoded. Intended to help identify if there are devices where decoding fails 100% of the time. Be wary at looking at other numbers in the histogram, as a value emitted at, say, 50% may represent one failure in a two-item decoding or 50 failures in a hundred-item decoding. These are qualitatively different experiences for a user.

histogram.name: Android.PhotoPicker.DecoderHostVideoUnknownError
histogram.summary: The percentage of unknown decoding error failures, per batch of videos (not photos) decoded. Intended to help identify if there are devices where decoding fails 100% of the time. Be wary at looking at other numbers in the histogram, as a value emitted at, say, 50% may represent one failure in a two-item decoding or 50 failures in a hundred-item decoding. These are qualitatively different experiences for a user.

histogram.name: Android.PhotoPicker.DialogAction
histogram.summary: Records which action the user takes in the PhotoPickerDialog.

histogram.name: Android.PhotoPicker.EnumeratedFiles
histogram.summary: Measures the total number of files returned from the enumeration operation. Measured once at the end of the enumeration operation.

histogram.name: Android.PhotoPicker.EnumeratedRate
histogram.summary: Measures the enumeration rate (files per tenths of a second). Measured once at the end of the enumeration operation.

histogram.name: Android.PhotoPicker.EnumerationTime
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time the BitmapScaler spent enumerating files on disk. Measured once at the end of the enumeration operation.

histogram.name: Android.PhotoPicker.ExifOrientation
histogram.summary: Records whether the EXIF orientation directive is present and what it is set to. Collected once per decoded image, but only on Android N and above (and when no IO error occurs).

histogram.name: Android.PhotoPicker.ImageByteCount
histogram.summary: Measures the byte count of a decoded image. Only recorded on successful decodes.

histogram.name: Android.PhotoPicker.ImageDecodeTime
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time it takes the decoder to decode one image. Only recorded on successful decodes.

histogram.name: Android.PhotoPicker.RequestProcessTime
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time it takes to process one image decode request (not video) end to end (RPC and actual decoding of the bits by utility process). Recorded whether or not the RPC/decode request was successful.

histogram.name: Android.PhotoPicker.RequestProcessTimeAnimation
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time it takes to decode all the frames needed to show the animation -- end to end (RPC and actual decoding of the bits by utility process). Recorded whether or not the RPC/decode request was successful.

histogram.name: Android.PhotoPicker.RequestProcessTimeThumbnail
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time it takes to process one video frame request end to end (RPC and actual decoding of the bits by utility process). Recorded whether or not the RPC/decode request was successful.

histogram.name: Android.PhotoPicker.UpscaleLowResBitmap
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time it takes to upscale a bitmap from the low-res cache. Recorded only when there a cache miss from the high-res bitmap but a cache hit from the low-res (placeholder) bitmap.

histogram.name: Android.PhotoPicker.VideoByteCount
histogram.summary: Measures the byte count of the first video thumbnail. Only recorded on successful decodes.

histogram.name: Android.PhotoPicker.VideoDecodeTimeAnimation
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time it takes the decoder to decode all the frames needed to display an animated thumbnail. Only recorded on successful decodes.

histogram.name: Android.PhotoPicker.VideoDecodeTimeThumbnail
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time it takes the decoder to decode the first frame of a video. Only recorded on successful decodes.

histogram.name: Android.PrepareMenu.OpenWebApkVisibilityCheck
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time spent querying for whether a WebAPK is already installed.

histogram.name: Android.RecentTabsManager.OtherDevices
histogram.summary: Records the number of other devices listed in recent tabs page when recent tabs page is loaded.

histogram.name: Android.RecentTabsManager.RecentlyClosedTabs
histogram.summary: Records the number of recently-closed tabs shown in recent tabs page when recent tabs page is loaded.

histogram.name: Android.RecentTabsManager.TotalTabs
histogram.summary: Records the total number of tabs listed in recent tabs page (sum of all tab from other devices plus recently-closed tabs) when recent tabs page is loaded.

histogram.name: Android.RestoreResult
histogram.summary: Whether Chrome tried to restore its settings from a previous device or installation, and what happened when it did. Only recorded on first run.

histogram.name: Android.SearchEngineChoice.ChosenSearchEngine
histogram.summary: The type of the search engine chosen by the user in Search Engine Choice flow.

histogram.name: Android.SearchEngineChoice.Events
histogram.summary: Counts occurences of various events related to Search Engine Choice feature.

histogram.name: Android.SearchEngineChoice.EventsV2
histogram.summary: Counts occurences of various events related to Search Engine Choice V2 feature.

histogram.name: Android.SearchEngineChoice.SearchEngineBeforeChoicePrompt
histogram.summary: The type of the search engine used before Search Engine Choice flow was presented.

histogram.name: Android.SeccompStatus.PhotoPickerSandbox
histogram.summary: Reports the status of the seccomp-bpf sandbox in photo picker decoding processes. Anything other than "Sandbox Engaged" indicates the sandbox is not turned on. See https://crbug.com/477049.

histogram.name: Android.SeccompStatus.Prctl
histogram.obsolete: Removed from the code in July 2019. See https://crbug.com/975749.
histogram.summary: Reports the level of kernel support for the seccomp-bpf sandbox using prctl(PR_SET_SECCOMP).

histogram.name: Android.SeccompStatus.RendererSandbox
histogram.summary: Reports the status of the seccomp-bpf sandbox in renderer processes. Anything other than "Sandbox Engaged" indicates the sandbox is not turned on. See https://crbug.com/477049.

histogram.name: Android.SeccompStatus.Syscall
histogram.summary: Reports the level of kernel support for the seccomp-bpf sandbox using the seccomp system call.

histogram.name: Android.SelectFileDialogImgCount
histogram.summary: Records the number of images selected in either the new Photo Picker or the standard Android file picker. Note: Only recorded in situations determined to warrant the new Photo Picker (not, for example, if mixed content, like images and text files, was requested).

histogram.name: Android.SelectFileDialogScope
histogram.summary: Records the scope of accepted content for a select file dialog when shown by a Web developer.

histogram.name: Android.StrictMode.CheckGooglePlayServicesTime
histogram.obsolete: Removed from code June 2019. See https://crbug.com/577190 for context.
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time due to a StrictMode violation from checking for whether play services is available.

histogram.name: Android.StrictMode.CheckGoogleSignedTime
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time due to a StrictMode violation from checking for whether a package is Google signed.

histogram.name: Android.StrictMode.DocumentModeSharedPrefs
histogram.obsolete: Removed 05/2016 in Issue 582539 with the removal of document mode.
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time due to a StrictMode violation from fetching the DocumentMode shared preferences file.

histogram.name: Android.StrictMode.DocumentTabStateLoad
histogram.obsolete: Removed 05/2016 in Issue 582539 with the removal of document mode.
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time due to a StrictMode violation from document mode loading its prioritized tab's state.

histogram.name: Android.StrictMode.DownloadsDir
histogram.obsolete: Removed from code June 2019. See https://crbug.com/508615 for context.
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time due to a StrictMode violation from fetching and possibly creating the downloads directory.

histogram.name: Android.StrictMode.NotificationUIBuildTime
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time due to a StrictMode violation from custom notification builder through date formatting.

histogram.name: Android.StrictMode.OverrideUrlLoadingTime
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time due to a StrictMode violation from calling out to Android activity manager.

histogram.name: Android.StrictMode.SnippetUIBuildTime
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time due to a StrictMode violation from snippet UI through date formatting.

histogram.name: Android.StrictMode.TabPersistentStore
histogram.summary: Measures time spent during StrictMode-whitelisted code in TabPersistentStore.

histogram.name: Android.StrictMode.ThumbnailCacheDir
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time due to a StrictMode violation from fetching for the thumbnail cache directory.

histogram.name: Android.StrictMode.WebappAuthenticatorMac
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time due to a StrictMode violation from looking up a webapp's MAC on startup.

histogram.name: Android.StrictMode.WebappDir
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time due to a StrictMode violation from fetching for creating the webapp directory.

histogram.name: Android.StrictMode.WebappSaveState
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time due to a StrictMode violation from reading and saving webapp state.

histogram.name: Android.StrictMode.WebappSharedPrefs
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time due to a StrictMode violation from fetching the Webapp shared preferences file.

histogram.name: Android.Survey.DownloadRequested
histogram.summary: Recorded when a survey download is requested. If the client already has successfully downloaded a survey, 'not requested' is recorded.

histogram.name: Android.Survey.DownloadResponseCode
histogram.summary: The response code of the completed survey download request.

histogram.name: Android.Survey.InfoBarClosingState
histogram.summary: If the infobar was visible when it was closed and if it was closed directly or not.

histogram.name: Android.Survey.ShowSurvey
histogram.summary: Whether or not the survey was successfully shown after its download.

histogram.name: Android.Survey.SurveyFilteringResults
histogram.summary: The result of the survey filtering process. Each enum represents a different filter that caught the user. This is recorded on cold starts when we check if a user qualifies for a survey.

histogram.name: Android.SysUtilsLowEndMatches
histogram.summary: Measures whether Chrome low-end detection logic based on RAM size matches the Android system utility value for isLowRamDevice(). This is recorded once per browser start and may be recorded in the renderer if isLowEndDevice() bit is checked.

histogram.name: Android.TabNavigationInterceptResult
histogram.summary: The distribution of results when running ExternalNavigationHandler, this shows how often we send intents to be handled by other applications.

histogram.name: Android.TabPersistentStore.MergeStateMetadataFileSize
histogram.summary: The number of bytes read for the other tab state's metadata file when merging tab states in Android N+.

histogram.name: Android.TabPersistentStore.MergeStateTabCount
histogram.summary: The number of normal and incognito tabs merged (for Android N+ multi-instance). This will be logged for both cold-start and non-cold-start merging assuming that the native library is loaded.

histogram.name: Android.TabPersistentStore.MergeStateTimePerTab
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time it takes to restore state for each merged tab (for Android N+ multi-instance). This will only be logged for non-cold-start merging, because restoring merged tabs is not distingushed from restoring regular tabs on cold start.

histogram.name: Android.TabPersistentStore.MetadataFileSize
histogram.summary: The number of bytes written for the tab metadata file.

histogram.name: Android.ThumbnailDiskStorage.CachedBitmap.Found
histogram.summary: Records the status of a thumbnail query from the thumbnail disk storage.

histogram.name: Android.ThumbnailDiskStorage.Size
histogram.summary: Measures the size of the thumbnail disk storage used. Recorded during initialization.

histogram.name: Android.ThumbnailProvider.BitmapCache.Size
histogram.summary: Measures the size of the bitmap cache used. Recorded whenever a bitmap is added to the cache.

histogram.name: Android.ThumbnailProvider.CachedBitmap.Found
histogram.summary: Records the status of a thumbnail query from the thumbnail provider in-memory cache.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.AndroidX.ApiCall
histogram.summary: Records calls to WebView APIs from AndroidX. Some AndroidX-originating calls will plumb through the framework, for example, if methods that are available in the framework are called from the equivalent method in AndroidX.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.AttachedToWindowIn30s
histogram.summary: Records if WebView was attached to a Android window in 30s since WebView was created. Nothing will be recorded if WebView has already been destroyed.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.AttachedToWindowIn3m
histogram.summary: Records if WebView was attached to a Android window in 3m since WebView was created. Nothing will be recorded if WebView has already been destroyed.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.AttachedToWindowIn5s
histogram.summary: Records if WebView was attached to a Android window in 5s since WebView was created. Nothing will be recorded if WebView has already been destroyed.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.AttachedToWindowTime
histogram.summary: Records how long it took for WebView to be attached to a Android window since WebView was created. If WebView is never attached to a window, nothing will be recorded.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.Callback.Counts
histogram.summary: Records the invocation count of WebView callbacks.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.ClearProxyOverride
histogram.summary: Records ClearProxyOverride calls.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.CookieManager.SameSiteAttributeValue
histogram.summary: The value of the cookie's SameSite attribute, if any. This is logged once per creation of a cookie via the CookieManager API. These cookies are double counted (once from CookieManager, logged in this histogram, and once from CanonicalCookie::Create, logged in Cookie.SameSiteAttributeValue).

histogram.name: Android.WebView.CookieManager.SameSiteNoneIsSecure
histogram.summary: If a cookie was SameSite=None, this records whether or not it was Secure. Logged once per attempt to set a SameSite=None cookie via CookieManager API. These cookies are double counted (once from CookieManager, logged in this histogram, and once from CookieMonster::SetCanonicalCookie, logged in Cookie.SameSiteNoneIsSecure).

histogram.name: Android.WebView.DevUi.AppLaunch
histogram.summary: Records the number of times the developer UI app was launched. This only ever logs the value "true" because we only care about the total count. This is logged in the developer UI app during Activity#onCreate.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.DevUi.CrashList.CollectionState
histogram.summary: Records the state of crash collection, as seen by the developer UI. This is recorded when launching the crash UI, but only if the UI is not already showing an error message for another problem (ex. WebView package error). This only logs the first state we detect: ex. if the user has enabled crash reporting by opting in and also with the flag UI, this only logs the flag UI case.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.DevUi.CrashList.NumberShown
histogram.summary: Records the number of crashes shown in the developer UI. This is logged each time the crash UI is launched and each time its list is refreshed. The UI currently limits the number of crashes at 20, so clients logging the maximum may have more logs sitting on disk.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.DevUi.DeveloperModeBlockingTime
histogram.summary: Records the time spent blocking WebView startup to check for developer mode (see "Android.WebView.DevUi.DeveloperModeEnabled"). This is recorded once during WebView startup, regardless of whether developer mode is enabled.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.DevUi.DeveloperModeEnabled
histogram.summary: Records whether or not WebView is starting up in "developer mode." This is a mode where the user has enabled debugging features through the WebView developer UI. This is recorded once during startup.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.DevUi.FlagLoadingBlockingTime
histogram.summary: Records the time spent blocking WebView startup to load flags from the developer UI. This is recorded only once during WebView startup, and only if we've already detected developer mode is enabled (see "Android.WebView.DevUi.DeveloperModeEnabled").

histogram.name: Android.WebView.DevUi.FragmentNavigation
histogram.summary: Records the user's navigation to a Fragment. "FromIntent" tracks navigations triggered by an Intent (ex. navigating to HomeFragment on launch) and "NavBar" tracks user interaction with the bottom navigation bar. "AnyMethod" should be the sum of the other two.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.DevUi.MenuSelection
histogram.summary: Records the user's selection in the developer UI overflow menu or action bar, recorded after each selection.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.DevUi.MonochromeIconStateToggled
histogram.summary: Records how the developer UI launcher icon for Monochrome was toggled: was it enabled or disabled? This can be recorded either from WebView's non-embedded processes or Chrome's browser process whenever either changes the icon state (nothing is logged if we don't need to change the icon state). This is only recorded in Monochrome (Android Nougat, Oreo, and Pie), since this logic is only relevant in that case.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.DevUi.SessionDuration
histogram.obsolete: Removed May 2020 because this only captures sessions up to 10 seconds long. Use Android.WebView.DevUi.SessionDuration2 instead, which captures sessions up to an hour.
histogram.summary: Records the time spent using a specific tool, from creation to destruction.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.DevUi.SessionDuration2
histogram.summary: Records the time spent using a specific tool, from creation to destruction.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.DevUi.ToggledFlagCount
histogram.summary: Records the number of flags we will toggle for this run of WebView. This is recorded only once during WebView startup, and only if we've already detected developer mode is enabled (see "Android.WebView.DevUi.DeveloperModeEnabled"). If developer mode is disabled, we will not toggle any flags through this mechanism.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.EverAttachedToWindow
histogram.summary: Records if WebView was ever attached to a window when WebView is destroyed. If WebView is killed by platform, nothing will be recorded.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.ExtraHeaders
histogram.summary: Record when extra headers are added to requests by AwURLLoaderThrottle and whether they were kept or removed during redirects.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.Gfx.FunctorStencilEnabled
histogram.obsolete: Removed in M79. Obtained required data from M78.
histogram.summary: Record in a functor draw whether stencil test is enabled by Android HWUI. This is recorded every frame. Note some OS versions never use stencil so worth checking result split by OS version.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.LoadDataWithBaseUrl.BaseUrl
histogram.summary: Records the scheme for the baseUrl parameter to loadDataWithBaseURL. This also records if this value is "empty", as determined by TextUtils.isEmpty().

histogram.name: Android.WebView.LoadDataWithBaseUrl.HistoryUrl
histogram.summary: Records whether the historyUrl parameter to loadDataWithBaseUrl is empty/ null, the same as the baseUrl parameter, or different from baseUrl.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.LoadUrl.DataUriHasOctothorpe
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019/10/11. This histogram was never well understood and the underlying feature has been shipped for some time anyway.
histogram.summary: Records if a data url passed to loadUrl had a '#' character. This is to be used in the effort to deprecate the incorrect treatment of '#' characters in data URIs; see http://crbug.com/823666#c30.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.LoadUrl.UrlScheme
histogram.summary: Records the scheme for the URL loaded by a browser-initiated navigation. Specifically, this is the scheme of the URL passed to loadUrl() (and its overloads), postUrl(), and loadData (this is implicitly "data:"). also records if this value is "empty", which means either null or "about:blank". This intentionally excludes loadDataWithBaseURL().

histogram.name: Android.WebView.Metrics.BackfillInstallDate
histogram.obsolete: Removed in April 2020. The backfill logic is deemed no longer necessary to keep around.
histogram.summary: Records whether WebView needed to backfill the install date pref with Android frameworks APIs. Recorded once during startup (metrics service initializtion).

histogram.name: Android.WebView.Mimetype.AppProvided
histogram.obsolete: Removed in April 2020. Network Service has launched, and we don't intend to follow up on this metric.
histogram.summary: Records information about the specified MIME types for app-provided content loaded in Android WebView. At the moment, this focuses on shouldInterceptRequest, ContentProvider-supplied content, and app assets/resources (where we expect the app to use proper file extensions to indicate MIME type), and is recorded as part of the process of loading each resource. This may apply to any type of resource (frame HTML, subresource, etc.).

histogram.name: Android.WebView.NonEmbeddedMetrics.HistogramRecordAge
histogram.summary: Records the time interval between when a histogram is recorded in a non-embedded WebView process and when it's actually sent to the UMA API in an embedded WebView. This is recorded during WebView startup for each histogram retrieved and replayed successfully from WebView non-embedded metrics service.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.NonEmbeddedMetrics.NumHistograms
histogram.summary: Records the number of histograms received from WebView's non-embedded histogram service. This is recorded once during WebView startup, after retrieving histograms from the service (if the proto can be parsed).

histogram.name: Android.WebView.NonEmbeddedMetrics.ParsingLogResult
histogram.summary: Records whether metrics parsing from file in non-embedded histogram service was successful. It's recorded during service startup by adding it to records list. It captures the most recent file parsing result for the last service launch before the client connects to the service.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.NonEmbeddedMetrics.TransmissionResult
histogram.summary: Records whether metrics transmission from the non-embedded histogram service was successful. This is recorded once during WebView startup, after retrieving histograms from the service.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.onReceivedError.ErrorCode
histogram.summary: The WebViewClient error code as returned by the onReceivedError callback This is recorded regardless of whether the network service is enabled or disabled.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.onReceivedHttpError.StatusCode
histogram.summary: The WebViewClient http resonse status code as returned by the onReceivedHttpError callback This is recorded regardless of whether the network service is enabled or disabled.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.onReceivedSslError.ErrorCode
histogram.summary: The WebViewClient SSL error code as received by onReceivedSslError.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.onSafeBrowsingHit.ThreatType
histogram.obsolete: Removed 04/2020. These metrics are tracked generally for SafeBrowsing, and WebView no longer needs specializations.
histogram.summary: Records the WebViewClient safe browsing threat type as returned by onSafeBrowsingHit callback.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.PrefLoadTime
histogram.summary: Records how long WebView blocks startup to load prefs from disk. This is recorded once per browser process startup.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.SecureCookieAction
histogram.summary: Records the action WebView took (if any) to "fix up" secure cookies for backwards compatibility.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.SetProxyOverride.BypassRules
histogram.summary: Records whether bypass rules were present in SetProxyOverride calls.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.SetProxyOverride.ProxySchemeFilterType
histogram.summary: Records the type of scheme filter in SetProxyOverride calls.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.SetProxyOverride.ProxyUrlType
histogram.summary: Records the type of url in SetProxyOverride calls.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.ShouldInterceptRequest.InterceptionType
histogram.summary: Whether the request was intercepted by the embedder of Android WebView, and if so which url scheme was used. Intercepted request are requests for which the shouldInterceptRequest() callback returned a non-null response.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.ShouldInterceptRequest.NullInputStream.ResponseStatusCode
histogram.summary: Records the custom response status code for the intercepted requests where input stream is null. In case status code is invalid (or has not been specified by the app) a zero status code is recorded. This UMA is needed in order to track specific usages of request interception where the behavior with network service enabled is different from the old code path (for more details see go/wv-ns-behavior-differences). This data is recorded regardless of whether the network service is enabled or disabled.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.SplitApkWorkaroundResult
histogram.obsolete: Removed in M73. Bundles have shipped.
histogram.summary: Result of applying a workaround to fix a crash on Android O if the WebView provider is a split APK (see crbug.com/889954 for more details).

histogram.name: Android.WebView.Startup.CreationTime.Stage1.FactoryInit
histogram.summary: How long it takes to initialize a WebViewChromiumFactoryProvider. This is the first major phase of the WebViewChromium construction.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.Startup.CreationTime.Stage2.ProviderInit.Cold
histogram.summary: How long it takes to initialize a WebViewProvider, the first time that one is initialized. WebViewProvider initialization is the second major phase of WebViewChromium construction. The first initialization is recorded separately because it is usually much slower than subsequent ones.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.Startup.CreationTime.Stage2.ProviderInit.Warm
histogram.summary: How long it takes to initialize a WebViewProvider, the first time that one is initialized. WebViewProvider initialization is the second major phase of WebViewChromium construction. When it is not the first time, it is faster and thus recorded separately.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.Startup.DataDirLockAttempts
histogram.summary: How many attempts it took to acquire the WebView data directory lock. A value of 0 indicates that we reached the max retry count and failed to acquire the lock successfully at all.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.Startup.InitAlreadyStarted
histogram.summary: Records whether we already tried to initialize WebView in the current process previously, upon entry to the initialization function. This should never be true under normal circumstances and may indicate an application catching an exception thrown by initialization and discarding it.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.SupportLibrary.ClientIsCompat
histogram.summary: Records whether the WebViewClient provided by the app is an instance of WebViewClientCompat (and therefore exposes AndroidX callbacks). This is recorded each time the app sets a new WebViewClient via WebView#setWebViewClient().

histogram.name: Android.WebView.TargetSdkVersion
histogram.summary: The version of the target SDK of the app WebView is running in. This is recorded at WebView creation.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.VariationsEnableState
histogram.obsolete: Removed from code July 2018. Variations in WebView has launched.
histogram.summary: Indicates whether variations is enabled. We want to know how enabling variations affects metrics, but we can't enable variations as a variations study, since variations can't enable/disable itself. Instead, WebView hard-codes a random variable for enabling variations, and reports the result in this histogram.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.Visibility.Global
histogram.summary: Records the global visibility state of WebView in seconds. The Visible bucket counts total seconds that any WebView was visible (the view itself was visible, and was attached to the view hierarchy of a visible window). The NotVisble bucket counts time since WebView initialization for which no WebView was considered visible, or no WebView existed. The total of these two buckets reflects the amount of time covered by metrics collection.

histogram.name: Android.WebView.Visibility.PerWebView
histogram.summary: Records per-WebView visibility duration in seconds. The Visible bucket counts the total time in seconds that each WebView was considered visible (the view itself was visible, and was attached to the view hierarchy of a visible window). If more than one WebView is considered visible, then each WebView contributes independently. The NotVisible bucket counts the duration that each WebView exists but not in a visible state. As with the Visible bucket, each WebView contributes independently, and thus this bucket is not incremented when no WebViews exist.

histogram.name: AndroidSearchEngineLogo.Events
histogram.summary: Counts occurences of various events related to the Search Engine Logo feature. When enabled, the feature will show the logo of the default search engine in the omnibox. These events record the performance of fetching non-Google logos.

histogram.name: AndroidSms.EffectivePWAInstallationSuccess
histogram.summary: Records effective final success/failure when trying to install Android Messages for Web PWA. This is recorded once when any one of the installation attempts succeed or when all attempts fails, irrespective of number of retries.

histogram.name: AndroidSms.FcmMessageDispatchFailure
histogram.summary: Records message types for which all retry attempts failed when dispatching to Android Messages for Web Service-Worker. This is recorded when using FCM web push for background notificaitons.

histogram.name: AndroidSms.FcmMessageDispatchRetry
histogram.summary: Records message types for which a retry was attempted when dispatching to Android Messages for Web Service-Worker. This is recorded when using FCM web push for background notificaitons.

histogram.name: AndroidSms.FcmMessageDispatchSuccess
histogram.summary: Records message types for which dispatching to Android Messages for Web Service-Worker succeeded. This is recorded when using FCM web push for background notificaitons.

histogram.name: AndroidSms.MultiDeviceFeatureState
histogram.summary: Indicates the feature state of the Messages feature. This metric is emitted to at the creation of the user session (when the user logs in), and when feature state updates occur.

histogram.name: AndroidSms.NumAttemptsForSuccessfulInstallation
histogram.summary: Records the number of installations attempted for Android Messages for Web PWA before succeeding.

histogram.name: AndroidSms.PWAInstallationResult
histogram.summary: Records installation result code for every installation attempt for Android Messages for Web PWA initiated through Multidevice setup or settings. This metric also includes installation result for every failed retry.

histogram.name: AndroidSms.PWAUninstallationResult
histogram.summary: Records success/failure for when Android Messages for Web PWA is uninstalled. The PWA is uninstalled when the messages URL changes, resulting in the PWA being uninstalled at the old URL and reinstalled at the new URL.

histogram.name: AndroidSms.ServiceWorkerLifetime
histogram.summary: Tracks the duration of time for which the Android Messages for Web Service- Worker is handling background connection.

histogram.name: AndroidSms.ServiceWorkerMessageDispatchStatus
histogram.summary: Tracks dispatch success/failure status of the start streaming message sent to the Android Messages for Web Service-Worker.

histogram.name: AndroidTabCloseUndo.Toast
histogram.summary: When a user closes a tab an undo toast will popup on certain devices giving the user the chance to undo closing that tab. This stat tracks how the user interacts with that UI and what actions they take. A cold start means that the undo bar wasn't showing when it was triggered to show again. A warm start means that it was. Warm starts can happen when the user closes multiple tabs close together. When the undo bar is dismissed, all closes that were queued up to be undone are committed. This can happen either by a timeout or by an action by the user to move to another part of the UI.

histogram.name: AnimatedImage.NumOfFramesSkipped
histogram.summary: If the frame rate for the image animation can not be reached, frames in the animation are skipped to catch up to the desired frame. This metric tracks the number of frames skipped during catch up, and can be used to assess the smoothness of these animations. It records the number of frames skipped each time the animation is ticked forward to draw the next frame. In the ideal case, where the animation can be drawn at the desired rate, 0 frames should be skipped. Note that skipping of frames can also be triggered if the animation was intentionally paused (on becoming off-screen, or the tab being hidden).

histogram.name: Animation.AnimationWorklet.Dispatcher.AsynchronousMutateDuration
histogram.summary:
The time it takes the WorkletMutatorDispatcher to complete an asynchronous mutation request of animation worklet. The measured time is the total elapsed time from when a request is queued until the results are received.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Animation.AnimationWorklet.Dispatcher.SynchronousMutateDuration
histogram.summary:
The time it takes the WorkletMutatorDispatcher to dispatch to and mutate all animation worklet mutators synchronously. This includes the time that it takes each AnimationWorkletGlobalScope to produce its output (which includes V8 script execution) and also any queuing cost associated with posting mutation tasks and receiving responses.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Animation.AnimationWorklet.GlobalScope.MutateDuration
histogram.summary: The time it takes for AnimationWorkletGlobalScope to produce a mutation update. This includes all V8 script execution cost.

histogram.name: Animation.AnimationWorklet.MutateDuration
histogram.summary:
The time it takes for an AnimationWorklet to produce a mutation update. This includes all V8 script execution cost.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: API.StorageAccess.AllowedRequests
histogram.summary: Records if various requests to access storage are allowed or not and if the Storage Access API unblocked the request or not. Recorded whenever a decision to access storage like getting/setting cookies or opening an indexeddb connection is made in CookieSettings.

histogram.name: API.StorageAccess.GrantIsImplicit
histogram.summary: Records if a generated grant was implicit or explicit at the time it was created.

histogram.name: API.StorageAccess.RequestStorageAccess
histogram.summary: Records requests to use document.requestStorageAccess and reasons the request may be approved or rejected.

histogram.name: AppBanners.BeforeInstallEvent
histogram.summary: App banners promote an application related to the current website, and are requested specifically through the current page's HTML. This stat tracks usage of the BeforeInstallPromptEvent, which allows developers to control when an app banner appears. The events in this histogram are not mutually exclusive - for example, preventDefault() must be called if prompt() is called.

histogram.name: AppBanners.DismissEvent
histogram.summary: App banners promote an application related to the current website, and are requested specifically through the current page's HTML. This stat tracks the different ways that an app banner left the screen, e.g. through an automatic dismissal after navigation or an explicit user action.

histogram.name: AppBanners.DisplayEvent
histogram.summary: App banners promote an application related to the current website, and are requested specifically through the current page's HTML. This stat tracks when an app banner was requested and how the request was handled, e.g. the user blocked its appearance or the banner was ultimately created.

histogram.name: AppBanners.InstallableStatusCode
histogram.summary: App banners promote an application related to the current website, and are requested specifically through the current page's HTML. This stat tracks the status code logged by the app banner system as it processes a site's eligibility for an app banner. Every request for a banner will be logged in a bucket of this histogram.

histogram.name: AppBanners.InstallEvent
histogram.summary: App banners promote an application related to the current website, and are requested specifically through the current page's HTML. This stat tracks when a user triggers an install dialog for the app promoted by the banner, as well as whether or not the user ultimately decided to install the app.

histogram.name: AppBanners.MinutesFromFirstVisitToBannerShown
histogram.summary: App banners promote an application related to the current website, and are requested specifically through the current page's HTML. This stat tracks the number of minutes between the first recorded visit to an origin and the time when the banner is actually shown.

histogram.name: AppBanners.UserResponse
histogram.summary: This stat tracks the user reponse when the add-to-homescreen dialog is shown either: (A) Via the website's JavaScript (B) Via the user tapping the app banner which promotes the application related to the current site.

histogram.name: appcache.CheckResponseResult
histogram.summary: AppCache check response result code.

histogram.name: appcache.CompletionQueueTime
histogram.summary: Time elapsed between a completion task being queued and run.

histogram.name: appcache.CompletionRunTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time taken to run a completion task on the IO thread.

histogram.name: appcache.CorruptionDetected
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of times corruption is detected in the sql database.

histogram.name: appcache.InitResult
histogram.summary: AppCache initialization result code.

histogram.name: appcache.JobStartDelay.AppCache
histogram.summary: How long URLRequests to be retrieved from the appcache are delayed.

histogram.name: appcache.JobStartDelay.Error
histogram.summary: How long URLRequests that result in a synthesized error are delayed.

histogram.name: appcache.JobStartDelay.Network
histogram.summary: How long URLRequests to be retrieved over the network are delayed.

histogram.name: appcache.MainPageLoad
histogram.summary: Counts the number of appcached page loads for secure vs insecure origins.

histogram.name: appcache.MainResourceResponseRetrieval
histogram.summary: Tracks the success rate of retrieving a main resource from the appcache.

histogram.name: appcache.Manifest.ChromeHeader
histogram.summary: The proportion of manifests that use the Chrome-specific header. Only tracks manifests that were successfully parsed.

histogram.name: appcache.Manifest.DangerousMode
histogram.summary: The proportion of manifests parsed with dangerous features enabled. Only tracks manifests that were successfully parsed.

histogram.name: appcache.Manifest.FallbackPattern
histogram.summary: The proportion of manifests that use patterns in valid FALLBACK: entries. Only tracks manifests that were successfully parsed.

histogram.name: appcache.Manifest.InterceptUsage
histogram.summary: Usage of CHROMIUM-INTERCEPT entries in AppCache manifests. Only tracks manifests that were successfully parsed.

histogram.name: appcache.Manifest.NetworkPattern
histogram.summary: The proportion of manifests that use patterns in valid NETWORK: entries. Only tracks manifests that were successfully parsed.

histogram.name: appcache.Manifest.Pattern
histogram.summary: The proportion of manifests that use patterns in any valid entries. Only tracks manifests that were successfully parsed.

histogram.name: appcache.Manifest.ValidManifestURL
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 5/2019. No longer needed.
histogram.summary: The proportion of ParseManifest calls that are triggered using a valid vs an invalid manifest URL.

histogram.name: appcache.MissingManifestDetectedAtCallsite
histogram.summary: Identifies where a missing manifest was detected occured.

histogram.name: appcache.MissingManifestEntry
histogram.summary: Logged on each occurrence of there being no record for the manifest file in the entries table.

histogram.name: appcache.ReinitAttempt
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of times the appcache reinit process occurs. The boolean value indicates whether it was a repeated attempt to reinitialize during a browsing session.

histogram.name: appcache.SubResourceResponseRetrieval
histogram.summary: Tracks the success rate of retrieving a sub resource from the appcache.

histogram.name: appcache.TaskQueueTime
histogram.summary: Time elapsed between a background task being queued and run.

histogram.name: appcache.TaskRunTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time taken to run a background task.

histogram.name: appcache.UpdateJob.Canceled
histogram.summary: Tracks whether the update job was canceled.

histogram.name: appcache.UpdateJob.ExistingCorruptionFixedInUpdate
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of times we encountered corruption and fixed it while handling a 304 response. We only attempt to recover corruption during 304 response handling, so for any corrupt caches that exist and don't trigger a conditional request, these cases can't result in the possibility of fixing corruption through this path.

histogram.name: appcache.UpdateJob.ExistingResourceCheck
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of times we plan to check whether we can reuse existing resources.

histogram.name: appcache.UpdateJob.ExistingResourceCorrupt
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of times we encounter a corrupt resource while checking if we can reuse an existing resource. This is only called when we find a corrupt resource, so any resources that are corrupt but aren't tested won't be included in this count.

histogram.name: appcache.UpdateJob.ExistingResourceCorruptionRecovery
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of times we encountered a corrupt resource and chose to recover the corruption by refetching the resource. This is only called when we find a corrupt resource and have the corruption recovery feature enabled. The count won't be incremented otherwise.

histogram.name: appcache.UpdateJob.ExistingResourceNotCorrupt
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of times we encounter a non-corrupt resource while checking if we can reuse an existing resource. This is only called when we find a non-corrupt resource, so any resources that aren't corrupt but aren't tested won't be included in this count.

histogram.name: appcache.UpdateJob.ExistingResourceReused
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of times while checking existing resources that we determined we can reuse that resource.

histogram.name: appcache.UpdateJob.ExistingVaryDuring304
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of times while handling a 304 response a cached response has a Vary header while the incoming 304 response doesn't.

histogram.name: appcache.UpdateJob.FinalInternalState
histogram.summary: Tracks the final internal state for the update job.

histogram.name: appcache.UpdateJob.ResourceFreshness
histogram.summary: Tracks the resource freshness we cached in a response as we consider whether we can reuse an existing resource. This metric is in days and typically reflects the Cache-Control: max-age=X server response header. It may indicate the Expires: header in some cases at the time of the response if a max-age header was not provided. Note that max-age is in seconds and we're generally interested in a range of 0 days to ~1 year.

histogram.name: appcache.UpdateJobResult
histogram.summary: AppCache update job result code.

histogram.name: appcache.UpdateProgressAtPointOfFaliure
histogram.summary: Percent completion at point of failure of an update job.

histogram.name: appcache.UpdateWasOffOriginAtPointOfFailure
histogram.summary: Whether the resource causing the failure was from a different origin.

histogram.name: appcache.UpdateWasStalledAtPointOfFailure
histogram.summary: Whether any progresss had been made in the 5 minutes preceeding failure.

histogram.name: AppleScript.CommandEvent
histogram.summary: The number of times an AppleScript event gets called.

histogram.name: AppManagement.AppDetailViews
histogram.summary: The actions taken by the user when they are viewing the app details of an app in App Management (chrome://os-settings/app-management).

histogram.name: AppManagement.EntryPoints
histogram.summary: The ways the user opens up the App Management interface.

histogram.name: Apps.AppInfoDialog.Launches
histogram.summary: The number of times the app info dialog is launched. This is gathered each time the app info dialog is opened, from each of the places the dialog can be opened from.

histogram.name: Apps.AppInfoDialog.OpenedForLocation
histogram.summary: The location of the app that the dialog was opened for. This is gathered each time the app info dialog is opened.

histogram.name: Apps.AppInfoDialog.OpenedForType
histogram.summary: The type of the app that the dialog was opened for. This is gathered each time the app info dialog is opened.

histogram.name: Apps.AppLaunch
histogram.summary: The number of times v2 packaged apps are launched grouped by extension_misc::AppLaunchBuckets. See also Extensions.AppLaunch.

histogram.name: Apps.AppLauncherPromo
histogram.obsolete: Removed 11/2017 in issue 600915 with the removal of app list on platforms other than Chrome OS.
histogram.summary: Interactions with the App Launcher promo dialog.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.AggregatedMlAppRankFail
histogram.summary: The number of apps the were not able to be ranked by the Aggregated ML app ranker (Top Cat).

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.AggregatedMlAppRankSuccess
histogram.summary: The number of apps successfully ranked by the Aggregated ML app ranker (Top Cat).

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.AggregatedSearchRankerError
histogram.summary: The type of errors that occur during the inference of Search Ranking aggregated model.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.AnswerCardSearchProvider.SearchAnswerLoadingTime
histogram.summary: The time between sending an answer card request and receiving the notification that answer card has finished loading.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.AnswerCardSearchProvider.SearchAnswerNavigationTime
histogram.summary: The time between sending an answer card request and receiving the notification navigation has been finished with a valid answer card.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.AppSearchProvider.QueryTime
histogram.summary: The time between sending a non-empty query to AppSearchProvider and receiving results back.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.AppSearchProvider.ZeroStateLatency
histogram.summary: The time between sending an empty query to AppSearchProvider and receiving Zero State recommendation results back.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.AppsGridAddPage
histogram.summary: The different ways to add page in app list's apps grid. Logged when the page is created.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.DriveQuickAccessProvider.ApiResults
histogram.summary: The number of results returned from a call to the Drive QuickAccess API. Only reported if the API call did not error.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.DriveQuickAccessProvider.CacheEmpty
histogram.summary: Whether or not the results cache is empty. An empty cache means either poor network conditions, the user opening zero-state very quickly, or an API error.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.DriveQuickAccessProvider.DriveFSMounted
histogram.summary: Whether or not DriveFS is mounted on a call to DriveQuickAccessProvider::Start or ::GetQuickAccessItems.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.DriveQuickAccessProvider.FileError
histogram.summary: The error code returned from a call to the Drive QuickAccess API.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.DriveQuickAccessProvider.GetQuickAccessItemsLatency
histogram.summary: The time between when GetQuickAccessItems was last called, and receiving Drive QuickAccess results back. Only reported if the API call did not error.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.DriveQuickAccessProvider.Latency
histogram.summary: The time between sending a zero state query and receiving file recommendations back from the DriveQuickAccessProvider.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.DriveQuickAccessProvider.TimeFromFetchToZeroStateStart
histogram.summary: The time between when GetQuickAccessItems was last called, and the user entering zero state search.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.DriveQuickAccessProvider.ValidResults
histogram.summary: Counts how many results from one call to the QuickAccess API exist in the DriveFS mount and are OK for display.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.LauncherSearchProvider.QueryTime
histogram.summary: The time between sending a non-empty query and receiving results back from the extension which performs the file search.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.OmniboxProvider.QueryTime
histogram.summary: The time between sending an non-empty query and receiving search results back from OmniboxProvider which queries chrome AutocompelteController's search providers to get the results.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.OmniboxProvider.ZeroStateLatency
histogram.summary: The time between sending an empty query and receiving zero state recommendations back from OmniboxProvider which queries chrome ZeroStateProvider and gets recent queries back from Google Autocomplete server.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.OsSettingsProvider.Error
histogram.summary: Various error states of the provider for OS settings in the cros launcher. Emitted only in the case of an unexpected error, the bucket proportion is not meaningful.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.SearchQueryLength.Apps
histogram.summary: The length of the query that resulted in an app launch from search box. This metric only records non-empty queries.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.SearchSuccess.Apps
histogram.summary: The number of app launches from the search box with non-empty queries.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.SuggestedFiles.ChipLaunched
histogram.summary: Emitted when a suggestion chip is launched. Currently only 'true' is used, but we may emit 'false' in future to track abandonment.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.SuggestedFiles.LaunchIndex
histogram.summary: Emitted when a suggestion chip is launched, records the index (position in the bar) of that chip. The left-most chip is index 0 and the maximum index is 4.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.SuggestedFiles.LaunchType
histogram.summary: Emitted when a suggestion chip is launched, records the type of that chip.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.UserEvent.Error
histogram.summary: Records error when processing user events into Apps.AppList.UserEvent.* metrics. These are expected to be rare and bucket proportion is not meaningful.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.UserEvent.Overall
histogram.summary: Emitted on a usage of the launcher, and records overall impressions, launches, and abandons for a launcher UI view. The bucket proportion is not meaningful for this metric, because impressions are a superset of launches and abandons. Instead, the ratio between buckets can be used to calculate accurate overall CTR.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.UserEvent.TypeAbandon
histogram.summary:
Emitted after an impression, if the user then closes the launcher or moves to a different view without launching any search result. This is emitted once per abandon, per displayed result. Records the displayed result's type. See Apps.AppList.UserEvent.TypeImpression for more details.
This metric is completed by a histogram suffix describing which UI surface the results are recorded from.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.UserEvent.TypeImpression
histogram.summary:
When a set of results is displayed for 2 seconds and the user has probably seen it, or the user interacted with the results and has definitely seen it, we consider it an impression.
Emitted once per impression for each displayed result. Records the type of the displayed result, eg. native app or omnibox bookmark.
The Apps.AppList.UserEvent.Type* metrics are directly comparable per-result-type. So, for example, (launches / impressions) for a result type gives an accurate CTR.
This metric is completed by a histogram suffix describing which UI surface the results are recorded from.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.UserEvent.TypeLaunch
histogram.summary:
Emitted after an impression, if the user launches a result. Records the type of the launched result. See Apps.AppList.UserEvent.TypeImpression for more details.
This metric is completed by a histogram suffix describing which UI surface the results are recorded from.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.ZeroStateFileProvider.Latency
histogram.summary: The time between sending a zero state query and receiving file recommendations back from the ZeroStateFileProvider.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.ZeroStateFileProvider.NotifierCreationSuccess
histogram.summary: Emitted when the ZeroStateFileProvider is created, records whether the FileTasksNotifier creation succeeded for the given profile.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.ZeroStateResults.LaunchedItemType
histogram.summary: The type of item that was launched from a zero state launcher search result.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.ZeroStateResults.ReceivedScore.DriveQuickAccess
histogram.summary: Logs the result score received from the Drive Quick Access provider. The original float score is scaled to range from 0 to 100 and its floor is recorded.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.ZeroStateResults.ReceivedScore.OmniboxSearch
histogram.summary: Logs the result score received from the Omnibox search provider. The original float score is scaled to range from 0 to 100 and its floor is recorded.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.ZeroStateResults.ReceivedScore.ZeroStateFile
histogram.summary: Logs the result score received from the zero state file provider. The original float score is scaled to range from 0 to 100 and its floor is recorded.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.ZeroStateResultsList.Clicked
histogram.summary: Whether an item was clicked from zero state search results, or if results were displayed for some amount time but not clicked. These clicks and impressions are used for calculating CTR metrics.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.ZeroStateResultsList.ContainsDriveFiles
histogram.summary: Whether a set of search result impressions contains any Drive QuickAccess file results.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.ZeroStateResultsList.FileImpressions
histogram.summary: The number of zero state impressions that contained at least one result of the specified file type.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.ZeroStateResultsList.LaunchedItemPosition
histogram.obsolete: Removed October 2019, replaced by Apps.AppList.ZeroStateResultsList.LaunchedItemPositionV2, which uses different bucketing.
histogram.summary: The position index of an item launched from zero state search results.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.ZeroStateResultsList.LaunchedItemPositionV2
histogram.summary: The position index of an item launched from zero state search results.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.ZeroStateResultsList.NumImpressionTypes
histogram.obsolete: Removed October 2019, replaced by Apps.AppList.ZeroStateResultsList.NumImpressionTypesV2, which uses different bucketing.
histogram.summary: The number of item types included in each zero state impression set.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.ZeroStateResultsList.NumImpressionTypesV2
histogram.summary: The number of item types included in each zero state impression set.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.ZeroStateSearchResultRemovalDecision
histogram.summary: The decision of the user whether to remove a zero state search result. This is gathered per click of a remove or cancel button of a search result removal confirmation dialog.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.ZeroStateSearchResultUserActionType
histogram.summary: The type of the action user performed on zero state search result. This is gathered per click of a remove or append button of a search result.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.ZeroStateSearchResutRemovalDecision
histogram.summary: The decision of the user whether to remove a zero state search result. This is gathered per click of a remove or cancel button of a search result removal confirmation dialog.

histogram.name: Apps.AppList.ZeroStateSuggestionOpenType
histogram.summary: The type of the app list suggestion that was opened by user in zero state.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListAppLaunched
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 04/2019 in favor Apps.AppListAppLaunchedV2.
histogram.summary: The number of apps launched from the launcher. This is logged each time an app is launched. The bucket denotes whether the app is suggested.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListAppLaunchedFullscreen
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 04/2019 in favor Apps.AppListAppLaunchedV2.
histogram.summary: The number of apps launched from the fullscreen launcher. This is logged each time an app is launched. The bucket denotes whether the app is suggested.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListAppLaunchedV2
histogram.summary: The number of apps launched from the launcher and shelf for each launcher state. This is incremented each time an app is launched. Each bucket represents where in the launcher or shelf the app was launched from.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListAppMovingType
histogram.summary: The different ways to move an app in app list's apps grid. Logged when the app moving succeeds.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListAppTypeClicked
histogram.summary: The type of app clicked in the launcher on ChromeOS, recorded after the icon is clicked, before it is launched.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListBadgedAppsCount
histogram.summary: Number of apps (including ones in folders) with Chrome badge, across all Launcher pages. This is gathered per open-launcher event.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListCreationTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time it takes to build the app list UI. This is logged each time the app list is built from scratch.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListDefaultSearchResultOpenType
histogram.summary: The type of the default result opened by user by pressing ENTER key. Launcher UI shows suggested apps, recent queries, etc when user activates the search box, or shows matched apps (installed apps, play store app results, etc.) and other results (query suggestions, web site, bookmark, files, etc) when user types a query. It will set the focus on the first result as the default. User can open the default result by simply pressing ENTER key. This metrics logs the type of the default result (installed app, play store result, query suggestion, etc) opened by user pressing ENTER key.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListDoodleAction
histogram.obsolete: App list doesn't support doodles anymore.
histogram.summary: The number of user interactions with the app list doodle. This is logged once per action.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListDriveQuickAccessProvider.ResultPresence
histogram.obsolete:
Removed January 2020, this was a debugging metric that was too expensive to keep in the code.
Results on beta were: 60% present and shown, 5% present and not shown, and 35% absent.
histogram.summary: Emitted when the launcher updates the displayed zero-state search results. Records whether a) a Drive QuickAccess result was displayed, b) a DQA result was present in the candidate results list but wasn't one of the top 5 so was not displayed, or c) no DQA results were in the results list.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListFolder.ShowHide.AnimationSmoothness
histogram.summary: Relative smoothness of animations of showing and hiding app list folder. 100% represents ideally smooth 60 frames per second. 50% represents only 30 frames per second is achieved during the animations. 0% should not happen. This metric is recorded exactly once when the user starts the folder animation in the launcher.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListFolderNameLength
histogram.summary: The folder name length in characters when an app list folder gets renamed. Logged when the folder name is changed and the texfield is no longer in focus.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListFolderOpened
histogram.summary: The number of times folders are opened in the app list. This is logged when the folder is clicked.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListHide.InputLatency
histogram.summary: Elapsed time from the input event to hide the launcher UI. This is logged each time the launcher is dismissed by pressing search key, clicking shelf button, or focusing out side of the launcher.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListHowEnabled
histogram.summary: The trigger that caused the app list to be enabled. Recorded when the user first shows the app list. If not shown after one hour, will be recorded then. If Chrome was not running at the one-hour mark, will be recorded during the next Chrome startup.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListInternalApp.Activate
histogram.summary: The internal app in apps grid was opened by the user. This is gathered per click of an internal app.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListLauncherIssuedSearchQueryLength
histogram.summary: The UMA histogram that logs the length of user typed queries app list launcher issues to the search providers.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListLaunchRecorderError
histogram.obsolete: Removed January 2020, the app list launch recorded has been removed.
histogram.summary: Reports error states of the app list launch recorder system.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListPageOpened
histogram.summary: The page that the app list goes to. This is gathered every time the user initiates a transition to another page in the launcher.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListPageSwitcherSource
histogram.summary: The source used to switch pages in the app list's app grid. Logged when the page switch succeeds.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListPeekingToFullscreen
histogram.summary: Replaced with Apps.AppListPeekingToFullscreenSource on 9/2017.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListPeekingToFullscreenSource
histogram.summary: The source which transitions the app list from Peeking to Fullscreen. This is logged when the transition method is used.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListPlayStoreAppLaunchedIndex
histogram.summary: The index of the app within all play store apps displayed when it is launched.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListPlayStoreQueryState
histogram.summary: The state of a Play Store app search request.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListPlayStoreSearchAppsDisplayed
histogram.summary: The number of the play store apps displayed to user for a query.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListRecommendedImpResultCountAfterOpen
histogram.summary: When a user opens an app recommendation result, this is the number of times we showed the result before opening. Number of times shown is reset to 0 every 30 days, and is held on device.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListRecommendedResponse
histogram.summary: Response when parsing the recommended app list from Play Store for candidate app list, recorded every time a response from the play store is parsed in client.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListRecommendedResponse.Count
histogram.summary: Number of responses from server in Apps.AppListRecommendedResponse.count when fetching appListRecommendded GetAppReinstallCandidates.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListRecommendedResponse.Latency
histogram.summary: Milliseconds elapsed to fetch appListRecommended GetAppReinstallCandidates.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListResultLaunchIndexAndQueryLength
histogram.summary: The index of a clicked result in the search result box and the length of the search query. The index is relative to the SearchResultListView, not the overall position in the suggestion window.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListResultLaunchIsEmptyQuery
histogram.summary: This boolean records if the query that introduces a launch in the results list is empty or not. This will be recorded every time the user clicks an item in the launcher results list.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListSearchAbandonQueryLength
histogram.summary: The length of the query when the user abandons results of a queried search or recommendations of zero state (zero length query) in launcher UI.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListSearchBoxActivated
histogram.summary: The number of times the applist searchbox has been activated. This is split by keystroke, mouse press, and gesture tap activations. This is gathered each time the searchbox goes from inactive to active, not necessarily empty to non-empty.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListSearchCommenced
histogram.summary: The number of searches that are started in the app list. This is gathered each time the app list search box transitions from empty to non-empty.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListSearchOmniboxResultOpenType
histogram.summary: The type of app list search omnibox result that was opened by the user. This is gathered per OmniboxResult opened in the app list's launcher suggestion window.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListSearchQueryLength
histogram.summary: The length of the app list search query when a result is opened. This is gathered per click of a search result.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListSearchResultDistanceFromOrigin
histogram.summary: The minimum number of arrow keys a user would need to press to navigate to the opened search result. This is gathered per click of a search result.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListSearchResultInternalApp.Open
histogram.summary: The app list search result of an internal app that was opened by the user. This is gathered per click of a search result.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListSearchResultInternalApp.Show
histogram.summary: The app list search result of an internal app that was shown to the user. This is gathered per creation of a search result. Note: Typing a word with multiple letters could trigger multiple creations of the search result.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListSearchResultOpenDisplayType
histogram.summary: The display type of the app list search result that was opened by the user. This is gathered per click of a search result.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListSearchResultOpenedSource
histogram.summary: The state of the app list when a search result was opened.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListSearchResultOpenType
histogram.summary: The type of app list search result that was opened by the user. This is gathered per SearchResult opened. Only recorded for search results shown in the search result page of the launcher, except for M-62 where suggestion chips were recorded as search results (https://crbug.com/919550). M-62 should be filtered out when viewing this histogram to ignore the metrics caused by the above mentioned bug.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListSearchResultOpenTypeV2
histogram.summary: The type of app list search result that was opened by the user. This is gathered per SearchResult opened. Only recorded for search results shown in the search result page of the launcher (suggestion chips are not recorded).

histogram.name: Apps.AppListShow.InputLatency
histogram.summary: Elapsed time from the input event to show the launcher UI. This is logged each time the app list is shown by pressing search key, clicking shelf button, or swiping from shelf.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListShowSource
histogram.summary: The number of times the different sources for showing the app list are used. This is logged when the app list is shown. Does not count the app list shown when the last window is closed in tablet mode. This is not the total number of times the app list is shown to the user; for that consult the user action AppList_WindowFocused.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListStateTransitionSource
histogram.summary: The number of transitions from the different AppListStates. This is gathered each time the launcher transitions from various states in AppListView::SetState (Peeking, Half, Fullscreen All Apps, Fullscreen Search, and Closed). Not all state transitions are collected because they are not meaningful in measuring user activity.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListSuggestedChipLaunched
histogram.summary: The number of times suggested chips are launched at each suggested-apps tile. An index i, ranged from 0 to kNumStartPageTiles - 1, is logged each time a suggested chip is launched from i-th tile.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListSuggestedChipOpenType
histogram.summary: The type of app list suggestion chip that was opened by the user. This is gathered per SuggestionChip opened.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListTileLaunchIndexAndQueryLength
histogram.obsolete: Removed 04/2020. The related app ranking experiments have finished.
histogram.summary: The index of a clicked result in the search result app tiles and the length of the search query. The index is relative to the SearchTileItemListView, not the overall position in the suggestion window.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListTimeToDiscover
histogram.summary: Time between enabling the app list, and a user explicitly choosing to show it. If the app list is not shown after one hour, an entry in the last bucket is recorded. If the user installs a second packaged app within one hour, or if the app list was not enabled by installing a packaged app from the Web Store, no time value is recorded - only Apps.AppListHowEnabled.

histogram.name: Apps.AppListWarmupDuration
