histogram.obsolete: Removed 07/2016 in Issue 600915 with the removal of the app list on Windows.
histogram.summary: The amount of time spent in warmup (in WarmupForProfile call). This will tell us how long warmup blocks the UI.

histogram.name: Apps.AppShimErrorVersion
histogram.summary: Counts which major milestone versions of app_mode_loader are sending --app-shim-error. --app-shim-error is sent as a command line argument to Chrome when app_mode_loader was unable to dyload the Chrome Framework and call ChromeAppModeMain. For example, when Chrome updates from 32-bit to 64-bit, an older shim will find the new framework version but fail to dyload it.

histogram.name: Apps.AppsInFolders
histogram.summary: The total number of apps in folders ignoring OEM folders. This is logged each time the app list is initialized. The "FullscreenAppListDisabled" suffix has been deprecated.

histogram.name: Apps.Bounced
histogram.summary: Records whether an application has bounced (been uninstalled in less than seven days). Recorded when an application is uninstalled.

histogram.name: Apps.ContextMenuExecuteCommand
histogram.summary: The number of times a certain command was executed by a context menu, split by whether the context menu came from an app, or another part of the system UI.

histogram.name: Apps.ContextMenuShowSource
histogram.summary: The show source (touch, mouse, keyboard, etc) for a context menu. Split by the component from which the context menu originated.

histogram.name: Apps.ContextMenuUserJourneyTime
histogram.summary: The user journey time for a ContextMenu, defined as the time between launching a context menu and the context menu's close callback being activated. Not recorded whenever the context menu is not for an app icon on the shelf.

histogram.name: Apps.CreateShortcutIcon.Linux.Result
histogram.summary: Records the result of creating shortcut image for web app on Linux.

histogram.name: Apps.CreateShortcuts.Linux.Result
histogram.summary: Records the result of creating shortcut for web app on Linux.

histogram.name: Apps.CreateShortcuts.Mac.Result
histogram.summary: Records the result of creating shortcut for web app on MacOS.

histogram.name: Apps.DefaultAppLaunch
histogram.summary: Records when a user attempts to launch a particular Chrome OS app.

histogram.name: Apps.FileHandler.Registration.Linux.RecreateShortcut.Result
histogram.summary: Records the result of re-creating shortcut during file handler registration process for PWA on Linux

histogram.name: Apps.FileHandler.Registration.Linux.Result
histogram.summary: Records the result of file handler registration for PWA on Linux

histogram.name: Apps.FileHandler.Registration.Mac.Result
histogram.summary: Records the result of file handler registration for PWA on MacOS

histogram.name: Apps.FileHandler.Registration.Win.Result
histogram.summary: Records the result of file handler registration for PWA on Windows

histogram.name: Apps.HomeLauncherTransition.AnimationSmoothness
histogram.summary: Relative smoothness of animations of launcher transitions in tablet mode. 100% represents ideally smooth 60 frames per second. 50% represents only 30 frames per second is achieved during the animations. 0% should not happen. This metric is recorded exactly once when the user triggers launcher animation in tablet mode.

histogram.name: Apps.LockScreen.AppsProfile.Creation.Duration
histogram.summary: The amount of time needed to create a lock screen apps profile. This metric is recorded only if the profile creation was successful. The lock screen apps profile is created if the user has an app enabled on Chrome OS lock screen, and is used as the context in which the app is launched on top of the lock screen.

histogram.name: Apps.LockScreen.AppsProfile.Creation.Success
histogram.summary: Boolean indicating whether the lock screen apps profile creation succeeded. The lock screen apps profile is created if the user has an app enabled on Chrome OS lock screen, and is used as the context in which the app is launched on top of the lock screen.

histogram.name: Apps.LockScreen.DataItemStorage.ClearTextItemSize
histogram.summary: The size of a data item stored in the lock screen data item storage using chrome.lockScreen.data API as sent from the app - the item will be encrypted before getting stored in the storage.

histogram.name: Apps.LockScreen.DataItemStorage.EncryptedItemSize
histogram.summary: The size of an encrypted data item stored in the lock screen data item storage using chrome.lockScreen.data API.

histogram.name: Apps.LockScreen.DataItemStorage.FailedOperationDuration
histogram.summary: The amount of time it took to complete a lock screen data item storage operation. Reported only on the operation failure.

histogram.name: Apps.LockScreen.DataItemStorage.OperationDuration
histogram.summary: The amount of time it took to complete a lock screen data item storage operation. Reported only on the operation success.

histogram.name: Apps.LockScreen.DataItemStorage.OperationResult
histogram.summary: The result of a lock screen data item storage operation returned through chrome.lockScreen.data extension API.

histogram.name: Apps.LockScreen.DataItemStorage.RegisteredItemsCount
histogram.summary: The number of data items saved in the lock screen data item storage per app. This is recorded on startup, when the app attempts to use the chrome.lockScreen.data API.

histogram.name: Apps.LockScreen.NoteTakingApp.AppStatusOnNoteLaunch
histogram.summary: Reported when a user attempts to launch a note taking app on the lock screen. It reports the note taking app state in the lock screen apps profile at the launch attempt time - for example, whether the app was enabled, or whether the app had been terminated and an app reload was attempted.

histogram.name: Apps.LockScreen.NoteTakingApp.AppWindowLifeTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time a lock screen enabled app window spent in a certain state during the app window activity. The state to which the histogram refers to is defined by the suffix. The metric is logged upon leaving the associated state.

histogram.name: Apps.LockScreen.NoteTakingApp.AvailabilityOnScreenLock
histogram.summary: The note taking action availability state on the lock screen, recorded when the user session is locked.

histogram.name: Apps.LockScreen.NoteTakingApp.FinalAppSessionState
histogram.summary: The state in which lock screen enabled note taking app was when the note taking session ended.

histogram.name: Apps.LockScreen.NoteTakingApp.LaunchDurationAtLaunchCancel
histogram.summary: The amount time a lock screen app had been launching when the app launch was canceled. Logged if the lock screen app session ends before the lock screen app window is shown.

histogram.name: Apps.LockScreen.NoteTakingApp.LaunchRequestOrdinalNumber
histogram.summary: Ordinal number of a note taking app launch request from a lock screen within a lock screen session. If a note taking app is launched n times during a single lock screen session, this histogram will be reported with values 1 through n. The launch counter is reset on screen unlock.

histogram.name: Apps.LockScreen.NoteTakingApp.LaunchRequestReason
histogram.summary: The user action that launched note taking from the lock screen.

histogram.name: Apps.LockScreen.NoteTakingApp.LockScreenAppUnloaded
histogram.summary: Reported when a note taking app is unloaded from the lock screen apps profile while lock screen note taking is available. Reports the unload reason type, which can be used to deduce whether the app can be reloaded in the profile. The app unload event is not reported if the app is unloaded due to lock screen note taking becoming unavailble (e.g. when the user unlocks the screen).

histogram.name: Apps.LockScreen.NoteTakingApp.LockScreenInstallationDuration
histogram.summary: Amount of time needed to install a copy of a lock screen note taking app into lock screen apps profile.

histogram.name: Apps.LockScreen.NoteTakingApp.NoteTakingExitReason
histogram.summary: The reason the note taking on lock screen was ended (and lock screen app window was closed).

histogram.name: Apps.LockScreen.NoteTakingApp.ReloadCountOnAppTermination
histogram.summary: Reported when a note taking app is terminated in the lock screen apps profile. It reports the number of times the app was reloaded in the lock screen apps profile during the current lock screen session (prior to the termination).

histogram.name: Apps.LockScreen.NoteTakingApp.TimeToLoadAppWindowContents
histogram.summary: The amount of time needed to load a note taking app window contents on the lock screen - i.e. the time passed from the user requesting an app launch to the app window contents being loaded.

histogram.name: Apps.LockScreen.NoteTakingApp.TimeToShowWindow
histogram.summary: The amount of time needed to launch a note taking app window from the lock screen - i.e. the time passed from the user requesting an app launch to the app window appearing on the screen.

histogram.name: Apps.LockScreen.NoteTakingApp.UnlockUIAction
histogram.obsolete: Obsolete - tracks actions in UI that did not launch.
histogram.summary: The action the user took on the lock screen UI shown when lock screen app window is in background, shown under the lock screen.

histogram.name: Apps.LogDisplayTypeClickedResultZeroState
histogram.summary: Records number of clicks on search result for different display types in zero-state.

histogram.name: Apps.NoteTakingApp.DefaultLaunchResult
histogram.summary: The result of attempting to launch a default note-taking app on Chrome OS. Only reported if the preferred app was unspecified or failed to launch.

histogram.name: Apps.NoteTakingApp.PreferredLaunchResult
histogram.summary: The result of attempting to launch the user-specified preferred note-taking app, if any, on Chrome OS.

histogram.name: Apps.NumberOfFolders
histogram.summary: The number of folders that users have in their Launcher. Includes the OEM folder. This metric is recorded every time the launcher is shown.

histogram.name: Apps.NumberOfPages
histogram.summary: The number of pages that users have in the top level apps grid of their Launcher. This metric is recorded every time the launcher is shown.

histogram.name: Apps.NumberOfPagesNotFull
histogram.summary: The number of pages that are not full in the top level apps grid of Launcher. This metric is recorded every time the launcher is shown.

histogram.name: Apps.PaginationTransition.AnimationSmoothness
histogram.summary:
Base name is deprecated as of 3/2019 in favor of Apps.PaginationTransition.AnimationSmoothness.ClamshellMode and Apps.PaginationTransition.AnimationSmoothness.TabletMode
Relative smoothness of animations of launcher pagination transitions. 100% represents ideally smooth 60 frames per second. 50% represents only 30 frames per second is achieved during the animations. 0% should not happen. This metric is recorded exactly once when the user starts the pagination transition animation of the launcher.

histogram.name: Apps.PaginationTransition.DragScroll.PresentationTime
histogram.summary: Presentation time for the apps grid scrolling operation. This starts when drag starts by gesture and ends before the patination animation starts. The smoothness of the pagination animation is recorded in Apps.PaginationTransition.AnimationSmoothness.{TabletMode|ClamshellMode}.

histogram.name: Apps.PaginationTransition.DragScroll.PresentationTime.MaxLatency
histogram.summary: Maximum latecy reported during one drag to scroll operation on apps grid.

histogram.name: Apps.PreferredApps.FileIOAction
histogram.summary: Recorded at the time a read/write operation happens for preferred app to indicate whether the I/O succeeded or not.

histogram.name: Apps.PreferredApps.UpdateAction
histogram.summary: Recorded at the time a update operation happens for preferred apps to indicate the type of update action.

histogram.name: Apps.ScrollableShelf.AnimationSmoothness
histogram.summary: Relative smoothness of animations of the scrollable shelf when scrolling. 100% represents ideally smooth 60 frames per second. 50% represents only 30 frames per second is achieved during the animations. 0% should not happen. This metric is recorded exactly once when the user scrolls over the scrollable shelf.

histogram.name: Apps.ScrollableShelf.Drag.PresentationTime
histogram.summary: Presentation time of the drag scroll in the scrollable shelf in milliseconds.

histogram.name: Apps.ScrollableShelf.Drag.PresentationTime.MaxLatency
histogram.summary: Maximum latency of the presentation time of the drag scroll in the scrollable shelf.

histogram.name: Apps.ShortcutsMenu.Registration.Win.Result
histogram.summary: Records the result of shortcuts menu registration for PWA on Windows

histogram.name: Apps.ShortcutsMenu.Unregistration.Win.Result
histogram.summary: Records the result of shortcuts menu unregistration for PWA on Windows

histogram.name: Apps.StateTransition.AnimationSmoothness
histogram.summary: Relative smoothness of animations of peeking launcher state transitions. 100% represents ideally smooth 60 frames per second. 50% represents only 30 frames per second is achieved during the animations. 0% should not happen. This metric is recorded exactly once when the user switches states of the launcher.

histogram.name: Apps.StateTransition.Drag.PresentationTime
histogram.summary: Presentation time of the app launcher drag operation in milliseconds.

histogram.name: Apps.StateTransition.Drag.PresentationTime.MaxLatency
histogram.summary: Maximum latency of the presentation time of the app laucnher drag operation.

histogram.name: Apps.Window.Type
histogram.summary: The type of app window opened (through the chrome.app.window API).

histogram.name: Arc.AdbSideloadingEnablingScreen
histogram.summary: Record user's choice or error during ADB sideloading opt-in UI after reboot.

histogram.name: Arc.AndroidBootTime
histogram.summary: The time elapsed for booting up the ARC instance.

histogram.name: Arc.AppCount
histogram.summary: The number of ARC apps open at any given time. Collected every 5 minutes. This indicates both how many users are engaging with apps and, when they do engage, how many apps they are engaging with at a time.

histogram.name: Arc.AppInstalledReason
histogram.summary: Record a reason when an arc application completes installation successfully, including policy based applications, default applications and user prompted installations.

histogram.name: Arc.AppListRecommendedImp.AllImpression
histogram.summary: Record a count of an impression of FAR result. This is a count _any_ time it's shown, even for a millisecond.

histogram.name: Arc.AppListRecommendedImp.CountedImpression
histogram.summary: Record a count of an impression of FAR result. This is a count of heuristic real impressions: a new showing after a while, a longer showing, etc.

histogram.name: Arc.AppShortcuts.BuildMenuTime
histogram.summary: Record the time to build ARC app shortcut items on menu model. We get the needed metadata from Android and decode icons in the utility process. It is an asynchronous procedure so we need to know the longest waiting time.

histogram.name: Arc.AppsInstalledAtStartup
histogram.summary: Total number of ARC apps installed on a device measured at startup. Note: This includes launchable apps that are installed by default, such as Play Store and Settings, as well as any vendor specific apps.

histogram.name: Arc.AppUninstallReason
histogram.summary: Record a reason why an application was uninstalled when uninstall is completed.

histogram.name: Arc.boot_progress
histogram.summary: The relative time of a boot progress event since the ARC starts.

histogram.name: Arc.BootContinueCodeInstallationResult
histogram.summary: Code installation result for ARC boot continue.

histogram.name: Arc.CodeIntegrityCheckingTotalTime
histogram.summary: Total time needed to check the integrity of host generated code. If signature checking fails, it also includes the time to regenerate and sign the code.

histogram.name: Arc.CodeRelocationResult
histogram.summary: Host code relocation result.

histogram.name: Arc.CodeRelocationTime
histogram.summary: Time needed to relocate boot*.art files.

histogram.name: Arc.CodeSigningTime
histogram.summary: Time needed to sign boot*.art files.

histogram.name: Arc.CodeVerificationResult
histogram.summary: Code verification result for host generated code for boot for login screen.

histogram.name: Arc.CodeVerificationTime
histogram.summary: Time needed to verify host generated code.

histogram.name: Arc.CompanionLibraryApisCounter
histogram.summary: Records the number of times ChromeOS Companion Library API called. Counter adding when its Stub library receive the call from applications.

histogram.name: Arc.ComplianceReportSinceUpdateNotificationTime
histogram.summary: Elapsed time from the policy update notification sent to ARC to the corresponding successfully obtained compliance report from CloudDPS.

histogram.name: Arc.ContainerLifetimeEvent
histogram.summary: The number of ARC container lifetime events. One START event and up to one non-START event are recorded per ARC session, which ends when the user opts out from ARC or signs out.

histogram.name: Arc.ContainerRestartAfterCrashCount
histogram.summary: The number of times ARC container crashes and automatically restarts in one ARC session, which ends when the user opts out from ARC or signs out.

histogram.name: Arc.CpuRestrictionDisabled
histogram.summary: Records the time that throttling was disabled due to a particular throttle observer. The duration begins when a new throttle observer becomes active, and measures until it becomes inactive or another throttle observer becomes effective.

histogram.name: Arc.CumulativeUseTime
histogram.summary: ARC use time. The use time is incremented in regular intervals while android container is active. Reported at most once a day, at which point accumulated time is reset (which means that sum of reported times for a OS version would give an estimate of ARC usage time per OS version).

histogram.name: Arc.CupsPrinting.PageCount
histogram.summary: The number of pages sent to a native printer by ARC. Reported for every print job originating in ARC container and sent to a CUPS printer.

histogram.name: Arc.CustomTabs.SessionEndReason
histogram.summary: Reason why a custom tab session ended. Recorded when a session ends.

histogram.name: Arc.CustomTabs.SessionLifetime.All
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2019. Replaced by Arc.CustomTabs.SessionLifetime2.All to extend the max value.
histogram.summary: Lifetime of each session. Recorded when a session ends.

histogram.name: Arc.CustomTabs.SessionLifetime.Closed
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2019. Replaced by Arc.CustomTabs.SessionLifetime2.Closed to extend the max value.
histogram.summary: Lifetime of each session. Recorded when a session is closed.

histogram.name: Arc.CustomTabs.SessionLifetime.ForwardedToNormalTab
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2019. Replaced by Arc.CustomTabs.SessionLifetime2.ForwardedToNormalTab to extend the max value.
histogram.summary: Lifetime of each session. Recorded when a session ends because the tab was forwarded to a normal tab.

histogram.name: Arc.CustomTabs.SessionLifetime2.All
histogram.summary: Lifetime of each session. Recorded when a session ends.

histogram.name: Arc.CustomTabs.SessionLifetime2.Closed
histogram.summary: Lifetime of each session. Recorded when a session is closed.

histogram.name: Arc.CustomTabs.SessionLifetime2.ForwardedToNormalTab
histogram.summary: Lifetime of each session. Recorded when a session ends because the tab was forwarded to a normal tab.

histogram.name: Arc.EngagementTime.ArcTotal
histogram.summary: Total of Arc.EngagementTime.Background and Arc.EngagementTime.Foreground. Note that this differs from Arc.EngagementTime.Total in that this is the ARC++ engagement time, while Arc.EngagementTime.Total is the CrOS total engagement time on devices with ARC++ enabled. See Arc.EngagementTime.Total for definition of engagement and further details.

histogram.name: Arc.EngagementTime.Background
histogram.summary: Times when user is engaged and ARC++ apps are running in the background, but the user isn't focusing on an ARC++ app window. See Arc.EngagementTime.Total for definition of engagement and details.

histogram.name: Arc.EngagementTime.Foreground
histogram.summary: Times when user is engaged and focuses on an ARC++ app window. See Arc.EngagementTime.Total for definition of engagement and details.

histogram.name: Arc.EngagementTime.Total
histogram.summary: Total CrOS user session time (from login to logout) excluding times when user "disengages": screen is locked or dims down due to user idle. Engagement time metrics, along with foreground and background time, are only collected on users with ARC++ enabled. All four metrics are accumulated and recorded to UMA once a day. For Total ARC++ engagement time, please see Arc.EngagementTime.ArcTotal.

histogram.name: Arc.ExternalStorage.MountedMediaVisibility
histogram.summary: When a removable media is mounted, whether Android apps have full access to the media. Users can allow full access in Settings. Changing the preference in Settings is tracked by Arc.ExternalStorage.SetVisible.

histogram.name: Arc.ExternalStorage.SetVisible
histogram.summary: When a user clicks a toggle switch in "External storage preferences" screen, where the new value is ENABLED or not.

histogram.name: Arc.FirstAppLaunchDelay.TimeDelta
histogram.summary: How long the user sees the spinning icon waiting for ARC container boot. Zero when the container has already been up and running when the user launches the ARC app.

histogram.name: Arc.FirstAppLaunchRequest.TimeDelta
histogram.summary: Elapsed time from the when UI is shown after login to when the user activates first ARC app.

histogram.name: Arc.FirstComplianceReportTime.SinceSignIn
histogram.summary: Elapsed time from the signing in process start to a successfully obtained compliance report from CloudDPS.

histogram.name: Arc.FirstComplianceReportTime.SinceStartup
histogram.summary: Elapsed time from ARC startup to a successfully obtained compliance report from CloudDPS. This value is not reported during the first startup when signing in happens.

histogram.name: Arc.IconLoadFromFileTime.compressedFirst5
histogram.summary: Elapsed time of first 5 compressed app icons is loaded from file system. Recorded when the app icon loading completes.

histogram.name: Arc.IconLoadFromFileTime.compressedOthers
histogram.summary: Elapsed time of other compressed app icons is loaded from file system. Recorded when the app icon loading completes.

histogram.name: Arc.IconLoadFromFileTime.uncompressedFirst5
histogram.summary: Elapsed time of first 5 uncompressed app icons is loaded from file system. Recorded when the app icon loading completes.

histogram.name: Arc.IconLoadFromFileTime.uncompressedOthers
histogram.summary: Elapsed time of other uncompressed app icons is loaded from file system. Recorded when the app icon loading completes.

histogram.name: Arc.ImeCount
histogram.summary: The number of IME apps installed. Collected at startup and when an IME app is installed or uninstalled.

histogram.name: Arc.IntentHandlerAction
histogram.obsolete: Removed April 2018 and replaced by ChromeOS.Apps.IntentPickerAction.
histogram.summary: ARC intent handler action taken by user.

histogram.name: Arc.IntentHandlerDestinationPlatform
histogram.summary: ARC intent handler destination platform. The destination may be specified due to the user explicit selection or a previously stored preference.

histogram.name: Arc.IntentHelper.OpenType
histogram.summary: Types of things opened via ARC IntentHelperHost interface. Recorded when one of the "open" methods of the interface is called.

histogram.name: Arc.LowMemoryKiller.Count
histogram.summary: Cumulative count of low memory kills in one user session.

histogram.name: Arc.LowMemoryKiller.FirstKillLatency
histogram.summary: When Chrome OS is in low memory state, TabManager kills some victims to free memory. This value is the elapsed time between TabManager receiving the low memory notification and the first kill.

histogram.name: Arc.LowMemoryKiller.FreedSize
histogram.summary: The memory size freed by each low memory kill event.

histogram.name: Arc.LowMemoryKiller.TimeDelta
histogram.summary: The elapsed time to last low memory kill event.

histogram.name: Arc.NativeBridge
histogram.summary: Native bridge used for ARC. Can be unknown and none. This is collected along with Arc.State during every metrics recording interval, so it is in every record uploaded to the server (despite crbug.com/929583 which was fixed in M73). This is required because this value is used to categorize all other data in the dashboard as collected with specific native bridge in use. Since M74 this is also included in initial stability logs.

histogram.name: Arc.OOMKills.Count
histogram.summary: Cumulative count of OOM kills in one user session.

histogram.name: Arc.OOMKills.Score
histogram.summary: The oom_badness score of a OOM killed process as reported by kernel.

histogram.name: Arc.OOMKills.TimeDelta
histogram.summary: The elapsed time since last OOM kill event.

histogram.name: Arc.OptInAction
histogram.summary: Arc OptIn action taken by user.

histogram.name: Arc.OptInCancel
histogram.summary: Arc OptIn cancelation reason.

histogram.name: Arc.OptInResult
histogram.summary: Arc OptIn flow result.

histogram.name: Arc.OptInSilentAuthCode
histogram.summary: Arc Silent Auth Code status. This status is set during the ARC OptIn flow.

histogram.name: Arc.OptInSilentAuthCode.Reauthorization
histogram.summary: Arc Silent Auth Code status. This status is set during the ARC Device/Primary Account reauthorization flow.

histogram.name: Arc.OptInSilentAuthCode.SecondaryAccount
histogram.summary: Arc Silent Auth Code status. This status is set during the minting of an auth code for a Secondary Account. It can be set for Secondary Account signin or reauthorization.

histogram.name: Arc.PlayAutoInstallRequest.State
histogram.summary: State of PlayAutoInstall flow after the request.

histogram.name: Arc.PlayAutoInstallRequest.TimeDelta
histogram.summary: Elapsed time from the when the PlayAutoInstall request was issued and completed.

histogram.name: Arc.PlayStoreLaunch.TimeDelta
histogram.summary: Elapsed time from the when the user launches the Play Store app and to when the Play Store window is shown to the user.

histogram.name: Arc.PlayStoreSearch.DefaultResultClickLatency
histogram.summary: Time between a default play store result being activated by user pressing ENTER key and being displayed.

histogram.name: Arc.PlayStoreSearch.QueryTime
histogram.summary: Time between sending an Play Store app discovery request and the storing returned results.

histogram.name: Arc.PlayStoreSearch.ResultClickLatency
histogram.summary: Time between a play store result being clicked and being displayed.

histogram.name: Arc.PlayStoreSearch.ReturnedAppsTotal
histogram.summary: The total number of returned apps of a Play Store app discovery query.

histogram.name: Arc.PlayStoreSearch.ReturnedInstantApps
histogram.summary: The number of returned instant apps of a Play Store app discovery query.

histogram.name: Arc.PlayStoreSearch.ReturnedUninstalledApps
histogram.summary: The number of returned uninstalled apps of a Play Store app discovery query.

histogram.name: Arc.PlayStoreShown.TimeDelta
histogram.summary: Elapsed time from the when the user agrees ToS to when the Play Store window is shown to the user.

histogram.name: Arc.PrintPreview.PreviewEvent
histogram.summary: Print preview events for ARC. Recorded when specific print preview events occur (print preview requested, preview document created, etc.).

histogram.name: Arc.PrintPreview.RenderAndGeneratePDFTime
histogram.summary: Time taken for ARC to render and generate a PDF for print preview. Includes time to reflow the page back to normal, but not the time to reflow the page to prepare for printing.

histogram.name: Arc.PrintPreview.RenderAndGeneratePDFTimeAvgPerPage
histogram.summary: Time taken for ARC to render and generate a PDF for print preview divided by the number of pages. Includes time to reflow the page back to normal, but not the time to reflow the page to prepare for printing.

histogram.name: Arc.PrintPreview.RendererError
histogram.summary: Count how frequently a set of pre-defined print preview errors occur for ARC.

histogram.name: Arc.PrintPreview.RenderToPDFTime
histogram.summary: Time taken for ARC to render a PDF for print preview.

histogram.name: Arc.Provisioning.Result
histogram.summary: The result (success or the type of failure) of ARC provisioning. The base value has been deprecated on 2016-09-15 in favour of per user type values.

histogram.name: Arc.Provisioning.TimeDelta.Failure
histogram.summary: Elapsed time from the signing in process start to call to onSignInFailed.

histogram.name: Arc.Provisioning.TimeDelta.Success
histogram.summary: Elapsed time from the signing in process start to successful call to onSignInComplete.

histogram.name: Arc.Reauthorization.Result
histogram.summary: The result (success or the type of failure) of ARC reauthorization.

histogram.name: Arc.Runtime.Performance.CommitDeviation
histogram.summary: Standard deviation for commit time delta from ideal time.

histogram.name: Arc.Runtime.Performance.FPS
histogram.summary: Render frames per second.

histogram.name: Arc.Runtime.Performance.RenderQuality
histogram.summary: Render quality with maximum 100%.

histogram.name: Arc.SdkVersionUpgradeType
histogram.summary: The types of ARC SDK version upgrade. (e.g. Upgrade from N to P) Reported when SDK version upgrade is detected during ARC boot.

histogram.name: Arc.Secondary.Signin.Result
histogram.summary: The result (success or the type of failure) of ARC Secondary Account signin/ reauthorization.

histogram.name: Arc.Session.MojoDisconnection
histogram.summary: This is updated when the mojo connections to processes in detailing in ArcMojoConnectionType are closed for reasons other than the ARC container shutting down or crashing.

histogram.name: Arc.Session.StopReason
histogram.summary: This is updated in case ARC++ stops for the reasons detailed in ArcStopReason. For example, this is recorded in case of a graceful ARC++ shutdown or if ARC++ crashes.

histogram.name: Arc.ShareFilesOnExit
histogram.summary: Exit code coming from ARC's share files feature. Used to observe stability for the feature.

histogram.name: Arc.ShutdownTime
histogram.summary: The time it takes for ARC to shut down. This value is recorded on ARC shutdown as the time is takes for the init process to exit after setting 'sys.powerctl shutdown'. It is reported on the next ARC startup to avoid sending data during shutdown.

histogram.name: Arc.State
histogram.summary: Whether ARC is enabled or not. Before M56 this was collected only on login and when ARC was enabled or disabled. From M56 forward this is collected during every metrics recording interval, so it is in every record uploaded to the server (despite crbug.com/929583 which was fixed in M73). This is required because this value is used to categorize all other data in the dashboard as collected with ARC enabled or not. This is true even for users who are running on ecryptfs and skipped Ext4 migration; even though ARC Apps do not run until migration is complete on N or later systems. Since M74 this is also included in initial stability logs.

histogram.name: Arc.StateByUserType
histogram.summary: Similar to "Arc.State" metric but has separation by user type.

histogram.name: Arc.Supervision.Transition.Result
histogram.summary: The result (success or the type of failure) of ARC supervision transition events.

histogram.name: Arc.Supervision.Transition.Screen.Success.TimeDelta
histogram.summary: Elapsed time for a successful supervision transition, during which the supervision transition screen was displayed.

histogram.name: Arc.Supervision.Transition.Screen.Successful
histogram.summary: Whether supervision transition screen exited due to a successful transition.

histogram.name: Arc.Tracing.Tool
histogram.summary: Action of ARC++ Graphics Tracing tool.

histogram.name: Arc.UiAvailable.AlreadyProvisioned.TimeDelta
histogram.summary: Elapsed time from the when ARC++ is started in already provisioned state to when the ARC is available for the user.

histogram.name: Arc.UiAvailable.InSessionProvisioning.TimeDelta
histogram.summary: Elapsed time from the when ARC++ is opted in from the user's session to when the ARC is available for the user.

histogram.name: Arc.UiAvailable.OobeProvisioning.TimeDelta
histogram.summary: Elapsed time from the when ARC++ is opted in from OOBE to when the ARC is available for the user.

histogram.name: Arc.UserInteraction
histogram.summary: Counts user interactions with ARC by type, such as ARC app launches via the shelf or launcher.

histogram.name: ArcAuth.AccountCheckStatus
histogram.summary: The status of account check before GMS Sign-in.

histogram.name: ArcAuth.AccountCheckTime
histogram.summary: Elapsed time waiting for the account status check to be completed. This is not recorded in case of account status check failure.

histogram.name: ArcAuth.CheckinAttempts
histogram.summary: Number of attempts done while waiting for the check-in task to be completed. 0 means that device was already checked-in. This is recorded in case check-in task is completed successfully.

histogram.name: ArcAuth.CheckinTime
histogram.summary: Elapsed time waiting for the check-in task to be completed. This is recorded in case check-in task is completed successfully.

histogram.name: ArcAuth.MainAccountResolutionStatus
histogram.summary: Contains the status of main account resolution.

histogram.name: ArcAuth.NetworkWaitTime
histogram.summary: Elapsed time waiting for network connection before starting other authorization operations.

histogram.name: ArcAuth.SignInTime
histogram.summary: Elapsed time waiting for GMS sign-in to complete.

histogram.name: ArcRuntime.LowMemoryKiller.FreedSize
histogram.summary: The memory size freed by each low memory kill event.

histogram.name: ArcRuntime.LowMemoryKiller.TimeDelta
histogram.summary: The elapsed time to last low memory kill event.

histogram.name: Ash.Accelerators.Deprecated.LockScreen
histogram.summary: The lock screen action has two accelerators: - Ctrl+Shift+L which is deprecated. - Search+L which is new. This histogram shows the number of times each accelerator (deprecated and new) is used.

histogram.name: Ash.Accelerators.Deprecated.NextIME
histogram.summary: The switch to the next IME action has two accelerators: - Shift+Alt which is deprecated. - Ctrl+Shift+Space which is new. This histogram shows the number of times each accelerator (deprecated and new) is used.

histogram.name: Ash.Accelerators.Deprecated.ShowImeMenuBubble
histogram.summary: Showing the IME menu bubble has two accelerators: - Alt+Shift+K which is deprecated. - Search+Shift+K which is new. This histogram shows the number of times each accelerator (deprecated and new) is used.

histogram.name: Ash.Accelerators.Deprecated.ShowTaskManager
histogram.summary: Opening the task manager has two accelerators: - Shift+Esc which is deprecated. - Search+Esc which is new. This histogram shows the number of times each accelerator (deprecated and new) is used.

histogram.name: Ash.Accelerators.Deprecated.ToggleHighContrast
histogram.summary: Toggling the high contrast display mode has two accelerators: - Search+Shift+H which is deprecated. - Ctrl+Search+H which is new. This histogram shows the number of times each accelerator (deprecated and new) is used.

histogram.name: Ash.Accelerators.Rotation.Usage
histogram.summary: Captures the result of a user using the rotation accelerator - Ctrl+Shift+Refresh. This histogram shows how frequently users accidentally hit the key combination.

histogram.name: Ash.Accelerators.WindowSnap
histogram.summary: Captures usage of Alt+[ and Alt+].

histogram.name: Ash.ActiveTouchPoints
histogram.obsolete: Removed 02/2017 due to lack of usage.
histogram.summary: Number of active touch-points when a new touch-point is added.

histogram.name: Ash.ActiveWindowShowTypeOverTime
histogram.summary: The show type of the active window tracked over time by logging on a regular basis (30 minutes).

histogram.name: Ash.AmbientMode.AnimationSmoothness.PhotoTransition
histogram.summary: Emitted when the ambient mode photo transition animation completes to report the smoothness of this animation. 100% represents ideally smooth 60 frames per second.

histogram.name: Ash.AppList.TimeBetweenTaskSwitches
histogram.summary: The amount of time between selecting an item from the Ash app list. Not recorded on the first time an item is selected from the app list after startup.

histogram.name: Ash.BackGesture.EndScenarioType
histogram.summary: End scenario type while swiping back from the left side of the window in tablet mode.

histogram.name: Ash.BackGesture.StartScenarioType
histogram.summary: Start scenario type while swiping back from the left side of the window in tablet mode.

histogram.name: Ash.BackGesture.UnderneathWindowType
histogram.summary: Underneath window type while swiping back from the left side of the window in tablet mode.

histogram.name: Ash.ContextualNudge
histogram.summary: Tracks usage of the contextual nudge for the user gesture education.

histogram.name: Ash.ContextualNudgeDismissContext
histogram.summary: Tracks the reason contextual nudges for user gesture education are dismissed.

histogram.name: Ash.ContextualNudgeDismissTime
histogram.summary: Tracks time between showing contextual nudge and gesture being performed.

histogram.name: Ash.Desks.AnimationSmoothness.DeskActivation
histogram.summary: Emitted when the virtual desks activation animation completes to report the smoothness of this animation when running on the display on which the active window resides (if any) or the display of the primary root window. 100% represents ideally smooth 60 frames per second.

histogram.name: Ash.Desks.AnimationSmoothness.DeskRemoval
histogram.summary: Emitted when the virtual desks removal animation completes to report the smoothness of this animation when running on the display on which the active window resides (if any) or the display of the primary root window. 100% represents ideally smooth 60 frames per second.

histogram.name: Ash.Desks.DesksCount
histogram.obsolete: Removed 03/2020, and replaced by Ash.Desks.DesksCount2. This was broken as it did report desks count during the creation of the first-ever created desk after boot.
histogram.summary: Emitted when there's a change in the virtual desks count whether due to desk creation or removal. Specifies the number of available desks.

histogram.name: Ash.Desks.DesksCount2
histogram.summary: Emitted when there's a change in the virtual desks count whether due to desk creation or removal. Specifies the number of available desks. It is not emitted for the first-ever created default desk.

histogram.name: Ash.Desks.DesksSwitch
histogram.summary: Emitted when the active desk is changed to specify the source of this action, i.e. whether due to new-desk shortcut, desk removed, window activated, switch-desk shortcut, or a press on the desk mini_view.

histogram.name: Ash.Desks.MoveWindowFromActiveDesk
histogram.summary: Emitted when a window is moved from the current active desk to another desk. Specifies the source of this action, i.e. whether to window drag-and-drop, or keyboard shortcut.

histogram.name: Ash.Desks.NewDesk
histogram.obsolete: Removed 03/2020, and replaced by Ash.Desks.NewDesk2. This was broken as it did report the creation of the first-ever created default desk after boot as being created by the new-desk button.
histogram.summary: Emitted when a virtual desk is created to specify the source of this action, i.e. whether from the new-desk button, keyboard shortcut.

histogram.name: Ash.Desks.NewDesk2
histogram.summary: Emitted when a virtual desk is created to specify the source of this action, i.e. whether from the new-desk button, keyboard shortcut, or due to desks restore. It is not emitted for the first-ever created default desk.

histogram.name: Ash.Desks.NumberOfWindowsOnDesk_1
histogram.summary: The number of windows on the first desk. Emitted when a desk is removed, or a window is moved to another desk.

histogram.name: Ash.Desks.NumberOfWindowsOnDesk_2
histogram.summary: The number of windows on the second desk. Emitted when a desk is removed, or a window is moved to another desk.

histogram.name: Ash.Desks.NumberOfWindowsOnDesk_3
histogram.summary: The number of windows on the third desk. Emitted when a desk is removed, or a window is moved to another desk.

histogram.name: Ash.Desks.NumberOfWindowsOnDesk_4
histogram.summary: The number of windows on the fourth desk. Emitted when a desk is removed, or a window is moved to another desk.

histogram.name: Ash.Desks.RemoveDesk
histogram.summary: Emitted when a virtual desk is removed to specify the source of this remove operation, i.e. whether from the close-desk button, or keyboard shortcut.

histogram.name: Ash.Desktop.TimeBetweenNavigateToTaskSwitches
histogram.summary: The number of seconds between task window activations triggered by users clicking or tapping on a window, as recorded by the Desktop_SwitchTask user action. Recorded on Chrome OS only. Task switches from other sources are ignored and do not affect this metric. In other words, if a user performs the following steps (1) click on task window 'A' (2) Alt+Tab to task 'Z'(3) click on task window 'B', then this will result in a sample recorded for the time delta between activating task window 'A'and 'B'.

histogram.name: Ash.Display.PrimaryDisplayZoomAtStartup
histogram.summary: The display zoom setting for the primary display, recorded on startup.

histogram.name: Ash.DisplayChangeDialog.Saved
histogram.summary: Emitted when the user chooses an option on the Display Change Dialog that is shown when a user changes the resolution of an external display.

histogram.name: Ash.DisplayColorManager.HasColorCorrectionMatrix
histogram.summary: Whether a display has a "CTM" matrix properties that can be used to set a color transform matrix. Recorded every time there is a display configuration update.

histogram.name: Ash.DisplayColorManager.IccFileDownloaded
histogram.summary: This boolean keeps track if an ICC file was downloaded correctly (if true) or a local file was reused instead.

histogram.name: Ash.DisplayColorManager.IccFileFound
histogram.summary: This boolean keeps track if a request for an ICC for a specific product id has been successful or not.

histogram.name: Ash.DisplayColorManager.ValidDisplayColorSpace
histogram.summary: This boolean keeps track if a Display update (i.e. when connecting a new one) carried valid ColorSpace information.

histogram.name: Ash.DisplayColorManager.ValidProductId
histogram.summary: Whether we could parse a valid product id from a display EDID. Recorded every time there is a display configuration update.

histogram.name: Ash.Dock.Action
histogram.summary: User-initiated action taken that affects docked windows such as docking, undocking, minimizing, restoring, closing or just dragging a docked window.

histogram.name: Ash.Dock.ActionSource
histogram.summary: Source (mouse, touch, keyboard or unknown) of the user-initiated action for docked windows.

histogram.name: Ash.Dock.ItemsAll
histogram.summary: Number of all docked windows or panels including hidden or minimized. Recorded on every user action that interacts with docked windows.

histogram.name: Ash.Dock.ItemsLarge
histogram.summary: Number of large (wider than dock maximum width) windows that had to be shrunk to get docked among the visible docked windows. Recorded on every user action that interacts with docked windows.

histogram.name: Ash.Dock.ItemsPanels
histogram.summary: Number of docked visible panels. Recorded on every user action that interacts with docked windows.

histogram.name: Ash.Dock.ItemsVisible
histogram.summary: Number of visible docked windows or panels. Recorded on every user action that interacts with docked windows.

histogram.name: Ash.Dock.TimeBetweenUse
histogram.summary: Time elapsed between instances of docking, undocking or any other action affecting docked state of a window.

histogram.name: Ash.Dock.Width
histogram.summary: Width of the docked area in pixels. Recorded every time it changes after a user window resize operation is completed.

histogram.name: Ash.DragWindowFromShelf.PresentationTime
histogram.summary: Presentation time in ms when a window is dragged from the bottom of the shelf. Recorded when the drag action is in progress.

histogram.name: Ash.DragWindowFromShelf.PresentationTime.MaxLatency
histogram.summary: Maximum latency of the presentation time in ms when a window is dragged from the bottom of the shelf. Recorded when the drag action is finished.

histogram.name: Ash.GestureCreated
histogram.obsolete: Removed 08/2014 in Issue 352654, and replaced by Event.GestureCreated.
histogram.summary: The gesture-events recognized and dispatched by the browser gesture recognizer.

histogram.name: Ash.GestureTarget
histogram.summary: The gesture-events recognized and dispatched for UI components owned by Ash. For browser gestures, see Event.Touch.GestureType.

histogram.name: Ash.Homescreen.AnimationSmoothness
histogram.summary: Relative smoothness of animations when performing tablet mode homescreen animations, recorded when the animation completes. 100% represents ideally smooth 60 frames per second.

histogram.name: Ash.HotseatGesture
histogram.summary: Gestures supported by the in-app hotseat.

histogram.name: Ash.HotseatTransition.AnimationSmoothness
histogram.summary: Tracks the animation smoothness for the ShelfWidget's animating background during transitions of the hotseat to shown, extended, and hidden hotseat states. Check Ash.HotseatWidgetAnimation.AnimationSmoothness for smoothness of the HotseatWidget.

histogram.name: Ash.HotseatTransition.Drag.PresentationTime
histogram.summary: Presentation time in ms of the hotseat when it changes between the hidden and the extended state as a result of a drag action. Recorded when the drag action is in progress.

histogram.name: Ash.HotseatTransition.Drag.PresentationTime.MaxLatency
histogram.summary: Maximum latency of the presentation time in ms of the hotseat when it changes between the hidden and the extended state as a result of a drag action. Recorded when the drag action is finished.

histogram.name: Ash.HotseatWidgetAnimation.AnimationSmoothness
histogram.summary: Tracks the animation smoothness for the bounds animation of the hotseat widget's elements during transitions of the hotseat to shown, extended, and hidden hotseat states. Check Ash.HotseatTransition.AnimationSmoothness for smoothness of the shelf's animating background.

histogram.name: Ash.ImmersiveFullscreen.WindowType
histogram.summary: The type of the window which is put into immersive fullscreen. Immersive fullscreen is entered via the F4 key.

histogram.name: Ash.InteractiveWindowResize.TimeToPresent
histogram.summary: Time between when the size of a window changes during an interactive window resize and the results are drawn (presented) on screen. This is recorded for each movement of the mouse/pointer that results in a resize.

histogram.name: Ash.InteractiveWindowResize.TimeToPresent.MaxLatency
histogram.summary: Maximum time between when the size of a window changes during an interactive window resize and the results are drawn (presented) on screen. This is recorded for each movement of the mouse/pointer that results in a resize.

histogram.name: Ash.Login.Lock.AuthMethod.Switched
histogram.summary: The count of auth method switching actions in ChromeOS lock screen.

histogram.name: Ash.Login.Lock.AuthMethod.Used.ClamShellMode
histogram.summary: The usage of different auth methods (PIN / Password / Smartlock / Fingerprint) in ChromeOS lock screen clamshell mode.

histogram.name: Ash.Login.Lock.AuthMethod.Used.TabletMode
histogram.summary: The usage of different auth methods (PIN / Password / Smartlock / Fingerprint) in ChromeOS lock screen tablet mode.

histogram.name: Ash.Login.Lock.NbPasswordAttempts.UntilFailure
histogram.summary: The number of incorrect passwords entered in Chrome OS login/lock screen until the user gives up (user signs out the current session or shuts down the device).

histogram.name: Ash.Login.Lock.NbPasswordAttempts.UntilSuccess
histogram.summary: The number of incorrect passwords entered in Chrome OS login/lock screen until a successful attempt.

histogram.name: Ash.Login.Lock.NumPasswordAttempts.UntilFailure
histogram.obsolete: Removed 27/05/2020, the histogram has been revised as we do not count pod switching anymore and the metric was not recorded on shutdown. New histogram: Ash.Login.Lock.NbPasswordAttempts.UntilFailure
histogram.summary: The number of incorrect password entered in ChromeOS login/lock screen until the user gives up (switch pods or user sign out the current session or shutdown the device).

histogram.name: Ash.Login.Lock.NumPasswordAttempts.UntilSuccess
histogram.obsolete: Removed 27/05/2020, the histogram has been revised as we did not count successful authentification on login screen and successful authentification on lock screen wasn't recorded due to a bug. New histogram: Ash.Login.Lock.NbPasswordAttempts.UntilSuccess
histogram.summary: The number of incorrect password entered in ChromeOS login/lock screen until a successful attempt.

histogram.name: Ash.Login.Lock.UserClicks
histogram.summary: The numbers of times that users click on the shelf buttons, trays and lock screen note on the ChromeOS lock screen.

histogram.name: Ash.Login.Login.MigrationBanner
histogram.summary: Whether the cryptohome migration banner was shown on the Chrome OS login screen.

histogram.name: Ash.Login.Login.UserClicks
histogram.summary: The numbers of times that users click on the shelf buttons and trays on the ChromeOS login screen.

histogram.name: Ash.Login.OOBE.UserClicks
histogram.summary: What shelf buttons or trays are clicked in the OOBE.

histogram.name: Ash.NavigationWidget.AnimationSmoothness
histogram.summary: Relative smoothness of animations of the navigation widget's elements, which are the widget itself and its child views. 100% represents ideally smooth 60 frames per second. 50% represents only 30 frames per second is achieved during the animations. Recorded every time an animation is triggered in the Navigation Widget. We record this metric for each element separately.

histogram.name: Ash.NightLight.AutoNightLightNotificationShown
histogram.summary: Emitted when the Auto Night Light notification is shown.

histogram.name: Ash.NightLight.AutoNightLightNotificationState
histogram.summary: Emitted when the user interacts with the Auto Night Light notification to report the state of the notification as a result of this interaction.

histogram.name: Ash.NightLight.DisplayCrtcCtmSupport
histogram.summary: The type of CRTC color transorm matrix support for the currently connected displays. Emitted when cursor compositing status is refreshed, but only when Night Light is on. The cursor compositing status is refreshed whenever there is a change in Night Light status or color temperature.

histogram.name: Ash.NightLight.ScheduleType
histogram.summary: The selected Night Light schedule type. Emitted when the user changes the Night Light schedule type from the Display System Settings.

histogram.name: Ash.NightLight.Temperature
histogram.summary: The ranges in which the selected values of the Night Light color temperature reside. Emitted when the user drags the Night Light color temperature slider to change its value from the Display System Settings.

histogram.name: Ash.NumberOfVisibleWindowsInPrimaryDisplay
histogram.summary: An upper bound on the number of windows visible to the user on the primary display. Determined by processing the windows in increasing z-order and counting all non-minimized windows until a maximized or fullscreen window is processed. This metric is logged periodically every 30 minutes.

histogram.name: Ash.Overview.AnimationSmoothness.Close
histogram.summary: Relative smoothness of animations when closing a window, recorded when the animation completes. 100% represents ideally smooth 60 frames per second.

histogram.name: Ash.Overview.AnimationSmoothness.Enter
histogram.summary: Relative smoothness of animations when entering overview mode, recorded when the animation completes. 100% represents ideally smooth 60 frames per second.

histogram.name: Ash.Overview.AnimationSmoothness.Exit
histogram.summary: Relative smoothness of animations when exiting overview mode, recorded when the animation completes. 100% represents ideally smooth 60 frames per second.

histogram.name: Ash.Overview.Scroll.PresentationTime.MaxLatency.TabletMode
histogram.summary: Maximum latency of the presentation time for scrolling the overview grid in overview mode for a scrolling session.

histogram.name: Ash.Overview.Scroll.PresentationTime.TabletMode
histogram.summary: Presentation time for scrolling the overview grid in overview mode. Each time the grid is updated by a scroll, the time it takes to update the new visuals onto the screen is updated.

histogram.name: Ash.Overview.WindowDrag.PresentationTime
histogram.summary: Presentation time for dragging a window in overview mode.

histogram.name: Ash.Overview.WindowDrag.PresentationTime.MaxLatency
histogram.summary: Maximum latency of the presentation time for dragging a window in overview mode.

histogram.name: Ash.Overview.WindowDrag.Workflow
histogram.summary: Recorded when a drag from overview (not from the top or shelf) is completed, and when an overview item is closed by vertically swiping or flinging (even if you fling without dragging).

histogram.name: Ash.PersistentWindow.NumOfWindowsRestored
histogram.summary: The number of windows restored in multi-display scenario, such as due to disconnecting and reconnecting display, enabling and disabling mirror mode, entering and leaving dock mode. Zero is not recorded.

histogram.name: Ash.Pip.AndroidPipUseTime
histogram.summary: How long Android PIP was used for, upon ending the session.

histogram.name: Ash.Pip.Events
histogram.summary: Various individiual Picture-in-picture related events. See AshPipEvents.

histogram.name: Ash.Pip.FreeResizeFinishArea
histogram.summary: Area of a Picture-in-picture window when ending a free-resize, as a percentage of the area it takes up on the display it is on.

histogram.name: Ash.Pip.FreeResizeInitialArea
histogram.summary: Initial area of a Picture-in-picture window when beginning a free-resize, as a percentage of the area it takes up on the display it is on.

histogram.name: Ash.Pip.Position
histogram.summary: The position that a Picture-in-picture window was moved to by a user drag.

histogram.name: Ash.PowerButtonScreenshot.DelayBetweenAccelKeyPressed
histogram.summary: Record the delay when power button and volume down key are both pressed, which indicates user might want to use accelerator to take screenshot. This will help us determine the best chord delay among metrics.

histogram.name: Ash.ProcessCreationToFirstPresent
histogram.obsolete: Removed 05/2019; no longer need to track this.
histogram.summary: The delta between when chrome main starts to when the Chrome OS system UI (ash) is visible to the user (specifically the first time pixels are lit up on the primary display showing the Chrome OS system UI).

histogram.name: Ash.Rotation.AnimationSmoothness
histogram.summary: Relative smoothness of animations when rotating screen. 100% represents ideally smooth 60 frames per second. 50% represents only 30 frames per second is achieved during the animations. 0% should not happen. This metric is recorded exactly once when the user rotates the screen.

histogram.name: Ash.Shelf.Menu.NumItemsEnabledUponSelection
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of menu items that are enabled in a shelf item's secondary menu. This metric is only recorded when a menu item is selected.

histogram.name: Ash.Shelf.Menu.SelectedMenuItemIndex
histogram.summary: Tracks the index of the selected menu item in a shelf item's secondary menu. This metric is only recorded when a menu item is selected.

histogram.name: Ash.Shelf.NavigationButtonsInTabletMode.ReasonShown
histogram.summary: The reason shelf navigation buttons are shown in tablet mode. The buttons are shown when the device is in tablet mode only if certain accessibility settings are enabled. This histogram records which accessibility setting caused the buttons to be shown. Shown when navigation buttons visibility changes in tablet mode, and only if HideShelfControlsInTabletMode feature is enabled.

histogram.name: Ash.Shelf.NumberOfItems
histogram.summary: The number of icons in the shelf, not including the App Launcher icon. This metric is recorded every 30 minutes.

histogram.name: Ash.Shelf.NumberOfPinnedItems
histogram.summary: The number of pinned icons in the shelf, not including the App Launcher icon. This metric is recorded every 30 minutes.

histogram.name: Ash.Shelf.NumberOfUnpinnedItems
histogram.summary: The number of unpinned icons in the shelf. This metric is recorded every 30 minutes.

histogram.name: Ash.Shelf.Palette.Assistant.CircledPercentage
histogram.summary: The percentage of the screen area circled by the Assistant pen. Recorded when the "closed shape" gesture is recognized.

histogram.name: Ash.Shelf.Palette.Assistant.GestureDuration
histogram.summary: The duration of an Assistant pen gesture. Recorded at the end of every gesture.

histogram.name: Ash.Shelf.Palette.Assistant.GestureInterval
histogram.summary: The interval between subsequent Assistant pen gestures within a session. Session is defined as the period while the Assistant tool is enabled. Recorded at the end of each gesture except for the first one in the session. The interval is computed excluding the gesture duration.

histogram.name: Ash.Shelf.Palette.Assistant.GesturesPerSession
histogram.summary: The number of Assistant pen gestures per session. Session is defined as the period while the Assistant tool is enabled. Recorded at the end of the session.

histogram.name: Ash.Shelf.Palette.Assistant.GesturesPerSession.Recognized
histogram.summary: The number of recognized Assistant pen gestures per session. Session is defined as the period while the Assistant tool is enabled. Recorded at the end of the session.

histogram.name: Ash.Shelf.Palette.Assistant.GestureType
histogram.summary: The type of the Assistant pen gesture. Recorded at the end of each gesture.

histogram.name: Ash.Shelf.Palette.Assistant.HighlighterLength
histogram.summary: The length of a recognized "horizontal stroke" Assistant pen gesture. Recorded at the and of the gesture.

histogram.name: Ash.Shelf.Palette.InAssistantMode
histogram.summary: The amount of time spent in Palette Assistant mode. Recorded when the Assistant mode is exited.

histogram.name: Ash.Shelf.Palette.InLaserPointerMode
histogram.summary: The amount of time spend in Palette Laser pointer mode. Recorded when the Laser pointer mode is exited.

histogram.name: Ash.Shelf.Palette.InMagnifyMode
histogram.summary: The amount of time spend in Palette Magnify mode. Recorded when the Magnify mode is exited.

histogram.name: Ash.Shelf.Palette.ModeCancellation
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of times a palette mode is explicitly cancelled or switched out of.

histogram.name: Ash.Shelf.Palette.Usage
histogram.summary: Recorded every time that the palette option has been selected from the palette that has been opened manually (not via a stylus eject event).

histogram.name: Ash.Shelf.Palette.Usage.AutoOpened
histogram.summary: Recorded every time that the palette option has been selected from the palette that has been opened automatically (by a stylus eject event).

histogram.name: Ash.Shelf.Palette.Usage.Shortcut
histogram.summary: Recorded every time that the palette option has been selected by means other that the palette menu (e.g. stylus barrel button or a keyboard accelerator).

histogram.name: Ash.Shelf.TimeBetweenNavigateToTaskSwitches
histogram.summary: The number of seconds between contiguous task switch user actions triggered by the Shelf buttons where the user activates a different user-predictable task. Task switches from other sources are ignored and do not affect this metric. In other words, if a user performs the following steps (1) launch task 'A' from the Shelf (2) Alt+Tab to task 'Z' (3) launch task 'B' from the Shelf, then this will result in a sample recorded for the time delta between launching tasks 'A' and 'B'.

histogram.name: Ash.Shelf.TimeBetweenWindowMinimizedAndActivatedActions
histogram.summary: Tracks the amount of time between a window being minimized by the shelf and subsequently activated. In other words this metric is only recorded if a shelf button pressed action causes a window to be minimized and the very next shelf button pressed action causes the same window to be activated.

histogram.name: Ash.Shelf.UkmLogger.HasNightLightSchedule
histogram.summary: Whether or not the user had set up a night light schedule at the time of night light event logging.

histogram.name: Ash.Shelf.UkmLogger.NumAvailableBluetoothDevices
histogram.summary: The number of Bluetooth devices available at the time of Bluetooth event logging.

histogram.name: Ash.ShelfAlignmentOverTime
histogram.summary: The current state of the shelf (alignment) tracked over time by logging on a regular basis (30 minutes), this is used instead of log in or shelf usage to track users that do not lock/unlock or log in frequently and use a small number of browser instances or otherwise infrequently interact with the shelf launcher.

histogram.name: Ash.ShelfAlignmentUsage
histogram.summary: The current state of the shelf (alignment) when the shelf launcher is used to launch an app/window/etc, this is used instead of log in to give data on users that do not lock/unlock or log in frequently.

histogram.name: Ash.ShelfIcon.AnimationSmoothness
histogram.summary: Relative smoothness of animations of the shelf icons. 100% represents ideally smooth 60 frames per second. Recorded when shelf icon animations are completed, including icon move, icon fade-in and icon fade-out.

histogram.name: Ash.SplitView.TimeInMultiDisplaySplitView
histogram.summary: The amount of time that the user spent in multi-display split view mode, meaning that split view is active on more than one display. The time is measured from when the number of displays in split view becomes more than one to when it drops back down to one.

histogram.name: Ash.SplitView.TimeInSplitView
histogram.summary: The amount of time that the user spent in split view mode. The time is measured from the moment a window is snapped to one side of the screen to when split view mode is ended.

histogram.name: Ash.SplitViewResize.PresentationTime
histogram.summary: Presentation time while resizing one or two split view windows.

histogram.name: Ash.SplitViewResize.PresentationTime.MaxLatency
histogram.summary: Maximum latency of the presentation time while resizing one or two split view windows.

histogram.name: Ash.StationaryTouchDuration
histogram.obsolete: Removed 02/2017 due to lack of usage.
histogram.summary: The duration of mostly stationary long-duration touches.

histogram.name: Ash.SwipeDownDrag.Tab.PresentationTime.MaxLatency.TabletMode
histogram.summary: Maximum latency of the presentation timestamp while dragging a tab in tablet mode.

histogram.name: Ash.SwipeDownDrag.Tab.PresentationTime.TabletMode
histogram.summary: Presentation time while dragging a tab in tablet mode.

histogram.name: Ash.SwipeDownDrag.Window.PresentationTime.MaxLatency.TabletMode
histogram.summary: Maximum latency of the presentation timestamp while dragging maximized window in tablet mode.

histogram.name: Ash.SwipeDownDrag.Window.PresentationTime.TabletMode
histogram.summary: Presentation time while dragging maximized window in tablet mode.

histogram.name: Ash.SwipeHomeToOverviewGesture
histogram.summary: Boolean indicating whether a swipe from hotseat on home screen resulted in a transition to overview.

histogram.name: Ash.SystemMenu.DefaultView.VisibleRows
histogram.summary: The visible rows displayed in the system menu's default view. Recorded each time the menu is opened.

histogram.name: Ash.SystemMenu.PercentageOfWorkAreaHeightCoveredByMenu
histogram.summary: The height of the system menu divided by the height of the Ash desktop work area; a value over 100% indicates that a portion of the system menu is not visible to the user. Recorded each time the menu is opened.

histogram.name: Ash.SystemMenu.Rows
histogram.summary: The number of user-visible rows in the system menu's default view. Recorded each time the menu is opened.

histogram.name: Ash.Tab.TimeBetweenSwitchToExistingTabUserActions
histogram.summary: The number of seconds between tab switches triggered by a user gesture (e.g. Ctrl+T, Ctrl+1, tapping or clicking the tab strip, etc).

histogram.name: Ash.TabletMode.AnimationSmoothness.Enter
histogram.summary: Relative smoothness of animations when entering tablet mode, recorded when the animation completes. 100% represents ideally smooth 60 frames per second.

histogram.name: Ash.TabletMode.AnimationSmoothness.Exit
histogram.summary: Relative smoothness of animations when exiting tablet mode, recorded when the animation completes. 100% represents ideally smooth 60 frames per second.

histogram.name: Ash.TimeBetweenTaskSwitches
histogram.summary: The number of seconds between contiguous task switch user actions triggered by any of the other task switch actions that are tracked. (e.g., Ash.Shelf.TimeBetweenNavigateToTaskSwitches, Ash.Tab.TimeBetweenSwitchToExistingTabUserActions, Ash.WindowCycleController.TimeBetweenTaskSwitches, Ash.AppList.TimeBetweenTaskSwitches, Ash.WindowSelector.TimeBetweenActiveWindowChanges, etc). Note the Ash.AppList.TimeBetweenTaskSwitches and Ash.Tab.TimeBetweenSwitchToExistingTabUserActions histograms were deprecated and removed in 10/2016.

histogram.name: Ash.TouchDuration
histogram.obsolete: Removed 12/2013 in r239809, and replaced by Ash.TouchDuration2.
histogram.summary: The duration of a touch-sequence.

histogram.name: Ash.TouchDuration2
histogram.obsolete: Removed 08/2014 in Issue 352654, and replaced by Event.TouchDuration.
histogram.summary: The duration of a touch-sequence.

histogram.name: Ash.TouchMaxDistance
histogram.obsolete: Removed 08/2014 in Issue 352654, and replaced by Event.TouchMaxDistance.
histogram.summary: The maximum euclidean distance in dips which a touch point has travelled away from its starting point. Only measured for single finger gestures.

histogram.name: Ash.TouchMoveInterval
histogram.obsolete: Removed 02/2017 due to lack of usage.
histogram.summary: The interval between touch-move events.

histogram.name: Ash.TouchMoveSteps
histogram.summary: The distance between touch-move events.

histogram.name: Ash.TouchPositionX
histogram.obsolete: Removed 07/2018 due to lack of usage.
histogram.summary: The position of the touch-events along the X axis.

histogram.name: Ash.TouchPositionY
histogram.obsolete: Removed 07/2018 due to lack of usage.
histogram.summary: The position of the touch-events along the Y axis.

histogram.name: Ash.TouchRadius
histogram.obsolete: Removed 07/2018 due to lack of usage.
histogram.summary: The radius of a touch event.

histogram.name: Ash.TouchStartAfterEnd
histogram.summary: The interval between the end of a touch-sequence and the start of the next touch-sequence.

histogram.name: Ash.TouchStartBurst
histogram.obsolete: Removed 02/2017 due to lack of usage.
histogram.summary: The number of rapid touch-starts that happened within a short interval. Logged once for each such burst group.

histogram.name: Ash.TouchView.LidAngle
histogram.summary: Chrome OS only. The computed angle between the lid and the keyboard panel. These values are imprecise and may be tens of degrees off from reality. Reported once per hour that the system is awake, and not reported if the system doesn't have two accelerometers.

histogram.name: Ash.TouchView.TouchViewActive
histogram.summary: The length of time that TouchView is active, for each activation.

histogram.name: Ash.TouchView.TouchViewActivePercentage
histogram.summary: The proportion of time spent in TouchView during a session.

histogram.name: Ash.TouchView.TouchViewActiveTotal
histogram.summary: The total time that TouchView is active during a session.

histogram.name: Ash.TouchView.TouchViewInactive
histogram.summary: The length of time between TouchView activations.

histogram.name: Ash.TouchView.TouchViewInactiveTotal
histogram.summary: The total time that TouchView is not active during a session.

histogram.name: Ash.Wallpaper.Apps
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 2/2018. Currently there is only one type of Wallpaper App.
histogram.summary: The Wallpaper App that the user is using right now on Chrome OS. It's the app that is used when the user right clicks on desktop and selects "Set wallpaper" or when the user selects "Set wallpaper" from chrome://settings page. This is recorded at user login.

histogram.name: Ash.Wallpaper.ColorExtraction.Durations
histogram.summary: The time taken to extract colors from wallpapers. Recorded each time the wallpaper image changes. It may contain multiple colors extracted.

histogram.name: Ash.Wallpaper.ColorExtraction.UserDelay
histogram.summary: The time taken to extract colors from 'expensive' wallpapers. Recorded each time the wallpaper image changes and the color extraction is expected to be expensive, e.g. image size > 100 pixels. This includes time spent switching threads. It may contain multiple colors extracted.

histogram.name: Ash.Wallpaper.ColorExtractionResult
histogram.summary: Tracks the success rate for wallpaper color extraction. Recorded each time the wallpaper image changes.

histogram.name: Ash.Wallpaper.ColorExtractionResult2
histogram.summary: Tracks the success rate for wallpaper color extraction. Recorded each time the wallpaper image changes for each extracted wallpaper prominent color.

histogram.name: Ash.Wallpaper.CustomLayout
histogram.summary: The custom wallpaper layout type. Recorded when the user sets a new custom wallpaper or changes the existing custom wallpaper's layout.

histogram.name: Ash.Wallpaper.DefaultIndex
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 11/2012. Use of indices has been removed.
histogram.summary: The wallpaper index if one of the default wallpapers has been selected. Recorded at user login. Currently only for the old wallpaper picker UI.

histogram.name: Ash.Wallpaper.Source
histogram.summary: Recorded when a new wallpaper is set, either by the built-in Wallpaper Picker App, or by a third party App. Note the wallpaper change triggered by Sync file system event doesn't count.

histogram.name: Ash.Wallpaper.TimeSpentExtractingColors
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 04/2017 in favor of Ash.Wallpaper.ColorExtraction.Durations and Ash.Wallpaper.ColorExtraction.UserDelay.
histogram.summary: The time taken to extract colors from wallpapers. Recorded each time the wallpaper image changes. NOTE, this measure also included the time spent jumping between threads, thus it was deprecated in favor of Ash.Wallpaper.ColorExtraction.Durations.

histogram.name: Ash.Wallpaper.TimeSpentResizing
histogram.summary: The time taken to resize wallpapers. Recorded once each time the wallpaper changes (e.g. initial wallpaper loading at boot, switching user avatar at login screen, switching between logged in users, user selects new wallpaper, etc).

histogram.name: Ash.Wallpaper.Type
histogram.summary: The wallpaper type. Recorded at user login.

histogram.name: Ash.Window.AnimationSmoothness.CrossFade
histogram.summary: Relative smoothness of cross fade animation when setting window bounds. 100% represents ideally smooth 60 frames per second. 50% represents when only 30 frames per second is achieved during the animations. 0% should not happen. This metric is recorded exactly once when SetBoundsDirectCrossFade is called, such as when window is maximized.

histogram.name: Ash.Window.AnimationSmoothness.Hide
histogram.summary: Relative smoothness of hiding window animation. 100% represents ideally smooth 60 frames per second. 50% represents when only 30 frames per second is achieved during the animations. 0% should not happen. This metric is recorded exactly once when AnimateHideWindowCommon is called, such as when window is closed.

histogram.name: Ash.Window.AnimationSmoothness.Snap
histogram.summary: Relative smoothness of snapping window animation. 100% represents ideally smooth 60 frames per second. 50% represents when only 30 frames per second is achieved during the animations. 0% should not happen. This metric is recorded exactly once when a window is snapped to the left or right in clamshell mode using the alt + [ or ] accelerator, but only if the size of the window's bounds change.

histogram.name: Ash.WindowCycleController.CycleTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time the Alt key is held after pressing Alt+Tab to begin cycling through windows.

histogram.name: Ash.WindowCycleController.DesksSwitchDistance
histogram.summary: Emitted when the user ends window cycling via Alt-Tab, if and only if user had 2 or more desks and a new window was activated, to report the distance between the active desks before and after window cycling. A distance of 0 means no desks switch occurred.

histogram.name: Ash.WindowCycleController.Items
histogram.summary: The number of windows in the Alt+Tab selector. Only recorded once when cycling starts, not every time when Tab is pressed without releasing Alt.

histogram.name: Ash.WindowCycleController.SelectionDepth
histogram.summary: When a window is selected after pressing Alt+Tab, records that window's position in the global MRU ordering. 1 represents the most-recently used window, 2 represents the next most-recently used window, and so on. Recorded when Alt+Tab cycling stops, i.e., when Alt key is released.

histogram.name: Ash.WindowCycleController.TimeBetweenTaskSwitches
histogram.summary: The number of seconds between task switches triggered by the next window and previous window accelerator keys (ie Alt+Tab, Alt+Shift+Tab).

histogram.name: Ash.WindowCycleView.AnimationSmoothness.Container
histogram.summary: Relative smoothness of showing window animation when the window cycle view moves the next window into position when alt + tab is pressed. 100% represents ideally smooth 60 frames per second. 50% represents when only 30 frames per second is achieved during the animations. 0% should not happen.

histogram.name: Ash.WindowCycleView.AnimationSmoothness.HighLight
histogram.obsolete: Removed 01/2020 in Issue 989794 since we are now using focus rings instead of a highlight. The focus rings have no animations.
histogram.summary: Relative smoothness of showing window animation when the highlight moves to the next window in the window cycle view when alt + tab is pressed. 100% represents ideally smooth 60 frames per second. 50% represents when only 30 frames per second is achieved during the animations. 0% should not happen.

histogram.name: Ash.WindowCycleView.AnimationSmoothness.Show
histogram.summary: Relative smoothness of showing window animation when the window cycle view starts. 100% represents ideally smooth 60 frames per second. 50% represents when only 30 frames per second is achieved during the animations. 0% should not happen.

histogram.name: Ash.WindowDragFromShelfResult
histogram.summary: The final state after window drag gesture that started from the system shelf in tablet mode.

histogram.name: Ash.WindowManager.Lock.Success
histogram.summary: How long it took for the screen lock process to complete. This does not include timeouts.

histogram.name: Ash.WindowManager.Lock.Timeout
histogram.summary: How long it took for the screen lock process to complete, for timeouts only.

histogram.name: Ash.WindowSelector.AnimationSmoothness.Close
histogram.summary: Relative smoothness of animations when closing a window in overview mode. 100% represents ideally smooth 60 frames per second.

histogram.name: Ash.WindowSelector.AnimationSmoothness.Enter
histogram.summary: Relative smoothness of animations when entering overview mode. 100% represents ideally smooth 60 frames per second.

histogram.name: Ash.WindowSelector.AnimationSmoothness.Exit
histogram.summary: Relative smoothness of animations when exiting overview mode. 100% represents ideally smooth 60 frames per second.

histogram.name: Ash.WindowSelector.ArrowKeyPresses
histogram.summary: The number of times the arrow keys are pressed in overview mode per session, i.e. between bringing up overview mode and ending it. This is only measured for the sessions that end by selecting a window with the enter key.

histogram.name: Ash.WindowSelector.CycleTime
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 06/2014. No longer relevant since alt-tab switching was separated from WindowSelector.
histogram.summary: The amount of time the Alt key is held after pressing Alt+Tab to begin cycling through windows.

histogram.name: Ash.WindowSelector.Items
histogram.summary: The number of items (single windows or groups of windows such as panels) in the overview mode, present at the start of each session.

histogram.name: Ash.WindowSelector.ItemsWhenTextFilteringUsed
histogram.summary: The number of items showing in overview mode at the moment when an item is selected or when selection is canceled. Only recorded if the text filtering textfield contains a non-empty string.

histogram.name: Ash.WindowSelector.KeyPressesOverItemsRatio
histogram.summary: The ratio between the arrow key presses and the number of overview items, expressed as a percentage for a single session.

histogram.name: Ash.WindowSelector.OverviewClosedItems
histogram.summary: The number of items closed from the window overview for a single session.

histogram.name: Ash.WindowSelector.SelectionDepth
histogram.summary: When a window is selected in overview mode, records that window's position in the global MRU ordering. 1 represents the most-recently used window, 2 represents the next most-recently used window, and so on.

histogram.name: Ash.WindowSelector.TextFilteringStringLength
histogram.summary: The length of the string entered into the text filtering textfield at the moment when an item is selected or when selection is canceled.

histogram.name: Ash.WindowSelector.TextFilteringTextfieldCleared
histogram.summary: The number of times the text filtering textfield has had all of its text removed within a single overview mode session. Measured from the time overview mode is invoked to when an item is selected or when selection is canceled.

histogram.name: Ash.WindowSelector.TimeBetweenActiveWindowChanges
histogram.summary: The amount of time between endings of overview mode sessions which were caused by the user selecting a window which was not previously active. Only recorded on the second and later times after startup that the user selected a window which was not previously active.

histogram.name: Ash.WindowSelector.TimeBetweenUse
histogram.summary: The amount of time between uses of overview mode, recorded when overview mode is entered. Only recorded on the second and later times after startup that the user entered overview mode.

histogram.name: Ash.WindowSelector.TimeInOverview
