histogram.summary: The HTTP response code returned on a query.

histogram.name: Autofill.Query.HttpResponseOrErrorCode
histogram.summary: The http response code or net error code returned on a query.

histogram.name: Autofill.Query.Method
histogram.summary: The HTTP method used to query the autofill server.

histogram.name: Autofill.Query.RequestDuration
histogram.summary: The duration of a network request for a query.

histogram.name: Autofill.Query.WasInCache
histogram.summary: Was the autofill query response retrieved from the HTTP cache.

histogram.name: Autofill.RationalizationQuality.PhoneNumber
histogram.summary: The quality of fields rationalization, putting rationalization result into good/ok/bad categories.

histogram.name: Autofill.ResetFullServerCards.NumberOfCardsReset
histogram.obsolete: Were added as part of Issue 851295; no longer necessary. Removed 2019/07.
histogram.summary: The number of full server cards that were reset.

histogram.name: Autofill.ResetFullServerCards.NumberOfCardsReset.DryRun
histogram.summary: The number of full server cards that would have been reset.

histogram.name: Autofill.ResetFullServerCards.SyncServiceNotActiveOnInitialized
histogram.obsolete: Were added as part of Issue 851295; no longer necessary. Removed 2019/07.
histogram.summary: Records whether a sync service in a not active state is passed to Autofill OnSyncServiceInitialized.

histogram.name: Autofill.ResetFullServerCards.SyncServiceNullOnInitialized
histogram.obsolete: Were added as part of Issue 851295; no longer necessary. Removed 2019/07.
histogram.summary: Records whether a null sync service is passed to Autofill OnSyncServiceInitialized.

histogram.name: Autofill.ResetFullServerCards.SyncServiceStatusOnStateChanged
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 04/2019 (M76). No longer necessary since we don't mask data for USS Autofill Wallet anymore.
histogram.summary: Records the sync service state when OnStateChanged is called.

histogram.name: Autofill.SaveCardCardholderNamePrefilled
histogram.summary: If the cardholder name fix flow is shown when credit card upload is offered, records if the cardholder name textfield was prefilled with the name from the user's Google Account.

histogram.name: Autofill.SaveCardCardholderNameWasEdited
histogram.summary: If the cardholder name fix flow is shown during credit card upload and the user accepts upload, logs whether the final cardholder name was changed from its prefilled value or not.

histogram.name: Autofill.SaveCardReachedPersonalDataManager
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 05/2019. Was a temporary metric to gauge the impact of http://crbug.com/892299.
histogram.summary: Records if the PersonalDataManager was notified to attempt saving of a card.

histogram.name: Autofill.SaveCardRequestExpirationDateReason
histogram.summary: Records the reason expiration date was explicitly requested from the user during credit card save.

histogram.name: Autofill.SaveCardWithFirstAndLastNameComplete
histogram.summary: Records if a card was saved from a form that had split name fields.

histogram.name: Autofill.SaveCardWithFirstAndLastNameOffered
histogram.summary: Records if a card was offered to be saved from a form that had split name fields.

histogram.name: Autofill.SaveCreditCardPrompt
histogram.summary: The relative frequency with which users accept or deny the Autofill save credit card prompt.

histogram.name: Autofill.SaveCreditCardPromptOffer
histogram.summary: The relative frequency with which the Autofill save credit card prompt is shown or not. Logged when the flow is triggered and the prompt should be shown.

histogram.name: Autofill.SaveCreditCardPromptResult
histogram.summary: The relative frequency with which users accept or deny the Autofill save credit card prompt. Logged when users interacted with the prompt.

histogram.name: Autofill.ScanCreditCard.Completed
histogram.summary: Whether a credit card scan was completed or cancelled.

histogram.name: Autofill.ScanCreditCard.Duration
histogram.summary: How long a credit card took to scan.

histogram.name: Autofill.ScanCreditCardPrompt
histogram.summary: Usage of the "Scan card" control item.

histogram.name: Autofill.ServerCardLinkClicked
histogram.summary: Logged when the user clicks on the server credit card link in the settings page.

histogram.name: Autofill.ServerExperimentId
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 6/2011, replaced by Autofill.ServerExperimentId.Query.
histogram.summary: The experiment ID received in response to an Autofill server query.

histogram.name: Autofill.ServerExperimentId.Query
histogram.summary: The experiment ID received in response to an Autofill server query.

histogram.name: Autofill.ServerExperimentId.Upload
histogram.summary: The experiment ID received at the time of an Autofill upload.

histogram.name: AutoFill.ServerQueryResponse
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 3/2011, replaced by Autofill.ServerQueryResponse.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The usefulness of AutoFill server information.

histogram.name: Autofill.ServerQueryResponse
histogram.summary: The usefulness of Autofill server information.

histogram.name: Autofill.ServerResponseHasDataForForm
histogram.summary: Tracks whether Autofill server had at least some prediction data for a given form at query response time.

histogram.name: Autofill.StoredCreditCardCount
histogram.summary: The number of credit cards a user has stored, measured at Chrome profile launch time.

histogram.name: Autofill.StoredCreditCardDisusedCount
histogram.summary: The number of disused credit cards a user has stored, measured at Chrome profile launch time. This is only reported for users who have at least one stored credit card.

histogram.name: Autofill.StoredLocalCreditCardCount
histogram.summary: The number of local credit cards a user has stored, measured at Chrome profile launch time.

histogram.name: Autofill.StoredProfileCount
histogram.summary: The number of Autofill addresses a user has stored, measured at launch time.

histogram.name: Autofill.StoredProfileCountAtAutofillableFormSubmission
histogram.summary: The number of Autofill addresses a user has stored, measured when an autofillable form is submitted.

histogram.name: Autofill.StoredProfileDisusedCount
histogram.summary: The number of stored Autofill address profiles which have not been used in a sufficiently long time for autofill to consider them disused. Measured once per Chrome user profile launch.

histogram.name: Autofill.StoredServerCreditCardCount
histogram.summary: The number of server credit cards a user has stored, measured at Chrome profile launch time.

histogram.name: Autofill.StrikeDatabase.CreditCardSaveNotOfferedDueToMaxStrikes
histogram.summary: Records when credit card save is not offered (either at all on mobile or by simply not showing the bubble on desktop) due to the candidate card having too many strikes.

histogram.name: Autofill.StrikeDatabase.LocalCardMigrationNotOfferedDueToMaxStrikes
histogram.summary: Records when local card migration is not offered due to the candidate's LocalCardMigration strike count reaching maximum strikes.

histogram.name: Autofill.StrikeDatabase.NthStrikeAdded.CreditCardSave
histogram.summary: Records the number of "strikes" a given card has, when a user dismisses a prompt to save a new credit card. The strike count is incremented each time the user dismisses the prompt.

histogram.name: Autofill.StrikeDatabase.NthStrikeAdded.LocalCardMigration
histogram.summary: Records the number of "strikes" a user has, when a prompt for local card migration is dismissed. The strike count is incremented each time the user dismisses the prompt.

histogram.name: Autofill.StrikeDatabase.StrikeDatabaseInitFailed
histogram.summary: When a StrikeDatabase fails to initialize, records the number of consecutive failed initialization attempts.

histogram.name: Autofill.StrikeDatabase.StrikesPresentWhenLocalCardMigrationAccepted
histogram.summary: Records the number of "strikes" a user has when local card migration is accepted.

histogram.name: Autofill.StrikeDatabase.StrikesPresentWhenLocalCardSaved
histogram.summary: Records the number of "strikes" a given card had when a user opted to save the card to Chrome.

histogram.name: Autofill.StrikeDatabase.StrikesPresentWhenServerCardSaved
histogram.summary: Records the number of "strikes" a given card had when a user successfully uploaded the card to Google Payments.

histogram.name: Autofill.StrikeDatabase.StrikesPresentWhenStrikeExpired
histogram.summary: Records the number of "strikes" present in a StrikeDatabase entry when a strike expires.

histogram.name: Autofill.SubmittedCardState
histogram.summary: Metric to measure credit card state when form is submitted, specifically if it had a valid card number and/or expiration date.

histogram.name: Autofill.SubmittedServerCardExpirationStatus
histogram.summary: Metric to measure if a submitted card's expiration date matches the same server card's expiration date (unmasked or not). Cards are considered to be the same if they have the same card number (if unmasked) or if they have the same network and last four digits (if masked).

histogram.name: Autofill.SubmittedValuePatterns
histogram.summary: Metric to measure if a submitted form contains any recognizable value patterns (e.g., Unified Payment Interface Virtual Payment address (UPI VPA)). Reported for every submitted non-empty visible field observed by Autofill.

histogram.name: Autofill.SuggestionAccepted.OffTheRecord
histogram.summary: Records acceptance of autofill suggestions in regular and off-the-record modes.

histogram.name: Autofill.SuggestionAcceptedIndex
histogram.summary: The index of the accepted Autofill suggestion in the popup. Due to crbug.com/966411, the iOS data is incorrect.

histogram.name: Autofill.SuggestionAcceptedIndex.Autocomplete
histogram.summary: The index of the accepted Autocomplete suggestion in the popup. Due to crbug.com/966411, the iOS data is incorrect.

histogram.name: Autofill.SuggestionShown.OffTheRecord
histogram.summary: Records showing of autofill suggestions in regular and off-the-record modes.

histogram.name: Autofill.Timing.DetermineHeuristicTypes
histogram.summary: Number of milliseconds passed between the start and end of FormStructure::DetermineHeuristicTypes().

histogram.name: Autofill.Timing.ParseForm
histogram.summary: Number of milliseconds passed between the start and end of parsing a single form.

histogram.name: Autofill.Unknown.BackoffDelay
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 01/2019, as it never occurs.
histogram.summary: The delay of a network request for which the download manager seems to have lost the request type due to exponential backoff. This should never be logged.

histogram.name: Autofill.Unknown.FailingPayloadSize
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 01/2019, as it never occurs.
histogram.summary: The number of bytes that were sent in a request for which the download manager seems to have lost the request type. This should never be logged.

histogram.name: Autofill.Unknown.HttpResponseCode
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 8/2018, replaced by Autofill.Unknown.HttpResponseOrErrorCode.
histogram.summary: The HTTP response code returned on a request for which the download manager seems to have lost the request type. This should never be logged.

histogram.name: Autofill.Unknown.HttpResponseOrErrorCode
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 01/2019, as it never occurs.
histogram.summary: The http response code or net error code returned on a request for which the download manager seems to have lost the request type. This should never be logged.

histogram.name: Autofill.Unknown.RequestDuration
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 01/2019, as it never occurs.
histogram.summary: The duration of a network request for which the download manager seems to have lost the request type. This should never be logged.

histogram.name: Autofill.UnmaskPrompt.Duration
histogram.summary: Duration of the interaction with the UnmaskPrompt since it was shown until it was closed. This prompt is triggered when a user selects a masked card in an autofill dropdown.

histogram.name: Autofill.UnmaskPrompt.Events
histogram.summary: Events tracking the usage of the unmasking prompt. This prompt is triggered when a user selects a masked card in a autofill dropdown.

histogram.name: Autofill.UnmaskPrompt.GetRealPanDuration
histogram.summary: Duration of the GetRealPan API call. This API call is triggered by the unmasking prompt when the user types information to verify their ownership of the card being fetched.

histogram.name: Autofill.UnmaskPrompt.GetRealPanResult
histogram.summary: Tracks the result of the GetRealPan API call. This API call is triggered by the unmasking prompt when the user types information to verify their ownership of the card being fetched.

histogram.name: Autofill.UnmaskPrompt.TimeBeforeAbandonUnmasking
histogram.summary: Time the user waited before abandoning an unmasking operation. In such a scenario, the user is cancelling a pending unmasking operation (possibly because it is taking too long). The time measured here corresponds to the time between the user clicking the "Verify" button and then clicking "Cancel".

histogram.name: Autofill.UnmaskPrompt.UnmaskingDuration
histogram.summary: Duration between the user clicking the Verify button within the unmasking prompt until we get a response back from the server. This prompt is triggered when a user selects a masked card in an autofill dropdown.

histogram.name: Autofill.UnownedFieldsWereFiltered
histogram.summary: Records incidents where an implicit (formless) form has elements filtered out of it. This happens to unowned fields (directly embedded in the page with no enclosing <form> tag) without autocomplete attributes if formless forms are restricted to "checkout" pages. Logged when parsing forms/fields.

histogram.name: Autofill.Upload.BackoffDelay
histogram.summary: The delay of a network request for an upload due to exponential backoff.

histogram.name: Autofill.Upload.FailingPayloadSize
histogram.summary: The number of bytes that were sent in an upload that subsequently failed. This is to help determine if there are certain upload sizes which are prone to failure.

histogram.name: Autofill.Upload.HttpResponseCode
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 8/2018, replaced by Autofill.Upload.HttpResponseOrErrorCode.
histogram.summary: The HTTP response code returned on an upload.

histogram.name: Autofill.Upload.HttpResponseOrErrorCode
histogram.summary: The http response code or net error code returned on an upload.

histogram.name: Autofill.Upload.MetadataConfigIsValid
histogram.summary: Tracks whether or not the configuration fo metadata upload encoding is valid or invalid. Note that if the configuration is invalid, the client will fall back to a default configuration that sends 4 bits per byte (i.e., either the even or odd bits encoding).

histogram.name: Autofill.Upload.MetadataEncodingType
histogram.summary: The metadata encoding scheme used by the client to encode rich uploads.

histogram.name: Autofill.Upload.RequestDuration
histogram.summary: The duration of a network request for an upload.

histogram.name: Autofill.UploadAcceptedCardOrigin
histogram.summary: Measures if a card for which upload was accepted is already stored as a local card on the device or if it has not yet been seen.

histogram.name: Autofill.UploadEvent
histogram.summary: Whether or not an upload was sent after having been triggered by a form submission or proxy-form-submission.

histogram.name: Autofill.UploadOfferedCardOrigin
histogram.summary: Measures if a card for which upload was offered is already stored as a local on the device or if it has not yet been seen.

histogram.name: Autofill.UsedCachedServerCard
histogram.summary: Records the number of times that the cache for unmasked server cards has been accessed for a given card. For example, if the cache is being accessed and it has already been accessed for the card twice before, then "3" is recorded.

histogram.name: Autofill.UserHappiness
histogram.summary: Measures the frequency of various events in the Autofill user interaction flow. By comparing frequencies, we can compute several interesting "user happiness" metrics.

histogram.name: Autofill.WalletAddressConversionType
histogram.summary: Tracks how the different wallet addresses are converted to local autofill profiles.

histogram.name: Autofill.WalletAddresses
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 12/2018, replaced by Autofill.WalletAddresses2.
histogram.summary: The number of Wallet addresses that were added to Chrome or removed from Chrome via Sync. Recorded when receiving an AUTOFILL_WALLET_DATA update from the Sync server. Recorded only if both the user had previously non-empty data set locally and the new data set is also non-empty (to rule out initial sync and wiping out data). This metric has a flaw that the Directory implementation (unlike the USS implementation) records the metric also on every startup and the resulting histograms are hard to compare.

histogram.name: Autofill.WalletAddresses2
histogram.summary: The number of Wallet addresses that were added to Chrome or removed from Chrome via Sync. Recorded when receiving an AUTOFILL_WALLET_DATA update from the Sync server. Recorded only if both the user had previously non-empty data set locally and the new data set is also non-empty (to rule out initial sync and wiping out data).

histogram.name: Autofill.WalletAddressesAdded
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 11/2018, replaced by Autofill.WalletAddresses.Added.
histogram.summary: The number of Wallet addresses that were added to Chrome via Sync. Recorded when receiving an AUTOFILL_WALLET_DATA update from the Sync server.
histogram.details: Can probably be removed around Q1 2019, after project Dice has rolled out.

histogram.name: Autofill.WalletAddressesAddedOrRemoved
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 11/2018, replaced by Autofill.WalletAddresses.AddedOrRemoved.
histogram.summary: The total number of Wallet addresses that were added to or removed from Chrome via Sync. Recorded when receiving an AUTOFILL_WALLET_DATA update from the Sync server.
histogram.details: Can probably be removed around Q1 2019, after project Dice has rolled out.

histogram.name: Autofill.WalletAddressesRemoved
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 11/2018, replaced by Autofill.WalletAddresses.Removed.
histogram.summary: The number of Wallet addresses that were removed from Chrome via Sync. Recorded when receiving an AUTOFILL_WALLET_DATA update from the Sync server.
histogram.details: Can probably be removed around Q1 2019, after project Dice has rolled out.

histogram.name: Autofill.WalletCards
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 12/2018, replaced by Autofill.WalletCards2.
histogram.summary: The number of Wallet credit cards that were added to Chrome or removed from Chrome via Sync. Recorded when receiving an AUTOFILL_WALLET_DATA update from the Sync server. Recorded only if both the user had previously non-empty data set locally and the new data set is also non-empty (to rule out initial sync and wiping out data). This metric has a flaw that the Directory implementation (unlike the USS implementation) records the metric also on every startup and the resulting histograms are hard to compare.

histogram.name: Autofill.WalletCards2
histogram.summary: The number of Wallet credit cards that were added to Chrome or removed from Chrome via Sync. Recorded when receiving an AUTOFILL_WALLET_DATA update from the Sync server. Recorded only if both the user had previously non-empty data set locally and the new data set is also non-empty (to rule out initial sync and wiping out data).

histogram.name: Autofill.WalletCardsAdded
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 11/2018, replaced by Autofill.WalletCards.Added.
histogram.summary: The number of Wallet credit cards that were added to Chrome via Sync. Recorded when receiving an AUTOFILL_WALLET_DATA update from the Sync server.
histogram.details: Can probably be removed around Q1 2019, after project Dice has rolled out.

histogram.name: Autofill.WalletCardsAddedOrRemoved
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 11/2018, replaced by Autofill.WalletCards.AddedOrRemoved.
histogram.summary: The total number of Wallet credit cards that were added to or removed from Chrome via Sync. Recorded when receiving an AUTOFILL_WALLET_DATA update from the Sync server.
histogram.details: Can probably be removed around Q1 2019, after project Dice has rolled out.

histogram.name: Autofill.WalletCardsRemoved
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 11/2018, replaced by Autofill.WalletCards.Removed.
histogram.summary: The number of Wallet credit cards that were removed from Chrome via Sync. Recorded when receiving an AUTOFILL_WALLET_DATA update from the Sync server.
histogram.details: Can probably be removed around Q1 2019, after project Dice has rolled out.

histogram.name: Autofill.WalletUseDate.Address
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 04/2019 because most measurements were in the last bucket, replaced by Autofill.WalletUseDateInMinutes.Address.
histogram.summary: The time since last use date of a wallet address, as known by sync. Recorded for each wallet address on startup.

histogram.name: Autofill.WalletUseDate.Card
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 04/2019 because most measurements were in the last bucket, replaced by Autofill.WalletUseDateInMinutes.Card.
histogram.summary: The time since last use date of a wallet card, as known by sync. Recorded for each wallet card on startup.

histogram.name: Autofill.WalletUseDateInMinutes.Address
histogram.summary: The time since last use date of a wallet address, as known by sync. Recorded for each wallet address on startup.

histogram.name: Autofill.WalletUseDateInMinutes.Card
histogram.summary: The time since last use date of a wallet card, as known by sync. Recorded for each wallet card on startup.

histogram.name: Autofill.WebView.AutofillSession
histogram.summary: Records the state of an autofill session.

histogram.name: Autofill.WebView.CreatedByActivityContext
histogram.summary: Whether the autofill is created by activity context.

histogram.name: Autofill.WebView.Enabled
histogram.summary: Whether the autofill service is enabled in Android platform.

histogram.name: Autofill.WebView.SubmissionSource
histogram.summary: Records the source of form submission.

histogram.name: Autofill.WebView.SuggestionTime
histogram.summary: The time taken to display suggestion.

histogram.name: Autofill.WebView.UserChangedAutofilledField
histogram.summary: Whether the user changed autofilled field.

histogram.name: AutogeneratedTheme.ColorGenerationTime
histogram.summary: Time that it takes to calculate all the theme properties for autogenerated themes in ms.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.AdapterDecisionAtUserChange.AlsDelta
histogram.summary: When user changes brightness manually, we will ask the model whether it also thinks brightness should be changed. We also log the als-delta from the previous log-lux (when brightness was last changed) to the current log-lux, which includes both the sign of the als-delta and its absolute value. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.AdapterDecisionAtUserChange.AlsStd
histogram.summary: When user changes brightness manually, we will ask the model whether it also thinks brightness should be changed. We also log the als-stddev in its averaging period. We log it separately for the case when the associated als-delta is positive and negative. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.AdapterDecisionAtUserChange.BrightnessChange.Cause
histogram.summary: When user changes brightness manually, we will ask the model whether it also thinks brightness should be changed. This enum states the reason why the model also thinks brightness should be changed. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.AdapterDecisionAtUserChange.ModelIteration
histogram.summary: When user changes brightness manually, we will ask the model whether it also thinks brightness should be changed. We also log the number of model iterations if we've set to use personal curve only. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.AdapterDecisionAtUserChange.NoBrightnessChange.Cause
histogram.summary: When user changes brightness manually, we will ask the model whether it also thinks brightness should be changed. This enum states the reason why the model doesn't think brightness should be changed. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.AdapterDecisionAtUserChange.Unknown.AlsStd
histogram.summary: When user changes brightness manually, we will ask the model whether it also thinks brightness should be changed. We also log the als-stddev in its averaging period. This metric records the als-stddev when the associated als-delta is unknown. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.AlsReaderStatus
histogram.summary: Whether the ALS is enabled or the error otherwise. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.BrightnessChange.Cause
histogram.summary: Reason for the model to change brightness. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.BrightnessChange.ElapsedTime
histogram.summary: The time between two consecutive auto brightness changes. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.BrightnessChange.ModelIteration
histogram.summary: Number of model iterations when brightness is changed by a personal curve. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.BrightnessMonitorStatus
histogram.summary: Whether the brightness monitor is enabled or the error otherwise. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.DailyUserAdjustment.Atlas
histogram.summary: Number of times that a user has made brightness adjustments on an Atlas device with an ambient sensor that is supported by the auto screen brightness model. Reported daily. The count is accumulated through the day, spanning reboots, and sent once the system clock indicates that a full day or more has passed since the last report. If the system is suspended or off for more than a day, the current count will be reported immediately the next time the system boots, but the skipped days will not be reported. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.DailyUserAdjustment.Eve
histogram.summary: Number of times that a user has made brightness adjustments on an Eve device with an ambient sensor that is supported by the auto screen brightness model. Reported daily. The count is accumulated through the day, spanning reboots, and sent once the system clock indicates that a full day or more has passed since the last report. If the system is suspended or off for more than a day, the current count will be reported immediately the next time the system boots, but the skipped days will not be reported. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.DailyUserAdjustment.Kohaku
histogram.summary: Number of times that a user has made brightness adjustments on a Kohaku device with an ambient sensor that is supported by the auto screen brightness model. Reported daily. The count is accumulated through the day, spanning reboots, and sent once the system clock indicates that a full day or more has passed since the last report. If the system is suspended or off for more than a day, the current count will be reported immediately the next time the system boots, but the skipped days will not be reported. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.DailyUserAdjustment.NoAls
histogram.summary: Number of times that a user has made brightness adjustments on a device without an ambient sensor. Reported daily. The count is accumulated through the day, spanning reboots, and sent once the system clock indicates that a full day or more has passed since the last report. If the system is suspended or off for more than a day, the current count will be reported immediately the next time the system boots, but the skipped days will not be reported. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.DailyUserAdjustment.Nocturne
histogram.summary: Number of times that a user has made brightness adjustments on a Nocturne device with an ambient sensor that is supported by the auto screen brightness model. Reported daily. The count is accumulated through the day, spanning reboots, and sent once the system clock indicates that a full day or more has passed since the last report. If the system is suspended or off for more than a day, the current count will be reported immediately the next time the system boots, but the skipped days will not be reported. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.DailyUserAdjustment.SupportedAls
histogram.summary: Number of times that a user has made brightness adjustments on a device with an ambient sensor that is supported by the auto screen brightness model. Reported daily. The count is accumulated through the day, spanning reboots, and sent once the system clock indicates that a full day or more has passed since the last report. If the system is suspended or off for more than a day, the current count will be reported immediately the next time the system boots, but the skipped days will not be reported. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.DailyUserAdjustment.UnsupportedAls
histogram.summary: Number of times that a user has made brightness adjustments on a device with an ambient sensor that is not supported by the auto screen brightness model. Reported daily. The count is accumulated through the day, spanning reboots, and sent once the system clock indicates that a full day or more has passed since the last report. If the system is suspended or off for more than a day, the current count will be reported immediately the next time the system boots, but the skipped days will not be reported. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.DataError
histogram.summary: Type of error seen when handling data related to automatic screen brightness adjustments. Only reported on Chrome OS.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.ElapsedTimeBetweenModelAndUserAdjustments
histogram.summary: The elapsed time from a model brightness adjustment to the next user manual brightness change. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.GlobalCurveResetOnInitialization
histogram.summary: Whether the global curve is reset at initialization.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.MissingAlsWhenBrightnessChanged
histogram.summary: Whether there was no recorded ALS reading when brightness was changed.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.MissingPriorUserBrightnessRequest
histogram.summary: Under the current implementation of brightness monitor, user brightness request should be received before brightness change signal. Adapter operates under this assumption. This metric logs an error if a brightness change signal is received without a prior brightness request signal. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.ModelIterationCountAtInitialization
histogram.summary: Personal model iteration count when modeller is initialized. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.ModelLoadingStatus
histogram.summary: Status of the model loading from disk. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.ModelTraining.BrightnessChange
histogram.summary: Whether a new brightness exceeds the preset bound that defines reasonble range of change. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.ModelTraining.BrightnessOutlier
histogram.summary: Whether a training data is an outlier and should be discarded by the model during training. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.ModelTraining.Inaccuracy.NoUpdate
histogram.summary: When training data comes in, the model may or may not be updated. We measure the error of the model as compared with the target value from training data. This metric measures the error when model is not updated. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.ModelTraining.Inaccuracy.Update
histogram.summary: When training data comes in, the model may or may not be updated. We measure the error of the model as compared with the target value from training data. This metric measures the error when model is updated. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.ModelTraining.ModelUserConsistent
histogram.summary: Whether user brightness change is consistent with the model so that model is not updated.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.NewCurveSaved.Duration
histogram.summary: The time elapsed between training start and a new curve saved to disk. Only reported if a new curve was created and saved successfully to disk. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.NewCurveSaved.Success
histogram.summary: Whether a new curve was successfully saved to disk. Only reported if a new curve was created during the training process. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.Opposite.UserModelBrightnessAdjustments
histogram.summary: When user changes brightness manually, if the previous change was caused by the model, then we log bucketized absolute brightness changes of the two adjustments. This metric records changes if they are in the opposite directions. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.ParameterError
histogram.summary: When there is an error in the automatic screen brightness parameters, this records which kind of parameter is invalid. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.PersonalCurveValid
histogram.summary: Whether the user's personal brightness curve is valid. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.Same.UserModelBrightnessAdjustments
histogram.summary: When user changes brightness manually, if the previous change was caused by the model, then we log bucketized absolute brightness changes of the two adjustments. This metric records changes if they are in the same direction. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.TrainingCompleteDuration.NewCurve
histogram.summary: The time elapsed to complete training after which a new curve was generated. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.TrainingCompleteDuration.NoNewCurve
histogram.summary: The time elapsed to complete training after which no new curve was generated. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.UserAdjustment
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 11/2018. It has been replaced by DailyUserAdjustment.
histogram.summary: Type of user manual screen brightness adjustment.

histogram.name: AutoScreenBrightness.UserAdjustmentEffect
histogram.summary: How user manual screen brightness adjustment changes the model. Chrome OS only.

histogram.name: Availability.Prober.CacheEntryAge
histogram.summary: How old a cached probe result was when it was used, in hours.

histogram.name: Availability.Prober.DidSucceed
histogram.summary: Records the completion status of a probe when it completes each attempt.

histogram.name: Availability.Prober.DidSucceed.AfterReportedFailure
histogram.summary: Records the completion status of a probe retry after an externally reported failure.

histogram.name: Availability.Prober.FinalState
histogram.summary: Records the end state of a probe just before it goes inactive. This happens when the prober succeeds, fails and has no more retries, or the delegate stops probing.

histogram.name: Availability.Prober.NetError
histogram.summary: Records the net error of a completed or timed out probe. Note that if a HTTP response does not occur within the probe's TTL, when a sample will also be added to the ERR_TIMED_OUT bucket.

histogram.name: Availability.Prober.NumAttemptsBeforeSuccess
histogram.summary: Records the number of attempts that are made to achieve a successful probe result. Only recorded on success. This metric is 1-based so if a probe succeeds the first time, a sample of 1 will be recorded.

histogram.name: Availability.Prober.ResponseCode
histogram.summary: Records the HTTP response code of a completed probe, when a HTTP response is received.

histogram.name: Availability.Prober.TimeUntilFailure
histogram.summary: Records the amount of time spent working on a single probe attempt to get to a failed state. Recorded every time a probe fails.

histogram.name: Availability.Prober.TimeUntilFailure2
histogram.summary: Records the amount of time spent working on a single probe attempt to get to a failed state. Recorded every time a probe fails.

histogram.name: Availability.Prober.TimeUntilSuccess
histogram.summary: Records the amount of time spent working on a single probe attempt to get to a successful state. Recorded every time a probe succeeds.

histogram.name: Availability.Prober.TimeUntilSuccess2
histogram.summary: Records the amount of time spent working on a single probe attempt to get to a successful state. Recorded every time a probe succeeds.

histogram.name: BackForwardCache.AllSites.EvictedAfterDocumentRestoredReason
histogram.summary:
Records the reason why the page is evicted from the back-forward cache but after the document is restored. This happens when there is a race between eviction and restoring. This also records when the page is restored to calculate how often this eviction happens.
This is recorded for all websites regardless whether back-forward cache is enabled or not.

histogram.name: BackForwardCache.AllSites.HistoryNavigationOutcome
histogram.summary:
When navigating back to a page in the session history, this records whether the page was restored from the BackForwardCache or not.
This is recorded for all websites regardless whether back-forward cache is enabled or not.

histogram.name: BackForwardCache.AllSites.HistoryNavigationOutcome.BlocklistedFeature
histogram.summary:
When navigating back to a page in the session history, this records what blocklisted features were used when the page was loaded and prevented this page from being stored in the back-forward cache. Multiple blocklisted features can be recorded for the same page.
This is recorded for all websites regardless whether back-forward cache is enabled or not.

histogram.name: BackForwardCache.AllSites.HistoryNavigationOutcome.BrowsingInstanceNotSwappedReason
histogram.summary:
When navigating back to a page in the session history and the BackForwardCache wasn't used because of the related browsing instances, this records the reason why the browsing instance wasn't swapped.
This is recorded for all websites regardless whether back-forward cache is enabled or not.

histogram.name: BackForwardCache.AllSites.HistoryNavigationOutcome.DisabledForRenderFrameHostReason
histogram.summary:
For history navigations, records the reasons passed to BackForwardCache::DisableForRenderFrameHost calls. These reasons are specified as strings, so this histograms contains their hashes computed as base::HashMetricName.
The metric is recorded when a history navigation commits and emits a sample for each reason. Compare this with BackForwardCache.HistoryNavigationOutcome, which contains the total number of navigations which where not cached
This is recorded for all websites regardless whether back-forward cache is enabled or not.

histogram.name: BackForwardCache.AllSites.HistoryNavigationOutcome.NotRestoredReason
histogram.summary:
When navigating back to a page in the session history, record why it wasn't restored from the cache. The page might not have entered the back-forward in the first place based on the features used, or it might have been evicted while being in the cache.
This is recorded for all websites regardless whether back-forward cache is enabled or not.

histogram.name: BackForwardCache.EvictedAfterDocumentRestoredReason
histogram.summary:
Records the reason why the page is evicted from the back-forward cache but after the document is restored. This happens when there is a race between eviction and restoring. This also records when the page is restored to calculate how often this eviction happens.
This is recorded when back-forward cache is enabled for this navigation (back-forward cache feature is enabled and the domain is in the scope of the experiment).

histogram.name: BackForwardCache.Eviction.TimeUntilProcessKilled
histogram.summary: Records the time duration between the last time when the entry goes to the back-forward cache and the time when the process is killed.

histogram.name: BackForwardCache.HistoryNavigationOutcome
histogram.summary:
When navigating back to a page in the session history, this records whether the page was restored from the BackForwardCache or not.
This is recorded when back-forward cache is enabled for this navigation (back-forward cache feature is enabled and the domain is in the scope of the experiment).

histogram.name: BackForwardCache.HistoryNavigationOutcome.BlocklistedFeature
histogram.summary:
When navigating back to a page in the session history, this records what blocklisted features were used when the page was loaded and prevented this page from being stored in the back-forward cache. Multiple blocklisted features can be recorded for the same page.
This is recorded when back-forward cache is enabled for this navigation (back-forward cache feature is enabled and the domain is in the scope of the experiment).

histogram.name: BackForwardCache.HistoryNavigationOutcome.BrowsingInstanceNotSwappedReason
histogram.summary:
When navigating back to a page in the session history and the BackForwardCache wasn't used because of the related browsing instances, this records the reason why the browsing instance wasn't swapped.
This is recorded when back-forward cache is enabled for this navigation (back-forward cache feature is enabled and the domain is in the scope of the experiment).

histogram.name: BackForwardCache.HistoryNavigationOutcome.DisabledForRenderFrameHostReason
histogram.summary:
For history navigations, records the reasons passed to BackForwardCache::DisableForRenderFrameHost calls. These reasons are specified as strings, so this histograms contains their hashes computed as base::HashMetricName.
The metric is recorded when a history navigation commits and emits a sample for each reason. Compare this with BackForwardCache.HistoryNavigationOutcome, which contains the total number of navigations which where not cached
This is recorded when back-forward cache is enabled for this navigation (back-forward cache feature is enabled and the domain is in the scope of the experiment).

histogram.name: BackForwardCache.HistoryNavigationOutcome.NotRestoredReason
histogram.summary:
When navigating back to a page in the session history, record why it wasn't restored from the cache. The page might not have entered the back-forward in the first place based on the features used, or it might have been evicted while being in the cache.
This is recorded when back-forward cache is enabled for this navigation (back-forward cache feature is enabled and the domain is in the scope of the experiment).

histogram.name: BackForwardCache.MainFrameHasPageshowListenersOnRestore
histogram.summary: Records whether a page restored from the back-forward cache has pageshow handlers installed in its main frame. Recorded when the renderer resumes page when restoring it from the back-forward cache.

histogram.name: BackForwardCache.ReloadsAfterHistoryNavigation
histogram.summary:
Records the number of reloads after a history navigation both for the cases when page is served and not served from back-forward cache.
This is recorded when navigation is committed, only for the domains which are in the scope of back-forward cache experiment and they are of same NavigationEntry (e.g. back and reload in different tab do not count).

histogram.name: BackForwardCache.ReloadsAndHistoryNavigations
histogram.summary:
Records the total number of history navigations along with the number of reloads if the previous navigation was a history one.
This is recorded when navigation is committed, only for the domains which are in the scope of back-forward cache experiment.

histogram.name: BackForwardCache.Restore.NavigationToFirstPaint
histogram.summary:
Duration between start of navigation restoring a document from back-forward cache and the first paint.
This is recorded when a history navigation is served from back-forward cache but in a foreground tab.

histogram.name: BackgroundFetch.EventDispatchFailure.Dispatch
histogram.summary: Records the Service Worker status code that caused a Background Fetch API event to fail dispatching whilst trying to dispatch an event on the active Service Worker.

histogram.name: BackgroundFetch.EventDispatchFailure.FindWorker
histogram.summary: Records the Service Worker status code that caused a Background Fetch API event to fail dispatching whilst trying to find the Service Worker.

histogram.name: BackgroundFetch.EventDispatchFailure.StartWorker
histogram.summary: Records the Service Worker status code that caused a Background Fetch API event to fail dispatching whilst trying to start the active Service Worker.

histogram.name: BackgroundFetch.EventDispatchResult
histogram.summary: Records the result of dispatching one of the Background Fetch API events to the Service Worker associated with the event.

histogram.name: BackgroundFetch.HasDuplicateRequests
histogram.summary: Records whether the fetch requests contains any duplicate URLs. This is called after the provided requests are verified, but before the fetch is started.

histogram.name: BackgroundFetch.HasRequestsWithBody
histogram.summary: Records whether the background fetch contains any requests with a body. Called before the fetch is started.

histogram.name: BackgroundFetch.IncompleteFetchesOnStartup
histogram.summary: Records the number of Background Fetch registrations found on browser start-up. This is called after the initialization database task runs successfully.

histogram.name: BackgroundFetch.Manager
histogram.summary: Records the duration of the mojo call for the exposed BackgroundFetchManager APIs.

histogram.name: BackgroundFetch.MatchCalledFromDocumentScope
histogram.summary: Records whether match() or matchAll() was called from Document Scope. Called when match() or matchAll() is invoked in the renderer process.

histogram.name: BackgroundFetch.MatchCalledWhenFetchIsIncomplete
histogram.summary: Records whether match() or matchAll() was called when the background fetch was incomplete. Called when match() or matchAll() is invoked in the renderer process.

histogram.name: BackgroundFetch.PercentOfRequestsForWhichUpdatesAreSent
histogram.summary: Records the percent of requests for which updates are received in the renderer process from the browser process. Called when the completion event for a background fetch has finished processing.

histogram.name: BackgroundFetch.RegistrationCreatedError
histogram.obsolete: Removed 10/2018 since DB tasks record this more accurately.
histogram.summary: Records the error after the Background Fetch Data Manager creates a registration.

histogram.name: BackgroundFetch.RegistrationDeletedError
histogram.obsolete: Removed 10/2018 since DB tasks record this more accurately.
histogram.summary: Records the error after the Background Fetch Data Manager deletes a registration.

histogram.name: BackgroundFetch.SchedulerFinishedError
histogram.obsolete: Removed 10/2018 since DB tasks record this more accurately.
histogram.summary: Records the error after the Background Fetch Data Manager marks a registration for deletion. This is called when the Scheduler Controller is finished.

histogram.name: BackgroundFetch.Storage
histogram.summary: Records the storage error when running a database task.

histogram.name: BackgroundMode.BackgroundApplicationsCount
histogram.summary: The number of background applications (extensions) for a profile that were loaded after the profile was registered with the BackgroundModeManager.

histogram.name: BackgroundMode.BackgroundModeEnabledPrefChanged
histogram.summary: Logged if BackgroundModeManager is running and listening for pref changes, and the pref does indeed change. The new value is logged.

histogram.name: BackgroundMode.MenuItemClick
histogram.summary: Logged when an item in the system tray icon menu is clicked.

histogram.name: BackgroundMode.OnStartup.AutoLaunchState
histogram.summary: Logged during BackgroundModeManager's initialization. Indicates the AutoLaunchState the current browser process was launched in.

histogram.name: BackgroundMode.OnStartup.IsBackgroundModePrefEnabled
histogram.summary: Logged during BackgroundModeManager's initialization. Indicates the state of the kBackgroundModeEnabled pref on startup.

histogram.name: BackgroundMode.TimeBeforeOptimizedRestart
histogram.summary: The duration of a session before the browser got restarted in the background by the BackgroundOptimizer to purge the memory.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Event.BatchSize
histogram.summary: Records the number of `sync` events which were fired in a batch. A batch is defined as the set of `sync` events dispatched at the same time by the BackgroundSyncManager. One-shots usually run individually (a batch of one), unless the device was offline and multiple are waiting for the device to go back online.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Event.FromWakeupTask
histogram.summary: Records whether the fired `sync` events upon completion orginated from a background wakeup task or the foreground.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Event.OneShotResult
histogram.obsolete: As of 10/2015 this has been replaced by BackgroundSync.Event.OneShotResultPattern.
histogram.summary: Records whether a one-shot sync event succeeded or failed.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Event.OneShotResultPattern
histogram.summary: Records whether a `sync` event succeeded or failed and whether the sync event finished in the foreground or background.
histogram.details: A `sync` event finished in the foreground if the associated Service Worker Registration has a client at the time that the event finished.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Event.OneShotStartedInForeground
histogram.summary: Records whether a one-shot sync started firing in the foreground or background. Called shortly before the event is fired.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Event.PeriodicResult
histogram.obsolete: As of 10/2015 this has been replaced by BackgroundSync.Event.PeriodicResultPattern.
histogram.summary: Records whether a periodic sync event succeeded or failed.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Event.PeriodicResultPattern
histogram.summary: Records whether a periodic sync event succeeded or failed and whether the sync event finished in the foreground or background.
histogram.details: A sync event finished in the foreground if the associated Service Worker Registration has a client at the time that the event finished.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Event.PeriodicStartedInForeground
histogram.summary: Records whether a periodic sync event started firing in the foreground or background. Called shortly before the event is fired.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Event.Time
histogram.summary: Time taken to execute a batch of one-shot sync events. A batch is defined as the set of sync events dispatched at the same time by the BackgroundSyncManager. One-shots usually run individually (a batch of one), unless the device was offline and multiple are waiting for the device to go back online.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.LaunchTask.CancelSuccess
histogram.summary: Records the result of attempting to cancel a future browser launch using the GCM Network Manager on Android.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.LaunchTask.PlayServicesAvailable
histogram.summary: Records whether Google Play Services is available to the Background Sync system on Android, for scheduling future sync events when the browser is not running. This will be logged at most once per run.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.LaunchTask.ScheduleSuccess
histogram.summary: Records the result of attempting to schedule a future browser launch using the GCM Network Manager on Android.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.NetworkObserver.HasPermission
histogram.summary: Records whether the browser has sufficient permissions to create a BackgroundSyncNetworkObserver object on Android, at the point when it tries to create it. This should never be false, but is being tracked in order to help resolve crbug.com/545473.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.OptionConditionsChanged
histogram.summary: Whether the Background Sync dispatch conditions changed between scheduling the operation and dispatching the event. This is collected right before the event is dispatched.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Periodic.Wakeup.DelayTime
histogram.summary: Records time elapsed since the soonest expected wake-up time. Recorded whenever a periodic Background Sync Chrome wake-up task is called after loading the native parts of the browser.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Registration.OneShot
histogram.summary: Records the result of attempting to register a one-shot sync.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Registration.OneShot.CouldFire
histogram.summary: Records the result of attempting to register a one-shot sync in a situation where the sync could fire immediately.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Registration.OneShot.EventSucceededAtCompletion
histogram.summary: Records whether the dispatched sync event succeeded at the completion of the one-shot Background Sync registration.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Registration.OneShot.IsDuplicate
histogram.summary: Records whether a one-shot sync registration exactly duplicates an existing registered sync.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Registration.OneShot.NumAttemptsForSuccessfulEvent
histogram.summary: Records the number of times a sync event was dispatched for a one-shot Background Sync registration before it succeeded.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Registration.Periodic
histogram.summary: Records the result of attempting to register a periodic sync. This is called at each successful registration.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Registration.Periodic.IsDuplicate
histogram.summary: Records whether a periodic sync registration duplicates the tag of an existing registered sync. Recorded upon each successful periodic Background Sync registration.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Registration.Periodic.MinInterval
histogram.summary: Records the minInterval requested, in seconds, when a periodic sync is successfully registered.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Unregistration.OneShot
histogram.summary: Records the result of attempting to unregister a one-shot sync.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Unregistration.Periodic
histogram.summary: Records the result of attempting to unregister a periodic sync.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.Wakeup.DelayTime
histogram.summary: If BackgroundSync uses BackgroundTaskScheduler to wake up Chrome, this records the time elapsed since the soonest expected wake-up time. Recorded whenever a BackgroundSync background task is called after loading the native parts of the browser.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.WakeupTaskFiredEvents.OneShot
histogram.summary: If Chrome is woken up by the one shot Background Sync Background task, this records whether any sync events were fired as a result. Recorded whenever Chrome is woken up to process one-shot Background Sync registrations on Android.

histogram.name: BackgroundSync.WakeupTaskFiredEvents.Periodic
histogram.summary: If Chrome is woken up by the periodic Background Sync Background task,this records whether any periodicsync events were fired as a result. Recorded whenever Chrome is woken up to process periodic Background Sync registrations on Android.

histogram.name: BackgroundTracing.SamplingProfilerUnwindResult
histogram.summary: Records the reason for unwind failues in sampling profiler.

histogram.name: Badging.AppBadgeUpdate.Mac.Result
histogram.summary: The result from updating the app badge on macOS.

histogram.name: Badging.AppBadgeUpdate.Win.Result
histogram.summary: The result from updating the app badge on Windows.

histogram.name: BatteryStatus.NumberBatteriesLinux
histogram.summary: Number of batteries reported by the UPower service on Linux at the start of the Battery Status API.

histogram.name: BatteryStatus.NumberBatteriesMac
histogram.summary: Number of internal batteries reported by MacOS at the start of the Battery Status API.

histogram.name: BatteryStatus.NumberBatteriesWin
histogram.summary: Number of batteries reported by Windows at the start of the Battery Status API.

histogram.name: BatteryStatus.StartAndroid
histogram.summary: Whether the Battery Status API was successfully started up on Android.

histogram.name: Blacklist.Blocked
histogram.obsolete: The blacklist is deprecated in favor of third-party DLL blocking.
histogram.summary: Records the name hashes of all the dlls that are blocked from the browser process.

histogram.name: Blacklist.PatchedInRenderer
histogram.summary: Counts the number of times a renderer process is started with the browser blacklist patch. This should never be hit.

histogram.name: Blacklist.RetryAttempts.Success
histogram.obsolete: Replaced by ChromeElf.Beacon.RetryAttemptsBeforeSuccess after the old blacklist was deprecated.
histogram.summary: Records the number of attempts needed before the blacklist is properly set up. This is logged immediately after a successful setup.

histogram.name: Blacklist.Setup
histogram.obsolete: Replaced by ChromeElf.Beacon.SetupStatus after the old blacklist was deprecated.
histogram.summary: Records the successes and failures when running the browser blacklist setup code. Used to determine if the blacklist is working as intended during startup (since the blacklist runs before crash reporting is set up). This only occurs on Windows.

histogram.name: Blimp.Compositor.CommitSizeKb
histogram.summary: Records the size of the uncompressed commit messages received by Blimp compositor.

histogram.name: Blimp.Connected
histogram.summary: Records whether connection attempt to the Blimp engine was successful.

histogram.name: Blimp.Supported
histogram.summary: Records whether Blimp is supported.

histogram.name: Blimp.Tab.Visible
histogram.summary: Records whenever a Blimp tab toggles visibility.

histogram.name: Blink.Animate.UpdateTime
histogram.summary:
Time spent processing main frame animations during a main frame update.
This histogram does not record metrics on machines with low-resolution clocks.

histogram.name: Blink.Animation.CompositedAnimationFailureReason
histogram.summary:
Reasons an Animation is not suitable for running on the compositor thread.
Note that a given Animation may have more than one reason, so this histogram cannot be used to determine the overall rate of 'composited' vs 'noncomposited' animations.

histogram.name: Blink.Binding.CreateV8ContextForMainFrame
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 04/2019 with the removal of field trial experiment settings.
histogram.summary:
Time spent to create a v8::Context instance during a page loading in the main-frame window.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Binding.CreateV8ContextForNonMainFrame
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 04/2019 with the removal of field trial experiment settings.
histogram.summary:
Time spent to create a v8::Context instance during a page loading in a non-main-frame window, e.g. iframe.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Binding.InitializeMainLocalWindowProxy
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 04/2019 with the removal of field trial experiment settings.
histogram.summary:
Time spent initializing LocalWindowProxy during a page loading in main windows.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Binding.InitializeMainRemoteWindowProxy
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 04/2019 with the removal of field trial experiment settings.
histogram.summary:
Time spent initializing RemoteWindowProxy during a page loading in main frame of OOPIF.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Binding.InitializeMainWindowProxy
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 03/2017. This metric was split into two metrics depending if it figures time for local window proxies or remote ones.
histogram.summary: Time spent initializing WindowProxy during a page loading in main windows.

histogram.name: Blink.Binding.InitializeNonMainLocalWindowProxy
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 04/2019 with the removal of field trial experiment settings.
histogram.summary:
Time spent initializing LocalWindowProxy during a page loading in non-main windows, e.g. iframe.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Binding.InitializeNonMainRemoteWindowProxy
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 04/2019 with the removal of field trial experiment settings.
histogram.summary:
Time spent initializing RemoteWindowProxy during a page loading in OOPIF.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Binding.InitializeNonMainWindowProxy
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 03/2017. This metric was split into two metrics depending if it figures time for local window proxies or remote ones.
histogram.summary: Time spent initializing WindowProxy during a page loading in non-main windows, e.g. iframe.

histogram.name: Blink.Binding.InitializeWindowProxy
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 10/2016. This metric was split into two metrics depending if it figures time for main windows.
histogram.summary: Time spent initializing WindowProxy during a page loading.

histogram.name: Blink.BudgetAPI.QueryBudget
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 08/2018 (M70) with the removal of the Budget API.
histogram.summary: Whenever the Budget API framework receives a query for the current budget, this records the total budget available to the origin, which is an internal Chrome value for the amount of background processing an origin can do without visibly alerting the user. Scale for the budget is 0 to 100. Care should be taken when drawing conclusions from this metric, as budget is based on both engagement and usage, so could vary significantly based on user behaviour and origin.

histogram.name: Blink.BudgetAPI.Reserve
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 08/2018 (M70) with the removal of the Budget API.
histogram.summary: Whenever the Budget API framework receives a request to reserve budget for a future background operation, this records whether the reserve request succeeded or not.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.2DLayerBridgeIsDeferred
histogram.obsolete: Removed as the deferral path is always used now - 11/2019.
histogram.summary: Records if a 2D Layer Bridge is using deferred rendering when it is destroyed. Emitted in Canvas2DLayerBridge::~Canvas2DLayerBridge.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.2DPrintingAsVector
histogram.summary: Records if a Canvas is being printed as a vector or as an image.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.ContentChangeMode
histogram.summary: Records whether the contents in the canvas buffer needed to be preserved via a copy when replacing the target buffer for drawing a new frame. This metric is recorded each time the target buffer needs to be replaced. It's recorded once per frame.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.ContextType
histogram.summary: Records the context type names used to create canvas rendering contexts.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.CreateImageBitmapSource
histogram.summary: The source from which an ImageBitmap is created by a createImageBitmap call.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.DrawImage
histogram.summary:
Time spent on 2D canvas drawImage API call.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.DrawImage.Duration
histogram.summary:
Time spent on the main thread during a 2D canvas drawImage API call.
Note: This metric drops reports on clients with low-resolution clocks, which means these reports will be biased against a portion of the population on Windows. See Windows.HasHighResolutionTimeTicks for the affected sample.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.DrawImage.SqrtNumberOfPixels
histogram.summary: Stores the square root of the number of pixels drawn into a Canvas. Different histograms per CanvasImageSource.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.GetImageData
histogram.summary:
Time spent on 2D canvas getImageData API call.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.GetImageDataScaledDuration
histogram.summary:
Time in microseconds * 10 (10e-5 seconds) spent on the 2D canvas getImageData API call, divided by the square root of the total number of pixels of the image extracted from the canvas. Smaller is faster.
The idea behind this elaborated unit is that the bigger the area of a canvas the longer the operation GetImageData takes, so we want a way to measure the overall performance regardless of the size of the canvas.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.GPUAccelerated2DCanvasDisableDeferralReason
histogram.obsolete: Canvas doesn't disable deferral anymore since July 2019.
histogram.summary: The reasons why a GPU accelerated canvas stopped deferring its rendering operations.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.HasRendered
histogram.summary: Records if the canvas has been rendered at least once. Recorded on disposal.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.HibernationEvents
histogram.summary: Records the occurrence of events related to 2D canvas GPU resource hibernation.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.IsComposited
histogram.summary: Records if the canvas context is composited or not. Recorded in HTMLCanvasElement::GetCanvasRenderingContext.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.MaximumInflightResources
histogram.summary: Reports the maximum number or canvas resources used at any one time by a canvas resource provider. Logged in the destructor of CanvasResourceProvider.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.NumCanvasesPerPage
histogram.summary: When a Document is shutdown, reports the number of canvases on that page.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.OffscreenCommitTimer
histogram.obsolete: Removed in 10/2018. Offscreen Canvas no longer needs commits.
histogram.summary:
Wall clock durations of OffscreenCanvas.commit() calls.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.PutImageData
histogram.summary:
Time spent on 2D canvas putImageData API call.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.PutImageDataScaledDuration
histogram.summary:
Time in microseconds * 10 (10e-5 seconds) spent on the 2D canvas putImageData API call, divided by the square root of the total number of pixels of the imageData painted into the canvas. Smaller is faster.
The idea behind this elaborated unit is that the bigger the area of a canvas the longer the operation PutImageData takes, so we want a way to measure the overall performance regardless of the size of the canvas.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.RasterDuration
histogram.summary:
Records the CPU, GPU and combined raster time for a random sampling of canvas frames for accelerated and deferred canvases. Records wall time for a random sampling of unaccelerated canvases.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.RequestedImageMimeTypes
histogram.summary: Records the occurence of image file formats passed into toDataURL and toBlob functions in canvas.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.ResourceProviderIsAccelerated
histogram.summary: Logs if the canvas resource provider is accelerated or not.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.ResourceProviderType
histogram.summary: Records the type of resource provider used for a canvas.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.ResourceProviderUsage
histogram.summary: Records the requested resource usage for a canvas resource provider.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.SqrtNumberOfPixels
histogram.summary: Stores the square root of the number of pixels in a new or resized offscreen canvas. Emitted from CanvasRenderingContextHost::RecordCanvasSizeToUMA.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.ToBlob.CompleteEncodingDelay
histogram.summary:
This metric measures the total time spent on completing encoding all the rows of an image (jpeg or png), as part of a canvas.toBlob API call. Encoding occurs during one or more idle periods on the main thread and the waiting time of the next idle period is included in the measurement. If the code has swtiched to force encoding path, we will not measure the delay in this metric.
This metric is useful in helping us adjust the IdleTaskCompleteTimeoutDelay in canvas.toBlob. When the encoding idle task is delayed for longer than IdleTaskCompleteTimeoutDelay, the browser will switch to a non-idle task to force encoding to happen on the main thread.
In addition, metric values from OffscreenCanvas.convertToBlob API call are also gathered into this histogram, because the logic flow is exactly the same as canvas.toBlob. It's worth to note that the values can come from idle tasks on either main or worker thread.
Note: This metric drops reports on clients with low-resolution clocks, which means these reports will be biased against a portion of the population on Windows. See Windows.HasHighResolutionTimeTicks for the affected sample.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.ToBlob.IdleEncodeDuration
histogram.summary: This metric measures the total time spent on encoding all the rows of an image (jpeg or png), excluding the waiting time of next idle periods. This is part of a canvas.toBlob API call. Encoding occurs during one or more idle periods on the main thread.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.ToBlob.IdleTaskStatus
histogram.summary:
Records the status of the idle task when finishing a toBlob call.
In addition, metric values from OffscreenCanvas.convertToBlob API call are also gathered into this histogram, because the logic flow is exactly the same as canvas.toBlob. It's worth to note that the values can come from idle tasks on either main or worker thread.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.ToBlob.InitiateEncodingDelay
histogram.summary:
This metric measures the time spent from initiating image encoding (jpeg or png) on idle task to the actual execution time of initiation, as part of a canvas.toBlob API call.
This metric is useful in helping us adjust the IdleTaskStartTimeoutDelay in canvas.toBlob. When the initialization idle task is delayed for longer than IdleTaskStartTimeoutDelay, the browser will switch to a non-idle task to force initialization and encoding to occur on the main thread.
In addition, metric values from OffscreenCanvas.convertToBlob API call are also gathered into this histogram, because the logic flow is exactly the same as canvas.toBlob. It's worth to note that the values can come from idle tasks on either main or worker thread.
Note: This metric drops reports on clients with low-resolution clocks, which means these reports will be biased against a portion of the population on Windows. See Windows.HasHighResolutionTimeTicks for the affected sample.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.ToBlob.ScaledDuration
histogram.summary:
Time in microseconds spent on the 2D canvas toBlob API call, divided by the square root of the total number of pixels of the canvas.
In addition, metric values from OffscreenCanvas.convertToBlob API call are also gathered into this histogram, because the logic flow is exactly the same as canvas.toBlob. It's worth to note that the values can come from idle tasks on either main or worker thread.
Note: This metric drops reports on clients with low-resolution clocks, which means these reports will be biased against a portion of the population on Windows. See Windows.HasHighResolutionTimeTicks for the affected sample.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.ToBlobDuration
histogram.summary:
Time spent on 2D canvas toBlob API call.
In addition, metric values from OffscreenCanvas.convertToBlob API call are also gathered into this histogram, because the logic flow is exactly the same as canvas.toBlob. It's worth to note that the values can come from idle tasks on either main or worker thread.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.ToDataURL
histogram.summary:
Time spent on 2D canvas toDataURL API call.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.Canvas.ToDataURLScaledDuration
histogram.summary:
Time spent on 2D canvas toDataURL API call divided by the square root of the total number of pixels of the image. Smaller is faster.
The idea behind this unit is that the bigger the area of a canvas the longer the operation ToDataURL takes, so we want a way to measure the overall performance regardless of the size of the canvas.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Blink.CheckerableImageCount
histogram.summary: The number of images which could have been checkered (async decoded) for a performance gain, split by the DOM element type on which they were used. This is recorded each time an image for an element created by the page is loaded, either from the network or the local cache. The browser heuristics define an image to be checkerable if it is expected to have a long decode duration. Since these images may be on the rendering critical path, decoding them asychronously from painting other content can provide better performance be reducing overall content checkerboarding.

histogram.name: Blink.ColorGamut.Destination
histogram.summary: Gamut properties of destination color space.

histogram.name: Blink.ColorGamut.Source
histogram.summary: Gamut properties of image color space.

histogram.name: Blink.ColorSpace.Destination
histogram.summary: Gamma properties of destination color space.

histogram.name: Blink.ColorSpace.Destination.ExtractedRawData
histogram.summary: Whether or not the output color space ICC profile was able to produce raw transfer function tables.

histogram.name: Blink.ColorSpace.Destination.ICCResult
histogram.summary: The result of analyzing the destiation color space's ICC profile.

histogram.name: Blink.ColorSpace.Destination.LinearFitError
histogram.obsolete: Linear fit found to be always inaccurate on 2017-02-28.
histogram.summary: The L-infinity error (in 8-bit values) of the numerical approximation of table-based ICC profile transfer functions, when falling back to a linear approximation.

histogram.name: Blink.ColorSpace.Destination.Matrix
histogram.summary: Whether or not to-XYZD50 matrix was extracted from the output color space ICC profile.

histogram.name: Blink.ColorSpace.Destination.NonlinearFitConverged
histogram.obsolete: Nonlinear fit code moved to skcms on 2018-04-25.
histogram.summary: Whether or not the nonlinear least squares fit of the table-based ICC profile transfer function for a single channel converged.

histogram.name: Blink.ColorSpace.Destination.NonlinearFitError
histogram.obsolete: Nonlinear fit code moved to skcms on 2018-04-25.
histogram.summary: The L-infinity error (in 8-bit values) of the numerical approximation of table-based ICC profile transfer functions for a single channel, when the nonlinear approximation converged.

histogram.name: Blink.ColorSpace.Destination.NonlinearFitErrorCombined
histogram.summary: The L-infinity error (in 8-bit values) of the numerical approximation of table-based ICC profile transfer functions for all channels combined, when the nonlinear approximation converged.

histogram.name: Blink.ColorSpace.Destination.Numerical
histogram.summary: Whether or not the output color space ICC profile has a numerical transfer function.

histogram.name: Blink.ColorSpace.Destination.OneMinusTMax
histogram.obsolete: TMax found to almost always be 1 on 2017-02-28.
