histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 3/2017. Replaced by using blink IDL annotation MeasureAs which appear in Blink.UseCounter.Features.
histogram.summary: Counts the number of times each call to a WebBluetooth function is done.

histogram.name: Bluetooth.Web.GetCharacteristic.Characteristic
histogram.summary: Records the UUIDs used when getting a characteristic. The recorded value is a 31-bit hash of the UUID. Used to know what types of characteristics to target when building libraries that use the Web Bluetooth API.

histogram.name: Bluetooth.Web.GetCharacteristic.Outcome
histogram.summary: Records the outcome of a call to getCharacteristic(). Used to understand what errors developers are getting so we can target efforts toward the most common ones.

histogram.name: Bluetooth.Web.GetCharacteristics.Characteristic
histogram.summary: Records the UUIDs used when getting characteristics. The recorded value is a 31-bit hash of the UUID. Used to know what types of characteristics to target when building libraries that use the Web Bluetooth API.

histogram.name: Bluetooth.Web.GetCharacteristics.Outcome
histogram.summary: Records the outcome of a call to getCharacteristics(). Used to understand what errors developers are getting so we can target efforts toward the most common ones.

histogram.name: Bluetooth.Web.GetDescriptor.Descriptor
histogram.summary: Records the UUIDs used when getting a descriptor. The recorded value is a 31-bit hash of the UUID. Used to know what types of descriptors to target when building libraries that use the Web Bluetooth API.

histogram.name: Bluetooth.Web.GetDescriptor.Outcome
histogram.summary: Records the outcome of a call to getDescriptor(). Used to understand what errors developers are getting so we can target efforts toward the most common ones.

histogram.name: Bluetooth.Web.GetDescriptors.Descriptor
histogram.summary: Records the UUIDs used when getting descriptors. The recorded value is a 31-bit hash of the UUID. Used to know what types of descriptors to target when building libraries that use the Web Bluetooth API.

histogram.name: Bluetooth.Web.GetDescriptors.Outcome
histogram.summary: Records the outcome of a call to getDescriptors(). Used to understand what errors developers are getting so we can target efforts toward the most common ones.

histogram.name: Bluetooth.Web.GetPrimaryService.Outcome
histogram.summary: Records the result of a GATTServer.getPrimaryService() call. Used to understand what errors developers are getting so we can target efforts toward the most common ones.

histogram.name: Bluetooth.Web.GetPrimaryService.Services
histogram.summary: Records what GATT Services are used when connected. The record value is a 31-bit hash of the Service UUID. This will help us know if we should build an API for specific services rather than a general Bluetooth API.

histogram.name: Bluetooth.Web.GetPrimaryServices.Outcome
histogram.summary: Records the result of a GATTServer.getPrimaryServices() call. Used to understand what errors developers are getting so we can target efforts toward the most common ones.

histogram.name: Bluetooth.Web.GetPrimaryServices.Services
histogram.summary: Records what GATT Services are used when connected. The record value is a 31-bit hash of the Service UUID. This will help us know if we should build an API for specific services rather than a general Bluetooth API.

histogram.name: Bluetooth.Web.RequestDevice.Filters.Count
histogram.summary: Records how many filters are used when scanning. These results will help us better understand the uses of the API and make changes according to developers' behavior.

histogram.name: Bluetooth.Web.RequestDevice.Filters.Services
histogram.summary: Records a 31-bit hash of the GATT Service UUIDs used when scanning. This will help us know if we should build an API for specific services rather than a general Bluetooth API.

histogram.name: Bluetooth.Web.RequestDevice.FilterSize
histogram.summary: Records how many services are used in a filter. These results will help us better understand the uses of the API and make changes according to developers' behavior.

histogram.name: Bluetooth.Web.RequestDevice.NumOfDevicesInChooserWhenNotAcceptingAllDevices
histogram.summary: In the case of not accepting all devices, records the number of devices that are in the chooser when a device is paired.

histogram.name: Bluetooth.Web.RequestDevice.OptionalServices.Count
histogram.summary: Records how many optional services are used when scanning. These results will help us better understand the uses of the API and make changes according to developers' behavior.

histogram.name: Bluetooth.Web.RequestDevice.OptionalServices.Services
histogram.summary: Records what optional services are used. The recorded value is a 31-bit hash of the Service UUID. These results will help us better understand the uses of the API and make changes according to developers' behavior.

histogram.name: Bluetooth.Web.RequestDevice.Options.AcceptAllDevices
histogram.summary: Records the value of 'acceptAllDevices' in RequestDeviceOptions. These results will help us better understand the uses of the API and make changes according to developers' behavior.

histogram.name: Bluetooth.Web.RequestDevice.Outcome
histogram.summary: Records the result of a navigator.bluetooth.requestDevice() call. Used to understand what errors developers are getting so we can target efforts toward the most common ones.

histogram.name: Bluetooth.Web.RequestDevice.RSSISignalStrength
histogram.summary: Raw RSSI values provided to chooser, before processing them for display in the Web Bluetooth chooser.

histogram.name: Bluetooth.Web.RequestDevice.RSSISignalStrengthLevel
histogram.summary: Displayed RSSI levels, after processing and as displayed to users in the Web Bluetooth chooser.

histogram.name: Bluetooth.Web.RequestDevice.ScanningDuration
histogram.summary: Records the duration scanning for devices is run, terminated by events such as the chooser being closed with a selected device, cancelled, or the scan duration timing out.

histogram.name: Bluetooth.Web.RequestDevice.UnionOfServices.Count
histogram.summary: Records how many services are used when scanning. This results will help us better understand the uses of the API and make changes according to developers' behavior.

histogram.name: Bluetooth.Web.RequestDevice.UnionOfServices.Services
histogram.summary: Records what services are used from either required or optional sets. The recorded value is a 31-bit hash of the Service UUID. These results will help us better understand the uses of the API and make changes according to developers' behavior.

histogram.name: BlueZ.AdapterLost
histogram.summary: This is specific to Chrome OS. Records a duration of a Bluetooth adapter being lost. This helps us better understand the Bluetooth controller drop issue in the field.

histogram.name: BlueZ.ChipLost
histogram.obsolete: Replaced 08/2018 by BlueZ.ChipLost2 for more suspend/resume filtering even when the Bluetooth adapter is turned off.
histogram.summary: This is specific to Chrome OS. Records a duration of a Bluetooth adapter being lost caused by hardware disconnection. This helps us better understand the Bluetooth controller drop issue in the field.

histogram.name: BlueZ.ChipLost2
histogram.summary: This is specific to Chrome OS. Records a duration of a Bluetooth adapter being lost caused by hardware disconnection. This helps us better understand the Bluetooth controller drop issue in the field.

histogram.name: BlueZ.NumberOfExistingAdvertisements
histogram.summary: This is specific to Chrome OS. Records the number of existing advertisements when registering for a new advertisement. This helps us better understand the common amount of advertisments used and the registration failures due to the limited number of advertisement slots.

histogram.name: BlueZ.ReasonOfDisconnection
histogram.summary: This is specific to Chrome OS. Records the reason of Bluetooth disconnection between the local device and the remote Bluetooth device. This helps us better understand the initiator of disconnection request.

histogram.name: BlueZ.ResultOfAdvertisementRegistration
histogram.summary: This is specific to Chrome OS. Records the outcomes of advertisement registration. This helps us better understand the persentage of successful advertisements against failures and the causes of failures.

histogram.name: BlueZ.ResultOfConnection
histogram.summary: This is specific to Chrome OS. Records the outcomes of connection requests to remote devices. This helps us better understand the persentage of successful connections against failures and the causes of failures.

histogram.name: BlueZ.ResultOfPairing
histogram.summary: This is specific to Chrome OS. Records the outcomes of pairing with remote devices. This helps us better understand the persentage of successful pairing against failures and the causes of failures.

histogram.name: BlueZ.TimeLengthOfAdvertisement
histogram.summary: This is specific to Chrome OS. Records the duration when the local device is performing Bluetooth advertisment and discoverable as a Bluetooth Low Energy device by other devices. This helps us better understand the amount of successful advertisment sessions and the duration.

histogram.name: BlueZ.TimeLengthOfDiscoverable
histogram.summary: This is specific to Chrome OS. Records the duration when the local device is discoverable by other devices as a Bluetooth classic device. This helps us better understand the amount of requests to expose the local device as a Bluetooth classic device and the duration.

histogram.name: BlueZ.TimeLengthOfDiscovering
histogram.summary: This is specific to Chrome OS. Records the duration for the local device to perform device discovery. This helps us better understand the amount of device discovery request and the duration for users to find the target device.

histogram.name: BlueZ.TimeLengthOfPairing
histogram.summary: This is specific to Chrome OS. Records the duration taken to finish a successful pairing between the local device and a remote Bluetooth device. This helps us better understand the latency of completing Bluetooth pairing.

histogram.name: BlueZ.TimeLengthOfSetupConnection
histogram.summary: This is specific to Chrome OS. Records the duration taken to finish a successful connection between the local device and a remote Bluetooth device. This helps us better understand the latency of connecting to a Bluetooth device.

histogram.name: BlueZ.TypeOfDiscovery
histogram.summary: This is specific to Chrome OS. Records the types of device discovery performed by the local device. This helps us better understand the most common type of device discovery performed.

histogram.name: BlueZ.TypeOfFoundDevice
histogram.summary: This is specific to Chrome OS. Records the types of discovered devices in terms of Bluetooth classic, Bluetooth low energy and Dual-mode supports. This helps us better understand the persentage of Bluetooth devices in terms of type.

histogram.name: BookmarkManager.CommandExecuted
histogram.summary:
Logs when a user action triggers a command in the bookmark manager. Commands can be triggered by keyboard shortcuts, menu items or other buttons in the UI.
Revised in M-76 when the "Open (double click/enter)" bucket was split into two separate buckets, one for bookmarks and one for folders.
This histogram is of special interest to the chrome-analysis-team@. Do not change its semantics or retire it without talking to them first.

histogram.name: BookmarkManager.CommandExecutedFromKeyboard
histogram.summary:
Logs when a keyboard shortcut triggers a command in the bookmark manager.
Revised in M-76 when the "Open (double click/enter)" bucket was split into two separate buckets, one for bookmarks and one for folders.

histogram.name: BookmarkManager.CommandMenuOpened
histogram.summary: Logs where the menu was opened from when a user action opens the command menu in the bookmark manager.

histogram.name: BookmarkManager.NumDragged
histogram.summary: Logs the number of bookmarks that were dragged simultaneously by a user.

histogram.name: BookmarkManager.NumDraggedInSession
histogram.summary: Logs the number of drag actions that are performed in one session (defined as the time between a user's opening a folder and closing said folder).

histogram.name: BookmarkManager.NumReorderButtonInSession
histogram.summary: Logs the number of uses of the Move Up / Down buttons in one session (defined as the time between a user's opening a folder and closing said folder).

histogram.name: BookmarkManager.ResultsRenderedTime
histogram.summary: Records the time taken to load the bookmarks Web UI and render. This is roughly equivalent to 'time to first meaningful paint' for the bookmark manager.

histogram.name: Bookmarks.AddedPerProfileType
histogram.summary: This histogram records when a bookmark is added sliced on profile type.

histogram.name: Bookmarks.BookmarkAllTabsWithTabsCount.Incognito
histogram.summary: This histogram records the count of tabs when a user bookmarks all open tabs, in incognito mode.

histogram.name: Bookmarks.BookmarkAllTabsWithTabsCount.Regular
histogram.summary: This histogram records the count of tabs when a user bookmarks all open tabs, in regular mode.

histogram.name: Bookmarks.BookmarksInFolder
histogram.summary: Logs the number of bookmark items inside a folder every time a folder is opened inside the bookmark manager.

histogram.name: Bookmarks.Count.OnProfileLoad
histogram.summary: The total number of bookmarks a user has saved. Recorded when a profile is opened - precisely, when bookmarks are loaded into storage from disk. The count includes all bookmarks both in the "Bookmarks Bar" and also under "Other Bookmarks".

histogram.name: Bookmarks.Count.OnProfileLoad.DuplicateUrl
histogram.summary: The number of bookmarks a user has saved with a URL that is also present in at least one other bookmark. This excludes folders (which don't have a URL). Recorded when bookmarks are loaded into storage from disk if there is at least one duplicate.

histogram.name: Bookmarks.Count.OnProfileLoad.EmptyTitle
histogram.summary: The number of bookmarks a user has saved with an empty title (strictly speaking, i.e. does not count whitespace-only titles), including folders and excluding the root. Recorded when bookmarks are loaded into storage from disk if there is at least one node with an empty title.

histogram.name: Bookmarks.Count.OpenInIncognito
histogram.summary: Records the number of bookmarks which are opened using the "Open in incognito tab" button from selection mode in the Android bookmark manager.

histogram.name: Bookmarks.Count.OpenInNewTab
histogram.summary: Records the number of bookmarks which are opened using the "Open in new tab" button from selection mode in the Android bookmark manager.

histogram.name: Bookmarks.DuplicateAndEmptyTitleDetectionTime
histogram.summary: Time to compute values to be logged for UMA metrics Bookmarks.Count.OnProfileLoad.*.

histogram.name: Bookmarks.EntryPoint
histogram.summary: How users add a new bookmark.

histogram.name: Bookmarks.FileSize
histogram.summary: The size of the file used to persist bookmarks. It's recorded every time the file is loaded in memory.

histogram.name: Bookmarks.LaunchDepth
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 6/2019. This histogram is no longer necessary.
histogram.summary: Logs the depth of the bookmark in the bookmark tree hierarchy every time a bookmark is launched. Depth indicates how many levels below a permanent bookmark folder the bookmark was found in (e.g. a bookmark immediately in the bookmark bar has depth 1).

histogram.name: Bookmarks.LaunchLocation
histogram.summary: Logs a UI location from which a bookmark is launched.

histogram.name: Bookmarks.OnFaviconsChangedIconURL
histogram.summary: Logs how frequently BookmarkModel::OnFaviconsChanged() is called with a non-empty |icon_url| to determine whether BookmarkNodes should be cached based on their favicon URLs (e.g. http://www.google.com/favicon.ico) in addition to their page URLs (e.g. http://www.google.com). OnFaviconChanged() is expected to be called less than 10 times per user per day with a non-empty |icon_url|.

histogram.name: Bookmarks.OpenAction
histogram.summary: Logs how user is opening a bookmark on Android. For example, user can long press and select "Open in a new tab" or "Open in incognito tab".

histogram.name: Bookmarks.OpenBookmarkType
histogram.summary: Logs whether the bookmark entry is a user bookmark or a partner bookmark when it is opened.

histogram.name: Bookmarks.UsageCountPerProfileType
histogram.summary: The number of times bookmarks are used in each profile type. Recorded when a bookmark is clicked.

histogram.name: BootTime.Total
histogram.obsolete: Replaced 08/2016 by BootTime.Total2 with larger range and more buckets.
histogram.summary: Time from power on to login panel ready (Chrome OS).

histogram.name: BootTime.Total2
histogram.summary: Time from power on to login panel ready (Chrome OS).

histogram.name: BrotliFilter.CompressionPercent
histogram.summary: Compressed/Decompressed size ratio.

histogram.name: BrotliFilter.ErrorCode
histogram.summary: More detailed failure reason.

histogram.name: BrotliFilter.GzipHeaderDetected
histogram.summary: Indicates whether the gzip-like header was detected.

histogram.name: BrotliFilter.Status
histogram.summary: The final state of BrotliFilter.

histogram.name: BrotliFilter.UsedMemoryKB
histogram.summary: Maximal amount of memory allocated by decoder.

histogram.name: Browser.DarkModeStatus
histogram.summary: Whether the user's system is in dark mode, light mode, or if this choice is unavailable due to lack of system support. Updated every UMA upload.

histogram.name: Browser.Metal.TestShaderCompileTime
histogram.obsolete: Removed 05/2020. Was not substantially different from results in the GPU process.
histogram.summary: Compiling a MTLLibrary will sometimes hang forever. When initializing the browser process, a test shader is compiled to see if the MTLCompilerService is responding or not. This records the time that it took for the compile to succeeded, up to 1 minute. After 1 minute, a timeout sentinel value of 3 minutes is reported.

histogram.name: Browser.Metal.TestShaderMethodTime
histogram.obsolete: Removed 05/2020. Did not reveal interesting results.
histogram.summary: Compiling a MTLLibrary will sometimes hang forever. When initializing the browser process, a test shader is compiled to see if the MTLCompilerService is responding or not. This records the time that it took for the compile method to finish (not including the time for the compile itself to finish), up to 1 minute. After 1 minute, a timeout sentinel value of 3 minutes is reported.

histogram.name: Browser.PaintPreview.Capture.NumberOfFramesCaptured
histogram.summary: Records the number of frames captured by a paint preview if it succeeded.

histogram.name: Browser.PaintPreview.Capture.Success
histogram.summary: Records a boolean indicating whether a capture attempt was successful.

histogram.name: Browser.PaintPreview.Capture.TotalCaptureDuration
histogram.summary: Records the total time spent capturing a paint preview of a page. This includes capture and serialization of all frames and IPC time.

histogram.name: Browser.PaintPreview.CaptureExperiment.CompressedOnDiskSize
histogram.summary: Records the compressed on-disk size of the capture if it succeeded.

histogram.name: Browser.PaintPreview.CaptureExperiment.Success
histogram.summary: Records the ratio of success to failure for the Paint Preview capture experiment.

histogram.name: Browser.Responsiveness.IOJanksTotalPerMinute
histogram.summary: Similar to Browser.Responsiveness.IOJankyIntervalsPerMinute but sums janky events across all foreground threads over the monitored minute rather than considering each interval a boolean. This aims to cover a blind spot of IOJankyIntervalsPerMinute as it cannot see an improvement where we unblock 19/20 threads performing I/O. Not reported across system sleep. More details @ https://bit.ly/chrome-io-jank-metric

histogram.name: Browser.Responsiveness.IOJankyIntervalsPerMinute
histogram.summary: This metric is emitted every minute. It reports a number between 0 and 60 which corresponds to the number of 1 second interval during the monitored minute for which a ScopedBlockingCall(BlockingType::MAY_BLOCK) stalled for more than 1 second on a foreground thread. This aims to capture moments where the main thread is responsive but the application is not (because things aren't loading). Not reported across system sleep. More details @ https://bit.ly/chrome-io-jank-metric

histogram.name: Browser.Responsiveness.JankyIntervalsPerThirtySeconds
histogram.summary:
This metric is emitted every 30 seconds [when there is user activity]. Each 30 second duration is divided into 100ms intervals. This metric counts the number of these intervals that were "janky". See https://docs.google.com/document/d/1vDSGFvJblh7yJ3U3RVB_7qZLubyfTbQdQjuN1GoUNkc/edit for more details.
On desktop platforms, this metric measures native events, as well as tasks posted to the UI and IO threads of the browser process. On Android, this metric only measures tasks posted to the UI and IO threads.
Update (October 27th 2019): The responsiveness::Watcher emitting this metric previously started observation before the main message loop was running, this resulted in the 99th percentile being mostly comprised of slow startups (before the message loop gets to run). This was fixed and a fake-improvement in the high percentiles is expected when the fix goes live.
Update (M82 March 2020): Stopped recording background intervals on Android.
Due to a bug, this metric does not measure long queueing time as jankiness. It only measures jankiness as individual tasks taking a long time to run. Given that it's a long-standing bug, this metric will not be modified. We are testing a new metric Browser.Responsiveness.JankyIntervalsPerThirtySeconds2 that correctly accounts for queueing time. See https://groups.google.com/a/google.com/forum/#!topic/chrome-scheduler/X32gKl6fW2A for more details.
This histogram is of special interest to the chrome-analysis-team@. Do not change its semantics or retire it without talking to them first.

histogram.name: Browser.Responsiveness.JankyIntervalsPerThirtySeconds2
histogram.summary:
This metric is emitted every 30 seconds after main message loop start, when there is user activity. Each 30 second duration is divided into 100ms intervals. This metric counts the number of these intervals that were "janky". An interval is janky if during it the UI or IO thread executes an "important" task that was queued more than 100 ms ago, or a non-important task or a native event handler that started executing more than 100 ms ago. See https://docs.google.com/document/d/1vDSGFvJblh7yJ3U3RVB_7qZLubyfTbQdQjuN1GoUNkc/edit for more details.
Update (M82 March 2020): Stopped recording background intervals on Android.
This histogram is of special interest to the chrome-analysis-team@. Do not change its semantics or retire it without talking to them first.

histogram.name: Browser.Tabs.InputEventToSelectionTime
histogram.summary: The time between input event timestamp for tab switch and when the TabStripModel begins processing the switch.

histogram.name: Browser.Tabs.SelectionToVisibilityRequestTime
histogram.summary: The time it takes for the browser to request a tab to be visible. Note that this only measures the time from the browser deciding to make the tab visible (after a click, or keyboard-shortcut to switch tabs), until the tab is requested to become visible. The time it takes to actually make the tab visible and show on screen is measured in a separate metric, in RWH_TabSwitchPaintDuration.

histogram.name: Browser.Tabs.TabSwitchResult
histogram.summary: Whether a frame was presented after a tab switch event.

histogram.name: Browser.Tabs.TotalIncompleteSwitchDuration
histogram.summary: The time between a tab switch input event timestamp and when the tab is hidden, if no frame is presented in the meantime.

histogram.name: Browser.Tabs.TotalSwitchDuration
histogram.summary: The time between the input event timestamp and the frame presentation time on a tab switch.

histogram.name: Browser.WindowCount.Guest
histogram.summary: Number of open guest windows at the same time. Recorded when a new guest window is created. Please note that this metric double counts the lower numbers, meaning that if user opens three windows (without closing any in between), then buckets 1, 2, and 3 will all be incremented in turn.

histogram.name: Browser.WindowCount.Incognito
histogram.summary: Number of open incognito windows at the same time. Recorded when a new incognito window is created. Please note that this metric double counts the lower numbers, meaning that if user opens three windows (without closing any in between), then buckets 1, 2, and 3 will all be incremented in turn.

histogram.name: BrowserActions.NumTabCreatedInBackground
histogram.obsolete: Removed in M77 with the removal of Browser Actions.
histogram.summary: The number of tabs currently opened when users tap "Open in new Chrome tab" from Browser Actions context menu. This includes the new tab created from the tap.

histogram.name: BrowserActions.SelectedOption
histogram.obsolete: Removed in M77 with the removal of Browser Actions.
histogram.summary: The option that the user selected from a Browser Actions context menu.

histogram.name: BrowserDialogs.ExternalProtocol.HandleState
histogram.summary: Whether or not the user checked the option in the external protocol dialog to remember their choice of opening or not opening the specified app. Recorded each time the user interacts with the External Protocol Dialog, when making a decision.

histogram.name: BrowserDialogs.ExternalProtocol.RememberCheckbox
histogram.obsolete: Removed 09/2017. This histogram has been replaced with ExternalProtocol.HandleState.
histogram.summary: Whether or not the user checked the option in the external protocol dialog to remember their choice of opening or not opening the specified app. Recorded each time the user interacts with the External Protocol Dialog (either accepting or cancelling/dismissing).

histogram.name: BrowserRenderProcessHost.BackgroundTime
histogram.summary: Record the time spent in the background after RenderProcessHost initialization of the process. Recorded when the RenderProcessHost for the process is destroyed.

histogram.name: BrowserRenderProcessHost.ChildCodeIntegrityFailures
histogram.summary: Count of renderer process terminations due to a code integrity failure, grouped by type. This is only reported on Windows.

histogram.name: BrowserRenderProcessHost.ChildCrashes
histogram.summary: Count of renderer process crashes grouped by type.

histogram.name: BrowserRenderProcessHost.ChildKills
histogram.summary: Count of renderer process kills grouped by type.

histogram.name: BrowserRenderProcessHost.ChildKills.OOM
histogram.summary: Out of BrowserRenderProcessHost.ChildKills, numer of kills due to SIGKILL, which is a strong signal of out of memory on Chrome OS, grouped by renderer type.

histogram.name: BrowserRenderProcessHost.ChildLaunchFailureCodes
histogram.summary: The launch error codes for failed renderer process launches.

histogram.name: BrowserRenderProcessHost.ChildLaunchFailures
histogram.summary: Count of renderer process launch failures grouped by type.

histogram.name: BrowserRenderProcessHost.DisconnectedAlive
histogram.summary: Count of renderer process crashes that we miscounted because we took the exit code too early. Grouped by type.

histogram.name: BrowserRenderProcessHost.FramePrioritiesSeen
histogram.summary: Determines what combination of low and normal priority frames were attached to a render process during its lifetime. Recorded when the host for the render process is destroyed.

histogram.name: BrowserRenderProcessHost.InvisibleMediaStreamFrameDepth
histogram.summary: Recorded at most once per renderer process when it first becomes invisible and has media stream. Record the frame depth of the process. Note this metric is only meaningful when some form of OOPIF is enabled; otherwise frame depth is always 0.

histogram.name: BrowserRenderProcessHost.KeepAliveDuration
histogram.summary: How long a render process is kept alive by additional customers such as shared workers and service workers.

histogram.name: BrowserRenderProcessHost.KeepAliveDuration.Fetch
histogram.summary: How long fetch is alive which in turn keeps the render process alive. TODO(panicker): Remove after investigation of crbug/823482, likely in M68.

histogram.name: BrowserRenderProcessHost.KeepAliveDuration.ServiceWorker
histogram.summary: How long service worker is alive which in turn keeps the render process alive. TODO(panicker): Remove after investigation of crbug/823482, likely in M68.

histogram.name: BrowserRenderProcessHost.KeepAliveDuration.SharedWorker
histogram.summary: How long shared worker is alive which in turn keeps the render process alive. TODO(panicker): Remove after investigation of crbug/823482, likely in M68.

histogram.name: BrowserRenderProcessHost.KeepAliveDuration.Unload
histogram.summary: How long the renderer process is kept alive by a subframe unload handler. TODO(panicker): Remove after investigation of crbug/823482.

histogram.name: BrowserRenderProcessHost.OnChannelError
histogram.summary: Number of times BrowserRenderProcessHost::OnChannelError was called.

histogram.name: BrowserRenderProcessHost.SpareProcessMaybeTakeAction
histogram.summary: Records what happens when an attempt is made to use a spare RenderProcessHost - logs that either the attempt succeeded or why it failed.

histogram.name: BrowserRenderProcessHost.TotalTime
histogram.summary: Record the lifetime of the RenderProcessHost after initialization of the process. Recorded when the RenderProcessHost for the process is destroyed.

histogram.name: BrowserServices.BrowsableIntentCheck
histogram.summary: Triggered when we check whether a Trusted Web Activity client app can handle Browsable Intents. It measures how long consulting the Android PackageManager takes.

histogram.name: BrowserServices.ClientAppDataLoad
histogram.summary: Triggered when any Android app is uninstalled or has its data cleared. This measures how long it takes to read data from Preferences to determine whether we need to act.

histogram.name: BrowserServices.ServiceTabResolveInfoQuery
histogram.summary: Triggered when an Android background service attempts to create a Tab. We time how long the Android package manager queryIntentActivities call takes.

histogram.name: BrowserServices.TwaOpenTime
histogram.obsolete: Retired in M77 in favour of BrowserServices.TwaOpenTime.V2 which uses a more appropriate timescale.
histogram.summary: Triggered when a TrustedWebActivity is paused, recording the time since it had been resumed.

histogram.name: BrowserServices.TwaOpenTime.V2
histogram.summary: Triggered when a TrustedWebActivity is paused, recording the time since it had been resumed.

histogram.name: BrowserServices.VerificationResult
histogram.summary: The result of Digital Asset Link verification performed when starting a Trusted Web Activity.

histogram.name: BrowserServices.VerificationTime.Offline
histogram.summary: Triggered when Trusted Web Activity verification completes. Records how long it takes the device to realise it is offline and fall back to the cached result.

histogram.name: BrowserServices.VerificationTime.Online
histogram.summary: Triggered when Trusted Web Activity verification completes. Records how long verification took when online.

histogram.name: BrowserSwitcher.AlternativeBrowser
histogram.summary: Records which browser the Legacy Browser Support feature is configured to open (which defaults to IE). Recorded on browser startup, and when the value changes. Only measured when Legacy Browser Support is enabled via the BrowserSwitcher* policies. Desktop Chrome only.

histogram.name: BrowserSwitcher.CacheFile.MkDirSuccess
histogram.summary: Records whether the "AppData/Local/Google/BrowserSwitcher" directory was successfully created. Windows only.

histogram.name: BrowserSwitcher.CacheFile.MkTempSuccess
histogram.summary: Records whether a temp file in "AppData/Local/Google/BrowserSwitcher" the directory was successfully created. Windows only.

histogram.name: BrowserSwitcher.CacheFile.MoveSuccess
histogram.summary: Records whether the temp file in "AppData/Local/Google/BrowserSwitcher" directory was successfully moved to its final destination ("cache.dat") after writing its contents. Windows only.

histogram.name: BrowserSwitcher.Decision
histogram.summary: On navigation and redirect, whether to switch browsers or not. Only measured when Legacy Browser Support is enabled via the BrowserSwitcher* policies. When this is true, Chrome attempts to launch an alternative browser (typically IE) with the navigation URL. Desktop Chrome only.

histogram.name: BrowserSwitcher.DecisionTime
histogram.summary: On navigation and redirect, records how long it took to compute a decision (stay in Chrome/open in IE). Only measured when Legacy Browser Support is enabled via the BrowserSwitcher* policies. Desktop Chrome only.

histogram.name: BrowserSwitcher.ExternalGreylistSize
histogram.summary: Number of rules that come from an external greylist XML file. Measured when the sitelist is loaded during browser startup. Desktop Chrome only.

histogram.name: BrowserSwitcher.ExternalSitelistSize
histogram.summary: Number of rules that come from an external sitelist XML file. Measured when the sitelist is loaded during browser startup. Desktop Chrome only.

histogram.name: BrowserSwitcher.GreylistSize
histogram.summary: Number of rules in the BrowserSwitcherUrlGreylist policy. Measured when the policy is loaded. Desktop Chrome only.

histogram.name: BrowserSwitcher.GreylistWildcard
histogram.summary: Whether there is a wildcard ("*") rule in the BrowserSwitcherUrlGreylist policy. Measured when the BrowserSwitcherUrlGreylist policy gets updated. Desktop Chrome only.

histogram.name: BrowserSwitcher.IeemSitelistSize
histogram.summary: Number of rules that come from Internet Explorer Enterprise Mode's sitelist. Measured when the sitelist is loaded during browser startup. Windows only.

histogram.name: BrowserSwitcher.LaunchSuccess
histogram.summary: Records whether an attempt to launch an alternate browser failed or succeeded. Desktop Chrome only.

histogram.name: BrowserSwitcher.LaunchTime
histogram.summary: Records how long it took to launch an alternate browser (regardless of whether it failed or succeeded). Desktop Chrome only.

histogram.name: BrowserSwitcher.UrlListSize
histogram.summary: Number of rules in the BrowserSwitcherUrlList policy. Measured when the policy gets updated. Desktop Chrome only.

histogram.name: BrowserSwitcher.UrlListWildcard
histogram.summary: Whether there is a wildcard ("*") rule in the BrowserSwitcherUrlList policy. Measured when the BrowserSwitcherUrlList policy gets updated. Desktop Chrome only.

histogram.name: BrowserWindow.Resize.Duration
histogram.obsolete: Obsolete since 01/2020: mus+ash project was canceled, and these metrics are no longer useful.
histogram.summary: Duration of an interactive resize from start to end. Measured only on Windows.

histogram.name: BrowserWindow.Resize.StepBoundsChange
histogram.obsolete: Obsolete since 01/2020: mus+ash project was canceled, and these metrics are no longer useful.
histogram.summary: Size changed between two consecutive steps during browser-window resize. Measured only on Windows.

histogram.name: BrowserWindow.Resize.StepCount
histogram.obsolete: Obsolete since 01/2020: mus+ash project was canceled, and these metrics are no longer useful.
histogram.summary: Number of intermediate resize-steps taken to complete the resize from start to end. Measured only on Windows.

histogram.name: BrowserWindow.Resize.StepInterval
histogram.obsolete: Obsolete since 01/2020: mus+ash project was canceled, and these metrics are no longer useful.
histogram.summary: Time-interval between two consecutive steps during browser-window resize. An interactive resize can have many number of small steps. This measures the interval between two steps. 'Duration' measures the interval between the first and last steps. Measured only on Windows.

histogram.name: Bubbles.Close.Accepted
histogram.obsolete: No longer used; generating code removed in M81.
histogram.summary: An open bubble was closed because the user accepted it.

histogram.name: Bubbles.Close.Canceled
histogram.obsolete: No longer used; generating code removed in M81.
histogram.summary: An open bubble was closed because the user didn't accept it.

histogram.name: Bubbles.Close.FocusLost
histogram.obsolete: No longer used; generating code removed in M81.
histogram.summary: An open bubble was closed because of a focus change.

histogram.name: Bubbles.Close.Forced
histogram.obsolete: No longer used; generating code removed in M81.
histogram.summary: An open bubble was forced to close.

histogram.name: Bubbles.Close.FrameDestroyed
histogram.obsolete: No longer used; generating code removed in M81.
histogram.summary: An open bubble was dismissed because a frame was destroyed.

histogram.name: Bubbles.Close.FullscreenToggled
histogram.obsolete: No longer used; generating code removed in M81.
histogram.summary: An open bubble was dismissed because fullscreen was toggled.

histogram.name: Bubbles.Close.Navigated
histogram.obsolete: No longer used; generating code removed in M81.
histogram.summary: An open bubble was dismissed because the page was navigated.

histogram.name: Bubbles.Close.TabDetached
histogram.obsolete: No longer used; generating code removed in M81.
histogram.summary: An open bubble was closed because a tab was detached.

histogram.name: Bubbles.Close.TabSwitched
histogram.obsolete: No longer used; generating code removed in M81.
histogram.summary: An open bubble was closed because a tab was switched.

histogram.name: Bubbles.Close.UserDismissed
histogram.obsolete: No longer used; generating code removed in M81.
histogram.summary: An open bubble was dismissed by the user without making a decission.

histogram.name: Bubbles.DisplayTime.All
histogram.obsolete: No longer used; generating code removed in M81.
histogram.summary: Log the amount of time any bubble was visible. Only bubbles that are shown will have a visible time.

histogram.name: Bubbles.NeverShown
histogram.obsolete: No longer used; generating code removed in M81.
histogram.summary: A bubble was given to the bubble manager but not shown.

histogram.name: CachedImageFetcher.Events
histogram.summary: Events that track the lifecycle and performance of the cached_image_fetcher. The events reported include: success/failure conditions, various recoverable errors and a couple of dead-end errors.

histogram.name: CachedImageFetcher.ImageLoadFromCacheTime
histogram.summary: The time it takes for cached_image_fetcher to load an image from the cache in native.

histogram.name: CachedImageFetcher.ImageLoadFromCacheTimeJava
histogram.summary: The time it takes for cached_image_fetcher to load an image from the cache in Java.

histogram.name: CachedImageFetcher.ImageLoadFromNativeTimeJava
histogram.summary: The time it takes for cached_image_fetcher to load an image from native code. Only recorded on successful loads.

histogram.name: CachedImageFetcher.ImageLoadFromNetworkAfterCacheHit
histogram.summary: The time it takes for cached_image_fetcher to load an image from the network after a cache hit.

histogram.name: CachedImageFetcher.ImageLoadFromNetworkTime
histogram.summary: The time it takes for cached_image_fetcher to load an image from the network.

histogram.name: CachedImageFetcher.LoadImageMetadata
histogram.summary: The time it takes to load an image's metadata from the metadata store.

histogram.name: CachedImageFetcher.TimeSinceLastCacheLRUEviction
histogram.summary: The time since the last LRU eviction from the image cache. Recorded when two LRU evictions occur within closure proximity to one another. Will be used to determine if LRU eviction is happening too frequently.

histogram.name: Canvas.ContextType
histogram.summary: Records the context type names used to create canvas rendering contexts.

histogram.name: Canvas.CreateImageBitmapSource
histogram.summary: The source from which an ImageBitmap is created by a createImageBitmap call.

histogram.name: Canvas.DisplayListFallbackReason
histogram.obsolete: Removed 11/2017 with removal of Display List Canvas 2D mode.
histogram.summary: The reasons why a canvas initially set to display list mode had to fall back to direct rasterization mode.

histogram.name: Canvas.GPUAccelerated2DCanvasDisableDeferralReason
histogram.summary: The reasons why a GPU accelerated canvas stopped deferring its rendering operations.

histogram.name: Canvas.HibernationEvents
histogram.summary: Records the occurrence of events related to 2D canvas GPU resource hibernation.

histogram.name: Canvas.Offscreen.CommitType
histogram.summary: The type of code path that OffscreenCanvas's commit API goes through.

histogram.name: Canvas.Repaint.Bounds.Percentage
histogram.obsolete: The experiment finishes and removed in 03/2020
histogram.summary: measure the percentage of Canvas is dirty before each repaint by using cc::Rect. Note that we record this metrics for all canvas that has an area of more than 65k pixel^2 (256x256).

histogram.name: Canvas.Repaint.Region.Percentage
histogram.obsolete: The experiment finishes and removed in 03/2020
histogram.summary: measure the percentage of Canvas is dirty before each repaint by using cc::Region. Note that we record this metrics for all canvas that has an area of more than 65k pixel^2 (256x256).

histogram.name: Canvas.RequestedImageMimeTypes
histogram.summary: Records the occurence of image file formats passed into toDataURL and toBlob functions in canvas.

histogram.name: Canvas.TextMetrics.MeasureText
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019/08, this metrics doesn't reflect the measurement speed because it is dependent on the length of text.
histogram.summary:
Time spent in microseconds to perform calls to measure TextMetrics for Canvas2d. It's measured each time TextMetrics is called.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Canvas.TextMetrics.SetFont
histogram.summary:
Time spent in microseconds to perform calls to SetFont in Canvas for Canvas2d. It's measured each time SetFont is called.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: CAPSUpdater.Step
histogram.obsolete: Removed 08/2016 with removal of Chrome Crash Service component.
histogram.summary: Tracks the component updater steps for the Chrome Crash Service.

histogram.name: CaptivePortal.DetectionResultSincePortal
histogram.summary: Within one minute of the default network reporting a portal network, if we see an offline detection result, it is likely the client got blacklisted. Record the boolean rate that Chrome observes offline vs non-offline detection results. See go/bad-portal for client blacklisting problem.

histogram.name: CaptivePortal.DetectResult
histogram.summary: Records the result of a captive portal probe.

histogram.name: CaptivePortal.Notification.Status
histogram.summary: Count of displayed and not displayed due to errors notifications about captive portal.

histogram.name: CaptivePortal.Notification.UserAction
histogram.summary: Count of clicked, closed and ignored captive portal notifications.

histogram.name: CaptivePortal.OOBE.DetectionDuration
histogram.summary: Duration of the captive portal detection process for a particular network at OOBE. Detection duration is recorded each time portal detection is completed for an active network.

histogram.name: CaptivePortal.OOBE.DetectionResult
histogram.summary: The result of captive portal detection attempts performed at OOBE. Detection result is recorded when portal detection is completed for an active network and when it differs from the previous result for the same network.

histogram.name: CaptivePortal.OOBE.DiscrepancyWithShill
histogram.summary: The result of captive portal detection attempts at OOBE if it diverges from network manager results. Detection result is recorded each time portal detection is completed for an active network.

histogram.name: CaptivePortal.OOBE.PortalToOnlineTransition
histogram.summary: Number of milliseconds passed between consecutive reports for the same network about portal and online states.

histogram.name: CaptivePortal.ProbeReason
histogram.summary: Reason for attempting a captive portal probe. This is logged only when a probe HTTP request is made. If there are multiple reasons that triggered this probe, only the first is logged.

histogram.name: CaptivePortal.RedirectTime
histogram.summary: Number of milliseconds between start of request to gstatic.com/generate_204 and receipt of response with redirect to captive portal login page.

histogram.name: CaptivePortal.Session.DetectionDuration
histogram.summary: Duration of the captive portal detection process for a particular network in user session. Detection duration is recorded each time portal detection is completed for an active network.

histogram.name: CaptivePortal.Session.DetectionResult
histogram.summary: The result of captive portal detection attempts performed in user session. Detection result is recorded when portal detection is completed for an active network and when it differs from the previous result for the same network.

histogram.name: CaptivePortal.Session.DiscrepancyWithShill
histogram.summary: The result of captive portal detection attempts in session if it diverges from network manager results. Detection result is recorded each time portal detection is completed for an active network.

histogram.name: CaptivePortal.Session.PortalToOnlineTransition
histogram.summary: Number of milliseconds passed between consecutive reports for the same network about portal and online states.

histogram.name: CaptivePortal.Session.SecureConnectionFailed
histogram.summary: The result of captive portal detection attempts performed in user session. Detection result is recorded when portal detection is completed for a failed secure connection.

histogram.name: CaptivePortal.Session.TimeoutDetectionResult
histogram.summary: The result of captive portal detection attempts performed in user session. Detection result is recorded when portal detection is completed for a request which timed out or hadn't yet replied within eight seconds.

histogram.name: Cast.Channel.Certificate
histogram.summary: Records the result of the Cast certificate verification on the Cast sender.

histogram.name: Cast.Channel.Nonce
histogram.summary: Records whether the correct nonce challenge was in the Cast receiver's response.

histogram.name: Cast.Channel.Signature
histogram.summary: Records whether the signature returned by the Cast receiver is verifiable with the challenge and device certificate.

histogram.name: Cast.Network.Down.Duration.In.Seconds
histogram.summary: Records the time the network has been down on a Cast device. Recorded when network goes up.

histogram.name: Cast.Network.Up.Duration.In.Minutes
histogram.summary: Records the time the network has been up on a Cast device. Recorded when network goes down.

histogram.name: Cast.Sender.CastButtonShown
histogram.summary: Records the number of times the cast button was shown to the user. The value will be true if the button is enabled, and false if the button is disabled. Note that depending on the current UX, it's possible that we hide the button entirely if it's disabled, so it's possible for the false values to be 0.

histogram.name: Cast.Sender.CastButtonShownInitialFullscreen
histogram.summary: Records the number of times the cast button was shown to the user when the video is fullscreened. The value will only be recorded on entering fullscreen. The value will be true if the button is enabled, and false if the button is disabled. Note that depending on the current UX,it's possible that we hide the button entirely if it's disabled, so it's possible for the false values to be 0.

histogram.name: Cast.Sender.CastMediaType
histogram.summary: Records the media type of every video being cast.

histogram.name: Cast.Sender.CastPlayerResult
histogram.summary: Records the result of a request to play remotely on a per player app basis within Chrome for Android.

histogram.name: Cast.Sender.CastPlaySuccess
histogram.summary: Records the result of a request to play remotely. The value will be true if the playback succeeded, and false if there was an error.

histogram.name: Cast.Sender.CastTimeRemainingPercentage
histogram.summary: Records the percentage of the video left at the time the remote playback is stopped. This will be recorded when the playback is stopped by the user, or when it's stopped by the cast device.

histogram.name: Cast.Sender.DeviceType
histogram.summary: Records the type of the remote playback device selected by the user. This will be recorded when the user has chosen the device from the media route chooser dialog.

histogram.name: Cast.Sender.FullscreenControlsActionWithMediaElement
histogram.summary: Record each interaction with the fullscreen remote playback controls in cases when the corresponding media element on the page was present.

histogram.name: Cast.Sender.FullscreenControlsActionWithoutMediaElement
histogram.summary: Record each interaction with the fullscreen remote playback controls in cases when the corresponding media element on the page was not present.

histogram.name: Cast.Sender.MediaElementPresentWhenShowFullscreenControls
histogram.summary: Indicates whether the corresponding media element on the page was present when the fullscreen remote playback controls were shown.

histogram.name: Cast.Sender.Overlay
histogram.obsolete: Removed July 2017 in favor of Media.Controls.CTR.CastOverlayButton.
histogram.summary: Records events and user interactions related to the Cast overlay shown on video elements.

histogram.name: Cast.Sender.SessionTimeWithoutMediaElementPercentage
histogram.summary: Records the ratio of the time the media element was detached from the remote playback session to the total duration of the session (as from when the element has been attached till when the session stopped or disconnected), in percents.

histogram.name: Cast.Sender.UrlResolveResult
histogram.summary: Records the result of resolving the video URL for cast, including checking its CORS header.

histogram.name: Cast.Sender.VideoEncodeAcceleratorInitializeSuccess
histogram.summary: Indicates whether initialization of a video encode accelerator for Cast sender was successful.

histogram.name: CastV2.Transport.Connect.Latency.Unknown
histogram.summary: The latency between when a Cast V2 connection is established and the first message is received on that connection. "Unknown" is the peer type, which is not known at this point in the CastV2 connection establishment. Naming conforms to the CastV2 sender-side histogram naming convention "CastV2.[action].[name].[peer_type]".

histogram.name: CastV2.Transport.SslHandshake.Error.Unknown
histogram.summary: On accepting a Cast V2 transport socket connection, if an error is encountered, the error code is logged here. "Unknown" is the peer type, which is not known at this point in the CastV2 connection establishment. "SslHandshake.Error" is the name of the action. Naming conforms to the CastV2 sender-side histogram naming convention "CastV2.[action].[name].[peer_type]".

histogram.name: CastV2.Transport.SslHandshake.Unknown
histogram.summary: The time taken to do the handshake during Cast V2 transport socket creation. "Unknown" is the peer type, which is not known at this point in the CastV2 connection establishment. Naming conforms to the CastV2 sender-side histogram naming convention "CastV2.[action].[name].[peer_type]".

histogram.name: Cellular.ActivationFailure
histogram.obsolete: Removed 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The count of cellular device activation failures (Chrome OS).

histogram.name: Cellular.ActivationTry
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The count of cellular device activation tries (Chrome OS).

histogram.name: Cellular.ConnectionFailed
histogram.obsolete: Removed 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The count of cellular reconnect failures during activation (Chrome OS).

histogram.name: Cellular.ConnectionRetry
histogram.obsolete: Removed 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The count of cellular device reconnect tries during activation (Chrome OS).

histogram.name: Cellular.MobileSetupFailed
histogram.obsolete: Removed 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The count of successful cellular plan established (Chrome OS).

histogram.name: Cellular.MobileSetupStart
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The count of initiated cellular device setup starts (Chrome OS).

histogram.name: Cellular.MobileSetupSucceeded
histogram.obsolete: Removed 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The count of failed cellular plan setup tries (Chrome OS).

histogram.name: Cellular.PaymentFailed
histogram.obsolete: Removed 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The count of failed cellular plan purchases (Chrome OS).

histogram.name: Cellular.PaymentReceived
histogram.obsolete: Removed 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The count of successfully completed cellular plan purchases (Chrome OS).

histogram.name: CertificateType
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 8/2013. This histogram only considered the leaf certificate expiry date as a proxy for whether a certificate was in-scope for the BRs, but did not consider the issuance date. As some CAs have issued long-lived certs prior to the BRs, this disproportionately reported those certs as being subject to the BRs, but non-compliant, when in reality they're not subject.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Information about the certificate algorithms and sizes in use on the web, to examine compliance with the CA/Browser Forum requirements and security best practice.

histogram.name: CertificateType2
histogram.summary: Information about the certificate algorithms and sizes in use on the web, to examine compliance with the CA/Browser Forum requirements and security best practice. This histogram considers the notBefore as the issuance date, for purposes of what requirements apply.

histogram.name: ChildAccountReconcilor.ForcedUserExitOnReconcileError
histogram.summary: Number of forced logouts for child accounts on an account reconciliation error. Check |ChromeOSChildAccountReconcilorDelegate|.

histogram.name: ChildProcess.BadMessgeTerminated
histogram.summary: Count of child processes killed because they sent an IPC that couldn't be deserialized.

histogram.name: ChildProcess.Crashed
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Count of child process crashes grouped by process type.

histogram.name: ChildProcess.Crashed.UtilityProcessExitCode
histogram.summary: Recorded when a crash occurs in a Utility process. On crash, the exit code will indicate the exception code of the crash.

histogram.name: ChildProcess.Crashed.UtilityProcessHash
histogram.summary: Count of child utility process crashes, bucketed by the hash of their process name. The process name is typically the service name of the mojo service.

histogram.name: ChildProcess.Crashed2
histogram.summary: Count of child process crashes grouped by process type.

histogram.name: ChildProcess.CrashedWasAlive
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Count of child process crashes that we miscounted because we took the exit code too early. Grouped by process type.

histogram.name: ChildProcess.Crashes
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Count of child process crashes grouped by process type.

histogram.name: ChildProcess.CrashesWasAlive
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Count of child process crashes that we miscounted because we took the exit code too early. Grouped by process type.

histogram.name: ChildProcess.DefaultCase
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Count of child process crashes for which we were not able to understand the exit code, grouped by process type.

histogram.name: ChildProcess.Disconnected
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Count of child process abnormal channel disconnects grouped by process type.

histogram.name: ChildProcess.Disconnected2
histogram.summary: Count of child process abnormal channel disconnects grouped by process type.

histogram.name: ChildProcess.DisconnectedAlive
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Count of child process abnormal channel disconnects that are not classified and reported because we took the exit code too early. Grouped by process type.

histogram.name: ChildProcess.DisconnectedAlive2
histogram.summary: Count of child process abnormal channel disconnects that are not classified and reported because we took the exit code too early. Grouped by process type.

histogram.name: ChildProcess.FieldTrials.CreateFromShmemSuccess
histogram.summary: Whether creating field trials from shared memory succeeded. Recorded by each child process on process startup.

histogram.name: ChildProcess.FieldTrials.CreateFromSwitchSuccess
histogram.summary: Whether creating field trials from --force-fieldtrials switch succeeded. Recorded by each child process on process startup.

histogram.name: ChildProcess.HangRendererType
histogram.summary: What the browser was waiting for from the renderer when it was reported as hung.

histogram.name: ChildProcess.HungRendererAvailableMemoryMB
histogram.summary: The amount of avaiable memory when a renderer hangs. Recorded for every renderer hang.

histogram.name: ChildProcess.HungRendererCause
histogram.summary: What the browser was waiting for from the renderer when it was reported as hung.

histogram.name: ChildProcess.HungRendererInForeground
histogram.summary: Whether a renderer hang happened in the foreground or background. Recorded for every renderer hang on Android.

histogram.name: ChildProcess.InvalidSandboxStateCrash.NoStartupWindow
histogram.summary: Whether the browser command line had the switch --no-startup-window when a child process crashed due to invalid sandbox state. Recorded when a child process crashes if the exit code from the child process is RESULT_CODE_INVALID_SANDBOX_STATE.

histogram.name: ChildProcess.Killed
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Count of child process kills grouped by process type.

histogram.name: ChildProcess.Killed2
histogram.summary: Count of child process kills grouped by process type.

histogram.name: ChildProcess.Killed2.OOM
histogram.summary: Out of ChildProcess.Killled, number of kills due to SIGKILL, which is a strong signal of out of memory on Chrome OS, grouped by process type.

histogram.name: ChildProcess.KilledByExtensionAPI
histogram.summary: Count of child processes killed by the extension API (processes.terminate).

histogram.name: ChildProcess.KilledWasAlive
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Count of child process kills that we miscounted because we took the exit code too early. Grouped by process type.

histogram.name: ChildProcess.Kills
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Count of child process kills grouped by process type.

histogram.name: ChildProcess.KillsWasAlive
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Count of child process kills that we miscounted because we took the exit code too early. Grouped by process type.

histogram.name: ChildProcess.Launched.UtilityProcessHash
histogram.summary: Count of child utility process launches, bucketed by the hash of their process name. The process name is typically the service name of the mojo service.

histogram.name: Chrome.Android.Activity.CrashCounts
histogram.obsolete: Removed 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Indicates how many times each particular type of Activity was in the foreground when a UMA session was terminated abnormally. UMA sessions last as long as Chrome remains in the foreground.

histogram.name: Chrome.Android.Activity.LaunchCounts
histogram.obsolete: Removed 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Indicates how many times each particular type of Activity was brought to the foreground when a UMA session was active (i.e. launched at some point). UMA sessions last as long as Chrome remains in the foreground.

histogram.name: Chrome.Browser.CrashedExecutionPhase
histogram.summary: Indicates the execution phase the browser was in when the browser crashed.

histogram.name: Chrome.Browser.ExecutionPhase
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Indicates the execution phase the browser was in when browser didn't exit cleanly.

histogram.name: Chrome.BrowserCrashDumpAttempts
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The total number of times the browser process has attempted to generate a crash dump. This should be the sum of Chrome.BrowserDumpsWithCrash and Chrome.BrowserDumpsWithNoCrash.

histogram.name: Chrome.BrowserDumpsWithCrash
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of times the browser process has attempted to generate a crash dump because of an actual browser crash.

histogram.name: Chrome.BrowserDumpsWithNoCrash
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of times the browser process has attempted to generate a crash dump in a non-crashing (i.e., reporting only) context.

histogram.name: Chrome.CommandLineFlagCount
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of command line switches that were present at the start of a chrome session.

histogram.name: Chrome.CommandLineUncommonFlagCount
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of command line switches that were present at the start of a chrome session, excluding App Mode and User Data Directory switches.

histogram.name: Chrome.MessageLoopProblem
histogram.summary: Track set of Windows errors inside the meessage loop/pump system, where system calls fail, and we try to gracefully recover. These errors should really never happen, so we should be sure to look closer if they do.

histogram.name: Chrome.ProcessSingleton.NotifyResult
histogram.summary: The result of every rendez-vous reported once per browser process launch.

histogram.name: Chrome.ProcessSingleton.ProcessTerminateErrorCode.Windows
histogram.summary: The error code of remote process termination on Windows in case when remote process hung. This histogram has been replaced by Chrome.ProcessSingleton.TerminateProcessErrorCode.Windows histogram.

histogram.name: Chrome.ProcessSingleton.RemoteHungProcessTerminateReason
histogram.summary: The reason of remote hang processes termination.

histogram.name: Chrome.ProcessSingleton.RemoteProcessInteractionResult
histogram.summary: Used to send the result of interaction with remote process as histograms in case when remote process influences on start.

histogram.name: Chrome.ProcessSingleton.TerminateProcessErrorCode.Posix
histogram.summary: The error code of remote process termination on Posix in case when remote process hung.

histogram.name: Chrome.ProcessSingleton.TerminateProcessErrorCode.Windows
histogram.summary: The error code of remote process termination on Windows in case when remote process hung.

histogram.name: Chrome.ProcessSingleton.TerminateProcessTime
histogram.summary: Time it takes from TerminateProcess call to the moment when we stop waiting for remote process switches to signal state.

histogram.name: Chrome.ProcessSingleton.TerminationWaitErrorCode.Windows
histogram.summary: The error code of wating for remote process termination on Windows.

histogram.name: Chrome.ProcessSingleton.TimeToCreate
histogram.summary: Time it takes to grab the process singleton. Reported once per successful attempt at obtaining the singleton.

histogram.name: Chrome.ProcessSingleton.TimeToFailure
histogram.summary: Time it takes to return an error from the process singleton algorithm (nor succeeding in obtaining it, nor in generating a rendez-vous). This does not include time spent in the fast notification path (chrome_exe_main_win.cc).

histogram.name: Chrome.ProcessSingleton.TimeToNotify
histogram.summary: Time it takes to find the process singleton owner and notify it. Reported once per successful rendez-vous. This does not include time spent in the fast notification path (chrome_exe_main_win.cc).

histogram.name: Chrome.SearchSelectExempt
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The default search engine selected by a user not in the search engine dialog experiment.

histogram.name: Chrome.SearchSelectExperiment
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The default search engine selected by a user in the search engine dialog experiment.

histogram.name: Chrome.SearchSelectExperimentSlot1
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The default search engine selected by a user in slot 1 of a randomized search engine dialog.

histogram.name: Chrome.SearchSelectExperimentSlot2
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The default search engine selected by a user in slot 2 of a randomized search engine dialog.

histogram.name: Chrome.SearchSelectExperimentSlot3
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The default search engine selected by a user in slot 3 of a randomized search engine dialog.

histogram.name: Chrome.SearchSelectExperimentSlot4
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The default search engine selected by a user in slot 4 of a randomized search engine dialog.

histograms.histogram:
yichenz@chromium.org s chromeos-wmp@google.com
Tracks the animation smoothness for the fade-in animation of tab hover card.

yichenz@chromium.org s chromeos-wmp@google.com
Tracks the animation smoothness for the fade-out animation of tab hover card.

histogram.name: Chrome.UmaPageloadCounter
histogram.summary: Records when a page load happens, based on the same logic as the PageLoad user action.

histogram.name: ChromeColors.AppliedColor
histogram.summary: Records applied color id every time its applied from Colors menu.

histogram.name: ChromeColors.ChangesConfirmed
histogram.summary: Records whether color/theme changes were confirmed or reverted by ChromeColorsService.

histogram.name: ChromeColors.ColorOnLoad
histogram.summary: Records installed color id every time theme service is initialized from prefs. Happens once per profile load.

histogram.name: ChromeColors.RevertReason
histogram.summary: Records the reason color/theme changes were reverted by ChromeColorsService.

histogram.name: ChromeElf.ApplyHookResult
histogram.summary: Records the NTSTATUS result that was returned when attempting to hook NtMapViewOfSection during chrome_elf.dll initialization. Emitted asynchronously shortly after startup.

histogram.name: ChromeElf.Beacon.RetryAttemptsBeforeSuccess
histogram.summary: Records the number of attempts needed before third-party DLL blocking was properly set up. This is logged immediately after a successful setup. Only recorded on Windows.

histogram.name: ChromeElf.Beacon.SetupStatus
histogram.summary: Records the successes and failures when running the third-party DLL blocking setup code, taking into account the safety beacon. Used to determine how often the third-party DLL blocking is disabled because it failed to initialize properly twice in a row. Only recorded on Windows.

histogram.name: ChromeElf.ThirdPartyStatus
histogram.summary: Records the different status codes that are emitted by chrome_elf.dll while initializing the NtMapViewOfSection hook used to blocked third-party DLLs. Multiple status codes can be emitted per initialization since some of those status codes represent non-fatal failures. E.g. Failure to clear the previous failure codes. This also means that duplicates are possible. This is recorded shortly after Chrome launches, since chrome_elf.dll doesn't have access to the //base API.

histogram.name: ChromeGeneratedCustomTab.IntentToFirstCommitNavigationTime
histogram.summary: Time between the intent arrival in a Chrome generated custom tab and the first navigation commit, if the navigation is successful. Similar in principle to Startup.FirstCommitNavigationTime.

histogram.name: ChromeGeneratedCustomTab.IntentToFirstCommitNavigationTime2
histogram.summary: In "Herb" mode shows the time between the intent arrival in a Chrome generated custom tab and the first navigation start (note: not the navigation commit, as the name suggests). Recorded when the page has finished loading.

histogram.name: ChromeGeneratedCustomTab.IntentToFirstNavigationStartTime
histogram.summary: For Custom Tabs generated by Chrome itself instead of external apps, this shows the time between the intent arrival in the CCT and the first navigation start. Recorded when the page has finished loading.

histogram.name: ChromeGeneratedCustomTab.IntentToPageLoadedTime
histogram.summary: For Custom Tabs generated by Chrome itself instead of external apps, this shows time between the intent arrival in the CCT and the first "page loaded" event, if the navigation is successful.

histogram.name: ChromeNotifierService.Actions
histogram.obsolete: Removed 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The actions to enable or disable services sending synced notifications. Synced Notification Sending services can be individually disabled by the user in the Chrome Notification center settings dialog.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Apps.ExternalProtocolDialog
histogram.summary: Records the response when the intent picker is shown to the user on Chrome OS to allow them to open an external protocol in an app.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Apps.ExternalProtocolDialog.Accepted
histogram.summary: Records the response when users that are shown the intent picker accept opening an external protocol in an app as well as whether the app is persisted.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Apps.ExternalProtocolDialog.Rejected
histogram.summary: Records the response when users that are shown the intent picker reject opening an external protocol in an app.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Apps.IntentPickerAction
histogram.summary: Records the response when the intent picker is shown to the user on Chrome OS to allow them to open a URL in an app.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Apps.IntentPickerDestinationPlatform
histogram.summary: Records the app platform chosen (or Chrome if the user did not choose an app) when the intent picker is shown to the user on Chrome OS. The destination may be specified due to the user explicit selection or a previously stored preference.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Apps.NumberOfAppsForNotification
histogram.summary: Records whether a notification matches multiple apps, when there is one or multiple web apps matching a web page nonpersistent notification.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Apps.OpenBrowser
histogram.summary: Records the type of app sending a link to the browser to be opened.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Camera.ConfigureStreams.Output.Resolution
histogram.summary: Records the resolution of output stream that is configured by Chrome OS camera service.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Camera.ConfigureStreamsLatency
histogram.summary:
Records the process time of ConfigureStreams() method in Chrome OS camera service.
This metric is reported for all users and will not cause abnormal distribution since the metric is Chrome OS specified.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Camera.ErrorType
histogram.summary: Records the type of the error which triggers the Notify() method in Chrome OS camera service.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Camera.Facing
histogram.summary: Records the camera facing of the camera session in Chrome OS camera service.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Camera.Jpeg.Latency
histogram.summary:
Records the process time of the JDA (Jpeg Decode Accelerator) or JEA (Jpeg Encode Accelerator) run on Chrome OS camera service. There are suffixes that record the runner method (hardware/software), the runner type (decode/encode).
This metric is reported for all users and will not cause abnormal distribution since the metric is Chrome OS specified.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Camera.Jpeg.Resolution
histogram.summary: Records the resolution of the image that JDA/JEA process by Chrome OS camera service. The resolution represents by the total pixels contained in the image. There are suffixes that record the runner method (hardware/software), the runner type (decode/encode).

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Camera.OpenDeviceLatency
histogram.summary:
Records the process time of OpenDevice() method in Chrome OS camera service.
This metric is reported for all users and will not cause abnormal distribution since the metric is Chrome OS specified.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Camera.SessionDuration
histogram.summary: Records the session duration in Chrome OS camera service.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.CertProvisioning.CsrSignTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time it took to sign one CSR by certificate provisioning worker.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.CertProvisioning.Event
histogram.summary: Number of times each event is reached during certificate provisioning flow.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.CertProvisioning.KeypairGenerationTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time it took to generate one key pair by certificate provisioning worker.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.CertProvisioning.Result
histogram.summary: For final states (see the list in cert_provisioning::IsFinalState): number of times certificate provisioning worker finished on every state. For other states: number of times the worker failed on every state.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.CertProvisioning.VaTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time it took to build one Verified Access response by certificate provisioning worker.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.CWP.CollectHeap
histogram.obsolete: Removed 01/2020 in Issue 1036859 with the removal of the heap collection code.
histogram.summary: A count of successes and various failure modes related to the collection and processing of Chrome heap sample profiles on Chrome OS.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.CWP.CollectPerf
histogram.summary: A count of successes and various failure modes related to collecting and processing profile data obtained via "perf events" on Chrome OS.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.CWP.CollectProcessTypes
histogram.summary: Record the collection status (success or failure) when the Chrome process and thread types are collected as part of the ChromeOS perf profile collection.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.CWP.JankinessTriggerStatus
histogram.summary: Records the status of collection attempts triggered by jankiness on Chrome OS.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.CWP.ParseCPUFrequencies
histogram.summary: A count of successes and various failure modes related to the parsing of logical CPU frequencies on Chrome OS. Recorded when the browser process starts.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.CWP.UploadHeap
histogram.obsolete: Removed 01/2020 in Issue 1036859 with the removal of the heap collection code.
histogram.summary: Records the number of "heap" reports included in an UMA upload on Chrome OS.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.CWP.UploadPerf
histogram.summary: Records the number of "perf events" reports included in an UMA upload on Chrome OS.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.DetachableBase.PairResult
histogram.summary: Result of a Chrome OS detachable base pair operation.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.DetachableBase.ROUpdateMetric
histogram.summary: Result of a Chrome OS detachable base RO firmware update.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.DetachableBase.RWUpdateMetric
histogram.summary: Result of a Chrome OS detachable base RW firmware update.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Display.ColorProfile
histogram.summary: The name of the current color calibration of the display on Chrome OS. This value is sent when the color calibration is changed by the user.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.GAIA.AuthenticatorContentWindowNull
histogram.summary: Whether the GAIA Authenticator unexpectedly encountered a null contentWindow value while handling WebView's onContentLoad event. This metric is only enabled for AuthMode = DEFAULT, which indicates ChromeOS online login. This is the only mode where the issue has been observed. Other modes not captured here are OFFLINE (offline ChromeOS login) and DESKTOP (for online GAIA login on desktop Chrome).

histogram.name: ChromeOS.GAIA.WebViewFlow
histogram.obsolete: Removed in 2020/06 as the histogram is no longer in use. The instrumentation code is long gone. The iframe flow is not used since 2015.
histogram.summary: Whether a user signed in using the new WebView-based GAIA flow. This value is sent after the GAIA screen has completed user authentication.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.HardwareVerifier.Report
histogram.summary: Qualification status of each component types. This entry is generated by hardware_verifier.conf at boot time.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.HardwareVerifier.Report.IsCompliant
