histogram.summary: Aggregated result of hardware verifier check.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.HardwareVerifier.TimeToFinish
histogram.summary: The amount of time it takes to finish one hardware verification run.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.HardwareVerifier.TimeToProbe
histogram.summary: The amount of time it takes to probe hardware components.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.MachineIdRegen.AgeSeconds
histogram.summary: The number of seconds of uptime since the last time the Chrome OS machine-id was regenerated. This is reported every time the machine-id is regenerated except for the first time since reboot.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.MachineIdRegen.Reason
histogram.summary: The reason why the Chrome OS machine-id was regenerated. This is reported when the machine-id is re-generated.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.MemoryPressureLevel
histogram.summary: The memory pressure level in Chrome OS, which is recorded periodically (once per second). This is used to show the relative frequency of the system being in a critical vs relaxed memory pressure state.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.MessageCenter.ScrollActionReason
histogram.summary: The cause of the scroll event by the user on the message center. Only the first event after the message center opens is recorded. For example, the user opens the message center and scrolls by mouse then by key, only the mouse event is recorded. If the user closes and reopens the message center before the second event, both events are recorded.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.PlatformVerification.Available
histogram.summary: Whether platform verification subsystem features are available at the time a platform verification request is made for content protection on Chrome OS.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.PlatformVerification.ExpiryStatus
histogram.obsolete: Removed in 2019/08 as the histogram is no longer in use. (crbug/975047)
histogram.summary: The result of a certificate expiry check during a platform verification attempt for content protection on Chrome OS.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.PlatformVerification.Result
histogram.summary: The result of a platform verification attempt for content protection on Chrome OS.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.SAML.APILogin
histogram.summary: Records every online login authenticated purely with GAIA or with SAML.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.SAML.APIUsed
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 10/2019. Replaced with ChromeOS.SAML.APILogin that correctly records various login flows.
histogram.summary: Whether a Chrome OS login via SAML used the principals API. This is recorded during login on Chrome OS if SAML is being used for authentication.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.SAML.InSessionPasswordChangeEvent
histogram.summary: Records how often users are guided through the SAML in-session password change flow, and how often it is completed succesfully.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.SAML.Provider
histogram.summary: Records SAML provider when SAML login flow is used.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.SAML.SamlChallengeKeyHandlerResult
histogram.summary: Tracks results of calculating response for a challenge from Verified Access server for remove attestation during SAML authentication.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.SAML.Scraping.PasswordCount
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 10/2019. Replaced with ChromeOS.SAML.Scraping.PasswordCountAll that fixes the main case of a single scraped password.
histogram.summary: The number of passwords that were scraped during a Chrome OS login via SAML. This is set only when the principals API is not used.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.SAML.Scraping.PasswordCountAll
histogram.summary: The number of passwords that were scraped during a Chrome OS login via SAML. This is set only when the Chrome Credentials Passing API is not used.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.SAML.Scraping.VerificationResult
histogram.summary: Whether one of the scraped passwords was successfully verified as the user's password. This is set only when the Chrome Credentials Passing API is not used.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Settings.BlurredWindowDuration
histogram.summary: The amount of time between when the settings window becomes blurred and when the user focuses it again.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Settings.BrowserBannerInteraction
histogram.summary: Records whether the "try your Chrome browser settings" banner was shown when the user opened a new Chrome OS settings window and also records interactions with the banner.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Settings.LoadCompletedTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time between the render frame host StartProvisionalLoad event and the render frame DocumentOnLoadCompleted event for the Chrome OS settings page.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Settings.LoadDocumentTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time between the render frame host StartProvisionalLoad and DidFinishDocumentLoad events for the Chrome OS settings page.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Settings.NumCharsOfQueries
histogram.summary: The number of characters for queries used to fetch results from the Settings Search Mojo API.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Settings.NumClicksUntilChange
histogram.summary: The number of clicks before changing a setting.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Settings.NumNavigationsUntilChange
histogram.summary: The number of navigations before changing a setting.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Settings.NumSearchesUntilChange
histogram.summary: The number of searches before changing a setting.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Settings.NumSearchResultsFetched
histogram.summary: The number of search results fetched each time a successful search request is made to the Settings Search Mojo API.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Settings.OsBannerInteraction
histogram.summary: Records whether the "try your Chrome OS settings" banner was shown when the user opened a new Chrome browser settings tab and also records interactions with the banner.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Settings.PathVisited
histogram.summary: Paths visited within chrome://os-settings. For evaluating popularity and priorities for OS Settings UI.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Settings.SearchLatency
histogram.summary: The time fetching search results from the Settings Search Mojo API. Recorded when getSearchHandler().search() completes.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Settings.SearchRequests
histogram.summary: The number of search requests made to the Settings Search Mojo API. For search requests that succeeded with a response, the number of search requests that had results which were discarded, and the number of search requests that were shown to the user. Recorded when getSearchHandler().search() completes.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Settings.SearchRequestsPerSession
histogram.summary: The number of search requests made to the Settings Search Mojo API in one session of the settings app.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Settings.SearchResultSectionSelected
histogram.summary: Section search results clicked by user in the OS settings search box.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Settings.SearchResultSettingSelected
histogram.summary: Setting search results clicked by user in the OS settings search box.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Settings.SearchResultSubpageSelected
histogram.summary: Subpage search results clicked by user in the OS settings search box.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Settings.SearchResultTypeSelected
histogram.summary: Type of search results clicked by user in the OS settings search box.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Settings.TimeUntilChange
histogram.summary: The time spent on the settings page before changing a setting.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Settings.UserActionOnSearchResultsShown
histogram.summary: The user action taken when search results exist and are displayed.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Settings.WindowOpenDuration
histogram.summary: The amount of time between when a Chrome OS settings window is opened and when it is closed.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.Sync.PreferencesMigrated
histogram.summary: Tracks whether browser sync preferences were migrated to the new OS sync feature (project SplitSettingsSync). Recorded once per session during login.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.SystemTray.AnimationSmoothness
histogram.summary: Tracks the animation smoothness for the collapse / expand animation of the system tray.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.SystemTray.BlockedNotifiersAfterUpdate
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of blocked notifiers in the system tray after the user blocks one or multiple notifiers. Note that this metric records the number of blocked notifiers *after* the update.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.SystemTray.BlockedNotifiersOnOpen
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of blocked notifiers in the system tray when it is opened.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.SystemTray.FeaturePodCountOnOpen
histogram.summary: The number of feature pods (e.g. WiFi, Bluetooth, IME, Accessibility, etc.) in the system tray quick settings when it is opened, including pods that overflow to other pages. See ChromeOS.SystemTray.Tablet.FeaturePodCountOnOpen for the corresponding histogram logged in tablet mode.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.SystemTray.FirstInteraction
histogram.summary: Records the first interaction the user has with the system tray, which can be a click/tap on the quick settings or message center bubble. Note that this interaction could be at any time after the user opens the tray (e.g. 30 seconds later before the first click).

histogram.name: ChromeOS.SystemTray.Interaction
histogram.summary: An enum value how system tray bubble is interacted e.g. by tap (touch screen), or click (mouse, trackpad, etc.) Reported every time the region inside system tray bubble is tapped or clicked.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.SystemTray.IsExpandedOnOpen
histogram.summary: If the value is true, SystemTray is expanded when it's opened. Otherwise, it's closed when it's opened.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.SystemTray.NotificationsRemovedByClearAll
histogram.summary: Records the number of notifications that are removed in a single clear all action by the user.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.SystemTray.OpenHelpPageForManaged
histogram.summary: Type of the support page which user opened by clicking the indicator of managed device type in the unified system tray bubble.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.SystemTray.Tablet.FeaturePodCountOnOpen
histogram.summary: The number of feature pods (e.g. WiFi, Bluetooth, IME, Accessibility, etc.) in the system tray quick settings when it is opened, including pods that overflow to other pages. This histogram is only logged when the device is in tablet mode, otherwise ChromeOS.SystemTray.FeaturePodCountOnOpen is logged.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.SystemTray.TimeToClick
histogram.summary: The amount of time a user took from clicking on the button in status area, to clicking on an item in the system tray bubble.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.SystemTray.ToggleExpanded
histogram.summary: An enum value how system tray bubble is expanded or collapsed. It can be toggled by the button and touch gesture.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.UrlXattrsCount
histogram.summary: The number of files with URL extended attributes found in the users' home directories. This is emitted once per boot. See crbug.com/919486 and crbug.com/939386 for more details.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.USB.DeviceAttached
histogram.summary: Timing and number of USB devices attached.

histogram.name: ChromeOS.UserAddingScreen.LoadTime
histogram.summary: The time spent before the multiprofile account picker is ready.

histogram.name: ChromiumAndroidLinker.BrowserLoadTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time it took to load the native libraries in the browser process.

histogram.name: ChromiumAndroidLinker.BrowserStates
histogram.summary: Whether relro sharing was attempted for the browser process, and if attempted, whether it succeeded.

histogram.name: ChromiumAndroidLinker.IsLowMemoryDevice
histogram.obsolete: Removed 10/2014 in Issue 419010, and replaced by ChromiumAndroidLinker.BrowserStates.
histogram.summary: Is low memory device.

histogram.name: ChromiumAndroidLinker.LibraryLoadFromApkStatus
histogram.summary: Whether the device supports loading a library directly from the APK file. The functionality is checked and reported during every Chromium browser process start up.

histogram.name: ChromiumAndroidLinker.LoadedAtFixedAddressFailed
histogram.obsolete: Removed 10/2014 in Issue 419010, and replaced by ChromiumAndroidLinker.RendererStates.
histogram.summary: Load at fixed address failed.

histogram.name: ChromiumAndroidLinker.LoadLibraryStatus
histogram.summary: Status of each attempt to load the native library with a custom linker. Recorded after each attempt to load. Not recorded when all attempts fail in a process.

histogram.name: ChromiumAndroidLinker.RelinkerFallbackCount
histogram.summary: The total number of times Chrome uses relinker fallback to extract and load native libraries.

histogram.name: ChromiumAndroidLinker.RendererLoadTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time it took to load the native libraries in the renderer process.

histogram.name: ChromiumAndroidLinker.RendererStates
histogram.summary: Whether relro sharing was attempted for a renderer process, and if attempted, whether it succeeded.

histogram.name: Chromoting.Connections
histogram.summary: Recorded every time a Chromoting connection enters a new state.

histogram.name: Chromoting.Connections.Durations.Closed
histogram.summary: The duration of sessions that were closed, normally by a user-initiated action.

histogram.name: Chromoting.Connections.Durations.Failed
histogram.summary: The duration of sessions that failed to connect.

histogram.name: Chromoting.Connections.Times.ToAuthenticate
histogram.summary: The time taken to authenticate as part of a Chromoting connection request.

histogram.name: Chromoting.Connections.Times.ToConnect
histogram.summary: The time taken to connect to a remote-host, after a request has been authenticated.

histogram.name: Chromoting.Input.EventLatency
histogram.summary:
Input event delivery latency from the browser to the plugin.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Chromoting.Video.Bandwidth
histogram.summary: The bandwidth of non-empty packets in Chromoting remote sessions, updated every time a video packet is processed and logged every 1s.

histogram.name: Chromoting.Video.CaptureLatency
histogram.summary: The time to capture a video frame on the host, returned as part of a video packet.

histogram.name: Chromoting.Video.CaptureOverhead
histogram.summary: Amount of time wasted on IPC and threading overhead when capturing a video frame.

histogram.name: Chromoting.Video.CapturePendingLatency
histogram.summary: The time from when an input event is received to when the next frame is captured.

histogram.name: Chromoting.Video.DecodeLatency
histogram.summary: The time to decode a video packet on the client.

histogram.name: Chromoting.Video.EncodeLatency
histogram.summary: The time to encode a video packet on the host, returned as part of a video packet.

histogram.name: Chromoting.Video.EncodePendingLatency
histogram.summary: Delay before a newly captured frame is passed to the encoder.

histogram.name: Chromoting.Video.FrameRate
histogram.summary: The frame-rate of non-empty packets in Chromoting remote sessions, updated every time a video packet is processed and logged every 1s.

histogram.name: Chromoting.Video.NetworkLatency
histogram.summary: Estimated network roundtrip latency calculated by subtracting total processing time on host and client from the total roundtrip time.

histogram.name: Chromoting.Video.PacketRate
histogram.summary: The packets/sec, including empty packets, in Chromoting remote sessions, updated every time a video packet is processed and logged every 1s.

histogram.name: Chromoting.Video.PaintLatency
histogram.summary: The time to paint a decoded video packet on the client.

histogram.name: Chromoting.Video.RoundTripLatency
histogram.summary: The time from an input being sent from the client to a response being received by the client after it is processed by the host.

histogram.name: Chromoting.Video.SendPendingLatency
histogram.summary: Delay before an encoded video packet is written to the send buffer.

histogram.name: clickjacking.discard_download
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The length of time between a dangerous download appearing on the downloads shelf, and the "Discard" button being clicked.

histogram.name: clickjacking.dismiss_download
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The length of time between a dangerous download appearing on the downloads shelf, and the "Dismiss" button being clicked. Deprecated since M54.

histogram.name: clickjacking.launch_url
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The length of time between the external protocol dialog being shown and the "Launch Application" button being clicked.

histogram.name: clickjacking.open_download
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The length of time between a download appearing on the download shelf, and the user opening it by clicking the item or pressing return.

histogram.name: clickjacking.report_and_discard_download
histogram.obsolete: Removed 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Time between "Report and Discard" button being shown and it being clicked.

histogram.name: clickjacking.save_download
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The length of time between a dangerous download appearing on the download shelf, and the "Keep" button being clicked.

histogram.name: ClientHints.CountRulesReceived
histogram.summary: Count of client hints that were received by the render process. Recorded approximately at the time of the renderer startup.

histogram.name: ClientHints.PersistDuration
histogram.summary: Duration for which the origin requested the client hints to be persisted. Collected on the renderer side.

histogram.name: ClientHints.UpdateEventCount
histogram.summary: Count of events when the client hints needs to be persisted to the disk by the browser.

histogram.name: ClientHints.UpdateSize
histogram.summary: Count of client hints that need to be persisted to the disk. Recorded by the renderer.

histogram.name: Clipboard.ConstructedHasher
histogram.obsolete: Launched briefly in M-59 dev, then refactoring made obsolete.
histogram.summary: Whether Android's Clipboard.java successfully constructed a hasher to hash clipboard entries. Recorded on construction of the class, which happens only on startup.

histogram.name: Clipboard.IncognitoUseCase
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 4/2013, experiment confirmed correctness of our patch.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Counts how often the user writes or reads from the clipboard and whether the write was from an incognito window or not.

histogram.name: Clipboard.RegisterClipboardFormatFailure
histogram.summary: Counts how often the Windows clipboard fails to register a format. Recorded when RegisterClipbaordFormat fails (returns 0). Failure rates will inform whether we implement error handling. https://crbug.com/1000919

histogram.name: Clipboard.X11StoreCopyPasteDuration
histogram.summary: The length of time that it takes to transfer ownership of Chrome's CLIPBOARD selection to the clipboard manager when Chrome exits.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.AuthEvent
histogram.summary: Event counts in CloudPrintAuth.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.AvailablePrinters
histogram.summary: The number of printers available for registration.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.AvailablePrintersList
histogram.summary: The number of printers available for registration in Windows Service.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.CapsUpdateInterval
histogram.summary: The amount of time between capabilities updates.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.JobHandlerEvent
histogram.summary: Event counts in PrinterJobHandler.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.JobsDonePerInterval
histogram.summary: The number of jobs successfully completed per hour.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.JobsStartedPerInterval
histogram.summary: The number of jobs started per hour.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.JobStatus
histogram.summary: Then number of job completion statuses.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.NativeJobStatus
histogram.summary: Event counts in PrintSystem.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.PrepareTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time needed to prepare job for spooling.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.PrinterBlacklistSize
histogram.summary: The number of printers user has blacklisted.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.PrinterWhitelistSize
histogram.summary: The number of printers user has whitelisted.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.PrintingTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time needed to finish print job.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.ServiceEvents
histogram.summary: Event counts in ServiceProcessControl.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.ServiceProcessSocketLength
histogram.summary: Temporarily histogram with size of IPC sockets in default location.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.ServiceUtilityCapsFailTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time used to fail to collect printer capabilities.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.ServiceUtilityCapsTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time used to collect printer capabilities.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.ServiceUtilityDisconnectTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time the utility process runs before disconnect.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.ServiceUtilityMetafileFailTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time used to fail to generate metafile.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.ServiceUtilityMetafileTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time used to generate metafile.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.ServiceUtilityProcessHostEvent
histogram.summary: Event counts in ServiceUtilityProcessHost.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.ServiceUtilitySemanticCapsFailTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time used to fail to collect printer capabilities.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.ServiceUtilitySemanticCapsTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time used to collect printer capabilities.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.SpoolingTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time needed to spool print job.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.UnregisterPrinters
histogram.summary: The number of printers to unregister.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.UrlFetcherDownloadSize
histogram.summary: The amount of data downloaded on cloud print request.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.UrlFetcherRequestTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time needed for cloud print request.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.UrlFetcherRequestType
histogram.summary: Request counts to cloud print service.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.UrlFetcherRetries
histogram.summary: The number of retries used to complete cloud print request.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.UrlFetcherUploadSize
histogram.summary: The amount of data uploaded with cloud print request.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.XmppPingTry
histogram.summary: Number of tries before successful ping. 99 means giving up.

histogram.name: CloudPrint.XmppTimeout
histogram.summary: Xmpp timeout option value provided by server.

histogram.name: ComponentUpdater.Calls
histogram.summary: The number of times the component updater called UpdateClient::Install or UpdateClient::Update. These correspond to the number of manual component update checks performed as a result of a user action, and the number of automated component update checks.

histogram.name: ComponentUpdater.ChromeOS.InstallResult
histogram.summary: Chrome OS only. Installation error code in CrosComponentManager.

histogram.name: ComponentUpdater.ChromeOS.MountTime
histogram.summary: Chrome OS only. Time it takes to mount a component image.

histogram.name: ComponentUpdater.UpdateCompleteError
histogram.summary: The result of an install or an update check.

histogram.name: ComponentUpdater.UpdateCompleteResult
histogram.summary: The result of an install or an update check.

histogram.name: ComponentUpdater.UpdateCompleteTime
histogram.summary: Time to complete an Install or an Update component update call.

histogram.name: CompositeDocToPdf.Status
histogram.summary: Tracks the status of PDF document composition and generation conducted in PDF compositor service.

histogram.name: CompositePageToPdf.Status
histogram.summary: Tracks the status of PDF page composition and generation conducted in PDF compositor service.

histogram.name: Compositing.Browser.CachedImagesCount
histogram.obsolete: Removed 05/2019 because it no longer reports. To be superseded by a measurement related to percentage of cache/discardable memory used by image caching.
histogram.summary: The maximum number of images that were cached in the browser over the lifetime of the image decode cache. This is recorded at the image cache destruction. It is further split up by Software and Gpu rasterization.

histogram.name: Compositing.Browser.CompositorFrame.Quads
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2019 because the research it was used for is complete.
histogram.summary: The total number of quads in all render passes in a CompositorFrame. This is logged as the CompositorFrame is submitted to its surface.

histogram.name: Compositing.Browser.DisplayListRecordingSource.UpdateInvalidatedAreaPerMs
histogram.obsolete: Removed 05/2016. See Blink.Paint.UpdateTime as a related alternate metric.
histogram.summary: Area of invalidated content, in pixels, divided by update (i.e. record), in milliseconds. Recorded when display list recording source is updated (in a browser process).

histogram.name: Compositing.Browser.DisplayListRecordingSource.UpdateUs
histogram.obsolete: Removed 05/2016. See Blink.Paint.UpdateTime as a related alternate metric.
histogram.summary: Time spent updating (i.e. recording) a display list, in microseconds. Recorded when display list is updated (in a browser process).

histogram.name: Compositing.Browser.DrawResult
histogram.obsolete: Removed 10/2018. This metric didn't end up being useful.
histogram.summary: How often the layer compositor successfully submitted content to the display compositor, or what caused an abort. This is reported fairly late in the pipeline, during the final stages of submitting content.

histogram.name: Compositing.Browser.GPUMemoryForTilingsInKb
histogram.summary:
The GPU memory allocated for tilings (in a browser process), summed over all picture layers. Emitted once per drawing. Nothing is recorded if there are no picture layers.
Tilings are used for decomposition of the layer's entire contents at a picture screenspace resolution to do threaded rasterization.
Team: paint-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Compositing.Browser.HitTestTimeToFindClosestLayer
histogram.summary:
Time spent finding the closest matching layer to a given point whenever we do hit testing on LayerTreeImpl (in a browser process).
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Compositing.Browser.LayersUpdateTime
histogram.summary:
Time spent updating layers, in microseconds. Recorded when layers are updated (in a browser process).
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Compositing.Browser.LayerTreeImpl.CalculateDrawPropertiesUs
histogram.summary:
Time spent updating layer draw properties, in microseconds. Recorded when a LayerTreeImpl's draw properties are updated (in a browser process).
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Compositing.Browser.NumActiveLayers
histogram.summary: The number of layers in the active tree for each compositor frame. This is logged once per frame, before the frame is drawn (in a browser process).

histogram.name: Compositing.Browser.NumActivePictureLayers
histogram.summary: The number of PictureLayers in the active tree for each compositor frame. This is logged once per frame, before the frame is drawn (in a browser process).

histogram.name: Compositing.Browser.NumRenderSurfaces
histogram.summary: The number of render surfaces in the render surface layer list. Recorded each time the render surface layer list is constructed (in a browser process).

histogram.name: Compositing.Browser.PictureMemoryUsageKb
histogram.obsolete: Has not produced data for over a year prior to removal in 03/2020.
histogram.summary: Total estimated memory used by SkPictures in the layer tree, in kilobytes. Recorded once per frame, before the frame is drawn (in a browser process).

histogram.name: Compositing.Browser.PicturePile.UpdateInvalidatedAreaPerMs
histogram.summary: Area of invalidated content, in pixels, divided by update (i.e. record), in milliseconds. Recorded when picture pile is updated (in a browser process).

histogram.name: Compositing.Browser.PicturePile.UpdateUs
histogram.summary:
Time spent updating (i.e. recording) a picture pile, in microseconds. Recorded when picture pile is updated (in a browser process).
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Compositing.Browser.RasterTask.RasterPixelsPerMs
histogram.obsolete: Removed 11/2016. Increased the max tracking range to accomendate GPU raster, and will require new baskets.
histogram.summary: Rasterized area, in pixels, divided by rasterization time, in milliseconds, of a compositor rasterization task. Recorded after the task finishes (in a browser process).

histogram.name: Compositing.Browser.RasterTask.RasterPixelsPerMs2
histogram.summary: Rasterized area, in pixels, divided by rasterization time, in milliseconds, of a compositor rasterization task. Recorded after the task finishes (in a browser process).

histogram.name: Compositing.Browser.RasterTask.RasterUs
histogram.summary:
Time spent completing a compositor rasterization task, in microseconds. Recorded after the task finishes (in a browser process).
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Compositing.Browser.RenderPass.AppendQuadData.NumMissingTilesNoImageContent
histogram.obsolete: Removed 02/2016. We'd learned as much as we could from this metric and it made no sense to keep it around.
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of missing tiles that had some image content in the browser process.

histogram.name: Compositing.Browser.RenderPass.AppendQuadData.NumMissingTilesSomeImageContent
histogram.obsolete: Removed 02/2016. We'd learned as much as we could from this metric and it made no sense to keep it around.
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of missing tiles that did not have any image content in the browser process.

histogram.name: Compositing.CompositorFrameSinkSupport.ShouldSendBeginFrame
histogram.summary: Recorded for each OnBeginFrame() message that could potentially be sent to a CompositorFrameSinkClient. The value indicates if the message was sent to the client and why it was/wasn't sent.

histogram.name: Compositing.CompositorFrameSinkSupport.SubmitResult
histogram.summary: The result of submitting a CompositorFrame to a CompositorFrameSink. ACCEPTED means the frame was accepted by the CompositorFrameSink. COPY_OUTPUT_REQUESTS_NOT_ALLOWED means an unprivileged client attempted to readback a texture. SURFACE_INVARIANTS_VIOLATION means size or device scale factor changed but a new viz::LocalSurfaceId was not allocated.

histogram.name: Compositing.CopyFromSurfaceTime
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The turn around time taken for the async readback of pixels is measured here.

histogram.name: Compositing.CopyFromSurfaceTimeSynchronous
histogram.obsolete: Removed 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The time taken for the sync readback of pixels is measured here.

histogram.name: Compositing.DirectRenderer.GL.DrawFrameUs
histogram.summary:
Time spent drawing of composited layers by GLRenderer, in microseconds. This is logged once per frame, when a frame should be drawn.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Compositing.DirectRenderer.PartialSwap.ExtraDamage
histogram.summary:
The area of the display damage that was added to current frame damage after all adjustments, including FrameBufferDamage from buffer queue and render pass filters.
This metric is reported once per frame only on platforms/devices that support partial swap and does not include "empty swap" (no frame damage) case.

histogram.name: Compositing.DirectRenderer.PartialSwap.FrameBufferDamage
histogram.summary:
The area of the display that was damaged since previous draw to this frame buffer.
This metric is reported once per frame only on platforms/devices that support partial swap and does not include "empty swap" (no frame damage) case.

histogram.name: Compositing.DirectRenderer.PartialSwap.RootDamage
histogram.summary:
The area of the display that was damaged in current frame.
This metric is reported only on platforms/devices that support partial swap and does not include "empty swap" (no frame damage) case.

histogram.name: Compositing.DirectRenderer.PartialSwap.TotalDamage
histogram.summary:
The total area of the display that will be redrawn this frame.
This metric is reported only on platforms/devices that support partial swap and does not include "empty swap" (no frame damage) case.

histogram.name: Compositing.DirectRenderer.Software.DrawFrameUs
histogram.summary:
Time spent drawing of composited layers by SoftwareRenderer, in microseconds. This is logged once per frame, when a frame should be drawn.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Compositing.DirectRenderer.VK.DrawFrameUs
histogram.summary:
Time spent drawing of composited layers by SkiaRenderer with Vulkan backend, in microseconds. This is logged once per frame, when a frame should be drawn.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Compositing.Display.Draw.Occlusion.Calculation.Time
histogram.summary:
Time spent to remove invisible quads from the quad_list in CompositorFrame.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Compositing.Display.Draw.Occlusion.Drawing.Area.Saved
histogram.summary: It records the total drawing area skipped to show on screen as a result of applying draw occlusion. This is logged once per frame, when the frame is drawn.

histogram.name: Compositing.Display.Draw.Occlusion.Drawing.Area.Saved2
histogram.summary: It records the total drawing area skipped to show on screen as a result of applying draw occlusion. This is logged once per frame, when the frame is drawn.

histogram.name: Compositing.Display.Draw.Occlusion.Percentage.Saved
histogram.summary: It records the percentage of total area drawing skipped by draw occlusion of the total area of damage rects in a compositor frame. This is logged once per frame, when the frame is drawn.

histogram.name: Compositing.Display.Draw.Quads
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2019 because the research it was used for is complete.
histogram.summary: This is logged once per frame, when the frame is drawn. The total number of quads in all render passes in the CompositorFrame that is produced by surface aggregation.

histogram.name: Compositing.Display.Draw.Quads.With.Complex.Transform.Area
histogram.obsolete: Removed 03/2018. We'd learned enough from this metric and it made no sense to keep it around.
histogram.summary: This is logged once per frame, when the frame is drawn. Captures area of all draw quads that need to go through no scale and no translation transforms.

histogram.name: Compositing.Display.DrawToSwapUs
histogram.summary: This is logged once per frame, if the output surface provides timing information. It measures the time from the display compositor starting a draw on the Viz Compositor thread to issuing all related draw calls to the driver on the Gpu Main thread. Only reported for platforms supporting high resolution clocks.

histogram.name: Compositing.Display.DrmOverlayManager.CacheHit
histogram.summary: Indicates whether DrmOverlayManager was able to find the overlay configuration in its cache. Recorded when DrmOverlayManager is asked to verify an overlay configuration.

histogram.name: Compositing.Display.DrmOverlayManager.TotalTestBufferMemorySize
histogram.summary: The amount of memory allocated for the purpose of performing a pageflip test. Recorded every time DRM thread is asked to validate an overlay configuration.

histogram.name: Compositing.Display.FlattenedRenderPassCount
histogram.summary: This is logged once per frame. It is the number of renderer passes after flattening all possible render passes. If the value is more than one, then an intermediate rendering target must be used during the rendering of the frame for each render pass greater than one.

histogram.name: Compositing.Display.OverlayProcessorOzone.IsCandidateSharedImage
histogram.summary: Indicates whether the OverlayCandidate passed to OverlayCandidateValidatorOzone is backed by a SharedImage (as opposed to a legacy mailbox). Recorded for every candidate in every overlay configuration passed to OverlayProcessorOzone.

histogram.name: Compositing.Display.OverlayProcessorOzone.SharedImageExists
histogram.summary: Indicates whether the SharedImage corresponding to the OverlayCandidate was found. Reported for every OverlayCandidate that is backed by a SharedImage.

histogram.name: Compositing.DisplayListRecordingSource.UpdateInvalidatedAreaPerMs
histogram.obsolete: Removed 09/2015, replaced by similar metrics under Compositing.Renderer and Compositing.Browser, depending on which process it occurs in.
histogram.summary: Area of invalidated content, in pixels, divided by update (i.e. record), in milliseconds. Recorded when display list recording source is updated.

histogram.name: Compositing.DisplayListRecordingSource.UpdateUs
histogram.obsolete: Removed 09/2015, replaced by similar metrics under Compositing.Renderer and Compositing.Browser, depending on which process it occurs in.
histogram.summary: Time spent updating (i.e. recording) a display list, in microseconds. Recorded when display list is updated.

histogram.name: Compositing.LayerTreeImpl.FindClosestMatchingLayerUs
histogram.obsolete: Removed 05/2017 in Issue 710306. Histogram was used temporarily for tracking a potential performance regression.
histogram.summary: Time spent finding the closest matching layer to a given point, in microseconds. Recorded each time we do hit testing on LayerTreeImpl.

histogram.name: Compositing.MainFrameSynchronization.Duration
histogram.summary: The amount of time it took for a main frame with unresolved dependencies to activate. Note that some activations may be triggered due to a deadline hitting.

histogram.name: Compositing.NumActiveLayers
histogram.obsolete: Removed 04/2016, replaced by similar metrics under Compositing.Renderer and Compositing.Browser, depending on which process it occurs in.
histogram.summary: The number of layers in the active tree for each compositor frame. This is logged once per frame, before the frame is drawn.

histogram.name: Compositing.PictureMemoryUsageKb
histogram.obsolete: Removed 09/2015, replaced by similar metrics under Compositing.Renderer and Compositing.Browser, depending on which process it occurs in.
histogram.summary: Total estimated memory used by SkPictures in the layer tree, in kilobytes. Recorded once per frame, before the frame is drawn.

histogram.name: Compositing.PicturePile.UpdateInvalidatedAreaPerMs
histogram.obsolete: Removed 09/2015, replaced by similar metrics under Compositing.Renderer and Compositing.Browser, depending on which process it occurs in.
histogram.summary: Area of invalidated content, in pixels, divided by update (i.e. record), in milliseconds. Recorded when picture pile is updated.

histogram.name: Compositing.PicturePile.UpdateUs
histogram.obsolete: Removed 09/2015, replaced by similar metrics under Compositing.Renderer and Compositing.Browser, depending on which process it occurs in.
histogram.summary: Time spent updating (i.e. recording) a picture pile, in microseconds. Recorded when picture pile is updated.

histogram.name: Compositing.RasterTask.RasterPixelsPerMs
histogram.obsolete: Removed 09/2015, replaced by similar metrics under Compositing.Renderer and Compositing.Browser, depending on which process it occurs in.
histogram.summary: Rasterized area, in pixels, divided by rasterization time, in milliseconds, of a compositor rasterization task. Recorded after the task finishes.

histogram.name: Compositing.RasterTask.RasterUs
histogram.obsolete: Removed 09/2015, replaced by similar metrics under Compositing.Renderer and Compositing.Browser, depending on which process it occurs in.
histogram.summary: Time spent completing a compositor rasterization task, in microseconds. Recorded after the task finishes.

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.CachedImagesCount
histogram.obsolete: Removed 05/2019 because it no longer reports. To be superseded by a measurement related to percentage of cache/discardable memory used by image caching.
histogram.summary: The maximum number of images that were cached in the renderer over the lifetime of the image decode cache. This is recorded at the image cache destruction. It is further split up by Software and Gpu rasterization.

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.CALayerResult
histogram.summary: The outcome of attempting to replace all renderer tiles with CALayers. Either success, or the first reason why a DrawQuad couldn't be replaced by a CALayer.

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.CheckerImagingDecision
histogram.summary: The outcome for whether an image could be sucessfully deferred for asynchronous decode in the renderer compositor. If not, the reason for using a synchronous decode. An image which is decoded asynchronously will be checkerboarded on the rasterized tiles until the decode is finished, as opposed to synchronous decodes where the tile rasterization is blocked on the image decode completing.

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.CompositorFrame.Quads
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2019 because the research it was used for is complete.
histogram.summary: The total number of quads in all render passes in a CompositorFrame. This is logged as the CompositorFrame is submitted to its surface.

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.DirectlyCompositedImage.AvoidRasterAdjustmentWithTransformTrigger
histogram.summary: Records instances of directly composited images that did not need to recalculate raster scale in response to scale changes. The boolean value indicates whether will-change:transform is set or a scale transforms is animating. This metric will be used to determine the impact on raster CPU usage if we were to scope the directly composited images feature.

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.DirectlyCompositedImage.RasterScaleDirectlyComposited
histogram.summary: Records whether or not a PictureLayerImpl that has a single drawImage paint operation is able to apply directly composited image optimization. See PictureLayerImpl::ShouldDirectlyCompositeImage for more details.

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.DirectlyCompositedImage.TileAreaAdded
histogram.summary: Records the number of tile pixels added when directly composited image is applied to a layer. If the raster scale is greater than it otherwise would be, the difference in pixels used for tiling the layer is logged in this histogram.

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.DirectlyCompositedImage.TileAreaMatches
histogram.summary: Records whether or not the chosen raster scale for a directly composited image matches the ideal raster scale. In cases where the scale does not match (i.e. false values for this histogram), either TileAreaAdded or TileAreaSaved will be logged to provide an indication on the effects on memory usage.

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.DirectlyCompositedImage.TileAreaSaved
histogram.summary: Records the number of tile pixels saved when directly composited image is applied to a layer. If the raster scale is lower than it otherwise would be, the difference in pixels used for tiling the layer is logged in this histogram.

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.DisplayListRecordingSource.UpdateInvalidatedAreaPerMs
histogram.obsolete: Removed 05/2016. See Blink.Paint.UpdateTime as a related alternate metric.
histogram.summary: Area of invalidated content, in pixels, divided by update (i.e. record), in milliseconds. Recorded when display list recording source is updated (in a renderer process).

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.DisplayListRecordingSource.UpdateUs
histogram.obsolete: Removed 05/2016. See Blink.Paint.UpdateTime as a related alternate metric.
histogram.summary: Time spent updating (i.e. recording) a display list, in microseconds. Recorded when display list is updated (in a renderer process).

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.DrawResult
histogram.obsolete: Removed 10/2018. This metric didn't end up being useful.
histogram.summary: How often the layer compositor successfully submitted content to the display compositor, or what caused an abort. This is reported fairly late in the pipeline, during the final stages of submitting content.

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.GPUMemoryForTilingsInKb
histogram.summary:
The GPU memory allocated for tilings (in a renderer process), summed over all picture layers. Emitted once per drawing. Nothing is recorded if there are no picture layers.
Tilings are used for decomposition of the layer's entire contents at a picture screenspace resolution to do threaded rasterization.
Team: paint-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.HitTestTimeToFindClosestLayer
histogram.summary:
Time spent finding the closest matching layer to a given point whenever we do hit testing on LayerTreeImpl (in a renderer process).
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.LayerSkippedForDrawPropertiesDueToBackface
histogram.summary: Records whether layer draw properties updates were skipped due to the layer being back-facing and not being double-sided, but were otherwise going to be updated.

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.LayersUpdateTime
histogram.summary:
Time spent updating layers, in microseconds. Recorded when layers are updated (in a renderer process).
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.LayerTreeImpl.CalculateDrawPropertiesUs
histogram.summary:
Time spent updating layer draw properties, in microseconds. Recorded when a LayerTreeImpl's draw properties are updated (in a renderer process).
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.LayerUpdateSkippedDueToBackface
histogram.summary: Records whether a layer update was skipped due to the layer being back-facing and not being double-sided, but was otherwise going to be updated.

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.LCDTextDisallowedReasonKPixels
histogram.summary: Records the approximate kilo-pixels covered by text drawing operations by LCD-text disallowed reasons. Hence the histogram value for each reason is the approximate number of non-LCD kilo-pixels resulting from that reason. Recorded when the renderer is idle, at minimum interval of the longer of 1 minute and 500 begin frames for each local frame tree.

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.LCDTextDisallowedReasonLayers
histogram.summary: Records the number of layers containing text by LCD-text disallowed reasons. Recorded along with Compositing.Renderer.LCDTextDisallowedReasonKPixels.

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.LCDTextEligiblePixelPercentage
histogram.summary: Records the percentage of approximate number of pixels covered by text drawing operations that are LCD-text eligible, in that of all text drawing operations. This is recorded when the renderer is idle, at minimum interval of the longer of 1 minute and 500 begin frames for each local frame tree. Note that LCD-text eligible text may rasterize in non-LCD-text mode when e.g. LCD-text is not supported by the font or LCD-text doesn't appreciably improve quality.

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.NumActiveLayers
histogram.summary: The number of layers in the active tree for each compositor frame. This is logged once per frame, before the frame is drawn (in a renderer process).

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.NumActivePictureLayers
histogram.summary: The number of PictureLayers in the active tree for each compositor frame. This is logged once per frame, before the frame is drawn (in a renderer process).

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.NumRenderSurfaces
histogram.summary: The number of render surfaces in the render surface layer list. Recorded each time the render surface layer list is constructed (in a renderer process).

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.PercentPictureLayersWithTextButLCDTextDisabled
histogram.obsolete: Removed in 03/2020 because changes in the data are difficult to reason about as the number of layers also tends to change at the same time.
histogram.summary: The number of PictureLayers in the active tree for each compositor frame that have both text drawing operations and do not permit lcd text. This is logged once per frame, before the frame is drawn (in a renderer process). Suffixed with the count of number of picture layers with text.

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.PictureMemoryUsageKb
histogram.obsolete: Removal in 03/2020 because it is not very useful.
histogram.summary: Total estimated memory used by SkPictures in the layer tree, in kilobytes. Recorded once per frame, before the frame is drawn (in a renderer process).

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.PicturePile.UpdateInvalidatedAreaPerMs
histogram.summary: Area of invalidated content, in pixels, divided by update (i.e. record), in milliseconds. Recorded when picture pile is updated (in a renderer process).

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.PicturePile.UpdateUs
histogram.summary:
Time spent updating (i.e. recording) a picture pile, in microseconds. Recorded when picture pile is updated (in a renderer process).
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.RasterTask.RasterPixelsPerMs
histogram.obsolete: Removed 11/2016. Increased the max tracking range to accomendate GPU raster, and will require new baskets.
histogram.summary: Rasterized area, in pixels, divided by rasterization time, in milliseconds, of a compositor rasterization task. Recorded after the task finishes (in a renderer process).

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.RasterTask.RasterPixelsPerMs2
histogram.summary: Rasterized area, in pixels, divided by rasterization time, in milliseconds, of a compositor rasterization task. Recorded after the task finishes (in a renderer process).

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.RasterTask.RasterUs
histogram.summary:
Time spent completing a compositor rasterization task, in microseconds. Recorded after the task finishes (in a renderer process).
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.RenderPass.AppendQuadData.NumMissingTilesNoImageContent
histogram.obsolete: Removed 02/2016. We'd learned as much as we could from this metric and it made no sense to keep it around.
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of missing tiles that had some image content in the renderer process.

histogram.name: Compositing.Renderer.RenderPass.AppendQuadData.NumMissingTilesSomeImageContent
histogram.obsolete: Removed 02/2016. We'd learned as much as we could from this metric and it made no sense to keep it around.
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of missing tiles that did not have any image content in the renderer process.

histogram.name: Compositing.RenderPass.AppendQuadData.CheckerboardedNeedRasterContentArea
histogram.summary: Checkerboarded area, in number of pixels, that has recording but does not have time to finish rastering yet. A sample is recorded everytime a frame is drawn while a scroll is in progress. Tracking bug 535732.

histogram.name: Compositing.RenderPass.AppendQuadData.CheckerboardedNoRecordingContentArea
histogram.summary: Checkerboarded area, in number of pixels, that has no recording to raster from. A sample is recorded everytime a frame is drawn while a scroll is in progress. Tracking bug 535732.

histogram.name: Compositing.RenderPass.AppendQuadData.MaskLayerPercent
histogram.obsolete: Removed as being no longer useful to track.
histogram.summary: Percent of layers drawn that frame that are mask layers. A sample is recorded on the first draw per commit.

histogram.name: Compositing.RenderPass.AppendQuadData.NumIncompleteTiles
histogram.summary: Keeps track of number of incomplete tiles in a drawn compositor frame while scrolling. This is a rough measurement of ugliness during user interaction. Incomplete tiles are non ideal scaled. A sample is recorded everytime a frame is drawn while a scroll is in progress. Tracking bug 381695.

histogram.name: Compositing.RenderPass.AppendQuadData.NumMissingTiles
histogram.summary: Keeps track of number of missing tiles in a drawn compositor frame while scrolling. This is a rough measurement of ugliness during user interaction. Incomplete tiles are checkerboard or solid color. A sample is recorded everytime a frame is drawn while a scroll is in progress. Tracking bug 381695.

histogram.name: Compositing.RenderPass.AppendQuadData.RCMaskArea
histogram.obsolete: Removed as being no longer useful to track.
histogram.summary: Total visible area of layers drawn that frame that are mask layers and also are simple rounded corner masks. A sample is recorded on the first draw per commit.

histogram.name: Compositing.RenderPass.AppendQuadData.RCMaskAreaPercent
histogram.obsolete: Removed as being no longer useful to track.
histogram.summary: Percent of visible area of layers drawn that frame that are mask layers and also are simple rounded corner masks. A sample is recorded on the first draw per commit.

histogram.name: Compositing.RenderPass.AppendQuadData.RCMaskLayerPercent
histogram.obsolete: Removed as being no longer useful to track.
histogram.summary: Percent of layers drawn that frame that are mask layers and also are simple rounded corner masks. A sample is recorded on the first draw per commit.

histogram.name: Compositing.SurfaceAggregator.AggregateUs
histogram.summary:
Time spent aggregating compositor frames from different surfaces in microseconds. This is logged once per frame.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Compositing.SurfaceAggregator.LatestInFlightSurface.ManhattanDistanceToPrimary
histogram.obsolete: Removed 10/2018. This metric didn't end up being useful.
histogram.summary: The distance between the latest inflight surface and the primary surface. This is logged for each call to HandleSurfaceQuad().

histogram.name: Compositing.SurfaceAggregator.SurfaceDrawQuad.MissingSurface
histogram.obsolete: Removed 07/2018 in issue 865597 in order to avoid spurious error messages on Viz process crash.
histogram.summary: The number of SurfaceDrawQuads where the surface doesn't exist. This is logged for each call to Aggregate().

histogram.name: Compositing.SurfaceAggregator.SurfaceDrawQuad.NoActiveFrame
histogram.obsolete: Removed 07/2018 in issue 865597 in order to avoid spurious error messages on Viz process crash.
histogram.summary: The number of SurfaceDrawQuads where the surface exists but doesn't have an active CompositorFrame. This is logged for each call to Aggregate().

histogram.name: Compositing.SurfaceAggregator.SurfaceDrawQuad.UsingFallbackSurface
histogram.obsolete: Removed 10/2018. This metric didn't end up being useful.
histogram.summary: The number of SurfaceDrawQuads where the primary SurfaceId does not have a corresponding active CompositorFrame and so the fallback surface is used instead. This is logged for each call to Aggregate().

histogram.name: Compositing.SurfaceAggregator.SurfaceDrawQuad.ValidSurface
histogram.obsolete: Removed 10/2018. This metric didn't end up being useful.
histogram.summary: The number of SurfaceDrawQuads where the surface exists and has an active CompositorFrame. This is logged for each call to Aggregate().

histogram.name: Compositing.SurfaceDependencyDeadline.DeadlineHit
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 10/2018. This metric didn't end up being useful.
histogram.summary: Tracks whether an activation of a surface was due to due to a deadline or the resolution of dependencies.

histogram.name: Compositing.SurfaceDependencyDeadline.Duration
histogram.summary: The amount of time it took for a Surface with unresolved dependencies to activate. Note that some activations may be triggered due to a deadline hitting. That is tracked in the DeadlineHit histogram.

histogram.name: Compositing.SurfaceInvariantsViolations
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 2018/09/10 in favor of Compositing.CompositorFrameSinkSupport.SubmitResult.
histogram.summary: The number of times a renderer in this browser session has violated a surface synchronization invariant. This manifests as a skipped frame. Ideally this metric should always report 0, but realistically there are races in the system and this tracks the frequency that we hit these races.

histogram.name: Compositing.SurfaceManager.AliveSurfaces
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of alive surfaces after each garbage collection operation.

histogram.name: Compositing.SurfaceManager.NumOldTemporaryReferences
histogram.summary: The number of temporary references that have existed for at least 10 seconds and are most likely orphaned. This is logged every 10 seconds.

histogram.name: Compositing.SurfaceManager.RemovedTemporaryReference
histogram.summary: Tracks the reason for removing a temporary reference.

histogram.name: Compositing.SurfaceManager.TemporaryReferences
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of temporary references after each garbage collection operation.

histogram.name: CompositorImplAndroid.TearDownDisplayTime
histogram.obsolete: Obsolete after 03/2019. For temporary debugging of CompositorImpl hangs.
histogram.summary: Tracks the amount of time it takes to synchronously tear down the display when the CompositorImpl becomes invisible or is destroyed. Recorded after display teardown completes in CompositorImpl:: TearDownDisplayAndUnregisterRootFrameSink.

histogram.name: CompositorLatency
histogram.summary:
Tracks the duration of various stages in the pipeline as a single frame goes through the various stages in the compositor. This is reported for frames that went through all the compositor stages in chromium, and was presented to the user.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: CompositorLatency.CompositorOnlyFrame
histogram.summary:
Tracks the duration of various stages in the pipeline as a single frame goes through the various stages in the compositor in the cases that there is no damage from main thread.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: CompositorLatency.Diagnostic.PresentationTimeDeltaFromDeadline
histogram.summary: For the compositor pipeline, measures how far the presentation time of a compositor frame is from the frame deadline (i.e. vsync time).

histogram.name: CompositorLatency.DroppedFrame
histogram.summary:
Tracks the duration of various stages in the pipeline as a single frame goes through the various stages in the compositor. This is reported for frames that the user did not see: this includes frames that are dropped in various stages inside chromium, or even frames that went through all stages in chromium, but ultimately was not presented to the user.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: CompositorLatency.MissedDeadlineFrame
histogram.summary:
Tracks the duration of various stages in the pipeline as a single frame goes through the various stages in the compositor. This is reported for frames that went through all the compositor stages in chromium, and was presented to the user, but were delayed and missed their deadline.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: CompositorLatency.MissedFrame
histogram.obsolete: Removed on 01/2020. MissedFrame changed to DroppedFrame for more clarity.
histogram.summary:
Tracks the duration of various stages in the pipeline as a single frame goes through the various stages in the compositor. This is reported for frames that the user did not see: this includes frames that are dropped in various stages inside chromium, or even frames that went through all stages in chromium, but ultimately was not presented to the user.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: CompositorLatency.MissedFrameLatencyIncrease
histogram.obsolete: Removed on 9/2019. Did not provide enough information about latency.
histogram.summary:
The latency increase of an abnormally long activation stage on the multithread thread compositor pipeline when the frame is missed.
These latency increases are determined by comparing the stage duration to the times from past non-missed frames. These times are also reported to "CompositorLatency.MissedFrame.<StageName>"

histogram.name: CompositorLatency.Type
histogram.summary: Whether frame is dropped or presented meeting or missing the deadline or is a compositor only frame.

histogram.name: ConfigureDisplays.External.Modeset.AttemptSucceeded
histogram.summary: Modeset attempt result of an external display. Every time an external display is connected/disconnected, or when the display mode of the external screen is changed by the user, Chrome attempts to modeset the display. This metric tracks the result of those attempts. Resolution and RefreshRate metrics will be recorded for each attempt.

histogram.name: ConfigureDisplays.External.Modeset.FinalStatus
histogram.summary: Modeset attempt result of an internal display. This is recorded after Chrome attempted all the available modes and finally modeset the display. All the previously attempted modes and refreshes, including the successful one will be recorded.

histogram.name: ConfigureDisplays.External.Modeset.RefreshRate
histogram.summary: Refresh rate of the mode for the display we're about to modeset. This is recorded every time an external display is connected/disconnected, or when the display mode of the external screen is about to change.

histogram.name: ConfigureDisplays.External.Modeset.Resolution
histogram.summary: DisplayResolution of the mode for the external display we're about to modeset. 0 means the display was powered off. This is recorded every time an external display is connected/disconnected, or when the display mode of the external screen is about to change.

histogram.name: ConfigureDisplays.Internal.Modeset.AttemptSucceeded
histogram.summary: Modeset attempt result of an internal display. Chrome might try additional modes after a failed attempt. Every time an external display is connected/disconnected, or when the display mode of the external screen is changed by the user, Chrome attempts to modeset the display. This metric tracks the result of those attempts. Resolution and RefreshRate metrics will be recorded for each attempt.

histogram.name: ConfigureDisplays.Internal.Modeset.FinalStatus
histogram.summary: Modeset attempt result of an internal display. This is recorded after Chrome attempted all the available modes and finally modeset the display. All the previously attempted modes and refreshes, including the successful one will be recorded.

histogram.name: ConfigureDisplays.Internal.Modeset.RefreshRate
histogram.summary: Refresh rate of the mode for the display we're about to modeset. This is recorded every time the internal display is powered on/off, or when an external monitor is plugged in.

histogram.name: ConfigureDisplays.Internal.Modeset.Resolution
histogram.summary: DisplayResolution of the mode for the external display we're about to modeset. 0 means the display was powered off. This is recorded every time the internal display is powered on/off, or when an external monitor is plugged in.

histogram.name: Conflicts.ConfirmedBadModules
histogram.summary: The number of confirmed bad modules found during module enumeration.

histogram.name: Conflicts.EnumerateLoadedModules
histogram.summary: The length of time it takes to enumerate the loaded modules in the browser process.

histogram.name: Conflicts.EnumerateShellExtensions
histogram.summary: The length of time it takes to enumerate the shell extensions.

histogram.name: Conflicts.EnumerateWinsockModules
histogram.summary: The length of time it takes to enumerate the Winsock LSP modules.

histogram.name: Conflicts.EnumerationInspectionTime
histogram.summary: The cumulative length of time it takes to inspect all modules on disk, extracting their certificates and version information. This work is actually spread out over a much longer period of time so as not to impact the user.

histogram.name: Conflicts.EnumerationTotalTime
histogram.summary: The cumulative length of time it takes to enumerate and inspect all modules. This work is actually spread out over a much longer period of time so as not to impact the user.

histogram.name: Conflicts.SuspectedBadModules
histogram.summary: The number of suspected bad modules found during module enumeration.

histogram.name: ConnectivityDetector.FromSystem
histogram.summary: Whether or not the network connectivity info, i.e. validated, is provided by the system. We will use it if it is available from the system. Otherwise, we will do our own http probes to find it out. This UMA will not be recorded if a flag in about:flags is set to bypass this logic to always favor the check by http probes.

histogram.name: ConnectivityDetector.Probe.HttpResponseCode
histogram.summary: The HTTP response code returned from a HTTP probe for the purpose of checking network connectivity.

histogram.name: ConnectivityDetector.Probe.Result
histogram.summary: The result from a HTTP probe for the purpose of checking network connectivity.

histogram.name: ConnectivityDetector.Probe.ValidationTime
histogram.summary: The total duration measuring all the HTTP probes and backoff time taken when the network connectivity is successfully validated.

histogram.name: ConnectivityDetector.Probe.ValidationUrl
histogram.summary: Records type of url, default or fallback URL, resulting in successful connectivity validation.

histogram.name: ConnectivityDiagnostics.ChromeOsSignalStrength
histogram.summary: Connectivity Diagnostics App: WiFi signal strength recorded during NIC_SIGNAL_STRENGTH test.
histogram.details: The "Strength" property of a WiFi signal is a partially-reversible function that linearly maps the RSSI range -120dBm to -20dBm to Strength values from 0 to 100.

histogram.name: ConnectivityDiagnostics.HTTP_LATENCY
histogram.summary: HTTP latency seen by the Connectivity Diagnostics.
histogram.details: HTTP latency is computed using the chrome.socket API to make an HTTP GET request to the /generate_204 page of three randomly generated Google hostnames (*-ccd-testing-v4.metric.gstatic.com). The time taken from issuing the HTTP request to receiving a response is clocked in JavaScript and the arithmetic mean of the three times is used as the HTTP latency.

histogram.name: ConnectivityDiagnostics.RESOLVER_LATENCY
histogram.summary: Resolution latency seen by the Connectivity Diagnostics.
histogram.details: Resolver latency is computed by using the chrome.dns API to query three randomly generated Google hostnames (*-ccd-testing-v4.metric.gstatic.com). The random hostnames guarantees that there will be no caching of DNS hostnames. The time taken from issuing the DNS request to receiving a response is clocked in JavaScript and the arithmetic mean of the three times is used as the resolver latency.

histogram.name: ConnectivityDiagnostics.TestVerdict
histogram.summary: Connectivity Diagnostics App: Outcome of the connectivity tests.

histogram.name: ConnectivityDiagnostics.TimeTaken
histogram.summary: Connectivity Diagnostics App: Amount of time taken to run each of the connectivity tests.

histogram.name: ContentCapture.CaptureContentDelayTime
histogram.summary: The time between a change in content and when the new capture is sent to the browser process.
histogram.details: A specific content change is hard to track. This is roughly calculated as the interval from the first content change after a prior capturing content to the first content being sent after the next capture of content.

histogram.name: ContentCapture.CaptureContentTime
histogram.summary:
The time taken to capture the on-screen content.
Note that this metrics is only recorded on clients on which a high-resolution clock is available.

histogram.name: ContentCapture.CaptureOneContentTime
histogram.summary: The estimated time taken to capture one on-screen content.

histogram.name: ContentCapture.GetBoundingBox
histogram.summary:
The time taken to get the bounding box of the batch of captured content.
Note that this metrics is only recorded on clients on which a high-resolution clock is available.

histogram.name: ContentCapture.SendContentTime
histogram.summary:
The time taken to send the content to the browser process in batch.
Note that this metrics is only recorded on clients on which a high-resolution clock is available.

histogram.name: ContentCapture.SentContentCount
histogram.summary: The total number of content captures sent for a document.

histogram.name: ContentIndex.ContentAdded
histogram.summary: Records the category of the content when registered.

histogram.name: ContentIndex.ContentDeleteEvent
histogram.summary: Records the status of dispatching the `contentdelete` event in every phase of dispatch.

histogram.name: ContentIndex.ContentOpened
histogram.summary: Records the category of the entry clicked on by a user.

histogram.name: ContentIndex.Database
histogram.summary: Records the result of the database operation.

histogram.name: ContentIndex.NumEntriesAvailable
histogram.summary: Records how many entries were found when loaded by the browser.

histogram.name: ContentIndex.RegistrationBlocked
histogram.summary: Records the category of an attempted registration that was blocked.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.DefaultAutoplaySetting
histogram.summary: The default autoplay setting at profile open.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.DefaultCookiesSetting
histogram.summary: The default cookies setting at profile open.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.DefaultHandlersSetting
histogram.obsolete: Removed 07/2014 since it is not referenced anywhere in the code.
histogram.summary: The default handler setting at profile open.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.DefaultImagesSetting
histogram.summary: The default image setting at profile open.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.DefaultJavaScriptSetting
histogram.summary: The default JavaScript setting at profile open.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.DefaultKeygenSetting
histogram.summary: The default setting for using key generation in forms at profile open.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.DefaultLocationSetting
histogram.summary: The default location setting at profile open.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.DefaultMediaStreamCameraSetting
histogram.summary: The default camera setting at profile open.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.DefaultMediaStreamMicSetting
histogram.summary: The default microphone setting at profile open.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.DefaultMediaStreamSetting
histogram.obsolete: This setting was deprecated in r322364 and replaced with separate settings for camera and microphone. See ContentSettings.DefaultMediaStreamCameraSetting and ContentSettings.DefaultMediaStreamMicSetting.
histogram.summary: The default MediaStream setting at profile open.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.DefaultMIDISysExSetting
histogram.summary: The default MIDI permission setting on sysex (system exclusive) messages at profile open.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.DefaultMouseCursorSetting
histogram.summary: The default mouse cursor setting at profile open.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.DefaultNotificationsSetting
histogram.summary: The default notification setting at profile open.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.DefaultPluginsSetting
histogram.summary: The default plugins setting at profile open.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.DefaultPopupsSetting
histogram.summary: The default popups setting at profile open.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.DefaultPushMessagingSetting
histogram.summary: The default permission setting for push messages at profile open.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.DefaultSoundSetting
histogram.summary: The default sound setting at profile open.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.DefaultSubresourceFilterSetting
histogram.summary: The default subresource filter setting at profile open

histogram.name: ContentSettings.DefaultUsbGuardSetting
histogram.summary: The default permission setting for WebUSB at profile open.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.DefaultWebBluetoothGuardSetting
histogram.summary: The default permission setting for Web Bluetooth at profile open.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.EphemeralFlashPermission
histogram.summary: Records the number of times Flash permission is granted for a host.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.Exceptions
histogram.summary: The number of user defined content setting exceptions at browser start. Suffixed with the name of content setting types.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.ExceptionScheme
histogram.summary: Records the schemes of content setting exceptions at browser start. Each exception provides one sample.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.ExceptionSchemeFile.HasPath
histogram.summary: Records how often a content settings exception for the file: scheme has a non-empty path. Recorded once per exception at browser start.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.ExceptionSchemeFile.Type.WithoutPath
histogram.summary:
Count of how often a specific content type has a content settings exception defined for a file: scheme with no path (i.e., wildcard path). Recorded once per exception at browser start.
Note: The values of this metric collected for Chrome 49 (early 2016) are innacurate and should not be trusted. crbug.com/589255.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.ExceptionSchemeFile.Type.WithPath
histogram.summary:
