Count of how often a specific content type has a content settings exception defined for a file: scheme with a valid, non-empty path. Recorded once per exception at browser start.
Note: The values of this metric collected for Chrome 49 (early 2016) are innacurate and should not be trusted. crbug.com/589255.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.ExtensionEmbeddedSettingSet
histogram.summary: Count of how often a specific content setting is set for an embedded URL by an extension. Only counts settings when the secondary pattern is different to the primary pattern. Exactly one of this or ContentSettings.ExtensionNonEmbeddedSettingSet will be emitted per call to contentSettings.set(), if the arguments to the call are valid.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.ExtensionNonEmbeddedSettingSet
histogram.summary: Count of how often a specific content setting is set when only a single pattern is specified. Exactly one of this or ContentSettings.ExtensionEmbeddedSettingSet will be emitted per call to contentSettings.set(), if the arguments to the call are valid.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.ImagePressed
histogram.summary: Counts which content setting buttons are pressed by the user.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.LastSettingParsed
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2015-10-05 in Issue 433475. Histogram was used temorarily for diagnosing crash causes.
histogram.summary: Numer of results of parsing WebsiteSettingsHandler::last_setting_ success or failure in WebsiteSettingsHandler methods.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.MixedScript
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2020-01-06. Histogram had already expired and metrics are no longer required after the mixed content shield removal.
histogram.summary: Tracks whether the mixed content shield was shown, and how the user interacted with it.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.NumberOfAllowCookiesExceptions
histogram.obsolete: Removed Oct 2018 in favor of ContentSettings.Exceptions.cookies.Allow
histogram.summary: The number of user defined cookies setting exceptions for allowing cookies at browser start.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.NumberOfBlockCookiesExceptions
histogram.obsolete: Removed Oct 2018 in favor of ContentSettings.Exceptions.cookies.Block
histogram.summary: The number of user defined cookies setting exceptions for blocking cookies at browser start.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.NumberOfExceptions
histogram.summary: The number of user defined hostname patterns for content settings at browser start.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.NumberOfSessionOnlyCookiesExceptions
histogram.summary: The number of user defined cookies setting exceptions for 'clearing cookies on browser exit' at browser start.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.PermissionActions
histogram.summary: Tracks whether a permission was granted, rejected, etc. The suffix of the histogram indicates which particular permission.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.PermissionActionsInsecureOrigin
histogram.summary: Tracks whether a permission was granted, rejected, etc on an insecure origin. The suffix of the histogram indicates which particular permission.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.PermissionActionsSecureOrigin
histogram.summary: Tracks whether a permission was granted, rejected, etc on a secure origin. The suffix of the histogram indicates which particular permission.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.PermissionRequested
histogram.summary:
Number of times a given permission was requested by a website and the user has the permission set to prompt (i.e. not blocked or allowed).
Note this is probably not the metric you want - it does not correspond to the total number of times websites request a permission. Also, because specific permissions have code that can automatically block or grant permissions based on things like incognito, installed extensions etc., this does also not correspond to the number of times users are prompted to allow permissions.
For a better metric to track how often users are prompted, either use ContentSettings.PermissionsActions*, or Permissions.Prompt.*.
See https://crbug.com/638076 for more details.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.PermissionRequested_InsecureOrigin
histogram.summary:
Number of times a given permission was requested by an insecure origin and the user has the permission set to prompt (i.e. not blocked or allowed).
Note this is probably not the metric you want - it does not correspond to the total number of times websites request a permission. Also, because specific permissions have code that can automatically block or grant permissions based on things like incognito, installed extensions etc., this does also not correspond to the number of times users are prompted to allow permissions.
For a better metric to track how often users are prompted, use ContentSettings.PermissionsActions*.
See https://crbug.com/638076 for more details.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.PermissionRequested_SecureOrigin
histogram.summary:
Number of times a given permission was requested by a secure origin and the user has the permission set to prompt (i.e. not blocked or allowed).
Note this is probably not the metric you want - it does not correspond to the total number of times websites request a permission. Also, because specific permissions have code that can automatically block or grant permissions based on things like incognito, installed extensions etc., this does also not correspond to the number of times users are prompted to allow permissions.
For a better metric to track how often users are prompted, use ContentSettings.PermissionsActions*.
See https://crbug.com/638076 for more details.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.Plugins
histogram.summary: Tracks whether the plugin content blocked puzzle piece was shown in the Omnibox, and how the user interacted with it.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.Popups
histogram.summary: Tracks whether the popup content blocked puzzle piece was shown in the Omnibox, and how the user interacted with it.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.Popups.BlockerActions
histogram.summary: Counts of various events related to the popup blocker. Including blocked popups and overridden (clicked through) popups. This is similar to the ContentSettings.Popups but is at the per-popup layer rather than at the UI layer.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.Popups.ClickThroughPosition
histogram.summary: The blocked popup list contains a list of links that Chrome has blocked via the popup blocker. If a user clicks on the link, the popup is opened. This metric records the position of the popup in the list that a user clicks through. Currently it should only be recorded on desktop platforms, because the mobile UI for popup blocking does not currently allow for specific exceptions.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.Popups.EngagementTime
histogram.summary: Measures the total duration a popup WebContents is visible / foregrounded, until it is closed.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.Popups.EngagementTime.GestureClose
histogram.summary: Measures the total duration a popup WebContents is visible / foregrounded, until it is closed via some user gesture (like Ctrl-W).

histogram.name: ContentSettings.Popups.FirstDocumentEngagementTime
histogram.summary: Measures the total duration a popup WebContents is visible / foregrounded for the duration of its first document load. This time is measured from the first navigation commit to the time either the WebContents is destroyed or when a new navigation commits.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.Popups.FirstDocumentEngagementTime2
histogram.summary: Measures the total duration a popup WebContents is visible / foregrounded for the duration of its first document load. This time is measured from the first navigation commit to the time either the WebContents is destroyed or when a new navigation commits.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.Popups.StrongBlocker.NumBlocked
histogram.summary: The total number of popups blocked by the strong blocker for a given page load. Logged when the page is navigated away from, or the tab is closed. Only logged for pages which are triggered for strong popup blocking, aka abusive experience enforcement.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.Popups.StrongBlockerActions
histogram.summary: Counts of various events related to the strong popup blocker (aka abusive experience enforcement), that is triggered via safe browsing.

histogram.name: ContentSettings.Popups.StrongBlockerActivationPosition
histogram.summary: For pages that trigger Safe Browsing triggered popup blocker (in warn or enforce modes), records the position in the redirect chain for the page the activation was triggered by. If SubresourceFilterConsiderRedirects is disabled, then always returns "Only navigation".

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.AppLifecycle.Events
histogram.summary: Android: count of app lifecycle events reported to the Feed library. Recorded as these events occur; e.g. when Chrome is foregrounded, backgrounded, or the user clears cached browsing data.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.AppLifecycle.NumRowsForDeletion
histogram.summary: Android: number of rows present in a history deletion that's causing all current suggestions to be deleted. Each row should correspond to a url that is beign removed from history. Is not emitted when entire history is being cleared.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.AppLifecycleEvents
histogram.summary: Android: count of app lifecycle events reported to the Feed library. Recorded as these events occur; e.g. when Chrome is foregrounded, backgrounded, or the user clears cached browsing data.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.AvailableOffline.Opened
histogram.summary: When an article on the NTP is opened by the user, records whether it is available offline. When an article is available offline, it will have an offline badge in the UI on the NTP. Opening the article occurs when the user navigates from the NTP to the article itself, which can be either in the current tab or a new tab. Note that this is independent of whether the article is actually opened as an offline page or not.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.AvailableOffline.Shown
histogram.summary: Records whether an article on the NTP that is shown to the user was also available offline. When an article is available offline, it will have an offline badge in the UI. The initial offline status of the article is used for this metric. While it is possible for the offline status to change while the article is on screen, this metric will not be re-emitted. The criteria for an article to be "shown" is for 2/3 of its vertical height to be on screen for any amount of time.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.CommitMutationCount
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of operations in a Feed mutation when database receive the commit request.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.Controls.Actions
histogram.summary: Actions related to the feed controls.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.Count
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of entries for the Feed storage after database perform get all keys request.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.DisplayStatusOnOpen
histogram.summary: Android: The display status of the content suggestions when users open NTPs. Whether content suggestions are disabled by policy (e.g. enterprise or supervised users), enabled but collapsed, or enabled and expanded when a new NTP is created.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.EngagementType
histogram.summary: Tracks each instance of user engagement with the feed, by scrolling or interacting with a card (opening the URL or using the menu). Note that each event type reports samples very differently and buckets can't be directly compared. We're mostly interested in unique user counts. We track every interaction, and we report ENGAGED and ENGAGED_SIMPLE once per user visit. A user visit is when the user scrolls or interacts with the NTP more than 5 minutes after the previous visit. The SCROLLED engagement type represents a single instance of the user scrolling, and is reported when the scroll direction changes, or 200ms pass with no further scroll events. The INTERACTED engagement type represents one instance of using the menu or opening a feed article into its own tab. SCROLLED and INTERACTED are reported for every occurrence, thus we could end up with more scrolled and interected than ENGAGED or ENGAGED_SIMPLE samples.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.FetchPendingSpinner.Shown
histogram.summary: Android: The type of spinner when the content suggestion shows the loading spinner. This is tracked based on if the spinner is shown, not how long it is visible.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.FetchPendingSpinner.VisibleDuration
histogram.summary: Android: How long the content suggestion loading spinner is shown when a loading spinner finishes showing. This is tracked based on when the spinner is enabled in the UI, not how long it is actually visible on screen.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.FetchPendingSpinner.VisibleDurationWithoutCompleting
histogram.summary: Android: How long the content suggestion loading spinner is shown when a spinner is destroyed without completing. This is tracked based on when the spinner is enabled in the UI, not how long it is actually visible on screen.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.Image.FetchResult
histogram.summary: Android: Represents success/failure of Feed image loading. Recorded upon each individual image retrieved from the feed_image_cache.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.Image.LoadFromCacheTime
histogram.summary: Android: The time it takes for Feed to load an image from the cache.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.Image.LoadFromNetworkTime
histogram.summary: Android: The time it takes for Feed to load an image from the network.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.InitialSuccess
histogram.summary: Tracks the Feed storage success rate during initial storage.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.InterestHeader.NotInterestedInSource
histogram.summary: The position of an interest header on the NTP that the user has indicated they aren't interested in the story's source.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.InterestHeader.NotInterestedInTopic
histogram.summary: The position of an interest header on the NTP that the user has indicated they aren't interested in the story's topic.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.InternalError
histogram.summary: The Feed library encountered an error at any time.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.LoadKeysSuccess
histogram.summary: Tracks the Feed storage success rate during load keys.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.LoadKeysTime
histogram.summary: The time it takes for the Feed to load all keys from the storage.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.LoadStreamStatus.BackgroundRefresh
histogram.summary: Status of loading new feed data in the background. Feed v2 only.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.LoadStreamStatus.Initial
histogram.summary: Status of loading the initial Feed stream, which happens when a feed surface is shown. Feed v2 only.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.LoadStreamStatus.InitialFromStore
histogram.summary: This is the store-specific status for the initial load of the Feed stream. This is provided along with ContentSuggestions.Feed.LoadStreamStatus.Initial because store errors can be shadowed by fallback to network fetch. Feed v2 only.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.LoadStreamStatus.LoadMore
histogram.summary: Status of loading additional Feed content onto the bottom of the stream. Feed v2 only.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.LoadSuccess
histogram.summary: Tracks the Feed storage success rate during load entries.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.LoadTime
histogram.summary: The time it takes for the Feed to load entries from the storage.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.ManageInterestsOpened
histogram.obsolete: This implementation is incorrect and we are now using NewTabPage.ActionAndroid2 instead. Marked obsolete in 01-2020
histogram.summary: The position of a card in the feed from which the user has opened the Manage Interests page. Recorded when the Manage Interest item is clicked in the card menu.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.Network.Duration
histogram.summary: The amount of time fetching articles takes, regardless of success. This includes the time it takes to get an auth token for signed in users.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.Network.RequestSizeKB.Compressed
histogram.summary: Android: the size in kb of gzip-compressed requests sent by the Feed's networking host. Recorded when a request is sent.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.Network.RequestStatusCode
histogram.summary: Android: The integer status code of network requests made by the feed library's networking host. This code includes both protocol(1xx-5xx) and non-protocol(-xxx) errors. Recorded when a request completes.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.Network.ResponseSizeKB
histogram.summary: Android: the uncompressed size in KB of responses received by the Feed's networking host. Recorded when a successful response is received.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.Network.ResponseStatus
histogram.summary: The HTTP response status for a network request made by the Feed component.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.Network.TokenDuration
histogram.summary: The amount of time it takes to get an access token for signed in users.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.Network.TokenFetchStatus
histogram.summary: Android: failure reason when attempting to fetch an OAuth token for the feed. Recorded when a token fetch completes.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.NetworkRequestStatusCode
histogram.summary: Android: The integer status code of network requests made by the feed library's networking host. This code includes both protocol(1xx-5xx) and non-protocol(-xxx) errors. Recorded when a request completes.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.NoticeCardFulfilled
histogram.summary: Android: Whether the notice card for feed action reporting has been fulfilled.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.Offline.GetStatusCount
histogram.summary: The number of urls that have offline status requested per call.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.Offline.GetStatusDuration
histogram.summary: The number of milliseconds to round trip offline status for a set of URLs from Offline Pages component.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.OperationCommitSuccess
histogram.summary: Tracks the Feed storage success rate during commit operations.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.OperationCommitTime
histogram.summary: The time it takes for the Feed to commit operations from the storage.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.PagePopulatingTime
histogram.summary: The number of milliseconds it takes for Feed content to be visible in the UI. If content is not immediately available, this is conceptually the amount of time a loading spinner is shown to the user.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.Piet.FrameRenderingErrorCode
histogram.summary: Android: The integer error code when Piet wants to report events that occurred during Frame rendering.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.RequestSizeKB.Compressed
histogram.summary: Android: the size in kb of gzip-compressed requests sent by the Feed's networking host. Recorded when a request is sent.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.ResponseSizeKB
histogram.summary: Android: the uncompressed size in KB of responses received by the Feed's networking host. Recorded when a successful response is received.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.Scheduler.ContentAgeDifference
histogram.summary: Difference in age of content between feed and host. Recorded when the Feed asks the scheduler how to behave when a NTP is opened.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.Scheduler.HasContent
histogram.summary: The match status between Feed and the host when the scheduler's ShouldSessionRequestData method is called. This occurs whenever the NTP is opened, and tries to track if the Feed and host state agrees or disagrees. If the device already has content, new requests are not necessarily required for a reasonable user experience.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.Scheduler.OutstandingRequest
histogram.summary: The match status between Feed and the host when the scheduler's ShouldSessionRequestData method is called. This occurs whenever the NTP is opened, and tries to track if the Feed and host state agrees or disagrees. The type of request that could be outstanding is to fetch new or more news articles, and when requests are outstanding new requests should not be started.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.Scheduler.RefreshTrigger
histogram.summary: The scheduler watches for various triggers, which cause it to decide if a refresh is currently warranted or not. This histogram is emitted when a trigger causes a refresh.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.Scheduler.RequestBehavior
histogram.summary: When NTP is opened, the scheduler host tells the Feed library how to act, if the existing content should be shown, immediately or with timeout, and if a refresh request should be started.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.Scheduler.ShouldRefreshResult
histogram.summary: When the Feed scheduler receives a refresh request, the scheduler will decide to refresh or not depending on the scheduler status. Recorded when the Feed scheduler receives the request.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.Scheduler.TimeSinceLastFetchOnClear
histogram.summary: When the previous last fetch attempt time is cleared, log the time since the last fetch attempt. This typically occurs when suggestions are cleared. A client that has never made a successful fetch (at least since it was last cleared) will report the time since epoch.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.SendFeedback
histogram.summary: Number of each type of feedback sent about the feed.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.ServerRequest.Reason
histogram.summary: The reason for a server request from the Feed's prespective, when the request is started.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.Task.DelayTime
histogram.summary: How long it took from enqueuing the task to when it was run.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.Task.TaskTime
histogram.summary: How long it took from the task starting to when it finished executing.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.TimeSpentInFeed
histogram.summary: How long a user used the Feed in one day (local time midnight to midnight). Reported at most once per day. Usage reported is typically from the previous day, but could be from several days ago if the user is not active. Time is calculated starting with the first Feed interaction, until the Feed surface is closed, or the user is idle (no scrolling or other actions) for 30 seconds.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.TokenCompleted.ContentCount
histogram.obsolete: Removed in favor of ContentSuggestions.Feed.TokenCompleted.ContentCount2. Removed 03/2019. http://crbug.com/943838
histogram.summary: How many top level features were in the continuation response, typically clusters. Recorded when the fetch completes successfully.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.TokenCompleted.ContentCount2
histogram.summary: How many top level features were in the continuation response, typically clusters. Recorded when the fetch completes successfully.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.TokenCompleted.TokenCount
histogram.summary: How many tokens were in the continuation response, typically ways to fetch more articles. Recorded when the fetch completes successfully.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.TokenFailedToCompleted
histogram.summary: When a token fails to complete, how many failures this token has seen. Record when the fetch fails.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.TokenFetchStatus
histogram.summary: Android: failure reason when attempting to fetch an OAuth token for the feed. Recorded when an artcile fetch completes.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.UploadActionsBatchStatus
histogram.summary: Result of attempting to upload a batch of one or more actions.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.UploadActionsStatus
histogram.summary: Result of an UploadActionsTask run in which zero or more batches of user actions are uploaded. See ContentSuggestions.Feed.UploadActionsBatchStatus for the status of a particular batch upload.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.UserActions
histogram.summary: Counts of user actions within the feed surface (Feed v2 only).

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.UserJourney.GetMore.FailureDuration
histogram.summary: Time between the UI requesting additional stream content and failure by either a 15 second timeout or a surface closure.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.UserJourney.GetMore.SuccessDuration
histogram.summary: Time between the UI requesting additional stream content and the content being provided within 15 seconds.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.UserJourney.OpenCard.Failure
histogram.summary: Reported when a user taps on a Feed card unless the page is not loaded successfully within 20 seconds.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.UserJourney.OpenCard.SuccessDuration
histogram.summary: The time between a user tapping on a Feed card and the page finishes loading successfully. Only reported when loading takes less than 20 seconds.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.UserJourney.OpenFeed.FailureDuration
histogram.summary: The time between a Feed surface opening and failing to provide the user content. This is reported when either the surface is closed or a timeout of 15 seconds expires.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.UserJourney.OpenFeed.SuccessDuration
histogram.summary: The time between a Feed surface opening and the user viewing the first content. If this takes more than 15 seconds, ContentSuggestions.Feed.UserJourney.OpenFeed.FailureDuration is reported instead.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.VisualElement.Clicked
histogram.summary: Android: The position of a clicked element in the stream not accounting for header views. The top being 0. Position does not change after initial layout. Specifically the position does not update if dismisses/removes are performed. This is similar to NewTabPage.ContentSuggestions.Opened, but records the specific elementType that was clicked.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.VisualElement.Clicked.TimeSinceElementFetched
histogram.summary: Android: The time since the content was made available on the device. This could be the time for when this content was retrieved from the server or the time the data was pushed to the device. Recorded when the user clicks the element.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.VisualElement.Viewed
histogram.summary: Android: The position of a viewed element in the stream not accounting for header views. The top being 0. Position does not change after initial layout. Specifically the position does not update if dismisses/removes are performed. This is similar to NewTabPage.ContentSuggestions.Shown, but records the view after it has been on the screen for 500 milliseconds of time instead of on predraw.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.VisualElement.Viewed.TimeSinceElementFetched
histogram.summary: Android: The time since the content was made available on the device. This could be the time for when this content was retrieved from the server or the time the data was pushed to the device. Recorded when the element has been on the screen for 500 milliseconds.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.ZeroStateRefreshCompleted.ContentCount
histogram.summary: How many top level features were in an initial article fetch, typically clusters. Recorded when an article fetch completes and were previously in zero state.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.ZeroStateRefreshCompleted.TokenCount
histogram.summary: How many tokens were in an initial article fetch, typically ways to fetch more articles. Recorded when an article fetch completes and were previously in zero state.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.Feed.ZeroStateShown.Reason
histogram.summary: The reason the zero state (no articles) is shown to a user.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.FetchPendingPlaceholder.VisibleDuration
histogram.summary: Android: How long the content suggestion placeholder is shown. This is tracked based on when the placeholder is enabled in the UI, not how long it is actually visible on screen. Depending on the screen size, the used value could be bigger.

histogram.name: ContentSuggestions.FetchPendingSpinner.VisibleDuration
histogram.summary: Android: How long the content suggestion loading spinner is shown. This is tracked based on when the spinner is enabled in the UI, not how long it is actually visible on screen. Depending on the screen size, the used value could be bigger.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.CancelSystemTouches
histogram.summary: Logs true when the system touches are cancelled in order to show the Context Menu instead of performing a default system action. Only logged on iOS.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.DelayedElementDetails
histogram.summary: Logged when DOM element details are not yet available when the user completes a long press. Also logs if the context menu was shown once the element details became available or if the display was cancelled. This ensures that all context menu gestures are eventually processed. Only logged on iOS.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.DOMElementFetchDuration
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2019. iFrame Context Menu feature shipped.
histogram.summary: How long it takes to fetch the details of the DOM element the user is selecting for the context menu. On iOS, JavaScript must be run in order to get the details of the DOM element. If the element is not inside the main frame, latency could be added due to the communication between frames. Only logged on iOS.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.DOMElementFrame
histogram.summary: Logged when the Context Menu will be displayed. Also logs if the element for the last touch was found in the main frame or a iframe. Only logged on iOS.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.iOS.CopyImage
histogram.summary: Logged when the copying image from context menu is invoked and relevant events happen during the copying image procedure.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.iOS.GetImageDataByJsResult
histogram.summary: Result of getting image data by JavaScript for CopyImage, SaveImage and SearchByImage from iOS context menu. Recorded when the JavaScript returns, or fails for timeout.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.LensSupportStatus
histogram.summary: Whether the user supports the Lens intent and if not, the reason why. Recorded only if the feature is enabled when the long-press context menu is shown for an image.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.SaveLinkType
histogram.summary: Type of the url when user saves the content through the save link context menu.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.SelectedOption
histogram.obsolete: Removed from code as of 5/2019. WARNING: The code emitting this histogram was broken on desktop, and logged many items that should not have been logged at all into bucket 0. The values for bucket 0 are wrong and should not be relied upon. The replacements are ContextMenu.SelectedOptionDesktop, ContextMenu.SelectedOptionAndroid, and ContextMenu.SelectedOptionIOS.
histogram.summary: The option that the user selected from a context menu. This mis-logged and thus was removed.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.SelectedOptionAndroid
histogram.summary:
The option that the user selected from a context menu on Android. This is logged on Android only; check out ContextMenu.SelectedOptionIOS for iOS, and ContextMenu.SelectedOptionDesktop for desktop. Note that if a menu is invoked on mixed content, only one of the submetrics receives the log; read the code to determine which one.
This histogram is of special interest to the chrome-analysis-team@. Do not change its semantics or retire it without talking to them first.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.SelectedOptionDesktop
histogram.summary: The option that the user selected from a context menu on desktop. This is logged on desktop only; check out ContextMenu.SelectedOptionIOS for iOS, and ContextMenu.SelectedOptionAndroid for Android. Note that if a menu is invoked on mixed content, only one of the submetrics receives the log; read the code to determine which one.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.SelectedOptionIOS
histogram.summary:
The option that the user selected from a context menu on iOS. This is logged on iOS only; check out ContextMenu.SelectedOptionAndroid for Android, and ContextMenu.SelectedOptionDesktop for desktop. Note that if a menu is invoked on mixed content, only one of the submetrics receives the log; read the code to determine which one.
This histogram is of special interest to the chrome-analysis-team@. Do not change its semantics or retire it without talking to them first.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.Shown
histogram.summary: Recorded when a context menu is shown, sliced by whether a web contents was present. (It generally should be; indeed, this code path should not be followed when it is absent. For instance, invoking a context menu from the new tab page or from the sad tab page will not cause an emission to this histogram.) Note that this refers to link context menus, not the cut-copy-paste context menus that can appear during long presses on text on a web page or long presses in the omnibox. This is an Android-only metric; compare with RenderViewContextMenu.Shown and RenderViewContextMenu.Used on desktop.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.ThumbnailFetched
histogram.summary: Logged when the callback to fetch the thumbnail returns a result: Success (bitmap data received) or Failure (null bitmap). Android only.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.TimeToTakeAction.Abandoned
histogram.summary: The time it takes for the user to abandon the context menu after it's shown by pressing back or touching outside the menu. This is only logged if the menu is abandoned. See ContextMenu.TimeToTakeAction.SelectedItem for the cases where it is closed as a result of user interaction with the menu. Android only.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.TimeToTakeAction.SelectedItem
histogram.summary: The time it takes for the user to select an item. This is only logged if the menu is closed as a result of the user clicking a menu item or a DirectShare button. See ContextMenu.TimeToTakeAction.Abandoned for the cases where it is abandoned by the user. Android only.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.UnexpectedFindElementResultHandlerMessage
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2019. iFrame Context Menu feature shipped.
histogram.summary: Logged when an unexpected FindElementResultHandler message was received by the application. An unexpected message may be sent by a malicious frame. Only logged on iOS.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.URLClicked
histogram.summary: Logged when the URL is clicked. Android only.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.ViewsTextServices.Emoji
histogram.summary: Number of times the emoji item in the views text services context menu is used.

histogram.name: ContextMenu.WaitingForElementDetails
histogram.summary: Logs true when the context menu gesture recognizer fully recognizes a long press, but is still waiting on the DOM element details before the context menu can be shown. Only logged on iOS.

histogram.name: ContextualSuggestions.EnabledState
histogram.obsolete: This feature was deprecated in M74
histogram.summary: Android: Whether contextual suggestions are enabled. Recorded when the enabled state changes. Note that this may be recorded multiple times per session.

histogram.name: ContextualSuggestions.Events
histogram.obsolete: This feature was deprecated in M74
histogram.summary: Records each event handled by the Contextual Suggestions backend. Recorded when the event is sent to the backend, typically when a UI or network action is registered. Implemented for Android.

histogram.name: ContextualSuggestions.FetchErrorCode
histogram.obsolete: This feature was deprecated in M74
histogram.summary: Android: The net::Error status code of contextual suggestion fetches. Recorded when a fetch completes.

histogram.name: ContextualSuggestions.FetchLatencyMilliseconds
histogram.obsolete: This feature was deprecated in M74
histogram.summary: Android: the amount of time taken elapsed between a contextual suggestion fetch starting and ending. Recorded when a fetch is completed.

histogram.name: ContextualSuggestions.FetchRequestProtoSizeKB
histogram.obsolete: This feature was deprecated in M74
histogram.summary: Android: The size of the serialized and base64-encoded proto sent to the server when fetching contextual suggestions. Recorded when a fetch is

histogram.name: ContextualSuggestions.FetchResponseCode
histogram.obsolete: This feature was deprecated in M74
histogram.summary: Android: The http status code of contextual suggestion fetches. Recorded when a fetch completes.

histogram.name: ContextualSuggestions.FetchResponseNetworkBytes
histogram.obsolete: This feature was deprecated in M75
histogram.summary: Android: The number of bytes in the response received when a contextual suggestions fetch completes. Recorded when the response is received.

histogram.name: ContextualSuggestions.FetchResponseSizeKB
histogram.obsolete: This feature was deprecated in M74
histogram.summary: Android: The size of the response body received when a contextual suggestions fetch completes. Recorded when the response is received.

histogram.name: ContextualSuggestions.PageViewClickLength
histogram.obsolete: This feature was deprecated in M74
histogram.summary: Android: The length of a visit on a page. Reported for HTTP and HTTPS pages provided there was something visible printed to the screen. Selecting a tab or loading a new URL in current tab starts timing a visit. Switching to a different tab, closing the tab or loading a new URL finishes timing and reports the time if the page did a visually non-empty paint or finished loading. Bucketed by short/medium/long.

histogram.name: ContextualSuggestions.PageViewTime
histogram.obsolete: This feature was deprecated in M74
histogram.summary: Android: The length of a visit on a page. Reported for HTTP and HTTPS pages provided there was something visible printed to the screen. Selecting a tab or loading a new URL in current tab starts timing a visit. Switching to a different tab, closing the tab or loading a new URL finishes timing and reports the time if the page did a visually non-empty paint or finished loading.

histogram.name: ContextualSuggestions.Preference.State
histogram.obsolete: This feature was deprecated in M74
histogram.summary: Android: Whether the user preference for contextual suggestions is enabled. Recorded on cold start and when the user has changed the preference state. Note that this may be recorded multiple times per session.

histogram.name: ContextualSuggestions.ResultsReturnedInOverviewMode
histogram.summary: Android: Whether the contextual suggestions results are returned to the UI-layer while the user is in overview mode.

histogram.name: ContextualSuggestions.SuggestionClickPosition.Cluster
histogram.obsolete: This feature was deprecated in M74
histogram.summary: Android: The position of the clicked contextual suggestion card within its cluster.

histogram.name: ContextualSuggestions.SuggestionClickPosition.Global
histogram.obsolete: This feature was deprecated in M74
histogram.summary: Android: The position of the clicked contextual suggestion card within the entire list of suggestions.

histogram.name: Conversions.ClearDataTime
histogram.summary: Records the time it takes to clear conversion data. Logged any time site data is cleared in Chrome.

histogram.name: Conversions.ExtraReportDelay
histogram.summary: Records the "extra" non-scheduled time it took to send a particular conversion report. This is primarily due to a report's scheduled report time taking place while the browser is closed. Recorded when a conversion report is sent.

histogram.name: Conversions.RegisteredConversionsPerPage
histogram.summary: Records the number of registered conversions (https://github.com/WICG/conversion-measurement-api) on a given top level page load. Recorded when the page navigates away or is otherwise closed. Only recorded for non-off-the-record profiles (OTR profiles have the feature disabled).

histogram.name: Conversions.ReportStatus
histogram.summary: Records the high level request status of a conversion report. Recorded for every conversion report sent.

histogram.name: Cookie.AgeFor
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019-10-31 as part of cookie histogram cleanup (https://crbug.com/993120).
histogram.summary: Records the age (in days) of the oldest cookie delivered along with a request. Recorded when setting the `Cookie` header for a given request.

histogram.name: Cookie.AllAgesFor
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019-10-31 as part of cookie histogram cleanup (https://crbug.com/993120).
histogram.summary: Records the age (in days) of all cookies delivered along with a request. Recorded when setting the `Cookie` header for a given request.

histogram.name: Cookie.BackingStoreUpdateResults
histogram.summary: Whether or not updates to the backing store succeeded or failed, recorded every update.

histogram.name: Cookie.BetweenAccessIntervalMinutes
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2015-08-17 as part of cookie histogram cleanup (https://crbug.com/521135).
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Intervals between access time updates for each cookie.

histogram.name: Cookie.CommitProblem
histogram.summary: Recorded when a problem occurs trying to commit changes to the cookie store back to disk, in the SQLite store.

histogram.name: Cookie.CookieDeleteEquivalent
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019-10-23 as part of cookie histogram cleanup (https://crbug.com/993120).
histogram.summary: Record when at attempt is made to delete an equivalent cookie on a set cookie operation and when such a cookie is found and deleted. Also measure when strict secure cookie rules affect this outcome and prevent a cookie from being deleted.

histogram.name: Cookie.CookieLineCookieValueValidity
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2017-07-14 as it demonstrated that making cookie parsing rules stricter was not feasible. See https://crbug.com/638117.
histogram.summary: Indicates whether a cookie initialised from a Set-Cookie header value was valid or not. This is only logged after cookies containing control characters have already been rejected, so "Invalid" here means that the cookie contains quotes, commas, backslashes or top-bit-set characters.

histogram.name: Cookie.CookiePrefix
histogram.summary: Number of times a cookie was set with a name prefixed by "__Secure-" or "__Host-" (prefixes reserved by https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-west-cookie-prefixes).

histogram.name: Cookie.CookiePrefixBlocked
histogram.summary: Number of times a cookie was set with a name prefixed by "__Secure-" or "__Host-" that violate cookie prefix rules. That is, a __Secure- cookie must be set over a secure connection with the Secure attribute, and a __Host- cookie must be set over a secure connection, with the Secure attribute, with no Domain attribute, and with a Path attribute of "/". (As defined in https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-west-cookie-prefixes.)

histogram.name: Cookie.CookieSchemeRequestScheme
histogram.summary: The scheme a cookie was set by and the scheme of the request that the cookie was sent to. This is recorded once per cookie each time a URL request has its headers set.

histogram.name: Cookie.CookieSourceScheme
histogram.summary: For each cookie added to the store, record whether its source URL has a cryptographic scheme, broken down by Secure and not Secure.

histogram.name: Cookie.CorruptMetaTable
histogram.summary: Records the detection of a corrupted meta table. See http://crbug.com/111376 .

histogram.name: Cookie.CorruptMetaTableRecoveryFailed
histogram.summary: Records a failure to recover a corrupted meta table. See http://crbug.com/111376 .

histogram.name: Cookie.Count
histogram.summary: Number of cookies in the store (recorded every 10 minutes of active browsing time)

histogram.name: Cookie.DBSizeInKB
histogram.summary: The size, on disk, of the cookie database as it is being loaded.

histogram.name: Cookie.DeletionCause
histogram.obsolete: Removed Jul 2017 for lack of use.
histogram.summary: For each cookie removed from the store, the reason it was removed.

histogram.name: Cookie.DomainCount
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2015-08-17 as part of cookie histogram cleanup (https://crbug.com/521135).
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: For each domain, number of cookies in that domain (recorded every 10 minutes of active browsing time).

histogram.name: Cookie.DomainPerEtldp1Count
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2015-08-17 as part of cookie histogram cleanup (https://crbug.com/521135).
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: For every top level domain, number of subdomains in that top level domain (recorded every 10 minutes of active browsing time).

histogram.name: Cookie.Etldp1Count
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2015-08-17 as part of cookie histogram cleanup (https://crbug.com/521135).
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: For every top level domain, number of cookies in that domain (recorded every 10 minutes of active browsing time).

histogram.name: Cookie.EvictedLastAccessMinutes
histogram.obsolete: Removed Jul 2017 for lack of use.
histogram.summary: For each evicted (not expired) cookie, the amount of time since it was last used

histogram.name: Cookie.ExpirationDurationMinutes
histogram.summary: Number of minutes until cookie expires when set.

histogram.name: Cookie.GetCookieListCompletionTime
histogram.obsolete: Removed Feb 2019 as further data collection is no longer necessary.
histogram.summary: The length of time between when the network stack receives a request for a cookie list and when it actually responds to the request or abandons it (for e.g. a closed renderer).

histogram.name: Cookie.HeaderLength
histogram.summary: The size of each outgoing request's 'Cookie' header field's value, in bytes.

histogram.name: Cookie.IncludedRequestEffectiveSameSite
histogram.summary: The value of the effective samesite for cookies that will be included in a request. Logged once per successful inclusion.

histogram.name: Cookie.IncludedResponseEffectiveSameSite
histogram.summary: The value of the effective samesite for cookies that will be accepted from a response. Logged once per successful inclusion.

histogram.name: Cookie.KillDatabaseResult
histogram.summary: Whether killing the database because it was corrupted beyond repair succeeded.

histogram.name: Cookie.LaxAllowUnsafeCookieIncludedAge
histogram.summary: The age in milliseconds of a cookie that activates the Lax-allow-unsafe intervention. Emitted whenever a cookie is included in a request with an unsafe HTTP method, where the cookie has an unspecified SameSite attribute and is only included because it is new enough to fall under the age threshold for allowing Lax-allow-unsafe.

histogram.name: Cookie.LoadProblem
histogram.summary: Recorded when a problem is recorded when loading the persistent cookie store.

histogram.name: Cookie.NetworkSecurity
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019-10-31 as part of cookie histogram cleanup (https://crbug.com/993120).
histogram.summary: Degree of protection against cookie theft. Recorded for each cookie when setting the `Cookie` header for a request.

histogram.name: Cookie.NumberOfLoadedCookies
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019-07-03. Cookie.Count measures similar things with different timing.
histogram.summary: This histogram records the total number of cookies loaded from disk, including any cookies that are discarded during loading (for whatever reason).

histogram.name: Cookie.NumDomainPurgedKeys
histogram.summary: Records the number of distinct keys (eTLD+1's) that have experienced eviction of a non-expired cookie due to reaching the max number of cookies per key. This is not persisted across network service restarts, so it represents the number of such eTLD+1's that have relatively recently added new cookies that went over the limit (since the last browser restart, crash, etc). Recorded every 10 minutes of active browsing time.

histogram.name: Cookie.NumKeys
histogram.summary: Records the number of distinct keys (eTLD+1's) that have any stored cookies (which may or may not be expired). Recorded every 10 minutes of active browsing time.

histogram.name: Cookie.ParsedCookieStatus
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 9/2013. Experiment to measure control characters in cookies is finished.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: When parsing a cookie, indicates if control characters were present in any of the cookie values and if any of the cookie values were invalid. Specifically, checks that all of the parsed values are valid according to the valid token definition in Section 2.2 of RFC2616 which specifies a token must have no separators (i.e. no characters from the following string, ignoring the starting and ending single quote: '()<>@,;:\"/[]?={} \t') and no control characters.

histogram.name: Cookie.PriorityBlockingTime
histogram.summary: This histogram records the total duration of time during which at least one web request was blocked waiting for the cookie store to load. If no requests were affected, the value is 0. If two requests are simultaneously blocked for 1 second, the value is 1 second. If two requests are consecutively blocked for 1 second each, the value is two seconds.

histogram.name: Cookie.PriorityLoadCount
histogram.summary: The number of distinct Effective Top-Level Domains Plus One (ETLD+1, i.e., google.com, bbc.co.uk) for which a priority cookie load occurred.

histogram.name: Cookie.ReinstatedCookies
histogram.summary: The duration in seconds between a cookie getting evicted (due to the number of cookies exceeding a domain limit), and subsequently reinstated.

histogram.name: Cookie.RequestSameSiteContext
histogram.summary: The same-site context of a request, i.e. the relationship between the request and the navigational environment. Logged on every request to access a cookie.

histogram.name: Cookie.SameSiteAttributeValue
histogram.summary: The value of the cookie's SameSite attribute, if any. This is logged once per created cookie, upon parsing of the SameSite attribute from a string.

histogram.name: Cookie.SameSiteContextDowngradeRequest
histogram.summary:
The type of breaking same-site context downgrade a cookie is experiencing along with the secureness of the origin that we're attempting to send the cookie to. Logged once per included cookie or if the cookie was excluded only due to insufficient same-site context.
For more information on breaking context downgrades please see the comment block above net::CanonicalCookie::CookieInclusionStatus::WARN_STRICT_LAX_DOWNGRADE_STRICT_SAMESITE.

histogram.name: Cookie.SameSiteContextDowngradeResponse
histogram.summary:
The type of breaking same-site context downgrade a cookie is experiencing along with the secureness of the origin that is attempting to set the cookie. Logged once per included cookie or if the cookie was excluded only due to insufficient same-site context.
For more information on breaking context downgrades please see the comment block above net::CanonicalCookie::CookieInclusionStatus::WARN_STRICT_LAX_DOWNGRADE_STRICT_SAMESITE.

histogram.name: Cookie.SameSiteDifferentSchemeRequest
histogram.obsolete: Removed Apr 2020 as this metric doesn't capture the correct information. https://crbug.com/1066231
histogram.summary: The value of the SameSiteCookieContext if a Lax or Strict cookie is being sent http-to-https or https-to-http for the site-for-cookies to the request. Logged once per cookie sent with the above conditions.

histogram.name: Cookie.SameSiteDifferentSchemeResponse
histogram.obsolete: Removed Apr 2020 as this metric doesn't capture the correct information. https://crbug.com/1066231
histogram.summary: The value of the SameSiteCookieContext if a Lax or Strict cookie is being set http-to-https or https-to-http for the site-for-cookies from the response. Logged once per cookie sent with the above conditions.

histogram.name: Cookie.SameSiteNoneIsSecure
histogram.summary: If a cookie was SameSite=None, this records whether or not it was Secure. Logged once per attempt to set a SameSite=None cookie.

histogram.name: Cookie.SameSiteUnspecifiedEffective
histogram.summary: If a cookie had an unspecified SameSite attribute, this records what mode it was treated as, i.e. what the effective SameSite mode is. Logged once per attempt to determine SameSite inclusion/exclusion for an unspecified-SameSite cookie.

histogram.name: Cookie.SameSiteUnspecifiedTooOldToAllowUnsafe
histogram.summary: The age in milliseconds of a cookie that would have activated the Lax-allow-unsafe intervention except for the fact that it is too old. Emitted whenever a cookie is excluded from being sent on a request with an unsafe method, where the cookie has an unspecified SameSite attribute and is too old to fall under the age threshold to allow Lax-allow-unsafe.

histogram.name: Cookie.SetAttributePairCharsValidity
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 9/2013. Experiment to measure control characters in cookies is finished.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Indicates whether a cookie attribute pair was set with both a valid key and a valid attribute value or not. For the key, this implies that it was a valid token as defined in Section 2.2 of RFC2616 which specifies a token must have no separators (i.e. no characters from the following string, ignoring the starting and ending single quote: '()<>@,;:\"/[]?={} \t') and no control characters. For the value, this implies that it contained no control characters and no semicolon.

histogram.name: Cookie.SetNameValidity
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 9/2013. Experiment to measure control characters in cookies is finished.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Indicates whether a cookie name was set with a valid token. A valid token is defined in Section 2.2 of RFC2616 which specifies a token must have no separators (i.e. no characters from the following string, ignoring the starting and ending single quote: '()<>@,;:\"/[]?={} \t') and no control characters.

histogram.name: Cookie.SetValueCookieValueValidity
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 9/2013. Experiment to measure control characters in cookies is finished.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Indicates whether a cookie value was valid or invalid when there was an attempt to set it, where a valid value is defined in RFC 6265 as ASCII characters excluding controls, whitspace, comma, semicolon, and backslash.

histogram.name: Cookie.Shutdown.NumberOfCookiesDeleted
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 9/2015. The associated experiment was flawed.
histogram.summary: The number of session cookies deleted on shutdown. This metric is emitted even if no cookies are deleted.

histogram.name: Cookie.Shutdown.TimeSpentDeletingCookies
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 9/2015. The associated experiment was flawed.
histogram.summary: The amount of time required to delete session cookies on shutdown. This metric is emitted even if no cookies are deleted.

histogram.name: Cookie.Startup.NumberOfCookiesDeleted
histogram.summary: The number of session cookies deleted on startup. This metric is emitted even if no cookies are deleted. This metric is not emitted if session cookies are restored, rather than deleted on startup.

histogram.name: Cookie.Startup.TimeSpentDeletingCookies
histogram.summary: The amount of time required to delete session cookies on startup. This metric is emitted even if no cookies are deleted. This metric is not emitted if session cookies are restored, rather than deleted on startup.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeBlockedOnLoad
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) between the cookie store load starting and completing.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeDatabaseMigrationToV10
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) to migrate a v9 cookie database to v10.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeDatabaseMigrationToV11
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) to migrate a v10 cookie database to v11. Migration occurs upon first startup of a browser version with v11 database code.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeDatabaseMigrationToV12
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) to migrate a v11 cookie database to v12. Migration occurs upon first startup of a browser version with v12 database code.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeDatabaseMigrationToV5
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 04/2015. The migration has finished for most users.
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) to migrate a v4 database to v5.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeDatabaseMigrationToV6
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 04/2015. The migration has finished for most users.
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) to migrate a v5 database to v6.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeDatabaseMigrationToV7
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 04/2015. The migration has finished for most users.
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) to migrate a v6 database to v7.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeDatabaseMigrationToV8
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 04/2015. The migration has finished for most users.
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) to migrate a v7 database to v8.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeDatabaseMigrationToV9
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) to migrate a v8 database to v9.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeGet
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) to get cookies for each URL request.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeInitializeDB
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) to initialize the cookies database.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeInitializeDomainMap
histogram.obsolete: Removed as part of histogram expiry. See discussion in crbug.com/975202.
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) to read and parse the domain map from the cookies database.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeKeyLoadDBQueueWait
histogram.summary: This histogram records the wall-clock delay between a priority load task being posted to the DB-thread and its execution.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeKeyLoadTotalWait
histogram.summary: This histogram records the the total amount of time spent fetching the cookies associated with an eTLD+1 from disk, including time spent waiting in task queues.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeLoad
histogram.summary: This histogram records the sum of the durations of all initial tasks loading cookies from the database.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeLoadDBQueueWait
histogram.summary: This histogram records the wall-clock delay between the Load task being posted to the DB-thread and its execution.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeLoadDomains
histogram.obsolete: Removed as part of histogram expiry. See discussion in crbug.com/975202.
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) to read the domain map from the cookies database.

histogram.name: Cookie.TimeParseDomains
histogram.obsolete: Removed as part of histogram expiry. See discussion in crbug.com/975202.
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) to parse the domains already loaded from the cookies database and put them in the domain map.

histogram.name: Cookie.Type
histogram.summary: For each cookie added to the store, record it's type(s).

histogram.name: CopylessPaste.CacheHit
histogram.summary: The cache hit status of metadata extraction for AppIndexing.

histogram.name: CopylessPaste.ExtractionFailedUs
histogram.summary:
The time spent on scanning the document in the main frame to extract the metadata. The metadata would be used to feed Icing for CopylessPaste feature on Android. This only counts pages with failed or empty JSON extraction.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: CopylessPaste.ExtractionStatus
histogram.summary: The error status of metadata extraction for AppIndexing.

histogram.name: CopylessPaste.ExtractionUs
histogram.summary:
The time spent on scanning the document in the main frame to extract the metadata. The metadata would be used to feed Icing for CopylessPaste feature on Android. This only counts pages with successful JSON extraction.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Cras.Busyloop
histogram.summary: For each audio thread running state, records the number of busyloops. Busyloop means the audio thread doen't sleep during more than two loops, which may make the system busy. Depending on the time of running state, the records is seperated into three logs: ShortPeriod(shorter than 10 minutes), MediumPeriod(between 10 and 60 minutes), LongPeriod (longer than 60 minutes).

histogram.name: Cras.DeviceTypeInput
histogram.summary: It is used for logging input device usage of ChromeOS users. The count indicates the number of each input device has been used and is recorded when a device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.DeviceTypeOutput
histogram.summary: It is used for logging output device usage of ChromeOS users. The count indicates the number of each input device has been used and is recorded when a device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.HfpBatteryIndicatorSupported
histogram.summary: For each HFP audio device record whether it supports battery indicator.

histogram.name: Cras.HfpBatteryReport
histogram.summary: For each HFP battery level change event record if it comes from the command defined by Apple's Spec or BT HFP spec. The count indicates how frequent user might see this event and can help us decide which data source we prefer to show if the headset supports both.

histogram.name: Cras.HfpScoConnectionError
histogram.summary: For each SCO connection record the returned event. The event indicates if the setup of the connection is successful or not.

histogram.name: Cras.HfpWidebandSpeechPacketLoss
histogram.summary: For each HFP audio device supporting WBS record how many packets are lost per 1000 packets when the device is closed. This is for tracking how bad the packet loss can be for WBS in real world.

histogram.name: Cras.HfpWidebandSpeechSupported
histogram.summary: For each single connection of bluetooth HFP audio device record whether it supports wideband-speech feature or not.

histogram.name: Cras.HighestDeviceDelayInput
histogram.summary: The highest hardware delay of input device in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). It is calculated by 1000 * highest_hw_level / largest_cb_level. The highest_hw_level is the highest remaining frames in the device. The largest_cb_level is the largest callback threshold of streams which have attached to this device. The value is recorded when the device is removed.

histogram.name: Cras.HighestDeviceDelayOutput
histogram.summary: The highest hardware delay of output device in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). It is calculated by 1000 * highest_hw_level / largest_cb_level. The highest_hw_level is the highest remaining frames in the device. The largest_cb_level is the largest callback threshold of streams which have attached to this device. The value is recorded when the device is removed.

histogram.name: Cras.HighestInputHardwareLevel
histogram.summary: The highest hardware level of input device in CRAS(Chrome OS audio server). The hardware level is the remaining frames in the device. For input device, it can show the latency between capturing and actual reading.

histogram.name: Cras.HighestOutputHardwareLevel
histogram.summary: The highest hardware level of output device in CRAS(Chrome OS audio server). The hardware level is the remaining frames in the device. For output device, it can show the latency between writing and actual playout.

histogram.name: Cras.InputDeviceA2DPRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of input device A2DP in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.InputDeviceAbnormalFallbackRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of fallback device in the abnormal situation. The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.InputDeviceBluetoothNarrowBandMicRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of input device Bluetooth narrowband in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.InputDeviceBluetoothRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of Bluetooth input device that doesn't fall to any other category we defined. i.e HSP, narrowband or wideband. The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.InputDeviceBluetoothWideBandMicRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of input device Bluetooth wideband in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.InputDeviceFrontMicRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of input device InternalMic in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.InputDeviceHFPRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of input device HFP in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.InputDeviceHotwordRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of input device Hotword in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.InputDeviceHSPRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of input device HSP in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.InputDeviceInternalMicRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of input device InternalMic in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.InputDeviceKeyboardMicRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of input device InternalMic in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.InputDeviceMicRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of input device Mic in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.InputDeviceNormalFallbackRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of fallback device in the normal situation. The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.InputDevicePostDspLoopbackRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of input device PostDspLoopback in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.InputDevicePostMixLoopbackRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of input device PostMixLoopback in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.InputDeviceRearMicRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of input device InternalMic in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.InputDeviceSilentHotwordRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of silent hotword device in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.InputDeviceUnknownRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of input device Unknown in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.InputDeviceUSBRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of input device USB in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.InputStreamRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of input stream in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between creating and destroying a stream. The value is recorded when the stream is destroyed.

histogram.name: Cras.kHfpWidebandSpeechSelectedCodec
histogram.summary: For each single connection of bluetooth HFP record the result of codec negotiation, if supported.

histogram.name: Cras.MissedCallbackFirstTimeInput
histogram.summary: The first time missed callbacks happened on input streams in CRAS (Chrome OS audio server). In normal situation, audio thread should follow the callback schedule to send frames to clients. However, sometimes it may miss schedule because of some reasons. When the first time that audio thread misses callback schedule, record how many seconds have passed since stream started running.

histogram.name: Cras.MissedCallbackFirstTimeOutput
histogram.summary: The first time missed callbacks happened on output streams in CRAS (Chrome OS audio server). In normal situation, audio thread should follow the callback schedule to fetch frames from clients. However, sometimes it may miss schedule because of some reasons. When the first time that audio thread misses callback schedule, record how many seconds have passed since stream started running.

histogram.name: Cras.MissedCallbackFrequencyAfterReschedulingInput
histogram.summary: The number of missed callbacks of input streams in a day after rescheduling. It is calculated by number of the missed callbacks / runtime of the stream after rescheduling. This value is recorded when the stream is removed.

histogram.name: Cras.MissedCallbackFrequencyAfterReschedulingOutput
histogram.summary: The number of missed callbacks of output streams in a day after rescheduling. It is calculated by number of the missed callbacks / runtime of the stream after rescheduling. This value is recorded when the stream is removed.

histogram.name: Cras.MissedCallbackFrequencyInput
histogram.summary: The number of missed callbacks of input streams in a day. It is calculated by number of the missed callbacks / runtime of the stream. This value is recorded when the stream is removed.

histogram.name: Cras.MissedCallbackFrequencyOutput
histogram.summary: The number of missed callbacks of output streams in a day. It is calculated by number of the missed callbacks / runtime of the stream. This value is recorded when the stream is removed.

histogram.name: Cras.MissedCallbackSecondTimeInput
histogram.summary: The second time missed callbacks happened on input streams in CRAS (Chrome OS audio server). When the second time audio thread misses callback schedule, record how many seconds have passed since the first time.

histogram.name: Cras.MissedCallbackSecondTimeOutput
histogram.summary: The second time missed callbacks happened on output streams in CRAS (Chrome OS audio server). When the second time audio thread misses callback schedule, record how many seconds have passed since the first time.

histogram.name: Cras.OutputDeviceA2DPRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of output device A2DP in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.OutputDeviceAbnormalFallbackRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of fallback device in the abnormal situation. The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.OutputDeviceBluetoothRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of Bluetooth output device in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server) that doesn't belong to any category we defined. i.e A2DP, HFP or HSP. The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.OutputDeviceHapticRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of output device Haptic in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.OutputDeviceHDMIRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of output device HDMI in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.OutputDeviceHeadphoneRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of output device Headphone in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.OutputDeviceHFPRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of output device HFP in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.OutputDeviceHSPRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of output device HSP in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.OutputDeviceInternalSpeakerRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of output device InternalSpeaker in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.OutputDeviceLineoutRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of output device Lineout in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.OutputDeviceNormalFallbackRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of fallback device in the normal situation. The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.OutputDeviceUnknownRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of output device Unknown in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.OutputDeviceUSBRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of output device USB in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between opening and closing a device. The value is recorded when the device is closed.

histogram.name: Cras.OutputStreamRuntime
histogram.summary: The runtime of output stream in CRAS(ChromeOS audio server). The runtime is the duration between creating and destroying a stream. The value is recorded when the stream is destroyed.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamCallbackThreshold
histogram.summary: The callback threshold of stream in CRAS(Chrome OS audio server). It is the number of frames when to request more from the client. This value is recorded when the stream is created.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamClientTypeInput
histogram.summary: The client type of input stream in CRAS(Chrome OS audio server). This value is recorded when the stream is created.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamClientTypeOutput
histogram.summary: The client type of output stream in CRAS(Chrome OS audio server). This value is recorded when the stream is created.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamEffects
histogram.summary: The bit map of effects to be enabled on this stream. This value is recorded when the stream is created.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamFlags
histogram.summary: The flags of stream in CRAS(Chrome OS audio server). It is the special handling for stream. This value is recorded when the stream is created.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamSamplingFormat
histogram.summary: The sampling format of stream in CRAS(Chrome OS audio server). This value is recorded when the stream is created.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamSamplingRate
histogram.summary: The sampling rate of stream in CRAS(Chrome OS audio server). This value is recorded when the stream is created.

histogram.name: Cras.StreamTimeoutMilliSeconds
histogram.summary: The longest additional time CRAS(Chrome OS audio server) ever waits for a stream exceeding the timeout threshold. This value is recorded per stream when it gets removed and used to investigate the audio glitch/skip problem on Chrome OS.

histogram.name: Cras.UnderrunsPerDevice
histogram.summary: The number of underruns happened on one audio device. Underrun here means there are no samples in device buffer while device is playing. It is recorded when device is closed by CRAS(Chrome OS audio server).

histogram.name: CrashExitCodes.Extension
histogram.summary: The exit codes for crashed extension processes. Note: Due to https://crbug.com/805754, the enum labels are off by 1 for M56 and earlier.

histogram.name: CrashExitCodes.Renderer
histogram.summary: The exit codes for crashed renderer processes. Note: Due to https://crbug.com/805754, the enum labels are off by 1 for M56 and earlier.

histogram.name: Crashpad.CrashReportPending
histogram.summary: The reason that a crash report entered the upload pending state.

histogram.name: Crashpad.CrashReportSize
histogram.summary: The size of a crash report minidump on disk when captured.

histogram.name: Crashpad.CrashUpload.AttemptSuccessful
histogram.summary: Logs whether a crash uploaded succeeded or failed.

histogram.name: Crashpad.CrashUpload.Skipped
histogram.summary: The reason that a crash report upload was skipped.

histogram.name: Crashpad.ExceptionCaptureResult
histogram.summary: The outcome of execution of the Crashpad exception handler.

histogram.name: Crashpad.ExceptionCode.Mac
histogram.summary: The exception code encountered in a crash on Mac OS X.

histogram.name: Crashpad.ExceptionCode.Win
histogram.summary: The exception code encountered in a crash on Windows.

histogram.name: Crashpad.ExceptionEncountered
histogram.summary: A count of the number of exceptions handled by Crashpad with two buckets for starting and finishing.

histogram.name: Crashpad.HandlerCrash.ExceptionCode.Mac
histogram.summary: The exception code encountered for a crash of the crash handler process on Mac OS X.
