histogram.name: Crashpad.HandlerCrash.ExceptionCode.Win
histogram.summary: The exception code encountered for a crash of the crash handler process on Windows.

histogram.name: Crashpad.HandlerLifetimeMilestone
histogram.summary: Handler start/crash/exit events.

histogram.name: CrashReport.BreakpadCrashDumpOutcome
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 04/2016 as CrashPad does not implement this.
histogram.summary: Reports the result of an attempt to report a crash dump via Breakpad. Success indicates only that the out-of-process crash reporter succesfully generated a dump. The success or failure of the eventual upload is not measured. This stability metric is logged from the first Chrome launch post-dump.

histogram.name: CrashReport.BreakpadDumpWithoutCrashOutcome
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 04/2016 as CrashPad does not implement this.
histogram.summary: Reports the result of an attempt to report a dump via Breakpad without crashing. Success indicates only that the out-of-process crash reporter succesfully generated a dump. The success or failure of the eventual upload is not measured. This stability metric is logged from the first Chrome launch post-dump.

histogram.name: CrashReport.BreakpadIOSUploadOutcome
histogram.summary: Reports the Foundation error code of an attempt to upload a breakpad crash report. "NSNoError" if the upload was successful.

histogram.name: CrashReport.CrashBackgroundUploadDelay
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 03/2018 when Chrome for iOS stopped uploading crash dumps in the background.
histogram.summary: Time delta between when the crash took place and when the crash was uploaded by the app as a background task. Only logged on iOS.

histogram.name: CrashReport.DumpWithoutCrashingHandler.FromInitSharedMemoryIfNeeded
histogram.summary: Which crash handler (Breakpad or Crashpad) is handling ProducerClient::InitSharedMemoryIfNeeded()'s attempt to do a DumpWithoutCrashing. Investigating why Breakpad never seems to generate InitSharedMemoryIfNeeded reports on some ChromeOS devices.

histogram.name: CrashReport.DumpWithoutCrashingResult.FromInitSharedMemoryIfNeeded
histogram.summary: Did the attempt to dump without crashing in ProducerClient::InitSharedMemoryIfNeeded() succeed or fail? Investigating why Breakpad never seems to generate InitSharedMemoryIfNeeded reports on some ChromeOS devices.

histogram.name: CrashReport.DumpWithoutCrashingResult.FromInitSharedMemoryIfNeeded2
histogram.summary: Did the attempt to dump without crashing in ProducerClient::InitSharedMemoryIfNeeded() succeed or fail? Investigating why Breakpad never seems to generate InitSharedMemoryIfNeeded reports on some ChromeOS devices.

histogram.name: CrashReport.PendingReportsOnBackgroundWakeUp
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 03/2018 when Chrome for iOS stopped uploading crash dumps in the background.
histogram.summary: The number of crash reports present when the app wakes up as a background task to upload crash reports. Only logged on iOS.

histogram.name: CrashReport.PermanentUploadFailure
histogram.obsolete: Removed 01/2017 in Issue 680162 with the removal of Kasko.
histogram.summary: Counts crash reports that the Kasko crash reporter fails to upload and hands off to Chrome. Kasko stores the failed reports in a location specified by Chrome. Chrome records this metric when it detects new minidump files in that location. Currently the reports are discarded, but they could eventually be uploaded via the Chrome network stack.

histogram.name: Cros.ClickOnShelf
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 12/2013. Default pinned apps trial is finished.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Chrome OS shelf clicks.

histogram.name: Cros.CrOSActionRecorderEvent
histogram.summary: This histogram is emitted each time when CrOSAction is recorded by the CrOSActionRecorder. For each CrOSAction multiple values can be emitted, corresponding to different events happened during the action processing by CrOSActionRecorder.

histogram.name: CrosDisks.ArchiveType
histogram.summary: The type of archive file that Chrome OS cros-disks daemon is requested to mount.

histogram.name: CrosDisks.DeviceMediaType
histogram.summary: The media type of removable device that Chrome OS cros-disks daemon is requested to mount.

histogram.name: CrosDisks.FilesystemType
histogram.summary: The type of file system that Chrome OS cros-disks daemon is requested to mount.

histogram.name: CrosDisks.Fuse.Rar2fs
histogram.summary: The error code returned by RAR mounting program rar2fs, which is called every time a user opens a RAR archive in ChromeOS Files App.

histogram.name: CrosDisksClient.FormatCompletedError
histogram.summary: The error code of disk format signals received from the Chrome OS cros-disks daemon.

histogram.name: CrosDisksClient.FormatTime
histogram.summary: Time taken for the Chrome OS cros-disks daemon to perform a format operation.

histogram.name: CrosDisksClient.MountCompletedError
histogram.summary: The error code of disk mount signals received from the Chrome OS cros-disks daemon.

histogram.name: CrosDisksClient.MountErrorMountType
histogram.summary: The {mount type}x{mount error code} received from the Chrome OS cros-disks daemon.

histogram.name: CrosDisksClient.MountTime
histogram.summary: Time taken by the Chrome OS cros-disks daemon to perform a Mount operation.

histogram.name: CrosDisksClient.UnmountError
histogram.summary: The error code of disk unmount operations returned from the Chrome OS cros-disks daemon.

histogram.name: CrosDisksClient.UnmountTime
histogram.summary: Time taken by the Chrome OS cros-disks daemon to perform an Unmount operation.

histogram.name: CrosFirstRun.DialogShown
histogram.summary: Records the number of times when first-run dialog was shown.

histogram.name: CrosFirstRun.FurthestStep
histogram.summary: Index of furthest step that was reached during tutorial. Since order of steps could change eventially and new steps could apear we use index here instead of step name.

histogram.name: CrosFirstRun.TimeSpent
histogram.summary: The total time that user spent on first-run tutorial.

histogram.name: CrosFirstRun.TimeSpentOnStep
histogram.summary: The time that user spent on some step of tutorial.

histogram.name: CrosFirstRun.TutorialCompletion
histogram.summary: Tracks the way how user left tutorial.

histogram.name: CrosFirstRun.TutorialLaunched
histogram.summary: Records the number of times when first-run tutorial has been launched.

histogram.name: Crostini.AppLaunch
histogram.summary: Recorded each time a Crostini app is launched, recording whether the app is the built in terminal, a registered app, or an unknown app.

histogram.name: Crostini.AppLaunchResult
histogram.summary: The result of attempting to launch a Crostini app (including Terminal).

histogram.name: Crostini.AppsInstalledAtLogin
histogram.summary: Records the number of Crostini apps that surface in the launcher at login time (not including the Terminal). This only logs if Crostini is enabled for the current user.

histogram.name: Crostini.AvailableDiskCancel
histogram.summary: The available disk space at the start of the crostini install flow, recorded when installation was canceled. This is recorded any time the user cancels the install before it finishes. This includes cases where e.g. they previously tried installing and got an error.

histogram.name: Crostini.AvailableDiskError
histogram.summary: The available disk space at the start of the crostini install flow, recorded when installation returned an error. This is recorded any time the user tries to install install crostini and gets an error. This includes cases where e.g. they previously tried installing and got an error.

histogram.name: Crostini.AvailableDiskSuccess
histogram.summary: The available disk space at the start of the crostini install flow, recorded when installation succeeded. This is recorded any time the user successfully installs crostini. This includes cases where e.g. they previously tried installing and got an error.

histogram.name: Crostini.Backup
histogram.summary: Result of crostini backup.

histogram.name: Crostini.BackupCompressedSizeLog2
histogram.summary: log base 2 of compressed container backup size in bytes, rounded to the nearest integer. Value is between 0 and 50, to give good granularity for common sizes, while maintaining a range that can support very large sizes.

histogram.name: Crostini.BackupContainerSizeLog2
histogram.summary: log base 2 of uncompressed container image size in bytes, rounded to the nearest integer. Value is between 0 and 50, to give good granularity for common sizes, while maintaining a range that can support very large sizes.

histogram.name: Crostini.BackupSizeRatio
histogram.summary: 100 * compressed size / container size. The conventional compression ratio of input / output has not been used as the resulting value is unbounded.

histogram.name: Crostini.BackupTimeFailed
histogram.summary: Time taken for failed backup.

histogram.name: Crostini.BackupTimeSuccess
histogram.summary: Time taken for successful backup.

histogram.name: Crostini.ContainerOsVersion
histogram.summary: Recorded each time a Crostini container is launched, recording the OS version running inside the container.

histogram.name: Crostini.Crosvm.CpuPercentage
histogram.summary: The percentage of the system CPU that crosvm processes used during the 10-minute measuring period. This is recorded once every 10 minutes when crosvm is running.

histogram.name: Crostini.Crosvm.Processes.Count
histogram.summary: The number of crosvm processes that are running at the end of the 10-minute interval. This is recorded once every 10 minutes when crosvm is running.

histogram.name: Crostini.Crosvm.RssPercentage
histogram.summary: The percentage of the system memory that crosvm processes used at the end of the 10-minute measuring period. This is recorded once every 10 minutes when crosvm is running.

histogram.name: Crostini.DiskImageSize
histogram.obsolete: Removed 10/2019 because it was never used.
histogram.summary: The maximum space allowed for a VM disk image, as set at install time. This is recorded each time a disk image is successfully created.

histogram.name: Crostini.DiskResize.Result
histogram.summary: Records the result (e.g. succeeded) whenever an attempt to resize a Crostini disk finishes.

histogram.name: Crostini.DiskResize.Started
histogram.summary: Recorded each time a user tries resizing a Crostini disk to infer attempts that started but never finished (e.g. code bug missing a timeout). This won't exactly line up with the total of Crostini.DiskResizeResult due to how metrics work (e.g. start one day and result the next) but should be close.

histogram.name: Crostini.DiskType
histogram.summary: Records the type (e.g. sparse qcow2) of the user's disk. Emitted often, you probably want to look at the unique user numbers of this metric. Certain disk types are vulnerable to disk corruption in certain scenarios so this metric tracks our progress in moving people off them and tells us if we need to do additional work to migrate users off.

histogram.name: Crostini.FilesystemCorruption
histogram.summary: Filesystem corruption events in the crostini VM, recorded every time corruption is observed to affect the state of the system.

histogram.name: Crostini.RecoverySource
histogram.summary: Recorded each time the CrostiniRecoveryView is show, on detecting that a VM is still running after a Chrome crash.

histogram.name: Crostini.Restarter.Started
histogram.summary: Emitted whenever a run of CrostiniRestarter is triggered except during the initial install.

histogram.name: Crostini.RestarterResult
histogram.summary: The result of a single run of CrostiniRestarter. This is recorded any time the crostini restart flow is triggered, except during the initial install.

histogram.name: Crostini.Restore
histogram.summary: Result of crostini restore.

histogram.name: Crostini.RestoreTimeFailed
histogram.summary: Time taken for failed restore.

histogram.name: Crostini.RestoreTimeSuccess
histogram.summary: Time taken for successful restore.

histogram.name: Crostini.SettingsEvent
histogram.summary: Record user's choice in Crostini Settings

histogram.name: Crostini.Setup.Started
histogram.summary: Emitted whenever a run of CrostiniRestarter is triggered during the initial install.

histogram.name: Crostini.SetupResult
histogram.summary: Recorded each time the user completes the Crostini setup UI, recording the result of the setup.

histogram.name: Crostini.SetupSource
histogram.summary: Recorded each time the user initiates the Crostini setup UI, recording the UI surface that invoked the setup.

histogram.name: Crostini.TerminalSettingsChanged
histogram.summary: Record which settings in terminal are changed by users. This is captured each time terminal is launched and fetches the current settings, and not just when settings are changed, in order to give the best information about the current state of active users.

histogram.name: Crostini.TimeFromDeviceSetupToInstall
histogram.summary: The time from a user setting up their device, to the user enabling Crostini.

histogram.name: Crostini.TimeToInstallCancel
histogram.summary: The time taken for the crostini installer to be canceled by the user. This is recorded any time the user cancels the install before it finishes. This includes cases where e.g. they previously tried installing and got an error.

histogram.name: Crostini.TimeToInstallError
histogram.summary: The time taken for the crostini installer to fail due to an error. This is recorded any time the user tries to install install crostini and gets an error. This includes cases where e.g. they previously tried installing and got an error.

histogram.name: Crostini.TimeToInstallSuccess
histogram.summary: The time taken for the crostini installer to finish successfully. This is recorded any time the user successfully installs crostini. This includes cases where e.g. they previously tried installing and got an error.

histogram.name: Crostini.UninstallResult
histogram.summary: Recorded each time the user completes the Crostini uninstall UI, recording the result of the uninstall.

histogram.name: Crostini.UninstallSource
histogram.summary: Recorded each time the user initiates the Crostini uninstall UI, recording the UI surface that initiated the uninstall.

histogram.name: Crostini.UnsupportedNotification.Reason
histogram.summary: Recorded each time we display (or would display, see suffixes) a notification that the user is trying to do something Crostini doesn't support.

histogram.name: Crostini.UpgradeDialogEvent
histogram.summary: A collection of events that can occur while upgrading the crostini container, recorded as they occur.

histogram.name: Crostini.UpgradeSource
histogram.summary: Recorded each time the user sees the Crostini upgrade UI, recording the UI surface that initiated the upgrade.

histogram.name: CrosUsb.NotificationClosed
histogram.summary: Records the reason why the "Cros USB device detected" notification was dismissed. Recorded on notificiation close, after usb attached.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.ClientAppMetadata.IsManufacturerEmpty
histogram.summary: Indicates whether or not the device manufacturer name returned in the hardware info is empty. Recored during the construction of a ClientAppMetadata proto.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.ClientAppMetadata.IsModelEmpty
histogram.summary: Indicates whether or not the device model name returned in the hardware info is empty. Recored during the construction of a ClientAppMetadata proto.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.ClientAppMetadataInstanceIdTokenFetch.Result
histogram.summary: Indicates the result of fetching an InstanceId token during the construction of a ClientAppMetadata proto messages.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.ClientAppMetadataInstanceIdTokenFetch.Retries
histogram.summary: Counts the number of times the InstanceId token fetch got retried because it did not match with the expected InstanceId. Logged during the construction of a ClientAppMetadata proto message.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSync.Result
histogram.summary: Indicates success of performing a DeviceSync.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncSoftwareFeaturesResult
histogram.summary: Indicates that there was no issue retrieving supported and enabled software features, or that there were enabled features which were not in the supported feature set. See the histogram "CryptAuth.DeviceSyncSoftwareFeaturesResult.Failures" for a breakdown of failures by feature type.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncSoftwareFeaturesResult.Failures
histogram.summary: Breaks down by feature type "CryptAuth.DeviceSyncSoftwareFeaturesResult"'s count of enabled features which were not in the supported feature set.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.DeviceActivityGetter.ApiCallResult.GetDevicesActivityStatus
histogram.summary: Records the result of the async GetDevicesActivityStatus API call to CryptAuth during multi-device setup. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.DeviceActivityGetter.AsyncTaskResult.ClientAppMetadataFetch
histogram.summary: Records the result of the async ClientAppMetadata retrieval needed for the device activity getter. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.DeviceActivityGetter.ExecutionTime.ClientAppMetadataFetch
histogram.summary: Records the execution time of the async ClientAppMetadata retrieval needed for the device activity getter. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.DeviceActivityGetter.ExecutionTime.GetDevicesActivityStatus
histogram.summary: Records the execution time of the async GetDevicesActivityStatus API call during multi-device setup. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.DeviceManager.AsyncTaskResult.ClientAppMetadataFetch
histogram.summary: Records the result of the async ClientAppMetadata retrieval during the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.DeviceManager.ExecutionTime.ClientAppMetadataFetch
histogram.summary: Records the execution time of the ClientAppMetadataFetch during the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.DeviceNotifier.ApiCallResult.NotifyGroupDevices
histogram.summary: Records the result of the async BatchNotifyGroupDevices API call to CryptAuth. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.DeviceNotifier.AsyncTaskResult.ClientAppMetadataFetch
histogram.summary: Records the result of the async ClientAppMetadata retrieval for the CryptAuth device notifier. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.DeviceNotifier.ExecutionTime.ClientAppMetadataFetch
histogram.summary: Records the execution time of the async ClientAppMetadata retrieval for the CryptAuth device notifier. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.DeviceNotifier.ExecutionTime.NotifyGroupDevices
histogram.summary: Records the execution time of the async BatchNotifyGroupDevices API call to CryptAuth. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.DeviceSyncer.AsyncTaskResult.DeviceMetadataDecryption
histogram.summary: Records the result of the async device metadata decryption during the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow. Records a success if the operation did not timeout; a separate metric, CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.DeviceSyncer.MetadataDecryptionSuccess, tracks individual encryption failures.Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.DeviceSyncer.AsyncTaskResult.GroupPrivateKeyDecryption
histogram.summary: Records the result of the async group private key decryption during the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.DeviceSyncer.ExecutionTime.DeviceMetadataDecryption
histogram.summary: Records the execution time of the async device metadata decryption during the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.DeviceSyncer.ExecutionTime.GroupPrivateKeyDecryption
histogram.summary: Records the execution time of the async group private key decryption during the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.DeviceSyncer.ExecutionTime.ShareGroupPrivateKey
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2020-01-02 because metric was never used.
histogram.summary: Records the execution time of the async ShareGroupPrivateKey API call during the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.DeviceSyncer.IsGroupPrivateKeyConsistent
histogram.summary: Records whether or not the group private key sent by CryptAuth matches the existing group private key when they are expected to agree. Emitted during a CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync if the group private key is not being rotated.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.DeviceSyncer.IsLocalDeviceMetadataConsistent
histogram.summary: Records whether or not the local device metadata agrees with that sent by CryptAuth in the SyncMetadata response. Emitted during the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow, after device metadata is decrypted.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.DeviceSyncer.MetadataDecryptionSuccess
histogram.summary: Records whether or not device metadata sent by CryptAuth in the SyncMetadata response was able to be decrypted. Emitted during the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow for each device metadata packet.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.DeviceSyncer.MetadataParsingSuccess
histogram.summary: Records whether or not device metadata sent by CryptAuth in the SyncMetadata response was able to be parsed from a decrypted serialized byte string into a proto. Emitted during the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow for each device metadata packet.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.FeatureStatusGetter.ApiCallResult.GetFeatureStatuses
histogram.summary: Records the result of the async BatchGetFeatureStatuses API call during the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.FeatureStatusGetter.CorrectNumberOfDevicesInResponse
histogram.summary: Records whether or not the expected number of devices were sent in the BatchGetFeatureStatuses response. Emitted when a BatchGetFeatureStatuses response is received from the CryptAuth server.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.FeatureStatusGetter.ExecutionTime.GetFeatureStatuses
histogram.summary: Records the execution time of the async BatchGetFeatureStatuses API call during the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.FeatureStatusGetter.IsDuplicateDeviceId
histogram.summary: Records whether or not a device ID in the BatchGetFeatureStatuses response has been seen already in the same response. Emitted for each device in the BatchGetFeatureStatuses response. Emitted when a BatchGetFeatureStatuses response is received from the CryptAuth server.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.FeatureStatusGetter.IsKnownFeatureType
histogram.summary: Records whether or not the feature type strings returned in the BatchGetFeatureStatuses response are known feature types. Emitted for each feature type for each device in the BatchGetFeatureStatuses response. Emitted when a BatchGetFeatureStatuses response is received from the CryptAuth server.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.FeatureStatusGetter.IsUnsupportedFeatureMarkedEnabled
histogram.summary: Records whether or not a feature is marked as enabled even though it is listed as unsupported. This should never happen. Emitted for all feature types, for each device in the BatchGetFeatureStatuses response. Emitted when a BatchGetFeatureStatuses response is received from the CryptAuth server.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.FeatureStatusGetter.WasDeviceInResponseRequested
histogram.summary: Records whether or not a device in the BatchGetFeatureStatuses response was included in the BatchGetFeatureStatuses request. Emitted for each device in the BatchGetFeatureStatuses response.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.FeatureStatusSetter.ApiCallResult.SetFeatureStatuses
histogram.summary: Records the result of the async BatchSetFeatureStatuses API call to CryptAuth. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.FeatureStatusSetter.AsyncTaskResult.ClientAppMetadataFetch
histogram.summary: Records the result of the async ClientAppMetadata retrieval for the CryptAuth feature status setter. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.FeatureStatusSetter.ExecutionTime.ClientAppMetadataFetch
histogram.summary: Records the execution time of the async ClientAppMetadata retrieval for the CryptAuth feature status setter. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.FeatureStatusSetter.ExecutionTime.SetFeatureStatuses
histogram.summary: Records the execution time of the async BatchSetFeatureStatuses API call to CryptAuth. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.GroupPrivateKeySharer.ApiCallResult.ShareGroupPrivateKey
histogram.summary: Records the result of the async ShareGroupPrivateKey API call during the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.GroupPrivateKeySharer.AsyncTaskResult.GroupPrivateKeyEncryption
histogram.summary: Records the result of the async group private key encryption during the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow. Records a success if the operation did not timeout; a separate metric, CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.GroupPrivateKeySharer.EncryptionSuccess, tracks individual encryption failures. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.GroupPrivateKeySharer.EncryptionSuccess
histogram.summary: Records whether or not the group private key was able to be encrypted using a device's encrypting key. Recorded during the ShareGroupPrivateKey phase of the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow. Recorded for each device with whom we intend to share the group private key.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.GroupPrivateKeySharer.ExecutionTime.GroupPrivateKeyEncryption
histogram.summary: Records the execution time of the async group private key encryption during the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.GroupPrivateKeySharer.ExecutionTime.ShareGroupPrivateKey
histogram.summary: Records the execution time of the async ShareGroupPrivateKey API call during the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.GroupPrivateKeySharer.IsEncryptingKeyEmpty
histogram.summary: Records whether or not a device's key used to encrypt the group private key is empty. If the key is empty, the group private key cannot be encrypted and shared with the device. Recorded during the ShareGroupPrivateKey phase of the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow. Recorded for each device with whom we intend to share the group private key.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.InvocationReason
histogram.summary: The clients' reasons for making CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync requests. Recorded when a DeviceSync is requested.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.MetadataSyncer.ApiCallResult.FirstSyncMetadata
histogram.summary: Records the result of the first async SyncMetadata API call during the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.MetadataSyncer.ApiCallResult.SecondSyncMetadata
histogram.summary: Records the result of the second async SyncMetadata API call during the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.MetadataSyncer.AsyncTaskResult.GroupKeyCreation
histogram.summary: Records the result of the async group key creation during the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.MetadataSyncer.AsyncTaskResult.LocalDeviceMetadataEncryption
histogram.summary: Records the result of the async local device metadata encryption during the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.MetadataSyncer.ExecutionTime.FirstSyncMetadata
histogram.summary: Records the execution time of the first async SyncMetadata API call during the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.MetadataSyncer.ExecutionTime.GroupKeyCreation
histogram.summary: Records the execution time of the async group key creation during the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.MetadataSyncer.ExecutionTime.LocalDeviceMetadataEncryption
histogram.summary: Records the execution time of the async local device metadata encryption during the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.MetadataSyncer.ExecutionTime.SecondSyncMetadata
histogram.summary: Records the execution time of the second async SyncMetadata API call during the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.MetadataSyncer.IsDeviceMetadataPacketValid
histogram.summary: Records whether or not the device metadata packets sent by CryptAuth have all of the necessary fields populated. Emitted for each device metadata packet during the SyncMetadata phase of the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.MetadataSyncer.IsDuplicateDeviceId
histogram.summary: Records whether or not the device ID from a device metadata packet sent by CryptAuth in the SyncMetadata response has the same ID as a packet from the same response. Emitted for each device metadata packet during the SyncMetadata phase of the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync flow.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.RemoteDeviceProvider.NumV1Devices
histogram.summary: Records the number of devices returned by a v1 DeviceSync. Only recorded when v1 and v2 DeviceSync are running in parallel. Only recorded if a v1 and v2 DeviceSync has ever previously occurred. Emitted when v1 and v2 devices are merged together, which occurs after either a v1 or v2 DeviceSync.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.RemoteDeviceProvider.NumV2Devices
histogram.summary: Records the number of devices returned by a v2 DeviceSync. Only recorded when v1 and v2 DeviceSync are running in parallel. Only recorded if a v1 and v2 DeviceSync has ever previously occurred. Emitted when v1 and v2 devices are merged together, which occurs after either a v1 or v2 DeviceSync.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.RemoteDeviceProvider.PercentageOfV1DevicesReplacedByV2Devices
histogram.summary: Records the percentage of devices returned by a v2 DeviceSync that replace their v1 counterparts. This will only occur if the v2 device has a decrypted public key that matches a v1 device. Only recorded when v1 and v2 DeviceSync are running in parallel. Only recorded if a v1 and v2 DeviceSync has ever previously occurred. Emitted when v1 and v2 devices are merged together, which occurs after either a v1 or v2 DeviceSync.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.RemoteDeviceProvider.PercentageOfV2DevicesWithDecryptedPublicKey
histogram.summary: Records the percentage of devices returned by a v2 DeviceSync that have a decrypted user public key. Only recorded when v1 and v2 DeviceSync are running in parallel. Only recorded if a v1 and v2 DeviceSync has ever previously occurred. Emitted when v1 and v2 devices are merged together, which occurs after either a v1 or v2 DeviceSync.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.RemoteDeviceProvider.RatioOfV2ToV1Devices
histogram.summary: Records the ratio of the number of devices returned by a v2 DeviceSync to the number returned by a v1 DeviceSync. Devices returned by a v2 DeviceSync should be a subset of the devices returned by a v1 DeviceSync, barring any race conditions. This ratio should be greater than or equal to PercentageOfV1DevicesReplacedByV2Devices because a v2 device can only replace its v1 counterpart if it has a decrypted public key. Only recorded when v1 and v2 DeviceSync are running in parallel. Only recorded if a v1 and v2 DeviceSync has ever previously occurred. Emitted when v1 and v2 devices are merged together, which occurs after either a v1 or v2 DeviceSync.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.Result.DidDeviceRegistryChange
histogram.summary: Indicates whether or not any device data in the device registry changed as a result of the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync attempt. Recorded when a DeviceSync attempt finishes.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.Result.ResultCode
histogram.summary: The CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync attempt results broken down by result code. Recorded when a DeviceSync attempt finishes.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.DeviceSyncV2.Result.ResultType
histogram.summary: Indicates whether the CryptAuth v2 DeviceSync attempt succeeded, failed, or finished with non-fatal errors. Recorded when a DeviceSync attempt finishes.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.Enrollment.Result
histogram.summary: The top-level result of the CryptAuth device enrollment process.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.EnrollmentV2.ApiCallResult.EnrollKeys
histogram.summary: Records the result of the async EnrollKeys API call to CryptAuth during the CryptAuth v2 Enrollment flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.EnrollmentV2.ApiCallResult.SyncKeys
histogram.summary: Records the result of the async SyncKeys API call to CryptAuth during the CryptAuth v2 Enrollment flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.EnrollmentV2.AsyncTaskResult.ClientAppMetadataFetch
histogram.summary: Records the result of the async ClientAppMetadata retrieval during the CryptAuth v2 Enrollment flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.EnrollmentV2.AsyncTaskResult.GcmRegistration
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019-12-11 in favor of CryptAuth.EnrollmentV2.GcmRegistration.Success because the timeout was removed.
histogram.summary: Records the result of the async GCM registration call during the CryptAuth v2 Enrollment flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.EnrollmentV2.AsyncTaskResult.KeyCreation
histogram.summary: Records the result of the async key creation call during the CryptAuth v2 Enrollment flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.EnrollmentV2.ExecutionTime.ClientAppMetadataFetch
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019-09-12 in favor of CryptAuth.EnrollmentV2.ExecutionTime.ClientAppMetadataFetch2 because too many results were reaching the max time limit.
histogram.summary: Records the execution time of the async ClientAppMetadata retrieval during the CryptAuth v2 Enrollment flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.EnrollmentV2.ExecutionTime.ClientAppMetadataFetch2
histogram.summary: Records the execution time of the async ClientAppMetadata retrieval during the CryptAuth v2 Enrollment flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.EnrollmentV2.ExecutionTime.EnrollKeys
histogram.summary: Records the execution time of the async EnrollKeys API call to CryptAuth during the CryptAuth v2 Enrollment flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.EnrollmentV2.ExecutionTime.GcmRegistration
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019-12-11 in favor of CryptAuth.EnrollmentV2.GcmRegistration.AttemptTime because too many results were reaching the max time limit.
histogram.summary: Records the execution time of the async GCM registration call during the CryptAuth v2 Enrollment flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.EnrollmentV2.ExecutionTime.KeyCreation
histogram.summary: Records the execution time of the async key creation call during the CryptAuth v2 Enrollment flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.EnrollmentV2.ExecutionTime.SyncKeys
histogram.summary: Records the execution time of the async SyncKeys API call to CryptAuth during the CryptAuth v2 Enrollment flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked or when the call times out.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.EnrollmentV2.GcmRegistration.AttemptTime
histogram.summary: Records the execution time of the async GCM registration call during the CryptAuth v2 Enrollment flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.EnrollmentV2.GcmRegistration.Success
histogram.summary: Records the success or failure of the async GCM registration call during the CryptAuth v2 Enrollment flow. Recorded when the async callback is invoked.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.EnrollmentV2.InvocationReason
histogram.summary: The clients' reasons for making CryptAuth v2 Enrollment requests. Recorded when an enrollment is requested.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.EnrollmentV2.Result.ResultCode
histogram.summary: The CryptAuth v2 Enrollment attempt results broken down by result code. Recorded when an enrollment attempt finishes.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.EnrollmentV2.Result.Success
histogram.summary: The success or failure of CryptAuth v2 Enrollment requests. Recorded when an enrollment attempt finishes.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.EnrollmentV2.UserKeyPairState
histogram.summary: The state--at system start time--of persisted user key pairs, generated during CryptAuth v1 and/or v2 Enrollment: whether they exist and if they agree. Recorded on CryptAuth v2 Enrollment manager construction.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.Gcm.Message.FeatureType
histogram.summary: Records the multi-device feature type sent in a GCM message from CryptAuth v2. Emitted when a GCM message with a feature type key/value pair is received from CryptAuth.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.Gcm.Message.IsDeviceSyncGroupNameValid
histogram.summary: Records whether or not the DeviceSync group name sent in a GCM message from CryptAuth is valid. Currently the only valid group name is "DeviceSync:BetterTogether". Emitted when a GCM message with a DeviceSync group name key/value pair is received from CryptAuth.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.Gcm.Message.IsKnownFeatureType
histogram.summary: Records whether or not the multi-device feature type sent in a GCM message from CryptAuth v2 is recognized by the Chrome OS client. Emitted when a GCM message with a feature type key/value pair is received from CryptAuth.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.Gcm.Message.TargetService.AreTickleTypeAndTargetServiceBothSpecified
histogram.summary: Records whether or not the CryptAuth TargetService was specified in a GCM message by both the CryptAuth v1 registration tickle type key/value pair as well as the CryptAuth v2 target service key/value pair. Both key/value pairs should not exist in same GCM message. Emitted when a GCM message is received from CryptAuth.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.Gcm.Message.TargetService.FromRegistrationTickleType
histogram.summary: The CryptAuth TargetService--Enrollment or DeviceSync--indicated by the registration tickle type key/value pair of an incoming GCM message. The registration tickle type is used in GCM messages sent by CryptAuth v1. Emitted when a GCM message from CryptAuth is received.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.Gcm.Message.TargetService.FromTargetServiceValue
histogram.summary: The CryptAuth TargetService--Enrollment or DeviceSync--indicated by the target service key/value pair of an incoming GCM message. This key/value pair is used in GCM messages sent by CryptAuth v2. Emitted when a GCM message from CryptAuth is received.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.Gcm.Registration.AttemptTimeWithRetries
histogram.summary: The time it takes the GCM driver to return the result of a GCM registration request made by the CryptAuth GCM manager. CryptAuth registration with GCM is only performed once, if successful, for a given user on a device as the first step in CryptAuth v1 or v2 Enrollment. Recorded when the CryptAuth GCM manager is notified by the GCM driver that the registration attempt finished, successfully or not. The GCM driver might quietly retry failures before notifying the CryptAuth GCM manager. The time recorded here encompasses the possible retry attempts and delays from the GCM driver.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.Gcm.Registration.Result
histogram.summary: The result code from a GCM registration request made by the CryptAuth GCM manager. CryptAuth registration with GCM is only performed once, if successful, for a given user on a device as the first step in CryptAuth v1 or v2 Enrollment. Recorded when the CryptAuth GCM manager is notified by the GCM driver that the registration attempt finished, successfully or not. The GCM driver might quietly retry failures before notifying the CryptAuth GCM manager. This metric will not include results codes that were quietly retried by the GCM driver.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.InstanceId.DidInstanceIdChange
histogram.summary: Indicates whether or not the Instance ID used by the CryptAuth client differs from the previously retrieved ID. Recorded while fetching client app metadata if a previous ID has been set.

histogram.name: CryptAuth.InstanceId.DidInstanceIdTokenChange
histogram.summary: Indicates whether or not the Instance ID token used by the CryptAuth client differs from the previously retrieved token. Recorded while fetching client app metadata if a previous token has been set.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.AsyncDBusRequest
histogram.summary: Records the total running time of every async dbus calls of Cryptohome at server side.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.AsyncDBusRequest.Inqueue
histogram.summary: Records the total inqueue time of every async dbus calls of Cryptohome at server side. Recorded when the first stage of this requests is served.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.ChecksumStatus
histogram.summary: Checksum status of critical persistent data used by Cryptohome. This status is recorded every time the cryptohomed daemon reads a file.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DeletedUserProfiles
histogram.summary: In low disk space scenarios, Cryptohome erases whole user profiles. This is a number of user profiles deleted during cleanup. Only reported when non-zero.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DeprecatedApiCalled
histogram.summary: Records when a deprecated API function in cryptohome is called, so we know which exposed DBus API can be removed without side effect.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DircryptoMigrationEndStatus
histogram.summary: The status of the user home directory migration from ecryptfs to ext4-crypto. This is logged once at the end of each migration.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DircryptoMigrationFailedErrorCode
histogram.summary: The error code of file operation by which the user home directory migration from ecryptfs to ext4-crypto has failed. This is logged once the migration encounters a file I/O error.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DircryptoMigrationFailedOperationType
histogram.summary: The type of file operation at which the user home directory migration from ecryptfs to ext4-crypto has failed. This is logged once the migration encounters a file I/O error.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DircryptoMigrationFailedPathType
histogram.summary: The category of the path where the user home directory migration from ecryptfs to ext4-crypto has failed. This is logged once the migration encounters a file I/O error.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DircryptoMigrationInitialFreeSpaceInMb
histogram.summary: The total amount of free disk space on the system when the user started the migration from ecryptfs to ext4-crypto. This is only logged when the user encounters an insufficient space error.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DircryptoMigrationNoSpaceFailureFreeSpaceInMb
histogram.summary: The total amount of free disk space on the system when the user received an insufficient space error during the migration from ecryptfs to ext4-crypto. This is only logged when the user encounters an insufficient space error.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DircryptoMigrationNoSpaceXattrSizeInBytes
histogram.summary: The total number of bytes of xattr data that we are trying to store on a file when we receive an insufficient space error. This includes both xattr data already present on the file as well as the new xattr we are trying to set. This is only logged during ecryptfs to ext4-crypto migration if we receive an insufficient space error when setting a new extended attribute.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DircryptoMigrationStartStatus
histogram.summary: The status of the user home directory migration from ecryptfs to ext4-crypto. This is logged once at the start of each migration.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DircryptoMigrationTotalByteCountInMb
histogram.summary: The total byte count (MB) of the data in a user's home directory which is being migrated from ecryptfs to ext4-crypto. This is logged once when a new migration starts.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DircryptoMigrationTotalFileCount
histogram.summary: The total file count in a user's home directory which is being migrated from ecryptfs to ext4-crypto. This is logged once when a new migration starts.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DircryptoMinimalMigrationEndStatus
histogram.summary: The status of the user home directory minimal migration from ecryptfs to ext4-crypto. This is logged once at the end of each minimal migration.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DircryptoMinimalMigrationStartStatus
histogram.summary: The status of the user home directory minimal migration from ecryptfs to ext4-crypto. This is logged once at the start of each minimal migration.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.DiskCleanupProgress
histogram.summary: In low disk space scenarios, Cryptohome erases caches while target amount of free disk space is not reached. It starts from low priority categories. This histogram reports which topmost priority was reached to fulfill a cleanup request.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.Errors
histogram.summary: Cryptohome errors.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.FreedGCacheDiskSpaceInMb
histogram.obsolete: Removed 05/2018 in favor of Cryptohome.GCache.FreedDiskSpaceInMb. Reports zero in 99% of hits.
histogram.summary: The amount of drive cache (MB) which was evicted by cryptohome due to shortage of remaining disk space.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.FreeDiskSpaceTotalFreedInMb
histogram.summary: In low disk space scenarios, Cryptohome performs a lot of disk space operations to erase data. This is the amount of space that was released by the automatic cleanup.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.FreeDiskSpaceTotalTime
histogram.summary:
Being replaced by Cryptohome.FreeDiskSpaceTotalTime2
In low disk space scenarios, Cryptohome performs a lot of disk space operations to erase data. This is a number of milliseconds taken to perform a cleanup. Reported only if longer than 5 ms.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.FreeDiskSpaceTotalTime2
histogram.summary: In low disk space scenarios, Cryptohome performs a lot of disk space operations to erase data. This is a number of milliseconds taken to perform a cleanup. Reported only if longer than 5 ms. Increased maximum and number of buckets compared to FreeDiskSpaceTotalTime.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.GCache.FreedDiskSpaceInMb
histogram.summary: This is an amount of Google Drive cache (MB) which was evicted by cryptohome due to shortage of remaining disk space. Reported only if something was deleted (greater than zero).

histogram.name: Cryptohome.HomedirEncryptionType
histogram.summary: The encryption type used for a user's cryptohome directory. This is logged each time the cryptohome is mounted.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.InstallAttributesUsage
histogram.summary: Records the result of using the install_attributes.pb cache. This is logged at each device boot.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.InstallAttributesValidation
histogram.summary: Records the result of validating install_attributes.pb. This is logged at each device boot.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.LECredential
histogram.summary: Events related to Low Entropy (LE) credential management. These are logged every time an operation involving a LE credential is performed.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.MigrationToGaiaId
histogram.summary: This is the status of cryptohome migration to GaiaId. Every time user logs in current migration status is recorded.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.MigrationUI.ConsumedBatteryPercent
histogram.summary: The amount of consumed battery level during cryptohome encryption migration. This is logged when the battery level decreases during migration, and the data is used to check if the minimum battery level required to start migration is appropriate.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.MigrationUI.FirstScreen
histogram.summary: The first screen in the encryption migration UI, which is shown when a user attempts to log in to the system and old encryption (eCryptfs) is detected.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.MigrationUI.MigrationResult
histogram.summary: The result of encryption migration from eCryptfs to Ext4 dircrypto. The recorded result is what the migration UI in Chrome side is notified from cryptohomed.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.MigrationUI.RemoveCryptohomeResult
histogram.summary: The result of the removal of user's cryptohome. When the migration UI is notified that the migration failed, the UI tries to remove the user's cryptohome to make sure that the user can create clean crytohome directory in the next sign-in.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.MigrationUI.UserChoice
histogram.summary: User's choice when the system is ready to migrate encryption. The user can start migration or skip it. It is used to know how many users have skipped migration.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.MigrationUI.VisibleScreen
histogram.summary: How many times each screen in migration UI is shown to the user. A screen is recorded as a visible screen when the screen is kept displayed at least for a second.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.OOPMountCleanupResult
histogram.summary: The result of a cryptohome out-of-process mount cleanup. Recorded once per logout.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.OOPMountOperationResult
histogram.summary: The result of a cryptohome out-of-process mount operation. Recorded once per login attempt.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.ParallelTasks
histogram.summary: How many tasks runs in parallel typically in the mount thread of cryptohome. Recorded when a requests comes in. Counts of 1 will not reported.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.TimeBetweenFreeDiskSpace
histogram.summary: The amount of time (s) between disk cleanup runs. If there is sufficient disk space, nothing is reported. The first cleanup run after boot is not reported.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.TimeSessionUnlock
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) for Chrome OS cryptohome daemon to verify the user's password during an unlock operation.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.TimeToCompleteDircryptoMigration
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) for the user's home directory to be migrated from ecryptfs to ext4-crypto. If the migration is interrupted and subsequently resumed (such as due to power loss) then no value is recorded for either attempt.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.TimeToCompleteDircryptoMinimalMigration
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) for the user's home directory to be migrated from ecryptfs to ext4-crypto if the minimal migration method was used. If the minimal migration is interrupted and subsequently resumed (such as due to power loss) then no value is recorded for either attempt.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.TimeToInitPkcs11
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) for Chrome OS cryptohome daemon to initialize the PKCS#11. Initializations under 1 second represent checks on a previously initialized PKCS#11, and should be discarded for analysis.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.TimeToMountAsync
histogram.obsolete: Removed 07/2019 as it is not used since crrev.com/c/1393048
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) for Chrome OS cryptohome to mount the encrypted home directory asynchronously.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.TimeToMountEx
histogram.summary: The amount of time for Chrome OS cryptohome to mount the encrypted home directory.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.TimeToMountGuestAsync
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) for Chrome OS cryptohome to mount the encrypted guest home directory asynchronously.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.TimeToMountGuestEx
histogram.summary: The amount of time for Chrome OS cryptohome to mount the encrypted guest home directory.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.TimeToMountGuestSync
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2020 as it is not used since crrev.com/c/1183895 (8/2018).
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) for Chrome OS cryptohome to mount the encrypted guest home directory synchronously.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.TimeToMountSync
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) for Chrome OS cryptohome to mount the encrypted home directory synchronously.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.TimeToPerformOOPMountCleanup
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) cryptohome spends terminating and reaping the out-of-process mounter, which includes the time spent by the mounter itself tearing down the user home directory.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.TimeToPerformOOPMountOperation
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) cryptohome spends running the out-of-process mounter, until the user home directory is finalized.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.TimeToTakeTpmOwnership
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) for Chrome OS cryptohome daemon to take ownership of the TPM.

histogram.name: Cryptohome.TpmResults
histogram.summary: The errors resulting from interacting with the Trusted Platform Module (TPM) device.

histogram.name: CryptohomeClient
histogram.summary: Records the time duration of every dbus outgoing calls issued from the client of Crypthome in Chrome side.

histogram.name: CustomTab.SessionDuration
histogram.summary: Records the time duration in CustomTab Activity from onStart/onResume to onStop/onPause, if that activity is opened by Webapp/WebApk/Trusted Web Activity.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.ClientAppId
histogram.summary: Android: AppId declared by the launching application in EXTRA_APPLICATION_ID in a custom tab. This histogram is used as a means to count the total instances of custom tabs created meanwhile binning them across launching apps that have identified themselves. Starting with M84 this is is also recorded for webapps/WebAPKs. Filter the platform for just CCT/TWA data.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.CloseButton.ChildTab.ScopeAlgorithm.ClosesOneTab
histogram.summary: Records whether the CustomTab/TrustedWebActivity "navigate to landing page" algorithm for child tabs - e.g. tabs opened via window.open() - produces the same behaviour (closes the current tab) as the webapp "close current tab" algorithm. Recorded when the user clicks the CustomTab/TrustedWebActivity close button on a child tab. Starting with M84 this is is also recorded for webapps/WebAPKs. Filter the platform for just CCT/TWA data.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.ConnectionStatusOnReturn.GSA
histogram.summary: Android: Recorded whenever user closes the custom tab by hitting the close button or back to return to the caller app. Keeps track of the connection status and whether the app was using a KeepAlive connection. Recorded only for AGSA. Starting with M84 this is is also recorded for webapps/WebAPKs. Filter the platform for just CCT/TWA data.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.ConnectionStatusOnReturn.NonGSA
histogram.summary: Android: Recorded whenever user closes the custom tab by hitting the close button or back to return to the caller app. Keeps track of the connection status and whether the app was using a KeepAlive connection. Recorded for all apps other than AGSA. Starting with M84 this is is also recorded for webapps/WebAPKs. Filter the platform for just CCT/TWA data.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.DetachedResourceRequest.Duration
histogram.summary: Time between the start of a detached resource request and its completion (failure or success). Android only. Starting with M84 this is is also recorded for webapps/WebAPKs. Filter the platform for just CCT/TWA data.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.DetachedResourceRequest.FinalStatus
histogram.summary: Reports the final status of the detached request, including success. Recorded once per detached resource request. Android only. Starting with M84 this is is also recorded for webapps/WebAPKs. Filter the platform for just CCT/TWA data.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.DetachedResourceRequest.RedirectsCount
histogram.summary: Number of redirects followed by a detached resource request until success or failure. Android only. Starting with M84 this is is also recorded for webapps/WebAPKs. Filter the platform for just CCT/TWA data.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.DynamicModule.CreateActivityDelegateTime
histogram.summary: Time to create an activity delegate for a custom tabs dynamic module. Android only.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.DynamicModule.CreatePackageContextTime
histogram.summary: Time to create the package context for a custom tabs dynamic module. Android only.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.DynamicModule.DestructionReason
histogram.summary: Possible reasons for destroying a custom tabs dynamic module. Android only.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.DynamicModule.EntryPointInitTime
histogram.summary: Time to initialize the entry point class for a custom tabs dynamic module. Android only.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.DynamicModule.EntryPointLoadClassTime
histogram.summary: Time to load the entry point class for a custom tabs dynamic module. Android only.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.DynamicModule.EntryPointNewInstanceTime
histogram.summary: Time to instantiate the entry point class for a custom tabs dynamic module. Android only.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.DynamicModule.LoadResult
histogram.summary: Possible results when loading a custom tabs dynamic module. Android only.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.DynamicModule.ProportionalSet.OnModuleDestroy
histogram.summary: The proportional set size (PSS) of code pages occupied by a custom tabs dynamic module. Recorded before the module destruction. Android only.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.DynamicModule.ProportionalSet.OnModuleLoad
histogram.summary: The proportional set size (PSS) of code pages occupied by a custom tabs dynamic module. Recorded when a module is loaded. Android only.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.DynamicModule.ResidentSet.OnModuleDestroy
histogram.summary: The resident set size (RSS) of code pages occupied by a custom tabs dynamic module. Recorded before the module destruction. Android only.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.DynamicModule.ResidentSet.OnModuleLoad
histogram.summary: The resident set size (RSS) of code pages occupied by a custom tabs dynamic module. Recorded when a module is loaded. Android only.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.Experimental.Visible
histogram.summary: Experimental version of CustomTabs.Visible that is logged on a log start, rather than log end, to check if this results in more logs being tagged. Starting with M84 this is is also recorded for webapps/WebAPKs. Filter the platform for just CCT/TWA data.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.IntentToFirstCommitNavigationTime
histogram.summary: Time between the intent arrival in Chrome and the first navigation commit, if the navigation is successful. Similar in principle to Startup.FirstCommitNavigationTime.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.IntentToFirstCommitNavigationTime2
histogram.summary: Time between the intent arrival to a Custom Tab and the navigation start (note: not the navigation commit, as the name suggests). Recorded when the page has finished loading. Non-"Herb" mode.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.IntentToFirstCommitNavigationTime3
histogram.summary: Time between the intent arrival to a Custom Tab and the first navigation commit. Recorded for every custom tabs intent when the page has finished loading.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.IntentToFirstNavigationStartTime
histogram.summary: Time between the intent arrival to a Custom Tab and the navigation start. Recorded when the page has finished loading. Non-"Herb" mode.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.IntentToPageLoadedTime
histogram.summary: Time between the intent arrival in Chrome and the first "page loaded" event, if the navigation is successful. Non-"Herb" mode.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.MayLaunchUrlType
histogram.summary: Android only. Which flavors of mayLaunchUrl() were used before a tab launch. Can be low and/or high confidence. Recorded at the same time as CustomTabs.WarmupStateOnLaunch for the buckets "Session, No Warmup, Warmup called from another UID", "Session, No Warmup" and "Session, Warmup".

histogram.name: CustomTabs.NonDefaultSessionPrerenderMatched
histogram.obsolete: Removed 4/2018 when prerender was removed from Custom Tabs.
histogram.summary: Android: When there is a prerender for a session with non-default parameters, whether the prerender was matched when navigating.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.ParallelRequestStatusOnStart
histogram.summary: Recorded only on Android. Whether a parallel request was requested by the calling application, and if so its initial status. Recorded once per handled intent, when the request is initiated. Starting with M84 this is is also recorded for webapps/WebAPKs. Filter the platform for just CCT/TWA data.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.PredictionStatus
histogram.summary: For Custom Tabs, records whether mayLaunchUrl() has been called, and if so, whether the call was later matched by a URL launch.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.PredictionToLaunch
histogram.summary: When a URL prediction succeeds, time in ms between the prediction and the actual launch.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.PrerenderSessionUsesDefaultParameters
histogram.obsolete: Removed 4/2018 when prerender was removed from Custom Tabs.
histogram.summary: Android: When prerendering a URL for Custom Tabs, whether the client session uses the default parameters.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.ResourcePrefetch.Duration
histogram.summary: Time between the start of a detached resource request for resource prefetch and its completion (failure or success). Android only.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.ResourcePrefetch.FinalStatus
histogram.summary: Reports the final status of the detached request for resource prefetch, including success. Recorded once per resource prefetch request. Android only.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.SpareWebContents.Status
histogram.summary: Android: When a spare WebContents is created from Custom Tabs, record creation, and whether it was used, killed or destroyed. Creation is recorded to get the "abandoned" case, that is when Chrome is killed before the renderer.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.SpareWebContents.Status2
histogram.summary: Android: When a spare WebContents is created from Custom Tabs, record creation, and whether it was used by CCT, stolen by another Chrome Activity, killed or destroyed. Creation is recorded to get the "abandoned" case, that is when Chrome is killed before the renderer. Starting with M84 this is is also recorded for webapps/WebAPKs. Filter the platform for just CCT/TWA data.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.SpareWebContents.TimeBeforeDeath
histogram.summary: Android: When a spare WebContents is created from Custom Tabs, record how long is took before the render process was killed. Starting with M84 this is is also recorded for webapps/WebAPKs. Filter the platform for just CCT/TWA data.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.SpeculationStatusOnStart
histogram.summary: Recorded only for Android. How a speculation was started or why it was disallowed. This is recorded when a speculation of any kind is requested, with either a "Speculation allowed" value, or a reason why it was not allowed. If the speculation is allowed and is attempted to be started, the status on start will be counted in addition: one of "Prefetch", "Prerender", "Background Tab" or "Prerender not started". The last case is when a prerender is abandoned during start. Starting with M84 this is is also recorded for webapps/WebAPKs. Filter the platform for just CCT/TWA data.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.SpeculationStatusOnSwap
histogram.summary: Recorded only for Android. Recorded when a speculation is requesting to swap into a visible tab. Starting with M84 this is is also recorded for webapps/WebAPKs. Filter the platform for just CCT/TWA data.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.Visible
histogram.summary: Recorded only for Android. Records on every metrics upload whether the currently visible tab is a custom tab. The custom tab is launched by a client app with customized UI. Some metrics like page load time will behave differently for these tabs since it will be possible to warm up the browser process and prepare resources for specific urls ahead of time via a service connection. This histogram can be used to cluster these metric differently.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.WarmupStateOnLaunch
histogram.summary: Recorded only on Android. Reports whether warmup() has been called when a Custom Tabs Intent is received from an application, and if so, whether the call could be matched with a session. Starting with M84 this is is also recorded for webapps/WebAPKs. Filter the platform for just CCT/TWA data.

histogram.name: CustomTabs.WebContentsStateOnLaunch
histogram.summary: Android: When a CustomTabActivity creates its Tab, whether we use a prerendered WebContents, a spare renderer or create a new WebContents.

histogram.name: DataPack.Load
histogram.summary: Errors when trying to load DataPack files.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.AutoLoFiAccuracy
histogram.summary: Counts the accuracy of estimated network quality when using Lo-Fi. Counters are incremented when a main frame URL request is handled by Data Reduction Proxy and session was in Auto Lo-Fi enabled field trial.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.AutoLoFiRequestHeaderState
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 8/2017, per server-directed preview decision.
histogram.summary: Counts the changes in the state of Lo-Fi request header (q=low) being added to the Chrome proxy request header. Counters are incremented when a main frame URL request is handled by Data Reduction Proxy and session was in Auto Lo-Fi enabled field trial.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.BlockTypeFallback
histogram.summary: Counts various events that trigger Chrome to block the fallback configuration of the data reduction proxy.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.BlockTypePrimary
histogram.summary: Counts various events that trigger Chrome to block the primary configuration of the data reduction proxy.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.BypassedBytes
histogram.summary: Counts the response bytes that did not go through the data reduction proxy as the result of a bypass event.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.BypassedBytes.NotBypassed
histogram.summary: Counts the response bytes that went through the data reduction proxy and were not bypassed.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.BypassInfoFallback
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 6/2014, replaced by DataReductionProxy.BypassTypeFallback.
histogram.summary: Counts various events that trigger Chrome to bypass the fallback configuration of the data reduction proxy.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.BypassInfoPrimary
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 6/2014, replaced by DataReductionProxy.BypassTypePrimary.
histogram.summary: Counts various events that trigger Chrome to bypass the primary configuration of the data reduction proxy.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.BypassOnNetworkErrorFallback
histogram.summary: Positive net error code that caused the fallback data reduction proxy to be bypassed and put on the proxy retry list. Called after a failure to connect or resolve a host name.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.BypassOnNetworkErrorPrimary
histogram.summary: Positive net error code that caused the primary data reduction proxy to be bypassed and put on the proxy retry list. Called after a failure to connect or resolve a host name.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.BypassTypeFallback
histogram.summary: Counts various events that trigger Chrome to bypass the fallback configuration of the data reduction proxy.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.BypassTypePrimary
histogram.summary: Counts various events that trigger Chrome to bypass the primary configuration of the data reduction proxy.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.CaptivePortalDetected.Platform
histogram.summary: Records if the platform detected that a captive portal is present on the current network. Recorded at the time of Chrome startup and on IP change event. Recorded only for users that have data saver enabled.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigFetchLostBytesCL
histogram.summary: Size of the response body. This is the actual number of bytes received, which usually agrees with but is not necessarily the same as the size specified by the Content-Length header. Only recorded if the request is sent while a simulated Data Reduction Proxy configuration fetch is taking place.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigFetchLostBytesDiff
histogram.summary: The difference between the size specified in the X-Original-Content-Length header and the size of the response body. Only recorded if the request is sent while a simulated Data Reduction Proxy configuration fetch is taking place. Only positive values are logged, so if X-Original-Content-Length is not specified or if it equals or exceeds the content length, it is not logged.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigFetchLostBytesOCL
histogram.summary: Size specified in the X-Original-Content-Length header. If this header is not present in the response, the size of the response body is used. Only recorded if the request is sent while a simulated Data Reduction Proxy configuration fetch is taking place.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigService.AuthExpired
histogram.summary:
Total number of authentication required responses received from the Data Reduction Proxy.
An "Unexpired" value is recorded every time a new client config is fetched and parsed successfully. An "Expired" value is recorded every time Chrome receives an authentication failure from the data saver proxy.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigService.AuthExpiredSessionKey
histogram.summary:
On receiving an authentication failure from the data reduction proxy, records whether the session key used in the request matches the current session key.
Recorded every time Chrome receives an authentication failure from the data saver proxy.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigService.AuthFailure.LatencyPenalty
histogram.summary:
Time from when the request starts to the time when the auth failure is received from the data saver proxy.
Recorded every time an auth failure is received from the data saver proxy.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigService.ConnectionSetupTime
histogram.summary: The time delta between connectStart and connectEnd of the client config request. This is recorded only on successful requests when connectStart and connectEnd were non-null.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigService.FetchFailedAttemptsBeforeSuccess
histogram.summary:
The number of failed attempts when retrieving the configuration from the Data Reduction Proxy API before a successful attempt took place.
Recorded every time a new client config is fetched and parsed successfully. If the device has a valid connection type but no Internet connectivity, this count will keep increasing.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigService.FetchLatency
histogram.summary:
Time from when the Data Reduction Proxy configuration request starts until it completes.
Recorded every time a new client config is fetched and parsed successfully.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigService.FetchResponseCode
histogram.summary: Count per HttpResponseCode when attempting to retrieve the Data Reduction Proxy configuration from the Data Reduction Proxy API.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigService.HttpRequestRTT
histogram.summary: The time delta between requestStart and requestEnd of the client config request. This is recorded only on successful requests.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigService.HTTPRequests
histogram.summary:
Upper bound on the count of HTTP requests that did or did not go through the data saver proxy.
An HTTP request may not go through data saver proxy if the config was unavailable, due to local bypass rules, or due to server side bypasses. If data saver is turned on, this metric is recorded every time proxy is resolved for an HTTP request.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigService.MainFrames
histogram.summary:
Upper bound on the count of main frame requests that did or did not go through the data saver proxy.
A main frame request may not go through data saver proxy if the config was unavailable, due to local bypass rules, or due to server side bypasses. If data saver is turned on, this metric is recorded every time proxy is resolved for an HTTP request.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigService.PersistedConfigIsExpired
histogram.summary: Records whether the persisted client config for data saver proxies read from the disk at the time of Chrome startup was expired or not. Recorded once at the time of Chrome startup.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.ConfigService.SentVariationHeaders
histogram.summary: Records whether the client config client sent variation headers in the config request.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.DaysSinceEnabled
histogram.summary: Records the number of days since data reduction proxy was last enabled by the user. Recorded only if the data reduction proxy was last enabled since this metric was added to Chromium (M-56). Recorded at Chrome startup if data reduction proxy is already enabled, and when the user enables the data reduction proxy.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.DaysSinceSavingsCleared.NegativeSystemClock
histogram.summary: Records the number of days since the data reduction savings were cleared because the system clock moved back by more than 1 day. Recorded at Chrome startup if data reduction proxy is enabled, and if the data reduction proxy savings were cleared in some previous Chrome session.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.DeleteBrowsingHistory.NumBuckets
histogram.summary: When the data savings are cleared because the user deletes their browsing history, record how many buckets were deleted. See also DataReductionProxy.SavingsCleared.Reason/User action - cleared browsing history.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.EnabledState
histogram.summary:
Samples of user interactions with the Data Saver settings menu across all platforms (android, IOS, desktop extension). Data is collected whenever user toggles the ON/OFF switch in the settings menu for reducing data usage.
For desktop, Off-to-On/On-to-Off histogram count will increment when the data saver extension is installed/uninstalled respectively as well.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.HeaderTamperDetectionHTTP
histogram.summary: For each carrier, the total number of HTTP responses that have been checked for tampering. This assumes the data reduction proxy injected fingerprints have not been tampered with. Only the data reduction proxy responses with 200 OK response code are checked.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.HeaderTamperDetectionHTTPS
histogram.summary: For each carrier, the total number of HTTPS responses that have been checked for tampering. This assumes the data reduction proxy injected fingerprints have not been tampered with. Only the data reduction proxy responses with 200 OK response code are checked.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.HeaderTamperDetectionPassHTTP
histogram.summary: For each carrier, the total number of HTTP responses that passed the tamper detection. This assumes the data reduction proxy injected fingerprints have not been tampered with. Only the data reduction proxy responses with 200 OK response code are checked.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.HeaderTamperDetectionPassHTTPS
histogram.summary: For each carrier, the total number of HTTPs responses that passed the tamper detection. This assumes the data reduction proxy injected fingerprints have not been tampered with. Only the data reduction proxy responses with 200 OK response code are checked.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.HeaderTamperedHTTP
histogram.summary: The total number of HTTP responses that some part (specified by suffix name) have been tampered with. This assumes the data reduction proxy injected fingerprints have not been tampered with. Only the data reduction proxy responses with 200 OK response code are checked.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.HeaderTamperedHTTPS
histogram.summary: The total number of HTTPS responses that some part (specified by suffix name) have been tampered with. This assumes the data reduction proxy injected fingerprints have not been tampered with. Only the data reduction proxy responses with 200 OK response code are checked.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.HistoricalDataUsageLoadTime
histogram.summary: Time taken to load historical data usage from Level DB into memory. This UMA is reported each time the method to load historical data usage is called, which happens when user views data usage history.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.InvalidResponseHeadersReceived.NetError
histogram.summary: Net error codes observed when invalid response headers were received from data reduction proxy.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.LastWeekAggregateKB.Services
histogram.summary: Records the aggregate data use of services for the last week. Different conditions are added as suffixes to this histogram. Recorded when Chrome is started, so this does not include the current session. The aggregation happens when requests complete. The current week calculation is based on week starting from the Epoch day(Thursday, January 1, 1970), and every 7 days is treated as a week.

histogram.name: DataReductionProxy.LastWeekAggregateKB.UserTraffic.Downstream.ContentType
