histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2020 because enough data had been gathered.
histogram.summary: The number of profiles that miss at least one report and may not be able to catch the next round due to browser shutdown. The metric is recorded during browser shutdown process.

histogram.name: Enterprise.CloudReportingUploadTrigger
histogram.summary: The trigger detected by the ReportScheduler responsible that led to an attempt to upload an enterprise report.

histogram.name: Enterprise.DeviceLocalAccountPolicyInvalidations2
histogram.summary:
Events for counting device local account policy invalidations received with and without payloads. Invalidations indicate that a policy has been updated and should be refreshed. Payloads provide context about the policy update, but may be absent if dropped by the invalidation service.
As opposed to current histogram, Enterprise.DevicePolicyInvalidations2 counts only events of device policy invalidations.

histogram.name: Enterprise.DeviceLocalAccountPolicyRefresh3
histogram.summary:
Events measuring effectiveness of refreshing device local account policy when invalidations are received from a service. For each refresh, indicates whether the policy changed, and whether the policy was invalidated at the time of the refresh.
As opposed to current histogram, Enterprise.DevicePolicyRefresh3 counts only events of refreshing device policies.

histogram.name: Enterprise.DevicePolicyDeviceIdValidity
histogram.summary: Result of the device ID validation in the device policy blob.

histogram.name: Enterprise.DevicePolicyInvalidations
histogram.obsolete: Removed 03/2020 in order to separate device and device local account. Superseded by Enterprise.DevicePolicyInvalidations2 and Enterprise.DeviceLocalAccountPolicyInvalidations2.
histogram.summary: Events for counting both device and device local account policies invalidations received with and without payloads. Invalidations indicate that a policy has been updated and should be refreshed. Payloads provide context about the policy update, but may be absent if dropped by the invalidation service.

histogram.name: Enterprise.DevicePolicyInvalidations2
histogram.summary:
Events for counting device policy invalidations received with and without payloads. Invalidations indicate that a policy has been updated and should be refreshed. Payloads provide context about the policy update, but may be absent if dropped by the invalidation service.
As opposed to current histogram, Enterprise.DeviceLocalAccountPolicyInvalidations2 counts only events of device local account policy invalidations.

histogram.name: Enterprise.DevicePolicyRefresh
histogram.obsolete: Removed 10/2019 because was recorded incorrectly. Superseded by Enterprise.DevicePolicyRefresh2.
histogram.summary: Events measuring effectiveness of refreshing device policy when invalidations are received from a service. For each refresh, indicates whether the policy changed, and whether the policy was invalidated at the time of the refresh.

histogram.name: Enterprise.DevicePolicyRefresh2
histogram.obsolete: Removed 03/2020 in order to separate device and device local account. Superseded by Enterprise.DevicePolicyRefresh3 and Enterprise.DeviceLocalAccountPolicyRefresh3.
histogram.summary: Events measuring effectiveness of refreshing both device and device local account policies when invalidations are received from a service. For each refresh, indicates whether the policy changed, and whether the policy was invalidated at the time of the refresh.

histogram.name: Enterprise.DevicePolicyRefresh3
histogram.summary:
Events measuring effectiveness of refreshing device policy when invalidations are received from a service. For each refresh, indicates whether the policy changed, and whether the policy was invalidated at the time of the refresh.
As opposed to current histogram, Enterprise.DeviceLocalAccountPolicyRefresh3 counts only events of refreshing device local account policies.

histogram.name: Enterprise.DeviceRemoteCommand.Executed
histogram.summary: Events for counting status of executed device remote commands. The metric includes only signed device remote commands. Reports a final status of finished remote command (e.g. success, failure or termination).

histogram.name: Enterprise.DeviceRemoteCommand.Executed.Unsigned
histogram.summary: Events for counting status of executed device remote commands. The metric includes only unsigned device remote commands. Reports a final status of finished remote command (e.g. success, failure or termination).

histogram.name: Enterprise.DeviceRemoteCommand.Received
histogram.summary: Events for counting received device remote commands. The metric includes signed device remote commands, both valid and invalid. Valid remote command is counted by its type. Invalid remote command is counted by a reason why it is considered invalid (e.g. unknown type or duplication).

histogram.name: Enterprise.DeviceRemoteCommand.Received.Unsigned
histogram.summary: Events for counting received device remote commands. The metric includes unsigned device remote commands, both valid and invalid. Valid remote command is counted by its type. Invalid remote command is counted by a reason why it is considered invalid (e.g. unknown type or duplication).

histogram.name: Enterprise.DeviceRemoteCommandInvalidations
histogram.summary:
Events for counting device remote commands invalidations received with and without payloads. Invalidations indicate that there is a remote command to execute. Payloads provide context about the remote commands update, but may be absent if dropped by the invalidation service.
Metric is similar to Enterprise.DevicePolicyInvalidations2. Device local account scope does not exist for remote commands so there is no metric for remote commands similar to Enterprise.DeviceLocalAccountPolicyInvalidations2.

histogram.name: Enterprise.DeviceSettings.MissingPolicyMitigated
histogram.summary: Recorded every time policy file is missing or corrupted on Chrome OS. Should be compared to Enterprise.DeviceSettings.UpdatedStatus as happens only if status is STORE_NO_POLICY and device being not managed.

histogram.name: Enterprise.DeviceSettings.UpdatedStatus
histogram.summary: Recorded every time as DeviceSettingsProvider updates its current state. Used to compare rate of Enterprise.DeviceSettings.MissingPolicyMitigated event.

histogram.name: Enterprise.DMServerRequestSuccess
histogram.summary: Number of retries the client did to execute a DeviceManagementServer request. It's recorded after the request has been completed, either successfully after 0 or more retries, with a failure (too many retries or non-retriable error) or with a server error.

histogram.name: Enterprise.DMToken
histogram.obsolete: Removed 03/2019, since no code reports it anymore.
histogram.summary: Events related to fetching, saving and loading DM server tokens. These are used to retrieve cloud policies.

histogram.name: Enterprise.DomainWhitelistRegexFailure
histogram.obsolete: Removed 03/2019, since the issues has been long fixed.
histogram.summary: Temporary metric tracking which regex caused an icu::RegexMatcher initialization failure, to help figure out the cause of http://crbug.com/365351 which we can't repro locally.

histogram.name: Enterprise.DomainWhitelistRegexFailureStatus
histogram.obsolete: Removed 03/2019, since the issues has been long fixed.
histogram.summary: Temporary metric tracking the type of an icu::RegexMatcher initialization failure, to help figure out the cause of http://crbug.com/365351 which we can't repro locally.

histogram.name: Enterprise.DomainWhitelistRegexSuccess
histogram.obsolete: Removed 03/2019, since the issues has been long fixed.
histogram.summary: Temporary metric tracking the success of icu::RegexMatcher IcuMatcher initialization, to help figure out the cause of http://crbug.com/365351.

histogram.name: Enterprise.EnrolledPolicyHasDMToken
histogram.summary: Whether loading of device policy from file on an enterprise-enrolled (checked against install_attributes.pb) Chrome OS device yields an enterprise policy with a DM token. Filled once during session startup, after first successful device policy read.

histogram.name: Enterprise.Enrollment
histogram.summary: Events related to device enrollment on new installs of Chrome OS devices.

histogram.name: Enterprise.EnrollmentAttestationBased
histogram.summary: Events related to attestation-based enrollment (Zero-Touch) of Chrome OS devices.

histogram.name: Enterprise.EnrollmentConfiguration
histogram.summary: Events related to Chrome OS enterprise enrollment automated by OOBE configuration.

histogram.name: Enterprise.EnrollmentForced
histogram.summary: Events related to forced re-enrollment (FRE) of Chrome OS devices.

histogram.name: Enterprise.EnrollmentForcedAttestationBased
histogram.summary: Events related to attestation-based re-enrollment (Auto RE) of Chrome OS devices.

histogram.name: Enterprise.EnrollmentForcedInitial
histogram.summary: Events related to forced initial enrollment of Chrome OS devices during the first startup of a device.

histogram.name: Enterprise.EnrollmentForcedInitialAttestationBased
histogram.summary: Events related to attestation-based initial enrollment (Zero-Touch) of Chrome OS devices during the first startup of a device.

histogram.name: Enterprise.EnrollmentForcedInitialManualFallback
histogram.summary: Events related to manual fallback from attestation-based initial enrollment (Zero-Touch) of Chrome OS devices during the first startup of a device.

histogram.name: Enterprise.EnrollmentForcedManualFallback
histogram.summary: Events related to manual fallback from attestation-based re-enrollment (Auto RE) of Chrome OS devices.

histogram.name: Enterprise.EnrollmentRecovery
histogram.summary: Events related to Chrome OS enterprise enrollment recovery. Note that this only covers cases in which prior to recovery, the "private owner" of the device had UMA stats enabled.

histogram.name: Enterprise.EnrollmentTime.Cancel
histogram.summary: Elapsed time from *after* GAIA login until enrollment was cancelled.

histogram.name: Enterprise.EnrollmentTime.Failure
histogram.summary: Elapsed time from *after* GAIA login until enrollment failed.

histogram.name: Enterprise.EnrollmentTime.Success
histogram.summary: Elapsed time from *after* GAIA login until enrollment succeeded.

histogram.name: Enterprise.ExistingInstallAttributesLock
histogram.summary: Chrome OS only. Whether the successful locking of install attributes reused the previously existing locked attributes, or a fresh lock has being held.

histogram.name: Enterprise.FCMInvalidationService.DeviceLocalAccountPolicyInvalidations2
histogram.summary:
Events for counting device local account policy invalidations received with and without payloads. Invalidations indicate that a policy has been updated and should be refreshed. Payloads provide context about the policy update, but may be absent if dropped by the invalidation service. Subset of "Enterprise.DeviceLocalAccountPolicyInvalidations2". Gets recorded only when FCMInvalidationService is used as InvalidationService.
As opposed to current histogram, Enterprise.FCMInvalidationService.DevicePolicyInvalidations2 counts only events of device policy invalidations.

histogram.name: Enterprise.FCMInvalidationService.DeviceLocalAccountPolicyRefresh3
histogram.summary:
Events measuring effectiveness of refreshing device local account policy when invalidations are received from a service. For each refresh, indicates whether the policy changed, and whether the policy was invalidated at the time of the refresh. Subset of "Enterprise.DeviceLocalAccountPolicyRefresh3". Gets recorded only when FCMInvalidationService is used as InvalidationService.
As opposed to current histogram, Enterprise.FCMInvalidationService.DevicePolicyRefresh3 counts only events of refreshing device policies.

histogram.name: Enterprise.FCMInvalidationService.DevicePolicyInvalidations
histogram.obsolete: Removed 03/2020 in order to separate device and device local account. Superseded by Enterprise.FCMInvalidationService.DevicePolicyInvalidations2 and Enterprise.FCMInvalidationService.DeviceLocalAccountPolicyInvalidations2.
histogram.summary: Events for counting both device and device local account policies invalidations received with and without payloads. Invalidations indicate that a policy has been updated and should be refreshed. Payloads provide context about the policy update, but may be absent if dropped by the invalidation service. Subset of "Enterprise.DevicePolicyInvalidations". Gets recorded only when FCMInvalidationService is used as InvalidationService.

histogram.name: Enterprise.FCMInvalidationService.DevicePolicyInvalidations2
histogram.summary:
Events for counting device policy invalidations received with and without payloads. Invalidations indicate that a policy has been updated and should be refreshed. Payloads provide context about the policy update, but may be absent if dropped by the invalidation service. Subset of "Enterprise.DevicePolicyInvalidations2". Gets recorded only when FCMInvalidationService is used as InvalidationService.
As opposed to current histogram, Enterprise.FCMInvalidationService.DeviceLocalAccountPolicyInvalidations2 counts only events of device local account policy invalidations.

histogram.name: Enterprise.FCMInvalidationService.DevicePolicyRefresh
histogram.obsolete: Removed 10/2019 because was recorded incorrectly. Superseded by Enterprise.FCMInvalidationService.DevicePolicyRefresh2.
histogram.summary: Events measuring effectiveness of refreshing device policy when invalidations are received from a service. For each refresh, indicates whether the policy changed, and whether the policy was invalidated at the time of the refresh. Subset of "Enterprise.DevicePolicyRefresh". Gets recorded only when FCMInvalidationService is used as InvalidationService.

histogram.name: Enterprise.FCMInvalidationService.DevicePolicyRefresh2
histogram.obsolete: Removed 03/2020 in order to separate device and device local account. Superseded by Enterprise.FCMInvalidationService.DevicePolicyRefresh3 and Enterprise.FCMInvalidationService.DeviceLocalAccountPolicyRefresh3.
histogram.summary: Events measuring effectiveness of refreshing both device and device local account policies when invalidations are received from a service. For each refresh, indicates whether the policy changed, and whether the policy was invalidated at the time of the refresh. Subset of "Enterprise.DevicePolicyRefresh2". Gets recorded only when FCMInvalidationService is used as InvalidationService.

histogram.name: Enterprise.FCMInvalidationService.DevicePolicyRefresh3
histogram.summary:
Events measuring effectiveness of refreshing device policy when invalidations are received from a service. For each refresh, indicates whether the policy changed, and whether the policy was invalidated at the time of the refresh. Subset of "Enterprise.DevicePolicyRefresh3". Gets recorded only when FCMInvalidationService is used as InvalidationService.
As opposed to current histogram, Enterprise.FCMInvalidationService.DeviceLocalAccountPolicyRefresh3 counts only events of refreshing device local account policies.

histogram.name: Enterprise.FCMInvalidationService.PolicyInvalidations
histogram.summary: Events for counting user policy invalidations received with and without payloads. Invalidations indicate that a policy has been updated and should be refreshed. Payloads provide context about the policy update, but may be absent if dropped by the invalidation service. Subset of "Enterprise.PolicyInvalidations". Gets recorded only when FCMInvalidationService is used as InvalidationService.

histogram.name: Enterprise.FCMInvalidationService.PolicyInvalidationsRegistrationResult
histogram.summary: Tracks the result of registration for policy invalidations. It will fail when duplicate invalidation names are used for different policies due to http://b/119860379. Subset of "Enterprise.PolicyInvalidationsRegistrationResult". Gets recorded only when FCMInvalidationService is used as InvalidationService.

histogram.name: Enterprise.FCMInvalidationService.PolicyRefresh
histogram.obsolete: Removed 10/2019 because was recorded incorrectly. Superseded by Enterprise.FCMInvalidationService.PolicyRefresh2.
histogram.summary: Events measuring effectiveness of refreshing user policy when invalidations are received from a service. For each refresh, indicates whether the policy changed, and whether the policy was invalidated at the time of the refresh. Subset of "Enterprise.PolicyRefresh". Gets recorded only when FCMInvalidationService is used as InvalidationService.

histogram.name: Enterprise.FCMInvalidationService.PolicyRefresh2
histogram.summary: Events measuring effectiveness of refreshing user policy when invalidations are received from a service. For each refresh, indicates whether the policy changed, and whether the policy was invalidated at the time of the refresh. Subset of "Enterprise.PolicyRefresh2". Gets recorded only when FCMInvalidationService is used as InvalidationService.

histogram.name: Enterprise.HeartbeatSignalSuccess
histogram.summary: Result of a single attempt to signal a device heartbeat, which is used for monitoring the device connectivity. By default heartbeats per a single device are sent every 2 minutes.

histogram.name: Enterprise.InitialEnrollmentRequirement
histogram.summary: Chrome OS only. Encodes if initial enrollment should be done, and if not, why. This histogram is recorded when the Auto Enrollment Check screen is reached in the Chrome OS Out Of The Box Experience (OOBE) sequence and the device has not been enrolled or in consumer mode before, i.e. when the 'check_enrollment' and 'ActivateDate' VPD keys are not present.

histogram.name: Enterprise.InvalidDevicePolicyFiles
histogram.obsolete: Removed 10/2019, since no code reports it anymore. Superseded by Enterprise.InvalidDevicePolicyFilesStatus.
histogram.summary: Chrome OS only. Number of policy files that turned out to be invalid when reading the device policy data. In success scenario the latest policy file read is successful and has valid data submitting zero for this statistic.

histogram.name: Enterprise.InvalidDevicePolicyFilesStatus
histogram.summary: Chrome OS only. Result of checking if device policy files are valid when reading the device policy data.

histogram.name: Enterprise.IOSPolicies
histogram.summary: Number of policies loaded at startup on iOS, and when a change is detected at runtime.

histogram.name: Enterprise.LockToSingleUserResult
histogram.summary: Chrome OS only. The result of D-Bus call to lock device to single user. Estimated that till version M82 we should get enough stats to understand if there are big problems with the D-Bus call.

histogram.name: Enterprise.MachineLevelUserCloudPolicyEnrollment.InstallLevel_Win
histogram.obsolete: Remove in M82. Because we decide not to handle user level enrollment separately.
histogram.summary: Records whether the Chrome install is system level or user level when trying to enroll into the machine level user cloud policy on Windows. This is intended to count the number of enrollments for each install type.

histogram.name: Enterprise.MachineLevelUserCloudPolicyEnrollment.RequestFailureTime
histogram.summary: Time since the enrollment request was made until an error is returned from the server.

histogram.name: Enterprise.MachineLevelUserCloudPolicyEnrollment.RequestSuccessTime
histogram.summary: Time since the enrollment request was made until a successful enrollment response is returned from the server.

histogram.name: Enterprise.MachineLevelUserCloudPolicyEnrollment.Result
histogram.summary: The result of machine level user cloud policy enrollment.

histogram.name: Enterprise.MachineLevelUserCloudPolicyEnrollment.StartupDialog
histogram.summary: Records whether the machine level user cloud policy enrollment dialog is shown to the user and the action they took.

histogram.name: Enterprise.MachineLevelUserCloudPolicyEnrollment.StartupDialogTime
histogram.summary: The length of time the machine level user cloud policy enrollment dialog was visible to the user.

histogram.name: Enterprise.MachineLevelUserCloudPolicyEnrollment.UnenrollSuccess
histogram.summary: Records whether a browser unenrollment was completed succcessfully by writing an invalid DM token to storage or not.

histogram.name: Enterprise.ONC.PolicyValidation
histogram.summary: Result of the OpenNetworkConfiguration policy validation.

histogram.name: Enterprise.Policies
histogram.summary: A set of enterprise policy rules that are in use. This is recorded every 24 hours and at startup, if the last recording was earlier than a day before.

histogram.name: Enterprise.Policies.IgnoredByPolicyGroup
histogram.summary: A set of enterprise policy rules that are ignored because they do not share the highest priority from their policy atomic group. This is recorded at startup, then every 24 hours. If chrome is not running at the 24 hours mark, this will be recorded at the next startup.

histogram.name: Enterprise.Policies.SourceConflicts
histogram.summary: A set of policy atomic groups that have at least one policy disabled because of its source. This is recorded at startup, then every 24 hours. If chrome is not running at the 24 hours mark, this will be recorded at the next startup.

histogram.name: Enterprise.Policy
histogram.obsolete: Removed 03/2019, since no code reports it anymore.
histogram.summary: Events related to fetching, saving and loading user policies, and also device policies on Chrome OS.

histogram.name: Enterprise.PolicyHasVerifiedCachedKey
histogram.summary: Boolean tracking whether there is a valid policy signing key on disk.

histogram.name: Enterprise.PolicyInvalidations
histogram.summary: Events for counting user policy invalidations received with and without payloads. Invalidations indicate that a policy has been updated and should be refreshed. Payloads provide context about the policy update, but may be absent if dropped by the invalidation service.

histogram.name: Enterprise.PolicyInvalidationsRegistrationResult
histogram.summary: Tracks the result of registration for policy invalidations. It will fail when duplicate invalidation names are used for different policies due to http://b/119860379.

histogram.name: Enterprise.PolicyInvalidationsStartupTime
histogram.summary: Time since startup of the cloud policy code until the policy invalidation service first reported its online status.

histogram.name: Enterprise.PolicyKeyVerification
histogram.summary: Tracking the results of policy key verification.

histogram.name: Enterprise.PolicyLoadStatus
histogram.summary: Load status from the policy loaders which pull policy settings from the underlying platform, such as Windows Group Policy.

histogram.name: Enterprise.PolicyRefresh
histogram.obsolete: Removed 10/2019 because was recorded incorrectly. Superseded by Enterprise.PolicyRefresh2.
histogram.summary: Events measuring effectiveness of refreshing user policy when invalidations are received from a service. For each refresh, indicates whether the policy changed, and whether the policy was invalidated at the time of the refresh.

histogram.name: Enterprise.PolicyRefresh2
histogram.summary: Events measuring effectiveness of refreshing user policy when invalidations are received from a service. For each refresh, indicates whether the policy changed, and whether the policy was invalidated at the time of the refresh.

histogram.name: Enterprise.PolicyUpdatePeriod.Device
histogram.summary: Time since last device policy fetch.

histogram.name: Enterprise.PolicyUpdatePeriod.MachineLevelUser
histogram.summary: Time since last machine level user policy fetch.

histogram.name: Enterprise.PolicyUpdatePeriod.User
histogram.summary: Time since last user policy fetch.

histogram.name: Enterprise.PolicyUserVerification
histogram.summary: Tracking the results of policy user verification.

histogram.name: Enterprise.PublicSession.ExtensionPermissions
histogram.summary: Extensions (and apps) are quite restricted in public sessions for security and privacy concerns - some permissions are blocked which means the extension itself is blocked (prevented from loading). This histogram counts requested permissions on extension installation in public sessions (both installed and blocked extensions).

histogram.name: Enterprise.PublicSession.SessionLength
histogram.summary: A public session starts when a user signs in using the policy configured public account. It stops when the user signs out. Session length is the duration between the start and stop events. It is recorded during signing out and reported on the next chrome run. This metric tracks the recorded session length rounded down to the nearest 10-minute bucket and capped at 24 hours.

histogram.name: Enterprise.RealtimeReportingSuccess
histogram.summary: Number of retries the client did to execute a real-time reporting upload request. It's recorded after the request has been completed, either successfully after 0 or more retries, with a failure (too many retries or non-retriable error) or with a server error.

histogram.name: Enterprise.RegularUserSession.SessionLength
histogram.summary: A regular user session starts when a user signs in using his/her user account. It stops when the user signs out. Session length is the duration between the start and stop events. It is recorded during signing out and reported on the next chrome run. This metric tracks the recorded session length on an enrolled device rouned down to the nearest 10-minute bucket and capped at 24 hours.

histogram.name: Enterprise.RemoteCommand.RemoteSession.DeviceIsIdle
histogram.summary: When an enterprise admin attempts to establish a remote session to a device running in kiosk mode, the command will fail if device is not considered idle. This histogram is emitted when that happens.

histogram.name: Enterprise.RemoteCommand.RemoteSession.IdlenessOverride
histogram.summary: When an enterprise admin attempts to establish a remote session to a device running in kiosk mode, it is possible to override usual idleness check and connect anyway.

histogram.name: Enterprise.RemoteCommandInvalidationsRegistrationResult
histogram.summary:
Tracks whether remote commands invalidator registered for corresponding invalidations. In case of success the invalidator is able to receive incoming invalidations.
Metric is similar to Enterprise.PolicyInvalidationsRegistrationResult.

histogram.name: Enterprise.ResourceCacheTiming
histogram.obsolete: Removed 01/2020 since the histogram indicators were stable.
histogram.summary: The time to perform operations on the ResourceCache object.

histogram.name: Enterprise.RetrievePolicyResponse
histogram.summary: On Chrome OS, the response obtained to retrieve policy request.

histogram.name: Enterprise.SchemaMismatchedValueIgnored
histogram.obsolete: Removed 01/2020 because SCHEMA_ALLOW_INVALID strategy was removed.
histogram.summary: Signals whether mismatched value was ignored while validating while using SCHEMA_ALLOW_INVALID strategy. The strategy is used only for ManagedBookmarks policy and is considered for deprecation. Recorded when policy is validated using SCHEMA_ALLOW_INVALID strategy.

histogram.name: Enterprise.SystemFeaturesDisableList
histogram.summary: Shows which system features are set to be disabled by SystemFeaturesDisableList policy. Recorded when the policy preference is updated with a new system feature. No-op updates aren't counted.

histogram.name: Enterprise.SystemLogPIILeak
histogram.obsolete: Removed and removed from code as of 01/2016.
histogram.summary: Events for counting sensitive data occurrences in system logs to upload. (Email addresses, IP addresses, SSIDs, URLs). The lines with this data will be removed before upload.

histogram.name: Enterprise.SystemLogUploadResult
histogram.summary: Result of a single attempt to upload system logs.

histogram.name: Enterprise.TiclInvalidationService.DevicePolicyInvalidations
histogram.summary: Events for counting device policy invalidations received with and without payloads. Invalidations indicate that a policy has been updated and should be refreshed. Payloads provide context about the policy update, but may be absent if dropped by the invalidation service. Subset of "Enterprise.DevicePolicyInvalidations". Gets recorded only when TiclInvalidationService is used as InvalidationService;

histogram.name: Enterprise.TiclInvalidationService.DevicePolicyRefresh
histogram.obsolete: Removed 10/2019 because was recorded incorrectly. Superseded by Enterprise.TiclInvalidationService.DevicePolicyRefresh2.
histogram.summary: Events measuring effectiveness of refreshing device policy when invalidations are received from a service. For each refresh, indicates whether the policy changed, and whether the policy was invalidated at the time of the refresh. Subset of "Enterprise.DevicePolicyRefresh". Gets recorded only when TiclInvalidationService is used as InvalidationService.

histogram.name: Enterprise.TiclInvalidationService.DevicePolicyRefresh2
histogram.summary: Events measuring effectiveness of refreshing device policy when invalidations are received from a service. For each refresh, indicates whether the policy changed, and whether the policy was invalidated at the time of the refresh. Subset of "Enterprise.DevicePolicyRefresh2". Gets recorded only when TiclInvalidationService is used as InvalidationService.

histogram.name: Enterprise.TiclInvalidationService.PolicyInvalidations
histogram.summary: Events for counting user policy invalidations received with and without payloads. Invalidations indicate that a policy has been updated and should be refreshed. Payloads provide context about the policy update, but may be absent if dropped by the invalidation service. Subset of "Enterprise.PolicyInvalidations". Recorded only when TiclInvalidationService was used as InvalidationService.

histogram.name: Enterprise.TiclInvalidationService.PolicyInvalidationsRegistrationResult
histogram.summary: Tracks the result of registration for policy invalidations. It will fail when duplicate invalidation names are used for different policies due to http://b/119860379. Subset of "Enterprise.PolicyInvalidationsRegistrationResult". Gets recorded only when TiclInvalidationService is used as InvalidationService.

histogram.name: Enterprise.TiclInvalidationService.PolicyRefresh
histogram.obsolete: Removed 10/2019 because was recorded incorrectly. Superseded by Enterprise.TiclInvalidationService.PolicyRefresh2.
histogram.summary: Events measuring effectiveness of refreshing user policy when invalidations are received from a service. For each refresh, indicates whether the policy changed, and whether the policy was invalidated at the time of the refresh. Subset of "Enterprise.PolicyRefresh". Recorded only when TiclInvalidationService was used as InvalidationService.

histogram.name: Enterprise.TiclInvalidationService.PolicyRefresh2
histogram.summary: Events measuring effectiveness of refreshing user policy when invalidations are received from a service. For each refresh, indicates whether the policy changed, and whether the policy was invalidated at the time of the refresh. Subset of "Enterprise.PolicyRefresh2". Recorded only when TiclInvalidationService was used as InvalidationService.

histogram.name: Enterprise.UploadJobSuccess
histogram.summary: Number of retries the client did to execute an UploadJob. It's recorded after the request has been completed, either successfully after 0 or more retries, with a failure because of too many retries or if the UploadJob is interrupted (destroyed before it could succeed or fail).

histogram.name: Enterprise.UserCloudPolicyStore.LoadStatus
histogram.summary: Result of the attempted policy load during profile initialization.

histogram.name: Enterprise.UserCloudPolicyStore.LoadValidationStatus
histogram.summary: Result of validating the policy that has just been loaded from disk.

histogram.name: Enterprise.UserCloudPolicyStore.StoreValidationStatus
histogram.summary: Result of validating the policy sent down from the server, before writing to disk.

histogram.name: Enterprise.UserPolicyChromeOS.ChildUser.OAuthTokenError
histogram.summary: Failure reason for OAuth token fetch for child user.

histogram.name: Enterprise.UserPolicyChromeOS.DelayInitialization
histogram.summary: Initialization delay due to loading the user policy cache.

histogram.name: Enterprise.UserPolicyChromeOS.InitialFetch.ClientError
histogram.summary: Policy client error during initial policy fetch.

histogram.name: Enterprise.UserPolicyChromeOS.InitialFetch.DelayClientRegister
histogram.summary: Delay for registering the client with the policy server.

histogram.name: Enterprise.UserPolicyChromeOS.InitialFetch.DelayOAuth2Token
histogram.summary: Delay for minting an OAuth2 acccess token.

histogram.name: Enterprise.UserPolicyChromeOS.InitialFetch.DelayPolicyFetch
histogram.summary: Delay for fetching policy from the policy server.

histogram.name: Enterprise.UserPolicyChromeOS.InitialFetch.DelayTotal
histogram.summary: Total delay for the initial policy fetch.

histogram.name: Enterprise.UserPolicyChromeOS.InitialFetch.OAuth2Error
histogram.summary: Service error during OAuth2 access token fetch.

histogram.name: Enterprise.UserPolicyChromeOS.InitialFetch.OAuth2NetworkError
histogram.summary: Network error during OAuth2 access token fetch.

histogram.name: Enterprise.UserPolicyChromeOS.ReregistrationResult
histogram.summary: Re-registration results after failed policy fetch.

histogram.name: Enterprise.UserPolicyChromeOS.SessionAbort
histogram.summary: Session abort due to a fatal error of Chrome OS user policy initialization.

histogram.name: Enterprise.UserPolicyValidationFailure
histogram.summary: Source of policy validation errors on Chrome OS.

histogram.name: Enterprise.UserPolicyValidationLoadStatus
histogram.summary: Validation result when loading user policy from the policy store.

histogram.name: Enterprise.UserPolicyValidationStoreStatus
histogram.summary: Validation result when writing user policy to the policy store.

histogram.name: Enterprise.UserRemoteCommand.Executed
histogram.summary: Events for counting status of executed user remote commands. The metric includes only signed user remote commands. Reports a final status of finished remote command (e.g. success, failure or termination).

histogram.name: Enterprise.UserRemoteCommand.Executed.Unsigned
histogram.summary: Events for counting status of executed user remote commands. The metric includes only unsigned user remote commands. Reports a final status of finished remote command (e.g. success, failure or termination).

histogram.name: Enterprise.UserRemoteCommand.Received
histogram.summary: Events for counting received user remote commands. The metric includes signed user remote commands, both valid and invalid. Valid remote command is counted by its type. Invalid remote command is counted by a reason why it is considered invalid (e.g. unknown type or duplication).

histogram.name: Enterprise.UserRemoteCommand.Received.Unsigned
histogram.summary: Events for counting received user remote commands. The metric includes unsigned user remote commands, both valid and invalid. Valid remote command is counted by its type. Invalid remote command is counted by a reason why it is considered invalid (e.g. unknown type or duplication).

histogram.name: Enterprise.UserRemoteCommandInvalidations
histogram.summary:
Events for counting user remote commands invalidations received with and without payloads. Invalidations indicate that there is a remote command to execute. Payloads provide context about the remote commands update, but may be absent if dropped by the invalidation service.
Metric is similar to Enterprise.PolicyInvalidations.

histogram.name: Enterprise.UserSession.Logins
histogram.summary: Tracks the sign-in events on an enrolled device.

histogram.name: Enterprise.UserSigninChoice
histogram.summary: Choice the user made when presented with enterprise signin dialog.

histogram.name: Enterprise.VpdCheck
histogram.summary: The status code (exit code or signal) from sanity check of RW_VPD read and matching the contents with data from dump_vpd_log (Chrome OS).

histogram.name: Enterprise.VpdUpdateStatus
histogram.summary: The status code (exit code or signal) from VPD update operation (Chrome OS).

histogram.name: Enterprise.WildcardLoginCheck.DelayPolicyTokenFetch
histogram.summary: Delay incurred by the token fetching step of the wildcard login check.

histogram.name: Enterprise.WildcardLoginCheck.DelayTotal
histogram.summary: Total delay incurred by the wildcard login check.

histogram.name: Enterprise.WildcardLoginCheck.DelayUserInfoFetch
histogram.summary: Delay incurred by the user info fetching step of the wildcard login check.

histogram.name: EnterpriseCheck.DeprecatedDomainBindSucceeded
histogram.summary: Whether we were able to contact the AD Domain Controller. This check is performed once at start-up on Windows.

histogram.name: EnterpriseCheck.DeprecatedDomainCheckFailed
histogram.summary: Enum of possible things that can fail while checking for enterprise env. This check is performed once at start-up on Windows.

histogram.name: EnterpriseCheck.InDomain
histogram.summary: Whether the machine is part of an AD domain. This check is performed once at start-up on Windows.

histogram.name: EnterpriseCheck.InvalidJsonPolicies
histogram.summary: A set of policy rules that failed validation due to unparsable JSON, or JSON that did not match the schema. This validation happens on start-up and when the policy has changed.

histogram.name: EnterpriseCheck.InvalidPolicies
histogram.summary: A set of policy rules that were ignored due to integrity violations while parsing the policy data which happens on start-up and when the policy has changed.

histogram.name: EnterpriseCheck.InvalidPoliciesDetected
histogram.summary: The number of disabled policy entries on Windows due to integrity violations while parsing the policy data which happens on start-up and when the policy has changed.

histogram.name: EnterpriseCheck.IsDomainJoined
histogram.summary: Whether the machine is joined to an AD domain. This check is performed once at start-up on Windows.

histogram.name: EnterpriseCheck.IsEnterpriseUser
histogram.summary: Whether the machine is considered an enterprise user. An enterprise user is either in a domain or is managed via MDM. This check is performed once at start-up on Windows.

histogram.name: EnterpriseCheck.IsFullyManaged
histogram.summary: On Android (with version M and higher): Whether the device has a "device owner" app installed. This check is performed once at start-up.

histogram.name: EnterpriseCheck.IsLocalMachine
histogram.summary: Whether the full machine name is the same as the short one, identifying a local machine. This check is performed once at start-up on Windows.

histogram.name: EnterpriseCheck.IsLocalUser
histogram.summary: Whether the currently logged in user is a local user or not, based on its SAM compatible user name and machine name. This check is performed once at start-up on Windows.

histogram.name: EnterpriseCheck.IsManaged
histogram.summary: On Windows: Whether the machine is managed via MDM. On macOS: Whether Chrome found a file of managed policies to follow. On Android (with version M and higher): Whether the device has a profile owner app. This check is performed once at start-up. Note that this data is bogus before M81 on the Mac.

histogram.name: EnterpriseCheck.IsRunningOnManagedProfileDuration
histogram.summary: Records the time it takes to calculate whether there is a profile owner app on the device. Android only.

histogram.name: EnterpriseCheck.Mac.IsCurrentUserDomainUser
histogram.summary: On the Mac: Whether the current user has an enterprise identity in Open Directory. This check is performed once at start-up.

histogram.name: EnterpriseCheck.Mac.IsDeviceDomainJoined
histogram.summary: On the Mac: Whether the machine is joined to a domain. This check is performed once at start-up.

histogram.name: EnterpriseCheck.Mac.IsDeviceMDMEnrolledNew
histogram.summary: On the Mac: Whether the machine is managed via MDM, with data obtained via a new method (profiles) that is only available in 10.13.4 or later. This check is performed once at start-up.

histogram.name: EnterpriseCheck.Mac.IsDeviceMDMEnrolledOld
histogram.summary: On the Mac: Whether the machine is managed via MDM, with data obtained via an old method (system_profiler). This check is performed once at start-up.

histogram.name: EnterpriseCheck.NetGetJoinInformationAddress
histogram.summary: Whether we could obtain the address of the NetGetJoinInformation function. This check is performed once at start-up on Windows.

histogram.name: EnterpriseCheck.OSType
histogram.summary: The rough Windows suite we are running on. This check is performed once at start-up on Windows.

histogram.name: EphemeralTab.BottomSheet.CloseReason
histogram.summary: Records the reason that the BottomSheet-based Ephemeral Tab panel was closed. Recorded when the UX is hidden. Implemented for Android.

histogram.name: EphemeralTab.CloseReason
histogram.summary: Records the reason that the Ephemeral Tab panel was closed. Recorded when the UX is hidden. Implemented for Android.

histogram.name: EphemeralTab.Ctr
histogram.summary: Records whether the user opened the Ephemeral Tab panel when it was shown. Recorded when the UX is hidden. Implemented for Android.

histogram.name: EphemeralTab.DurationOpened
histogram.summary: Records the duration in milliseconds that the Ephemeral Tab was left open. Recorded when the UX is hidden. Implemented for Android.

histogram.name: EphemeralTab.DurationPeeked
histogram.summary: Records the duration in milliseconds that the Ephemeral Tab was peeking. Recorded when the UX is hidden. Implemented for Android.

histogram.name: Event.ActionAfterDoubleTapNoDelay
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2017 due to lack of usage.
histogram.summary:
On non-mobile sites, gesture taps are delayed to prevent double taps from sending a click event. This stat tracks the user's first action within 5 seconds after a double tap gesture when the gesture tap delay is disabled.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.ActionAfterDoubleTapWithDelay
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2017 due to lack of usage.
histogram.summary:
On non-mobile sites, gesture taps are delayed to prevent double taps from sending a click event. This stat tracks the user's first action within 5 seconds after a double tap gesture when gesture tap events are delayed.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.AggregatedLatency.Renderer2
histogram.summary:
Time between initiation of any input event and the renderer receiving and starting to process it.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Event.AndroidActionDown.ToolType
histogram.summary:
Tracks the number of Android MotionEvents with ACTION_DOWN or ACTION_POINTER_DOWN or ACTION_BUTTON_PRESS for each ToolType (unknown, finger, mouse or stylus).
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.AsyncTargeting.AsyncClientDepth
histogram.summary: Tracks how many clients were queried (asynchronously) to determine the target for an input event.

histogram.name: Event.AsyncTargeting.ResponseTime
histogram.summary:
Tracks how long it takes for a client to respond to an asynchronous request to find a target for an input event.
Team: event-targeting@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.AsyncTargeting.TimeInQueue
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 01/2020. The feature has been launched.
histogram.summary:
Tracks how long an event has to wait in the queue while a previous event is being targeted asynchronously.
Team: event-targeting@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.ClickNotFiredDueToDomManipulation
histogram.summary:
Whether click target was affected by DOM manipulation or not.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.ClickTargetChangedDueToInteractiveElement
histogram.summary:
Whether click target was affected by interactive element being in the ancestor path.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histograms.histogram: tdresser@chromium.org Team: input-dev@chromium.org. Number of Mouse events coalesced.
tdresser@chromium.org Team: input-dev@chromium.org. Number of Touch events coalesced.

histogram.name: Event.CoalescedLatency.Mouse
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2017 due to lack of usage.
histogram.summary:
Time between the first and last events in a coalesced mouse events group.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.CoalescedLatency.Touch
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2017 due to lack of usage.
histogram.summary:
Time between the first and last events in a coalesced touch events group.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.CompositorThreadEventQueue.CoalescedCount
histogram.obsolete: Removed 01/2019 due to lack of usage.
histogram.summary:
Number of continuous gesture events (GestureScrollUpdate, GesturePinchUpdate) coalesced inside the compositor thread event queue per event. This field is recorded just before the event is processed on the compositor thread.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.CompositorThreadEventQueue.Continuous.HeadQueueingTime
histogram.obsolete: Removed 01/2019 due to lack of usage.
histogram.summary:
Time between the first event in a coalesced continuous gesture events group (GestureScrollUpdate, GesturePinchUpdate) and when it was processed on the compositor thread.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Event.CompositorThreadEventQueue.Continuous.TailQueueingTime
histogram.obsolete: Removed 01/2019 due to lack of usage.
histogram.summary:
Time between the last event in a coalesced continuous gesture events group (GestureScrollUpdate, GesturePinchUpdate) and when it was processed on the compositor thread.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Event.CompositorThreadEventQueue.NonContinuous.QueueingTime
histogram.obsolete: Removed 01/2019 due to lack of usage.
histogram.summary:
Time between when a non-continuous gesture event (GestureScrollStart/End, GesturePinchStart/End) was placed into the queue and when it was processed on the compositor thread.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Event.DownEventCount.PerDestination
histogram.summary: The number of down events received per destination. Every down event that is targeted to each destination will be counted, including those that don't have an effect. For example: Tapping on a disabled button inside the browser frame will be treated as down events on browser window.

histogram.name: Event.DownEventCount.PerFormFactor
histogram.summary: Counts the number of down events generated by clamshell/touchview.

histogram.name: Event.DownEventCount.PerInput
histogram.summary: Counts the number of down events generated by Mouse/Touch/Stylus.

histogram.name: Event.DownEventCount.PerInputFormFactorDestinationCombination
histogram.summary:
The number of down events received per input, form factor, and destination combination.
Input is down events generated by mouse/touch/stylus. Form factor is down events generated by clamshell/touchviewLandscape/touchviewPortrait. Destination: Every down event that is targeted to each destination will be counted including those that don't have an effect. For example: Tapping on a disabled button inside the browser frame will be treated as down events on browser window.

histogram.name: Event.DownEventCount.PerInputFormFactorDestinationCombination2
histogram.summary:
The number of down events received per destination, input and form factor combination.
Input is down events generated by mouse/touch/stylus. Form factor is down events generated by clamshell/touchviewLandscape/touchviewPortrait. Destination: Every down event that is targeted to each destination will be counted including those that don't have an effect. For example: Tapping on a disabled button inside the browser frame will be treated as down events on browser window.

histogram.name: Event.DragDrop.AcceptDragUpdate
histogram.summary:
Reports whether chrome accepts or rejects a drag and drop operation.
During a drag and drop operation, there are many 'drag update' events that happen for each mouse-move event. For each such drag-update event, chrome reports whether it is able to accept the drop event or not.

histogram.name: Event.DragDrop.Cancel
histogram.summary: Counts the number of times the user cancelled a drag and drop operation.

histogram.name: Event.DragDrop.Drop
histogram.summary: Counts the number of times the user completed a drag and drop operation.

histogram.name: Event.DragDrop.ExternalOriginDrop
histogram.summary: Counts the number of times a drag and drop operation originating outside of a Chrome window successfuly drops.

histogram.name: Event.DragDrop.Start
histogram.summary: Counts the number of times the user started a drag and drop operation.

histogram.name: Event.FractionOfTimeWithoutUserInput
histogram.summary: For each 10 second window, reports the fraction of the time there was no user input. We consider there to be no user input if we haven't received any events for > 50ms.

histogram.name: Event.FrameEventRouting.NoGestureTarget
histogram.summary: Record whether we unexpectedly encounter an empty gesture queue while routing gesture events to frames' renderers: true => empty queue, false => non-empty queue.

histogram.name: Event.FrameEventRouting.TouchEventAckQueueSize
histogram.summary: The size of the TouchEventAckQueue, recorded each time we process an event ack (the queue usually, but not always, decreases in size during ack processing). Ideally this queue will operate at length close to zero, depending on how long it takes for events to travel to a renderer and be processed and an ack sent back. As the size increases it indicates decreased responsiveness of renderers with respect to touch events. One purpose of this metric is to develop a sense of normal queue sizes for different platforms. Initially we expect queue length to be less than 20 most of the time, but this is a guess.

histogram.name: Event.Frequency.Renderer.FlingAnimate
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2017 due to lack of usage.
histogram.summary:
Emitted after a renderer process main-thread fling curve animation terminates, for any reason, reporting the average animation frequency (animations/second) of the fling instance over its lifetime. This is computed as the number of fling animation ticks divided by the fling animation duration.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.Frequency.RendererImpl.FlingAnimate
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2017 due to lack of usage.
histogram.summary:
Emitted after a renderer process impl-thread fling curve animation terminates, for any reason, reporting the average animation frequency (animations/second) of the fling instance over its lifetime. This is computed as the number of fling animation ticks divided by the fling animation duration.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.GestureCreated
histogram.summary:
The gesture-events recognized and dispatched by the browser gesture recognizer. This replaces Ash.GestureCreated, which did not record events on Android and Windows.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.HitTest
histogram.summary:
For each hit test, records whether it was a hit or miss, and why.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.HitTestValidity
histogram.summary:
For hit tests that are validated, records whether it was correct or not, and why.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.HitTestValidityScore
histogram.summary:
For hit tests that are invalid, records the score (a series of bits indicating what was matched and what wasn't).
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.InputEventPrediction.Accuracy.Mouse
histogram.summary:
Records the euclidean distance from a mouse event's position to the predicted position at the event time when prediction is available. Prediction is available after the predictor selected by feature flag gets a few continuous mouse events.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.InputEventPrediction.Accuracy.Scroll
histogram.summary:
Records the euclidean distance from a ScrollUpdate event's absolute scroll position (count by accumulated delta) to the predicted scrolling position at the event time when prediction is available. Prediction is available after the predictor selected by feature flag gets a few continuous GestureScrollUpdate events.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.InputEventPrediction.Accuracy.Scroll.OverPredict
histogram.summary:
Records y direction distance from a ScrollUpdate event's absolute scroll position (count by accumulated delta) to the predicted scrolling position at the event time when prediction is available and also the predicted result is 'OverPredict'. Over predict means the absolute value of predicted scroll delta is larger than the real delta in the same time period.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.InputEventPrediction.Accuracy.Scroll.UnderPredict
histogram.summary:
Records y direction distance from a ScrollUpdate event's absolute scroll position (count by accumulated delta) to the predicted scrolling position at the event time when prediction is available and also the predicted result is 'UnderPredict'. Under predict means the absolute value of predicted scroll delta is smaller than the real delta in the same time period. GestureScrollUpdate events.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.InputEventPrediction.Accuracy.Touch
histogram.summary:
Records the euclidean distance from a touch event's position to the predicted position at the event time when prediction is available. Prediction is available after the predictor selected by feature flag gets a few continuous touches with same ids.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.InputEventPrediction.Scroll.OverPrediction
histogram.summary:
Overshoot distance in pixels between a predicted scroll position and the corresponding interpolated real scroll position that occurred at the prediction time.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.InputEventPrediction.Scroll.PredictionJitter
histogram.summary:
Euclidean distance in pixels between two successive variations of touch scroll deltas which are the difference between a predicted scroll position and its corresponding interpolated real position that occurred at prediction time.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.InputEventPrediction.Scroll.UnderPrediction
histogram.summary:
Undershoot distance in pixels between a predicted scroll position and the corresponding interpolated real scroll position that occurred at the prediction time.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.InputEventPrediction.Scroll.VisualJitter
histogram.summary:
Euclidean distance in pixels between two successive variations of touch scroll deltas which are the difference between a predicted scroll position and the interpolated real position that occurred at frame time.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.InputEventPrediction.Scroll.WrongDirection
histogram.summary:
Boolean that indicates whether the prediction is in the same direction as the real trajectory at the same prediction time. True means the direction is not the same (wrong direction).
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.BlockingTime.KeyPressDefaultAllowed
histogram.summary: Time between the renderer main thread receiving a keyboard event and acking it, for events which were not preventDefaulted. Only recorded for key presses.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.BlockingTime.KeyPressDefaultPrevented
histogram.summary: Time between the renderer main thread receiving a keyboard event and acking it, for events which were preventDefaulted. Only recorded for key presses.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.BlockingTime.TouchEndDefaultAllowed
histogram.summary:
Time between the renderer main thread receiving a touchend event and acking it, for events which were not preventDefaulted.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.BlockingTime.TouchEndDefaultPrevented
histogram.summary:
Time between the renderer main thread receiving a touchend event and acking it, for events which were preventDefaulted.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.BlockingTime.TouchMoveDefaultAllowed
histogram.summary:
Time between the renderer main thread receiving a touchmove event and acking it, for events which were not preventDefaulted.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.BlockingTime.TouchMoveDefaultPrevented
histogram.summary:
Time between the renderer main thread receiving a touchmove event and acking it, for events which were preventDefaulted.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.BlockingTime.TouchStartDefaultAllowed
histogram.summary:
Time between the renderer main thread receiving a touchstart event and acking it, for events which were not preventDefaulted.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.BlockingTime.TouchStartDefaultPrevented
histogram.summary:
Time between the renderer main thread receiving a touchstart event and acking it, for events which were preventDefaulted.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.Browser.TouchUI
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2017 due to lack of usage.
histogram.summary:
Time between touch events received by Chrome and sent from RWH to renderer.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.Browser.WheelAcked
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2017 due to lack of usage.
histogram.summary:
Time between wheel events sent from RWH to renderer and acked by renderer.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.Browser.WheelUI
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2017 due to lack of usage.
histogram.summary:
Time between wheel events received by Chrome and sent from RWH to renderer.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.EndToEnd.KeyPress
histogram.summary:
Time between the OS receiving a keyboard event and the resulting GPU frame swap. If no swap was induced by the event, no recording is made. Only recorded for key presses.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.EndToEnd.Mouse
histogram.summary:
Time between the OS receiving a mouse event and the resulting GPU frame swap. If no swap was induced by the event, no recording is made.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.EndToEnd.TouchpadPinch
histogram.summary: Time between the OS receiving a touchpad pinch event and the resulting GPU frame swap. If no swap was induced by the event, no recording is made.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.EndToEnd.TouchpadPinch2
histogram.summary: Time between the OS receiving a touchpad pinch event and the resulting GPU frame swap. If no swap was induced by the event, no recording is made.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.EventToRender.TouchpadPinch
histogram.summary: Time between the OS receiving a touchpad pinch event and RenderWidgetHost sending event to render.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.HitTest
histogram.summary:
Duration of a non-resurive hitTest operation. The hit test is non-recursive when the AllowChildFrameContent flag is clear. See also Event.Latency.HitTestRecusrive.
Team: paint-dev@chromium.org.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.HitTestRecursive
histogram.summary:
Duration of a recursive hitTest operation. The hit test is recursive when the AllowChildFrameContent flag is set. See also Event.Latency.HitTest.
Team: paint-dev@chromium.org.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histograms.histogram:
tdresser@chromium.org Team: input-dev@chromium.org.
Time between input event received by OS and sent to Chrome.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.OS_NO_VALIDATION.NEGATIVE
histogram.summary:
For Mac, a version of Event.Latency.OS that has the negative values it would contain if ValidateEventTimeClock() were not called on the timestamps.
This metric is intended to debug http://crbug.com/1039833, where very high numbers are seen for PageLoad.InputDelay3. The units of this metric are the same as PageLoad.InputDelay3 for consistency while debugging.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.OS_NO_VALIDATION.POSITIVE
histogram.summary:
For Mac, a version of Event.Latency.OS that has the positive values it would contain if ValidateEventTimeClock() were not called on the timestamps.
This metric is intended to debug http://crbug.com/1039833, where very high numbers are seen for PageLoad.InputDelay3. The units of this metric are the same as PageLoad.InputDelay3 for consistency while debugging.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.QueueingTime.KeyPressDefaultAllowed
histogram.summary:
Time between sending a keyboard event to the renderer main thread and when the renderer begins to process that event, for events which were not preventDefaulted. Only recorded for key presses.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.QueueingTime.KeyPressDefaultPrevented
histogram.summary:
Time between sending a keyboard event to the renderer main thread and when the renderer begins to process that event, for events which were preventDefaulted. Only recorded for key presses.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.QueueingTime.TouchEndDefaultAllowed

Time between sending a touchend event to the renderer main thread and when the renderer begins to process that event, for events which were not preventDefaulted.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.QueueingTime.TouchEndDefaultPrevented
histogram.summary:
Time between sending a touchend event to the renderer main thread and when the renderer begins to process that event, for events which were preventDefaulted.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.QueueingTime.TouchMoveDefaultAllowed

Time between sending a touchmove event to the renderer main thread and when the renderer begins to process that event, for events which were preventDefaulted.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.QueueingTime.TouchStartDefaultAllowed
histogram.summary:
Time between sending a touchstart event to the renderer main thread and when the renderer begins to process that event, for events which were not preventDefaulted.

Time between sending a touchstart event to the renderer main thread and when the renderer begins to process that event, for events which were preventDefaulted.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.Renderer
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2017 due to lack of usage.
histogram.summary:
Time between initiation of all input events and renderer processing. This is soon to be replaced by Event.Latency.Renderer2.*
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.Renderer2
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2017 due to lack of usage.
histogram.summary:
Time between input event creation and the renderer receiving and starting to process the event. For touch events on Windows, we measure from when the event reaches Chrome, whereas on other platforms we use the timestamp from the kernel. On Windows, this metric is only reported when |IsHighResolution| is true, which will introduce some sampling bias.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.RendererImpl
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2017 due to lack of usage.
histogram.summary:
Time between input event creation and the renderer impl thread receiving and starting to process the event. For touch events on Windows, we measure from when the event reaches Chrome, whereas on other platforms we use the timestamp from the kernel. On Windows, this metric is only reported when |IsHighResolution| is true, which will introduce some sampling bias.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.RendererImpl.GestureScroll
histogram.summary: Time between initial creation of touch event and when the resulting ScrollGesture reaches Impl thread. Maximum is 200ms.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.RendererImpl.GestureScroll2
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2017 due to lack of usage.
histogram.summary:
Time between touch event creation and when the resulting GestureScroll reaches the Impl thread. Maximum is 1000ms. On Windows, we measure from when the touch event reaches Chrome, whereas on other platforms we use the timestamp from the kernel. On Windows, this metric is only reported when |IsHighResolution| is true, which will introduce some sampling bias. This supersedes the Event.Latency.RendererImpl.GestureScroll metric.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.Scroll.Wheel.TimeToHandled2
histogram.summary:
Time between initial creation of a wheel event and the generated ScrollUpdate gesture event is handled on main/impl thread (specified by suffix). If no swap was induced by the ScrollUpdate gesture event, no recording is made. Unlike Event.Latency.ScrollUpdate.Wheel.TimeToHandled2 this metric includes the first GSU of every scrolling sequence as well.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.Scroll.Wheel.TimeToScrollUpdateSwapBegin2
histogram.summary:
Time between initial creation of a wheel event and start of the frame swap on the GPU service caused by the generated ScrollUpdate gesture event. If no swap was induced by the event, no recording is made. Unlike Event.Latency.ScrollUpdate.Wheel.TimeToScrollUpdateSwapBegin2 this metric includes the first GSU of every scrolling sequence as well.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.ScrollBegin.Scrollbar.BrowserNotifiedToBeforeGpuSwap2
histogram.summary:
Time between the display compositor receives the notification of the first ScrollUpdate gesture event in a given scroll gesture event sequence with scrollbar source induced renderer swap and GPU starts to swap. The name contains BrowserNotified for consistency with the existing ScrollBegin/ScrollUpdate metrics - historically the display compositor lived in the browser process.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.ScrollBegin.Scrollbar.GpuSwap2
histogram.summary:
Time between gpu starts to swap the first ScrollUpdate gesture event in a given scroll gesture event sequence with scrollbar source induced frame and the swap finishes.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.ScrollBegin.Scrollbar.HandledToRendererSwap2
histogram.summary:
Time between the first ScrollUpdate gesture event in a given scroll gesture event sequence with scrollbar source, is handled on main/impl thread (specified by suffix) and before renderer starts to swap.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.ScrollBegin.Scrollbar.RendererSwapToBrowserNotified2
histogram.summary:
Time between the renderer starts to swap a frame (generates compositor frame) induced by the first ScrollUpdate gesture event in a given scroll gesture event sequence with scrollbar source, and display compositor receives the submitted compositor frame. The name contains BrowserNotified for consistency with the existing ScrollBegin/ScrollUpdate metrics - historically the display compositor lived in the browser process.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.ScrollBegin.Scrollbar.TimeToHandled2
histogram.summary:
Time between initial creation of a scrollbar event and the first generated ScrollUpdate gesture event in a given scroll gesture event sequence is handled on main/impl thread (specified by suffix). If no swap was induced by the ScrollUpdate gesture event, no recording is made.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.ScrollBegin.Scrollbar.TimeToScrollUpdateSwapBegin4
histogram.summary:
Time between initial creation of an input event that resulted in a scrollbar scroll and the start of the frame swap on the GPU service caused by the generated ScrollUpdate gesture event if that ScrollUpdate is the first such event in a given scroll gesture event sequence. If no swap was induced by the event, no recording is made.
Do not modify this metric in any way without contacting speed-metrics-dev@chromium.org.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.ScrollBegin.TimeToScrollUpdateSwapBegin
histogram.summary:
Time between initial creation of a wheel/touch event and start of the frame swap on the GPU service caused by the generated ScrollUpdate gesture event if that ScrollUpdate is the first such event in a given scroll gesture event sequence. If no swap was induced by the event, no recording is made. If no swap was induced by the event, no recording is made.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.ScrollBegin.TimeToScrollUpdateSwapBegin2
histogram.summary:
Time between initial creation of a wheel/touch event and start of the frame swap on the GPU service caused by the generated ScrollUpdate gesture event if that ScrollUpdate is the first such event in a given scroll gesture event sequence. If no swap was induced by the event, no recording is made. If no swap was induced by the event, no recording is made.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: Event.Latency.ScrollBegin.TopControlsMoved
histogram.summary: Records whether the browser top-controls (i.e. omnibox, toolbar) moved because of the scroll-begin event (i.e. the first scroll-update event of that sequence).

histogram.name: Event.Latency.ScrollBegin.Touch.AverageLag
histogram.summary:
Measures an average distance that represents how the page sticks to the finger when user scrolls. Only reports touch scrolling. See https://docs.google.com/document/d/154jddNiKtxELBvrjLz9v6A7sA1J3iwQQzySHgtY12Oo/
