histogram.summary: The number of extensions that the user has pinned on the toolbar. Recorded once per profile during initialization if the user has at least one extension with an action in the toolbar.

histogram.name: Extensions.Toolbar.PinnedExtensionPercentage
histogram.summary: The percentage of extensions that the user has pinned to the toolbar (i.e., this will be 100 if the user has every extension pinned). Recorded once per profile during initialization.

histogram.name: Extensions.Toolbar.PinnedExtensionPercentage2
histogram.summary: The percentage of extensions that the user has pinned to the toolbar (i.e., this will be 100 if the user has every extension pinned). Recorded once per profile during initialization if the user has at least one extension with an action in the toolbar.

histogram.name: Extensions.ToolstripLoadTime
histogram.obsolete: Removed 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Time taken to load a toolstrip.

histogram.name: Extensions.UnifiedExtensionUpdaterDownloadErrors
histogram.summary: Records the error codes of the unified extension updater download errors. These events are triggered when the extension updater fails to download the extension update package of an extension, which can happen only after the updater has successfuly determined that there is an update for the extension.

histogram.name: Extensions.UnifiedExtensionUpdaterUpdateCalls
histogram.summary: The number of extensions that are passed over to the unified extension updater for update check. Triggered when the new extension updater starts doing update check.

histogram.name: Extensions.UnifiedExtensionUpdaterUpdateCheckErrors
histogram.summary: Records the error codes of the unified extension updater update check errors. These events are triggered only when the new extension updater gets an error in the update check phase.

histogram.name: Extensions.UnifiedExtensionUpdaterUpdateResults
histogram.summary: Records the update results of extensions in an unified extension updater session, grouped by ExtensionUpdaterUpdateResult.

histogram.name: Extensions.UnifiedExtensionUpdaterUpdateServiceErrors
histogram.summary: The update service errors that the update service encouters during an update check session using update client.

histogram.name: Extensions.UninstallDialogAction
histogram.summary: The action that was taken from an extension uninstall dialog. Recorded once per dialog shown. Only recorded if all possible actions (including report abuse) were available on the dialog.

histogram.name: Extensions.UninstallSource
histogram.summary: The source for the extension uninstall. This is only recorded if an uninstall dialog is shown to the user (i.e., it is not recorded for internal "cleanups").

histogram.name: Extensions.UninstallType
histogram.summary: Uninstalls grouped by Extension::HistogramType.

histogram.name: Extensions.UnpackFailureInstallCause
histogram.summary: Count failing CRX installs, grouped by the way an extension can be installed.

histogram.name: Extensions.UnpackFailureInstallSource
histogram.summary: The install source of an extension that failed to unpack correctly. Emitted each time an extension fails to unpack from the CrxInstaller.

histogram.name: Extensions.UnpackSuccessInstallCause
histogram.summary: Count successful CRX installs, grouped by the cause of the install.

histogram.name: Extensions.UnpackSuccessInstallSource
histogram.summary: The install source of an extension that succeeded in unpacking correctly. Emitted each time an extension successfully unpacks from the CrxInstaller.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateCheckApp
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of legacy packaged apps and hosted apps that were checked during an update check.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateCheckExtension
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of extensions that were checked during an update check.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateCheckGap
histogram.summary: Time in minutes between update checks.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateCheckGoogleUrl
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of crx's with a Google-hosted update URL that were checked during an update check.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateCheckNoUrl
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of crx's with no update URL checked during an update check.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateCheckOtherUrl
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of crx's with a non-Google update URL that were checked during an update check.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateCheckPackagedApp
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of packaged apps that were checked during an update check.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateCheckTheme
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of themes that were checked during an update check.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateFrequencyCommandLineFlagIsUsed
histogram.obsolete: Removed 3/2018 in https://crbug.com/357781 with the removal of the extensions-update-frequency command line flag.
histogram.summary: Logs whether or not the extensions-update-frequency command line flag is used. Recorded at profile creation if extensions autoupdate is enabled.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateManifestDuplicateEntryCount
histogram.summary: Counts the number of entries with the same extension ID found in the update manifest of an update check request. When the update manifest does not contain any update entry for an extension ID in the update check request, the count will be 0.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateManifestHasProdVersionMinCounts
histogram.summary: The number of prodversionmin attributes appearing in an update manifest of an update check request.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateOnLoad
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of extensions that were updated at browser startup.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdaterWriteCrx
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: What happened when the extension updater tried to write a file?

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateSource
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Updates grouped by the location property in prefs.

histogram.name: Extensions.UpdateType
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Updates grouped by Extension::HistogramType.

histogram.name: Extensions.UsedMimeTypeHandler
histogram.summary: Tracks the usage of different MIME type handlers used to view files. It is recorded when the browser intercepts navigation with one of the type handlers.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.EventListenerFlag
histogram.summary: Records any flags that were registered in the extraInfoSpec of a webRequest listener, as well as the total number of listeners registered. Recorded each time a new listener is registered.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.InitiatorAccess
histogram.summary: Describes the different cases pertaining to host permissions check for the initiator URL of a network request. This is emitted whenever an extension is found to have access to a request url, when checked by the WebRequest API.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.InitiatorAccess2
histogram.summary: Describes the different cases pertaining to host permissions check for the initiator URL of a network request. This is emitted whenever an extension is found to have access to a request url for the WebRequest or Declarative Net Request API.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.ModifiedResponseHeaders
histogram.obsolete: Removed 11/2018 as this is no longer needed.
histogram.summary:
Tracks whether any interesting response header modifications were made for each request seen by the Web Request API. Logged for every network request as long as one or more enabled extensions is using a declarative or blocking filter to process onHeadersReceived events.
This histogram is temporary, for investigating https://crbug.com/827582. It can probably be removed by M69.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.OnBeforeSendHeadersEventResponse
histogram.summary:
For every network request visible to the Web Request API, this indicates whether any registered declarative rules or event listeners observed the onBeforeSendHeaders event.
This histogram is temporary, for investigating https://crbug.com/827582. It can probably be removed by M69.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.OnHeadersReceivedEventResponse
histogram.summary:
For every network request visible to the Web Request API, this indicates whether any registered declarative rules or event listeners observed the onHeadersReceived event.
This histogram is temporary, for investigating https://crbug.com/827582. It can probably be removed by M69.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.RequestHeaderAdded
histogram.summary: Records the request header added by extensions using the WebRequest API. Recorded for each network request during the OnBeforeSendHeaders stage. Multiple samples can be recorded per request.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.RequestHeaderChanged
histogram.summary: Records the request header modified by extensions using the WebRequest API. Recorded for each network request during the OnBeforeSendHeaders stage. Multiple samples can be recorded per request.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.RequestHeaderRemoved
histogram.summary: Records the request header removed by extensions using the WebRequest API. Recorded for each network request during the OnBeforeSendHeaders stage. Multiple samples can be recorded per request.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.ResponseHeaderAdded
histogram.summary: Records the response header added by extensions using the WebRequest API. This is logged for the cumulative delta across all the web request extensions. Recorded for each network request during the OnHeadersReceived stage. Multiple samples can be recorded per request.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.ResponseHeaderChanged
histogram.summary: Records the response header modified by extensions using the WebRequest API. This is logged for the cumulative delta across all the web request extensions. Recorded for each network request during the OnHeadersReceived stage. Multiple samples can be recorded per request.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.ResponseHeaderRemoved
histogram.summary: Records the response header removed by extensions using the WebRequest API. This is logged for the cumulative delta across all the web request extensions. Recorded for each network request during the OnHeadersReceived stage. Multiple samples can be recorded per request.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.SetCookieResponseHeaderChanged
histogram.summary:
Whether the set-cookie response header was changed by an extension using the Web Request API. Logged for every network request as long as one or more enabled extensions is using a declarative or blocking filter to process onHeadersReceived events.
This histogram is temporary, for investigating https://crbug.com/827582. It can probably be removed by M73.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.SetCookieResponseHeaderRemoved
histogram.summary:
Whether the set-cookie response header was removed by an extension using the Web Request API. Logged for every network request as long as one or more enabled extensions is using a declarative or blocking filter to process onHeadersReceived events.
This histogram is temporary, for investigating https://crbug.com/827582. It can probably be removed by M73.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.SpecialHeadersRemoved
histogram.obsolete: Removed 11/2018 as this is no longer needed.
histogram.summary:
Whether either or both of the special Accept-Language or User-Agent request headers were removed by an extension using the Web Request API. Logged for every network request as long as one or more enabled extensions is using a declarative or blocking filter to process onBeforeSendHeaders events.
This histogram is temporary, for investigating https://crbug.com/827582. It can probably be removed by M69.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.SpecialRequestHeadersChanged
histogram.summary:
Whether Accept-Language, Accept-Encoding, User-Agent, Cookie, Referer, or mulitple of these request headers were changed by an extension using the Web Request API. Logged for every network request as long as one or more enabled extensions is using a declarative or blocking filter to process onBeforeSendHeaders events.
This histogram is temporary, for investigating https://crbug.com/827582. It can probably be removed by M73.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.SpecialRequestHeadersRemoved
histogram.summary:
Whether Accept-Language, Accept-Encoding, User-Agent, Cookie, Referer, or mulitple of these request headers were removed by an extension using the Web Request API. Logged for every network request as long as one or more enabled extensions is using a declarative or blocking filter to process onBeforeSendHeaders events.
This histogram is temporary, for investigating https://crbug.com/827582. It can probably be removed by M73.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.TotalBlockingRequestTime
histogram.summary: The total time a network request took when at least one Web Request listener was registered at the start of the request, and a listener was blocked on the request. Measures from onBeforeRequest to onCompleted/onErrorOccurred, and does not include canceled or redirected requests.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.TotalExtraHeadersRequestTime
histogram.summary: The total time a network request took when at least one Web Request listener with 'extraHeaders' in the extraInfoSpec was registered at the start of the request. Measures from onBeforeRequest to onCompleted/onErrorOccurred, and does not include canceled or redirected requests.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.TotalRequestTime
histogram.summary: The total time a network request took when at least one Web Request listener was registered at the start of the request. Measures from onBeforeRequest to onCompleted/onErrorOccurred, and does not include canceled or redirected requests.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.WS_CookiesAreModifiedOnBeforeSendHeaders
histogram.summary:
Whether cookies on a websocket connection are modified in OnBeforeSendHeaders.
This histogram is temporary, for investigating https://crbug.com/827582.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.WS_CookiesAreModifiedOnHeadersReceived
histogram.summary:
Whether cookies on a websocket connection are modified in OnHeadersReceived.
This histogram is temporary, for investigating https://crbug.com/827582.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.WS_RequestHeaders
histogram.summary: Whether certain headers are modified in OnBeforeSendHeaders.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.WS_RequestHeadersModification
histogram.summary:
Tracks request headers modifications on a websocket connection in OnBeforeSendHeaders.
This histogram is temporary, for investigating https://crbug.com/827582.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequest.WS_ResponseHeadersAreModified
histogram.summary:
Whether response headers on a websocket connection are modified in OnHeadersReceived.
This histogram is temporary, for investigating https://crbug.com/827582.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequestAction
histogram.summary: Counts the number of times an action is requested by extensions as part of the Web Request API. For a given web request stage, multiple actions may be logged.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequestBlockingCount
histogram.summary: Number of enabled extensions which have requested the "webRequestBlocking" permission. Includes all kinds of extensions, including component and unpacked extensions. Recorded during profile initialization.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequestCount
histogram.summary: Number of enabled extensions which have requested the "webRequest" permission. Includes all kinds of extensions, including component and unpacked extensions. Recorded during profile initialization.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebRequestEventFoundFrame
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 11/2017. No longer relevant after refactoring.
histogram.summary: Whether or not the render frame lookup for a given webRequest event succeeded.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebstoreDownload.FileDownload
histogram.summary: The time spent to download the crx file from the webstore to local disk. This is recorded once per successful download.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebstoreDownload.InterruptReason
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The reason a webstore download was interrupted.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebstoreDownload.InterruptReceivedKBytes
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of KBytes received for a webstore download before it was interrupted.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebstoreDownload.InterruptTotalKBytes
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The total expected size in KBytes of an interrupted webstore download.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebstoreDownload.InterruptTotalSizeUnknown
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Tracks whether the total size of an interrupted webstore download was known.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebUi.DocumentLoadedInMainFrameTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time between starting the provisional load and fully loading the document in the main frame of the chrome://extensions page. This corresponds to the WebContentsObserver::DocumentLoadedInFrame method.

histogram.name: Extensions.WebUi.LoadCompletedInMainFrame
histogram.summary: The amount of time between starting the provisional load and having completed the onload handler in the main frame of the chrome://extensions page. This corresponds to the WebContentsObserver::DocumentOnLoadCompletedInMainFrame method.

histogram.name: ExtensionService.AddVerified
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Records whether adding a new/updated extension to the install verifier succeeded.

histogram.name: ExtensionService.VerifyAllSuccess
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The outcome of a call to ExtensionService::VerifyAllExtensions, whether it was called for bootstrapping or another reason (extension installed/uninstalled, etc.).

histogram.name: ExtensionService.ZipUnpackerDisabledReason
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 09/2018, with the removal of the "Zip Unpacker" component extension.
histogram.summary: Records the reason why the ZIP unpacker extension was disabled when the service attempted to re-enable the extension upon startup. Each entry indicates that the extension was disabled unintentionally and a fix was attempted.

histogram.name: ExtensionSettings.ShouldDoVerificationCheck
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: When loading the chrome://extensions page, this records whether we decided to do a verification check against the server (because the user had one or more extensions disabled due to verification failure).

histogram.name: ExtensionToolbarModel.BrowserActionsCount
histogram.summary: The number of icons the Browser Actions Container knows about (visible or in the overflow bucket). Does not count icons that have been permanently hidden by the user. Measured once per startup per (non-incognito) profile.

histogram.name: ExtensionToolbarModel.BrowserActionsPermanentlyHidden
histogram.summary: The number of Browser Action icons the user has elected to permanently hide (as opposed to putting them in the overflow bucket). Measured once per startup per (non-incognito) profile.

histogram.name: ExtensionToolbarModel.BrowserActionsVisible
histogram.summary: The number of visible icons in the Browser Actions Container (visible as in number of icons not in the overflow bucket). 0 means all icons are in the overflow bucket. MAX_INT means the toolbar is always showing all icons. Measured once per startup per (non-incognito) profile but only for those profiles that have one or more browser actions showing in the toolbar.

histogram.name: ExtensionUrlRequest.Latency
histogram.summary: The time taken to complete an extension url request.

histogram.name: ExtensionUrlRequest.OnReadCompleteError
histogram.obsolete: Removed at 07/2019 because refactored DataPipeProducer does not provide the net::Error code any more, and it reaches the planned expiry milestone.
histogram.summary: The error code for failures of incremental reads of a file stream for a chrome-extension:// URL. (See also ExtensionUrlRequest.OnReadCompleteResult for the success case).

histogram.name: ExtensionUrlRequest.OnReadCompleteResult
histogram.obsolete: Removed at 07/2019 because refactored DataPipeProducer does not provide the reported value, and this metric was already broken, reporting always 0.
histogram.summary: The result of an incremental read of a file stream for a chrome-extension:// URL, representing a byte count. Logged in success cases (see also ExtensionUrlRequest.OnReadCompleteError).

histogram.name: ExtensionUrlRequest.SeekPosition
histogram.summary: When fetching a chrome-extension:// URL, this indicates the first byte position we read from. This will be greater than 0 in cases such as XHR's with a Range header, but will normally be 0 in the typical case of reading the entire file. This helps identify how frequently partial file reads are taking place.

histogram.name: ExtensionUrlRequest.TotalKbRead
histogram.summary: The total number of bytes read for a chrome-extension:// URL, logged when the job is finished (either successfully or not).

histogram.name: FactoryPingEmbargo.EndDateValidity
histogram.summary: Record the validity of |rlz_embargo_end_date| in RW_VPD. This is recorded when the VPD value is accessed, which may be multiple times in the same chrome invocation. This is not recorded when the VPD value is missing.

histogram.name: FaultTolerantHeap
histogram.summary: Records several bits of information about whether the Windows FaultTolerantHeap might be enabled. This is logged (from a delayed task) when the browser process starts up.

histogram.name: Favicon.OnDemandIconExistsInDb
histogram.summary: Records whether an icon already existed in the thumbnail DB when downloading an on-demand one. On-demand icons are the ones added to local cache without the user visiting the corresponding page.

histogram.name: Favicons.CandidatesCount
histogram.summary: Records the number of favicon link tags on a page once the page has finished loading. The count includes the automatically added favicon.ico entry.

histogram.name: Favicons.CandidatesWithDefinedSizesCount
histogram.summary: Records the number of favicon link tags with a non empty sizes attribute once the page has finished loading.

histogram.name: Favicons.CandidatesWithTouchIconsCount
histogram.summary: Records the number of apple-touch-icon and apple-touch-icon-precomposed link tags once the page has finished loading.

histogram.name: Favicons.DownloadAttempts
histogram.summary: Records the number of icons requested until the best-fitting candidate was found or there were no candidates left to check. More than 15 attempts are unlikely and will be stored in overflow bucket 16. Less than 1 attempt means an error happened; these cases are in bucket 0.

histogram.name: Favicons.DownloadOutcome
histogram.summary: Records whether a download succeeded, failed or was skipped because it has failed previously.

histogram.name: Favicons.LargeIconService.BlacklistedURLMismatch
histogram.summary: Records the number of large icons that were fetched from the local cache (e.g. during NTP impression), where the URL (of the page or the icon) matches a hardcoded blacklist. Suspicious mismatches are reported as errors.

histogram.name: Favicons.LargeIconService.DownloadedSize
histogram.summary: Records the size (concretely the width) in pixel of the favicon downloaded from Google favicon server (size 0 denotes that download has failed).

histogram.name: Favicons.LargeIconService.FallbackSize
histogram.summary: Records the size (concretely the width) in pixel of the favicon that is used to generate a fallback style in the case when large enough favicon is not available (size 0 denotes that no favicon is available at all).

histogram.name: FCMInvalidations.FailedSubscriptionsErrorCode
histogram.summary: Net error codes for failed subscription requests.

histogram.name: FCMInvalidations.FCMMessagesDeleted
histogram.summary: Recorded whenever the GCM client announces that some FCM messages were deleted. Note that this does not record the number of deleted messages.

histogram.name: FCMInvalidations.FCMMessageStatus
histogram.summary: Status of the message from the FCM channel. Recorded upon receiving response from the FCM channel.

histogram.name: FCMInvalidations.InitialTokenRetrievalStatus
histogram.summary: Status of the initial attempt to retrieve the instance id token.

histogram.name: FCMInvalidations.ResetClientIDStatus
histogram.summary: Status of resetting the client instance ID, which includes unregistering from FCM servers.

histogram.name: FCMInvalidations.SubscriptionRequestStatus
histogram.summary: Status of subscription request to the Per User Topic server. Recorded upon receiving response from server.

histogram.name: FCMInvalidations.SubscriptionResponseCode
histogram.summary: For each subcription request to the FCM Per-User-Topic server, log the response received from the server.

histogram.name: FCMInvalidations.SubscriptionResponseCodeForTopic
histogram.summary: For each subcription request to the FCM Per-User-Topic server, log the response received from the server per topic. Note: This is only recorded with a data type suffix. The base version is never recorded.

histogram.name: FCMInvalidations.TokenStateOnRegistrationRequest
histogram.obsolete: Replaced in 2019-12 by FCMInvalidations.TokenStateOnRegistrationRequest2. This first version did not record the "Token was cleared" bucket.
histogram.summary: Records the outcome of instance ID token requests (i.e. whether the token changed). Such requests are made when invalidations are first enabled (e.g. on sign-in), and then periodically every 24 hours.

histogram.name: FCMInvalidations.TokenStateOnRegistrationRequest2
histogram.summary: Records the outcome of instance ID token requests (i.e. whether the token changed). Such requests are made when invalidations are first enabled (e.g. on sign-in), and then periodically every 24 hours.

histogram.name: FCMInvalidations.UnsubscriptionRequestStatus
histogram.summary: Status of unsubscription request to the Per User Topic server. Recorded upon receiving response from server.

histogram.name: Feed.Offline.GetStatusCount
histogram.summary: The number of urls that have offline status requested per call.

histogram.name: Feed.Offline.GetStatusDuration
histogram.summary: The number of milliseconds to round trip offline status for a set of URLs from Offline Pages component.

histogram.name: Feed.Scheduler.RefreshTrigger
histogram.summary: The scheduler watches for various triggers, which cause it to decide if a refresh is currently warranted or not. This histogram is emitted when a trigger causes a refresh.

histogram.name: Feed.Scheduler.RequestBehavior
histogram.summary: When NTP is opened, the scheduler host tells the Feed library how to act, if the existing content should be shown, immediately or with timeout, and if a refresh request should be started.

histogram.name: Feedback.HappinessTrackingSurvey.BubbleUsage
histogram.summary: Records how user interacts with Happiness Tracking Surveys (HaTS) bubble prompt which is shown for a subset of users. Each time when the bubble is shown, there should be one and only one enum type is recorded.

histogram.name: Feedback.HappinessTrackingSurvey.DesktopResponse
histogram.summary: Reports on the responses to showing Happiness Tracking Surveys (HaTS) to a subset of users. Records each time HaTS is shown.

histogram.name: Feedback.HappinessTrackingSurvey.ShouldShowSurveyReason
histogram.summary: Records the first reason why the Happiness Tracking Surveys (HaTS) bubble was or was not shown. While the request to show may not meet multiple criteria, the first criterion is reported here.

histogram.name: Feedback.RequestSource
histogram.summary: Records the source that requested showing the feedback app.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.ChangeDirectory.RootType
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: Counts the number of directory-changed events, bucketed by the RootType of the directory newly displayed.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.CloudImport.UserAction
histogram.obsolete: Was removed from the code at an unknown time.
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser - Specific actions taken by user such initiating cloud import, canceling import, selecting a directory, or opting-in to Drive>Photos sync.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.ComputersCount
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: number of Computers a user has available in the Files app. Computed every time the File Browser is opened (including file picker dialogs). NOTE: This data is biased towards users that use the Files App more often.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.Create
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser opening mode.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.CrostiniShareDialog
histogram.summary: Dialog shown when using crostini app to open a file.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.CrostiniSharedPaths.Depth
histogram.summary: The depth from volume root of the path being shared. This is equivalent to how many '/' are in the path. E.g. 'Downloads' or 'My Drive' has depth 0. 'Downloads/foo/bar' has depth 2.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.DirectoryScan
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: time to scan a directory. Measured on every File Browser directory change.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.DownloadDestination.IsGoogleDrive.Changed
histogram.summary: Tracks whether download destination is set to a Google Drive folder when the download destination is changed by the user in the settings page.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.DownloadDestination.IsGoogleDrive.Started
histogram.summary: Tracks whether download destination is set to a Google Drive folder on startup.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.Downloads.DirectoryPercentageOfDiskUsage
histogram.summary: The size of the space consumed by a users files in "My Files" folder and it's children, calculated as a percentage of the total disk space. Caluclated on user login.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.Downloads.DirectorySizeMiB
histogram.summary: The total size of all of the users files stored in the "My Files" folder and it's children. Caluclated on user login.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.DownloadsCount
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: number of files and directories in the Downloads directory (not including the contents of nested directories). Computed every time the File Browser current directory changes to Downloads.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.DriveDuplicateFinder.LongComputeHash
histogram.summary: The time taken to calculate the hash of a file, only recorded if the time exceeds a local threshold that is currenty 5 seconds.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.DriveDuplicateFinder.LongSearchByHash
histogram.summary: The time taken to search for a file using it's hash value, only recorded if the time exceeds a local threshold that is currently 1 second.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.FileSystemProviderMounted
histogram.summary: The type of file system provider that has been mounted. This metric is emmitted on mounting of the filesystem.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.FolderShortcut.Add
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: this is recorded when the user adds a folder shortcut.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.FolderShortcut.Count
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: number of saved folder shorcuts. This is recorded when the Files app is launched.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.FolderShortcut.Navigate
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: this is recorded when the user clicks or selects a folder shortcut and is navigated to the target folder.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.FolderShortcut.Remove
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: this is recorded when the user removes a folder shortcut.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.FormatFileSystemType
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: this records the filesystem selected when formatting an external drive.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.HardUnpluggedAroundSuspend.TimeSinceResume
histogram.obsolete: The bug which the UMA was investigating got fixed.
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: time from the SuspendDone event to the DiskRemoved event. The UMA is added temporarily for crbug.com/433734.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.HardUnpluggedAroundSuspend.TimeUntilSuspend
histogram.obsolete: The bug which the UMA was investigating got fixed.
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: time from the DiskRemoved event to the Suspend event. The UMA is added temporarily for crbug.com/433734.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.ImportController.DeviceYanked
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Files App: Whether an external media device was removed during the upload process.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.ImportController.ImportCancelled
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Files App: Whether the media import process was cancelled.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.LauncherSearch.Drive
histogram.summary: The time taken to execute launcher search for drive files. Recorded when the complete result set is returned from drive.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.LauncherSearch.Local
histogram.summary: The time taken to execute launcher search for local files. Recorded when the complete result set has been calculated for files on the local disk.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.Load
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser is an built-in extension without a background page. Its main.html file is loaded every time the user opens a File Browser tab or a file chooser dialog. The file is fairly large and the initialization is pretty expensive.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.Location.OnEntryExpandedOrCollapsed.NonTopLevel
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Files App: The locations (root types) of non-top-level entries when they are expanded or collapsed (expand icon clicked) in the directory tree.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.Location.OnEntryExpandedOrCollapsed.TopLevel
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Files App: The locations (root types) of top-level entries (root entries) when they are expanded or collapsed (expand icon clicked) in the directory tree.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.Location.OnEntrySelected.NonTopLevel
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Files App: The locations (root types) of non-top-level entries when they are clicked in the directory tree.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.Location.OnEntrySelected.TopLevel
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Files App: The locations (root types) of top-level entries (root entries) when they are clicked in the directory tree.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.MediaImport.Cancelled
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Files App: Whether the media import (Photos/Video) from external media was cancelled.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.MediaImport.Duplicates
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Files App: Count of duplicate media (Photos/Videos) files that were skipped during a single upload session.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.MediaImport.ErrorCount
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Files App: Number of errors that occured during an upload session.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.MediaImport.ImportCount
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Files App: Count of individual media (Photos/Videos) uploaded from a single upload session.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.MediaImport.ImportMB
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Files App: Size of the media (Photos/Videos) uploaded from a single upload session.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.MediaImport.Started
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Files App: Whether the media import (Photos/Video) from external media was started.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.MenuItemSelected
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Files App: The commands selected in the menu by the files app.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.MountCrostiniContainer
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: time to start and mount the crostini container in order to show Linux Files.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.Notification.Show
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: Notification types what were shown to the user.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.Notification.UserAction
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: User actions responding to a notification.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.OpenFiles.RootType
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: The locations (root types) of files which are opened by the file picker.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.OpeningFileType
histogram.summary: File types that were tried to be opened through browser.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.PhotoEditor.DisplayTime
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Photo Editor: time to display an image. Measured from the moment the user selected the image till the moment it is displayed (not counting the low resolution preview).

histogram.name: FileBrowser.PhotoEditor.FileType
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Photo Editor: the type of the file opened.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.PhotoEditor.LoadMode
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Photo Editor: the way the image has been loaded.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.PhotoEditor.LoadTime
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Photo Editor: time to load an image from a file.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.PhotoEditor.SaveResult
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Photo Editor: the result of a file save operation.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.PhotoEditor.SaveTime
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Photo Editor: time to save an image to a file.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.PhotoEditor.Size.MB
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Photo Editor: size of an image file in megabytes. Measured on every image load.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.PhotoEditor.Size.MPix
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Photo Editor: size of an image in megapixels. Measured on every image load.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.PhotoEditor.Tool
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Photo Editor: the button which the user clicked.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.PhotoImport.Action
histogram.obsolete: Code seems to have been removed at an unknown time.
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Photo Import flow: action chosen in the Action Choice dialog for the external device.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.PhotoImport.ImportCount
histogram.obsolete: Code seems to have been removed at an unknown time.
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Photo Import flow: the number of photos imported. Measured on every successfull import operation.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.PhotoImport.ImportPercentage
histogram.obsolete: Code seems to have been removed at an unknown time.
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Photo Import flow: the percent of photos imported among all the photos on the device. Measured on every successfull import operation.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.PhotoImport.Load
histogram.obsolete: Code seems to have been removed at an unknown time.
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Photo Import flow: time to load the action dialog. Measured between the moment window appears and the moment user see all available actions for the device.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.PhotoImport.Scan
histogram.obsolete: Code seems to have been removed at an unknown time.
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Photo Import flow: time to scan the external device.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.QuickView.DialogType
histogram.summary: File dialog type (e.g. Full page, Save as file) when quick view is launched.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.QuickView.FileType
histogram.summary: File types that were tried to be opened with quick view.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.QuickView.FileTypeOnLaunch
histogram.summary: File types that were selected when quick view is launched.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.QuickView.VolumeType
histogram.summary: The volume type where quick view is opened.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.QuickView.WayToOpen
histogram.summary: How quick view was opened.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.Recent.LoadArcMedia
histogram.summary: Time to load a recently modified file list from Media Views. It is triggered when the user opens or reloads Recent view in the Files app.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.Recent.LoadCrostini
histogram.summary: Time to load a recently modified file list from Crostini. It is triggered when the user opens or reloads Recent view in the Files app.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.Recent.LoadDownloads
histogram.summary: Time to load a recently modified file list from Downloads. It is triggered when the user opens or reloads Recent view in the Files app.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.Recent.LoadDrive
histogram.summary: Time to load a recently modified file list from Drive. It is triggered when the user opens or reloads Recent view in the Files app.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.Recent.LoadTotal
histogram.summary: Time to load a recently modified file list from all sources. It is triggered when the user opens or reloads Recent view in the Files app.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.Share.ActionSource
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: the source of file sharing action.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.Share.FileCount
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: The number of files shared via a single Share action.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.Share.FileType
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: The type of the file shared via Share action. Recorded for each file being shared by a share action

histogram.name: FileBrowser.SuggestApps.CloseDialog
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: the reason why the suggest apps dialog was closed.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.SuggestApps.Install
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: whether the Webstore item user selected was successfully installed or not.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.SuggestApps.Load
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: whether the initialization of the dialog succeeded or not.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.SuggestApps.LoadTime
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: time to load the suggest apps dialog. Measured between the moment window appears and the moment all the contents in the dialog including the Chrome Webstore widget are ready.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.TeamDrivesCount
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: number of Team Drives a user has available in the Files app. Computed every time the File Browser is opened (including file picker dialogs). NOTE: This data is biased towards users that use the Files App more often.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.ToggleFileListType
histogram.summary: Chrome OS Files App: Recorded when the Grid View/List View toggle menu icon is selected.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.ViewingFileType
histogram.summary: File types that were tried to be viewed through browser. This is recorded when the user tries to view a file from the Files app.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.ViewingFileType.Offline
histogram.summary: File types that were tried to be viewed through browser while the user is offline. This is recorded when the user tries to view a file from the Files app.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.ViewingFileType.Online
histogram.summary: File types that were tried to be viewed through browser while the user is online. This is recorded when the user tries to view a file from the Files app.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.ViewingRootType
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: The locations (root types) of files which are opened in stand-alone mode. This does not include files opened in file picker mode.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.ViewingRootType.Offline
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: The locations (root types) of files which are opened in stand-alone mode while the user is offline. This does not include files opened in file picker mode.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.ViewingRootType.Online
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: The locations (root types) of files which are opened in stand-alone mode while the user is online. This does not include files opened in file picker mode.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.ViewingTaskType
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: The type of the handler to be used to open files. This is recorded when the user tries to view a file from the Files app.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.ViewingTaskType.Offline
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: The type of the handler to be used to open files when the user is offline. This is recorded when the user tries to view a file from the Files app.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.ViewingTaskType.Online
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: The type of the handler to be used to open files when the user is online. This is recorded when the user tries to view a file from the Files app.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.VolumeType
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: counts the number of times volumes are mounted for each volume type.

histogram.name: FileBrowser.ZipFileTask
histogram.summary: Chrome OS File Browser: counts the number of times ZIP file was opened or created, categorized by component extensions and its operation types.

histogram.name: FileReaderSync.WorkerType
histogram.summary: For each FileReaderSync instance records the type of the web worker that created the instance.

histogram.name: FileSystem.DirectoryDatabaseInit
histogram.summary: The result of FileSystemDirectoryDatabase initialization.

histogram.name: FileSystem.DirectoryDatabaseRepair
histogram.summary: The result of FileSystemDirectoryDatabase repair.

histogram.name: FileSystem.OpenFileSystem
histogram.summary: The success or the cause of failure for each call to OpenFileSystem().

histogram.name: FileSystem.OpenFileSystemDetail
histogram.summary: The result of opening a filesystem.

histogram.name: FileSystem.OpenFileSystemDetailNonthrottled
histogram.summary: The result of opening a non-throttled filesystem.

histogram.name: FileSystem.OriginDatabaseInit
histogram.summary: The result of FileSystemOriginDatabase initialization.

histogram.name: FileSystem.OriginFailedCanCommitURL
histogram.summary: Logged when we fail the permission validation of the filesystem URL origin, from ChildProcessSecurityPolicy::HasPermissionsForFileSystemFile. This is not expected to happen in normal operation.

histogram.name: FileSystem.PersistentOriginsCount
histogram.summary: Number of origins that have persistent filesystem. Measured when the Quota system queries the filesystem subsystem about its entire usage, which usually happens when one of the storage subsystem methods is called for the first time.

histogram.name: FileSystem.TemporaryOriginsCount
histogram.summary: Number of origins that have temporary filesystem. Measured when the Quota system queries the filesystem subsystem about its entire usage, which usually happens when one of the storage subsystem methods is called for the first time.

histogram.name: Fingerprint.Reset.ResetContextMode
histogram.summary: The mode FPMCU was in when we reset context.

histogram.name: Fingerprint.SetContext.SetContextMode
histogram.summary: The mode FPMCU was in when we set its context.

histogram.name: Fingerprint.SetContext.Success
histogram.summary: Whether setting FPMCU mode succeeded.

histogram.name: Fingerprint.Unlock.AttemptsCountBeforeSuccess
histogram.summary: Counts the number of fingerprint attempts until successful screen unlock.

histogram.name: Fingerprint.Unlock.AuthSuccessful
histogram.summary: Counts the number of times that the fingerprint match successfully vs. rejected.

histogram.name: Fingerprint.Unlock.EnrolledFingerCount
histogram.summary: Counts the number of fingers enrolled by the user.

histogram.name: Fingerprint.Unlock.Match.Duration.Capture
histogram.summary: Measures the time it took to capture the fingerprint image in the 'match' case.

histogram.name: Fingerprint.Unlock.Match.Duration.Matcher
histogram.summary: Measures the time it took to run matcher in the 'match' case.

histogram.name: Fingerprint.Unlock.Match.Duration.Overall
histogram.summary: Measures the time it took between the detection of a finger and the 'match' event being sent to the AP.

histogram.name: Fingerprint.Unlock.Match.PositiveMatchSecretCorrect
histogram.summary: Whether the hash of the positive match secret read from FPMCU matches the record.

histogram.name: Fingerprint.Unlock.MatchIgnoredDueToPowerButtonPress
histogram.summary: Whether fingerprint touch was ignored due to parallel power button press.

histogram.name: Fingerprint.Unlock.MigrationForPositiveMatchSecretResult
histogram.summary: Whether migrating a record to positive match secret succeeded.

histogram.name: Fingerprint.Unlock.NoMatch.Duration.Capture
histogram.summary: Measures the time it took to capture the fingerprint image in the 'no-match' case.

histogram.name: Fingerprint.Unlock.NoMatch.Duration.Matcher
histogram.summary: Measures the time it took to run the matcher in the 'no-match' case.

histogram.name: Fingerprint.Unlock.NoMatch.Duration.Overall
histogram.summary: Measures the time it took between the detection of a finger and the 'no-match' event being sent to the AP.

histogram.name: Fingerprint.Unlock.ReadPositiveMatchSecret.Success
histogram.summary: Whether successfully read positive match secret from FPMCU when needed.

histogram.name: Fingerprint.Unlock.RecordFormatVersion
histogram.summary: Format version of a fingerprint template record read from storage.

histogram.name: Fingerprint.UnlockEnabled
histogram.summary: Track whether fingerprint is enabled to unlock the screen, when the user logs in.

histogram.name: Fingerprint.Updater.NoUpdate.Duration.Overall
histogram.summary: Measures the total time it takes to run the fingerprint firmware updater when an update was not necessary.

histogram.name: Fingerprint.Updater.Reason
histogram.summary: Tracks the fingerprint firmware updater's reason(s) for re-flashing.

histogram.name: Fingerprint.Updater.Status
histogram.summary: Tracks the fingerprint firmware updater's overall status.

histogram.name: Fingerprint.Updater.Update.Duration.Overall
histogram.summary: Measures the total time it takes to run the fingerprint firmware updater when an update was necessary.

histogram.name: FirstRun.IOSFirebaseConfigured
histogram.summary: Track whether Firebase is enabled or not at app startup.

histogram.name: FirstRun.LaunchSource
histogram.summary: Counts the number of times First Run experience was triggered by another application launching Chrome. This metric measures the effectiveness of Smart App Banner promotions on installation of Chrome.

histogram.name: FirstRun.NewUserExperience.EmailInterstitialInteraction
histogram.summary: Records when a user interacted with the email interstitial of the onboarding process. We record that the page was seen and what action the user took.

histogram.name: FirstRun.NewUserExperience.EmailProvidersInteraction
histogram.summary: Records when a user interacted with the Email Providers new user experience. We record the number of times the page is seen, how they interacted with the page (i.e. interacting with presented options and button clicks), and through what method they leave the page (i.e. confirm, cancel, neither). The new user experience should only be shown once to new profiles. Hence, the leaving method should be recorded at most once.

histogram.name: FirstRun.NewUserExperience.EmailProvidersSelection
histogram.summary: Records what email provider was selected as part of the Email Providers New User Experience. Histogram is only recorded when a user confirms adding an email provider.

histogram.name: FirstRun.NewUserExperience.GoogleAppsInteraction
histogram.summary: Records when a user interacted with the Google Apps new user experience. We record both accept and reject but are not recording when a user ignored the prompt completely. Users can ignore this prompt by closing the tab or navigating away, we record when the prompt is shown.

histogram.name: FirstRun.NewUserExperience.GoogleAppsSelection
histogram.summary: Records what Google apps were selected as part of the Google Apps New User Experience. Histogram is only recorded when a user accepts the prompt to add Google Apps.

histogram.name: FirstRun.NewUserExperience.LandingPageInteraction
histogram.summary: Records when a user interacted with the landing page of the onboarding process. We record that the page was seen and what action the user took.

histogram.name: FirstRun.NewUserExperience.NtpBackgroundInteraction
histogram.summary: Records when a user interacts with the NTP Backgrounds new user experience. We record the number of times the page is seen, how they interacted with the page (i.e. interacted with the options and buttons), and through what method they leave the page (i.e, confirm, cancel, neither). The new user experience should only be shown once to new profiles.

histogram.name: FirstRun.NewUserExperience.NtpBackgroundLoadTime
histogram.summary: Records the time taken to load a NTP background image for preview. This is recorded when a user clicks on a background to preview and the full-size version of the background finishes loading.

histogram.name: FirstRun.NewUserExperience.SetAsDefaultInteraction
histogram.summary: Records when a user interacted with the set default page of the onboarding process. We record that the page was seen and what action the user took.

histogram.name: FirstRun.NewUserExperience.SignInInterstitialInteraction
histogram.summary: Records when a user interacted with the sign in interstitial of the onboarding process. We record that the page was seen and what action the user took.

histogram.name: FirstRun.Sentinel.Created
histogram.summary: Result of sentinel file has been written.

histogram.name: FirstRun.Sentinel.CreatedFileError
histogram.summary: File error when the sentinel file was not written.

histogram.name: FirstRun.SignIn
histogram.summary: Result of the sign in flow during first run on iOS.

histogram.name: FirstUserAction.BackgroundTime
histogram.summary:
WARNING: The list of actions used to identify new tasks hasn't been attentively updated for several years (as of 2017). Some actions that should likely be considered new tasks are probably missing. Take this histogram with a grain of salt.
The amount of time (in seconds) that the app was in the background before the user started it

histogram.name: FirstUserAction.BackgroundTime.MainIntent
histogram.summary: The amount of time (in minutes) that the app was in the background before the user started it (via an ACTION_MAIN intent) and performed a user action that describes the intent of resuming the app. Only recorded if the action took place within the first 10 seconds of starting Chrome. The actions are further described in the FirstUserActionTypes suffix.

histogram.name: FirstUserAction.HandsetUserActionType
histogram.summary:
WARNING: The list of actions used to identify new tasks hasn't been attentively updated for several years (as of 2017). Some actions that should likely be considered new tasks are probably missing. Take this histogram with a grain of salt.
On handsets, the type of the first user action performed after the app was started or unbackgrounded.

histogram.name: FirstUserAction.TabletUserActionType
histogram.summary:
WARNING: The list of actions used to identify new tasks hasn't been attentively updated for several years (as of 2017). Some actions that should likely be considered new tasks are probably missing. Take this histogram with a grain of salt.
On tablets, the type of the first user action performed after the app was started or unbackgrounded.

histogram.name: FontFallback.GetLinkedFonts.CacheMissTiming
histogram.summary: Time to retrieve the fallback fonts on the system when not found in cache.

histogram.name: FontFallback.GetLinkedFonts.FontCount
histogram.summary: The amount of font returned for a given font family.

histogram.name: FontFallback.GetLinkedFonts.Timing
histogram.summary: Time to retrieve the fallback fonts on the system.

histogram.name: ForceDiceMigration.RevokeTokenAction
histogram.summary: Tracks revoke actions taken by the account reconcilior to force Dice migration. To enforce consistency, the reconcilior may invalidate the primary account or revoke one or more secondary account tokens, if no equivalent valid gaia cookie exist for those accounts.

histogram.name: Gaia.AuthFetcher.ListAccounts.NetErrorCodes
histogram.summary: Reports the network error code for requests to the ListAccounts Gaia endpoint.

histogram.name: Gaia.AuthFetcher.ListAccounts.NetErrorCodes.Retry
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019-09. Enough data has been collected for investigation of https://crbug.com/876306
histogram.summary: Reports the network error code for requests to the ListAccounts Gaia endpoint. There are suffixes for individual tries (0 is the initial request, and 1-8 are retries).

histogram.name: Gaia.AuthFetcher.ListAccounts.ProcessUptime.Error
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019-09. Enough data has been collected for investigation of https://crbug.com/876306
histogram.summary: Reported when a request to the ListAccounts Gaia endpoint fails to complete for network reasons (i.e. when the error is not OK). This histogram can be used in conjunction with "Gaia.AuthFetcher.ListAccounts.ProcessUptime.Success" in order to compute the failure rate of ListAccounts depending on the process uptime (measured as a base::TimeDelta between the start of the process and the start of the request).

histogram.name: Gaia.AuthFetcher.ListAccounts.ProcessUptime.Success
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019-09. Enough data has been collected for investigation of https://crbug.com/876306
histogram.summary: Reported when a request to the ListAccounts Gaia endpoint completes without network error (i.e. when the network error is OK). This histogram can be used in conjunction with "Gaia.AuthFetcher.ListAccounts.ProcessUptime.Error" in order to compute the failure rate of ListAccounts depending on the process uptime (measured as a base::TimeDelta between the start of the process and the start of the request).

histogram.name: Gaia.AuthFetcher.ListAccounts.SystemUptime.Error
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019-09. Enough data has been collected for investigation of https://crbug.com/876306
histogram.summary: Reported when a request to the ListAccounts Gaia endpoint fails to complete for network reasons (i.e. when the network error is not OK). This histogram can be used in conjunction with "Gaia.AuthFetcher.ListAccounts.SystemUptime.Success" in order to compute the failure rate of ListAccounts depending on the system uptime (measured as a base::TimeDelta between the start of the system and the start of the request).

histogram.name: Gaia.AuthFetcher.ListAccounts.SystemUptime.Success
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019-09. Enough data has been collected for investigation of https://crbug.com/876306
histogram.summary: Reported when a request to the ListAccounts Gaia endpoint completes without network error (i.e. when the network error is OK). This histogram can be used in conjunction with "Gaia.AuthFetcher.ListAccounts.SystemUptime.Error" in order to compute the failure rate of ListAccounts depending on the system uptime (measured as a base::TimeDelta between the start of the system and the start of the request).

histogram.name: Gaia.BadRequestTypeForOAuth2AccessToken
histogram.summary: Detailed error type in bad requests for OAuth2 refresh tokens.

histogram.name: Gaia.ResponseCodesForOAuth2AccessToken
histogram.summary: URLFetcher response codes for OAuth2AccessToken. Positive values are HTTP status codes, negative values are network error codes.

histogram.name: Gaia.SuccessCountBeforeBadRequestForOAuth2AccessToken
histogram.summary: Number of successful requests preceding a bad request for an OAuth2 refresh token.

histogram.name: Gamepad.KnownGamepadConnected
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 01/2018. Use Gamepad.KnownGamepadConnectedWithId instead.
histogram.summary: Records an enumeration value that can be used to identify the connected device from a list of known gamepads. Reported when a device with a vendor and product ID matching a known gaming input device is detected during Gamepad API enumeration.

histogram.name: Gamepad.KnownGamepadConnectedWithId
histogram.summary: Records an integer value that can be used to identify a connected gamepad device. The value is constructed from the 16-bit vendor ID in the high-order bytes and the 16-bit product ID in the low-order bytes, then cast to a 32-bit signed integer. The GamepadVendorProduct enum lists IDs for several popular gamepads but is incomplete. To identify a device not included in the enum, convert back to the vendor/product IDs and look up the human-friendly product name in go/gamepad-ids. This histogram records a new entry when a device with a vendor and product ID matching a known gaming input device is detected during Gamepad API enumeration.

histogram.name: Gamepad.UnknownGamepadConnected
histogram.summary: Records an enumeration value identifying the active data fetcher when a fetcher enumerates a device that is not on the list of known gamepads, but is recognized as a gamepad by the fetcher.

histogram.name: GCM.AccountMappingMessageReceived
histogram.summary: Records that a message has been received from a delivery channel powered by the GCM account mappings.

histogram.name: GCM.AndroidGcmReceiverError
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 01/2016. The error has been fixed by GCM. (crbug/580367)
histogram.summary: Result of a message received by the GcmReceiver on Android.

histogram.name: GCM.APICallUnregister
histogram.summary: Number of times when gcm.unregister API is called.

histogram.name: GCM.CheckinCompleteTime
histogram.summary: Length of time taken to complete a GCM checkin request successfully. If the checkin is retried multiple times, the length of time is counted for the last successful retry.

histogram.name: GCM.CheckinRequestStatus
histogram.summary: Status code of the outcome of a GCM checkin request.

histogram.name: GCM.CheckinRequestStatusNetError
histogram.summary: Network error code for a GCM checkin request that failed because of a network error.

histogram.name: GCM.CheckinRetryCount
histogram.summary: Number of retries before a GCM checkin succeeds.

histogram.name: GCM.ConnectedViaProxy
histogram.summary: Whether the GCM connection was made via a proxy or not.

histogram.name: GCM.ConnectionDisconnectErrorCode
histogram.summary: URL response and error codes from GCM disconnect events.

histogram.name: GCM.ConnectionEndpoint
histogram.summary: Number of connections made to each specific MCS endpoint.

histogram.name: GCM.ConnectionFailureErrorCode
histogram.summary: URL response and error codes from GCM connection attempts.

histogram.name: GCM.ConnectionLatency
histogram.summary: The time between the initiation of a connection and the successful completion of it.

histogram.name: GCM.ConnectionResetReason
histogram.summary: Reasons for GCM connection resets.

histogram.name: GCM.ConnectionSuccessRate
histogram.summary: GCM connection success rate. Does not take into account login success. See GCM.ConnectionFailureErrorCode for a breakdown of connection failure reasons.

histogram.name: GCM.ConnectionUpTime
histogram.summary: Time (from login until reset) that a GCM connection was active.

histogram.name: GCM.Crypto.CreateKeySuccessRate
histogram.summary: Success rate of writing new keying material to the GCM key store.

histogram.name: GCM.Crypto.DecryptMessageResult
histogram.summary: Result of decryption on a received GCM message, which includes unencrypted messages, successfully decrypted messages and decryption failures.

histogram.name: GCM.Crypto.EncryptMessageResult
histogram.summary: Result of encryption of outgoing GCM message, which includes successfully encrypted messages and encryption failures.

histogram.name: GCM.Crypto.GCMDatabaseUpgradeResult
histogram.summary: Result of upgrading GCM Key Store Database from deprecated format to current format. We upgrade the GCM Key store when it first initialized, if it contains any EncryptionData object with a KeyPair in it, and then record whether the upgrade was successful in this metric.

histogram.name: GCM.Crypto.GetKeySuccessRate
histogram.summary: Success rate of reading keying material from the GCM key store.

histogram.name: GCM.Crypto.InitKeyStoreSuccessRate
histogram.summary: Success rate of initializing the LevelDB back-end of the GCM key store.

histogram.name: GCM.Crypto.LoadKeyStoreSuccessRate
histogram.summary: Success rate of loading existing keying material in the GCM key store.

histogram.name: GCM.Crypto.RemoveKeySuccessRate
histogram.summary: Success rate of removing keying material from the GCM key store.

histogram.name: GCM.Database.Open
histogram.summary: The result of a database open attempt by the GCM store.

histogram.name: GCM.DataMessageBurstReceivedInterval
histogram.summary: Interval between two successive received data message bursts.

histogram.name: GCM.DataMessageReceived
histogram.summary: Number of DATA_MESSAGE messages received from Google Cloud Messaging for which a matching registration exists. Recorded when processing the received message. Prior to M56 this also counted received DELETED_MESSAGES messages (whether or not a registration existed).

histogram.name: GCM.DataMessageReceivedHasCollapseKey
histogram.summary: Records whether a non-empty collapse_key token is set for each received DATA_MESSAGE message from Google Cloud Messaging for which a matching registration exists. Recorded while processing the received message.

histogram.name: GCM.DataMessageReceivedHasRegisteredApp
histogram.obsolete: No longer used starting with Chrome 61 as the check was removed.
histogram.summary: Records whether a matching registration was found for each received DATA_MESSAGE message from Google Cloud Messaging. Recorded while processing the received message. Prior to M56 this also counted received DELETED_MESSAGES messages.

histogram.name: GCM.DeletedMessagesReceived
histogram.summary: For every DELETED_MESSAGES message received from Google Cloud Messaging (whether or not a matching registration exists), this records the provided count of how many messages were simultaneously deleted, or zero if the count is unknown.

histogram.name: GCM.DeliveredToAppHandler
histogram.summary: Whether a received GCM message could be delivered to an app handler. Recorded for each received GCM message that didn't have encryption issues.

histogram.name: GCM.FirstReceivedDataMessageLatencyAfterConnection
histogram.summary: The time between the successful completion of the connection and the arrival of the first data message.

histogram.name: GCM.GetInstanceIDData.ClientStarted
histogram.summary: Reports the client-started state when the GCM driver requested a read of the instance id although the client was not ready. If != Success, the client is likely leaking a registration.

histogram.name: GCM.HeartbeatMissedDelta
histogram.summary: If a heartbeat was missed, the time delta by which it was missed (in system time, not ticks).

histogram.name: GCM.IgnoredWriteResult
histogram.summary: Result of the outcome of persisting a change in the GCMStore. This histogram is to meausure the frequency of such events and assess their severity. For example, failing to write when calling AddInstanceIDData() likely results in leaked FCM registrations.

histogram.name: GCM.IncomingSendErrors
histogram.summary: Number of SEND_ERROR messages received from Google Cloud Messaging. Recorded when processing the received message.

histogram.name: GCM.LoadStatus
histogram.summary: Status code of loading from the persistent GCM store.

histogram.name: GCM.LoadSucceeded
histogram.summary: Success indicates successfully loading an initialized persistent GCM store at startup time. Failure indicates a failure loading the store.

histogram.name: GCM.NumThrottledApps
histogram.summary: Number of applications hitting GCM per-app outstanding message limits at startup time.

histogram.name: GCM.NumUsers
histogram.summary: Number of GCM users associated with this client at startup time.

histogram.name: GCM.OutgoingMessageTTL
histogram.summary: Category of TTL specified in the outgoing message: 0, less than or equal to 1 minute, less than or equal to 1 hour and etc.

histogram.name: GCM.PendingConnectionEventsAtShutdown
histogram.summary: Number of connection events which have not been sent to GCM. This is recorded only at the time of shutdown to capture the events which are being lost due to shutdown. Because this is recorded at shutdown, this is not guaranteed to be complete and should be treated as a best effort record.

histogram.name: GCM.ReceivedDataMessageBurstSize
histogram.summary: Number of messages in a received GCM data message burst.

histogram.name: GCM.ReceivedDataMessageIntervalWithinBurst
histogram.summary: Interval between messages within a received GCM data message burst.

histogram.name: GCM.RegistrationCacheStatus
histogram.summary: Reports the status of cached GCM registration. This is recorded when a new registration request is considered on the GCM client.

histogram.name: GCM.RegistrationCompleteTime
histogram.summary: Length of time taken to complete a GCM registration request successfully. If the registration is retried multiple times, the length of time is counted for the last successful retry.

histogram.name: GCM.RegistrationRequest
histogram.summary: Number of registration requests sent to Google Cloud Messaging. Recorded immediately after the request has started.

histogram.name: GCM.RegistrationRequest.NetErrorCode
histogram.summary: Net error code from a GCM registration attempt. Recorded when URL loading completes.

histogram.name: GCM.RegistrationRequestStatus
histogram.summary: Status code of the outcome of a GCM registration request. The Unknown error case was split up in M56 (merged mid-beta to M55) to separate out the Response parsing failed, Internal server error, Quota exceeded, and Device has too many registrations cases.

histogram.name: GCM.RegistrationRetryCount
histogram.summary: Number of retries before a GCM registration succeeds.

histogram.name: GCM.RegistrationSenderIdCount
histogram.summary: Number of sender IDs specified in a registration request.

histogram.name: GCM.RemoveAccountMappingWhenLoading
histogram.summary: Number of times that account mapping entries are deleted from or kept in the GCM store database while loading them in memory. Used during the account ID migration from email to Gaia ID.

histogram.name: GCM.ResetStore
histogram.obsolete: Removed in M56 in favor of GCM.ResetStoreError
histogram.summary: Reports the problem encountered when resetting the GCM store.

histogram.name: GCM.ResetStoreError
histogram.summary: Reports the problem encountered when resetting the GCM store.

histogram.name: GCM.RestoredIncomingMessages
histogram.summary: Number of unacknowledged incoming messages restored from the persistent store at startup.

histogram.name: GCM.RestoredOutgoingMessages
histogram.summary: Number of pending outgoing messages restored from the persistent store at startup.

histogram.name: GCM.RestoredRegistrations
histogram.summary: Number of registrations restored from the persistent store at startup.

histogram.name: GCM.SendMessageStatus
histogram.summary: Result of sending an upstream message through Google Cloud Messaging. Recorded when the message has either been queued, sent, or has been dropped because it expired or an error happened.

histogram.name: GCM.SendWebPushMessageForbiddenBody
histogram.summary: Categorized response body when 403: Forbidden is received from sending web push messages. Recorded when received response after message has been sent. All platforms.

histogram.name: GCM.SendWebPushMessagePayloadSize
histogram.summary: Size of web push messages payload. Recorded right before the message is sent.

histogram.name: GCM.SendWebPushMessageResult
histogram.summary: Result of sending web push messages. Recorded when the message has either been sent, or has been dropped because an error happened.

histogram.name: GCM.SendWebPushMessageStatusCode
histogram.summary: Result code from sending web push messages. Logs net::Error if it's not net::OK. Logs net::OK if response header is not present. Otherwise, logs HTTP status code returned. Recorded when received response after message has been sent.

histogram.name: GCM.StoreDestroySucceeded
histogram.summary: Success indicates successfully destroying the GCM persistent store. Failure indicates a failure destroying the persistence store. GCM store will be destroyed when the profile has been signed out.

histogram.name: GCM.StoreSizeKB
histogram.summary: Size of the GCM persistent store in kilobytes at startup.

histogram.name: GCM.StoreUpdateSucceeded
histogram.summary: Success indicates successfully updating the GCM persistent store on message update. Failure indicates a failure updating the persistence store.

histogram.name: GCM.SuspendTime
histogram.summary: Time elapsed from machine suspend until resume.

histogram.name: GCM.UnregistrationCompleteTime
histogram.summary: Length of time taken to complete a GCM unregistration request successfully. If the unregistration is retried multiple times, the length of time is counted for the last successful retry.

histogram.name: GCM.UnregistrationRequest
histogram.summary: Number of unregistration requests sent to Google Cloud Messaging. Recorded immediately after the request has started.

histogram.name: GCM.UnregistrationRequestStatus
histogram.summary: Status code of the outcome of a GCM unregistration request.

histogram.name: GCM.UnregistrationRetryCount
histogram.summary: Number of retries before a GCM unregistration succeeds.

histogram.name: GCM.UserSignedIn
histogram.summary: Indicates whether the user was signed in when GCM started up.

histogram.name: GCM.WebPushReceived.DeviceState
histogram.summary: Device state when receiving a web push message. Indicates if the message was sent with a high priority and if the device was in idle mode. Recorded when a web push message got received.

histogram.name: GCMInvalidations.IncomingMessageStatus
histogram.summary: Status of parsing incoming invalidations message from GCM channel.

histogram.name: GCMInvalidations.OutgoingMessageStatus
histogram.summary: Status of sending outgoing invalidations message through GCM.

histogram.name: GData.AuthSuccess
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Result of the authentication for Drive.

histogram.name: GData.EntireFeedLoadTime
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Time spent to load the entire file system information from the server

histogram.name: GData.EntryKind
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Provides breakdown of specific formats for hosted documents. Recorded when feed is loaded from the server.

histogram.name: GData.InitialFeedLoadTime
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Time spent to load the initial part of the file system information from the server

histogram.name: GData.NumberOfHostedDocuments
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Number of hosted documents (spreadsheets etc.) on Drive. Logged when Drive is first accessed.

histogram.name: GData.NumberOfRegularFiles
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Number of regualr files on Drive. Logged when Drive is first accessed.

histogram.name: GData.NumberOfTotalFiles
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Number of total files (regualr files + hosted documents) on Drive. Logged when Drive is first accessed.

histogram.name: Geolocation.AuthorizationActionExistingUser
histogram.summary: Records the action performed by an existing user when prompted to authorize the use of location by Chrome.

histogram.name: Geolocation.AuthorizationActionNewUser
histogram.summary: Records the action performed by a new user when prompted to authorize the use of location by Chrome.

histogram.name: Geolocation.GeolocationDispatcherHostImpl.EnableHighAccuracy
histogram.obsolete: Removed 01/2020 because the metric is not needed anymore. See https://crbug.com/1036861 for discussion on this metric.
histogram.summary: Whether high accuracy geolocation information was requested.

histogram.name: Geolocation.Header.LocationAge
histogram.summary: For Geolocation requests from the omnibox on Android, the time since the location was most recently acquired. If no location has been acquired, the maximum bucket is incremented.

histogram.name: Geolocation.Header.PermissionState
histogram.summary: Counts Geolocation requests for omnibox searches sliced by various permission combinations. The location mode abbreviations indicate one of: High Accuracy, Battery Saving, GPS On or Location Mode Off.

histogram.name: Geolocation.Header.TimeListening
histogram.summary: For Geolocation requests from the omnibox on Android, the time since the location was first acquired. If no location has been acquired, the minimum bucket is incremented.

histogram.name: Geolocation.HeaderSentOrNot
histogram.summary: Reason why the X-Geo header was or was not included in a Google search from the omnibox.

histogram.name: Geolocation.InfoBarDelegate.Event
histogram.summary: Events in GeolocationInfoBarDelegate.

histogram.name: Geolocation.InfoBarDelegateAndroid.Event
histogram.summary: Events in GeolocationInfoBarDelegateAndroid.

histogram.name: Geolocation.LocationUpdate.ErrorCode
histogram.summary: Error code for the geoposition sent to the renderers.

histogram.name: Geolocation.NetworkLocationRequest.AccessPoints
histogram.summary: The number of WiFi access points used to determine geolocation.

histogram.name: Geolocation.NetworkLocationRequest.Event
histogram.summary: Events in NetworkLocationRequest.

histogram.name: Geolocation.NetworkLocationRequest.ResponseCode
histogram.summary: Http response codes in NetworkLocationRequest.

histogram.name: Geolocation.PositionCache.CacheHit
histogram.summary: For each query into the position cache used by the network location provider, records whether a position was returned from the cache.

histogram.name: Geolocation.PositionCache.CacheSize
histogram.summary: For each query into the position cache used by the network location provider, records the number of items in the cache.

histogram.name: Geolocation.SettingsDialog.AcceptEvent
histogram.summary: Records the backoff level when the Location Settings Dialog is accepted by the user.

histogram.name: Geolocation.SettingsDialog.DenyEvent
histogram.summary: Records the backoff level when the Location Settings Dialog is rejected by the user.

histogram.name: Geolocation.SettingsDialog.ShowEvent
