See History.InitializationFailureStep for which particular step of history initialization failed.

histogram.name: History.ClearBrowsingData.Duration.FilteredDeletion
histogram.summary: The time that passed while performing a browsing data deletion with an origin filter. The deletion might be for all time or specific time range.

histogram.name: History.ClearBrowsingData.Duration.FullDeletion
histogram.summary: The time that passed while performing a full browsing data deletion.

histogram.name: History.ClearBrowsingData.Duration.OriginDeletion
histogram.summary: The time that passed while performing a browsing data deletion for a set of origins.

histogram.name: History.ClearBrowsingData.Duration.PartialDeletion
histogram.obsolete: Removed May 2020, replaced with more detailed histograms: History.ClearBrowsingData.Duration.TimeRangeDeletion, History.ClearBrowsingData.Duration.OriginDeletion, History.ClearBrowsingData.Duration.FilteredDeletion.
histogram.summary: The time that passed while performing a browsing data deletion for a restricted amount of time (e.g. "Last hour") or with an origin filter.

histogram.name: History.ClearBrowsingData.Duration.SlowTasks180s
histogram.summary: Record tasks from BrowsingDataRemoverImpl that took more than 180s to finish.

histogram.name: History.ClearBrowsingData.Duration.SlowTasks180sChrome
histogram.summary: Record tasks from ChromeBrowsingDataRemoverDelegate that took more than 180s to finish.

histogram.name: History.ClearBrowsingData.Duration.TimeRangeDeletion
histogram.summary: The time that passed while performing a browsing data deletion for a restricted amount of time (e.g. "Last hour") without an origin filter.

histogram.name: History.ClearBrowsingData.HistoryNoticeShownInFooterWhenUpdated
histogram.summary: Whether a notice about other forms of browsing history was shown in the footer of the Clear Browsing Data dialog. This is recorded every time the dialog is opened.

histogram.name: History.ClearBrowsingData.ImportantDeselectedNum
histogram.summary: Recorded when the user presses the 'clear' button when presented with the list of important sites they might want to exclude from clearing browsing data. We record the number of sites the user deselected, or protected, from clearing. The default has all sites selected.

histogram.name: History.ClearBrowsingData.ImportantDeselectedPercent
histogram.summary: Recorded when the user presses the 'clear' button when presented with the list of important sites they might want to exclude from clearing browsing data. We record the percent of sites the user deselected, or protected, from clearing. The default has all sites selected, which is 0%. Note: To reduce the number of empty buckets, the percent is divided by 5.

histogram.name: History.ClearBrowsingData.ImportantDialogShown
histogram.summary: Recorded when the user presses the 'clear' button in the clear browsing dialog. We record 'true' if the important sites dialog is shown.

histogram.name: History.ClearBrowsingData.InstalledAppExcluded
histogram.summary: Recorded when the user presses the 'clear' button when presented with the list of installed apps they might want to exclude from clearing browsing data. This histogram will be true if the user choose to exclude at least one installed app from clearing. The default has all apps selected, in which case this enum would be false.

histogram.name: History.ClearBrowsingData.InstalledDeselectedNum
histogram.summary: Recorded when the user presses the 'clear' button when presented with the list of installed apps they might want to exclude from clearing browsing data. We record the number of apps the user deselected, or protected, from clearing. The default has all apps selected.

histogram.name: History.ClearBrowsingData.InstalledDeselectedPercent
histogram.summary: Recorded when the user presses the 'clear' button when presented with the list of installed apps they might want to exclude from clearing browsing data. We record the percent of apps the user deselected, or protected, from clearing. The default has all apps selected, which is 0%.

histogram.name: History.ClearBrowsingData.NumImportant
histogram.summary: Recorded when we successfuly fetched important sites in the clear browsing data screen. This is the number of sites that we think are important to the user.

histogram.name: History.ClearBrowsingData.NumInstalled
histogram.summary: Recorded when we successfuly fetched installed apps in the clear browsing data screen. This is the number of apps that we think are important to the user and allow the users to exclude from clearing browsing data.

histogram.name: History.ClearBrowsingData.PasswordsDeletion.AdditionalDatatypesCount
histogram.summary: Recorded when the user deletes their passwords in the ClearBrowsingData dialog. Counts how many other data types were selected for deletion.

histogram.name: History.ClearBrowsingData.ShownHistoryNoticeAfterClearing
histogram.summary: Whether a dialog with a notice about other forms of browsing history was shown after the user deleted data from the Clear Browsing Data dialog.

histogram.name: History.ClearBrowsingData.SpannableStringAppliedCorrectly
histogram.obsolete: Removed 02/2020 since the histogram indicators were stable.
histogram.summary: Track whether the SpannableString is applied correctly in ClearBrowsingDataCheckbox.java. (crbug.com/783866)

histogram.name: History.ClearBrowsingData.TaskQueueAtShutdown
histogram.summary: The number of tasks that are in the task queue when the BrowsingDataRemover is destroyed.

histogram.name: History.ClearBrowsingData.TimeSpentInDialog
histogram.summary: Time spent in Clear Browsing Data dialog. From opening the dialog until data is cleared. This does not record users who exit the dialog without clearing.

histogram.name: History.ClearBrowsingData.UserDeletedCookieOrCache
histogram.summary: Recorded when the user deletes their browsing data. Shows whether cookies and/or cache were among the selected data types.

histogram.name: History.ClearBrowsingData.UserDeletedCookieOrCacheFromDialog
histogram.summary: Recorded when the user deletes their browsing data manually from the ClearBrowsingData dialog. Shows whether cookies and/or cache were among the selected data types.

histogram.name: History.ClearBrowsingData.UserDeletedFromTab
histogram.summary: Recorded when the user deletes their browsing data. Shows which tab was used to perform the deletion.

histogram.name: History.DatabaseAdvancedMetricsTime
histogram.summary: Time spent on advanced History DB metrics calculation (weekly and monthly URL, host, and category counts). Advanced metrics are logged on initialization of the History DB on 0.3% of starts.

histogram.name: History.DatabaseBasicMetricsTime
histogram.summary: Time spent on basic History DB metrics calculation (file size and table counts). Basic metrics are logged on initialization of the History DB on 1% of starts.

histogram.name: History.DatabaseFileMB
histogram.summary: Size of History DB file. Duplicate of Profile.HistorySize and Sqlite.SizeKB.History. Logged on initialization of the History DB on 1% of starts.

histogram.name: History.DatabaseMonthlyHostCountTime
histogram.summary: Time spent on computing the monthly count of hosts visited. Reported every time computation is performed, which in turn is done approximately weekly.

histogram.name: History.DeleteFTSIndexDatabases
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Count of "History Index *" databases deleted. These databases stored the full-text-search data for history, which was removed at r213442, this histogram tracks cleanup.

histogram.name: History.DomainCount1Day
histogram.summary:
Number of unique domains visited by the user within a calendar day in user's local timezone. For each profile, reported at profile open (which usually happens at startup) for each unreported day (up to 7 days) prior to the current date, and also reported once every 24 hours thereafter for the latest unreported day, while the profile remains open. If no domains are visited in a given day, a count of 0 will be reported for that day.
Note: for users syncing between multiple devices, this count may include some URLs/domains that weren't visited on this device. In other words, some domains may be counted for multiple client_ids even though they were only visited once, on one device. However, at this time (Feb 2020), only URLs navigated to via the omnibox ("typed URLs") are synced, not all visited URLs. There are no plans to change this behavior. As such, because the mis-counting only occurs for users who are syncing across multiple devices and only for a subset of visited URLs, the effect of double-counting is likely to be small.

histogram.name: History.DomainCount28Day
histogram.summary:
Number of unique domains visited by the user within a 28 calendar day period in user's local timezone. For each profile, reported at profile open (which usually happens at startup) for each unreported 28-day period (up to 7 periods) ending on the current day, current day - 1, current day - 2, etc. Also reported once every 24 hours thereafter for the latest unreported period, while the profile remains open. If no domains are visited during a 28-day period, a count of 0 will be reported for that period.
Note: for users syncing between multiple devices, this count may include some URLs/domains that weren't visited on this device. In other words, some domains may be counted for multiple client_ids even though they were only visited once, on one device. However, at this time (Feb 2020), only URLs navigated to via the omnibox ("typed URLs") are synced, not all visited URLs. There are no plans to change this behavior. As such, because the mis-counting only occurs for users who are syncing across multiple devices and only for a subset of visited URLs, the effect of double-counting is likely to be small.

histogram.name: History.DomainCount7Day
histogram.summary:
Number of unique domains visited by the user within a 7 calendar day period in user's local timezone. For each profile, reported at profile open (which usually happens at startup) for each unreported 7-day period (up to 7 periods) ending on the current day, current day - 1, current day - 2, etc. Also reported once every 24 hours thereafter for the latest unreported period, while the profile remains open. If no domains are visited during a 7-day period, a count 0 will be reported for that period.
Note: for users syncing between multiple devices, this count may include some URLs/domains that weren't visited on this device. In other words, some domains may be counted for multiple client_ids even though they were only visited once, on one device. However, at this time (Feb 2020), only URLs navigated to via the omnibox ("typed URLs") are synced, not all visited URLs. There are no plans to change this behavior. As such, because the mis-counting only occurs for users who are syncing across multiple devices and only for a subset of visited URLs, the effect of double-counting is likely to be small.

histogram.name: History.DomainCountQueryTime
histogram.summary: Time spent on a scheduled computation task of domain visit counts. Each such task computes a number of (at least 1 and up to 7) sets of domain counts, where each set includes 3 results, for a 1-day, 7-day and 28-day period respectively. Note that for each computation task, only one query time is reported which covers the entire duration of the task. A task is scheduled once at startup and every 24 hours thereafter while the browser remains open.

histogram.name: History.ExpireVisits.GetRedirectsDurationPercentage
histogram.summary: The percentage of time taken in ExpireHistoryBackend::ExpireVisits for calculating the redirect parent.

histogram.name: History.ExpireVisits.TotalDuration
histogram.summary: The time taken to expire a list of visits. This is not scaled to the number of visits deleted, but gives an idea of how long a single request to ExpireHistoryBackend::ExpireVisits takes.

histogram.name: History.FaviconDatabaseAdvancedMetricsTime
histogram.summary: The wall-clock time taken to gather favicon database metrics. Metrics are logged on initialization of the Favicon DB on 1% of starts.

histogram.name: History.FaviconDatabaseSizeMB
histogram.summary: Size of the Favicons database in MB. This is calculated as the number of pages consumed by the database multiplied by the page size. Logged on initialization of the Favicon DB on 1% of starts.

histogram.name: History.FaviconsRecoveredPercentage
histogram.obsolete: No longer tracked as of March 2017.
histogram.summary: Size of the recovered Favicons database relative to the original corrupt database. Recovery is VACUUM-like, so the resulting database should always be smaller. Substantial 100% results would indicate empty databases being recovered, substantial low% results would indicate very little data being recovered.

histogram.name: History.FaviconsRecoveredRowsFaviconBitmaps
histogram.obsolete: No longer tracked as of March 2017.
histogram.summary: Rows recovered from [favicon_bitmaps] table in Favicons recovery.

histogram.name: History.FaviconsRecoveredRowsFavicons
histogram.obsolete: No longer tracked as of March 2017.
histogram.summary: Rows recovered from [favicons] table in Favicons recovery.

histogram.name: History.FaviconsRecoveredRowsIconMapping
histogram.obsolete: No longer tracked as of March 2017.
histogram.summary: Rows recovered from [icon_mapping] table in Favicons recovery.

histogram.name: History.FaviconsRecovery
histogram.obsolete: No longer tracked as of March 2017.
histogram.summary: Track results of SQLite database recovery code in thumbnail_database.cc.

histogram.name: History.FirstSetTopSitesDeltaSize
histogram.summary: The count of differences between cached top sites and up-to-date top sites when function TopSitesImpl::SetTopSites is firstly called at startup.

histogram.name: History.FirstUpdateTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time for function history::TopSitesBackend::UpdateTopSitesOnDBThread to execute when this function is called during startup. Excludes the case where local TopSitesDatabase db_ is unavailable, i.e. where the update doesn't really happen.

histogram.name: History.GetFavIconFromDB
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Time to check the thumbnail database for an url's favicon.

histogram.name: History.HistoryPageView
histogram.summary: The page of the History page shown to the user. Logged each time the user loads the History page or switches pages.

histogram.name: History.HistoryServiceInitTime
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 02/2020. The vast majority of the time this is under 50ms.
histogram.summary: The amount of time that elapsed during HistoryService::Init.

histogram.name: History.InitializationFailureStep
histogram.summary: The phase of history initialization that failed. This histogram is only logged on database initialization failure. History.AttemptedToFixProfileError will tell how often initialization fails overall.

histogram.name: History.InitTime
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: TBD

histogram.name: History.InMemoryDBItemCount
histogram.summary: The number of typed URLs in the history database that are read into the "in memory" history database. This in memory copy of the typed URLs is used for synchronous inline autocomplete on the UI thread, while the larger slower service is run off of disk on a background thread. Recorded on profile open.

histogram.name: History.InMemoryDBKeywordTermsCount
histogram.summary: Number of items in in-memory keyword_search_terms table. Recorded on profile open.

histogram.name: History.InMemoryDBKeywordTermsPopulate
histogram.summary: Time to load in-memory keyword_search_terms table from disk. Recorded on profile open.

histogram.name: History.InMemoryDBKeywordURLItemCount
histogram.summary: Number of items in in-memory urls table. Recorded on profile open.

histogram.name: History.InMemoryDBKeywordURLPopulate
histogram.summary: Time to load in-memory urls table from disk urls table. Recorded on profile open.

histogram.name: History.InMemoryDBPopulate
histogram.summary: The time it takes to read the typed URLs from the main history database into the "in memory" history database. This in memory copy of the typed URLs is used for synchronous inline autocomplete on the UI thread, while the larger slower service is run off of disk on a background thread. Recorded on profile open.

histogram.name: History.InMemoryTypedUrlVisitCount
histogram.obsolete: No longer needed. Removed Aug 2019 as part of crbug.com/969934 cleanup.
histogram.summary: The sum of the visit_count fields for all URLs in the "in memory" history database. This corresponds to the number of times the user has visited the typed URLs in the last 30 days (excluding page reloads, since the history database does not increment the visit_count in that case). Recorded on profile open.

histogram.name: History.InMemoryURLCacheSize
histogram.summary: Size of on-disk cache for in-memory url index. Recorded on profile open when restoring from a cache file.

histogram.name: History.InMemoryURLChars
histogram.summary: Number of items in the in-memory url index char_word_map_. Recorded on profile open when restoring from a cache file and again shortly after profile open when rebuilding the in-memory url index from history.

histogram.name: History.InMemoryURLHistoryItems
histogram.summary: Number of history items in the in-memory url index. Recorded on profile open when restoring from a cache file and again shortly after profile open when rebuilding the in-memory url index from history.

histogram.name: History.InMemoryURLIndexingTime
histogram.summary: Time to rebuild in-memory url index from main history database. Recorded once shortly after profile open.

histogram.name: History.InMemoryURLIndexRestoreCacheTime
histogram.summary: Time to restore the in-memory url index from disk cache. Recorded on profile open.

histogram.name: History.InMemoryURLIndexSaveCacheTime
histogram.summary: Time to write in-memory url index to disk cache. Recorded once shortly after profile open (after rebuilding the in-memory url index from history) and again upon (clean) shutdown.

histogram.name: History.InMemoryURLWords
histogram.summary: Number of items in in-memory url index word_map_. Recorded on profile open when restoring from a cache file and again shortly after profile open when rebuilding the in-memory url index from history.

histogram.name: History.MigrateFailureFromVersion
histogram.summary: History database version from which history migration failed. If there are higher than normal migration failures, this histogram will indicate which migration step failed.

histogram.name: History.MonthlyHostCount
histogram.summary: Unique hostnames in History database urls table with last-visit times in the last 30 days. Metrics are logged on initialization of the History DB on 0.3% of starts. See also ExploreSites.MonthlyHostCount metric which is similar but is reported on a elapsed time basis to help avoid bias toward more active users.

histogram.name: History.MonthlyURLCount
histogram.summary: Unique URLs in History database urls table with last-visit times in the last 30 days. Metrics are logged on initialization of the History DB on 0.3% of starts.

histogram.name: History.MonthlyVisitCount
histogram.summary: Number of rows in History visits table with visit times in the last 30 days. Metrics are logged on initialization of the History DB on 1% of starts.

histogram.name: History.NumFaviconBitmapsInDB
histogram.summary: The total number of favicon bitmaps (of all sizes) cached in a user's Favicon database. A given favicon URL may be associated with multiple bitmaps (of different sizes). Logged on initialization of the Favicon DB on 1% of starts.

histogram.name: History.NumFaviconMappingsInDB
histogram.summary: The number of page URL (e.g., http://www.google.com) to favicon URL (e.g., http://www.google.com/favicon.ico) mappings stored in a user's Favicon database. Logged on initialization of the Favicon DB on 1% of starts.

histogram.name: History.NumFaviconsInDB
histogram.summary: The total number of favicon URLs (e.g. http://www.google.com/favicon.ico) tracked in a user's Favicon database. This metric tracks knowledge of a favicon URL not whether there are cached bitmaps for that favicon URL. See History.NumFaviconBitmapsInDB for that. Logged on initialization of the Favicon DB on 1% of starts.

histogram.name: History.NumLargeFaviconBitmapsInDB
histogram.summary: The total number of large (>= 64 pixels wide) favicons being tracked in a user's Favicon database. This is a subset of History.NumFaviconBitmapsInDB, which includes all sizes in its count. Logged on initialization of the Favicon DB on 1% of starts.

histogram.name: History.NumTouchIconsInDB
histogram.summary: The total number of touch icons being tracked in a user's Favicon database. This is a subset of History.NumFaviconsInDB, which includes all icon types in its count. Logged on initialization of the Favicon DB on 1% of starts.

histogram.name: History.QueryMostVisitedURLsTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time for function HistoryBackend::QueryMostVisitedURLs to execute. Excludes the case where local HistoryDatabase db_ is unavailable, i.e. where the query doesn't really happen.

histogram.name: History.ResultsRenderedTime
histogram.summary: Records the time taken to load the history Web UI and render (at least) a screen full of items. This is roughly equivalent to 'time to first meaningful paint' for the history page.

histogram.name: History.ShownHeaderAboutOtherFormsOfBrowsingHistory
histogram.summary: Whether the notice about other forms of browsing history was shown in the header of the History page when the header was displayed. The header is refreshed with every history query.

histogram.name: History.TopHostsVisitsByRank
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 11/2018, not used in production since 2/2018.
histogram.summary: Page visits to each of a user's top 50 hosts. The bucket is the 1-based rank of the host. Visits to all other hosts go into the 51st bucket. Android-only. Only counts the last URL in a redirect chain and does not include reloads. The list of top hosts is computed approximately nightly. This means that a given day will likely include a recalculation of top hosts, and therefore a given bucket may represent two different hosts at different times of day.

histogram.name: History.TopSitesRecoveredPercentage
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Size of the recovered TopSites database relative to the original corrupt database. Recovery is VACUUM-like, so the resulting database should always be smaller. Substantial 100% results would indicate empty databases being recovered, substantial low% results would indicate very little data being recovered.

histogram.name: History.TopSitesRecoveredRowsThumbnails
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Rows recovered from [thumbnails] table in TopSites recovery.

histogram.name: History.TopSitesRecovery
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The TopSites recovery code is written conservatively, with successful recovery committed and any failure leading to rollback. This tracks the outcomes to determine which cases are high-frequency enough to warrant adding additional code to handle them (versus simply deleting the data).

histogram.name: History.TopSitesVisitsByRank
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Page visits to each of a user's top 50 sites. Visits to all other sites go into the 51st bucket. Only count the page visit if it came from user browsing and only count it once when cycling through a redirect chain.

histogram.name: History.UpdateTopSitesOnDBThreadTime
histogram.obsolete: Removed because only the execution time at startup is of interest. See histogram History.UpdateTopSitesOnDBThread_Startup_Time.
histogram.summary: The amount of time for function history::TopSitesBackend::UpdateTopSitesOnDBThread to execute. Excludes the case where local TopSitesDatabase db_ is unavailable, i.e. where the update doesn't really happen.

histogram.name: History.URLTableCount
histogram.summary: Number of rows in urls table in History DB. Metrics are logged on initialization of the History DB on 1% of starts.

histogram.name: History.VisitTableCount
histogram.summary: Number of rows in visits table in History DB. Metrics are logged on initialization of the History DB on 1% of starts.

histogram.name: History.WeeklyHostCount
histogram.summary: Unique hostnames in History database urls table with last-visit times in the last 7 days. Metrics are logged on initialization of the History DB on 0.3% of starts.

histogram.name: History.WeeklyURLCount
histogram.summary: Unique URLs in History database urls table with last-visit times in the last 7 days. Metrics are logged on initialization of the History DB on 0.3% of starts.

histogram.name: History.WeeklyVisitCount
histogram.summary: Number of rows in History visits table with visit times in the last 7 days. Metrics are logged on initialization of the History DB on 1% of starts.

histogram.name: HistoryPage.ClickAgeInDays
histogram.summary: Recorded each time an entry on the history page is clicked. Measures the age in days (defined as 24 hour periods, not using local time) of that entry. Values are ROUNDED UP, i.e. "1" means at most one day old etc.

histogram.name: HistoryPage.ClickAgeInDaysSubset
histogram.summary: Subset of the HistoryPage.ClickAgeInDays histogram, with better bucket resolution at lower numbers.

histogram.name: HistoryPage.ClickPosition
histogram.summary: Number of entries that the clicked entry is older than in History page. Last bucket is any entry of that value or higher.

histogram.name: HistoryPage.ClickPositionSubset
histogram.summary: Subset of the Click Position histogram. Contains only the first smaller subset of entries on the page. Number of entries that the clicked entry is older than in History page. Last bucket is entries of that value or higher.

histogram.name: HistoryPage.OtherDevicesMenu
histogram.summary:
Histogram for usage of the section in the history page that allows the user to access tabs from other devices.
OtherSessionsActions.OPENED_LINK_VIA_CONTEXT_MENU was added for M-76.
This histogram is of special interest to the chrome-analysis-team@. Do not change its semantics or retire it without talking to them first.

histogram.name: HistoryPage.OtherDevicesMenu.OpenAll.TabsPerWindow
histogram.summary: The number of tabs created in each window when a user executes "Open all" menu item from a session menu in the "Tabs from other devices" section of the history page.

histogram.name: HistoryPage.OtherDevicesMenu.OpenAll.TotalTabs
histogram.summary: Total number of tabs opened when a user executes "Open all" menu item from a session menu in the "Tabs from other devices" section of the history page.

histogram.name: HistoryPage.OtherDevicesMenu.OpenAll.TotalWindows
histogram.summary: Total number of windows opened when a user executes "Open all" menu item from a session menu in the "Tabs from other devices" section of the history page.

histogram.name: HistoryPage.RemoveEntryPosition
histogram.summary: Number of entries that the deleted entry is older than in History page. Last bucket is any entry of that value or higher. Confirmed removal is not guaranteed, just an initiation of 'Remove selected items'.

histogram.name: HistoryPage.RemoveEntryPositionSubset
histogram.summary: Subset of Remove Entry Position histogram. Contains only the first smaller subset of entries on the page. Number of entries that the deleted entry is older than in History page. Last bucket is any entry of that value or higher. Confirmed removal is not guaranteed, just an initiation of 'Remove selected items'.

histogram.name: HostedAppFrame.ContentSettings.ImagePressed
histogram.summary: Counts which content setting buttons in the hosted app frame are pressed by the user.

histogram.name: HostedAppFrame.WrenchMenu.MenuAction
histogram.summary: Number of times that each menu item is clicked from the hosted app window menu button.

histogram.name: Hotword.AudioLoggingEnabled
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 10/2017. Feature removed with crbug/761426.
histogram.summary: The state of the hotword audio logging preference. This value is emitted each time the hotword availability is requested by the extension if the user is also opted in to hotword voice search. This check typically happens each time a hotword search is initiated.

histogram.name: Hotword.Enabled
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 10/2017. Feature removed with crbug/761426.
histogram.summary: The state of the hotword preference. This value is emitted during HotwordService initialization which happens during Profile initialization.

histogram.name: Hotword.ExtensionAvailability
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 10/2017. Feature removed with crbug/761426.
histogram.summary: Whether the external component hotword extension exists (i.e., not pending download, disabled, etc.). This value is emitted each time the hotword availability is requested by the extension which typically happens each time a hotword search is initiated.

histogram.name: Hotword.HotwordError
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 10/2017. Feature removed with crbug/761426.
histogram.summary: Errors reported by the hotword service when determining if hotwording is available. Non-errors are also reported so that errors can be seen as a percentage of total requests.

histogram.name: Hotword.HotwordMediaStreamResult
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 10/2017. Feature removed with crbug/761426.
histogram.summary: Success or error when attempting to open a MediaStream for the microphone. At most one success or error will be logged for an attempt to open a stream.

histogram.name: Hotword.HotwordNaClMessageTimeout
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 10/2017. Feature removed with crbug/761426.
histogram.summary: Timeout occured while waiting for a message from the NaCl hotword detector plugin. This value is the message that was expected to be received from the plugin.

histogram.name: Hotword.HotwordNaClPluginLoadResult
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 10/2017. Feature removed with crbug/761426.
histogram.summary: Success or error when attempting to load the NaCl hotword detector plugin.

histogram.name: Hotword.HotwordTriggerSource
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 10/2017. Feature removed with crbug/761426.
histogram.summary: Location of hotword trigger. Emitted every time the hotword is triggered by the user saying 'Ok Google'.

histogram.name: Hotword.SharedModuleReinstallLanguage
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 10/2017. Feature removed with crbug/761426.
histogram.summary: Language, switched to by the user, that triggered a hotword shared module reinstall.

histogram.name: HTMLImport.UnexpectedRequest
histogram.summary: Logs the type of requests made by HTML imports with wrong fetcher.

histogram.name: HttpCache.AccessToDone
histogram.summary: For every http cache transaction with a pattern (see HttpCache.Pattern), the time from the first access to the disk cache until the transaction was done.

histogram.name: HttpCache.AfterSend
histogram.summary: For http cache transactions in which a network request was sent, the time elapsed between sending it over the network until the transaction was done.

histogram.name: HttpCache.AsyncValidationDuration
histogram.summary: The time spent performing an asynchronous revalidation that was triggered by a Cache-Control: stale-while-revalidate directive. This is recorded when the asynchronous revalidation completes, either after the response was completely read or an error occurred. One entry is recorded for each asynchronous revalidation that is performed.

histogram.name: HttpCache.BeforeSend
histogram.summary: For http cache transactions in which a network request was sent, the time elapsed between beginning the request and sending it over the network; this is the time spent accessing the disk cache.

histogram.name: HttpCache.CantConditionalizeCause
histogram.summary: For each request that results in an unconditionalizable network request, the cause for the request.

histogram.name: HttpCache.CantConditionalizeZeroFreshnessFromMemHint
histogram.summary: For each request with HttpCache.CantConditionalizeCause == Zero Freshness, this is true iff the zero freshness status was determined by looking at in-memory hints in disk cache index, rather than by opening the entry and looking at headers.

histogram.name: HttpCache.EntryLockWait
histogram.summary: The time spent waiting for write lock on a disk cache entry.

histogram.name: HttpCache.ExternallyConditionalized
histogram.obsolete: stale-while-revalidate implementation removed for now, see https://crbug.com/700568.
histogram.summary: Count of the number of external (ie. from Blink) conditionalized requests, and whether or not those requests could have been served from the browser cache.

histogram.name: HttpCache.HardReset
histogram.summary: Whether the HttpCache was forced to reset upon creation, effectively clearing out its previous content. This is recorded when the cache is created for the browsing session.

histogram.name: HttpCache.NetworkIsolationKeyPresent
histogram.summary: Records whether a cache request has a network isolation key set or not, which is used for double-keying.

histogram.name: HttpCache.NetworkIsolationKeyPresent2
histogram.summary: Records whether a cache request has a network isolation key set or not, which is used for double-keying.

histogram.name: HttpCache.OfflineStatus
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Result of a main page HttpCacheTransaction if offline mode had been enabled.

histogram.name: HttpCache.ParallelWritingPattern
histogram.summary: Records whether a transaction either created a Writers object when one didn't exist, or joined an existing Writers object, or why it failed to do so (e.g., it's a range request, it's not a GET request, etc.).

histogram.name: HttpCache.Pattern
histogram.summary: For each http cache transaction, the recorded pattern.

histogram.name: HttpCache.PercentBeforeSend
histogram.summary: For http cache transactions in which a network request was sent, the percentage of total request time that elapsed before sending the request over the network; this is the time spent accessing the disk cache.

histogram.name: HttpCache.ReadErrorNonRestartable
histogram.summary: Net error results from non-restartable cache read errors.

histogram.name: HttpCache.ReadErrorRestartable
histogram.summary: Net error results from restartable cache read errors.

histogram.name: HttpCache.StaleEntry.FreshnessPeriodsSinceLastUsed
histogram.obsolete: Removed in M77. One day Android results: 25.00% 1033 50.00% 4945 75.00% 42023 95.00% Infinity
histogram.summary: For each http cache transaction for which a cache entry exists but it cannot be used because the entry's age is beyond its freshness: counts how many freshness periods have elapsed since the entry was last used (x1000).

histogram.name: HttpCache.StaleEntry.Updated.Age
histogram.obsolete: Removed in M77. One day percentiles on Android: 25.00% 16146 50.00% 101342 75.00% 372632 95.00% Infinity
histogram.summary: For each http cache transaction for which a validation attempt is made due to a stale entry and is updated (e.g., 200), records the age of the entry in seconds.

histogram.name: HttpCache.StaleEntry.Updated.AgeInFreshnessPeriods
histogram.obsolete: Removed in M77. One day percentiles on Android: 25.00% 246.3 50.00% 1353 75.00% 9142 95.00% 147572 99.00% Infinity
histogram.summary: For each http cache transaction for which a validation attempt is made due to a stale entry and is updated (e.g., 200), records the age of the entry as percentage of freshness period.

histogram.name: HttpCache.StaleEntry.Validated.Age
histogram.obsolete: Removed in M77. One day percentiles on Android: 25.00% 10791 50.00% 70285 75.00% 223571 95.00% Infinity
histogram.summary: For each http cache transaction for which a validation attempt is made due to a stale entry and is validated (304), records the age of the entry in seconds.

histogram.name: HttpCache.StaleEntry.Validated.AgeInFreshnessPeriods
histogram.obsolete: Removed in M77. One day percentiles on Android: 25.00% 169.5 50.00% 476.3 75.00% 2451 95.00% 43218 99.00% 474791 99.50% Infinity
histogram.summary: For each http cache transaction for which a validation attempt is made due to a stale entry and is validated (304), records the age of the entry as percentage of freshness period.

histogram.name: HttpCache.TopFrameOriginPresent
histogram.summary: Records whether a cache request has a top frame origin set or not, which is used for double-keying.

histogram.name: HttpCache.ValidationCause
histogram.summary: For each validation attempt, the cause for the validation.

histogram.name: HttpCache.Vary
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The type of Vary header for a given GET response.

histogram.name: Hwsec.Attestation.Status
histogram.summary: The result of attestation-related operations on the Chrome OS side.

histogram.name: Hyphenation.Open
histogram.summary: The time it takes to open a hyphenation dictionary.

histogram.name: Hyphenation.Open.File
histogram.summary: The time it takes to open a hyphenation dictionary file.

histogram.name: ICU.CreateInstance
histogram.summary: This metric logs the creation of key ICU objects. These object creation called directly from Chrome code or indirectly from depending library such as blink and v8.

histogram.name: ICU.DataFile
histogram.summary: This metric logs the ICU built-in data file name opened and used by chrome. These file open calls are perform internally inside ICU while Chrome directly or indirectly call ICU to create object and cause ICU to load new data files, such as loading codeset conversion table, create break iterator, and open dictionary files for word break/line break iterator. It however does not include the opening of resource bundle files.

histogram.name: Image.ResampleMS
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Time it takes to do high-quality image resampling for images that are resized. Non-resized images and ones our heuristic decides can be done "badly" are not counted.

histogram.name: ImageAnnotationService.AccessibilityV1.CacheHit
histogram.summary: For each image processed by the image annotation service, records whether or not results for the image already existed in the client-side cache. A hit means that image data was not transmitted to the image annotation server.

histogram.name: ImageAnnotationService.AccessibilityV1.ClientResult
histogram.summary: For each request made by a client feature to the image annotation service, reports the final status of the service response.

histogram.name: ImageAnnotationService.AccessibilityV1.Confidence
histogram.summary: For each annotation of the given type returned by the image annotation server, records the confidence (as a percentage) produced by the ML model for the annotation.

histogram.name: ImageAnnotationService.AccessibilityV1.DescType
histogram.summary: For each annotation backend run by the image annotation server, records the gRPC status reported.

histogram.name: ImageAnnotationService.AccessibilityV1.Empty
histogram.summary: For each annotation of the given type returned by the image annotation server, records whether or not the text of the annotation was empty. This is sometimes valid, e.g. for text extraction (OCR) on an image that does not contain any text.

histogram.name: ImageAnnotationService.AccessibilityV1.EncodedJpegSizeKB
histogram.summary: For each image sent from a client feature to the image annotation service, reports the image's final size (in kilobytes) after resizing and reencoding.

histogram.name: ImageAnnotationService.AccessibilityV1.EngineKnown
histogram.summary: For each annotation backend run by the image annotation server, records whether or not the backend is recognised by this version of Chrome.

histogram.name: ImageAnnotationService.AccessibilityV1.ImageRequestIncludesDesc
histogram.summary:
For each image request sent to the image annotation server, records whether or not the description backend is included among the requested backends.
The description backend will not be included when the image is known to violate the description model policy (i.e. be too small or have too-high an aspect ratio).

histogram.name: ImageAnnotationService.AccessibilityV1.JsonParseSuccess
histogram.summary: For each non-empty response sent from the image annotation server, records whether or not JSON data were successfully parsed from the response body.

histogram.name: ImageAnnotationService.AccessibilityV1.PixelFetchSuccess
histogram.summary: For each pixel fetch request sent from the image annotation service to a client feature, records whether or not encoded pixel data were successfully returned to the service.

histogram.name: ImageAnnotationService.AccessibilityV1.ServerHttpResponseCode
histogram.summary: For each HTTP response from the image annotation server, reports the associated HTTP code.

histogram.name: ImageAnnotationService.AccessibilityV1.ServerLatencyMs
histogram.summary: For each HTTP response from the image annotation server, reports the time elapsed (in milliseconds) since the corresponding request was sent.

histogram.name: ImageAnnotationService.AccessibilityV1.ServerNetError
histogram.summary: For each HTTP request sent to the image annotation server, reports the net error (or OK status) of the request.

histogram.name: ImageAnnotationService.AccessibilityV1.ServerRequestSizeKB
histogram.summary: For each HTTP request sent to the image annotation server, reports the size of the request. Measured in kilobytes, since a request can contain the pixel data of multiple images.

histogram.name: ImageAnnotationService.AccessibilityV1.ServerResponseSizeBytes
histogram.summary: For each HTTP response from the image annotation server, reports the response size in bytes.

histogram.name: ImageAnnotationService.AccessibilityV1.SourcePixelCount
histogram.summary: For each image sent from a client feature to the image annotation service, reports the image's original (i.e. pre-scaling) area in pixels. Images with pixel count above a certain threshold will be resized prior to transmission.

histogram.name: ImageAnnotationService.AccessibilityV1.Status
histogram.summary: For each annotation backend run by the image annotation server, records the gRPC status reported.

histogram.name: ImageFetcher.CacheMetadataCount
histogram.summary: The total number of metadata entries in the image fetcher cache metadata database for a given cache strategy. Recorded during cache eviction process.

histogram.name: ImageFetcher.CacheSize
histogram.summary: The total size of the cache for a given cache strategy. Recorded during cache eviction process.

histogram.name: ImageFetcher.Events
histogram.summary: Events that track the lifecycle and performance of the cached_image_fetcher. The events reported include: success/failure conditions, various recoverable errors and a couple of dead-end errors.

histogram.name: ImageFetcher.ImageLoadFromCacheTime
histogram.summary: The time it takes for cached_image_fetcher to load an image from the cache in native.

histogram.name: ImageFetcher.ImageLoadFromCacheTimeJava
histogram.summary: The time it takes for cached_image_fetcher to load an image from the cache in Java.

histogram.name: ImageFetcher.ImageLoadFromNativeTimeJava
histogram.summary: The time it takes for cached_image_fetcher to load an image from native code. Only recorded on successful loads.

histogram.name: ImageFetcher.ImageLoadFromNetworkAfterCacheHit
histogram.summary: The time it takes for cached_image_fetcher to load an image from the network after a cache hit.

histogram.name: ImageFetcher.ImageLoadFromNetworkTime
histogram.summary: The time it takes for cached_image_fetcher to load an image from the network.

histogram.name: ImageFetcher.LoadImageMetadata
histogram.summary: The time it takes to load an image's metadata from the metadata store.

histogram.name: ImageFetcher.RequestStatusCode
histogram.summary: Android: The integer status code of network requests made by the image data fetcher. This code includes both protocol(1xx-5xx) and non-protocol(-xxx) errors. Recorded when a request completes.

histogram.name: ImageFetcher.TimeSinceLastCacheLRUEviction
histogram.summary: The time since the last LRU eviction from the image cache. Recorded when two LRU evictions occur within closure proximity to one another. Will be used to determine if LRU eviction is happening too frequently.

histogram.name: ImageLoader.Client.Cache.HitMiss
histogram.summary: For each image load request that requested caching, records whether or not it was found in the client-side cache. A hit means the request was not forwarded to the ImageLoader extension.

histogram.name: ImageLoader.Client.Cache.Usage
histogram.summary: Returns the percentage of the client-side cache that is used for loading images, before they are sent to the ImageLoader extension. Expressed as a percentage of ImageLoaderClient.CACHE_MEMORY_LIMIT (e.g. 20MB).

histogram.name: ImageLoader.Client.Cached
histogram.summary: For each image load request records whether or not it requested caching.

histogram.name: Import.ImportedHistorySize.AutoImportFromIE
histogram.summary: The size of the history on Auto Import from IE. This measure is only logged on a succesfull Auto Import.

histogram.name: Import.ImporterType.AutoImport
histogram.summary: The importer used on first run Auto Import.

histogram.name: Import.ImporterType.BookmarksAPI
histogram.summary: The importer used on import from the bookmarks file API.

histogram.name: Import.ImporterType.ImportDataHandler
histogram.summary: The importer used on import from the chrome://settings/importData UI.

histogram.name: Import.IncludesPasswords.Firefox
histogram.summary: Whether or not the password checkbox is checked when importing a profile of Firefox. Note that canceled imports could be counted also.

histogram.name: Import.NumberOfImportedPasswords.Firefox
histogram.summary: The number of passwords that are imported from Firefox. This is recorded when importing a profile of Firefox with the password checkbox checked. Note that the reported number of password could be slightly bigger than the actual number of imported passwords because some incomplete or blacklisted passwords that are dropped while importing might be included in the reported value.

histogram.name: Import.ShowDialog.FromBookmarkBarView
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The amount of time from install time to time that user opens import dialog from BookmarkBarView.

histogram.name: Import.ShowDialog.FromFloatingBookmarkBarView
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The amount of time from install time to time that user opens import dialog from NTP floating BookmarkBarView.

histogram.name: Import_ShowDlg.FromBookmarkBarView
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The amount of time from install time to time that user opens import dialog from BookmarkBarView.

histogram.name: Import_ShowDlg.FromFloatingBookmarkBarView
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The amount of time from install time to time that user opens import dialog from NTP floating BookmarkBarView.

histogram.name: ImportantFile.DeleteOnCloseError
histogram.summary: File error on Windows when ImportantFileWriter fails to mark the temporary file for deletion upon closure.

histogram.name: ImportantFile.FileCreateError
histogram.summary: File error happened upon temporary file creation at ImportantFileWriter.

histogram.name: ImportantFile.FileDeleteError
histogram.obsolete: Replaced by ImportantFile.FileDeleteNoRetryError, ImportantFile.FileDeleteRetryExceededError, and ImportantFile.FileDeleteRetrySuccessCount in M84.
histogram.summary: File error happened upon temporary file deletion at ImportantFileWriter.

histogram.name: ImportantFile.FileDeleteNoRetryError
histogram.summary: File error happened upon temporary file deletion at ImportantFileWriter when it was not possible to retry in a delayed task.

histogram.name: ImportantFile.FileDeleteRetryExceededError
histogram.summary: The final file error when all retries for deleting the temorary file fail.

histogram.name: ImportantFile.FileDeleteRetrySuccessCount
histogram.summary: The number of retries needed to successfully delete the temporary file. Only recorded when at least one retry (two attempts) is needed.

histogram.name: ImportantFile.FileOpenError
histogram.summary: File error happened upon opening temporary file at ImportantFileWriter.

histogram.name: ImportantFile.FileRenameError
histogram.summary: File error happened upon temporary file renaming at ImportantFileWriter.

histogram.name: ImportantFile.FileWriteError
histogram.summary: File error happened upon writing data to temporary file at ImportantFileWriter.

histogram.name: ImportantFile.TempFileFailures
histogram.summary: Count of various failures in ImportantFileWrite when manipulating a temp file to write to before moving it into place (replacing another file).

histogram.name: ImportantFile.TimeToWrite
histogram.summary: Time used to atomically write string into a file using ImportantFileWriter.

histogram.name: IncognitoModePrefs.WindowsParentalControlsInitThread
histogram.summary: The thread used to initialize the Windows parental controls.

histogram.name: IncognitoModePrefs.WindowsParentalControlsInitTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time that elapsed during a run of PlatformParentalControlsValue::IsParentalControlActivityLoggingOnImpl on Windows 7 and above.

histogram.name: IncompatibleApplicationsPage.AddedPostCrash
histogram.summary: Records whether the user was automatically shown the chrome://settings/incompatibleApplications page following a Chrome crash.

histogram.name: IncompatibleApplicationsPage.NumApplications
histogram.summary: The number of incompatible applications detected by Chrome at the time the chrome://settings/incompatibleApplications page was opened.

histogram.name: InertialSensor.AccelerometerAndroidAvailable
histogram.summary: Whether the Sensor.TYPE_LINEAR_ACCELERATION was available at the start of Device Motion.

histogram.name: InertialSensor.AccelerometerIncGravityAndroidAvailable
histogram.summary: Whether the Sensor.TYPE_ACCELEROMETER was available at the start of Device Motion.

histogram.name: InertialSensor.AccelerometerWindowsAvailable
histogram.summary: Whether the SENSOR_TYPE_ACCELEROMETER_3D was available at the start of Device Motion on the Windows platform.

histogram.name: InertialSensor.DeviceOrientationSensorAndroid
histogram.summary: Provides a histogram of the base sensors (if any) that are used at the start of the Device Orientation API. Currently Sensor.TYPE_GAME_ROTATION_VECTOR is the default sensor for orientation, if it's not available fall-back options are provided by using either Sensor.TYPE_ROTATION_VECTOR or a combination of Sensor.TYPE_ACCELEROMETER and Sensor.TYPE_MAGNETIC_FIELD.

histogram.name: InertialSensor.GyrometerWindowsAvailable
histogram.summary: Whether the SENSOR_TYPE_GYROMETER_3D was available at the start of Device Motion on the Windows platform.

histogram.name: InertialSensor.GyroscopeAndroidAvailable
histogram.summary: Whether the Sensor.TYPE_GYROSCOPE was available at the start of Device Motion.

histogram.name: InertialSensor.InclinometerWindowsAvailable
histogram.summary: Whether the SENSOR_TYPE_INCLINOMETER_3D was available at the start of Device Orientation on the Windows platform.

histogram.name: InertialSensor.MotionDefaultAvailable
histogram.summary: This histogram counts the number of Device Motion API invocations in the default implementation (Linux and CrOS). The default implementation does not provide any sensors so the result is always false.

histogram.name: InertialSensor.MotionMacAvailable
histogram.summary: Whether the sudden motion sensor was available at the start of Device Motion on the MacOS platform.

histogram.name: InertialSensor.OrientationDefaultAvailable
histogram.summary: This histogram counts the number of Device Orientation API invocations in the default implementation (Linux and CrOS). The default implementation does not provide any sensors so the result is always false.

histogram.name: InertialSensor.OrientationMacAvailable
histogram.summary: Whether the sudden motion sensor was available at the start of Device Orientation on the MacOS platform.

histogram.name: InertialSensor.RotationVectorAndroidAvailable
histogram.summary: Whether the Sensor.TYPE_ROTATION_VECTOR was available at the start of Device Orientation.

histogram.name: InfoBar.Shown
histogram.summary: Records how many times a particular infobar was shown to the user. On Android, the infobar may be partially hidden due to stacking. On desktop, a tab with a visible infobar will increment this histogram every time the user switches away from the tab and back to it with the infobar still visible.

histogram.name: InfoBar.Shown.Hidden
histogram.summary: Records how many times a particular infobar was displayed in a hidden state due to at least one other infobar existing. If there are no existing infobars, but the user scrolls at the exact moment a new infobar is added, the infobar may be hidden, but not recorded in this metric. Reliably triggering and detecting this combination is tricky so it isn't done. Only recorded on Android.

histogram.name: InfoBar.Shown.Hiding
histogram.summary: Records how many times a particular infobar was visible and hiding a new infobar behind it. Only recorded on Android.

histogram.name: InfoBar.Shown.Visible
histogram.summary: Records how many times a particular infobar was displayed in a visible state due to no other infobars existing. If there are no existing infobars, but the user scrolls at the exact moment a new infobar is added, the infobar may be hidden, but recorded here as visible. Reliably triggering and detecting this combination is tricky so it isn't done. Only recorded on Android.

histogram.name: InProductHelp.Config.ParsingEvent
histogram.summary: Records if in-product help configuration is parsed correctly, and the failure reasons for parsing failures.

histogram.name: InProductHelp.Db.Init
histogram.summary: Records if database initialization succeeds for in-product help.

histogram.name: InProductHelp.Db.Load
histogram.summary: Records if database successfully loads the data for in-product help.

histogram.name: InProductHelp.Db.TotalEvents
histogram.summary: Records the total number of event records in the database for in-product help after the event model is loaded.

histogram.name: InProductHelp.Db.Update
histogram.summary: Records if each database update succeeds for in-product help.

histogram.name: InProductHelp.NotifyEventReadyState
histogram.summary: Records if the model is ready when in-product help event happens.

histogram.name: InProductHelp.Promos.IPH_ReopenTab.DismissedAt
histogram.summary: Last step of in-product help (IPH) for reopening tabs that user followed before finishing the IPH flow or dismissing it early. Recorded when the IPH flow is dismissed, which is at one of three possible times: when the promo bubble times out, when the menu is closed after the user opens it for IPH, or when the user opens the last closed tab. The last is considered a success.

histogram.name: InProductHelp.ShouldTriggerHelpUI
histogram.summary: Records if in-product help is shown to the user, and the failure reasons if in-product help is not shown. Recorded on its own across all in-product help features, in addition to being a base name for feature-specific histograms.

histogram.name: InputMethod.ActiveCount
histogram.summary: The number of active input methods. Recorded when the user logs in to Chrome OS or each cold start of Chrome on Android. The active input methods are selected by user in the language settings page on Chrome OS and in system language settings on Android.

histogram.name: InputMethod.Assistive.Coverage
histogram.summary: The number of times each assistive action is triggered. Recorded when assistive actions are triggered.

histogram.name: InputMethod.Assistive.Disabled
histogram.summary: The number of times each assistive action could be triggered according to the surrounding text but was not triggered because the user turned off the feature. Recorded when the surrounding text could trigger assistive actions but the corresponding feature was disabled.

histogram.name: InputMethod.Assistive.EmojiSuggestAddition.AcceptanceIndex
histogram.summary: The number of times an emoji suggest addition is accepted at a given index. Recorded when an user accepts an emoji suggestion appeared after typing an emoji-triggered word.

histogram.name: InputMethod.Assistive.Match
histogram.summary: The number of times each assistive action could be triggered according to the surrounding text. This includes cases in InputMethod.Assistive.Coverage, and also includes cases when the feature is turned off or there is insufficient data. Recorded when the surrounding text could trigger assistive actions.

histogram.name: InputMethod.Assistive.Success
histogram.summary: The number of times each assistive action is accepted. Recorded when assistive actions are accepted by the users.

histogram.name: InputMethod.AutoCorrectLevel
histogram.summary: The auto-correction level for suggestion engine.

histogram.name: InputMethod.Category
histogram.summary: The breakdown of input method usage by input method category. Recorded when the system changes the current input method.

histogram.name: InputMethod.Commit.Index
histogram.summary: The suggestion index (1-based) of the suggestion list item which user chooses to commit.

histogram.name: InputMethod.Commit.Type
histogram.summary: The suggestion accuracy type which the user chooses to commit.

histogram.name: InputMethod.Commit.Type2
histogram.summary: The suggestion accuracy type which the user chooses to commit.

histogram.name: InputMethod.CommitLength
histogram.summary: The number of characters committed with composition text.

histogram.name: InputMethod.CompositionWithImm32BasedIme
histogram.summary: Whether the composition is composed by IMM32-based input method processor when TSF is disabled on Windows. Recorded when user starts new composition.

histogram.name: InputMethod.Handwriting.Actions
histogram.summary: Types of actions performed by the user during handwriting, recorded when the user performs a gesture action.

histogram.name: InputMethod.Handwriting.CharsEdited10s
histogram.summary: Number of characters written in a 10 second interval, recorded every 10s while the handwriting keyboard is active.

histogram.name: InputMethod.Handwriting.CharsEdited5s
histogram.summary: Number of characters written in a 5 second interval, recorded every 5s while the handwriting keyboard is active.

histogram.name: InputMethod.Handwriting.CharsEdited60s
histogram.summary: Number of characters written in a 60 second interval, recorded every 60s while the handwriting keyboard is active.

histogram.name: InputMethod.ID
histogram.obsolete: This is deprecated since M46, InputMethod.ID2 is used since then.
histogram.summary: The breakdown of input method usage by input method IDs. Recorded when the user presses keys on physical or on-screen keyboard.

histogram.name: InputMethod.ID2
histogram.summary: The breakdown of input method usage by input method IDs. Recorded when the user presses keys on physical or on-screen keyboard.

histogram.name: InputMethod.ImeMenu.ActivationChanged
histogram.summary: Whether the opt-in IME menu in the shelf has been activated.

histogram.name: InputMethod.ImeMenu.EmojiHandwritingVoiceButton
histogram.summary: The number of times users click emoji, handwriting or voice button in opt-in IME menu.

histogram.name: InputMethod.ImeSwitch
histogram.summary: The trigger type of input method switches by user.

histogram.name: InputMethod.KeyEventLatency
histogram.summary: Time taken by the engine to handle a key event.

histogram.name: InputMethod.MatchesSystemLanguage
histogram.summary: Whether the currently selected keyboard language matches the system language. Recorded once with every cold start of Chrome for Android.

histogram.name: InputMethod.ModeChangeKeyAction
histogram.summary: The result of the user pressing the mode change key.

histogram.name: InputMethod.Mojo.Extension.ActivateIMELatency
histogram.summary: The time taken to activate a new IME via IME Mojo service in the IME extension. It's from when the IME extension sends out an request to activate a new IME until the new IME is resolved in the IME service and its Mojo interface is bound to the IME extension.

histogram.name: InputMethod.Mojo.Extension.Event
histogram.summary: The events of Mojo service in the IME Extension.

histogram.name: InputMethod.Mojo.Extension.Rulebased.ProcessLatency
histogram.summary: The time taken to process a rulebased request via the IME Mojo service inside the IME extension.

histogram.name: InputMethod.Mojo.Extension.ServiceInitLatency
histogram.summary: The time taken to initialize the IME Mojo service in the IME extension. It's from the IME extension starts loading a private Mojo bundle for making a request to connect a IME service until the connection between the extension and the IME Mojo service is built.

histogram.name: InputMethod.PkCommit.Index
histogram.summary: The suggestion index (1-based) of the suggestion list item which user chooses to commit for physical keyboard autocorrect.

histogram.name: InputMethod.PkCommit.Type
histogram.summary: The suggestion accuracy type which the user chooses to commit for physical keyboard autocorrect.

histogram.name: InputMethod.RegisterProxyView
histogram.summary: The result of registering proxy view to InputMethodManager.

histogram.name: InputMethod.VirtualKeyboard.BackspaceCount
histogram.summary: The number of times the backspace key was pressed on the virtual keyboard, while the virtual keyboard was alive. Recorded when the virtual keyboard is closed.

histogram.name: InputMethod.VirtualKeyboard.BackspaceOnLayout
histogram.summary: The layout type of the virtual keyboard, recorded when backspace is pressed.

histogram.name: InputMethod.VirtualKeyboard.BackwardsMovesPerSwipe
histogram.summary: Chrome OS histogram that counts the number of times the cursor was moved to the previous word by swiping backwards on the selection track. The count is initialized when the user starts swiping from any side of the virtual keyboard, incremented based on how far they swipe backwards on the selection track, and recorded when they release their finger.

histogram.name: InputMethod.VirtualKeyboard.CharactersBetweenBackspaces
histogram.summary: Counts the length of text typed by the virtual keyboard between each backspace. This metric provides a rough approximation of an error rate for the virtual keyboard.

histogram.name: InputMethod.VirtualKeyboard.CharactersCommitted
histogram.summary: The total number of characters committed. Recorded when the virtual keyboard is closed.

histogram.name: InputMethod.VirtualKeyboard.ContainerBehavior
histogram.summary: Chrome OS histogram that counts the number of times each virtual keyboard display mode is used. Recorded when the virtual keyboard is opened or a user changes the keyboard mode from UI in the virtual keyboard.

histogram.name: InputMethod.VirtualKeyboard.DecoderEvent
histogram.summary: Chrome OS histogram that counts events from the decoder triggered by the virtual keyboard.

histogram.name: InputMethod.VirtualKeyboard.Duration
histogram.summary: How long the virtual keyboard was visible.

histogram.name: InputMethod.VirtualKeyboard.Emoji.TriggerType
histogram.summary: How emojis were inserted.

histogram.name: InputMethod.VirtualKeyboard.ErrorType
histogram.summary: Errors from the virtual keyboard extension

histogram.name: InputMethod.VirtualKeyboard.FocusedByStylus
histogram.summary: Whether an input field was focused by a stylus or not. Recorded when an input field is focused.

histogram.name: InputMethod.VirtualKeyboard.GestureTypingEvent
histogram.summary: Text input events related to gesture typing.

histogram.name: InputMethod.VirtualKeyboard.InitLatency
histogram.summary: The on-screen keyboard initialization latency in milliseconds.

histogram.name: InputMethod.VirtualKeyboard.InitLatency.KeyboardShownLongTail
histogram.summary: Time to show the on-screen keyboard in milliseconds. Similar to InputMethod.VirtualKeyboard.KeyboardShown but with a larger maximum to capture more of the distribution.

histogram.name: InputMethod.VirtualKeyboard.Layout
histogram.summary: The layout of the on-screen keyboard. Logged when the specific layout is shown.

histogram.name: InputMethod.VirtualKeyboard.LayoutSwitch
histogram.summary: The count of layout switching actions while virtual keyboard is alive. Recorded when the virtual keyboard window is hidden or destroyed.

histogram.name: InputMethod.VirtualKeyboard.MovesPerSwipe
histogram.summary: Chrome OS histogram that counts the number of times the cursor was moved to the next word by swiping forward on the selection track. The count is initialized when the user starts swiping from any side of the virtual keyboard, incremented based on how far they swipe forwards on the selection track, and recorded when they release their finger.

histogram.name: InputMethod.VirtualKeyboard.PreferredLayoutForStylus
histogram.summary: The layout of the virtual keyboard that is shown when the user focuses on an input field with a stylus. Recorded when the layout is shown after a focus.

histogram.name: InputMethod.VirtualKeyboard.ResizableWindowInitWidth
histogram.summary: The width of the virtual keyboard window, in pixels, when the floating keyboard is initialized. This is recorded only once on startup, when the virtual keyboard is initialized in floating mode.

histogram.name: InputMethod.VirtualKeyboard.ResizableWindowWidth
histogram.summary: The width of the virtual keyboard window, in pixels, after a user has completed resizing the virtual keyboard. This is recorded when the user lifts their finger at the end of a drag that initiates a resize.

histogram.name: InputMethod.VirtualKeyboard.SwitchMode
histogram.summary: The count of user actions to switch keyboard mode (floating, docked). Recorded when the user clicks in the docked/floating keyboard menu option.

histogram.name: InputMethod.VirtualKeyboard.TapCount
histogram.summary: The number of times the virtual keyboard was tapped while the virtual keyboard was alive. Recorded when the virtual keyboard is closed.

histogram.name: InputMethod.VirtualKeyboard.WordsDeletedPerSwipe
histogram.summary: Chrome OS histogram that tracks the total number of words that were deleted by swiping forward on the deletion track. The count is initialized when the user starts swiping on the backspace key, incremented based on how far they swipe forwards on the deletion track, and recorded when they release their finger.

histogram.name: InputMethod.VirtualKeyboard.WordsPerMinute
histogram.summary: The number of words typed per minute. Recorded when the virtual keyboard is closed.

histogram.name: InputMethod.VirtualKeyboard.WordsRestoredPerSwipe
histogram.summary: Chrome OS histogram that tracks the total number of words that were restored by swiping backwards on the deletion track. The count is initialized when the user starts swiping on the backspace key, incremented based on how far they swipe backwards on the deletion track, and recorded when they release their finger.

histogram.name: Installer.AttemptsCount.Total
histogram.summary: The number of update attempts until the update has been applied. This is reported every time the device has completed an update.

histogram.name: Installer.DevModeErrorCodes
histogram.summary: Errors from update_engine process when running in dev mode.

histogram.name: Installer.DownloadOverheadPercentage
histogram.summary: The overhead in downloading extra bytes due to errors/interruptions. Expressed as a percentage of the bytes that are actually needed to be downloaded for the update to be successful.

histogram.name: Installer.DownloadSourcesUsed
histogram.summary: The combinations of protocol and source server that were used to complete a successful update.

histogram.name: Installer.FullPayloadAttemptNumber
histogram.summary: The number of update attempts with a full update payload until the update has been applied. This is reported on every update attempt.

histogram.name: Installer.InstallDateProvisioningSource
histogram.summary: The source used to provision the install-date-days value sent to Omaha with every request. This is reported when OOBE completes (M34 or later) or when upgrading to a version with install-date-days support.

histogram.name: Installer.NormalErrorCodes
histogram.summary: Errors from update_engine process when running in normal mode.

histogram.name: Installer.OSAgeDays
histogram.summary: The age of the OS, defined as the age of the /etc/lsb-release file. This is reported on every update check but at most once a day.

histogram.name: Installer.PayloadAttemptNumber
histogram.summary: The number of update attempts until the update has been applied. This is reported on every update attempt.

histogram.name: Installer.PayloadFormat
histogram.summary: The type of update payload used to update the device. The difference between "Full" and "Forced Full" is that in the latter, the request sent to Omaha included a directive saying that a delta payload wasn't accepted. A "Full" payload is one where a delta payload was accepted but Omaha provided a full payload. This is reported every time the device has completed an update.

histogram.name: Installer.PowerwashCount
histogram.summary: The number of times a Chrome OS device has been powerwashed (factory reset) without subsequently going through recovery and/or changing the dev mode switch. Reported once after each powerwash.

histogram.name: Installer.RebootToNewPartitionAttempt
histogram.summary: The number of consecutive times a device has failed to boot an update that successfully applied. This metric is reported every time the firmware fails to boot the slot with the update and fell back to the slot it originally updated from.

histogram.name: Installer.Recovery.Count
histogram.summary:
The number of times the device has been recovered.
This is reported on reboot after a successful recovery. Cleared only when switching to dev mode or on factory reset.
This metric is specific to Chrome OS.

histogram.name: Installer.Recovery.Reason
histogram.summary:
The reason for entering recovery mode immediately prior to a recovery.
This is reported on reboot after a successful recovery.
This metric is specific to Chrome OS.

histogram.name: Installer.SuccessfulMBsDownloadedFrom
histogram.summary: Number of MBs downloaded from during an update that completed successfully.

histogram.name: Installer.TimeToRebootMinutes
histogram.summary: Wall-clock duration between when an update has successfully completed (and the user is presented with the "reboot arrow") and when the system has booted into the new update. This is reported every time the device is rebooted after an update has been applied.

histogram.name: Installer.TotalMBsDownloadedFrom
histogram.summary: Total number of MBs downloaded since the last successful update. This also includes all the bytes downloaded during any prior failed attempts.

histogram.name: Installer.UpdateDurationMinutes
histogram.summary: Absolute wall-clock time duration it took for the update to complete from the time an update first began. It includes not just the time the device was up, but also includes the time the device spent sleeping.

histogram.name: Installer.UpdateDurationUptimeMinutes
histogram.summary: Uptime duration it took for the update to complete from the time an update first began. It does not include the time the device spent sleeping, but it does include the uptime spent in waiting for the hourly update checks to happen.

histogram.name: Installer.UpdateNumReboots
histogram.summary: Number of times the device was rebooted by the user since an update began and until it completed successfully.

histogram.name: Installer.UpdatesAbandonedCount
histogram.summary: The number of update attempts that didn't complete because a newer update was detected during the update operation. This is reported every time the device has completed an update.

histogram.name: Installer.UpdatesAbandonedEventCount
histogram.summary: The number of consecutive different abandoned update payloads since the last successful update. This is reported every time an update payload is abandoned because a newer update payload is available.

histogram.name: Installer.UpdateTime
histogram.summary: The time in seconds it took to update a Chrome OS system -- from completing an update check to reboot pending.

histogram.name: Installer.UpdateURLSwitches
histogram.summary: Number of times the download URLs were switched due to failures.

histogram.name: InstallSigner.InvalidCount
histogram.obsolete: Removed 4/2015. It appears the code at some point changed to use the histogram name 'ExtensionInstallSigner.InvalidCount' and we forgot to update this histogram name.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: This is a count of the number of ids that we asked to be signed which the server response indicated were not in the webstore.

histogram.name: InstallSigner.InvalidSignature
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The extensions install signer got a well-formed result from the server but the signature check on it failed.

histogram.name: InstallVerifier.CallbackInvalidSignature
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The extension install verifier tried to get a new signature and received a response but it wasn't properly signed.

histogram.name: InstallVerifier.CallbackNoSignature
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The extension install verifier tried to get a new signature but was unable to (network error contacting the server, response from server was malformed, etc.).

histogram.name: InstallVerifier.CallbackValidSignature
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The extension install verifier got a new signature from the server that was valid.

histogram.name: InstallVerifier.InitGoodSignature
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The extension install verifier found a valid signature at startup, and this is a count of the number of signed ids it contained.

histogram.name: InstallVerifier.InitInvalidSignature
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The extension install verifier found a signature in the prefs at startup, and it parsed properly, but it was invalid (some ids may have been added/removed, could not verify it was signed with the correct private key, etc.).

histogram.name: InstallVerifier.InitNoSignature
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The extension install verifier did not find any signature in the prefs at startup.

histogram.name: InstallVerifier.InitUnparseablePref
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The extension install verifier found a signature in the prefs at startup, but it wasn't parseable (missing/wrong format of required keys, etc.).

histogram.name: InstallVerifier.SignatureFailedButNotEnforcing
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The extension install verifier would have disabled an extension but is not in enforcement mode.

histogram.name: InstanceID.DeleteToken.CompleteTime
histogram.summary: Length of time taken to complete the DeleteToken request successfully. If the request is retried multiple times, the length of time is counted for the last successful retry.

histogram.name: InstanceID.DeleteToken.RequestStatus
histogram.summary: Status code of the outcome of DeleteToken request.

histogram.name: InstanceID.DeleteToken.RetryCount
histogram.summary: Number of retries before DeleteToken succeeds.

histogram.name: InstanceID.Enabled
histogram.summary: Whether the Instance ID API is enabled. Checked when a chrome.instanceID function is called.

histogram.name: InstanceID.GeneratedNewID
histogram.summary: Counts the events when the GCM integration generates and registers a new instance ID with FCM. This histogram acts as a pure counter and only emits 'true' values. The goal of the histogram is to detect potential instance ID leaks. It's best interpreted in relation to the reported number of syncing/sync-transport users.

histogram.name: InstanceID.GetToken.CompleteTime
histogram.summary: Length of time taken to complete the GetToken request successfully. If the request is retried multiple times, the length of time is counted for the last successful retry.

histogram.name: InstanceID.GetToken.NetErrorCode
histogram.summary: Net error code from an Instance ID GetToken attempt. Recorded when URL loading completes.

histogram.name: InstanceID.GetToken.OptionsCount
histogram.summary: The number of options provided to the GetToken request. Recorded when the request has been received by the InstanceID instance.

histogram.name: InstanceID.GetToken.RequestStatus
histogram.summary: Status code of the outcome of GetToken request.

histogram.name: InstanceID.GetToken.RetryCount
histogram.summary: Number of retries before GetToken succeeds.

histogram.name: InstanceID.RestoredIDCount
histogram.summary: Number of Instance IDs restored from the persistent store at startup.

histogram.name: InstanceID.RestoredTokenCount
histogram.summary: Number of InstanceID tokens restored from the persistent store at startup.

histogram.name: Instant.InstantControllerEvent
histogram.obsolete: Removed 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Records various events of interest in the InstantController. E.g. When URLs are blacklisted.

histogram.name: Instant.SessionsStorageNamespace
histogram.obsolete: Removed 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: How often an Instant preview is committed onto a different tab than it was created from.

histogram.name: Instant.TimeToFirstShow
histogram.obsolete: Removed 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
