histogram.summary: The time between the first Omnibox interaction and when the Instant preview shows. If the instant preview was already showing when the user interacted with the omnibox, this histogram is not recorded.

histogram.name: InstantExtended.CacheableNTPLoad
histogram.summary: Records a histogram for how often the Cacheable NTP fails to load.

histogram.name: InstantExtended.FallbackToLocalOverlay
histogram.obsolete: Depcreated as of 10/2013. No longer relevant since the HTML overlay was abandoned.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Records the cause for falling back to a local overlay at the time of fallback.

histogram.name: InstantExtended.InstantNavigation
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 10/2013. This histogram is no longer relevant since the HTML overlay went away.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Records a histogram for instant extended (Local NTP and Online NTP) and non-extended navigations.

histogram.name: InstantExtended.NewOptInState
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Records, on startup, whether the user has chosen to opt-in to or opt-out of InstantExtended via chrome://flags.

histogram.name: InstantExtended.OptInState
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2013-06. As of m30 use InstantExtended.NewOptInState.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Records, on startup, whether the user has chosen to opt-in to or opt-out of InstantExtended via chrome://flags.

histogram.name: InstantExtended.PercentageMatchQuerytoQuery
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Records the number of matching characters at the start of the user's text as a percentage of average length between the old and new text when the user navigates from a search query to another search query.

histogram.name: InstantExtended.PercentageMatchQuerytoURL
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Records the number of matching characters at the start of the user's text as a percentage of average length between the old and new text when the user navigates from a search query to a url. Example: Accidental search for google.con, then navigation to google.com.

histogram.name: InstantExtended.PercentageMatchURLtoQuery
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Records the number of matching characters at the start of the user's text as a percentage of average length between the old and new text when the user navigates from a url to a search query.

histogram.name: InstantExtended.PercentageMatchURLtoURL
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Records the number of matching characters at the start of the user's text as a percentage of average length between the old and new text when the user navigates from a url to another url.

histogram.name: InstantExtended.PercentageMatchV2_QuerytoQuery
histogram.obsolete: Removed in August 2016 with the removal of query in the omnibox code.
histogram.summary: Records the number of matching characters at the start of the user's text as a percentage of average length between the old and new text when the user navigates from a search query to another search query.

histogram.name: InstantExtended.PercentageMatchV2_QuerytoURL
histogram.obsolete: Removed in August 2016 with the removal of query in the omnibox code.
histogram.summary: Records the number of matching characters at the start of the user's text as a percentage of average length between the old and new text when the user navigates from a search query to a url. Example: Accidental search for google.con, then navigation to google.com.

histogram.name: InstantExtended.PercentageMatchV2_URLtoQuery
histogram.obsolete: Removed in September 2016 because (i) this measurement was somewhat wrong, (ii) it's difficult to understand the data, and (iii) there are better ways to answer the same question. The measurement is somewhat wrong because reloads, link drops, and pasting URLs and pressing enter are all incorrectly logged to the URLtoQuery histogram, not the URLtoURL histogram. Futhermore, this histogram is difficult to analyze because it's not broken up by input length. Short URLs can by random chance have a sizable percentage match with a query. Finally, there are better source for data like this; ask mpearson for details.
histogram.summary: Records the number of matching characters at the start of the user's text as a percentage of average length between the old and new text when the user navigates from a url to a search query.

histogram.name: InstantExtended.PercentageMatchV2_URLtoURL
histogram.obsolete: Removed in September 2016 because (i) this measurement was somewhat wrong, and (ii) there are better ways to answer the same question. The measurement is somewhat wrong because reloads, link drops, and pasting URLs and pressing enter are all incorrectly logged to the URLtoQuery histogram, not the URLtoURL histogram. Furthermore, there are better source for data like this; ask mpearson for details.
histogram.summary: Records the number of matching characters at the start of the user's text as a percentage of average length between the old and new text when the user navigates from a url to another url.

histogram.name: InstantExtended.PrefValue
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2013-06. This preference has not been exposed or used for months, and we do not plan to use it in the future.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Records, on startup, the value of the "Allow your search engine to provide Instant result" preference setting for the first profile loaded.

histogram.name: InstantSearchClicks.PreviewScrollState
histogram.summary: Records the scroll state on the preview page when instant search clicks feature is triggered.

histogram.name: InstantSearchClicks.ReasonForSwap
histogram.summary: Records the reason that triggered the page swap when instant search clicks feature is triggered.

histogram.name: InstantSearchClicks.TimeInPreview
histogram.summary: The time spent by the user in preview page before swapping to original or navigating out of preview page.

histogram.name: InstantSearchClicks.TimeToSwap
histogram.summary: The time it took for swap to trigger for all swaps. The is the time between preview page load start to preview page swap with the original page.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.BluetoothAdvertisementRegistrationResult
histogram.summary:
Provides a breakdown of how many times each possible Bluetooth advertisement registration result occurs.
The bucket "Unknown result" indicates that the Bluetooth platform returned an unknown error code; if it has any counts, the client code should be changed to account for the new error code.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.BluetoothAdvertisementUnregistrationResult
histogram.summary:
Provides a breakdown of how many times each possible Bluetooth advertisement unregistration result occurs.
The bucket "Unknown result" indicates that the Bluetooth platform returned an unknown error code; if it has any counts, the client code should be changed to account for the new error code.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.BluetoothDiscoverySessionStarted
histogram.summary: Provides the success rate of starting a Bluetooth discovery session.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.BluetoothDiscoverySessionStopped
histogram.summary: Provides the success rate of stopping a Bluetooth discovery session.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.ConnectionToHostResult.Failure
histogram.summary:
Provides a top-level breakdown of the times a connection to a host has failed.
An "unknown error" is caused by the host returning an "unknown error" response code. Tethering timing out and client connection error are both broken down further in InstantTethering.ConnectionToHostResult.Failure.TetheringTimeout and InstantTethering.ConnectionToHostResult.Failure.ClientConnection, respectively.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.ConnectionToHostResult.Failure.ClientConnection
histogram.summary:
Breaks down the reasons why the client failed to connect to the hotspot, and the number of times they occurred.
Note that a client connection can be canceled by a user either via pressing the Disconnect button or beginning a connection attempt to another host. The "internal error" case is a general bucket that captures client errors we don't expect to be significant failure causes.
This histogram breaks down the "client connection error" count of InstantTethering.ConnectionToHostResult.Failure.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.ConnectionToHostResult.Failure.TetheringTimeout
histogram.summary:
Captures the number of times of whether or not first-time setup was required when tethering timed out.
Starting tethering on the host works like so: if first-time setup is required on the host, a first-time setup UI is shown on the host; once the user interacts with it, the provisioning app is called. If first-time setup is not required, then the provisioning app is directly called. To be clear: the provisioning app is always run.
There are two possible ways for tethering to time out: either the provisioning app flaked (crashed or hung, and never called back to Instant Tethering), or the user never interacted with the first-time setup UI flow (assuming first-time setup was required).
Because it's very unlikely for the provisioning app to flake, we can read the "was first-time setup" count as almost always indicative of the user not interacting with the first-time setup UI. We expect the "was not first-time setup" count to be low (because, as mentioned, it's very unlikely for the provisioning app to flake).
This histogram breaks down the "tethering timed out" count of InstantTethering.ConnectionToHostResult.Failure.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.ConnectionToHostResult.ProvisioningFailureRate
histogram.summary:
Provides a breakdown of the times a connection to a host was either unsuccessful due to provisioning failure (the carrier disallows tethering) or "other" (this is captured under InstantTethering.ConnectionToHostResult.SuccessRate.Background).
This metric captures the rough percentage of connections which are unsuccessful due to provisioning failure. It is separate from InstantTethering.ConnectionToHostResult.SuccessRate.Background and the metrics related to it because provisioning failure is neither truly a success nor an error.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.ConnectionToHostResult.SuccessRate
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019/02 in favor of MultiDevice.* equivalent.
histogram.summary:
Captures the count of successful and failed connection attempts.
This metric counts the top-level user action from beginning to connect, all the way through success or failure of the connection (excluding any programmatic retries within the connection attempt).
This metric provides an immediate understanding of the Instant Tethering connection success rate. The counts of failure are broken down in InstantTethering.ConnectionToHostResult.Failure.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.ConnectionToHostResult.SuccessRate.Background
histogram.summary:
Captures the count of successful and failed connection attempts.
This metric counts the top-level user action from beginning to connect, all the way through success or failure of the connection (excluding any programmatic retries within the connection attempt).
This metric provides an immediate understanding of the Instant Tethering connection success rate. The counts of failure are broken down in InstantTethering.ConnectionToHostResult.Failure.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.FeatureState
histogram.summary:
Breaks down the state of Instant Tethering during user sessions. All states, except for 'Enabled', indicate that the feature was not active. This histogram is emitted to each time a condition affecting Instant Tethering's state, e.g. the user enabling or disabling the feature, or Bluetooth becoming enabled or disabled.
Note: The "Screen locked" bucket is obsolete, and should not have any reports.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.GattConnectionAttempt.EffectiveSuccessRateWithRetries
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019/02 in favor of MultiDevice.* equivalent.
histogram.summary:
Captures the effective count of successful and failed GATT connection attempts, including retries. This means that if a GATT connection attempt fails but then succeeds on a future retry, this is counted as a single success. In the context of this metric, a failure represents a GATT failure in all retry attempts.
An individual attempt is considered successful if a GATT connection was created and authenticated successfully (i.e., the connection was ready for Instant Tethering to exchange protocol messages).

histogram.name: InstantTethering.GattConnectionAttempt.EffectiveSuccessRateWithRetries.Background
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019/02 in favor of MultiDevice.* equivalent.
histogram.summary:
Captures the effective count of successful and failed GATT connection attempts, including retries. This means that if a GATT connection attempt fails but then succeeds on a future retry, this is counted as a single success. In the context of this metric, a failure represents a GATT failure in all retry attempts.
An individual attempt is considered successful if a GATT connection was created and authenticated successfully (i.e., the connection was ready for Instant Tethering to exchange protocol messages).
This metric is the background advertisement analog of InstantTethering.GattConnectionAttempt.EffectiveSuccessRateWithRetries.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.GattConnectionAttempt.SuccessRate
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019/02 in favor of MultiDevice.* equivalent.
histogram.summary:
Captures the count of successful and failed GATT connection attempts.
An attempt is considered successful if a GATT connection was created and authenticated successfully (i.e., the connection was ready for Instant Tethering to exchange protocol messages).

histogram.name: InstantTethering.GattConnectionAttempt.SuccessRate.Background
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019/02 in favor of MultiDevice.* equivalent.
histogram.summary:
Captures the count of successful and failed GATT connection attempts.
An attempt is considered successful if a GATT connection was created and authenticated successfully (i.e., the connection was ready for Instant Tethering to exchange protocol messages).
This metric is the background advertisement analog of InstantTethering.GattConnectionAttempt.SuccessRate.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.HostScanBatchDuration
histogram.summary:
The duration of a batch of host scans. A batch is defined as a series of one or host scans separated by no more than one minute.
For example, if a scan occurs then stops and does not start back up again, the metric logged would be the amount of time taken for that scan. However, if a scan finishes and a new scan starts up again less than a minute after the previous scan finished, the metric logged would be the time difference between the start of the first scan and the finish of the second scan.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.HostScanResult
histogram.summary:
Provides a breakdown of the results of each host scan. This metric is recorded after each host scan.
Comparing the number of times no hosts were found against the other buckets can help determine how often a host scan is useful.
The sum of the buckets capturing that a notification was shown can be used as a baseline of comparison against InstantTethering.ConnectionToHostResult.ProvisioningFailureRate to roughly measure engagement with the notification.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.HotspotUsageDuration
histogram.summary: The duration of time between the start and end of a Tether connection. Timing starts when the device connects to the Wi-Fi hotspot and ends when the device becomes disconnected from the Wi-Fi hotspot.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.KeepAliveTickle.Result
histogram.summary: Captures the count of successful and failed attempts to send a 'KeepAliveTickle' message to the phone.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.MultiDeviceFeatureState
histogram.summary: Indicates the feature state of the Instant Tethering feature. This metric is emitted to at the creation of the user session (when the user logs in), and when feature state updates occur.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.NotificationInteractionType
histogram.summary: Counts the number of interactions a user has with each of the Instant Tethering notifications.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.Performance.AdvertisementToConnectionDuration
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019/02 in favor of MultiDevice.* equivalent.
histogram.summary:
The duration of time between when a client begins to scan and advertise, and connects to a host (before authentication).
This histogram only considers the time from when the client *sends* an advertisement, and not when the client *receives* an advertisement. See InstantTethering.Performance.StartScanToReceiveAdvertisementDuration and InstantTethering.Performance.ReceiveAdvertisementToConnectionDuration for histograms that consider when the client receives an advertisement.
This histogram is the foreground advertisement analog of InstantTethering.Performance.StartScanToConnectionDuration.Background.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.Performance.ConnectionToAuthenticationDuration
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019/02 in favor of MultiDevice.* equivalent.
histogram.summary: The duration of time between when a client connects to a host, and authenticates with that host.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.Performance.ConnectionToAuthenticationDuration.Background
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019/02 in favor of MultiDevice.* equivalent.
histogram.summary:
The duration of time between when a client connects to a host, and authenticates with that host.
This histogram is the background advertisement analog of InstantTethering.Performance.ConnectionToAuthenticationDuration.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.Performance.ConnectTetheringResponseDuration
histogram.summary: The duration of time between when a client authenticates with a host, and receives a ConnectTetheringResponse.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.Performance.ConnectToHostDuration
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019/02 in favor of MultiDevice.* equivalent.
histogram.summary: The duration of time it takes for the client to connect to the host, from the moment the user taps 'Connect', until the client connects to the host's hotspot. This does not include timeouts.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.Performance.ConnectToHostDuration.Background
histogram.summary: The duration of time it takes for the client to connect to the host, from the moment the user taps 'Connect', until the client connects to the host's hotspot. This does not include timeouts.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.Performance.ConnectToHotspotDuration
histogram.summary: The duration of time it takes for the client to connect to the provided hotspot after receiving a ConnectTetheringResponse. This does not include timeouts.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.Performance.DisconnectTetheringRequestDuration
histogram.summary: The duration of time it takes for the client to send off a DisconnectTetheringRequest.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.Performance.KeepAliveTickleResponseDuration
histogram.summary: The duration of time between when a client authenticates with a host, and receives a KeepAliveTickleResponse.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.Performance.ReceiveAdvertisementToConnectionDuration
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019/02 in favor of MultiDevice.* equivalent.
histogram.summary: The duration of time between when a client received a foreground advertisement from a host, and connected to that host (before authentication).

histogram.name: InstantTethering.Performance.ReceiveAdvertisementToConnectionDuration.Background
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019/02 in favor of MultiDevice.* equivalent.
histogram.summary:
The duration of time between when a client received a background advertisement from a host, and connected to that host (before authentication).
This histogram is the background advertisement analog of InstantTethering.Performance.ReceiveAdvertisementToConnectionDuration.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.Performance.StartScanToConnectionDuration.Background
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019/02 in favor of MultiDevice.* equivalent.
histogram.summary:
The duration of time between when a client begins to scan, and connects to a host (before authentication).
This histogram is the background advertisement analog of InstantTethering.Performance.AdvertisementToConnectionDuration.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.Performance.StartScanToReceiveAdvertisementDuration
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019/02 in favor of MultiDevice.* equivalent.
histogram.summary: The duration of time between when a client begins to scan and advertise, and receives a foreground advertisement from a host.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.Performance.StartScanToReceiveAdvertisementDuration.Background
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019/02 in favor of MultiDevice.* equivalent.
histogram.summary:
The duration of time between when a client begins to scan, and receives a background advertisement from a host.
This histogram is the background advertisement analog of InstantTethering.Performance.AdvertisementToConnectionDuration.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.Performance.TetherAvailabilityResponseDuration
histogram.summary: The duration of time between when a client authenticates with a host, and receives a TetherAvailabilityResponse.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.SessionCompletionReason
histogram.summary: Provides a breakdown of how each Tether session ended. A session begins when a device is first connected to a host's hotspot, and ends when the device is no longer connected to the hotspot. This metric is recorded at the end of each session.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.UserPreference.OnStartup
histogram.summary: Provides the count of the number of users who have the Instant Tethering toggle enabled vs. disabled on startup. This metric is logged each time a user logs into a Chromebook.

histogram.name: InstantTethering.UserPreference.OnToggle
histogram.summary: Provides the count of the number of times users have enabled or disabled the user preference for Mobile data. This metric is logged each time the value is toggled.

histogram.name: interstitial.authority_invalid_time
histogram.summary: The time between the SSL interstitial display and the user decision, which may be either accept or deny. This is only recorded for overridable SSL warnings with a CERT_AUTHORITY_INVALID warning. Timing begins when user first focuses on the page.

histogram.name: interstitial.captive_portal
histogram.summary: User action when the user is shown a captive portal error page.

histogram.name: interstitial.common_name_invalid_time
histogram.summary: The time between the SSL interstitial display and the user decision, which may be either accept or deny. This is only recorded for overridable SSL warnings with a CERT_COMMON_NAME_INVALID warning. Timing begins when user first focuses on the page.

histogram.name: interstitial.date_invalid_time
histogram.summary: The time between the SSL interstitial display and the user decision, which may be either accept or deny. This is only recorded for overridable SSL warnings with a CERT_DATE_INVALID warning. Timing begins when user first focuses on the page.

histogram.name: interstitial.decision
histogram.summary: User decision when presented with a security interstitial. As of M44, subresource interstitials were split into their own entries. As of M52, social_engineering_ads* and social_engineering_landing* interstitial reasons are split into their own entries from phishing*.

histogram.name: interstitial.decision.repeat_visit
histogram.summary: User decision when presented with a security interstitial, on a site that the user had previously visited. As of M44, subresource interstitials were split into their own entries. As of M52, social_engineering_ads* and social_engineering_landing* interstitial reasons are split into their own entries from phishing*.

histogram.name: interstitial.interaction
histogram.summary: User interactions with a security interstitial. As of M44, subresource interstitials were split into their own entries. As of M52, social_engineering_ads* and social_engineering_landing* interstitial reasons are split into their own entries from phishing*.

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl
histogram.obsolete: Replaced by interstitial.ssl_overridable.* and interstitial.ssl_nonoverridable.* in Jan 2015 (M42).
histogram.summary: User action when the user is shown a SSL interstitial. SHOW_ALL and MORE refer to the total number of SSL errors; all of the other numbers pertain to the number of actions related to SSL errors that are overridable. The counts do not sum to 100%; SHOW_ALL is a superset of SHOW_OVERRIDABLE, which in turn will be a supserset of the PROCEED/DONT_PROCEED variables. SHOW_UNDERSTAND is only being used by an experimental field trial. The interstitials due to captive portals are now recorded in the "SSLCaptivePortal".

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl.captive_portal
histogram.summary: Record possible states of captive portals. This histogram is emitted (possibly multiple times to different buckets) whenever a ssl interstitial page is displayed and captive portal detection is enabled. The captive portal technique forces a client on a network to see a special web page (usually for authentication purposes) before using the internet normally.

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl.cause.nonoverridable
histogram.summary: Possible non-attack causes of the non-overridable SSL interstitial. Many errors are not reported in this histogram and new errors may be added over time, therefore one should not look at the breakdown of this histogram (one bucket divided by the sum) because that will be inaccurate. Instead, one should look at each bucket count divided by the count of the ssl errors of that type. E.g. WWW mismatch is recorded only when the ssl error is CERT_COMMON_NAME_INVALID, so one should look at the bucket count of WWW mismatch divided by the bucket count of CERT_COMMON_NAME_INVALID in the histogram interstitial.ssl_error_type.

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl.cause.overridable
histogram.summary: Possible non-attack causes of the overridable SSL interstitial. Many errors are not reported in this histogram and new errors may be added over time, therefore one should not look at the breakdown of this histogram (one bucket divided by the sum) because that will be inaccurate. Instead, one should look at each bucket count divided by the count of the ssl errors of that type. E.g. WWW mismatch is recorded only when the ssl error is CERT_COMMON_NAME_INVALID, so one should look at the bucket count of WWW mismatch divided by the bucket count of CERT_COMMON_NAME_INVALID in the histogram interstitial.ssl_error_type.

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl.clockstate.build_time
histogram.summary: State of the system clock, as inferred from the build timestamp, when an SSL CERT_INVALID_DATE error is seen.

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl.clockstate.network
histogram.obsolete: Removed August 2016. Replaced with interstitial.ssl.clockstate.network2, which records reasons why network time might be unavailable.
histogram.summary: State of the system clock, relative to network time, when an SSL CERT_INVALID_DATE error is seen.

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl.clockstate.network2
histogram.obsolete: Removed October 2016. Due to a bug, data in this histogram is mislabelled and should be disregarded. Replaced with interstitial.ssl.clockstate.network3.
histogram.summary: State of the system clock, relative to network time, when an SSL CERT_INVALID_DATE error is seen.

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl.clockstate.network3
histogram.summary: State of the system clock, relative to network time, when an SSL CERT_INVALID_DATE error is seen.

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl.connection_type
histogram.summary: The histogram shows the connection types associated with SSL warnings. E.g., do more warnings occur on WiFi?

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl.did_user_revoke_decisions
histogram.summary: Specifies when a user enters the page info menu whether or not the user pressed the SSL decisions revoke button. This can only by done if the user is in the "Remember Certificate Error Decisions" experiment. This is logged when the page info UI is closed. Replaced by .did_user_revoke_decisions2 to keep data separate after changing functionality to only log when button was visible.

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl.did_user_revoke_decisions2
histogram.summary: Specifies when a user enters the page info menu whether or not the user pressed the SSL decisions revoke button. This is logged when the page info UI is closed, only if the button was visible in the page info.

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl.expiration_and_decision.nonoverridable
histogram.summary: Records when a user has made a decision to proceed on a nonoverridable SSL interstitial. Also records whether a prior decision had been made but the decision expired.

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl.expiration_and_decision.overridable
histogram.summary: Records when a user has made a decision to proceed on an overridable SSL interstitial. Also records whether a prior decision had been made but the decision expired.

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl.good_cert_seen
histogram.summary: Emitted when a good certificate is seen, specifying whether the user already gave an exception for a bad certificate for the same host.

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl.good_cert_seen_type_is_frame
histogram.summary: Whether the resource type for a request that succeeded with a good cert and revoked a certificate exception is for a frame (as opposed to subresource).

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl.severity_score.authority_invalid
histogram.summary: The likelihood of a |CERT_AUTHORITY_INVALID| error being an attack.

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl.severity_score.common_name_invalid
histogram.summary: The likelihood of a |CERT_COMMON_NAME_INVALID| error being an attack.

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl.severity_score.date_invalid
histogram.summary: The likelihood of a |CERT_DATE_INVALID| error being an attack.

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl.visited_site_after_warning
histogram.summary: Records when a user visits a site after clicking through a certificate warning.

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl_accept_time
histogram.summary: The time between the SSL interstitial display and the user decision, when the user accepts the SSL warning. This is only recorded for overridable SSL warnings. Timing begins when user first focuses on the page.

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl_error_handler
histogram.summary: The type of action that the SSL error handler takes.

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl_error_handler.cert_date_error_delay
histogram.summary: When handling a certificate date error, Chrome (under certain Variations Service experiments) will delay displaying the warning interstitial for up to 3 seconds while querying a network time service. This histogram records the amount of time for which the interstitial was delayed on this check. The histogram is recorded even when a time query is not sent (for example, because Chrome already had an up-to-date network timestamp that it could use, or because the client is not in the experimental group that enables network time queries).

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl_error_handler.superfish
histogram.summary: When encountering a certificate error, Chrome looks for the well-known Superfish certificate in the certificate chain and records this histogram as true if the Superfish certificate is present and false otherwise. The Superfish certificate is an indication that the user is vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks because of software installed on their computer.

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl_error_type
histogram.summary: The type of SSL error that the user encounters. This is recorded for all SSL warnings, regardless of whether they are overridable.

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl_nonoverridable.is_recurrent_error
histogram.summary: Recorded when a non-overridable SSL interstitial is shown. The value is true if the user has seen the same error multiple times (in which case a special message is added to the interstitial).

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl_nonoverridable.is_recurrent_error.ct_error
histogram.summary: Recorded when a non-overridable SSL interstitial is shown for a Certificate Transparency error. The value is true if the user has seen the same error multiple times (in which case a special message is added to the interstitial).

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl_overridable.is_recurrent_error
histogram.summary: Recorded when an overridable SSL interstitial is shown. The value is true if the user has seen the same error multiple times (in which case a special message is added to the interstitial).

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl_overridable.is_recurrent_error.ct_error
histogram.summary: Recorded when an overridable SSL interstitial is shown for a Certificate Transparency error. The value is true if the user has seen the same error multiple times (in which case a special message is added to the interstitial).

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl_recurrent_error.action
histogram.summary: Recorded whenever the user sees or proceeds through an SSL interstitial for an error that the user has seen multiple times (in which case a special message is added to the interstitial).

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl_recurrent_error.ct_error.action
histogram.summary: Recorded whenever the user sees or proceeds through an SSL interstitial for a Certificate Transparency error that the user has seen multiple times (in which case a special message is added to the interstitial).

histogram.name: interstitial.ssl_reject_time
histogram.summary: The time between the SSL interstitial display and the user decision, when the user rejects the SSL warning. This is only recorded for overridable SSL warnings. Timing begins when user first focuses on the page.

histogram.name: Invalidations.GCMUpstreamRequest
histogram.summary: Status of sending outgoing invalidation message through GCM upsteam.

histogram.name: Invalidations.NetworkChannel
histogram.summary: Network channel used for invalidations.

histogram.name: Invalidations.StatusChanged
histogram.summary: Status of the invalidation component. The metric is recorded on startup unless invalidations are disabled, plus when the state changes.

histogram.name: IOS.CanonicalURLResult
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2018-01. As of M65 use Mobile.CanonicalURLResult.
histogram.summary: The result of the operation to retrieve the page's canonical URL.

histogram.name: IOS.CommittedNavigationHasContext
histogram.summary: When a navigation is committed, it should have a non-null NavigationContext. This histogram counts the frequency of this invariant holding at the time when a new navigation is committed. It is used to verify the effectiveness of code fixes because this issue is not currently reproducible in testing (http://crbug.com/864769). True means a non-null NavigationContext is present.

histogram.name: IOS.CommittedURLMatchesCurrentItem
histogram.summary: When a navigation is committed, the web view URL is expected to match that of the current back-forward item. WKWebView seems to break this assumption at times. This histogram counts the frequency of this invariant violation at the time when a new navigation is committed. True means the URL matches the current back-forward item.

histogram.name: IOS.ContentExtension.DisplayCount
histogram.summary: The number of times the Content Extension was displayed since last Chrome foregrounding. Reported when Chrome is put in foregrounded.

histogram.name: IOS.ContentExtension.Index
histogram.summary: The index of the URL selected by the user in the Content Extension.

histogram.name: IOS.ContentRuleListProviderUpdateSuccess
histogram.summary: When the content rule list provider updates its lists, this can succeed or fail. Failure occurs when the rule lists are updated again before the first update finishes.

histogram.name: IOS.Cookies.GetCookiesForURLCallResult
histogram.summary: Recorded to indicate whether the GetCookiesForURL call found cookies or not when called on a specific system cookie store implementation. Most of the cookies fetching on iOS Chromium is done by the WKWebView and doesn't involve calling GetCookiesForURL. The method is only called when a request is made by the UrlRequestFetcher for cases that are not handled by WKWebView (eg. Downloads, Auto Suggestions and NTP tiles).

histogram.name: IOS.Cookies.GetCookiesForURLCallStoreType
histogram.summary: The system cookie store type used when GetCookiesForURL method is called. Most of the cookies fetching on iOS Chromium is done by the WKWebView and doesn't involve calling GetCookiesForURL. The method is only called when a request is made by the UrlRequestFetcher for cases that are not handled by WKWebView (eg. Downloads, Auto Suggestions and NTP tiles).

histogram.name: IOS.CredentialExtension.CopyPasswordCount
histogram.summary: The number of times the Credential Extension user copied a Password since last Chrome foregrounding. Reported when Chrome is put in foreground.

histogram.name: IOS.CredentialExtension.CopyURLCount
histogram.summary: The number of times the Credential Extension user copied a URL since last Chrome foregrounding. Reported when Chrome is put in foreground.

histogram.name: IOS.CredentialExtension.CopyUsernameCount
histogram.summary: The number of times the Credential Extension user copied a Username since last Chrome foregrounding. Reported when Chrome is put in foreground.

histogram.name: IOS.CredentialExtension.DisplayCount
histogram.summary: The number of times the Credential Extension was displayed since last Chrome foregrounding. Reported when Chrome is put in foreground.

histogram.name: IOS.CredentialExtension.PasswordUseCount
histogram.summary: The number of times the Credential Extension user selected for a Password since last Chrome foregrounding. Reported when Chrome is put in foreground.

histogram.name: IOS.CredentialExtension.QuickPasswordUseCount
histogram.summary: The number of times the Credential Extension selected a Password without user intervention since last Chrome foregrounding. Reported when Chrome is put in foreground.

histogram.name: IOS.CredentialExtension.ReauthCount
histogram.summary: The number of times the Credential Extension needed user reauthentication since last Chrome foregrounding. Reported when Chrome is put in foreground.

histogram.name: IOS.CredentialExtension.SearchCount
histogram.summary: The number of times the Credential Extension user searched for a Password since last Chrome foregrounding. Reported when Chrome is put in foreground.

histogram.name: IOS.CredentialExtension.ShowPasswordCount
histogram.summary: The number of times the Credential Extension user unobfuscated a Password since last Chrome foregrounding. Reported when Chrome is put in foreground.

histogram.name: IOS.CriticalActionBackgroundTimeRemaining
histogram.summary: Recorded when app is running in the background and posted a background task. This time is obtained by calling UIApplication.backgroundTimeRemaining. When backgroundTimeRemaining reaches 0 and there is at least one running background task, then iOS terminates the app instead of suspending it. The lower recorded value is, the higher chances that posted task will keep running when backgroundTimeRemaining reaches 0, causing the app termination. When app is relaunched after termination the app starts cold which is slower and does not fully restore the prior UI state, so terminations are worse than suspensions.

histogram.name: IOS.CRWWKNavigationStatesRemoveOldPending
histogram.summary: Workaround for a WKWebView bug where WKNavigations can leak, leaving a permanent pending URL, thus breaking the omnibox. While it is possible for navigations to finish out-of-order, it's an edge case that should be handled gracefully, as last committed will appear in the omnibox instead of the pending URL. This metric records whether there are old navigations to clean up each time a navigation finishes. See crbug.com/1010765 for details.

histogram.name: IOS.Dialogs.JavaScriptDialogClosed
histogram.summary: Tracks the way JavaScript dialogs are closed on iOS.

histogram.name: IOS.DragAndDrop.DragContent
histogram.summary: The type of content that the user is dragging into Chrome. Because a drag can contain multiple types of objects, multiple DragContent values may be recorded for a single drag event.

histogram.name: IOS.EnterTabSwitcherSnapshotResult
histogram.summary: Tracks the result of snapshotting when the user enters the tab switcher. Recorded whenever the user enters the tab switcher.

histogram.name: IOS.FinishedNavigationHasContext
histogram.summary: When a navigation is finished, it should have a non-null NavigationContext. This histogram counts the frequency of this invariant holding at the time when a new navigation is finished. It is used to verify the effectiveness of code fixes because this issue is not currently reproducible in testing (http://crbug.com/864769). True means a non-null NavigationContext is present.

histogram.name: IOS.FinishedNavigationHasItem
histogram.summary: When a navigation is finished, it should have a non-null NavigationItem. This histogram counts the frequency of this invariant holding at the time when a new navigation is finished. It is used to verify the effectiveness of code fixes because this issue is not currently reproducible in testing (http://crbug.com/864769). True means a non-null NavigationItem is present.

histogram.name: IOS.FinishedURLMatchesCurrentItem
histogram.summary: When a navigation is finished, the web view URL is expected to match that of the current back-forward item. WKWebView seems to break this assumption at times. This histogram counts the frequency of this invariant violation at the time when a new navigation is finished. True means the URL matches the current back-forward item.

histogram.name: IOS.FormInputAccessory.ExecuteFormAssistActionException
histogram.summary: Reports exceptions when trying to send a form input accessory action to the virtual keyboard.

histogram.name: IOS.Handoff.Origin
histogram.summary: This event is recorded each time a Handoff is received by Chrome on iOS. The enumeration indicates the source of the Handoff.

histogram.name: IOS.Inspect.Console
histogram.summary: Recorded when the iOS JavaScript Console logging is manually enabled or manually disabled.

histogram.name: IOS.IPHBubbleDismissalReason
histogram.summary: Tracks the reason for why the In Product Help bubble was dismissed.

histogram.name: IOS.MailtoURLRewritten
histogram.summary: Counts the times when a mailto: URL is tapped by user and whether the URL has been rewritten for an available native Mail client app or not.

histogram.name: IOS.MainThreadFreezeDetection.RecoveredAfter
histogram.summary: The time during which main thread was not responding. 0 if application was killed before recovering. Logged after an action posted on the main thread is not executed immediately. The histogram is sent when the task is finally executed or on next application startup.

histogram.name: IOS.MultiWindow.OpenInNewWindow
histogram.summary: The source of "Open in new Window" user requests. Recorded when a newly created window is added to a session.

histogram.name: IOS.NTP.Impression
histogram.summary: The type of NTP impressions on iOS, split by type of suggestions shown (local vs remote vs remote_collapsed).

histogram.name: IOS.OpenIn.DownloadResult
histogram.summary: The result of the download operation done when the user taps "open in" button to open a file by a different application.

histogram.name: IOS.PageLoadCount.Counts
histogram.summary: The number of navigation started events by navigation type.

histogram.name: IOS.PageLoadCount.LoadingStarted
histogram.summary: The "true" value of this boolean histogram counts the number of page loading started events. The "false" value will never be seen.

histogram.name: IOS.PageLoadCountMigration.Counts
histogram.summary: Counts different types of navigation and loading events that are relevant to counting page loads.

histogram.name: IOS.PageLoadedSnapshotResult
histogram.summary: Tracks the result of snapshotting when the page has been loaded.

histogram.name: IOS.PageLoadTiming.OmnibarToPageLoaded
histogram.summary: Measures the time from the end of user input in the omnibox to when the page is fully loaded. Only measures page loads initiated by user typing a URL or selecting a suggested entry from the URL bar.

histogram.name: IOS.ProviderIsValidOnShutdown
histogram.summary: Recorded when the user quits the app. True if ChromeBrowserProvider is a non-null pointer. Chrome for iOS has frequent crashes during shutdown caused by dereferencing null or dangling ChromeBrowserProvider pointer (crbug.com/983504). ChromeBrowserProvider can only be null if iOS did not call application:didFinishLaunchingWithOptions: callback. This metric should help to understand if ChromeBrowserProvider can even be null after app is launched.

histogram.name: IOS.RepeatedExternalAppPromptResponse
histogram.summary: The user reaction to the prompt that appears when a website tries to open an external application repeatedly.

histogram.name: IOS.RestoreNavigationItemCount
histogram.summary: [iOS] The number of items Navigation Manager was requested to restore. 100 is logged when the number of navigation items is greater than 100. This is just a requested count and actual number of restored items can be smaller. Restoration is triggered in the following cases (the list is not exhaustive): app cold start, remote tab was open, cookie were cleared, recently closed tab was restored.

histogram.name: IOS.RestoreNavigationTime
histogram.summary: [iOS] Time spent on restoring committed Navigation Manager's items. Recorded once per restoration, as a cumulative time across all items. Restoration is triggered in the following cases (the list is not exhaustive): app cold start, remote tab was open, cookies were cleared, recently closed tab was restored. This metric will be used for monitoring session restoration performance which relies on system's WKWebView.

histogram.name: IOS.SearchExtension.Action
histogram.summary: The action selected by the user in the Search Extension.

histogram.name: IOS.SearchExtension.DisplayCount
histogram.summary: The number of times the Search Extension was displayed since last Chrome foregrounding. Reported when Chrome is put in foreground.

histogram.name: IOS.ShareExtension.ReceivedEntriesCount
histogram.summary: The number of items received simultaneously in Chrome from the IOS share extension.

histogram.name: IOS.ShareExtension.ReceivedEntry
histogram.summary: Type of the item received from the iOS share extension.

histogram.name: IOS.ShareExtension.ReceivedEntryDelay
histogram.summary: The delay in milliseconds between the item creation in the extension and its processing in Chrome.

histogram.name: IOS.ShareExtension.Source
histogram.summary: The source application that sent the external command to Chrome.

histogram.name: IOS.SharePageLatency
histogram.summary: The latency in milliseconds between the start of the Share Page operation and when the UI is ready to be presented.

histogram.name: IOS.ShowTabSwitcherSnapshotResult
histogram.summary: Tracks the result of snapshotting when the user enters the tab switcher. Recorded whenever the user enters the tab switcher.

histogram.name: IOS.Spotlight.Action
histogram.summary: The Spotlight Action pressed by the user.

histogram.name: IOS.Spotlight.Availability
histogram.summary: Tracks the availability of the Spotlight indexation on the device. It is logged once at each cold start. Note: Spotlight may be available on the device but disabled by the user.

histogram.name: IOS.Spotlight.BookmarksIndexingDuration
histogram.summary: Time spent in Spotlight initial indexation of bookmarks.

histogram.name: IOS.Spotlight.BookmarksInitialIndexSize
histogram.summary: Number of bookmarks indexed during initial indexation.

histogram.name: IOS.Spotlight.Origin
histogram.summary: This event is recorded each time a Chrome Spotlight entry is selected by the user. The enumeration indicates the nature of the Spotlight entry.

histogram.name: IOS.StoreKit.ITunesURLsHandlingResult
histogram.summary: The result of StoreKit handling for a specific iTunes URL. Not Handled will be logged when the LinksHandler didn't handle a specific URL on purpose (not supported), and Failure will be logged when a URL that should have been handled wasn't handled.

histogram.name: IOS.StoreKitLoadedSuccessfully
histogram.summary: Whether the StoreKit loaded the required iTunes product successfully or not.

histogram.name: IOS.TabGridMediator.DidDetachNilWebStateList
histogram.summary: WebStateList should be guaranteed to be non-nil during a WebStateListObserver callback. This histogram counts the frequency of this invariant holding when webStateList:DidDetachWebState: is invoked in TabGridMediator. Violations of this invariant is the cause of real-world crashes (https://crbug.com/877792), which were not currently reproducible in testing. This histogram is used to retain visibility of the severity of this issue, while preventing the crashes with a no-op.

histogram.name: IOS.TabGridMediator.GetActiveTabIDNilWebStateList
histogram.summary: WebStateList should be guaranteed to be non-nil during a WebStateListObserver callback. This histogram counts the frequency of this invariant holding when GetActiveTabID() is invoked in TabGridMediator. Violations of this invariant is the cause of real-world crashes (https://crbug.com/877792), which were not currently reproducible in testing. This histogram is used to retain visibility of the severity of this issue, while preventing the crashes with a no-op.

histogram.name: IOS.TabSwitcher.PageChangeInteraction
histogram.summary: The UI interaction by which the user changed the visible page in the tab switcher.

histogram.name: IOS.Web.URLDidChangeToEmptyURL
histogram.summary: A boolean that is used to indicate that the URL property of the WKWebView has changed to a nil or empty URL. Recorded on the KVO of the URL of the WKWebView.

histogram.name: IOS.WKWebViewClobberedHistory
histogram.summary: Measures the number of main frame navigations in slim navigation manager that are affected by a WKWebView bug that corrupts back/forward history (http://crbug.com/887497). This is recorded during WKWebView's decidePolicyForNavigationAction callback. True means the bug is triggered. False means the bug is not triggered.

histogram.name: IOS.WKWebViewFinishBeforeCommit
histogram.summary: WKWebView should notify navigation commit before navigation finish, but exceptions have been observed. This histogram counts the frequency of this invariant violation at the time when a new navigation is finished. True means navigation finish callback happened before navigation commit.

histogram.name: IOS.WKWebViewLinkPreview
histogram.summary: Records the user actions related to the WKWebView 3D touch link preview API.

histogram.name: IOS.WKWebViewStartProvisionalNavigationWithEmptyURL
histogram.summary: On iOS9, didStartProvisionalNavigation may be called with empty URL. However in didCommitNavigation the URL will be about:blank. This historgram checks if this still happens in later iOS versions, and records it when it happens.

histogram.name: IPC.AttachmentBrokerPrivileged.BrokerAttachmentError
histogram.summary: Errors that the privileged attachment broker encounters while trying to broker attachments.

histogram.name: IPC.AttachmentBrokerUnprivileged.BrokerAttachmentError
histogram.summary: Errors that an unprivileged attachment broker encounters while trying to broker attachments.

histogram.name: IsolatedPrerender.Prefetch.Mainframe.BodyLength
histogram.summary: Records the response body length as cached in memory encountered when isolated prefetching a mainframe HTML resource from the Google Search Result Page. Recorded for non-network error prefetches only.

histogram.name: IsolatedPrerender.Prefetch.Mainframe.ConnectTime
histogram.summary: Records the time taken to establish a socket connection when isolated prefetching a mainframe HTML resource from the Google Search Result Page. Recorded for non-network error prefetches only.

histogram.name: IsolatedPrerender.Prefetch.Mainframe.NetError
histogram.summary: Records the Net Error encountered when isolated prefetching a mainframe HTML resource from the Google Search Result Page. Recorded for every completed prefetch.

histogram.name: IsolatedPrerender.Prefetch.Mainframe.RespCode
histogram.summary: Records the HTTP response code encountered isolated prefetching a mainframe HTML resource from the Google Search Result Page. Recorded for non-network error prefetches only. Redirects are not included here.

histogram.name: IsolatedPrerender.Prefetch.Mainframe.TotalRedirects
histogram.summary: Records the total number of redirects encountered while doing all the prefetches on an eligible Google Search Result page. Only recorded when at least one prefetch was attempted.

histogram.name: IsolatedPrerender.Prefetch.Mainframe.TotalTime
histogram.summary: Records the total time taken when isolated prefetching a mainframe HTML resource from the Google Search Result Page. Recorded for successful non-network error prefetches only.

histogram.name: JSDialogs.CharacterCount
histogram.summary: The count of the number of characters in JavaScript dialog messages.

histogram.name: JSDialogs.CharacterCountUserSuppressed
histogram.summary: The count of the number of characters in JavaScript dialog messages that were suppressed by the user. Compare with JSDialogs.CountOfJSDialogMessageCharacters.

histogram.name: JSDialogs.CountOfJSDialogMessageCharacters
histogram.summary: The count of the number of characters in JavaScript dialog messages.

histogram.name: JSDialogs.CountOfJSDialogMessageNewlines
histogram.summary: The count of the number of newlines in JavaScript dialog messages. (This does not count breaks inserted by the UI toolkit in wrapping the messages.)

histogram.name: JSDialogs.CountOfOnBeforeUnloadMessageCharacters
histogram.summary: The count of the number of characters in onbeforeunload messages.

histogram.name: JSDialogs.CountOfOnBeforeUnloadMessageNewlines
histogram.summary: The count of the number of newlines in onbeforeunload messages. (This does not count breaks inserted by the UI toolkit in wrapping the messages.)

histogram.name: JSDialogs.DismissalCause
histogram.summary: The cause of dismissal of JavaScript dialogs.

histogram.name: JSDialogs.FineTiming.TimeBetweenDialogClosedAndNextDialogCreated
histogram.summary: Fine-grained (in msec) time between closing a Javascript dialog and opening another, to track very frequent dialogs.

histogram.name: JSDialogs.FineTiming.TimeBetweenDialogCreatedAndNextDialogCreated
histogram.summary: Fine-grained (in msec) time between opening a Javascript dialog and opening another, to track very frequent dialogs.

histogram.name: JSDialogs.FineTiming.TimeBetweenDialogCreatedAndSameDialogClosed
histogram.summary: Fine-grained (in msec) time between opening a Javascript dialog and closing it, to track very short-lived dialogs.

histogram.name: JSDialogs.IsForemost
histogram.summary: For dialogs, whether or not they were spawned by a tab that was foremost.

histogram.name: JSDialogs.OnBeforeUnloadStayVsLeave
histogram.summary: For onbeforeunload dialogs, the user's choice between staying on the page and leaving it.

histogram.name: JSDialogs.OriginRelationship
histogram.summary: For dialogs, the relationship of the origins of the main frame vs the alerting frame.

histogram.name: JSDialogs.Scheme
histogram.summary: The scheme of the URL showing a JavaScript dialog.

histogram.name: JSDialogs.SiteEngagementOfBeforeUnload
histogram.summary: The site engagement values of sites showing onbeforeunload dialogs. Logged once per dialog, whether shown or suppressed.

histogram.name: JSDialogs.SiteEngagementOfDialogs
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2016-10. Site engagement needed to be measured in small buckets anyway so it was never high-resolution enough for use.
histogram.summary: The site engagement values of sites showing dialogs. Logged once per dialog, whether shown or suppressed.

histogram.name: Kerberos.AcquireKerberosTgtTime
histogram.summary: Time in milliseconds to acquire a Kerberos ticket. The value is recorded no matter if the operation was successful or not.

histogram.name: Kerberos.DailyActiveUsers
histogram.summary: Type of the Chrome OS login user (primary user) who requests Kerberos tickets. Reported at most once a day by the Kerberos daemon when a Kebreros ticket is acquired or Kerberos files are requested, which happens e.g. on Chrome startup.

histogram.name: Kerberos.EncryptionTypesAcquireKerberosTgt
histogram.summary: The Kerberos encryption types used while succesfully creating or refreshing a Kerberos TGT. This value is derived from the Kerberos configuration 'enctypes' fields.

histogram.name: Kerberos.NumberOfAccounts.Managed
histogram.summary: Number of managed Kerberos accounts owned by the current Chrome OS user. A managed account is an account that got added via the KerberosAccounts policy. Reported at most once a day similar to Kerberos.DailyActiveUsers.

histogram.name: Kerberos.NumberOfAccounts.RememberedPassword
histogram.summary: Number of Kerberos accounts owned by the current Chrome OS user that use the 'Remember password' feature, i.e. the account password is stored by the Kerberos daemon. Reported at most once a day similar to Kerberos.DailyActiveUsers.

histogram.name: Kerberos.NumberOfAccounts.Total
histogram.summary: Total number of Kerberos accounts owned by the current Chrome OS user. Reported at most once a day similar to Kerberos.DailyActiveUsers.

histogram.name: Kerberos.NumberOfAccounts.Unmanaged
histogram.summary: Number of unmanaged Kerberos accounts owned by the current Chrome OS user. An unmanaged account is an account that was added manually by the user. Reported at most once a day similar to Kerberos.DailyActiveUsers.

histogram.name: Kerberos.NumberOfAccounts.UseLoginPassword
histogram.summary: Number of Kerberos accounts owned by the current Chrome OS user that use the login password for authentication. These accounts were added via the KerberosAccounts policy, using the '${PASSWORD}' placeholder as password. Reported at most once a day similar to Kerberos.DailyActiveUsers.

histogram.name: Kerberos.Result.AcquireKerberosTgt
histogram.summary: Result from an attempt to acquire a Kerberos ticket. This happens whenever a new ticket is added or an existing ticket is reauthenticated.

histogram.name: Kerberos.Result.AddAccount
histogram.summary: Result from an attempt to add a new Kerberos account, either through the Kerberos Tickets settings page or via the KerberosAccounts policy.

histogram.name: Kerberos.Result.ClearAccounts
histogram.summary: Result from an attempt to clear Kerberos accounts, usually through toggling Kerberos related policies. For instance, disabling the KerberosEnabled policy clears all accounts.

histogram.name: Kerberos.Result.GetKerberosFiles
histogram.summary: Result from an attempt to retrieve a Kerberos ticket from the Kerberos daemon. Systems using Kerberos (Chrome, SMB file shares) perform this action during startup and when they get notified that the files changed.

histogram.name: Kerberos.Result.ListAccounts
histogram.summary: Result from an attempt to list Kerberos accounts, usually through opening the Kerberos Tickets settings page or making changes to accounts.

histogram.name: Kerberos.Result.RemoveAccount
histogram.summary: Result from an attempt to remove a new Kerberos account through the Kerberos Tickets settings page.

histogram.name: Kerberos.Result.SetConfig
histogram.summary: Result from an attempt to set Kerberos configuration. This happens right before acquiring a Kerberos ticket.

histogram.name: Kerberos.Result.ValidateConfig
histogram.summary: Result from an attempt to validate Kerberos configuration. This happens when the user tries to set configuration in the Advanced section of the dialog to add new Kerberos tickets.

histogram.name: Kerberos.ValidateConfigErrorCode
histogram.summary: Error code from an attempt validate Kerberos configuration. Contains more specific information than KerberosErrorType about the result of the validation.

histogram.name: Keyboard.KeystrokeDeltas
histogram.obsolete: Removed 07/2016 in crbug.com/631204 with KeyboardUmaEventFilter removal.
histogram.summary: The time between keystrokes in Aura text fields. The only keystrokes that are measured are ones that produce a printable character and are not over 5 seconds apart.

histogram.name: Keyboard.Shortcuts.CrosSearchKeyDelay
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019-07. Search key shortcut analysis is done.
histogram.summary: The time between a user pressing the Chrome OS "Search" key and the second key being pressed down to active a shortcut. e.g. Search + L would record the time between Search key down and L key down. If the user pressed multiple keys after Search, we only record the first key down.

histogram.name: Keyboard.ShortcutViewer.SearchUpdateTime
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019-06. Shortcut viewer has been in production for many milestones and its performance is adequate.
histogram.summary: Time delay to make the visual update in response to a search-query. Note that this does *not* include the initial delay to trigger the update after the initial keystroke to change the search-query string.

histogram.name: Keyboard.ShortcutViewer.SearchUpdateTimeVisual
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019-06. Shortcut viewer has been in production for many milestones and its performance is adequate.
histogram.summary: Time delay for the visual update to be visible on screen in response to a search-query. Note that this does *not* include the initial delay to trigger the update after the initial keystroke to change the search-query string.

histogram.name: Keyboard.ShortcutViewer.StartupTime
histogram.summary: Time delay between the user gesture that triggered the keyboard shortcut viewer dialog (e.g. pressing Ctrl-Alt-/) and the dialog widget being shown, including layout time for the views::Views.

histogram.name: KeyboardAccessory.AccessoryActionImpression
histogram.summary: Android only. Records whenever users faces an action in the accessory bar or one of its sheets.

histogram.name: KeyboardAccessory.AccessoryActionSelected
histogram.summary: Android only. Records whenever users select an action in the accessory bar or one of its sheets.

histogram.name: KeyboardAccessory.AccessoryBarShown
histogram.summary: Android only. Records how often users encounter the keyboard accessory bar. Its buckets show the contents when it came up. Every bucket may be logged up to one time per impression.

histogram.name: KeyboardAccessory.AccessorySheetSuggestionCount
histogram.summary: Android only. Records how many suggestions a user faced when opening a sheet. The base histogram counts impressions across all sheets.

histogram.name: KeyboardAccessory.AccessorySheetSuggestionsSelected
histogram.summary: Android only. Records which type of suggestion was selected from an open sheet.

histogram.name: KeyboardAccessory.AccessorySheetToggleClicked
histogram.summary: Android only. Records how often the user clicks on a certain toggle when opening an accessory sheet together with the state the toggle was in before clicking.

histogram.name: KeyboardAccessory.AccessorySheetToggleImpression
histogram.summary: Android only. Records how often the user sees a certain toggle when opening an accessory sheet together with the state the toggle was in.

histogram.name: KeyboardAccessory.AccessorySheetTriggered
histogram.summary: Android only. Records how often the bottom sheet was opened or closed by a user and the overall count of closures. Closing buckets may be logged up to one time per trigger. There are suffixes for each specific sheet type.

histogram.name: KeyboardAccessory.DisabledSavingAccessoryImpressions
histogram.summary:
Android only. Records the number of times that the keyboard accessory was shown on a form for which saving is disabled (with a crossed-out key icon). Recorded when the user focuses the password field.
Note: Only the "Shown" bucket should contain samples.

histogram.name: KeyboardAccessory.GeneratedPasswordDialog
histogram.summary: Android only. Records the interaction with the password generation dialog.

histogram.name: KeyboardAccessory.GenerationDialogChoice
histogram.summary: Android only. Records the interactions with the password generation dialog for automatic generation.

histogram.name: Kiosk.Launch.CryptohomeFailure
histogram.summary: Tracks cryptohome failure during kiosk launch.

histogram.name: Kiosk.Launch.Error
histogram.summary: Tracks kiosk launch errors.

histogram.name: Kiosk.LaunchType
histogram.summary: Records kiosk launches, whether it is an enterprise or consumer kiosk and whether it is an auto launch or manual launch.

histogram.name: KioskNextHome.Bridge.Action
histogram.summary: Records usage of methods in KioskNextHome Mojo bridge. Recorded on every method invocation.

histogram.name: KioskNextHome.Bridge.GetAndroidIdSuccess
histogram.summary: Records the result (success or failure) of every call from KioskNextHome bridge to get the Android id.

histogram.name: KioskNextHome.Bridge.LaunchIntentResult
histogram.summary: Records the result (success or error reason) of every call from KioskNextHome bridge to launch an intent.

histogram.name: KioskNextHome.StateTransition.AnimationSmoothness
histogram.summary: Relative smoothness of animations of KioskNext Home state transitions. 100% represents ideally smooth 60 frames per second. 50% represents only 30 frames per second is achieved during the animations. 0% should not happen. This metric is recorded exactly once when the user switches states of the KioskNext Home.

histogram.name: KioskNextShell.EnabledState
histogram.summary: Records when KioskNextShell becomes enabled or disabled.

histogram.name: KioskNextShell.Launched
histogram.summary: Records launches of KioskNextShell. Only true is recorded.

histogram.name: LanguageSettings.Actions
histogram.summary: The actions taken on languages settings, recorded every time they happen. This histogram will record every single event that happens separately.

histogram.name: LanguageSettings.PageImpression
histogram.summary: The type of panes which language settings loads.

histogram.name: LanguageUsage.AcceptLanguage
histogram.summary: Accept languages.

histogram.name: LanguageUsage.ApplicationLanguage
histogram.summary: Application languages used for UI.

histogram.name: Launch.FlagsAtStartup
histogram.summary: Logs which Chrome flags from about:flags were active on start up. Note that this flag is not really used on Chrome OS; on that platform, look at Login.CustomFlags instead. Android WebView supports flags differently than most platforms, see https://chromium.googlesource.com/chromium/src/+/HEAD/android_webview/docs/developer-ui.md for details.

histogram.name: Launch.HomeScreen
histogram.summary: Records how Chrome handled a launch from an Android Home screen shortcut.

histogram.name: Launch.HomeScreenSource
histogram.summary:
Records the source of an Android homescreen launch intent used to launch Chrome.
The Menu item bucket is deprecated as of M57, and will only contain records for shortcuts added to the homescreen until M56.
For M57+, the Menu item (standalone) bucket contains all launch intents originating from the add to homescreen menu item that launch to standalone mode and *are not* WebAPKs. These are sites which do not meet the PWA eligibility criteria, but have an app manifest that specifies either standalone or fullscreen display. This bucket also contains PWA *and* non-PWA intents that were added to the homescreen *prior* to M57, as we cannot distinguish between them after they are added.
For M57+, the Menu item (shortcut) bucket contains all launch intents originating from the add to homescreen menu item that launch to a browser tab, regardless of when the intent was added.

histogram.name: Launch.IntentFlags
histogram.summary: Records flags (and combinations of flags) that were attached to the Intent that started ChromeLauncherActivity.

histogram.name: Launch.InvalidIntent
histogram.summary: Records whether or not an invalid Android Intent was fired to launch Chrome. (http://crbug.com/445136)

histogram.name: Launch.MashService
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019-05 -- removed as part of removing mash code
histogram.summary: Records when a mojo ash UI service is started, for example the mojo app version of the keyboard shortcut viewer.

histogram.name: Launch.Modes
histogram.summary: The different ways Chrome is launched.

histogram.name: Launch.WebAppDisplayMode
histogram.summary: Records the display mode (as defined in the Web App Manifest spec) at the launch of the Added to Home screen or installed Web App (including WebAPKs).

histogram.name: Layout.MicroSecondsPerComplexText
histogram.summary:
For layouts that considered at least 100 nodes, at least half of which were complex text fragments, the average number of microseconds spent laying out each node.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Layout.MicroSecondsPerFloat
histogram.summary:
For layouts that considered at least 100 nodes, at least half of which were floats, the average number of microseconds spent laying out each node.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Layout.MicroSecondsPerNode
histogram.summary:
For layouts that considered at least 100 nodes, the average number of microseconds spent laying out each node.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Layout.MicroSecondsPerPositioned
histogram.summary:
For layouts that considered at least 100 nodes, at least half of which were fixed or absolute positioned, the average number of microseconds spent laying out each node.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Layout.MicroSecondsPerSimpleText
histogram.summary:
For layouts that considered at least 100 nodes, at least half of which were simple text fragments, the average number of microseconds spent laying out each node.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Layout.MicroSecondsPerTD
histogram.summary:
For layouts that considered at least 100 nodes, at least half of which were table cells, the average number of microseconds spent laying out each node.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Layout.ScrollAnchor.AdjustedScrollOffset
histogram.summary: Records the number of times ScrollAnchor adjusts the scroll offset.

histogram.name: Layout.ScrollAnchor.RestorationStatus
histogram.summary: Records the status of attempts to restore a serialized scroll anchor.

histogram.name: Layout.ScrollAnchor.SerializedAnchorSelectorLength
histogram.summary: The number of characters in the selector computed for a serialized scroll anchor. Recorded when a scroll anchor is serialized.

histogram.name: Layout.ScrollAnchor.SuppressedBySanaclap
histogram.summary: Records the number of times scroll anchoring is suppressed because of the SANACLAP bit being set on an ancestor (see bit.ly/sanaclap).

histogram.name: Layout.ScrollAnchor.TimeToComputeAnchorNodeSelector
histogram.summary:
The time it took to compute the selector of a serialized anchor element.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Layout.ScrollAnchor.TimeToFindAnchor
histogram.summary:
The time it took to find a suitable anchor element.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Layout.ScrollAnchor.TimeToRestoreAnchor
histogram.summary:
The time it took to restore the scroll anchor using the serialized anchor element.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Layout.ScrollRestoration.PrecededByJsScroll
histogram.summary: We are considering allowing JS scrolling to prevent browser scroll position restoration. This histogram measures the impact of that change, in terms of how often restoration would be prevented by JS Scroll. Recorded after each successful scroll restoration attempt.

histogram.name: LevelDB.ApproximateMemoryUse
histogram.summary: The approximate memory use of a LevelDB in bytes. Recorded right after initializing an on-disk database.

histogram.name: LevelDB.ApproximateMemTableMemoryUse
histogram.summary: The approximate MemTable memory use of a LevelDB in bytes. Recorded right after initializing an on-disk database.

histogram.name: LevelDB.Open
histogram.summary: The result of an open attempt of a leveldb.

histogram.name: LevelDB.SharedCache.BytesUsed
histogram.summary: The estimated size (in bytes) of the leveldb shared cache. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: LevelDB.SharedCache.DBCount
histogram.summary: The number of databases currently using the shared block cache. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: LevelDB.SharedCache.KBUsed
histogram.obsolete: No longer used. Removed in April, 2020.
histogram.summary: The estimated size (in kilobytes) of the leveldb shared cache. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: LevelDBEnv.All.SafeThreadAccess
histogram.summary: Linux and CrOS use unlocked_stdio(3). If it is used unsafely, record it here. If there is no record of unsafety after chrome 29 has been in the stable channel for a few weeks then revert this change.

histogram.name: LevelDBEnv.DeleteTableBackupFile
histogram.summary: Tracks the success rate of deleting an unused leveldb table backup file.

histogram.name: LevelDBEnv.IOError
histogram.summary: Methods where leveldb's Chromium environment has IO errors.

histogram.name: LevelDBEnv.IOError.
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2015-05. As of M43 use LevelDBEnv.IOError.BFE.
histogram.summary: PlatformFileErrors encountered by a single leveldb method.

histogram.name: LevelDBEnv.IOError.BFE
histogram.summary: Errors (base::File::Error) encountered by a single leveldb method.

histogram.name: LevelDBEnv.IOError.NewLogger
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2015-05. As of M43 use LevelDBEnv.IOError.BFE.NewLogger.
histogram.summary: Errno of errors encountered in NewLogger.

histogram.name: LevelDBEnv.IOError.NewSequentialFile
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2015-05. As of M43 use LevelDBEnv.IOError.BFE.NewSequentialFile.
histogram.summary: Errno of errors encountered in NewSequentialFile.

histogram.name: LevelDBEnv.IOError.RandomAccessFile
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2013-04. As of m28 use LevelDBEnv.IOError.NewRandomAccessFile.
histogram.summary: File errors in leveldb ChromiumEnv's NewRandomAccessFile method.

histogram.name: LevelDBEnv.IOError.WritableFileAppend
histogram.summary: Errno of errors encountered in WritableFileAppend.

histogram.name: LevelDBEnv.IOError.WritableFileFlush
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2015-05. As of M43 use LevelDBEnv.IOError.BFE.WritableFileFlush.
histogram.summary: Errno of errors encountered in WritableFileFlush.

histogram.name: LevelDBEnv.LockFileAncestorsNotFound
histogram.summary: Number of directories missing when Non-IDB LevelDBEnv tries to create a Lock file.

histogram.name: LevelDBEnv.MaxFDs
histogram.summary: File descriptor limit recorded every time LevelDB calls NewRandomAccessFile for clients other than IndexedDB.

histogram.name: LevelDBEnv.MissingFiles
histogram.summary: Number of backup files found without corresponding ldb files. As measured by GetChildren when used in LevelDB clients other than IndexedDB.

histogram.name: LevelDBEnv.RetryRecoveredFromErrorIn
histogram.summary: When Non-IDB LevelDBEnv successfully retries an operation that had failed, record the error from the most recent failed attempt.

histogram.name: LevelDBEnv.Table
histogram.obsolete: As of M54 no longer creating or using table backup files.
histogram.summary: Success indicates a successful backup or restore operation for .ldb table files when used by LevelDB clients other than IndexedDB.

histogram.name: LevelDBEnv.TimeTo
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2013-04. As of m28 use LevelDBEnv.TimeUntilSuccessFor.
histogram.summary: Time Non-IDB LevelDBEnv slept before successfully completing this operation. 0 means success on the first try.

histogram.name: LevelDBEnv.TimeUntilSuccessFor
histogram.summary: Time Non-IDB LevelDBEnv slept before successfully completing this operation. 0 means success on the first try, as LevelDBEnv only sleeps when retries are needed.

histogram.name: LevelDBWrapper.CommitDelay
histogram.summary: Delay between the browser process receiving changes and those changes being written to the DB.

histogram.name: LibraryLoader.LoadNativeLibraryWindows
