histogram.summary: This metric records the LoadLibraryExW and LoadLibraryW Windows API call results, which are used in native_library_win.cc.

histogram.name: LibraryLoader.NativeLibraryHack
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 11/2014, removed from code.
histogram.summary: A boolean that indicates whether the workaround of a Sony framework bug was used. The metric is Android-specific, and is logged when the browser starts. See more details at http://crbug.com/311644.

histogram.name: LibraryLoader.PercentageOfResidentCodeBeforePrefetch
histogram.summary: Percentage of the native library code pages resident in memory. Recorded immediately before the library prefetching kicks in, only on Android.

histogram.name: LibraryLoader.PrefetchDetailedStatus
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019-10. The code has stabilized, the failure rate is 0.04% on Stable.
histogram.summary: Detailed status of the library prefetch final status. This is an expanded version of LibraryLoader.PrefetchStatus. Android only, recorded at most once per browser startup.

histogram.name: LibraryLoader.PrefetchStatus
histogram.summary: Indicates whether the forking of a child process to prefetch the native library succeeded. Android only, recorded at most once per browser startup.

histogram.name: Linux.Distro
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 2020-04 in favor of Linux.Distro2 because distro-specific version metrics were added.
histogram.summary: The Linux distro used. Logged on each start up.

histogram.name: Linux.Distro.Debian
histogram.summary: The Debian version number (eg. 8: Jessie, 9: Stretch). Logged once on startup if Linux.Distro is Debian.

histogram.name: Linux.Distro.Fedora
histogram.summary: The Fedora version number (eg. 27, 28) Logged once on startup if Linux.Distro is Fedora.

histogram.name: Linux.Distro.OpenSuseLeap
histogram.summary: The openSUSE Leap version number, logged as 1000*major + minor (eg. 15001 = 15.1). Logged once on startup if Linux.Distro is openSUSE Leap.

histogram.name: Linux.Distro.Ubuntu
histogram.summary: The Ubuntu version number, logged as 1000*year + month (eg. 16004 = 16.04 Xenial). Logged once on startup if Linux.Distro is Ubuntu.

histogram.name: Linux.Distro2
histogram.summary: The Linux distro used. Logged on each start up.

histogram.name: Linux.GlibcVersion
histogram.summary: The version of glibc used. (Linux only) Logged on each start up.

histogram.name: Linux.SandboxStatus
histogram.summary: The Linux sandbox status. This describes what sandboxing features are enabled (such as the suid/namespace sandboxes, various namespaces, seccomp bpf, ...). Emitted once at startup.

histogram.name: Linux.WindowManager
histogram.summary: The window manager used. (Linux only) Logged on each start up.

histogram.name: Linux.X11.GbmSupportX11CreationTime
histogram.summary: The amount of time (ms) to construct the GbmSupportX11 singleton.

histogram.name: Linux.X11.ServerRTT
histogram.summary:
RTT between Chrome and the X11 server. Tracked in X11EventSource by measuring the latency to receive a property event after changing a property.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: LiteVideo.OriginHints.ParseResult
histogram.summary: Indicates whether the hints for configuring LiteVideo throttling provided via Finch were successfully parsed and loaded for use. Android only, recorded once per browser startup.

histogram.name: LoadingPredictor.OptimizationHintsReceiveStatus
histogram.summary: The status of when the optimization hints were received by the Loading Predictor. This will be recorded on navigations for which predictions from the Optimization Guide were received.

histogram.name: LoadingPredictor.PreconnectCount
histogram.summary: The number of origins that were preconnected for a page load. It includes preconnect attempts that don't result in new opened connection. Logged after the preconnect manager completes all jobs attached to the page load.

histogram.name: LoadingPredictor.PreconnectHitsPercentage
histogram.summary: The percentage of origins that were preconnected and requested by a page load to the total number of origins that were preconnected for a page load. Logged after the preconnect manager completes all jobs attached to the page load.

histogram.name: LoadingPredictor.PreconnectLearningCount
histogram.summary: When the loading predictor has origins in the local database for a given navigation to preconnect and preresolve, the count of predicted origins.

histogram.name: LoadingPredictor.PreconnectLearningPrecision
histogram.summary: When the loading predictor has origins in the local database for a given navigation to preconnect and preresolve, the precision of the predictions in percentage. This is computed as 100 \times \frac{correct predictions}{predictions}.

histogram.name: LoadingPredictor.PreconnectLearningRecall
histogram.summary: When the loading predictor has origins in the local database for a given navigation to preconnect and preresolve, the recall of the predictions, in percentage. This is computed as 100 \times \frac{correct predictions}{all prefetchable subresources}.

histogram.name: LoadingPredictor.PreconnectLearningRedirectStatus
histogram.summary: When the prefetch predictor has origins in the local database for a given navigation to preconnect and preresolve, records stats about whether redirect was predicted correctly or incorrectly.

histogram.name: LoadingPredictor.PreresolveCount
histogram.summary: The number of hosts that were preresolved for a page load. It includes only successful DNS lookups. Logged after the preconnect manager completes all jobs attached to the page load.

histogram.name: LoadingPredictor.PreresolveHitsPercentage
histogram.summary: The percentage of hosts that were preresolved and requested by a page load to the total number of hosts that were preresolved for a page load. Logged after the preconnect manager completes all jobs attached to the page load.

histogram.name: LoadingPredictor.SubresourceConnectDuration
histogram.obsolete: Removed at M66. No longer recorded.
histogram.summary: Records the time measured before starting DNS lookup until after the connection is complete for main frame subresources observed by the LoadingPredictor. Only resources that required network connection and happened before the first contentful paint are recorded.

histogram.name: LocalDiscovery.ClientRestartAttempts
histogram.summary: Records number of attempts to start local discovery.

histogram.name: LocalDiscovery.DetectorRestartTime
histogram.summary: Time between detector restarts.

histogram.name: LocalDiscovery.DetectorTriggerTime
histogram.summary: Time before detector trigger notifications.

histogram.name: LocalDiscovery.DevicesPage
histogram.summary: Records events related to devices page.

histogram.name: LocalDiscovery.FirewallAccessTime
histogram.summary: Windows only histogram that reports request time spend accessing firewall rules. It's logged once per browser process lifetime, when local discovery is used first time.

histogram.name: LocalDiscovery.IsFirewallReady
histogram.summary: Windows only histogram that reports, whether a firewall is set, so we can bind inbound sockets. It's logged once per browser process lifetime, when local discovery is used first time.

histogram.name: LocalDiscovery.PrivetNotificationsEvent
histogram.summary: Records events related to local discovery notifications.

histogram.name: LocalNetworkRequests.PrivatePage
histogram.summary: Number of requests made by a private page to resources besides itself.

histogram.name: LocalNetworkRequests.PublicPage
histogram.summary: Number of requests made by a public page to resources with reserved IP addresses.

histogram.name: LocalStorage.BrowserLocalStorageCachePurgedInKB
histogram.summary: Total size of the HTML5 LocalStorage browser-side cache purged in KB when memory pressure signal was triggered or cache size hit the limits.

histogram.name: LocalStorage.BrowserLocalStorageCacheSizeInKB
histogram.summary: Total size of the HTML5 LocalStorage databases in KB in the browser-side cache recorded each time a new database is opened in memory.

histogram.name: LocalStorage.BrowserLocalStorageSizeInKB
histogram.obsolete: No longer emitted after onion soup refactor.
histogram.summary: Size of the HTML5 LocalStorage DB in KB in the browser-side cache.

histogram.name: LocalStorage.BrowserTimeToPrimeLocalStorage
histogram.obsolete: No longer emitted after onion soup refactor.
histogram.summary: Time to load HTML5 LocalStorage into the browser-side cache.

histogram.name: LocalStorage.CommitDelay
histogram.obsolete: No longer emitted after onion soup refactor. Replaced with LevelDBWrapper.CommitDelay.
histogram.summary: Delay between a page making changes and those changes being written to the DB.

histogram.name: LocalStorage.MojoSizeInKB
histogram.summary: Size of the HTML5 LocalStorage DB in KB in the renderer-side cache using Mojo implementation.

histogram.name: LocalStorage.MojoTimeToPrime
histogram.summary: Time (in ms) to load HTML5 LocalStorage into the renderer-side cache using Mojo implementation.

histogram.name: LocalStorage.MojoTimeToPrimeFor
histogram.summary: Time (in ms) to load HTML5 LocalStorage into the renderer-side cache using Mojo implementation, split by size.

histogram.name: LocalStorage.RendererAreaCacheHit
histogram.summary: The renderer side cache hit rate metrics for new HTML5 LocalStorage DB opened.

histogram.name: LocalStorage.RendererLocalStorageSizeInKB
histogram.summary: Size of the HTML5 LocalStorage DB in KB in the renderer-side cache.

histogram.name: LocalStorage.RendererTimeToPrimeLocalStorage
histogram.obsolete: No longer omitted after onion soup refactor. Replaced by LocalStorage.MojoTimeToPrime.
histogram.summary: Time to load HTML5 LocalStorage into the renderer-side cache.

histogram.name: LocalStorage.TimeToPrimeLocalStorage
histogram.obsolete: No longer omitted after onion soup refactor. Replaced by LocalStorage.MojoTimeToPrime.
histogram.summary: Time to load HTML5 LocalStorage into the renderer-side cache. Note that the name is not 'Renderer' prefixed for continuity with the old naming.

histogram.name: LocalStorageContext.CachePurgedInKB
histogram.summary: Total size of the HTML5 LocalStorage browser-side cache purged in KB when memory pressure signal was triggered or cache size hit the limits.

histogram.name: LocalStorageContext.CacheSizeInKB
histogram.summary: Total size of the HTML5 LocalStorage databases in KB in the browser-side cache. Recorded each time a new database is opened in memory.

histogram.name: LocalStorageContext.CommitResult
histogram.summary: The result of an attempt to commit localstorage data to the database.

histogram.name: LocalStorageContext.DatabaseOpenError
histogram.summary: The result of a failed attempt to open a LevelDB database for LocalStorage.

histogram.name: LocalStorageContext.DatabaseOpenError.Disk
histogram.summary: The result of a failed attempt to open a LevelDB database for LocalStorage on disk.

histogram.name: LocalStorageContext.DatabaseOpenError.Memory
histogram.summary: The result of a failed attempt to open a LevelDB database for LocalStorage in memory.

histogram.name: LocalStorageContext.DestroyDBResult
histogram.summary: The result of attempting to delete the LocalStorage database.

histogram.name: LocalStorageContext.DirectoryOpenError
histogram.summary: The reason opening a directory for the LevelDB database that backs LocalStorage failed.

histogram.name: LocalStorageContext.MapLoadError
histogram.summary: The reason loading an initial snapshot of the localstorage data for an origin failed.

histogram.name: LocalStorageContext.MigrationFixUpNeeded
histogram.summary: Whether or not any fixes needed to be made to localstorage data, as a result of bugs in the migration code in M61/M62. Recorded every time the browser loads an initial snapshot of the localstorage data for an origin.

histogram.name: LocalStorageContext.OnConnectionDestroyed
histogram.summary: Recorded when the database connection is closed unexpectedly.

histogram.name: LocalStorageContext.OpenError
histogram.summary: The reason opening the LevelDB database that backs LocalStorage failed.

histogram.name: LocalStorageContext.OpenResultAfter
histogram.summary: The result of opening the LevelDB database that backs LocalStorage.

histogram.name: LocalStorageContext.ReadVersionError
histogram.summary: The reason reading the schema version of the LocalStorage database failed.

histogram.name: Login.ArcBugReportBackupTime
histogram.summary: Tracks the time to backup ARC bug report when starting ARC fails.

histogram.name: Login.BrowserShutdownTime
histogram.summary: Tracks the browser process shutdown time from when SIGTERM is sent to the browser process to when the browser process group exits (or gets killed by SIGABRT).

histogram.name: Login.ConsumerNewUsersAllowed
histogram.summary: Chrome OS histogram tracking, per consumer sign-in, whether the device owner is allowing arbitrary accounts to be used on the device, or only those on a specific whitelist.

histogram.name: Login.CustomFlags
histogram.summary: A set of chrome flags that required browser restart on Chrome OS user sign in.

histogram.name: Login.DefaultPageZoom
histogram.obsolete: Removed 01/2020. It was added to decide if the SplitSettings project could safely decouple browser page zoom from ARC++/OS zoom. We decided it was safe and did the decoupling in M78.
histogram.summary: The user's default page zoom setting, recorded on login.

histogram.name: Login.FailureReason
histogram.summary: Chrome OS login failure reason.

histogram.name: Login.LeastUsedAccountDays
histogram.summary: Chrome OS histogram that keeps track of the days since the least frequently used account signed in. Reported on every boot and once a day after that.

histogram.name: Login.MountNamespaceCreationSuccess
histogram.summary: Mount Namespace creation success status. Reported every time user signs in.

histogram.name: Login.NewUserPriorityPrefsSyncResult
histogram.summary: Records whether a new user's priority prefs sync timed out.

histogram.name: Login.NewUserPriorityPrefsSyncTime
histogram.summary: Time spent waiting for priority preferences to sync after new user sign in. The operation will time out after 10s.

histogram.name: Login.OfflineFailure.IsKnownUser
histogram.summary: On offline login failure, records whether it is for an existing user.

histogram.name: Login.OfflineSuccess.Attempts
histogram.summary: On offline login success, records number of attempts, including success.

histogram.name: Login.PasswordChanged.ReauthReason
histogram.summary: Tracks the reason why a user was sent through the GAIA re-auth flow which caused the local password change.

histogram.name: Login.PasswordChangeFlow
histogram.summary: Records the UI flow after a possible password change is detected.

histogram.name: Login.PasswordNotChanged.ReauthReason
histogram.summary: Tracks the reason why a user was sent through the GAIA re-auth flow which did not cause the local password change.

histogram.name: Login.PolicyFilesStatePerBoot
histogram.summary: The state of Chrome OS owner key and device policy files.

histogram.name: Login.PromptToCompleteLoginTime
histogram.summary: Time from first display of the login prompt until the user completes signing in.

histogram.name: Login.PromptToLoginTime
histogram.summary: Time from first display of the login prompt until the existing user completes signing in.

histogram.name: Login.ReauthReason
histogram.obsolete: Removed in M85. Splitted in two: Login.PasswordChanged.ReauthReason and Login.PasswordNotChanged.ReauthReason.
histogram.summary: Tracks the reason why a user was sent through the GAIA re-auth flow.

histogram.name: Login.SessionExitType
histogram.summary: Tracks whether a ChromeOS user was logged out because Chrome repeatedly crashed.

histogram.name: Login.StateKeyGenerationStatus
histogram.summary: Result of a state key generation operation.

histogram.name: Login.SuccessReason
histogram.summary: Chrome OS login success reason.

histogram.name: Login.TokenCheckResponseTime
histogram.summary: Time between sending a request to, and receiving a reply from, GAIA token endpoint to validate TokenInfo.

histogram.name: Login.TokenObtainResponseTime
histogram.summary: Time between sending a request to, and receiving a reply from, GAIA token endpoint to obtain TokenInfo.

histogram.name: Login.UsersActive28Days
histogram.summary: Chrome OS histogram that keeps track of number of users who have logged in in the last 28 days. Reported on every boot and once a day after that.

histogram.name: Login.UsersActiveDaily
histogram.summary: Chrome OS histogram that keeps track of number of users who have logged in in the last day. Reported on every boot and once a day after that.

histogram.name: Login.UsersActiveWeekly
histogram.summary: Chrome OS histogram that keeps track of number of users who have logged in in the last 7 days. Reported on every boot and once a day after that.

histogram.name: Login.UsersActiveWeekly.Percent
histogram.summary: Chrome OS histogram that keeps track of percentage of local users who have logged in in the last 7 days. Reported on every boot and once a day after that.

histogram.name: Login.UserType
histogram.summary: Chrome OS histogram that keeps track of the way a user logs in and whether Chrome OS is running normal or developer mode.

histogram.name: LowPriorityIframes.IframeRequestPriority
histogram.summary: Original Blink priority of the request that belongs to an iframe. Recorded for every request fetched via Blink, but only when the experiment to lower the priority of iframes is enabled.

histogram.name: LowPriorityIframes.MainFrameRequestPriority
histogram.summary: Original Blink priority of the request that belongs to the main frame. Recorded for every request fetched via Blink, but only when the experiment to lower the priority of iframes is enabled.

histogram.name: MachineLearningService.CpuTimeMicrosec
histogram.summary: CPU time (for one CPU core) of one Chrome OS ML Service request.

histogram.name: MachineLearningService.CpuUsageMilliPercent
histogram.summary: Fraction of total CPU resources used by Chrome OS ML Service, sampled every 5 minutes.

histogram.name: MachineLearningService.CreateGraphExecutorResult.Event
histogram.summary: The result of CreateGraphExecutor, which can be OK or some specific error.

histogram.name: MachineLearningService.ElapsedTimeMicrosec
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2020-02-01 because elapsed time should be measured by clients, if they need it.
histogram.summary: Elapsed time of one Chrome OS ML Service request.

histogram.name: MachineLearningService.ExecuteResult.Event
histogram.summary: The result of Execute, which can be OK or some specific error.

histogram.name: MachineLearningService.LoadModelResult
histogram.summary: The error result of LoadModel in cases when the model name could not be determined. See MachineLearningService.*.LoadModelResult.Event for the result broken down by model name when the name is known.

histogram.name: MachineLearningService.LoadModelResult.Event
histogram.summary: The result of LoadModel, which can be OK or some specific error.

histogram.name: MachineLearningService.MojoConnectionEvent
histogram.summary: Events related to the connection and disconnection of the Mojo IPC channel between Chrome and the Chrome OS ML Service.

histogram.name: MachineLearningService.PeakPrivateMemoryKb
histogram.obsolete: Removed 07/2019 because we change to record total (shared+unshared) memory rather than only private memory. Replaced by MachineLearningService.PeakTotalMemoryKb.
histogram.summary: Peak private (non-shared) memory used by Chrome OS ML Service over the last 24 hours. Sampled every 5 minutes, so may miss short-lived spikes.

histogram.name: MachineLearningService.PeakTotalMemoryKb
histogram.summary: Peak total (shared and non-shared) memory used by Chrome OS ML Service over the last 24 hours. Sampled every 5 minutes, so may miss short-lived spikes.

histogram.name: MachineLearningService.PrivateMemoryDeltaKb
histogram.obsolete: Removed 07/2019 because we change to record total (shared+unshared) memory rather than only private memory. Replaced by MachineLearningService.TotalMemoryDeltaKb.
histogram.summary: Memory usage increase caused by one Chrome OS ML Service request.

histogram.name: MachineLearningService.PrivateMemoryKb
histogram.obsolete: Removed 07/2019 because we change to record total (shared+unshared) memory rather than only private memory. Replaced by MachineLearningService.TotalMemoryKb.
histogram.summary: Private (non-shared) memory used by Chrome OS ML Service, sampled every 5 minutes.

histogram.name: MachineLearningService.TotalMemoryDeltaKb
histogram.summary: Memory usage increase caused by one Chrome OS ML Service request.

histogram.name: MachineLearningService.TotalMemoryKb
histogram.summary: Total (shared and non-shared) memory used by Chrome OS ML Service, sampled every 5 minutes.

histogram.name: ManagedUsers.BlockedFrameDepth
histogram.summary: The depth of blocked frame in the frame tree. The value is recorded when the renderer frame hosting the blocking page finishes loading.

histogram.name: ManagedUsers.BlockedIframeCount
histogram.summary: The number of blocked iframes per unblocked main frame load. The value is recorded when the main frame finishes loading.

histogram.name: ManagedUsers.ChromeOS.PasswordChange
histogram.summary: Chrome OS histogram that keeps track of supervised user password change result.

histogram.name: ManagedUsers.FilteringResult
histogram.summary: The counts of results from supervised user URL filtering. Each entry includes the outcome of the filter (i.e. allowed, blocked, or unknown) and the page transition type (how the user got there, e.g. typed URL, clicked link).

histogram.name: ManagedUsers.KidsManagementClassifyUrlFailureDelay
histogram.summary: The extra page load delays introduced by network requests due to the supervised user url filtering feature, measured once per page load, for failed requests. This is the delay to fetch the token and call the ClassifyUrl rpc, when there is a cache miss.

histogram.name: ManagedUsers.KidsManagementClassifyUrlSuccessDelay
histogram.summary: The extra page load delays introduced by network requests due to the supervised user url filtering feature, measured once per page load. This is the delay to fetch the token and call the ClassifyUrl rpc, when there is a cache miss. Only recorded for successful requests.

histogram.name: ManagedUsers.KidsManagementUrlCheckerResponseStatus
histogram.summary: The counts of response status from supervised user KidsManagementURLCheckerCLient. Each entry includes the outcome of a request (i.e. success, net error, parsing error).

histogram.name: ManagedUsers.RequestPermissionSource
histogram.summary: The counts of request for permission sent from main frame or sub-frame.

histogram.name: ManagedUsers.SafeSitesDelay
histogram.summary: The extra page load delays introduced by network requests due to the supervised user SafeSites feature, measured once per page load.

histogram.name: ManagedUsers.SafetyFilter
histogram.summary: The counts of results from the supervised user safety filter. Each entry includes the outcome of the filter (i.e. allowed, blocked, or unknown) and the page transition type (how the user got there, e.g. typed URL, clicked link). This only includes URL requests that were handled by the SafeSites filter (both online and the static blacklist).

histogram.name: ManagedUsers.Whitelist.Count
histogram.summary: The number of whitelists installed for a supervised user. Recorded at every profile startup.

histogram.name: ManagedUsers.Whitelist.JsonParseDuration
histogram.summary: The total amount of time taken to parse the JSON contents of a whitelist out of process.

histogram.name: ManagedUsers.Whitelist.ReadDuration
histogram.summary: The amount of time taken to read a whitelist file from disk.

histogram.name: ManagedUsers.Whitelist.TotalLoadDuration
histogram.summary: The total amount of time taken to load a whitelist from disk and parse it, including jumps to the blocking thread.

histogram.name: Manifest.FetchResult
histogram.summary: Tracks whether the Manifest fetch succeed and why it failed if it failed.

histogram.name: Manifest.HasProperty
histogram.summary: Tracks which properties of a Manifest were present when it was parsed. If a property can't be parsed, it will be recorded as not present. These histograms will only be used if the Manifest was correctly fetched and parsed and the parsed manifest is not empty.

histogram.name: Manifest.IsEmpty
histogram.summary: Tracks whether the parsed Manifest is the empty Manifest.

histogram.name: Manifest.ParseSuccess
histogram.summary: Tracks the Manifest parsing result. A failure will be recorded if the Manifest was incorrectly structured. This doesn't take into account parse failures for each individual properties.

histogram.name: ManualFallback.PresentedOptions.AllPasswords
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of credentials presented to the user in the "Use other password..." view in Manual Fallback. This is logged everytime the user opens this view.

histogram.name: ManualFallback.PresentedOptions.CreditCards
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of cards presented to the user in Manual Fallback. This is logged everytime the user opens this view.

histogram.name: ManualFallback.PresentedOptions.Passwords
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of credentials presented to the user in the Passwords Manual Fallback view. This is logged everytime the user opens this view.

histogram.name: ManualFallback.PresentedOptions.Profiles
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of profiles presented to the user in Manual Fallback. This is logged everytime the user opens this view.

histogram.name: ManualFallback.VisibleSuggestions.OpenCreditCards
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of autofill suggestions present when the user taps on the credit card button in manual fallback.

histogram.name: ManualFallback.VisibleSuggestions.OpenPasswords
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of password suggestions present when the user taps on the password button in manual fallback.

histogram.name: ManualFallback.VisibleSuggestions.OpenProfiles
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of autofill suggestions present when the user taps on the profiles (address) button in manual fallback.

histogram.name: Media.AcceleratedCompositingActive
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Whether accelerated compositing was used for HTML5 media rendering.

histogram.name: Media.AImageReaderGLOwner.AcquireImageResult
histogram.summary: Error codes to handle AImage and AImageReader api calls.

histogram.name: Media.Android.BackgroundVideoTime
histogram.summary: Android-specific metric. The time between the moment the backgrounded video playback is started (meaning the user just resumed the backgrounded video allowing playback) and the moment it stops being background playback by the current playing video either going foreground or being paused by the user. Note: on Android, videos are suspended when backgrounded.

histogram.name: Media.Android.IsHttpLiveStreamingMedia
histogram.summary: Android: Records whether a regular media URL is HLS (HTTP Live Streaming) media or not.

histogram.name: Media.Android.IsHttpLiveStreamingMediaPredictionResult
histogram.summary: Android: Records which HLS prediction based on the original URL was best.

histogram.name: Media.Android.MediaPlayerSuccess
histogram.summary: Android: Whether MediaPlayer exited without errors.

histogram.name: Media.Android.MediaPlayerWatchTime
histogram.summary: Wall time of MediaPlayer playbacks. Each count represents one second of media playback. Multiple MediaPlayer instances can together accumulate watch time faster than real time.

histogram.name: Media.Android.NumMediaServerCrashes
histogram.summary: Android: Number of consecutive media server crashes monitored before it is reset to 0. Reset happens if there are no crashes in a minute, or if user hits the "try again" button on the media throttle info bar.

histogram.name: Media.Android.ThrottleInfobarResponse
histogram.summary: Android: The distribution of responses to the media throttle infobar prompt.

histogram.name: Media.ArcGpuVideoDecodeAccelerator.InitializeResult
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of June 12, 2017. Replaced by Media.ChromeArcVideoDecodeAccelerator.InitializeResult.
histogram.summary: Counts of status values returned from calls to ArcGpuVideoDecodeAccelerator::Initialize().

histogram.name: Media.Audible.CloseNewestToExitConcurrentPlayback
histogram.summary: Records when an audible tab is closed and we now only have one audible tab open.

histogram.name: Media.Audible.ConcurrentTabsTime
histogram.summary: Records how long more than one tab is audible at the same time. The concurrent tabs might change during this period. The time recorded starts when the browser goes from one to two audbile tabs and stops when it is back below two.

histogram.name: Media.Audible.ConcurrentTabsWhenStarting
histogram.summary: Records how many tabs were audible when a new tab started to be audible.

histogram.name: Media.Audible.MaxConcurrentTabsInSession
histogram.summary: Records how many tabs are audible at the same time during the session. It is recording the maximum audible tab count everytime it increases. In other words, a session with N concurrent audible tabs will record entries from 1 through N exactly once.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.ActualInputBufferFrameSizeMac
histogram.summary: Size (in audio frames) of native I/O buffers that is set by the audio manager when calling AUAudioInputStream::Open(). I.e., it is the actual buffer size and it can differ from the one that the client requested. Only sampled when Media.Audio.InputStartupSuccessMac reports 'Failure'.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.AudioUnitRenderHasWorkedMac
histogram.summary: Stored in combination with Media.Audio.RenderFailsWhenBufferSizeChangesMac. It is set to true if calls to AudioUnitRender has ever worked, i.e., we did not start to see kAudioUnitErr_CannotDoInCurrentContext error messages directly from start but there has been at least on successful call to AudioUnitRender.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.AutomaticGainControlMac
histogram.summary: Indicates if the Automatic Gain Control (AGC) is enabled or not. Only sampled when Media.Audio.InputStartupSuccessMac reports 'Failure'.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Autoplay
histogram.summary: Records the autoplay source of audios.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Autoplay.Attribute.WaitTime
histogram.summary: Records time from load starts until audio starts based on autoplay attribute.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Autoplay.PlayMethod.WaitTime
histogram.summary: Records time from load starts until audio starts based on play method.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Bitstream
histogram.summary: Records whether the given bitstream audio output format is supported. Recorded when the browser or audio process AudioManager is constructed.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.CallbackError
histogram.summary: A boolean that reflects whether or not an error was reported during audio capture.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.DetectedMissingCallbacks
histogram.summary: Whether missing callbacks where detected during audio capture. Logged when AudioInputDevice is stopped.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.DeviceLatency
histogram.summary: The audio input device latency. The time from when a frame was captured until it reaches Chrome's platform audio implementation. Sampled for every buffer received from the device, and reports the latency for the first frame in the buffer.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.FramesProvided
histogram.summary: The number of captured audio frames that is provided by the operating system in each callback. Typically, Chrome will want to use a 10 ms buffer (e.g. 480 frames for a sample rate of 48 kHz), but the OS may decide to capture e.g. 256 frames of audio at a time. If the desired frame size is used, the emitted value is special-cased to be 0. This histogram then shows how frequent it is that the desired number of frames is used and if not, the number of frames used in practice. The number of frames per callback is normally constant or very rarely changed throughout the lifetime of the audio stream. The first value that differs from the desired value is stored and logged when an audio input stream is closed. Any further differences during the lifetime will be ignored.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.Glitches
histogram.summary: The number of glitches that were detected at the OS level while an audio stream was active. A glitch is detected if the OS has to skip providing a set of frames. This value is logged when an audio input stream is closed.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.HighLatencyCallbackError
histogram.summary: A boolean that reflects whether or not an error was reported during audio capture for a high latency stream.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.InputStreamDuration
histogram.summary: This histogram measures the duration of every input stream, as seen from the renderer. It counts from when it has been successfully started, until it is stopped. Streams that fail to start are not logged.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.LargestGlitchMs
histogram.summary: The length in milliseconds of the largest audio glitch that was detected at the OS level. This value is logged when an audio input stream that has suffered glitches is closed.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.LostFramesInMs
histogram.summary: The combined length in milliseconds of audio glitches that was detected at the OS level. This is useful to know in particular for audio processing such as echo cancellation. This value is logged when an audio input stream that has suffered glitches is closed.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.LowLatencyCallbackError
histogram.summary: A boolean that reflects whether or not an error was reported during audio capture for a low latency stream.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.Mac.MicSystemPermission.FixedTime.SinceFirstFailure
histogram.summary: The time from the first microphone access failure due to blocked system permission before the last browser restart to startup after browser restart. Logged once at browser startup if the system permission then is allowed.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.Mac.MicSystemPermission.FixedTime.SinceLastFailure
histogram.summary: The time from the last microphone access failure due to blocked system permission before the last browser restart to startup after browser restart. Logged once at browser startup if the system permission then is allowed.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.Mac.MicSystemPermission.Startup
histogram.summary: The Mac system permission state for microphone. Logged once at browser startup. For more information on the values, see https://developer.apple.com/documentation/avfoundation/avauthorizationstatus.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.Mac.MicSystemPermission.StartupAfterFailure
histogram.summary: The Mac system permission state for microphone. Logged once at browser startup if there was a failure accessing the microphone due to blocked system permission before the last browser restart. For more information on the values, see https://developer.apple.com/documentation/avfoundation/avauthorizationstatus.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.Mac.MicSystemPermission.UserMedia
histogram.summary: The Mac system permission state for microphone at the time of a user media request. Logged when the system permission is checked. If the permission is "not determined" this is logged, permission is requested, and the resulting permission (from the user response) is also logged. For more information on the values, see https://developer.apple.com/documentation/avfoundation/avauthorizationstatus.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.StreamBrokerDisconnectReason
histogram.summary: Error codes from AudioInputStreamBroker::ObserverBindingLost. They describe why an audio input stream ended.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.StreamCallbackError
histogram.summary: When AudioInputDevice is stopped, this stat is recorded with whether an error callback was ever received over IPC.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.StreamCallbackError2
histogram.summary: When AudioInputDevice is stopped, this stat is recorded with whether an error callback was ever received over IPC, and in that case whether the error occurred during or after stream creation.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.VirtualCallbackError
histogram.summary: A boolean that reflects whether or not an error was reported during audio capture for a virtual stream.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.Win.ConcurrentGlitchAndDiscontinuities
histogram.summary: The number of times a glitch was detected by detecting skipped frames (as with Media.Audio.Capture.Glitches) and disconinuity was flagged by the OS at the same time (as with Media.Audio.Capture.Win.Discontinuities). Sometimes these indications are not consistent with each other so this metric helps understanding how often they aren't. This value is logged when an audio input stream is closed.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.Win.DevicePeriod
histogram.summary: The audio engine's period between processing passes. See IAudioClient::GetDevicePeriod. This value is logged when an audio input stream is opened.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.Win.DevicePositionLessThanExpected
histogram.summary: The number of times the device position was less than expected during the lifetime of an audio stream. This value is logged when an audio input stream is closed.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.Win.Discontinuities
histogram.summary: The number of glitches that were detected at the OS level on Windows while an audio stream was active, as reported by the OS via the discontinuity flag (see IAudioCaptureClient::GetBuffer). This value is logged when an audio input stream is closed.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.Win.EndpointBufferSize
histogram.summary: The endpoint buffer size for input audio. This value is logged when an audio input stream is opened.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.Win.InitError
histogram.summary: Error codes from IAudioClient::Initialize() in WASAPIAudioInputStream::InitializeAudioEngine on Windows.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.Win.InitError.FormatRelated
histogram.summary: Errors from IAudioClient::Initialize() in WASAPIAudioInputStream::InitializeAudioEngine on Windows that are related to the audio format. Each error is split by whether format conversion was used or not.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.Win.Open
histogram.summary: Success/error cases for opening an audio input stream on Windows.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Capture.Win.StreamLatency
histogram.summary: The maximum stream latency as reported by the OS. See IAudioClient::GetStreamLatency. This value is logged when an audio input stream is opened.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.CoreAudioDispatchOverrideInitResult
histogram.summary: Tracks support and initialization success/failure of the CoreAudio dispatch override hotfix. Logged during initialization of AudioManagerMac. The fix is not supported on macOS earlier than version 10.10.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.CoreAudioDispatchOverrideLookupEvent
histogram.summary: Logged whenever the CoreAudio dispatch override hotfix needs to look up the calling function using dladdr(). Once ResumeIO and PauseIO have been identified, no more lookups should be performed during the session.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.InputBufferSizeWasChangedAudioWorkedMac
histogram.summary: Indicates if the size of the audio unit's IO buffer was changed when starting an input audio stream. Sampled each time an AUAudioInputStream instance is closed, i.e., we know that input audio has started as it should when this value is stored. Can be compared with Media.Audio.InputBufferSizeWasChangedMac which is only added when input audio fails to start.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.InputBufferSizeWasChangedMac
histogram.summary: Indicates if the size of the audio unit's IO buffer was changed when starting an input audio stream. Only sampled when Media.Audio.InputStartupSuccessMac reports 'Failure'.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.InputDevicePropertyChangedMac
histogram.obsolete: Removed Feb 2018. Was put in place to debug missing callback issues that have since been resolved.
histogram.summary: Lists device properties that have been changed during an audio input stream session. We update a map of different property changes during a session and all these values are recorded when an AUAudioInputStream object is closed, hence multiple enum values can be emitted when the histogram is stored.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.InputDevicePropertyChangedStartupFailedMac
histogram.obsolete: Removed Feb 2018. Was put in place to debug missing callback issues that have since been resolved.
histogram.summary: Lists device properties that have been changed during an audio input stream session. Only sampled when Media.Audio.InputStartupSuccessMac reports 'Failure' and multiple enum values can be emitted each time that happens.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.InputRestartAttemptsMac
histogram.obsolete: Removed May 2017. Restart mechanism is removed.
histogram.summary: Counts the total number of times an attempt to restart input audio has been done. The value is stored once when a stream is closed and updated when it has been detected that input audio callbacks are not generated as expected.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.InputStartupSuccessMac
histogram.summary: Indicates if audio capturing did start after stream startup was requested. Sampled once, a few seconds after a stream has been asked to start.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.InputStartWasDeferredAudioWorkedMac
histogram.summary: Indicates if audio capturing started with a small delay or not. Sampled each time an AUAudioInputStream instance is closed, i.e., we know that input audio has started as it should when this value is stored. Can be compared with Media.Audio.InputStartWasDeferredMac which is only added when input audio fails to start.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.InputStartWasDeferredMac
histogram.summary: Indicates if audio capturing started with a small delay or not. Only sampled when Media.Audio.InputStartupSuccessMac reports 'Failure'.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.InputVolumeStartsAtZeroWin
histogram.summary: Indicates if the volume level of the audio session is set to zero when the input audio session starts. Sampled once when the stream starts but only on Windows platforms. See http://crbug.com/1014443.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.IsOnBatteryPowerMac
histogram.summary: Indicates if the Mac OSX device is on battery power or not. Only sampled when Media.Audio.InputStartupSuccessMac reports 'Failure'.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.LogicalProcessorsMac
histogram.summary: The number of logical processors/cores available on the Mac OSX device. Only sampled when Media.Audio.InputStartupSuccessMac reports 'Failure'.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.NumberOfBasicInputStreamsMac
histogram.summary: Number of created default input audio streams. Only sampled when Media.Audio.InputStartupSuccessMac reports 'Failure'.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.NumberOfLowLatencyInputStreamsMac
histogram.summary: Number of created low-latency input audio streams. Only sampled when Media.Audio.InputStartupSuccessMac reports 'Failure'.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.NumberOfOutputStreamsMac
histogram.summary: Number of created output audio streams. Only sampled when Media.Audio.InputStartupSuccessMac reports 'Failure'.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.OutputDeviceAuthorizationTime
histogram.summary: Time delta between the moment output device authorization request is received by the browser and the moment its processessing is finished (and reply is to be sent back to the renderer if IPC is not closed yet).

histogram.name: Media.Audio.OutputStreamsCanceledByBrowser
histogram.summary: When a mojo audio stream factory is destructed, the streams it created are also destructed. This is a potential source of stat differences between the new mojo code and the old Chrome IPC code, where streams could potentially stick around until the destruction of the owning RenderProcessHost. This histogram measures how many output streams are destructed each time a mojo output stream factory is destructed.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.PhysicalProcessorsMac
histogram.summary: The number of physical processors/cores available on the Mac OSX device. Only sampled when Media.Audio.InputStartupSuccessMac reports 'Failure'.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Processing.CaptureDelayMs
histogram.summary: The time, in milliseconds, between when a block of audio is read from the soundcard and when it's presented to the audio processor. Used in calculating the total system delay for the audio processor.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Processing.CaptureDelayVarianceMs
histogram.summary: The variance of the capture delay in milliseconds (see Media.Audio.Processing.CaptureDelayMs). Logged once every second and calculated over the past second of data.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Processing.RenderDelayMs
histogram.summary: The time, in milliseconds, between when a block of audio is presented to the audio processor and the time it's played out on the soundcard. Used in calculating the total system delay for the audio processor.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Processing.RenderDelayVarianceMs
histogram.summary: The variance of the render delay in milliseconds (see Media.Audio.Processing.RenderDelayMs). Logged once every second and calculated over the past second of data.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Processing.TotalDelayMs
histogram.summary: The estimated total audio system delay presented to the audio processor. The sum of the individual capture and render delays at similar points in time.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Processing.TotalDelayVarianceMs
histogram.summary: The variance of the total delay in milliseconds (see Media.Audio.Processing.TotalDelayMs). Logged once every second and calculated over the past second of data.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.AudioInputsPerMixer.LatencyExact
histogram.summary: Number of simultaneous inputs coming to the AudioRendererMixer which renders audio with user specified latency. The value is logged each time it reaches a new maximum for the mixer. It is useful for evaluating how mixing audio in renderer reduces the number of output audio streams going from the renderer to the browser.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.AudioInputsPerMixer.LatencyInteractive
histogram.summary: Number of simultaneous inputs coming to the AudioRendererMixer which renders audio with interactive latency. The value is logged each time it reaches a new maximum for the mixer. It is useful for evaluating how mixing audio in renderer reduces the number of output audio streams going from the renderer to the browser.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.AudioInputsPerMixer.LatencyPlayback
histogram.summary: Number of simultaneous inputs coming to the AudioRendererMixer which renders audio with playback latency. The value is logged each time it reaches a new maximum for the mixer. It is useful for evaluating how mixing audio in renderer reduces the number of output audio streams going from the renderer to the browser.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.AudioInputsPerMixer.LatencyRtc
histogram.summary: Number of simultaneous inputs coming to the AudioRendererMixer which renders audio with real time latency. The value is logged each time it reaches a new maximum for the mixer. It is useful for evaluating how mixing audio in renderer reduces the number of output audio streams going from the renderer to the browser.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.AudioMixing.LatencyMap
histogram.summary: Subset of audio output latencies encountered by the renderer so far. Logged each time an audio output with a new latency starts playing. The values are bitsets where each bit represents corresponding latency according to AudioLatency::LatencyType. Useful for understanding usecases for audio output mixing in renderer.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.BrowserCallbackRegularity
histogram.summary: Reflects how regularly browser issues audio requests to renderer. Depends on how well the system buffer size the browser side renders audio at and the buffer size it requests from renderer are aligned. When zero, it means renderer buffer size is a multiple of system buffer size, and render calls from browser to renderer are issued evenly. When -1, it means the renderer buffer size is larger than system buffer size, but is not an exact multiple of it; in this case browser periodically skips a call to renderer. When positive, floor(value/2) reflects the number of extra consecutive render requests issued by browser to renderer each time to fulfill the system audio render request; if (value/2) is not integer, then plus one more extra call is issued periodically. The metric is recorded on the edge between browser and renderer. On Mac the system buffer size may vary dynamically, the metric does not capture that: only the buffer size mismatch introduced by audio output device configuration is captured.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.FramesRequested
histogram.summary: The number of frames audio is actually rendered at if the number is different from the desired number of frames. If the desired frame size is used, this value will be 0. Typically, Chrome will want to use a 10ms buffer (e.g. 480 frames for a sample rate of 48kHz), but we might have to render audio 256 frames at a time. This histogram gives us an indication as to how frequent that is and what buffer sizes are used in practice. This value is logged when an audio output stream is stopped.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.GetSourceDataTime.WebRTC
histogram.summary:
The time spent to get audio data from the source. For WebRTC, it's the time spent in WebRtcAudioRenderer::SourceCallback. Logged every function call, which is every 10 ms.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.GetSourceDataTimeMax.WebRTC
histogram.summary:
The maximum time spent to get audio data from the source during a session. For WebRTC, it's the time spent in WebRtcAudioRenderer::SourceCallback. A session is between Start() and Stop() of WebRtcAudioRenderer.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.Glitches
histogram.summary: The number of glitches that were detected at the OS level while an audio stream was active. A glitch is detected if the OS has to skip rendering a set of frames. This value is logged when an audio output stream is stopped.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.InputDeviceStartTime
histogram.summary: The time delta from an AudioInputDevice sending a RecordStream message to it getting its first data callback.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.InputDeviceStreamCreationTime
histogram.summary: The time delta between the moment a MojoAudioInputIPC instance on the renderer process requests a new stream from the browser to the moment it receives a response.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.LargestGlitchMs
histogram.summary: The length in milliseconds of the largest glitch that was detected at the OS level. This value is logged when an audio output stream that has suffered glitches is stopped.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.LostFramesInMs
histogram.summary: The combined length in milliseconds of audio glitches. This is useful to know in particular for audio processing such as echo cancellation. This value is logged when an audio output stream that has suffered glitches is stopped.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.OutputDeviceAuthorizationTime
histogram.summary: Similar to Media.Audio.OutputDeviceAuthorizationTime, this histogram measures the time required for device authorization, but it is measured on the renderer side when AudioOutputDevice calls RequestDeviceAuthorization on the IO thread until it receives OnDeviceAuthorized, also on the IO thread. Note that an authorization timeout will cause OnDeviceAuthorized to be called, so for platforms that use a timeout, very few times will be above the timeout value.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.OutputDeviceAuthorizationTimedOut
histogram.summary: Whether audio output device timed out waiting for authorization reply from the browser side.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.OutputDeviceStartTime
histogram.summary: The time delta from an AudioOutputDevice sending its first PlayStream message to it getting its first data callback.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.OutputDeviceStatus
histogram.summary: Device status received in response to device authorization request.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.OutputDeviceStreamCreationTime
histogram.summary: The time delta between the moment a MojoAudioOutputIPC instance on the renderer process requests a new stream from the browser to the moment it receives a response.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.OutputStreamDuration
histogram.summary: This histogram measures the duration of every output stream, as seen from the renderer. It counts from when it has been successfully started, until it is stopped. Streams that fail to start are not logged.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.SinkCache.GetOutputDeviceInfoCacheUtilization
histogram.summary: Whether a cached sink was used to get audio output device information, or a new sink was created for that.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.SinkCache.InfoSinkReusedForOutput
histogram.summary: When a new sink is created to get output device information, it is cached. This metric shows if such a sink is later reused for audio output (can happen no more than once) or deleted unused after timeout expires. Logged when one of the abovementioned events takes place.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.SinkCache.UsedForSinkCreation
histogram.summary: Whether a sink was created through the sink cache or directly.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.StreamBrokerDisconnectReason
histogram.summary: Error codes from AudioOutputStreamBroker::ObserverBindingLost. They describe why an audio output stream ended.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.StreamBrokerDisconnectReason2
histogram.summary: Describes why and in which state an audio output stream ended.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.StreamBrokerDocumentDestroyedAwaitingCreatedTime
histogram.summary: If the document was destroyed after the broker requested the output stream from the audio service, but before it received the reply: how long ago the stream was requested.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.StreamBrokerStreamCreationTime
histogram.summary: Time interval between the moment the broker requested the output stream from the audio service, and the moment it received the reply.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.StreamCallbackError
histogram.summary: When AudioOutputDevice is stopped, this stat is recorded with whether an error callback was ever received over IPC.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.Render.StreamCallbackError2
histogram.summary: When AudioOutputDevice is stopped, this stat is recorded with whether an error callback was ever received over IPC, and in that case whether the error occurred during or after stream creation.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.RenderFailsWhenBufferSizeChangesMac
histogram.summary: True if a call to AudioUnitRender failed with an error code of kAudioUnitErr_CannotDoInCurrentContext (-10863) and the call was preceded with a change in the native I/O buffer size. This value is logged while input audio is active but it should be a very rare event.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.RequestedInputBufferFrameSizeMac
histogram.summary: Size (in audio frames) the client has asked for when creating an AUAudioInputStream object. It might not be able to fulfill this request. The actual size is given by Media.Audio.ActualInputBufferFrameSizeMac. Only sampled when Media.Audio.InputStartupSuccessMac reports 'Failure'.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.ResumeEventsMac
histogram.summary: Counts the number of times the system has resumed from power suspension. Counting is reset each time Chrome restarts. Only sampled when Media.Audio.InputStartupSuccessMac reports 'Failure'.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.TrackAudioRenderer.DeviceStatus
histogram.summary: Device status received during TrackAudioRenderer initialization. This is a specific case of Media.Audio.Render.OutputDeviceStatus histogram.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.TrackAudioRenderer.SwitchDeviceStatus
histogram.summary: Device status received by TrackAudioRenderer when switching the output device. This is a specific case of Media.Audio.Render.OutputDeviceStatus histogram.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.UptimeMac
histogram.summary: Reports the system uptime in hours for Mac OS X devices. Only sampled when Media.Audio.InputStartupSuccessMac reports 'Failure'.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.WebRTCAudioRenderer.DeviceStatus
histogram.summary: Device status received during WebRTCAudioRenderer initialization. This is a specific case of Media.Audio.Render.OutputDeviceStatus histogram.

histogram.name: Media.Audio.WebRTCAudioRenderer.SwitchDeviceStatus
histogram.summary: Device status received by WebRTCAudioRenderer when switching the output device. This is a specific case of Media.Audio.Render.OutputDeviceStatus histogram.

histogram.name: Media.AudioBitsPerChannel
histogram.summary: Bits per channel of HTML5 audio sample data.

histogram.name: Media.AudioCapturerAudioGlitches
histogram.summary: Whether there is input audio glitches due to dropped input audio data or not. Sampled when a low-latency input audio stream is destructed.

histogram.name: Media.AudioCapturerDroppedData
histogram.summary: The percentage of input audio data that was dropped at the browser/renderer boundary due to for example buffer overflow in the writer side buffer or in the socket. Reported at the destruction of low-latency audio input stream.

histogram.name: Media.AudioCapturerMissedReadDeadline
histogram.summary: The percentage of input audio data blocks that had to be buffered because there was not room in the shared memory ring buffer on the browser/renderer boundary. This happens if the reading side hasn't read data in time. Reported at the destruction of low-latency audio input stream.

histogram.name: Media.AudioCapturerRepetition
histogram.summary: Captures bit-exact audio repetitions with pre-defined look back time. As soon as a repetition is detected, its look back time is reported. Ideally, no reports should be generated.

histogram.name: Media.AudioChannelLayout
histogram.summary: Audio channel layout in HTML5 media.

histogram.name: Media.AudioCodec
histogram.summary: Audio codec used in HTML5 media.

histogram.name: Media.AudioCodecProfile.AAC
histogram.summary: Audio codec profile used in HTML5 media for AAC playback.

histogram.name: Media.AudioInputCbErrorMac
histogram.summary: Error codes that AudioUnitRender() returns in AUAudioInputStream on Mac.

histogram.name: Media.AudioInputController
histogram.summary: Measures the time taken for AudioInputController::

histogram.name: Media.AudioInputControllerCaptureStartupSuccess
histogram.summary: Whether capture started successfully after an input stream startup was requested.

histogram.name: Media.AudioInputControllerSessionSilenceReport
histogram.summary: Every 15 seconds during the lifetime of an audio input stream, the sound level is measured, see AudioInputController::CheckAudioPower(). If the level is below a certain threshold, it's considered "silence". Otherwise it's considered "audio". This metric shows whether there was only silence, only audio, or both, during the lifetime of a stream. If logged before the first measurement, i.e. if the lifetime is less than 15 seconds, "no measurement" is reported. If there is no audio data provided by the OS during the lifetime of the stream, for example because of a bug, measurements will not be performed and "no measurement" is reported.

histogram.name: Media.AudioInputDeviceManager
histogram.summary: Measures the time taken for AudioInputDeviceManager::

histogram.name: Media.AudioOutputController
histogram.summary: Measures the time taken for AudioOutputController::

histogram.name: Media.AudioOutputController.CallbackError
histogram.summary: A boolean that reflects whether or not an error was reported during audio rendering, reported after a stream has stopped, if it was started.

histogram.name: Media.AudioOutputController.LifeTime
histogram.summary: Time interval from the output stream controller construction to its destruction.

histogram.name: Media.AudioOutputController.ProxyStreamCreationResult
histogram.summary: Whether an AudioOutputController succeeded in creating and opening an output stream proxy. Only logged for the initial creation, subsequent re-creations due to device change events are logged in the Media.AudioOutputController.ProxyStreamCreationResultForDeviceChange histogram.

histogram.name: Media.AudioOutputController.ProxyStreamCreationResultForDeviceChange
histogram.summary: Whether an AudioOutputController succeeded in creating and opening an output stream proxy after receiving a default device change event.

histogram.name: Media.AudioOutputControllerDataNotReady
histogram.summary: Time spent waiting in AudioOutputController::WaitTillDataReady() if the data was not initially available.

histogram.name: Media.AudioOutputControllerPlaybackStartupSuccess
histogram.summary: Whether playback started successfully after stream startup was requested.

histogram.name: Media.AudioOutputResampler.OpenLowLatencyStream
histogram.summary: Whether a low latency stream to be rendered through the resampler was opened successfully or which type of fallback stream was used instead. In case a fake fallback stream is used it means audio rendered through the resampler will be muted. In case fallback fails entirely, renderer callback error will be signalled.

histogram.name: Media.AudioOutputStreamProxy.GetPreferredOutputStreamParametersWin.CreateClientResult
histogram.summary: This histogram is the 3nd potential failure step in CoreAudioUtil::GetPreferredAudioParameters. The success% of this histogram is input for Media.AudioOutputStreamProxy.GetPreferredOutputStreamParametersWin.GetMixFormatResult histogram.

histogram.name: Media.AudioOutputStreamProxy.GetPreferredOutputStreamParametersWin.CreateDeviceEnumeratorResult
histogram.summary: This histogram is the 1st potential failure step in CoreAudioUtil::GetPreferredAudioParameters. The success% of this histogram is input for Media.AudioOutputStreamProxy.GetPreferredOutputStreamParametersWin.CreateDeviceResult histogram.

histogram.name: Media.AudioOutputStreamProxy.GetPreferredOutputStreamParametersWin.CreateDeviceResult
histogram.summary: This histogram is the 2nd potential failure step in CoreAudioUtil::GetPreferredAudioParameters. The success% of this histogram is input for Media.AudioOutputStreamProxy.GetPreferredOutputStreamParametersWin.CreateClientResult histogram.

histogram.name: Media.AudioOutputStreamProxy.GetPreferredOutputStreamParametersWin.GetDevicePeriodResult
histogram.summary: This histogram is the last potential failure step in CoreAudioUtil::GetPreferredAudioParameters.

histogram.name: Media.AudioOutputStreamProxy.GetPreferredOutputStreamParametersWin.GetMixFormatResult
histogram.summary: This histogram is the 4nd potential failure step in CoreAudioUtil::GetPreferredAudioParameters. The success% of this histogram is input for Media.AudioOutputStreamProxy.GetPreferredOutputStreamParametersWin.GetDevicePeriodResult histogram.

histogram.name: Media.AudioOutputStreamProxy.StreamFormat
histogram.summary:
Records format used by AudioManager to create audio output stream proxy. If a fake stream is created it results in muted audio playback.
On Windows, the failure reasons that can lead to fake stream creation are tracked with the following histograms: Media.AudioOutputStreamProxy.GetPreferredOutputStreamParametersWin.CreateDeviceEnumeratorResult Media.AudioOutputStreamProxy.GetPreferredOutputStreamParametersWin.CreateDeviceResult Media.AudioOutputStreamProxy.GetPreferredOutputStreamParametersWin.CreateClientResult Media.AudioOutputStreamProxy.GetPreferredOutputStreamParametersWin.GetMixFormatResult Media.AudioOutputStreamProxy.GetPreferredOutputStreamParametersWin.GetDevicePeriodResult

histogram.name: Media.AudioRendererAudioGlitches
histogram.summary: Captures if render-side audio glitches are detected or not. Sampled when a low-latency output audio stream is destructed.

histogram.name: Media.AudioRendererEvents
histogram.obsolete: Removed Mar 2016 in favor of a new PipelineStatus code.
histogram.summary: Captures statistics for various AudioRendererImpl events.

histogram.name: Media.AudioRendererImpl.SinkStatus
histogram.summary: Status of audio sink used by AudioRendererImpl. If not OK, a NullAudioSink will be used for audio output instead. This is logged for every call to AudioRendererImpl::Initialize, which generally occurs once per active audio session (i.e., between a play and pause). If audio track changes are ever enabled, it may additionally be called for every audio track change.

histogram.name: Media.AudioRendererIpcStreams
histogram.summary: The maximum number of simultaneous audio render streams over IPC created in AudioRendererHost for a render process. Logged at render process shutdown.

histogram.name: Media.AudioRendererIpcStreamsTotal
histogram.summary: The maximum number of simultaneous audio render streams over IPC created in AudioRendererHost for all render processes. Logged at render process shutdown.

histogram.name: Media.AudioRendererMissedDeadline
histogram.summary: Percentage of AudioSyncReader::Read() calls where the renderer missed its realtime deadline.

histogram.name: Media.AudioSampleFormat
histogram.summary: Audio sample format in HTML5 media. Logged when Audio Decoder initializes.

histogram.name: Media.AudioSamplesPerSecond
histogram.summary: Audio samples per second in HTML5 media.

histogram.name: Media.AudioSamplesPerSecondUnexpected
histogram.summary: Audio samples per second in HTML5 media (atypical values, in Hz).

histogram.name: Media.AudioService.AudioManagerStartupTime
histogram.summary: How long it take for the audio manager instance to be created by the audio service, in case the audio service owns the audio manager.

histogram.name: Media.AudioService.HasConnectionsDuration
histogram.summary: The time interval when the audio service has connections, computed from the moment the first bind request is received to the moment when the last connection is closed.

histogram.name: Media.AudioService.HasNoConnectionsDuration
histogram.summary: The time interval when the audio service has no connections, computed from the moment the last connection is closed until quit closure is called or a new bind request is received.

histogram.name: Media.AudioService.ObservedDowntime
histogram.summary: The audio service downtime, observed from the browser process. The downtime interval is measured either from when the audio service listener is initialized to the first audio service start, or from the audio service shutdown to the next startup.

histogram.name: Media.AudioService.ObservedDowntime2
histogram.summary: The audio service downtime, observed from the browser process. The downtime interval is measured from the audio service shutdown to the next startup.

histogram.name: Media.AudioService.ObservedInitialDowntime
histogram.summary: The audio service downtime, observed from the browser process. The downtime interval is measured from when the audio service listener is initialized to the first audio service start.

histogram.name: Media.AudioService.ObservedProcessTerminationStatus
histogram.obsolete: Removed 10/2018. Covered by the more generic stats ChildProcess.Crashed.UtilityProcessHash and ChildProcess.Launched.UtilityProcessHash.
histogram.summary: The termination status of the audio service process, observed from the browser process.

histogram.name: Media.AudioService.ObservedStartStatus
histogram.summary: The start result of the audio service, observed from the browser process.

histogram.name: Media.AudioService.ObservedStartupTime
histogram.summary: The startup time of the audio service, observed from the browser process. The startup time interval is measured from when a connect request results in the audio service being created to the moment when the audio service is started.

histogram.name: Media.AudioService.ObservedUptime
histogram.summary: The uptime of the audio service, observed from the browser process. The uptime interval is measured from when the audio service starts to when it is shut down (either normally or through kill/crash). This metric includes Media.AudioService.Uptime measurements, logged by the audio service on normal shutdown. A difference between Media.AudioService.ObservedUptime and Media.AudioService.Uptime indicates buggy audio service behavior.

histogram.name: Media.AudioService.SystemInfoClient
histogram.summary: The time interval between the moment when a client issues a system information request to the audio service, and the moment it receives the responce. Logged individually for each request type.

histogram.name: Media.AudioService.Uptime
histogram.summary: The uptime of the audio service, observed from the audio service. The uptime interval is measured from the audio service start to the audio service stop.

histogram.name: Media.AudioThreadStatus
histogram.summary: State of the audio thread. A value of "started" is emitted once the hang monitor has been initialized. If the thread is detected as hung later, a value of "hung" is emitted. If the hung thread later recovers a value of "recovered" is emitted.

histogram.name: Media.AudioTrackProcessingStates
histogram.summary: State of the media stream audio track processing, sampled once during the life time of a MediaStreamAudioProcessor.

histogram.name: Media.Autoplay.CrossOrigin.Result
histogram.summary: The result of autoplaying video elements in cross-origin iframes.

histogram.name: Media.AVDA.FrameInformation
histogram.summary: Record a count for each frame sent to the client by AVDA, separated by what type of frame it is: Non Overlay, secure overlay, etc.

histogram.name: Media.AVDA.FrameSentAsOverlay
histogram.summary: Record a count for each frame sent to the client by AVDA. True counts indicate that the frame was an overlay (SurfaceView). False counts are for Non Overlay frames. This will be deprecated in favor of Media.AVDA.FrameInformation in M63.

histogram.name: Media.AVDA.InputQueueTime
histogram.summary: The time that a bitstream buffer spends in AVDA's pending bitstream buffer queue before it is fed to MediaCodec.

histogram.name: Media.AVDA.MissingFormatChanged
histogram.summary: Number of times that AVDA stopped decoding because MediaCodec failed to provide a FORMAT_CHANGED message before sending decoded frames back. True counts indicate instances where FORMAT_CHANGE was missed, while false instances indicate any MediaCodec initialization by AVDA.

histogram.name: Media.AVDA.NumAVDAInstances
histogram.summary: The number of concurrently running AndroidVideoDecodeAccelerators. Logged during each AVDA initialization.

histogram.name: Media.AVDA.VirtualContext
histogram.summary: Number of times that AVDA's deferred rendering encountered a virtual GL context. True counts indicate virtual, false counts indicate not.

histogram.name: Media.AvdaCodecImage.WaitTimeForFrame
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2018. This has been renamed to Media.CodecImage.SurfaceTextureGLOwner.WaitTimeForFrame and Media.CodecImage.ImageReaderGLOwner.WaitTimeForFrame.
histogram.summary: Time spent waiting for a frame to become available in a Non Overlay after requesting that MediaCodec renders it.

histogram.name: Media.BytesReadFromCache
histogram.obsolete: Removed 10/2019 in issue 1000058; no longer needed.
histogram.summary: Kb read by media demuxer from MultiBuffer cache.

histogram.name: Media.BytesReadFromNetwork
histogram.obsolete: Removed 10/2019 in issue 1000058; no longer needed.
histogram.summary: Kb read by from network into MultiBuffer cache.

histogram.name: Media.BytesReceived
histogram.obsolete: Removed 10/2019 in issue 1000058; no longer needed.
histogram.summary: Total number of bytes buffered over the lifetime of a WebMediaPlayer, suffixed by type of playback.

histogram.name: Media.CacheUseful
histogram.summary: Whether a media response might be used to satisfy a future request.

histogram.name: Media.Capabilities.DecodingInfo.Time.Video
histogram.summary: Time spent between receiving a decodingInfo() call and resolving it. It only record successful calls and ignores audio-only.

histogram.name: Media.Capabilities.DecodingInfo.Time.Video.Clear
histogram.summary: Time spent between receiving a decodingInfo() call and resolving it. It only record successful calls and ignores audio-only. This is a subset of Media.Capabilities.DecodingInfo.RequestTime.Video that only takes into account clear content.

histogram.name: Media.Capabilities.DecodingInfo.Time.Video.Encrypted
histogram.summary: Time spent between receiving a decodingInfo() call and resolving it. It only record successful calls and ignores audio-only. This is a subset of Media.Capabilities.DecodingInfo.RequestTime.Video that only takes into account encrypted content.

histogram.name: Media.ChromeArcVideoDecodeAccelerator.InitializeResult
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of Dec 18, 2017. Replaced by Media.GpuArcVideoDecodeAccelerator.InitializeResult.
histogram.summary: Counts of status values returned from calls to ChromeArcVideoDecodeAccelerator::Initialize().

histogram.name: Media.ChromeCast.DelayedAndDroppedFramesPer5Sec
histogram.summary: The average number of delayed and dropped frames for the ChromeCast application. Reported every 5 seconds.

histogram.name: Media.ChromeCast.DisplayedFramesPerSecond
histogram.summary: The average number of displayed frames for the ChromeCast application. Reported every 5 seconds.

histogram.name: Media.ChromeCast.TimeToBufferAv
histogram.summary: Time needed to pre-buffer A/V data before the actual playback for the ChromeCast application.

histogram.name: Media.ChromeCast.TimeToBufferAvAfterAbort
histogram.summary: Time needed to buffer A/V data after an abort for the ChromeCast application.

histogram.name: Media.ChromeCast.TimeToBufferAvAfterUnderrun
histogram.summary: Time needed to buffer A/V data after an underrun for the ChromeCast application.

histogram.name: Media.CodecImage.CodecBufferWaitCoordinator.FrameTimedOut
histogram.summary: Whether we exceeded the timeout in CodecBufferWaitCoordinator:: WaitForFrameAvailable and will proceed without a frame ready.

histogram.name: Media.CodecImage.CodecBufferWaitCoordinator.WaitTimeForFrame
histogram.summary: Time spent waiting for a frame to become available in a Non Overlay (TextureOwner mode) so that MediaCodec can render it. This time is recorded before the frame is rendered.

histogram.name: Media.CodecImage.ImageReaderGLOwner.FrameTimedOut
histogram.obsolete: Removed 07/2019. This has been renamed to Media.CodecImage.CodecBufferWaitCoordinator.FrameTimedOut
histogram.summary: Removed 07/2019. This has been renamed to Media.CodecImage.CodecBufferWaitCoordinator.FrameTimedOut

histogram.name: Media.CodecImage.ImageReaderGLOwner.WaitTimeForFrame
histogram.obsolete: Removed 07/2019. This has been renamed to Media.CodecImage.CodecBufferWaitCoordinator.WaitTimeForFrame
histogram.summary: Removed 07/2019. This has been renamed to Media.CodecImage.CodecBufferWaitCoordinator.WaitTimeForFrame

histogram.name: Media.CodecImage.SurfaceTextureGLOwner.FrameTimedOut
histogram.obsolete: Removed 07/2019. This has been renamed to Media.CodecImage.CodecBufferWaitCoordinator.FrameTimedOut
histogram.summary: Removed 07/2019. This has been renamed to Media.CodecImage.CodecBufferWaitCoordinator.FrameTimedOut

histogram.name: Media.CodecImage.SurfaceTextureGLOwner.WaitTimeForFrame
histogram.obsolete: Removed 07/2019. This has been renamed to Media.CodecImage.CodecBufferWaitCoordinator.WaitTimeForFrame
histogram.summary: Removed 07/2019. This has been renamed to Media.CodecImage.CodecBufferWaitCoordinator.WaitTimeForFrame

histogram.name: Media.Controls.CTR
histogram.summary:
Records events related to click through rate (CTR) calculation.
The CTR can be calculated by computing "Interacted" / "Displayed". "Interacted", for buttons, means that they were clicked. For sliders, it will reflect any change in the internal state and includes other events in addition to clicks. "Displayed" means that the control element was meant to be displayed in the controls. It does not mean that the user have seen the element so is not to be confused with visibility. Furthermore, because the controls UI changes while loading, "Displayed" is only computed when metadata are available unless preloading was disabled on the element or the browser.

histogram.name: Media.Controls.Download
histogram.obsolete: Removed July 2017 in favor of Media.Controls.CTR.DownloadButton.
histogram.summary: Records events and user interactions related to the download button shown on media elements.

histogram.name: Media.Controls.Overflow.TimeToAction
histogram.summary: Records the amount of time the overflow menu is displayed until the user clicked on a menu item. The overflow menu is shown by clicking the three dots on the media controls and is used to show more controls when there is not enough space. This histogram will record up to 100 seconds with accuracy to the second.

histogram.name: Media.Controls.Overflow.TimeToDismiss
histogram.summary: Records the amount of time the overflow menu is displayed until the user dismissed the menu without clicking on a menu item. The overflow menu is shown by clicking the three dots on the media controls and is used to show more controls when there is not enough space. This histogram will record up to 100 seconds with accuracy to the second.

histogram.name: Media.Controls.Show
histogram.obsolete: Removed May 19 2016 in favor of Media.Controls.Show.{Audio,Video}
histogram.summary: Record whether the default media controls were shown and why every time they could be shown.

histogram.name: Media.Controls.Show.Audio
histogram.summary: Record whether the default media controls of an audio element were shown and why every time they could be shown. Note that audio elements that were not insterted to the DOM and do not have a controls attribute are not taken into account.

histogram.name: Media.Controls.Show.Video
histogram.summary: Record whether the default media controls of a video element were shown and why every time they could be shown.

histogram.name: Media.CrosBeamformingDeviceState
