histogram.summary: The state of the user selected virtual beamforming audio device on CrOS. It's logged whenever the format of the audio input stream is queried, which at least happens on stream startup, but could happen at other times as well.

histogram.name: Media.D3D11.DecoderLifetimeProgression
histogram.summary: This enum allows us to count D3D11VideoDecoder specific crashes and initialization failures. This is recorded whenever the decoder is initialized or destroyed.

histogram.name: Media.D3D11.WasVideoSupported
histogram.summary: This enum measures whether or not d3d11 is supported and if not, what the reason is.

histogram.name: Media.DetectedAudioCodec
histogram.obsolete: Removed Sep 15 2015 in favor of Media.DetectedAudioCodecHash
histogram.summary: Audio codec used in HTML5 media.

histogram.name: Media.DetectedAudioCodecHash
histogram.summary: Audio codec used in HTML5 media. Reporting corrected in M51 to include all detected audio codecs, not just those up to and including the first one supported.

histogram.name: Media.DetectedAudioCodecHash.Local
histogram.summary: Audio codec used in HTML5 media for a local file playback.

histogram.name: Media.DetectedContainer
histogram.summary: Container used for HTML5 media. Views that include pre-M34 data will categorize dash (38) and smooth streaming (39) in the "Other" bucket.

histogram.name: Media.DetectedContainer.Local
histogram.summary: Container used for HTML5 media for a local file playback.

histogram.name: Media.DetectedTrackCount.Audio
histogram.summary: Number of detected audio tracks in HTML5 media. Not all may be usable by the player.

histogram.name: Media.DetectedTrackCount.Text
histogram.summary: Number of detected text tracks in HTML5 media. Not all may be usable by the player.

histogram.name: Media.DetectedTrackCount.Video
histogram.summary: Number of detected video tracks in HTML5 media. Not all may be usable by the player.

histogram.name: Media.DetectedVideoCodec
histogram.obsolete: Removed Sep 15 2015 in favor of Media.DetectedVideoCodecHash
histogram.summary: Video codec used in HTML5 media.

histogram.name: Media.DetectedVideoCodecHash
histogram.summary: Video codec used in HTML5 media. Reporting corrected in M51 to include all detected video codecs, not just those up to and including the first one supported.

histogram.name: Media.DetectedVideoCodecHash.Local
histogram.summary: Video codec used in HTML5 media for a local file playback.

histogram.name: Media.DevicePermissionActions
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Measures the actions taken in the media infobar, which prompts the users for device permission.

histogram.name: Media.DroppedFrameCount
histogram.summary: Count of dropped frames between pipeline start and stop. Media pipeline starts/stops when an HTML5 video is loaded/unloaded respectively in the browser.

histogram.name: Media.Duration
histogram.obsolete: Removed 03/2017 in favor of Media.Duration2 with larger max bucket.
histogram.summary: Duration in milliseconds of HTML5 media (when known).

histogram.name: Media.Duration2
histogram.summary: Duration in milliseconds of HTML5 media (when known).

histogram.name: Media.DXVAVDA.CreateDecoderStatus
histogram.obsolete: Removed 12/2018 since we had enough data to make a decision.
histogram.summary: Did IsResolutionSupportedForDevice() succeed or fail during ID3D11VideoDevice::CreateVideoDecoder().

histogram.name: Media.DXVAVDA.ErrorLine
histogram.summary: Whenever an error is logged from the DXVAVDA, this records the line in dxva_video_decode_accelerator.cc where the failure occurred.

histogram.name: Media.DXVAVDA.GetDecoderConfigStatus
histogram.obsolete: Removed 12/2018 since we had enough data to make a decision.
histogram.summary: Did IsResolutionSupportedForDevice() succeed or fail during ID3D11VideoDevice::GetVideoDecoderConfig().

histogram.name: Media.DXVAVDA.PictureBufferErrorLine
histogram.summary: Whenever an error is logged from the DXVAVDA picture buffer code, this records the line in dxva_picture_buffer_win.cc where the failure occurred. This histogram can only meaningfully be compared between different versions of Chromium if no line numbers in that file changed between those versions.

histogram.name: Media.DXVAVDA.PictureBufferMechanism
histogram.summary: This enum measures how often each of the various DXVAVDA PictureBuffer display mechanisms are used. Each count represents the mechanism selected at the end of DXVA::Initialize; i.e., there is one count per decoder.

histogram.name: Media.DXVAVDA.UseD3D11
histogram.summary: Records how many times the DXVA VDA successfully initializes a D3D11 device vs. D3D9 device.

histogram.name: Media.EME
histogram.summary: Result of EME promises that were handled by Chromium code.

histogram.name: Media.EME.addKey
histogram.obsolete: Removed 02/2016 with removal of prefixed EME.
histogram.summary: addKey result

histogram.name: Media.EME.cancelKeyRequest
histogram.obsolete: Removed 02/2016 with removal of prefixed EME.
histogram.summary: cancelKeyRequest result.

histogram.name: Media.EME.CdmFileIO.FileSizeKBOnError
histogram.summary: Size in KB of the last file successfully read by the CDM through CDM FileIO before a specific error happens. This is reported only when the error happens, which should be rare.

histogram.name: Media.EME.CdmFileIO.FileSizeKBOnFirstRead
histogram.summary: Size in KB of the first file successfully read by the CDM through CDM FileIO. This is recorded once per CDM instance.

histogram.name: Media.EME.CdmFileIO.ReadTime
histogram.summary: The actual time spent by the CDM reading a file.

histogram.name: Media.EME.CdmFileIO.TimeTo
histogram.summary: The actual time spent by the CDM accessing a file. This is only recorded for successful operations.

histogram.name: Media.EME.CdmFileIO.WriteTime
histogram.summary: The actual time spent by the CDM writing a file.

histogram.name: Media.EME.CdmHostVerificationStatus
histogram.summary: The status of CDM host verification. This is reported per CDM load.

histogram.name: Media.EME.CdmInterfaceVersion
histogram.summary: Chromium could support multiple versions of CDM interface. This metric records the CDM interface version that the CDM created by Chromium uses. This is recorded once per CDM instance.

histogram.name: Media.EME.CdmLoadErrorCode
histogram.summary: The error code of a library CDM load failure. Only reported on Windows.

histogram.name: Media.EME.CdmLoadResult
histogram.summary: The result from an attempt to load a library CDM.

histogram.name: Media.EME.CdmLoadTime
histogram.summary: The time spent to load a library CDM.

histogram.name: Media.EME.CreateCdm
histogram.summary: Whether the CDM instance is created successfully. Reported each time a CDM instance creation is attempted, usually as a result of Javascript call of createMediaKeys().

histogram.name: Media.EME.CreateCdmTime
histogram.summary: The time it takes to create the CDM instance.

histogram.name: Media.EME.EncryptedEvent
histogram.summary: Whether EME encrypted event has been fired. Every time a WebMediaPlayerImpl object is created a "false" value is reported to provide a baseline. Every time an encrypted event is fired, a "true" value will be reported. Note that it is possible to have multiple encrypted events during the lifetime of a WebMediaPlayerImpl object.

histogram.name: Media.EME.EncryptedMediaEnabled
histogram.summary: Whether encrypted media is enabled when requestMediaKeySystemAccess() is called. User can enable and disable encrypted media in content settings. Recorded when requestMediaKeySystemAccess() is called but will be reported at most once per renderer process.

histogram.name: Media.EME.EncryptionScheme.Initial.Audio
histogram.summary: The encryption scheme used by the audio stream in an HTML5 video. Reported when metadata is available if the initial config is encrypted.

histogram.name: Media.EME.EncryptionScheme.Initial.Video
histogram.summary: The encryption scheme used by the video stream in an HTML5 video. Reported when metadata is available if the initial config is encrypted.

histogram.name: Media.EME.generateKeyRequest
histogram.obsolete: Removed 02/2016 with removal of prefixed EME.
histogram.summary: generateKeyRequest result.

histogram.name: Media.EME.IsIncognito
histogram.summary: Whether a given WebMediaPlayer instance, with a CDM attached, is played in an incognito window or in Chrome OS guest mode. Players that never started playback are excluded. Recorded once at time of player destruction.

histogram.name: Media.EME.KeyAdded
histogram.obsolete: Removed 02/2016 with removal of prefixed EME.
histogram.summary: KeyAdded event count.

histogram.name: Media.EME.KeyError
histogram.obsolete: Removed 02/2016 with removal of prefixed EME.
histogram.summary: KeyError event count.

histogram.name: Media.EME.KeyStatusSystemCode
histogram.summary: System code count associated with key status.

histogram.name: Media.EME.KeySystemSupport.Widevine
histogram.obsolete: Removed 02/2016 with removal of prefixed EME.
histogram.summary: Key system support query status and result. Each enum value will be reported at most once per renderer process.

histogram.name: Media.EME.LibraryCdmAvailable
histogram.summary: Whether the CDM is available for the key system or not. In normal cases, this is reported once per render process if the EME API is used. In rare cases it could be reported more than once if the CDM is not available and then is component updated.

histogram.name: Media.EME.MaxCdmProcessCount
histogram.summary: Reported at most once during browser process shutdown the max number of concurrent CDM processes at any time during the lifetime of that browser process. Will NOT be reported if no CDM process has ever been created.

histogram.name: Media.EME.MediaCryptoAvailable
histogram.summary: Whether MediaCrypto is available on a MediaDrm-based CDM. Reported once per MediaDrmBridge creation. In normal cases it should always be available.

histogram.name: Media.EME.MediaDrm.GetOriginIdResult
histogram.summary: The status of every attempt to obtain a pre-provisioned origin ID to be used by MediaDrm. This will only be reported for applications that use MediaDrmOriginIdManager.

histogram.name: Media.EME.MediaDrm.PreprovisionedOriginId.NonPerAppProvisioningDevice
histogram.summary: The number of pre-provisioned origin IDs available shortly after startup on devices that do not support per-application provisioning.

histogram.name: Media.EME.MediaDrm.PreprovisionedOriginId.PerAppProvisioningDevice
histogram.summary: The number of pre-provisioned origin IDs available shortly after startup on devices that support per-application provisioning.

histogram.name: Media.EME.MediaDrmBridge.KeySystemSupport
histogram.summary: Whether MediaDrmBridge supports the key system. Reported once per MediaDrmBridge creation. In normal cases it should always be supported.

histogram.name: Media.EME.MojoCdm.ConnectionError
histogram.summary: Whether connection error has happened for MojoCdm. Every time a MojoCdm object is created a "false" value is reported to provide a baseline. Every time a mojo connection error happened, a "true" value will be reported, which typically means a remote CDM process crash.

histogram.name: Media.EME.NeedKey
histogram.summary: EME NeedKey event count.

histogram.name: Media.EME.OutputProtection
histogram.summary: Output protection query status and result. One query and one positive (no unprotected external links) result (if any) are reported per CDM instance.

histogram.name: Media.EME.RequestMediaKeySystemAccess
histogram.summary: Key system support query status and result, as reported by RequestMediaKeySystemAccess. Each value will be reported at most once per renderer process.

histogram.name: Media.EME.SystemCode
histogram.summary: System code count in promise rejection.

histogram.name: Media.EME.TimeTo
histogram.summary: The time it takes to resolve a EME promise.

histogram.name: Media.EME.UrlProvisionFetcher.ResponseCode
histogram.summary: The net error or HTTP response code of provisioning requests made by UrlProvisionFetcher on Android and Cast devices.

histogram.name: Media.EME.Widevine.VideoCapability.HasEmptyRobustness
histogram.summary: Whether the robustness level of any video capability in the supported configuration passed into requestMediaKeySystemAccess() is empty. This is specific to the Widevine key system.

histogram.name: Media.Engagement.Clear
histogram.summary: Records the reason why the Media Engagement data was cleared. Partial changes and full wipeout will both be recorded as one event.

histogram.name: Media.Engagement.PreloadedList.CheckResult
histogram.summary: Recorded when the Media Engagement Preloaded List is checked whether a string is present on that list. If the check was successful then the result of the check is recorded in this histogram. If the check was not successful then the reason for the check failing is recorded.

histogram.name: Media.Engagement.PreloadedList.LoadResult
histogram.summary: Recorded when data is loaded into the Media Engagement Preloaded List. If the load is successful then "loaded" is recorded to this histogram. If the load was not successful then the reason why is recorded to this histogram.

histogram.name: Media.Engagement.PreloadedList.LoadTime
histogram.summary: Time taken to load the Media Engagement Preloaded List. It should be recorded once per load of the list which will be once per start up with additional loads when the list is updated while running.

histogram.name: Media.Engagement.PreloadedList.LookupTime
histogram.summary: Time taken to lookup an entry in the Media Engagement Preloaded List. This is recorded for every lookup, regardless of its success.

histogram.name: Media.Engagement.ScoreAtPlayback
histogram.summary: The media engagement score recorded at the time of playback. It is converted to a percentage so should be divided by 100 to get the actual score. It is only recorded for playback that is either lacking an audio track or not muted.

histogram.name: Media.Engagement.ScoreAtStartup
histogram.obsolete: Removed 11/2018 in Issue 900679 as we are no longer using this.
histogram.summary: Media engagement scores for each origin, recorded at Chrome startup. It is converted to a percentage so should be divided by 100 to get the actual score.

histogram.name: Media.Engagement.Session
histogram.summary: Records creation and playback status of a Media Engagement Session. This is recorded at the same time as the data is commited into the content settings database.

histogram.name: Media.Engagement.Session.Restored
histogram.summary: Records creation and playback status of a restored Media Engagement Session. This is recorded at the same time as the data is commited into the content settings database. Restored information are recorded in addition of regular ones (Media.Engagement.Session).

histogram.name: Media.Engagement.SignificantPlayers.PlayerNotAdded.AfterFirstTime
histogram.summary:
Recorded when the Media Engagement service doesn't determine a player to be significant. If a player is not significant the reasons why will be individually recorded to this histogram each time except for the first time which is recorded to a different histogram.
The significant state of a player is reevaluated any time the state of the player changes (e.g. muted, playing/paused). If the player was not considered signficiant and still is not then we will record the reasons why in this histogram.

histogram.name: Media.Engagement.SignificantPlayers.PlayerNotAdded.FirstTime
histogram.summary:
Recorded when the Media Engagement service doesn't determine a player to be significant. If a player is not significant the reasons why will be individually recorded to this histogram the first time only.
The significant state of a player is reevaluated any time the state of the player changes (e.g. muted, playing/paused). If the player was not considered signficiant and still is not then we will record the reasons why in this histogram.

histogram.name: Media.Engagement.SignificantPlayers.PlayerRemoved
histogram.summary:
Recorded when the Media Engagement service no longer determines a player to be significant. If a player is no longer significant, the reasons why will be individually recorded to this histogram.
The significant state of a player is re-evaluted any time the state of the player changes (e.g. muted, playing/paused). If the player was previously considered significant and is no longer considered that we will record the reasons why in this histogram.

histogram.name: Media.Engagement.URLsDeletedScoreReduction
histogram.summary: Recorded when the history is cleared and the media engagement data are similarly cleared. It records the reduction in score for each affected origin. The algorithm is made to reduce the score by 0.0 as much as possible and if it fails, the score will actually become 0.0 so every reduction also represent the value of the previous score.

histogram.name: Media.FallbackHardwareAudioBitsPerChannel
histogram.obsolete: Removed May 2018; has been 32-bit for years now.
histogram.summary: Bits per channel of the hardware audio device which failed to open in low latency mode and required high latency fallback.

histogram.name: Media.FallbackHardwareAudioChannelCount
histogram.summary: Channel count of the hardware audio device which failed to open in low latency mode and required high latency fallback.

histogram.name: Media.FallbackHardwareAudioChannelLayout
histogram.summary: Channel layout of the hardware audio device which failed to open in low latency mode and required high latency fallback.

histogram.name: Media.FallbackHardwareAudioSamplesPerSecond
histogram.summary: Samples per second of the hardware audio device which failed to open in low latency mode and required high latency fallback.

histogram.name: Media.FallbackHardwareAudioSamplesPerSecondUnexpected
histogram.summary: Samples per second of the hardware audio device (atypical values, in Hz) which failed to open in low latency mode and required high latency fallback.

histogram.name: Media.FallbackToHighLatencyAudioPath
histogram.summary: Whether Chrome had to fallback to the high latency audio path or not.

histogram.name: Media.Feeds.Feed.ReadResult
histogram.summary: Recorded when a Media Feed is loaded from the database. If there was an error reading from the database then the result will include the reason.

histogram.name: Media.Feeds.FeedItem.ReadResult
histogram.summary: Recorded when a Media Feed item is loaded from the database. If there was an error reading from the database then the result will include the reason.

histogram.name: Media.Feeds.Fetch.Size
histogram.summary: Records the size of the fetched feed when a Media Feed is periodically fetched in the background.

histogram.name: Media.Feeds.SafeSearch.Result
histogram.summary: Recorded when a Media Feed Item is checked against Safe Search. This happens automatically when Chrome fetches the feed so long as the user has opted into Safe Search checking.

histogram.name: Media.Fling.DelayedAndDroppedFramesPer5Sec
histogram.obsolete: Removed 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.

histogram.name: Media.GlobalMediaControls.DismissReason
histogram.summary: Recorded each time a media session is dismissed or removed from the Global Media Controls. Records the reason why it happened (e.g. user clicked the dismiss button or playback stopped).

histogram.name: Media.GlobalMediaControls.InteractionDelayAfterPause
histogram.summary: Recorded each time a paused media session is interacted with (e.g. playing the media or clicking on the notification to go back to the tab). Records the time passed since it was last interacted with (or since when it was paused if this is the first post-pause interaction).

histogram.name: Media.GlobalMediaControls.RepeatUsage
histogram.summary: Recorded each time the Global Media Controls dialog is opened. True if this was repeat usage of the dialog (e.g. if it's not the first time the dialog has been opened this session).

histogram.name: Media.GlobalMediaControls.UserActionFocus
histogram.summary: The focused state of the WebContents associated with the MediaSession that the user is interacting with via the Global Media Controls. Recorded when the user presses an action on the Global Media Controls dialog (e.g. play).

histogram.name: Media.GPU.HasEverLostContext
histogram.summary: Records when a context provider is acquired in media, and when it's released because a context loss has been detected.

histogram.name: Media.GpuArcVideoDecodeAccelerator.InitializeResult
histogram.summary: Counts of status values returned from calls to GpuArcVideoDecodeAccelerator::Initialize().

histogram.name: Media.GpuMemoryBufferVideoFramePool.UnsupportedFormat
histogram.summary: GpuMemoryBufferVideoFramePool is called with an unsupported VideoFrame pixel format. Emitted on VideoFrame arrival.

histogram.name: Media.GpuVideoDecoderError
histogram.obsolete: Removed in December 2019 because GpuVideoDecoderError is gone and crbug.com/902968 is fixed.
histogram.summary: Counts of video decode errors reported to GpuVideoDecoder.

histogram.name: Media.GpuVideoDecoderInitializeStatus
histogram.obsolete: Removed in December 2019 because GpuVideoDecoderError is gone and crbug.com/902968 is fixed.
histogram.summary: Results of attempts to GpuVideoDecoder::Initialize().

histogram.name: Media.GpuVideoFrameFactory.SharedContextStateResult
histogram.summary: The error code returned when GetSharedContextState() fails or MakeCurrent() on the SharedContextState fails.

histogram.name: Media.HardwareAudioBitsPerChannel
histogram.obsolete: Removed May 2018; has been 32-bit for years now.
histogram.summary: Bits per channel of the hardware audio device.

histogram.name: Media.HardwareAudioChannelCount
histogram.summary: Channel count of the hardware audio device.

histogram.name: Media.HardwareAudioChannelLayout
histogram.summary: Channel layout of the hardware audio device.

histogram.name: Media.HardwareAudioSamplesPerSecond
histogram.summary: Samples per second of the hardware audio device.

histogram.name: Media.HardwareAudioSamplesPerSecondUnexpected
histogram.summary: Samples per second of the hardware audio device (atypical values, in Hz).

histogram.name: Media.HardwareKeyPressed
histogram.summary: Records media key presses that are handled by the HardwareKeyMediaController.

histogram.name: Media.HasEverPlayed
histogram.summary: Whether a given WebMediaPlayer instance, after preload, started playback; recorded once at time of player destruction.

histogram.name: Media.HighLatencyAudioCaptureStartupSuccess
histogram.summary: Whether capture started successfully after a high-latency input stream startup was requested.

histogram.name: Media.History.DatabaseSize
histogram.summary: The size of the Media History database. Recorded once on startup.

histogram.name: Media.History.Init.Result
histogram.summary: Recorded when Media History is initialized when the browser is started. This is not recorded in incognito mode or if "save browsing history" is disabled.

histogram.name: Media.History.Playback.WriteResult
histogram.summary: Recorded when Media History records a playback to the database. Playbacks are recorded when the player is destroyed and are are recorded on a per-player level and might be called multiple times on a single page. Playbacks are not recorded in incognito mode or if "save browsing history" is disabled.

histogram.name: Media.History.Session.WriteResult
histogram.summary: Recorded when Media History records a sessions to the database. Sessions are recorded once per WebContents on navigation if there was playback on the page. Playbacks are not recorded in incognito mode or if "save browsing history" is disabled.

histogram.name: Media.InfoLoadDelay
histogram.obsolete: Removed June 2017. Found that the MediaInfoLoader class (the only user of this histogram) is no longer used by anyone.
histogram.summary: The time it takes to perform redirect tracking and a CORS access check while preparing to play a media file.

histogram.name: Media.Initialize.Windows
histogram.obsolete: Removed 05/2015 in Issue 1141703002. FFmpeg is now statically linked.
histogram.summary: Errors returned by LoadLibraryEx on Windows while attempting to load ffmpegsumo.dll.

histogram.name: Media.InputErrorMac
histogram.summary: Error codes that we encounter while setting up an AUAudioInputStream on Mac.

histogram.name: Media.InputInvalidSampleRateMac
histogram.obsolete: Removed and removed from code as of 02/2016.
histogram.summary: Invalid input sample rate when calling AUAudioInputStream::Open on Mac.

histogram.name: Media.InputStreamDuration
histogram.summary: Duration in milliseconds of low-latency audio input streams. Sampled when the stream is closed by the AudioInputController.

histogram.name: Media.InputStreamDurationWithoutCallback
histogram.summary: Duration in milliseconds of low-latency audio input streams which never got any callbacks.

histogram.name: Media.IsStreaming
histogram.summary: Whether the WMPI data source is streaming (does not support range requests).

histogram.name: Media.Learning.BinaryThreshold.Aggregate
histogram.summary:
Confusion matrix for UMA-reported regresssion tasks for media local learning experiments. Each bucket is interpreted as follows:
TrueNegative - prediction was negative, observation was negative. FalseNegative - prediction was negative, observation was positive. FalsePositive - prediction was positive, observation was negative. TruePositive - prediction was positive, observation was positive.
For those observations for which we could not make a prediction due to data sparsity, the "no-prediction" buckets indicate whether the observation was positive or negative.

histogram.name: Media.Learning.BinaryThreshold.ByFeature
histogram.summary:
Confusion matrices, split out by the feature index used to train the model. Each confusion matrix has 6 entries:
TrueNegative - prediction was negative, observation was negative. FalseNegative - prediction was negative, observation was positive. FalsePositive - prediction was positive, observation was negative. TruePositive - prediction was positive, observation was positive. No-prediction Negative - no prediction made, observation was negative. No-prediction Positive - no prediction made, observation was positive.
The confusion matrix entry will also be labelled with a bucket number, which for this histogram represents the index of the feature that was used. The feature index matches |LearningTask::feature_descriptions| for the appropriate task.

histogram.name: Media.Learning.BinaryThreshold.ByTrainingWeight
histogram.summary:
Confusion matrices, split out by the total amount of training data used to train the model. Each confusion matrix has 6 entries:
TrueNegative - prediction was negative, observation was negative. FalseNegative - prediction was negative, observation was positive. FalsePositive - prediction was positive, observation was negative. TruePositive - prediction was positive, observation was positive. No-prediction Negative - no prediction made, observation was negative. No-prediction Positive - no prediction made, observation was positive.
The bucket number mentioned in the enum label indicates how much weight the training set had. To convert from bucket number to an actual weight range, one must know the particular settings in the corresponding LearningTask.
Please see |LearningTask::max_reporting_weight| and |LearningTask::num_reporting_weight_buckets| for the the appropriate task.

histogram.name: Media.Learning.MediaCapabilities.DroppedFrameRatioTask
histogram.summary:
Confusion matrix for MediaCapabilities local learning experiment, using a lookup table and the base MediaCapabilities features (resolution, frame rate, video format). A positive outcome is smooth playback, while a negative outcome is not smooth. For example, a False Negative indicates that we predicted a negative result (not smooth playback), but we later observed that the result was positive (smooth playback).
For those observations for which we could not make a prediction due to data sparsity, the "no-prediction" buckets indicate whether the observation was positive or negative.
This is very similar to the existing MediaCapabilities implementation, but uses the same training set as other learners.
The "BaseTable" variant uses a lookup table as the model, and uses the original MediaCapabilities features (resolution, frame rate, video format).
The "BaseTree" variant uses ExtraTrees model and the same features as the BaseTable variant.
The "EnhancedTree" variant uses ExtraTrees, plus additional features such as NetworkType. The exact set of features is still in flux, so be sure to restrict reporting to a particular Chromium version.

histogram.name: Media.LinuxAudioIO
histogram.summary: Audio IO layer used by the Linux OS, sampled once at startup of the browser.

histogram.name: Media.LoadType
histogram.summary: Load type of HTML5 media, such as URL, MediaSource and MediaStream.

histogram.name: Media.LocalRendererSinkStates
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: State of the WebRtc local renderer, sampled once during the lifetime of a local renderer.

histogram.name: Media.LockScreenControls.Hidden
histogram.summary: The reason why the lock screen media controls were hidden if they were previously shown. Recorded when the controls are destroyed by the lock screen (e.g. unlock, switch user, shutdown).

histogram.name: Media.LockScreenControls.Shown
histogram.summary: Whether the lock screen media controls were shown and if they will not be shown the reason why.

histogram.name: Media.LockScreenControls.UserAction
histogram.summary: The action (e.g. pause) that a user clicked on the lock screen media controls.

histogram.name: Media.LowLatencyAudioCaptureStartupSuccess
histogram.summary: Whether capture started successfully after a low-latency input stream startup was requested.

histogram.name: Media.MCVD.ForwardVideoFrameTiming
histogram.summary: The time between MediaCodec::dequeueOutputBuffer() and the dispatch of the corresponding VideoFrame from MediaCodecVideoDecoder.

histogram.name: Media.MeanTimeBetweenRebuffers
histogram.summary: The total watch time (see Media.WatchTime) of a given playback divided by the number of rebuffering events that occured during that playback.

histogram.name: Media.MediaElement.ContentTypeParseable
histogram.summary: Whether the content type provided to HTMLMediaElement would parse with ParsedContentType or not.

histogram.name: Media.MediaElement.PlaybackPositionIsInfinity
histogram.obsolete: Removed and removed from code as of January 2019.
histogram.summary: Whether or not the MediaElement is at a playback position of infinity when checking if playback has ended.

histogram.name: Media.MediaElement.PlayPromiseReject
histogram.summary: Recorded when an HTMLMediaElement play() promise is rejected (handled or not) with the type of rejection.

histogram.name: Media.MediaKeysListener.RegisterHotKeyResult
histogram.summary: Whether or not the MediaKeysListener was able to successfully register a hot key. Failure typically indicates that the hotkey was already registered by a different application.

histogram.name: Media.MediaRecorder.VEAError
histogram.summary: The errors HW video encode encounters in MediaRecorder.

histogram.name: Media.MediaRecorder.VEAUsed
histogram.summary: Whether HW video encode is used in MediaRecorder.

histogram.name: Media.MicrophoneMuted
histogram.summary: Heuristically detects if the user has muted the microphone or not. Measured approximately four times per minute.

histogram.name: Media.MicrophoneVolume
histogram.obsolete: Removed 08/2018. Histogram Eraser marked this histogram as unnecessary. See https://crbug.com/871467 for details.
histogram.summary: Level of the microphone volume measured in percent. This value can be larger than 100% on Linux. Measured approximately four times per minute.

histogram.name: Media.Midi.InputPorts
histogram.summary: Connected input port numbers on the initialization.

histogram.name: Media.Midi.OutputPorts
histogram.summary: Connected output port numbers on the initialization.

histogram.name: Media.Midi.ResultOnShutdown
histogram.summary: The final status of MidiManager on destruction. This can monitor unexpected failures on initializing platform dependent MIDI stuff.

histogram.name: Media.Midi.SendReceiveUsage
histogram.summary: Reports whether any data was sent or received by a MidiManager. Recorded once per MidiManager instantiation, upon destruction. MidiManager is instantiated when the first Web MIDI client starts a session, and destroyed when the last client ends the session.

histogram.name: Media.Midi.SysExMessageSizeUpTo1MB
histogram.summary: Reports sysex message size.

histogram.name: Media.Midi.Usage
histogram.summary: The MidiManager instance use count, but this is not intended to understand the real API usage because this can be counted by feature scanning scripts that are often used for footprinting. You may want to track SendReceiveUsage to monitor only instances that actually send or receive MIDI messages.

histogram.name: Media.MojoVideoDecoder.ActiveInstances
histogram.summary: Records the number of active MojoVideoDecoderService instances that are alive each time an instance becomes active. An instance is active if Decode() has been called at least once. This provides an estimate of the concurrent hardware resource requirements.

histogram.name: Media.MojoVideoDecoder.Decode
histogram.summary: Delay between a DecodeBuffer (and encoded chunk) input and a decoded VideoFrame being produced by the remote MojoVideoDecoder. Output once per successful decode.

histogram.name: Media.MojoVideoDecoder.InitialPlaybackErrorCodecCounter
histogram.summary: Total number of instances of MojoVideoDecoder used by a page (i.e. render frame) when an instance of MojoVideoDecoder encountered a decoding error or a crash during the first 150 frames of a video stream.

histogram.name: Media.MojoVideoDecoder.InitialPlaybackSuccessCodecCounter
histogram.summary: Total number of instances of MojoVideoDecoder used by a page (i.e. render frame) when an instance of MojoVideoDecoder successfuly decode the first 150 frames of a video stream.

histogram.name: Media.MSE.AudioCodec
histogram.summary: Audio codec used in Media Source Extensions playback. Set when MediaSource addSourceBuffer() is successfully called during playback. Also set twice during a successful SourceBuffer changeType() operation. See issue 535738 for reworking MSE codec histograms to record on each successfully parsed initialization segment (possibly filtered to record only when actual new codec configurations are parsed).

histogram.name: Media.MSE.AudioSpliceDurationType
histogram.obsolete: Removed 05/2018. Splicing no longer performed on buffers with estimated duration. See https://crbug.com/396634.
histogram.summary: Categorizes MSE audio splicing by the type of duration used in the overlapped buffer.

histogram.name: Media.MSE.CodecChangeTime
histogram.summary: Duration of config changes that include codec changes, measured from before decoder flush until after the first output frame is returned. The HW/SW suffix indicates the type of the decoder that was ultimately selected.

histogram.name: Media.MSE.DemuxerDestructionTime
histogram.summary: Amount of time taken to destroy one ChunkDemuxer object, not including initial background task scheduling delay.

histogram.name: Media.MSE.DetectedShakaPackagerInMp4
histogram.summary: Tracks if ShakaPackager metadata was detected in the MP4 'moov' atom during MSE based media playback. Recorded for every 'moov' atom, which is expected once per asset. Multiple assets may be played within the same MSE session.

histogram.name: Media.MSE.DetectedTrackCount.Audio
histogram.summary: Number of detected audio tracks in Media Source Extensions playback. Not all may be usable by the player.

histogram.name: Media.MSE.DetectedTrackCount.Text
histogram.summary: Number of detected text tracks in Media Source Extensions playback. Not all may be usable by the player. This count includes only explicitly signalled tracks in MSE initialization segments parsed from WebM or ISO BMFF bytestreams.

histogram.name: Media.MSE.DetectedTrackCount.Video
histogram.summary: Number of detected video tracks in Media Source Extensions playback. Not all may be usable by the player.

histogram.name: Media.MSE.ExecutionContext
histogram.summary: For each MediaSource instance, records the type of thread that created the instance.

histogram.name: Media.MSE.LateAudioFrames
histogram.obsolete: Deleted along with browser side MSE implementation.
histogram.summary: Relative number of late audio frames wrt total number of audio frames in MSE playback, multiplied by one million. The audio frame is considered late if it might cause an underrun, i.e. comes from decoder when audio buffer is already depleted.

histogram.name: Media.MSE.LateVideoFrames
histogram.obsolete: Deleted along with browser side MSE implementation.
histogram.summary: Relative number of late video frames wrt total number of video frames in MSE playback, multiplied by one million. The video frame is late if it missed its presentation time as determined by PTS when it comes from decoder. The rendering policy (i.e. render or skip) does not affect it.

histogram.name: Media.MSE.Mp4ConsecutiveEmptySamples
histogram.summary: Reports the number of consecutive empty samples in a sequence within a track run. There can be zero or more such sequences in a track run, and each sequence is reported separately. This metric is expected to inform a heuristic to realistically bound the parsing complexity of track runs that may contain empty samples. See https://crbug.com/907268.

histogram.name: Media.MSE.Mp4EmptySamplesInTRun
histogram.summary: Reports the number of empty samples within a track run. This metric is expected to inform a heuristic to realistically bound the parsing complexity of track runs that may contain empty samples. See https://crbug.com/907268.

histogram.name: Media.MSE.Mp4SampleSize
histogram.summary: Reports the size of each sample parsed. This metric is expected to inform a heuristic to realistically bound the parsing complexity of track runs that may contain empty or huge samples. See https://crbug.com/907268.

histogram.name: Media.MSE.Mp4TrunSampleCount
histogram.summary: Reports the number of samples in each track run. This metric is expected to inform a heuristic to realistically bound the parsing complexity of track runs that may contain huge numbers of samples. See https://crbug.com/907268.

histogram.name: Media.MSE.NumberOfTracks
histogram.summary: Number of tracks specified to AddId() for Media Source Extensions playback. May be called multiple times per element if playback is dynamically altered.

histogram.name: Media.MSE.Playback
histogram.summary: Whether Media Source Extensions is specified for playback of Media elements. Sampled when media pipeline starts.

histogram.name: Media.MSE.PlaybackDuration
histogram.obsolete: Deleted along with browser side MSE implementation.
histogram.summary: Duration of an uninterrupted MSE playback. This is the time interval between the playback starts or resumes and the moment when user stops the playback by pressing pause, initiating a seek etc. Measured in media time in milliseconds.

histogram.name: Media.MSE.Starvations
histogram.obsolete: Deleted along with browser side MSE implementation.
histogram.summary: Relative number of starvations wrt total number of frames in MSE playback, multiplied by one million. Starvation happens when the player interrupts the regular playback and asks for more data, conditions are player-specific.

histogram.name: Media.MSE.VideoCodec
histogram.summary: Video codec used in Media Source Extensions playback. Set when MediaSource addSourceBuffer() is successfully called during playback. Also set twice during a successful SourceBuffer changeType() operation. See issue 535738 for reworking MSE codec histograms to record on each successfully parsed initialization segment (possibly filtered to record only when actual new codec configurations are parsed).

histogram.name: Media.MSE.VideoCodec.MP4
histogram.summary: Video codec used in Media Source Extensions playback if the media container is MP4. Set when MediaSource addSourceBuffer() is successfully called during playback. Also set twice during a successful SourceBuffer changeType() operation. See issue 535738 for reworking MSE codec histograms to record on each successfully parsed initialization segment (possibly filtered to record only when actual new codec configurations are parsed).

histogram.name: Media.MSE.VideoCodec.WebM
histogram.summary: Video codec used in Media Source Extensions playback if the media container is WebM. Set when MediaSource addSourceBuffer() is successfully called during playback. Also set twice during a successful SourceBuffer changeType() operation. See issue 535738 for reworking MSE codec histograms to record on each successfully parsed initialization segment (possibly filtered to record only when actual new codec configurations are parsed).

histogram.summary: The audio bit rate as reported by the Netflix application. May be reported multiple times as network conditions change during playback.

histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of audio channels as reported by the Netflix application. May be reported multiple times as network conditions change during playback.

histogram.obsolete: Removed 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The average number of delayed and dropped frames for the Netflix application. Reported every 5 seconds.

histogram.name: Media.Netflix.DisplayedFramesPerSecond
