histogram.name: Media.Netflix.VideoBitrate
histogram.obsolete: Removed 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Video bit rate as reported by the Netflix application. May be reported multiple times as network conditions change during playback.

histogram.name: Media.Netflix.VideoHeight
histogram.obsolete: Removed 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Video height as reported by the Netflix application. May be reported multiple times as network conditions change during playback.

histogram.name: Media.Notification.ArtworkPresent
histogram.summary: Whether the media notification (including ones for Cast sessions) is displaying artwork. This is recorded when the artwork is updated by the underlying media session.

histogram.name: Media.Notification.Cast.ArtworkPresent
histogram.summary: Whether the Cast media notification is displaying artwork. This is recorded a few seconds after a notification is shown for the first time, which is different from when Media.Notification.ArtworkPresent is recorded.

histogram.name: Media.Notification.Cast.Count
histogram.summary: The number of Cast media notifications that are currently being displayed. This is recorded when a media notification starts being displayed.

histogram.name: Media.Notification.Cast.MetadataPresent
histogram.summary: Whether the Cast media notification is displaying certain types of metadata. This is recorded a few seconds after a notification is shown for the first time, which is different from when Media.Notification.MetadataPresent is recorded.

histogram.name: Media.Notification.Cast.UserAction
histogram.summary: The action (e.g. pause) that a user clicked on a Cast media notification (including ones for Cast sessions).

histogram.name: Media.Notification.Click
histogram.summary: The type of media notification the user clicked to go back to Chrome.

histogram.name: Media.Notification.Count
histogram.summary: The number of media notifications that are currently being displayed (including ones for Cast sessions). This is recorded when a media notification starts being displayed.

histogram.name: Media.Notification.MetadataPresent
histogram.summary: Whether the media notification (including ones for Cast sessions) is displaying certain types of metadata. This is recorded when the metadata is updated by the underlying media session.

histogram.name: Media.Notification.Source
histogram.summary: The source of the underlying media session (e.g. ARC, web, Cast) that displayed the media notification. This is recorded when a notification is available to be shown.

histogram.name: Media.Notification.UserAction
histogram.summary: The action (e.g. pause) that a user clicked on a media notification (including ones for Cast sessions).

histogram.name: Media.OutputStreamDuration
histogram.summary: Duration of an audio output stream. Measured in AudioOutputController from when a stream is started until it is stopped. A stream is stopped when it is paused, closed (i.e. destructed), or when a device change event causes it to be restarted.

histogram.name: Media.Pepper.PlayedSound
histogram.summary: Whether Pepper players have played sound through its lifetime.

histogram.name: Media.PepperVideoDecoderError
histogram.summary: Counts of video decode errors reported to plugin.

histogram.name: Media.PepperVideoDecoderOutputPictureCount
histogram.summary: Counts every output picture emitted to a PPAPI client, categorized by size and decoder type. Flash will only use platform decoders, but NaCl clients can use either decoder type.

histogram.name: Media.PepperVideoDecoderPictureCount
histogram.summary: Number of PictureBuffers/textures requested per hardware decoder creation. This value varies by platform and video. A user visible video may trigger multiple decoder creations (sometimes every 5 seconds) but would normally not hold more than 2 sets of buffers at any given time in memory.

histogram.name: Media.PepperVideoDecoderPictureHeight
histogram.summary: Vertical video resolution rounded to the nearest bucket. (Corresponds roughly to the number in 720p.)

histogram.name: Media.PipelineStatus
histogram.summary: Status of the media pipeline at the end of its lifecycle suffixed by stream and video decoder type.

histogram.name: Media.PipelineStatus.Start
histogram.summary: Status of the media pipeline starting process (including demuxer and renderer initialization). If the media pipeline is destroyed during the starting process nothing will be reported, but this should relatively rare.

histogram.name: Media.PlatformVideoDecoding.Decode
histogram.summary: Time for the platform-specific (set of) video decode acceleration command(s) to be executed. Output once per decode request submit.

histogram.name: Media.PlaybackPower.BatteryDelta
histogram.summary: Total battery (0-100 percent) consumed by playback. When divided by Media.PlaybackPower.ElapsedTime, this provides the average rate of battery drain for that category of playback. Units are (total) percent. This is recorded when an entire percent (or more) is consumed during playback. Since devices have different battery sizes, methods of measuring battery, and different power consumption rates, it's a good idea to filter by a specific device type and OS version before trying to interpret these results. The magnitude of these numbers, alone, is also likely hard to use, for this reason. Instead, consider comparing A/B experiments to see if they get larger or smaller. Playbacks under one msec are ignored.

histogram.name: Media.PlaybackPower.ElapsedTime
histogram.summary: Total time, in msec, elapsed while consuming the battery described by Media.PlaybackPower.BatteryDelta. See that histogram for details. Recorded when one or more percent of battery is consumed during playback.

histogram.name: Media.PlayMovies.DelayedAndDroppedFramesPer5Sec
histogram.obsolete: Removed 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The average number of delayed and dropped frames for the PlayMovies application. Reported every 5 seconds.

histogram.name: Media.PlayMovies.DisplayedFramesPerSecond
histogram.summary: The average number of displayed frames for the PlayMovies application. Reported every 5 seconds.

histogram.name: Media.PreloadMetadataSuspendWasIdeal
histogram.summary: Indicates if a suspend initiated for preload=metadata was ideal. I.e. we did not immediately resume after completing the suspend when signaling ReadyState::HAVE_FUTURE_DATA.

histogram.name: Media.RebuffersCount
histogram.summary: Indicates the number of rebuffers a given watch time session had.

histogram.name: Media.Remoting.AllowedByPage
histogram.summary: Tracks whether a web page allows content to be remoted.

histogram.name: Media.Remoting.AudioBitrate
histogram.summary: Measured audio content transfer bitrate while remoting content.

histogram.name: Media.Remoting.AudioChannelLayout
histogram.summary: Audio channel layout used while remoting content.

histogram.name: Media.Remoting.AudioCodec
histogram.summary: Audio codec used while remoting content.

histogram.name: Media.Remoting.AudioSamplesPerSecond
histogram.summary: Audio sampling rate while remoting audio content.

histogram.name: Media.Remoting.AudioSamplesPerSecondUnexpected
histogram.summary: Audio sampling rate while remoting audio content (atypical values, in Hz).

histogram.name: Media.Remoting.CapacityOverMediaBitrate
histogram.summary: The difference between the estimated transmission capacity and the media bitrate when capacity is higher.

histogram.name: Media.Remoting.MediaBitrateOverCapacity
histogram.summary: The difference between the media bitrate and the estimated transmission capacity when media bitrate is higher.

histogram.name: Media.Remoting.PosterDownloadDuration.Fail
histogram.obsolete: Removed as no more poster image is being downloaded while remoting content.
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time it took to ultimately fail to download a poster image for an HTML5 video while remoting content.

histogram.name: Media.Remoting.PosterDownloadDuration.Success
histogram.obsolete: Removed as no more poster image is being downloaded while remoting content.
histogram.summary: Measures the amount of time it took to successfully download a poster image for an HTML5 video while remoting content.

histogram.name: Media.Remoting.SessionDuration
histogram.summary: Measures the duration of each remoting session.

histogram.name: Media.Remoting.SessionStartTrigger
histogram.summary: Tracks the trigger for starting a remoting session.

histogram.name: Media.Remoting.SessionStopTrigger
histogram.summary: Tracks the trigger for stopping a remoting session.

histogram.name: Media.Remoting.ShortSessionDuration
histogram.summary: Measures the duration of each remoting session shorter than 15 seconds.

histogram.name: Media.Remoting.StartMediaBitrate
histogram.summary: The estimated content bitrate (including both audio and video) when starting a remoting session.

histogram.name: Media.Remoting.TimeUntilFirstPlayout
histogram.summary: Measures how long, from the start of a remoting session, until content began playing out on the remote device.

histogram.name: Media.Remoting.TimeUntilRemoteInitialized
histogram.summary: Measures how long, from the start of a remoting session, until a message was received from the remote device indicating initialization succeeded.

histogram.name: Media.Remoting.TrackConfiguration
histogram.summary: Tracks whether audio or video or both are remoted.

histogram.name: Media.Remoting.TransmissionCapacity
histogram.summary: The estimated transmission capacity when starting a remoting session.

histogram.name: Media.Remoting.VideoAspectRatio
histogram.summary: Aspect ratio of video while remoting content.

histogram.name: Media.Remoting.VideoBitrate
histogram.summary: Measured video content transfer bitrate while remoting content.

histogram.name: Media.Remoting.VideoCodec
histogram.summary: Video codec used while remoting content.

histogram.name: Media.Remoting.VideoCodecProfile
histogram.summary: Video codec profile used while remoting content.

histogram.name: Media.Remoting.VideoNaturalWidth
histogram.summary: Video width while remoting content.

histogram.name: Media.RTCVideoDecoderError
histogram.summary: Counts of video decode errors reported to RTCVideoDecoder.

histogram.name: Media.RTCVideoDecoderInitDecodeStatus
histogram.summary: Results of attempts to RTCVideoDecoder::InitDecode().

histogram.name: Media.RTCVideoDecoderInitDecodeSuccess
histogram.summary: Indicates whether we were successful in initializing hardware video decoder for use in the RTC pipeline.

histogram.name: Media.RTCVideoDecoderProfile
histogram.summary: Video codec profile used in RTC video decoder.

histogram.name: Media.RTCVideoEncoderInitEncodeSuccess
histogram.summary: Indicates whether we were successful in initializing hardware video encoder for use in the RTC pipeline.

histogram.name: Media.RTCVideoEncoderProfile
histogram.summary: Video codec profile used in RTC video encoder.

histogram.name: Media.RTCVideoEncoderTimestampMatchSuccess
histogram.summary: Indicates whether we were successful in preserving timestamps in hardware video encoder session. It is tracked during the session and logged after it ends.

histogram.name: Media.Session.ActiveTime
histogram.summary: Time during which a media session has been active, in other words, the time between an activation and deactivation, without counting time while it was suspended. If a session is activated after being deactivated, there will be two entries. For example, if a user plays a song but is interrupted by a phone call, it will only account for the song playing time.

histogram.name: Media.Session.AudioFocus.Abandon
histogram.obsolete: Removed 8/2019 in Issue 995521 due to expiry and not needed anymore.
histogram.summary: The number of times a media session abandon audio focus and where the abandon audio focus call originated from. This is recorded every time a media session abandons audio focus because it has stopped playing.

histogram.name: Media.Session.AudioFocus.Request
histogram.obsolete: Removed 8/2019 in Issue 995521 due to expiry and not needed anymore.
histogram.summary: The number of times a media session requested audio focus and where that focus request originated from. This is recorded every time a media session requests audio focus because it wants to start playing.

histogram.name: Media.Session.AudioFocus.Type
histogram.obsolete: Removed 8/2019 in Issue 995521 due to expiry and not needed anymore.
histogram.summary: The number of times a media session requested audio focus for a specific audio focus type. This is recorded every time a media session requests a new audio focus type. This may be because the session has started playing or the type of media being played has changed.

histogram.name: Media.Session.Pause
histogram.summary: The number of times the user paused playback of a media session using other means than the pause button on the page.

histogram.name: Media.Session.Play
histogram.summary: The number of times the user started playback of a media session using other means than the play button on the page.

histogram.name: Media.Session.RequestAudioFocusResult
histogram.summary: Indicate whether the native requestAudioFocus() call was successful.

histogram.name: Media.Session.Stop
histogram.summary: The number of times the user stopped a media session using other means than the pause button on the page.

histogram.name: Media.Session.Suspended
histogram.summary: The number of times a media session is suspended and why it has been suspended.

histogram.name: Media.Session.UserAction
histogram.summary: The number of times user interacts with MediaSession sorted by action type.

histogram.name: Media.Session.UserAction.Focus
histogram.summary: The focused state of the WebContents associated with the MediaSession that the user is interacting with. Recorded when the user interacts with a MediaSession (e.g. by pressing the play/pause key on their keyboard).

histogram.name: Media.SRC.PreloadAutoHasPoster
histogram.summary: Whether a SRC= playback had a poster set at load() when the effective preload type is "auto". Note that this includes audio playbacks, since we do not have track metadata at load(); audio elements never have posters.

histogram.name: Media.SRC.PreloadMetaDataHasPoster
histogram.summary: Whether a SRC= playback had a poster set at load() when the effective preload type is "metadata". Note that this includes audio playbacks, since we do not have track metadata at load(); audio elements never have posters.

histogram.name: Media.SRC.VideoCodec.MP4
histogram.summary: Video codec used in plain src= (not MSE) HTML5 media if the media container is MP4.

histogram.name: Media.SRC.VideoCodec.WebM
histogram.summary: Video codec used in plain src= (not MSE) HTML5 media if the media container is WebM.

histogram.name: Media.Timeline.DragGestureDuration
histogram.summary: The duration of the user gesture (how long their finger is down), recorded at the end of each drag gesture. This histogram is split according to the width of the slider track in CSS px - see histogram name suffix.

histogram.name: Media.Timeline.DragPercent
histogram.summary: The net distance the media scrubber moved, recorded at the end of each drag gesture, as a percentage of the width of the slider track. This histogram is split according to the width of the slider track in CSS px - see histogram name suffix.

histogram.name: Media.Timeline.DragSumAbsTimeDelta
histogram.summary: The sum of absolute distances the media scrubber incrementally moved, recorded at the end of each drag gesture, each measured as the change in media current time. If the scrubber was dragged back and forth before being released, this will be larger than the value recorded for Media.Timeline.DragTimeDelta (and may even be larger than the media duration), otherwise it will be the same as DragTimeDelta. This histogram is split according to the width of the slider track in CSS px - see histogram name suffix.

histogram.name: Media.Timeline.DragTimeDelta
histogram.summary: The net distance the media scrubber moved, recorded at the end of each drag gesture, measured as the change in media current time. This histogram is split according to the width of the slider track in CSS px - see histogram name suffix.

histogram.name: Media.Timeline.SeekType
histogram.summary: The type of user gesture, recorded at the end of each input sequence. For example holding down the right arrow key with the scrubber focused will only be logged as a single event. This histogram is split according to the width of the slider track in CSS px - see histogram name suffix.

histogram.name: Media.Timeline.Width
histogram.summary: The width of the media timeline track in CSS pixels, recorded the first time a media element with controls starts playing (strictly speaking, it's the width in CSS pixels ignoring CSS transforms, multiplied by pageZoomFactor, but deliberately ignoring pinch zoom's pageScaleFactor).

histogram.name: Media.TimeToFirstFrame
histogram.summary: Time in milliseconds from when WebMediaPlayerImpl starts loading until the first video frame has been shown.

histogram.name: Media.TimeToFirstFrame.SRC.ManyVideos
histogram.summary: Time in milliseconds from when WebMediaPlayerImpl starts loading until the first video frame has been shown IFF six or more videos are loading in parallel.

histogram.name: Media.TimeToMetadata
histogram.summary: Time in milliseconds from when WebMediaPlayerImpl starts loading until metadata is known.

histogram.name: Media.TimeToPipelineStarted
histogram.summary: Time in milliseconds from HTML5 media pipeline creation to playing event.

histogram.name: Media.TimeToPlayReady
histogram.summary: Time in milliseconds from when WebMediaPlayer starts loading until it has buffered enough to start playback.

histogram.name: Media.TotalMBytes
histogram.summary: Size of HTML5 media (when known), in MB.

histogram.name: Media.UncacheableReason
histogram.summary: Reasons a media response won't be used to satisfy a future request.

histogram.name: Media.UnderflowCount
histogram.obsolete: Removed Feb 2017. Media.UnderflowDuration provides more useful counts.
histogram.summary: The number of times a src= playback has underflowed; i.e. ran out of data.

histogram.name: Media.UnderflowDuration
histogram.obsolete: Removed Aug 2017. Media.UnderflowDuration2.SRC removes zero-weighting so that we only report actual underflows.
histogram.summary: The amount of time taken to leave the underflow state (i.e. resume playback) for src= playbacks.

histogram.name: Media.UnderflowDuration.EME
histogram.obsolete: Removed Aug 2017. Media.UnderflowDuration2.EME removes zero-weighting so that we only report actual underflows.
histogram.summary: The amount of time taken to leave the underflow state (i.e. resume playback) for Encrypted Media Extensions (EME) based playbacks.

histogram.name: Media.UnderflowDuration.MSE
histogram.obsolete: Removed Aug 2017. Media.UnderflowDuration2.MSE removes zero-weighting so that we only report actual underflows.
histogram.summary: The amount of time taken to leave the underflow state (i.e. resume playback) for Media Source Extensions (MSE) based playbacks.

histogram.name: Media.UnderflowDuration2
histogram.summary: The amount of time taken to leave the underflow state (i.e. resume playback) for playbacks. This doesn't report initial zero samples, which was previously used to compensate for playbacks that don't rebuffer.

histogram.name: Media.URLScheme
histogram.obsolete: Replaced with Media.URLScheme2 which is only recorded for src=URL playbacks instead of including MediaSource playbacks which are always blob.
histogram.summary: URL scheme used with HTML5 media. (each URL provides one sample)

histogram.name: Media.URLScheme2
histogram.summary: URL scheme used with HTML5 media; only recorded for src=URL playbacks and not for Media Source Extensions playbacks. Each URL provides one sample.

histogram.name: Media.VaapiWrapper.VADisplayStateInitializeSuccess
histogram.summary: Whether the call to VaapiWrapper's VADisplayState::Initialize() succeeded or not.

histogram.name: Media.VAIP.VppFailure
histogram.summary: Count of VAAPI errors that occur inside the VaapiWrapper due to VPP functionality needed by the VaapiImageProcessor.

histogram.name: Media.VAJDA.DecoderFailure
histogram.summary: Count of VAAPI errors that occur inside the VaapiWrapper due to decoding functionality needed by the VaapiMjpegDecodeAccelerator.

histogram.name: Media.VAJDA.ResponseToClient
histogram.summary: The response given to the client by VaapiJpegDecodeAccelerator for a decode request. This is recorded when the decode succeeds or fails. The recorded value corresponds to a MjpegDecodeAccelerator::Error.

histogram.name: Media.VAJDA.VppFailure
histogram.summary: Count of VAAPI errors that occur inside the VaapiWrapper due to VPP functionality needed by the VaapiMjpegDecodeAccelerator.

histogram.name: Media.VAJDAWorker.DecoderFailure
histogram.summary: Count of VAAPI errors that occur inside the VaapiWrapper due to functionality needed by the VaapiJpegDecodeAcceleratorWorker.

histogram.name: Media.VAJEA.EncoderResult
histogram.summary: Result codes reported by jpeg encode using VA-API hardware jpeg encoder.

histogram.name: Media.VAJEA.VppFailure
histogram.summary: Count of VAAPI errors that occur inside the VaapiWrapper due to VPP functionality needed by the VaapiJpegEncodeAccelerator.

histogram.name: Media.VAVDA.DecoderFailure
histogram.summary: Error codes reported by video decode using VA-API hardware video decoder.

histogram.name: Media.VAVDA.VaapiWrapperCreationSuccess
histogram.summary: Whether the creation of VaapiWrapper succeeded or not inside VaVDA.

histogram.name: Media.VAVDAH264.DecoderFailure
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 4/2015, partially replaced by Media.VAVDA.DecoderFailure.
histogram.summary: Error codes reported by video decode using VA-API hardware video decoder.

histogram.name: Media.VAVEA.EncoderFailure
histogram.summary: Error codes reported by video encode using VA-API hardware video encoder.

histogram.name: Media.Video.Autoplay
histogram.summary: Records the autoplay source of videos. This includes Media.Video.Autoplay.Muted.

histogram.name: Media.Video.Autoplay.Attribute.WaitTime
histogram.summary: Records time from load starts until video starts based on autoplay attribute.

histogram.name: Media.Video.Autoplay.Muted
histogram.summary: Records the autoplay source of muted videos.

histogram.name: Media.Video.Autoplay.Muted.Attribute.OffscreenDuration
histogram.obsolete: Removed as autoplay muted video by attributed is paused when going offscreen since https://crbug.com/683141.
histogram.summary: Records the offscreen playing duration of a muted video autoplaying from autoplay attribute.

histogram.name: Media.Video.Autoplay.Muted.Blocked
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of M80, autoplaying muted videos can no longer be blocked.
histogram.summary: Records the reason why autoplay of muted videos was blocked.

histogram.name: Media.Video.Autoplay.Muted.PlayMethod.BecomesVisible
histogram.summary: Records muted video started playing with play() become visible at some point. The UMA for false is not complete since it is recorded in a destructor which is garbage-collected. Please subtract Media.Video.Autoplay.Muted "play() method" count with the true count of this histogram to obtain the real false count.

histogram.name: Media.Video.Autoplay.Muted.PlayMethod.OffscreenDuration
histogram.summary: Records the offscreen playing duration of a muted video autoplaying from play() method.

histogram.name: Media.Video.Autoplay.Muted.UnmuteAction
histogram.summary: Status of the unmute action on a video that autoplayed because it was muted.

histogram.name: Media.Video.Autoplay.PlayMethod.WaitTime
histogram.summary: Records time from load starts until video starts based on play method.

histogram.name: Media.Video.Capture.Mac.CameraSystemPermission.FixedTime.SinceFirstFailure
histogram.summary: The time from the first camera access failure due to blocked system permission before the last browser restart to startup after browser restart. Logged once at browser startup if the system permission then is allowed.

histogram.name: Media.Video.Capture.Mac.CameraSystemPermission.FixedTime.SinceLastFailure
histogram.summary: The time from the last camera access failure due to blocked system permission before the last browser restart to startup after browser restart. Logged once at browser startup if the system permission then is allowed.

histogram.name: Media.Video.Capture.Mac.CameraSystemPermission.Startup
histogram.summary: The Mac system permission state for camera. Logged once at browser startup. For more information on the values, see https://developer.apple.com/documentation/avfoundation/avauthorizationstatus.

histogram.name: Media.Video.Capture.Mac.CameraSystemPermission.StartupAfterFailure
histogram.summary: The Mac system permission state for camera. Logged once at browser startup if there was a failure accessing the camera due to blocked system permission before the last browser restart. For more information on the values, see https://developer.apple.com/documentation/avfoundation/avauthorizationstatus.

histogram.name: Media.Video.Capture.Mac.CameraSystemPermission.UserMedia
histogram.summary: The Mac system permission state for camera at the time of a user media request. Logged when the system permission is checked. If the permission is "not determined" this is logged, permission is requested, and the resulting permission (from the user response) is also logged. For more information on the values, see https://developer.apple.com/documentation/avfoundation/avauthorizationstatus.

histogram.name: Media.Video.FullscreenOrientationLock.AutoRotateEnabled
histogram.summary: Whether auto rotation of screen orientation is enabled by the user (if so the user has not locked the screen orientation at the OS level, though the orientation may still be locked by apps). Recorded each time MediaControlsOrientationLockDelegate locks the screen orientation in response to a video going fullscreen (or loading metadata whilst fullscreen).

histogram.name: Media.Video.FullscreenOrientationLock.LockResult
histogram.summary: Result of the orientation lock attempt when a video enters fullscreen.

histogram.name: Media.Video.FullscreenOrientationLock.MetadataAvailability
histogram.summary: Status of the metadata when attempting to lock the screen orientation for a fullscreen video.

histogram.name: Media.Video.KeyFrameDistance
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 11/2018 -- no longer used and expensive to monitor.
histogram.summary: Records the time distance between consequent keyframes in a video. The new value is recorded for each video key frame decoded.

histogram.name: Media.Video.TimeFromForegroundToFirstFrame
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 01/18/2017 in issue 670150. Replaced by Media.Video.TimeFromForegroundToFirstFrame.DisableTrack and Media.Video.TimeFromForegroundToFirstFrame.Paused.
histogram.summary: Records the time between the moment when the video element is brought to the foreground and when the video frame compositor outputs the next frame.

histogram.name: Media.Video.TimeFromForegroundToFirstFrame.DisableTrack
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 06/2019 -- no longer used after background track disable has launched.
histogram.summary: Records the time between the moment when the video element that had video track disabled in the background is brought to the foreground and when the video frame compositor outputs the next frame. Recorded even if disabling video track in the background is turned off to collect data for the control group.

histogram.name: Media.Video.TimeFromForegroundToFirstFrame.Paused
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 06/2019 -- no longer used after background track disable has launched.
histogram.summary: Records the time between the moment when the video element that was paused in the background is brought to the foreground and when the video frame compositor outputs the next frame. Recorded even if disabling pausing video in the background is turned off to collect data for the control group.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCapture.AspectRatio
histogram.summary: Video Capture Device captured aspect ratio, as a rounded integer multiplied by 100. The collection is made in the VideoCaptureController upon reception of the first frame.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCapture.BlacklistedDevice
histogram.summary: Counts appearances of Blacklisted Video Capture devices during enumeration. This collection happens during first enumeration in the appropriate platforms' VideoCaptureDeviceFactory.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCapture.DelayUntilFirstFrame
histogram.summary: Time it takes from the moment that a VideoCaptureController is requested to start to the moment the first video frame arrives at VideoCaptureController::OnFrameReadyInBuffer().

histogram.name: Media.VideoCapture.Error
histogram.summary: Breaks down the events counted in Media.VideoCaptureManager.Event bucket "Stopping video capture due to error" by the origin of the error. This allows narrowing down what is causing errors. An event for this histogram is emitted once for each time a client disconnects from a video capture session because the session has reported an error.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCapture.FrameDrop
histogram.summary: An event for this histogram is emitted once for each time a video frame is dropped on the way from a capture source, e.g. a webcam, to a corresponding media::VideoCaptureController instance running in the Browser process. If more than |VideoCaptureController::max_consecutive_frame_drop_for_same_reason_count()| frames are dropped consecutively for the same reason, no more events for the same reason will be counted for that session until either a frame is delivered successfully or the reason for the frame dropping changes.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCapture.FrameRate
histogram.summary: Video Capture Device frame rate requested by VideoCaptureManager on AllocateAndStart(). The collection is made in the VideoCaptureController upon reception of the first frame.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCapture.FramesReceived
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 10/2014 in issue 422822. Replaced by Media.VideoCaptureManager.Event and the two new values 3 and 4 in the enum VideoCaptureEvent.
histogram.summary: Whether any frames were received during a video capture session. This metric is recorded when a video source is stopped.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCapture.Height
histogram.summary: Video Capture Device captured frame height in pixels. The collection is made in the VideoCaptureController upon reception of the first frame.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCapture.MacBook.AttemptCountWhenNoCamera
histogram.summary: Indicates how many times Chrome has asked AVFoundation to enumerate devices since the process start when zero devices are returned. This event is only emitted for the first time that zero devices are returned since the process start. This is used in the context of investigating http://crbug.com/582931.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCapture.MacBook.HardwareVersionWhenNoCamera
histogram.summary: MacBook hardware version used when Chrome cannot enumerate a video device. This is used for tracking http://crbug.com/582931.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCapture.MacBook.NumberOfDevices
histogram.summary: Number of video capture devices detected by Chrome during device enumeration. Zero devices indicate a problem since all MacBooks should have a built-in camera. This is used for tracking http://crbug.com/582931.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCapture.MacBook.RetryGetDeviceInfosEvent
histogram.summary: Counts events that are emitted in the context of attempting to mitigate the issue of no cameras being found on Macbooks by restarting the video capture service and retrying to obtain device infos, see http://crbug.com/582931.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCapture.MaxFrameDropExceeded
histogram.summary: An event for this histogram is emitted when a video capture session drops more than |VideoCaptureController::max_consecutive_frame_drop_for_same_reason_count()| consecutive video frames for the same reason. After this event is emitted once, no additional events are emitted for consecutively dropped frames for the same reason until the count is reset by either a frame being delivered successfully or a frame being dropped for a different reason. See also Media.VideoCapture.FrameDrop.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCapture.PixelFormat
histogram.summary: Pixel format provided by a Video Capture Device. The collection is made in the VideoCaptureController upon reception of the first frame.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCapture.Width
histogram.summary: Video Capture Device captured frame width in pixels. The collection is made in the VideoCaptureController upon reception of the first frame.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCapture.Windows.BackendUsed
histogram.summary: Indicates which video capture backend is used on Windows. A count is recorded each time a new VideoCaptureDeviceFactoryWin instance is created.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCapture.Windows.ImageCaptureOutcome
histogram.summary: Counts number of times taking a still image on Windows fails/succeeds.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCapture.Windows.NumberOfRetriesNeededForMFGetAvailableDeviceMediaType
histogram.summary: Counts how many retries are needed for calls to MediaFoundation function GetAvailableDeviceMediaType before getting a result different from MF_E_INVALIDREQUEST or giving up.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCapture.Windows.NumberOfRetriesNeededForMFGetDeviceStreamCategory
histogram.summary: Counts how many retries are needed for calls to MediaFoundation function GetDeviceStreamCategory before getting a result different from MF_E_INVALIDREQUEST or giving up.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCapture.Windows.NumberOfRetriesNeededForMFGetDeviceStreamCount
histogram.summary: Counts how many retries are needed for calls to MediaFoundation function GetDeviceStreamCount before getting a result different from MF_E_INVALIDREQUEST or giving up.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCaptureApi.Mac
histogram.obsolete: Removed July 30th. This is no longer used.
histogram.summary: Video Capture and device enumeration/monitoring API used for Mac OS Lion or later. Collection is made only once when the flags are parsed and the appropriate library selected and/or loaded, successfully or not.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCaptureGpuJpegDecoder.InitDecodeSuccess
histogram.summary: Indicates whether we were successful in initializing hardware jpeg decoder for attempts to VideoCaptureGpuJpegDecoder::Initialize().

histogram.name: Media.VideoCaptureManager
histogram.summary: Measures the time taken for VideoCaptureManager::

histogram.name: Media.VideoCaptureManager.Event
histogram.summary: Counts video capture event, such as start and stop capture. Note that the ideal case is 50% start events and 50% normal stop events.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCaptureService.DurationFromLastConnectToClosingConnection
histogram.obsolete: Removed 07/2017 in favor of the more differentiated durations.
histogram.summary: Measures the duration from the time the Browser connected to the video capture service to the time it closed the connection.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCaptureService.DurationFromLastConnectToClosingConnectionAfterCapture
histogram.summary: Measures the duration from the time the Browser connected to the video capture service to the time it closed the connection. Entries are only logged if the service was used for creating an actual capture session as opposed to enumerating devices only.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCaptureService.DurationFromLastConnectToClosingConnectionAfterEnumerationOnly
histogram.summary: Measures the duration from the time the Browser connected to the video capture service to the time it closed the connection. Entries are only logged if the service was used for enumerating devices only, but not for creating an actual capture session.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCaptureService.DurationFromLastConnectToConnectionLost
histogram.summary: Measures the duration from the time the Browser connected to the video capture service to the time the connection was lost.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCaptureService.DurationUntilReconnect
histogram.obsolete: Removed 07/2017 in favor of the more differentiated durations.
histogram.summary: Measures the duration from the time the Browser last closed or lost connection to the video capture service to the time it reconnects.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCaptureService.DurationUntilReconnectAfterCapture
histogram.summary: Measures the duration from the time the Browser last closed or lost connection to the video capture service to the time it reconnects. This duration only gets logged for reconnects after usage of the service for capture (as opposed to enumeration-only usage).

histogram.name: Media.VideoCaptureService.DurationUntilReconnectAfterEnumerationOnly
histogram.summary: Measures the duration from the time the Browser last closed or lost connection to the video capture service to the time it reconnects. This duration only gets logged for reconnects after enumeration-only usage of the service.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCaptureService.Event
histogram.summary: Counts video capture service events, such as startup, shutdown, and connection lost.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCodec
histogram.summary: Video codec used in HTML5 media.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCodecProfile
histogram.obsolete: Removed 03/2020 in https://crbug.com/1053030. We have this in UKM.
histogram.summary: Video codec profile used in HTML5 media.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCodedAspectRatio
histogram.summary: Coded aspect ratio of HTML5 video.

histogram.name: Media.VideoCodedWidth
histogram.summary: Coded width of HTML5 video.

histogram.name: Media.VideoColorRange
histogram.summary: Pixel format color range of HTML5 video. Emitted on video load.

histogram.name: Media.VideoDecoderFallback
histogram.summary: Whether Chrome had to fall back to a secondary video decoder after the primary decoder failed reinitialization.

histogram.name: Media.VideoDecodeStatsDB.OpSuccess
histogram.summary: Indicates whether we were successful performing some database operation. See suffix VideoDecodeStatsDBOperations.

histogram.name: Media.VideoDecodeStatsDB.OpTiming
histogram.summary: Indicates duration of time performing some database operation.

histogram.name: Media.VideoFormat
histogram.summary: Pixel format used in HTML5 video. Emitted on video load.

histogram.name: Media.VideoFrame.ColorSpace
histogram.summary: Video frame color space. Emitted for each video frame.

histogram.name: Media.VideoFramePixelFormat
histogram.summary: Pixel format used in HTML5 video. Emitted on video load.

histogram.name: Media.VideoFrameSubmitter
histogram.summary: Delay between a VideoFrame being decoded and it being consumed by the OS presentation API (on ChromeOS, this includes the display controller too).

histogram.name: Media.VideoFrameSubmitter.PreSubmitBuffering
histogram.summary: Delay between a VideoFrame being decoded and it being handed over to the CompositorFrameSink, essentially capturing the buffering in the Renderer. This value is strictly included in Media.VideoFrameSubmitter

histogram.name: Media.VideoHeight.Initial
histogram.summary: The height of the first video frame in an HTML5 video. Reported when the first video frame is available.

histogram.name: Media.VideoPersistence.AttemptResult
histogram.summary: Every time a video persistence session could be triggered, it records the result of the attempt.

histogram.name: Media.VideoPersistence.ControlsType
histogram.summary: Record the type of controls a persisted video is using. This is recorded every time a video enters persistence mode

histogram.name: Media.VideoPersistence.Duration
histogram.summary: Records the length during which a video was in a persistent state. It is recorded once per video persistence session.

histogram.name: Media.VideoPersistence.EndReason
histogram.summary: Records the reason why a video persistence session has ended.

histogram.name: Media.VideoPixelFormat
histogram.summary: Pixel format used in HTML5 video. Emitted on video load.

histogram.name: Media.VideoPixelFormatUnion
histogram.obsolete: Pixel format has been removed from demuxers. 06/2019
histogram.summary: Pixel format used in capture and HTML5 video. Emitted on video load.

histogram.name: Media.VideoRenderer.CadenceChanges
histogram.summary: Indicates how many cadence changes have occurred during playback, a zero value is emitted at the beginning of playback. Subsequent values are emitted during video rendering for each cadence change (up to a maximum of 10).

histogram.name: Media.VideoRenderer.LowDelay
histogram.summary: Indicates whether video is rendering in low delay mode. It's recorded when a video starts playing.

histogram.name: Media.VideoRotation
histogram.summary: Metadata rotation in mp4 videos. Emitted during demuxing.

histogram.name: Media.VideoTrackAdapter.FramesReceived
histogram.summary: If any frames were received during a video capture session. It's recorded when a video source is stopped.

histogram.name: Media.VideoVisibleAspectRatio
histogram.summary: Visible aspect ratio of HTML5 video.

histogram.name: Media.VideoVisibleWidth
histogram.summary: Visible width of HTML5 video.

histogram.name: Media.VirtualAudioCaptureStartupSuccess
histogram.summary: Whether capture started successfully after a high-latency input stream startup was requested.

histogram.name: Media.Vpx.VideoDecoderBuffersInUseByDecoder
histogram.summary: Number of frame buffers used by Vpx decoder.

histogram.name: Media.Vpx.VideoDecoderBuffersInUseByDecoderAndVideoFrame
histogram.summary: Number of frame buffers currently in use by both Vpx decoder and a VideoFrame.

histogram.name: Media.VpxVideoDecoder.Vp9DecodeTime
histogram.obsolete: Removed 11/2018 -- shows nothing interesting; it's been static for years.
histogram.summary: Amount of time taken to decode one VP9 frame.

histogram.name: Media.VTVDA.HardwareAccelerated
histogram.summary: Whether a VTDecompressionSession is internally using hardware accelerated decoding.

histogram.name: Media.VTVDA.InitializationFailureReason
histogram.summary: Count of VideoToolbox initialization failure reasons. Successful initializations are counted as a special failure type. The sum of successes and failures gives a lower bound on the number of attempted initializations.

histogram.name: Media.VTVDA.SessionFailureReason
histogram.summary: Count of VTVDA session failure reasons. Successful initializations are counted as a special failure type. Since only successfully initialized session can fail, failures rates are computed as a simple ratio.

histogram.name: Media.WatchTime
histogram.summary:
Watch time is defined as the amount of elapsed media time for audio+video media aggregated per player instance. A minimum of 7 seconds of unmuted, foreground media must be watched to start watch time monitoring. Watch time is checked on a regular basis and reported to UMA upon one of the stop events mentioned below or at player destruction if none occur prior.
Any one of paused, hidden, or muted is sufficient to stop watch time metric reports. Each of these has a hysteresis where if the state change is undone within some time, the watch time will be counted as uninterrupted.
Power events (on/off battery power) have a similar hysteresis, but unlike the aforementioned properties, will not stop metric collection.
Each seek event will result in a new watch time metric being started and the old metric finalized as accurately as possible.

histogram.name: Media.WebAudioSourceProvider.SinkStatus
histogram.summary: Status of audio sink provided to WebMediaPlayer. If not OK, null sink will be used for audio output instead.

histogram.name: Media.WebMediaPlayerImpl.HLS.HasAccessControl
histogram.summary: When an HLS manifest is found during loading (on Android only), and the response is CORS cross-origin, this histogram records whether the response included an Access-Control-Allow-Origin header. Such requests are likely to be supported by fetch() if the mode is correctly configured.

histogram.name: Media.WebMediaPlayerImpl.HLS.IsCorsCrossOrigin
histogram.summary: When an HLS manifest is found during loading (on Android only), records whether the request was CORS cross-origin. These are cases that could not be implemented using fetch(). Note: subresources referenced by the manifest are not considered; they may have different origins or CORS configurations.

histogram.name: Media.WebMediaPlayerImpl.HLS.IsMixedContent
histogram.summary: When an HLS manifest is found during loading (on Android only), records whether the request would be mixed content. These are cases that could not be implemented using fetch(). Note: subresources referenced by the manifest are not considered; they may have different origins.

histogram.name: Media.WebMediaPlayerImpl.HLS.WouldTaintOrigin
histogram.obsolete: Removed because an overwhelming majority of pages do not set the crossorigin attribute and as a result most HLS content is counted. Superseded by Media.WebMediaPlayerImpl.HLS.HasAccessControl.
histogram.summary: When an HLS manifest is found during loading (on Android only), records whether the request was CORS cross-origin or redirected between origins. These are cases that may or may not be able to be implemented using fetch(), depending on details of the redirection and the setting of the crossorigin attribute. Note: subresources referenced by the manifest are not considered; they may have different origins or CORS configurations.

histogram.name: Media.WebMediaPlayerImpl.Memory
histogram.summary: Amount of memory used by the WebMediaPlayerImpl and its components.

histogram.name: Media.WebMediaPlayerImpl.WatchTime
histogram.summary: Wall time of WebMediaPlayer playbacks. Each count represents one second of media playback. Multiple WebMediaPlayer instances can together accumulate watch time faster than real time.

histogram.name: Media.WebView.UnsupportedContainer
histogram.obsolete: Removed 09/2018 since support for these containers has been deprecated.
histogram.summary: Media container extensions seen by WebView that are not supported by the standard HTML5 playback path. This value is recorded every time a WebMediaPlayer is created with one of the unsupported containers.

histogram.name: Media.WindowsCoreAudioInput
histogram.obsolete: Removed 01/2017 as Windows Core Audio is now the only audio input implementation on Windows.
histogram.summary: Whether Chrome is using Windows Core Audio for audio input or not. Updated on Windows only when a low-latency audio input stream is created.

histogram.name: Media.YouTube.DelayedAndDroppedFramesPer5Sec
histogram.obsolete: Removed 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The average number of delayed and dropped frames for the YouTube application. Reported every 5 seconds.

histogram.name: Media.YouTube.DisplayedFramesPerSecond
histogram.summary: The average number of displayed frames for the YouTube application. Reported every 5 seconds.

histogram.name: Media.YouTube.TimeToBufferAv
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Time needed to pre-buffer A/V data before the actual playback for the YouTube application.

histogram.name: Media.YouTube.TimeToBufferAvAfterAbort
histogram.obsolete: Removed 04/2016 as doesn't have data nor owner.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Time needed to buffer A/V data after an abort for the YouTube application.

histogram.name: Media.YouTube.TimeToBufferAvAfterUnderrun
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Time needed to buffer A/V data after an underrun for the YouTube application.

histogram.name: MediaGalleries.ScanCancelTime
histogram.summary: If a media scan was cancelled, the duration (in ms) it ran.

histogram.name: MediaGalleries.ScanDirectoriesFound
histogram.summary: The number of directories with media files found during a scan.

histogram.name: MediaGalleries.ScanFinishedTime
histogram.summary: Duration in milliseconds taken to do a media scan that ran to completion.

histogram.name: MediaGalleries.ScanGalleriesGranted
histogram.summary: The percentage of galleries accepted (not deselected) from the scan result dialog.

histogram.name: MediaGalleries.ScanGalleriesPopulated
histogram.summary: The number of galleries added or updated in preferences after a scan.

histogram.name: MediaGalleries.Usage
histogram.summary: Various usage counts for media galleries.

histogram.name: MediaLauncherActivity.MediaType
histogram.summary: The type of media that a user is opening via the MediaLauncherActivity.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Cast.App.Availability
histogram.summary: Round trip time for a Cast app availability request. Can be suffixed with Success or Failure.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Cast.Channel.ConnectResult
histogram.summary: Whether opening Cast channel succeeds or not. Recorded when all opening channel attempts (including retry attempts) finish.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Cast.Channel.Error
histogram.summary: Errors encountered on a Cast channel. Recorded when a Cast channel fails to open, or when an opened channel fails to respond to keepalive ping request and times out.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Cast.Discovery.CachedSinksAvailableCount
histogram.summary: The number of Cast devices available from the device cache at the time of a new network connection.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Cast.Discovery.ConnectedDevicesCount
histogram.summary: The number of connected Cast devices. Recorded when browser finishes discovering Cast devices. Recording happens roughly once per hour.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Cast.Discovery.KnownDevicesCount
histogram.summary: The number of known Cast devices. Recorded when browser finishes discovering Cast devices. Recording happens roughly once per hour.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Cast.Discovery.SinkSource
histogram.summary: The source of discovery for a newly-created Cast sink.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Cast.Mdns.Channel.Open_Failure
histogram.summary: Duration in milliseconds taken to fail to open a cast channel. Recorded when one opening channel attempt fails.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Cast.Mdns.Channel.Open_Success
histogram.summary: Duration in milliseconds taken to successfully open a cast channel. Recorded when one opening channel attempt succeeds.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.CastStreaming.Session.Launch
histogram.summary: Total time to launch a Cast Streaming mirror session.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.CastStreaming.Session.Length
histogram.summary: Total length of a Cast Streaming mirror session.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.CastStreaming.Start.Failure
histogram.summary: Whenever a Cast Streaming session fails to start, record the error associated with the event. This is recorded only for the extension Cast MRP. See MediaRouter.CastStreaming.Start.Failure.Native for the native MRP.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.CastStreaming.Start.Failure.Native
histogram.summary: Whenever a Cast Streaming session fails to start, record the error associated with the event. This is recorded only for the native Cast MRP. See MediaRouter.CastStreaming.Start.Failure for the extension MRP.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.CastStreaming.Start.Success
histogram.summary: Whenever a Cast Streaming session is successfully started, record if it was for a Tab or Desktop mirroring session.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Cloud.PrefAtDialogOpen
histogram.summary: The pref value to enable the cloud services. Recorded whenever the Cast dialog is opened.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Cloud.PrefAtInit
histogram.summary: The pref value to enable the cloud services. Recorded whenever the browser is initialized for a regular (not incognito or guest) profile.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Dial.AvailableDevicesCount
histogram.summary: The number of available DIAL devices. Recorded when browser finishes discovering DIAL devices. Recording happens roughly once per hour.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Dial.CreateRoute
histogram.summary: The result of a DIAL CreateRoute request. Recorded when the user requests to create a media route to a DIAL device.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Dial.FetchAppInfo
histogram.summary: The result of a DIAL app info request. Recorded when an app info request is issued to a DIAL device.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Dial.KnownDevicesCount
histogram.summary: The number of known DIAL devices. Recorded when browser finishes discovering DIAL devices. Recording happens roughly once per hour.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Dial.ParseMessage
histogram.summary: The result of parsing a Cast SDK message in the DIAL media route provider. Recorded when the DIAL media route prover finishes parsing a Cast SDK message sent from a Cast-enabled webpage.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Dial.ParsingError
histogram.summary: Error encountered while parsing DIAL device description XML text. Recorded when utility process returns an invalid DIAL device description object to browser.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Dial.TerminateRoute
histogram.summary: The result of a DIAL TerminateRoute request. Recorded the user requests to terminate a DIAL media route.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Icon.Click.Location
histogram.summary: Location the user clicked to open the Media Router dialog.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.MirroringService.SessionError
histogram.summary: Records errors encountered by the Mirroring Service. If an error occurs while media remoting, the service attempts to revert to mirroring. Otherwise mirroring is terminated.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.NetworkMonitor.ConnectionType
histogram.summary: Network connection type reported by DiscoveryNetworkMonitor whenever it detects a change in the set of networks connected to the host device which may affect local discovery.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.NetworkMonitor.NetworkChangeEventDelta
histogram.summary: Time between network change events reported by DiscoveryNetworkMonitor.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.PresentationRequest.AvailabilityUrlType
histogram.summary: The type of Presentation URL used in a PresentationRequest by a web page.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Provider.CreateRoute.Result
histogram.summary: Result of a request to a MediaRouteProvider to create a route. CreateRoute.Result (without a suffix) is recorded for the extension or an unknown MRP.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Provider.JoinRoute.Result
histogram.summary: Result of a request to a MediaRouteProvider to join a route. JoinRoute.Result (without a suffix) is recorded for the extension or an unknown MRP.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Provider.RouteControllerCreationOutcome
histogram.obsolete: Removed in M77. Route controller is specific to the old WebUI Cast dialog, and the new Views dialog does not have it.
histogram.summary: Records whether the Media Route Provider succeeded or failed to create a controller for a media route.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Provider.TerminateRoute.Result
histogram.summary: Result of a request to a MediaRouteProvider to terminate a route. TerminateRoute.Result (without a suffix) is recorded for the extension or an unknown MRP.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Provider.Version
histogram.summary: Records how the major version of the Media Router component extension compares to the major version of the browser.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Provider.WakeReason
histogram.summary: Reason that the Media Router woke the Media Route Provider event page.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Provider.Wakeup
histogram.summary: Records the outcome of waking the Media Router component's event page.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Route.CreationOutcome
histogram.summary: The number of times a Media Router create route response succeeds or fails. This breaks down the different failure types in to separate buckets.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Sink.SearchOutcome
histogram.obsolete: Removed in M77. Sink search is specific to the old WebUI Cast dialog, and the new Views dialog does not have it.
histogram.summary: The outcome of querying Media Route Providers for a sink by ID. "Success" indicates that a sink was found. This is recorded when the user attempts to cast to a sink with a manually entered ID.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Sink.SelectedType
histogram.summary: The type of the Media Sink that media is being Cast to. Recorded each time the user selects a Media Sink to start Casting.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Source.CastingSource
histogram.summary: The source of a Media Router session. This is recorded to keep track of what kind of media is being streamed.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Source.LocalFileFormat
histogram.summary: The file format of a local media Media Router session. This is recorded when a casting session begins to keep track of what kind of media is being streamed.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Source.LocalFileSize
histogram.summary: The file size of a local media Media Router session. This is recorded when a casting session begins to keep track of what kind of media is being streamed, specifically, whether it is a clip or a song, or a full length film or podcast.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Ui.Action.CloseLatency
histogram.summary: Duration in milliseconds taken from the user opening the Media Router dialog to the user closing the dialog. This is only recorded if closing the dialog is the first action the user takes.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Ui.Action.StartLocal.Latency
histogram.summary: Duration in milliseconds taken from the Media Router dialog showing the sink list and being populated with at least one device to the user selecting a device immediately after to create a new route.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Ui.Action.StartLocalPosition
histogram.summary: The index of the sink that was selected in the sink list.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Ui.Action.StartLocalSessionSuccessful
histogram.summary: The number of times a session was successfully started versus failed.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Ui.Action.StopRoute
histogram.summary: The number of times a user stops different types of routes.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Ui.Device.Count
histogram.summary: Counts the number of devices known and populated to the Media Router dialog three seconds after the dialog loads. Always expected to be non-negative.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Ui.Dialog.ActivationLocationAndCastMode
histogram.summary: This records how the Cast dialog was opened and what Cast mode was used whenever a Cast session starts.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Ui.Dialog.IconStateAtOpen
histogram.summary: Whether the Cast toolbar icon is in its ephemeral state (shown only when the feature is active), or is pinned to the toolbar by user pref / admin policy.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Ui.Dialog.LoadedWebUiRouteController
histogram.obsolete: Removed in M77. Route controller is specific to the old WebUI Cast dialog, and the new Views dialog does not have it.
histogram.summary: Duration in milliseconds it takes the WebUI route controls in the route details view to be populated after the view is opened.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Ui.Dialog.LoadedWithData
histogram.summary: Duration in milliseconds taken from the user click to open the Media Router dialog to initializing the dialog with data.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Ui.Dialog.Paint
histogram.summary: Duration in milliseconds taken from a user click to open the Media Router dialog to the initial paint.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Ui.FirstAction
histogram.summary: The first action taken by the user after opening the Media Router dialog.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Ui.IconStateAtInit
histogram.summary: Whether the Cast toolbar icon is in its ephemeral state (shown only when the feature is active), or is pinned to the toolbar by user pref / admin policy. Recorded whenever the browser is initialized for a regular (not incognito or guest) profile.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Ui.InitialState
histogram.obsolete: Removed in M77. This histogram was for tracking whether the old WebUI Cast dialog opened in sink list view or route details view.
histogram.summary: The view that was shown when the Media Router dialog is initially opened.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.Ui.Navigate.SourceSelection
histogram.summary: The number of times the user selects a specific cast source type.

histogram.name: MediaRouter.WiredDisplay.AvailableDevicesCount
histogram.summary: The number of Media Sinks available for Casting a Presentation API URL to local screens. Recorded at most once an hour, when the Wired Display Media Route Provider reports an update on the sink count.

histogram.name: MemCache.WriteResult
histogram.obsolete: Removed in M77. One day results for Android were 99.99% success, .01% Exceeded Cache Size, and an even smaller fraction Exceeded Single Entry Size.
histogram.summary: The outcome of Entry::WriteData in the memory cache.

histogram.name: Memory.AudioService.PrivateMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary: A rough estimate of the private memory footprint of the audio service process. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.AudioService.PrivateSwapFootprint
histogram.summary: An amount of private memory of the audio service process placed in swap (VmSwap). Available only on Android and Linux.

histogram.name: Memory.AudioService.ResidentSet
histogram.summary: The size of the resident memory in a audio service process. This is influenced by factors we control (e.g. memory that is not accessed can be swapped) and factors we don't control (e.g. an unrelated process using a lot of memory can force memory in our process to be swapped). Recorded once per UMA ping on Windows/Linux/ChromeOS/Android.

histogram.name: Memory.AudioService.SharedMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary: A rough estimate of the shared memory footprint of the audio service process. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.BackgroundTask.Browser.PrivateMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary: A rough estimate of the private memory footprint of the browser process while an Android background task is running. Recorded once per background task, with a random delay of 0s to 60s after it starts. Available only on Android.

histogram.name: Memory.BackgroundTask.Browser.PrivateSwapFootprint
histogram.summary: An amount of private memory the browser process placed in swap (VmSwap) by the browser process while an Android background task is running in Full Browser Mode. Recorded once per background task, with a random delay of 0s to 60s after it starts. Available only on Android.

histogram.name: Memory.BackgroundTask.Browser.ResidentSet
histogram.summary: The size of the resident memory in a browser process while an Android background task is running. Recorded once per background task, with a random delay of 0s to 60s after it starts. Available only on Android.

histogram.name: Memory.BackgroundTask.Browser.SharedMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary: A rough estimate of the shared memory footprint of the browser process while an Android background task is running. Recorded once per background task, with a random delay of 0s to 60s after it starts. Available only on Android.

histogram.name: Memory.BackgroundTask.OfflinePrefetch.Browser.PrivateMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary: A rough estimate of the private memory footprint of the browser process while the Android Offline Prefetch Android background task is running. Recorded once per background task, with a random delay of 0s to 60s after it starts. The same value is recorded into Memory.BackgroundTask.Browser.PrivateMemoryFootprint, which aggregates all task types. Available only on Android.

histogram.name: Memory.BackgroundTask.OfflinePrefetch.Browser.PrivateSwapFootprint
histogram.summary: An amount of private memory the browser process placed in swap (VmSwap) by the browser process while the Android Offline Prefetch Android background task is running in Full Browser Mode. Recorded once per background task, with a random delay of 0s to 60s after it starts. The same value is recorded into Memory.BackgroundTask.Browser.PrivateSwapFootprint, which aggregates all task types. Available only on Android.

histogram.name: Memory.BackgroundTask.OfflinePrefetch.Browser.ResidentSet
histogram.summary: The size of the resident memory in a browser process while the Android Offline Prefetch Android background task is running. Recorded once per background task, with a random delay of 0s to 60s after it starts. The same value is recorded into Memory.BackgroundTask.Browser.ResidentSet, which aggregates all task types. Available only on Android.

histogram.name: Memory.BackgroundTask.OfflinePrefetch.Browser.SharedMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary: A rough estimate of the shared memory footprint of the browser process while the Android Offline Prefetch background task is running. Recorded once per background task, with a random delay of 0s to 60s after it starts. The same value is recorded into Memory.BackgroundTask.Browser.SharedMemoryFootprint, which aggregates all task types. Available only on Android.

histogram.name: Memory.BackingStore
histogram.summary: TBD.

histogram.name: Memory.Browser
histogram.summary: The private working set used by the browser process. Recorded once per UMA ping. Note the existence of Memory.Browser.Large, which doesn't have overflow issues. TODO(rkaplow): This should be replaced with Memory.Browser.Large2 in M54.

histogram.name: Memory.Browser.Committed
histogram.summary: The total committed memory used by the browser process. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.Browser.Large
histogram.obsolete: Removed 07/2016 as it reports wrong numbers. crbug.com/629354. Replaced with Memory.Browser.Large2
histogram.summary: The private working set used by the browser process. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.Browser.Large2
histogram.summary: The private working set used by the browser process. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.Browser.MemoryFootprint
histogram.summary:
This metric is only recorded on iOS. This measures the sum of PrivateMemoryFootprint and SharedMemoryFootprint, which is possible because iOS is single-process.
This histogram is of special interest to the chrome-analysis-team@. Do not change its semantics or retire it without talking to them first.

histogram.name: Memory.Browser.PrivateMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary:
A rough estimate of the private memory footprint of the browser process. Recorded once per UMA ping.
This histogram is of special interest to the chrome-analysis-team@. Do not change its semantics or retire it without talking to them first.

histogram.name: Memory.Browser.PrivateSwapFootprint
histogram.summary: An amount of private memory of the browser process placed in swap (VmSwap). Available only on Android and Linux.

histogram.name: Memory.Browser.ResidentSet
histogram.summary: The size of the resident memory in the browser process. This is influenced by factors we control (e.g. memory that is not accessed can be swapped) and factors we don't control (e.g. an unrelated process using a lot of memory can force memory in our process to be swapped). Recorded once per UMA ping on Windows/Linux/ChromeOS/Android.

histogram.name: Memory.Browser.SharedMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary: A rough estimate of the shared memory footprint of the browser process. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.CachedFontAndDC
histogram.summary: TBD.

histogram.name: Memory.Chrome
histogram.obsolete: Removed 11/2017. No direct replacement, but Memory.Renderer.PrivateMemoryFootprint is similar.
histogram.summary: The private working set used by each chrome:// renderer process. Each process provides one sample. Recorded once per process per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.ChromeProcessCount
histogram.summary: The count of active chrome:// renderer processes. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.CompressibleStringCount
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of Aug 2016. CompressibleString has been reverted once at https://crrev.com/2227933002.
histogram.summary: This records the frequency with which JavaScript source strings are compressed and decompressed in foreground and background tabs. Compressing runs 10 seconds after the tab goes background and decompressing runs when JavaScript source string is required (e.g. V8 starts to compile). This measurement is a preparation to introduce CompressibleString class for JavaScript source strings to reduce Blink memory usage.

histogram.name: Memory.Coordinator.FreeMemoryUntilCritical
histogram.summary: Available free memory until the system will be in a critical state. Critical is as defined by the OS (swapping will occur, or physical memory will run out, etc).

histogram.name: Memory.Coordinator.StateDuration
histogram.summary: Time elapsed between the global state changes.

histogram.name: Memory.Coordinator.StateOnCriticalNotificationReceived
histogram.summary: The global state of memory coordinator when a critical pressure notification is received.

histogram.name: Memory.Coordinator.StateOnModerateNotificationReceived
histogram.summary: The global state of memory coordinator when a moderate pressure notification is received.

histogram.name: Memory.Coordinator.TotalPrivate
histogram.summary: The total private working set memory used by the browser and renderer processes when the memory coordinator changes the global memory state.

histogram.name: Memory.Coordinator.TrimMemoryLevel.Normal
histogram.summary: Android: Records trim memory level when the global state is NORMAL.

histogram.name: Memory.Coordinator.TrimMemoryLevel.Suspended
histogram.summary: Android: Records trim memory level when the global state is SUSPENDED.

histogram.name: Memory.Coordinator.TrimMemoryLevel.Throttled
histogram.summary: Android: Records trim memory level when the global state is THROTTLED.

histogram.name: Memory.DiscardableAllocationSize
histogram.summary: The discardable memory allocation size in KB. Recorded each time a new discardable memory instance is created.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Browser.EGLShaderCacheSize.Android
histogram.summary: The size of the EGL_ANDROID_blob_cache cache file on disk. Recorded once per Chrome launch, after the first page has loaded.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Browser.PrivateMemoryFootprint.MacOS
histogram.summary: A rough estimate of the private memory footprint of the browser process.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Browser.PurgedMemory
histogram.summary: Amount of reclaimed memory (in terms of the private working set size) after a purge request on the browser process. Recorded 2 seconds after the purge request.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Browser2
histogram.summary: The browser process's memory usage reported by the memory instrumentation service in MB.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Browser2.Custom
histogram.summary: The browser process's memory usage reported by the memory instrumentation service in bytes.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Browser2.Small
histogram.summary: The browser process's memory usage reported by the memory instrumentation service in KB.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Browser2.Tiny
histogram.summary: The browser process's memory usage reported by the memory instrumentation service in bytes.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.CompressedPagesPerSecond
histogram.summary: The number of pages compressed per second. Recorded every 60 seconds. Only recorded on macOS.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Debug.FailedProcessDumpsPerGlobalDump
histogram.summary: Records how many failed process dumps there were for a given global dump completed by the memory instrumentation service (resource_coordinator/memory_instrumentation/coordinator_impl.h). This is recorded once each time a global dump request is completed.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Debug.GlobalDumpDuration
histogram.summary: Records how long it took the memory instrumentation service (resource_coordinator/memory_instrumentation/coordinator_impl.h) from starting working on a global memory dump request until complating that request. This does not include queuing time (requests are handled serially). This is recorded once each time a request is completed.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Debug.GlobalDumpQueueLength
histogram.summary: Number of global memory dump requests queued in by the memory instrumentation service (resource_coordinator/memory_instrumentation/coordinator_impl.h). This includes the request currently being worked on if any. This is recorded once each time a new request is queued. This happens at least once per UMA ping to in order to compute Memory.Experimental.Renderer2, Memory.Experimental.Browser2 etc.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.DecompressedPagesPerSecond
histogram.summary: The number of pages decompressed per second. Recorded every 60 seconds. Only recorded on macOS.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Extension.PrivateMemoryFootprint.MacOS
histogram.summary: A rough estimate of the private memory footprint of an extension process.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Extension2
histogram.summary: The extension process's memory usage reported by the memory instrumentation service in MB.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Extension2.Custom
histogram.summary: The extension process's memory usage reported by the memory instrumentation service in bytes.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Extension2.Small
histogram.summary: The extension process's memory usage reported by the memory instrumentation service in KB.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Extension2.Tiny
histogram.summary: The extension process's memory usage reported by the memory instrumentation service in bytes.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Gpu.PhysicalFootprint.MacOS
histogram.summary: The physical footprint of the GPU process on macOS. Other measurements fail to correctly account for OpenGL memory usage. This metric also has flaws and is not intended for permanent use. It's an emergency measure added to help debug https://crbug.com/713854. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Gpu.PrivateMemoryFootprint.MacOS
histogram.summary: A rough estimate of the private memory footprint of the GPU process.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Gpu2

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.NetworkService2.Custom
histogram.summary: The network service process's memory usage reported by the memory instrumentation service in bytes. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.NetworkService2.Small
histogram.summary: The network service process's memory usage reported by the memory instrumentation service in KiB. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.NetworkService2.Tiny
histogram.summary: The network service process's memory usage reported by the memory instrumentation service in bytes. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.NewMemoryPressureMonitor.SessionDurations.CriticalPressure
histogram.summary: The amount of time spent in the critical memory pressure state. Recorded every time the level changes and at shutdown.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.NewMemoryPressureMonitor.SessionDurations.NoPressure
histogram.summary: The amount of time spent in the no memory pressure state. Recorded every time the level changes and at shutdown.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.BrowserMonitorStatus
histogram.obsolete: Removed 08/2018. Made a decision in issue 871507 that we no longer need this data.
histogram.summary: Records the status of OOM intervention if the feature is turned on, with various reasons for failure, in the browser process. Recorded once at browser startup.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.InterventionStateOnCrash
histogram.summary: Records state of the intervention (accepted or declined) when the foreground renderer is crashed after near-OOM intervention is triggered.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.NavigationAfterDetectionTime
histogram.summary: Records the time elapsed between a near-OOM detection and when a navigation is started.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.NearOomDetectionEndReason
histogram.summary: Records the reason for stopping near-OOM detection. This isn't recorded when a near-OOM situation was detected.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.OomProtectedCrashAfterDetectionTime
histogram.summary: Records the time elapsed between a near-OOM detection and when an OOM protected renderer was crashed.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.ReducedBlinkUsageAfter10secs
histogram.summary: Reduced amount of blink usage after 10 seconds of intervention. This reports negative numbers when reduced.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.ReducedBlinkUsageAfter10secs2
histogram.summary: Reduced amount of blink usage after 10 seconds of intervention. This reports positive numbers when reduced.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.ReducedBlinkUsageAfter20secs
histogram.summary: Reduced amount of blink usage after 20 seconds of intervention. This reports negative numbers when reduced.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.ReducedBlinkUsageAfter20secs2
histogram.summary: Reduced amount of blink usage after 20 seconds of intervention. This reports positive numbers when reduced.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.ReducedBlinkUsageAfter30secs
histogram.summary: Reduced amount of blink usage after 30 seconds of intervention. This reports negative numbers when reduced.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.ReducedBlinkUsageAfter30secs2
histogram.summary: Reduced amount of blink usage after 30 seconds of intervention. This reports positive numbers when reduced.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.ReducedRendererPMFAfter10secs
histogram.summary: Reduced amount of renderer pmf after 10 seconds of intervention. This reports negative numbers when reduced.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.ReducedRendererPMFAfter10secs2
histogram.summary: Reduced amount of renderer pmf after 10 seconds of intervention. This reports positive numbers when reduced.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.ReducedRendererPMFAfter20secs
histogram.summary: Reduced amount of renderer pmf after 20 seconds of intervention. This reports negative numbers when reduced.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.ReducedRendererPMFAfter20secs2
histogram.summary: Reduced amount of renderer pmf after 20 seconds of intervention. This reports positive numbers when reduced.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.ReducedRendererPMFAfter30secs
histogram.summary: Reduced amount of renderer pmf after 30 seconds of intervention. This reports negative numbers when reduced.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.ReducedRendererPMFAfter30secs2
histogram.summary: Reduced amount of renderer pmf after 30 seconds of intervention. This reports positive numbers when reduced.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.RendererBlinkUsage
