histogram.summary: The renderer process's memory usage reported every second when OOM intervention is enabled.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.RendererBlinkUsageAtOOM
histogram.summary: The renderer process' Blink memory usage, as sum of V8, PartitionAlloc and BlinkGC allocations, when a foreground OOM occurs. This was last recorded metric by renderer a few seconds before getting killed.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.RendererEnabledStatus
histogram.obsolete: Removed 08/2018. Made a decision in issue 871507 that we no longer need this data.
histogram.summary: Records the status of OOM intervention in renderer process if the feature is turned on, with various reasons for failure. Recorded at each navigation or reload.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.RendererGoneAfterDetectionTime
histogram.summary: Records the time elapsed between a near-OOM detection and when a foreground renderer process was gone. This histogram does not contain OOM protected crashes.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.RendererPrivateMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary: The renderer process's PMF size reported every second when OOM intervention is enabled.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.RendererPrivateMemoryFootprintAtOOM
histogram.summary: The renderer process' private memory footprint when a foreground OOM occurs. This was last recorded metric by renderer a few seconds before getting killed.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.RendererSwapFootPrint
histogram.summary: The renderer process's swap size reported every second when OOM intervention is enabled.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.RendererSwapFootprintAtOOM
histogram.summary: The renderer process' swap size when a foreground OOM occurs. This was last recorded metric by renderer a few seconds before getting killed.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.RendererTimeSinceLastNavigationAtDetection
histogram.summary: Records the time since last main frame navigation start on the renderer process when high memory usage is detected. Record only when navigation happened at least once.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.RendererTimeSinceLastNavigationAtIntervention
histogram.obsolete: Removed 1/2019 because RendererTimeSinceLastNavigationAtDetection replaced this metric by expanding coverage to include detection-only mode.
histogram.summary: Records the time since last main frame navigation start on the renderer process when intervention is triggered. Record only when navigation happened at least once.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.RendererTimeSinceLastNavigationAtOOM
histogram.summary: Records the time since last main frame navigation start on the renderer process when it OOMs in foreground visible state. Record 0 if no navigation was started.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.RendererTimeSinceStartMonitoringAtDetection
histogram.summary: Records the time since starting monitor on renderer when high memory usage is detected.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.RendererVMSize
histogram.summary: The renderer process's virtual memory usage reported every second when OOM intervention is enabled.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.RendererVmSizeAtOOM
histogram.summary: The renderer process' virtual memory usage, when a foreground OOM occurs. This was last recorded metric by renderer a few seconds before getting killed.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.RendererVmSizeAtOOMLarge
histogram.summary: The renderer process' virtual memory usage, when a foreground OOM occurs. This was last recorded metric by renderer a few seconds before getting killed.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.UserDecision
histogram.summary: Records user decisions on near-OOM intervention.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.V8UsageAfter10secs
histogram.summary: V8 memory usage 10 seconds after intervention.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.V8UsageAfter20secs
histogram.summary: V8 memory usage 20 seconds after intervention.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.V8UsageAfter30secs
histogram.summary: V8 memory usage 30 seconds after intervention.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.OomIntervention.V8UsageBefore
histogram.summary: V8 memory usage right before intervention.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Renderer
histogram.summary: The renderer process's memory usage at the specific timing, i.e. after a page load and 5/10/15minutes after backgrounded.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Renderer.HighestPrivateMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary: The highest private memory footprint of a renderer process in (X mins after the first page navigation, Y mins after the first page navigation]. (X, Y] is (0, 2], (2, 4], (4, 8], and (8, 16]. The metric is recorded at the Y's timing.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Renderer.LoadsInMainFrameDuringUptime
histogram.summary: The number of loads in a main frame during the lifetime of a render process (excludes extensions). Emitted when the processes quits.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Renderer.LocalFrameRootPurgeSignal
histogram.summary:
Recorded after every memory purge signal received by a foreground local frame root with accelerated compositing enabled. Records whether or not the signal was the first received by the local frame root.
This metric has changed over time and it is important to consider these changes when analyzing historical data.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Renderer.PeakResidentSet.AtHighestPrivateMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary: The peak RSS size(VmHMW) of a render process when the highest private memory footprint is observed after the first navigation start.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Renderer.PrivateMemoryFootprint.MacOS
histogram.summary: A rough estimate of the private memory footprint of a renderer process.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Renderer.PurgedMemory
histogram.summary: Amount of reclaimed memory (in terms of Memory.Experimental.Renderer.TotalAllocated) after a purge request on a renderer process. Recorded 2 seconds after the purge request.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Renderer.Uptime
histogram.summary: The uptime of a render process in time ticks (excludes extensions). Emitted when the processes quits.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Renderer.WebpageCount.AtHighestPrivateMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary: The number of webpages when the highest private memory footprint of renderer process is observed after the first navigation start.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Renderer2
histogram.summary: The renderer process's memory usage reported by the memory instrumentation service in MB.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Renderer2.Custom
histogram.summary: The renderer process's memory usage reported by the memory instrumentation service in bytes.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Renderer2.Small
histogram.summary: The renderer process's memory usage reported by the memory instrumentation service in KB.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Renderer2.Tiny
histogram.summary: The renderer process's memory usage reported by the memory instrumentation service in bytes.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.SwapInPerSecond
histogram.summary: The number of swap-ins per second. Recorded every 60 seconds.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.SwapOutPerSecond
histogram.summary: The number of swap-outs per second. Recorded every 60 seconds.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.SwapThrashingLevel
histogram.summary: The swap thrashing level, which is recorded periodically. This shows the cumulative number of seconds that systems spend in each of the swap thrashing states. Only available on Windows.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.SwapThrashingLevelChanges
histogram.summary: The number of swap-thrashing level state changes for each possible pairwise state change. Only available on Windows.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Total2.PrivateMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary: A rough estimate of the private memory footprint of all processes.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.UserLevelMemoryPressureSignal.RendererPrivateMemoryFootprintAfter
histogram.summary: The renderer process' private memory footprint 10 seconds after a memory pressure signal is generated.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.UserLevelMemoryPressureSignal.RendererPrivateMemoryFootprintBefore
histogram.summary: The renderer process' private memory footprint right before a memory pressure signal is generated.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Utility2
histogram.summary: The utility process's memory usage reported by the memory instrumentation service in MB. Recorded once per UMA ping for each process.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Utility2.Custom
histogram.summary: The utility process's memory usage reported by the memory instrumentation service in bytes. Recorded once per UMA ping for each process.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Utility2.Small
histogram.summary: The utility process's memory usage reported by the memory instrumentation service in KB. Recorded once per UMA ping for each process.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.Utility2.Tiny
histogram.summary: The utility process's memory usage reported by the memory instrumentation service in bytes. Recorded once per UMA ping for each process.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.WMIRefresher.Init.AddEnumDuration
histogram.summary: The time it takes for the call to |AddEnum| to complete during the initialization of the WMI Refresher.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.WMIRefresher.Init.AddEnumDuration2
histogram.summary: The time it takes for the call to |AddEnum| to complete during the initialization of the WMI Refresher.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.WMIRefresher.Init.CoCreateInstanceDuration
histogram.summary: The time it takes for the call to |CoCreateInstance| to complete during the initialization of the WMI Refresher.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.WMIRefresher.Init.CreateLocalWmiConnectionDuration
histogram.summary: The time it takes for the call to |CreateLocalWmiConnection| to complete during the initialization of the WMI Refresher.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.WMIRefresher.InitDiskIdleTimeConfigStatus
histogram.summary: The result of configuring the WMIRefresher to read the disk idle time values.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.WMIRefresher.InitializeDiskIdleTimeConfigDuration
histogram.summary: The time it takes to initialize the disk idle config in the WMI Refresher.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.WMIRefresher.InitializeDiskIdleTimeConfigDuration2
histogram.summary: The time it takes to initialize the disk idle config in the WMI Refresher.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.WMIRefresher.RefreshDiskIdleTimeDuration
histogram.summary: The time it takes to do a refresh of the disk idle time value in the WMI Refresher.

histogram.name: Memory.Experimental.WMIRefresher.RefreshDiskIdleTimeStatus
histogram.summary: The result of refreshing the disk idle time value in the WMIRefresher.

histogram.name: Memory.Extension
histogram.summary: The private working set used by each extension process. Each process provides one sample. Recorded once per process per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.Extension.PrivateMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary: A rough estimate of the private memory footprint of the extension process. Recorded once per process per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.Extension.ResidentSet
histogram.summary: The size of the resident memory in an extension process. This is influenced by factors we control (e.g. memory that is not accessed can be swapped) and factors we don't control (e.g. an unrelated process using a lot of memory can force memory in our process to be swapped). Recorded once per UMA ping on Windows/Linux/ChromeOS/Android.

histogram.name: Memory.Extension.SharedMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary: A rough estimate of the shared memory footprint of the extension process. Recorded once per process per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.ExtensionProcessCount
histogram.summary: The count of active extension processes. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.GlyphPagesPerLoad
histogram.summary: The number of glyph pages present in the renderer when it commits a load. Since this is per-sub-process, you can get the average number of glyph pages in the system by multiplying this number with the average number of renderers. Note that this typically won't count the glyph pages added as a result of the load that just committed, since layout will happen after the commit. There are 512 bytes per glyph page, but this number also very closely approximates the number of glyph width map pages in the same renderer. The only difference is that if you have font fallback, it will make a new glyph page and no width page, but in most common cases there is no fallback). Width pages are 1K each (256 floats), so you could think of this value as being the number of "1.5K units related to glyphs per renderer per page load".

histogram.name: Memory.Gpu
histogram.summary: The private working set used by the GPU process. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.GPU.PeakMemoryAllocationSource
histogram.summary: The maximum amount of memory of the GPU process allocated by a particular source during a user interaction (e.g. tab-switch, page-load, scroll etc.). See Memory.GPU.PeakMemoryUsage.

histogram.name: Memory.GPU.PeakMemoryUsage
histogram.summary: The maximum amount of memory of the GPU process during a particular interaction (e.g. tab-switch, page-load, scroll etc.).

histogram.name: Memory.Gpu.PrivateMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary:
A rough estimate of the private memory footprint of the gpu process. Recorded once per UMA ping.
This histogram is of special interest to the chrome-analysis-team@. Do not change its semantics or retire it without talking to them first.

histogram.name: Memory.Gpu.PrivateSwapFootprint
histogram.summary: An amount of private memory of the GPU process placed in swap (VmSwap). Available only on Android and Linux.

histogram.name: Memory.Gpu.ResidentSet
histogram.summary: The size of the resident memory in the GPU process. This is influenced by factors we control (e.g. memory that is not accessed can be swapped) and factors we don't control (e.g. an unrelated process using a lot of memory can force memory in our process to be swapped). Recorded once per UMA ping on Windows/Linux/ChromeOS/Android.

histogram.name: Memory.Gpu.SharedMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary: A rough estimate of the shared memory footprint of the gpu process. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.Graphics
histogram.summary: System-wide graphics driver memory consumption. Recorded on Chrome OS for platforms where it is exposed by the kernel (for example, Intel i915 and Exynos Mali). Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.HeapProfiler.Browser.Malloc
histogram.summary: Browser process memory allocated with malloc when UMA heap profile is taken.

histogram.name: Memory.IPCChannelReader.ReceivedMessageSize
histogram.summary: Size of messages received by IPC::ChannelReader.

histogram.name: Memory.MovableStringParkingAction
histogram.summary: Recorded each time a Movable String is (un)parked, with the context: foreground/background.

histogram.name: Memory.MovableStringsCount
histogram.summary: This records the number of movable Javascript source string resources in a given renderer, at the time page is backgrounded. Recorded alongside Memory.MovableStringsTotalSizeKb.

histogram.name: Memory.MovableStringsTotalSizeKb
histogram.summary: This records the total size of movable Javascript source string resources in a given renderer, at the time page is backgrounded. Recorded alongside Memory.MovableStringsCount.

histogram.name: Memory.NativeClient
histogram.summary: The private working set used by each Native Client loader process. Each process provides one sample. Recorded once per process per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.NativeClientBroker
histogram.summary: The private working set used by each Native Client broker process. Each process provides one sample. Recorded once per process per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.NativeLibrary.MappedAndResidentMemoryFootprint
histogram.obsolete: Removed 03/2019. Replaced by Memory.NativeLibrary.MappedAndResidentMemoryFootprint2 to add more precision to buckets.
histogram.summary: The size of the resident memory for the native library code across all processes. This metric is computed by parsing proc/self/pagemap and counting native library pages that are mapped and present in RAM for at least one Chrome process. Recorded once per UMA ping. Available only on Android.

histogram.name: Memory.NativeLibrary.MappedAndResidentMemoryFootprint2
histogram.summary: The size of the resident memory for the native library code across all processes. This metric is computed by parsing proc/self/pagemap and counting native library pages that are mapped and present in RAM for at least one Chrome process. Recorded once per UMA ping. Available only on Android.

histogram.name: Memory.NativeLibrary.MappedAndResidentMemoryFootprintCollectionStatus
histogram.summary: Result of an attempt to read /proc/self/pagemap when determining the amount of resident memory mapped by the current process. Reading the file is performed as part of computing the Memory.NativeLibrary.MappedAndResidentMemoryFootprint2 histogram. Recorded once per UMA ping. Available only on Android.

histogram.name: Memory.NativeLibrary.NotResidentOrderedCodeMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary: The size of the native library code which was ordered, yet is not resident in memory, across all processes. This metric is computed by parsing proc/self/pagemap and counting native library pages that are mapped and present in RAM for at least one Chrome process. Recorded once per UMA ping. Available only on Android.

histogram.name: Memory.NativeLibrary.ResidentNotOrderedCodeMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary: The size of the native library code which is outside the ordered section, yet is resident in memory, across all processes. This metric is computed by parsing proc/self/pagemap and counting native library pages that are mapped and present in RAM for at least one Chrome process. Recorded once per UMA ping. Available only on Android.

histogram.name: Memory.NetworkService.PrivateMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary: A rough estimate of the private memory footprint of the network service process. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.NetworkService.PrivateSwapFootprint
histogram.summary: An amount of private memory of the network service process placed in swap (VmSwap). Available only on Android and Linux.

histogram.name: Memory.NetworkService.ResidentSet
histogram.summary: The size of the resident memory in a network service process. This is influenced by factors we control (e.g. memory that is not accessed can be swapped) and factors we don't control (e.g. an unrelated process using a lot of memory can force memory in our process to be swapped). Recorded once per UMA ping on Windows/Linux/ChromeOS/Android.

histogram.name: Memory.NetworkService.SharedMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary: A rough estimate of the shared memory footprint of the network service process. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.OOMKill.Contents.MemAllocatedMB
histogram.summary: System-wide memory allocation right after a renderer was killed by oom-killer, roughly equivalent to the sum of memory allocated with malloc() in userspace plus graphics driver memory.

histogram.name: Memory.OOMKill.Contents.MemAvailableMB
histogram.summary: System-wide file-backed memory plus free memory right after a renderer was killed by oom-killer, which should be smaller than or close to what the kernel uses to trigger low-memory notifications for tab discards. If this is higher than the kernel's threshold for tab discards, renderers may be killed due to reasons other than out-of-memory.

histogram.name: Memory.OOMKill.Contents.MemGraphicsMB
histogram.summary: Graphics driver (GEM object) memory right after a renderer was killed by oom-killer.

histogram.name: Memory.OOMKill.Contents.MemShmemMB
histogram.summary: System-wide shared memory right after a renderer was killed by oom-killer. Used primarily for shared buffers in the graphics system. Tracked because it's a historical source of leaks on Chrome OS.

histogram.name: Memory.OOMKill.Extensions.MemAllocatedMB
histogram.summary: System-wide memory allocation right after a renderer was killed by oom-killer, roughly equivalent to the sum of memory allocated with malloc() in userspace plus graphics driver memory.

histogram.name: Memory.OOMKill.Extensions.MemAvailableMB
histogram.summary: System-wide file-backed memory plus free memory right after a renderer was killed by oom-killer, which should be smaller than or close to what the kernel uses to trigger low-memory notifications for tab discards. If this is higher than the kernel's threshold for tab discards, renderers may be killed due to reasons other than out-of-memory.

histogram.name: Memory.OOMKill.Extensions.MemGraphicsMB
histogram.summary: Graphics driver (GEM object) memory right after a renderer was killed by oom-killer.

histogram.name: Memory.OOMKill.Extensions.MemShmemMB
histogram.summary: System-wide shared memory right after a renderer was killed by oom-killer. Used primarily for shared buffers in the graphics system. Tracked because it's a historical source of leaks on Chrome OS.

histogram.name: Memory.OpenFDs
histogram.summary: The total number of open file descriptors opened per process. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.OpenFDsSoftLimit
histogram.summary: The limit of open file descriptors that can be opened per process. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.OtherProcessCount
histogram.summary: The count of other various utility processes (nacl, gpu, sandbox, zygote, utility). Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.ParkableString.CompressedSizeKb
histogram.summary: Total size of compressed ParkableStrings, in KB. Recorded at the same time as "Memory.ParkableString.TotalSizeKb".

histogram.name: Memory.ParkableString.Compression.Latency
histogram.summary: Time to compress a ParkableString, in us. Note: This metric drops reports on clients with low-resolution clocks, which means these reports will be biased against a portion of the population on Windows. See Windows.HasHighResolutionTimeTicks for the affected sample.

histogram.name: Memory.ParkableString.Compression.SizeKb
histogram.summary: Size of a compressed ParkableString, recorded at compression time.

histogram.name: Memory.ParkableString.Compression.ThroughputMBps
histogram.summary: Size of a compressed ParkableString, recorded at compression time.

histogram.name: Memory.ParkableString.CompressionRatio
histogram.summary: Average compression ratio, 100 * compressed_size / initial_size, for all compressed ParkableStrings. Recorded at the same time as "Memory.ParkableString.TotalSizeKb".

histogram.name: Memory.ParkableString.CompressionRatio.5min
histogram.summary: Average compression ratio, 100 * compressed_size / initial_size, for all compressed ParkableStrings. Recorded at the same time as "Memory.ParkableString.TotalSizeKb.5min".

histogram.name: Memory.ParkableString.Decompression.Latency
histogram.summary: Time to decompress a ParkableString, in us. Note: This metric drops reports on clients with low-resolution clocks, which means these reports will be biased against a portion of the population on Windows. See Windows.HasHighResolutionTimeTicks for the affected sample.

histogram.name: Memory.ParkableString.Decompression.SizeKb
histogram.summary: Original size of a compressed ParkableString, recorded at decompression time.

histogram.name: Memory.ParkableString.Decompression.ThroughputMBps
histogram.summary: Original size of a compressed ParkableString, recorded at decompression time.

histogram.name: Memory.ParkableString.DiskReadTime.5min
histogram.summary: Total main thread time used by ParkableStrings for reading over the first 5 minutes of a renderer lifetime. Starting time is from the first ParkableString being added.

histogram.name: Memory.ParkableString.DiskWriteTime.5min
histogram.summary: Total time used by ParkableStrings for writing to disk over the first 5 minutes of a renderer lifetime. Starting time is from the first ParkableString being added.

histogram.name: Memory.ParkableString.MainThreadTime.5min
histogram.summary: Total main thread time used by ParkableStrings over the first 5 minutes of a renderer lifetime. Starting time is from the first ParkableString being added.

histogram.name: Memory.ParkableString.MemorySavingsKb.5min
histogram.summary: Sum of memory saved by compression and disk discarding, in KB. Recorded at the same time as "Memory.ParkableString.TotalSizeKb.5min", when disk discarding is enabled.

histogram.name: Memory.ParkableString.OnDiskFootprintKb.5min
histogram.summary: Total disk footprint used by ParkableStrings including allocator waste, in KB. Recorded once, 5 minutes after the first ParkableString is added to a renderer, at the same time as "Memory.ParkableString.MemorySavings.5min".

histogram.name: Memory.ParkableString.OnDiskSizeKb.5min
histogram.summary: Total size of ParkableStrings discarded to disk, excluding allocator waste, in KB. Recorded once, 5 minutes after the first ParkableString is added to a renderer, at the same time as "Memory.ParkableString.MemorySavings.5min".

histogram.name: Memory.ParkableString.ParkingThreadTime.5min
histogram.summary: Total thread time used by ParkableStrings for parking over the first 5 minutes of a renderer lifetime. Starting time is from the first ParkableString being added.

histogram.name: Memory.ParkableString.Read.Latency
histogram.summary: Time to read a ParkableString from disk, in us. Note: This metric drops reports on clients with low-resolution clocks, which means these reports will be biased against a portion of the population on Windows. See Windows.HasHighResolutionTimeTicks for the affected sample.

histogram.name: Memory.ParkableString.Read.SizeKb
histogram.summary: Size read from disk for a ParkableString.

histogram.name: Memory.ParkableString.Read.ThroughputMBps
histogram.summary: Read throughput from disk for a ParkableString.

histogram.name: Memory.ParkableString.SavingsKb
histogram.summary: Sum of memory saved by compression, in KB. Recorded at the same time as "Memory.ParkableString.TotalSizeKb".

histogram.name: Memory.ParkableString.SavingsKb.5min
histogram.summary: Sum of memory saved by compression, in KB. Recorded at the same time as "Memory.ParkableString.TotalSizeKb.5min".

histogram.name: Memory.ParkableString.TotalSizeKb
histogram.summary: Total size of ParkableStrings assuming no compression, in KB. Recorded 30s after compression was triggered, provided that the renderer stayed backgrounded.

histogram.name: Memory.ParkableString.TotalSizeKb.5min
histogram.summary: Total size of ParkableStrings assuming no compression, in KB. Recorded once, 5 minutes after the first ParkableString is added to a renderer, at the same time as "Memory.ParkableString.MainThreadTime.5min".

histogram.name: Memory.ParkableString.Write.Latency
histogram.summary: Time to write a ParkableString to disk, in us. Note: This metric drops reports on clients with low-resolution clocks, which means these reports will be biased against a portion of the population on Windows. See Windows.HasHighResolutionTimeTicks for the affected sample.

histogram.name: Memory.ParkableString.Write.SizeKb
histogram.summary: Size of an on-disk ParkableString, recorded at write time.

histogram.name: Memory.ParkableString.Write.ThroughputMBps
histogram.summary: Throughput of a ParkableString disk write.

histogram.name: Memory.PartitionAlloc.MainThreadTime.5min
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated and removed from the code 06/2020.
histogram.summary: Total main thread time used by PartitionAlloc's decommit logic over the first 5 minutes of a renderer lifetime. Starting time is renderer initialization.

histogram.name: Memory.PepperFlashPlugin
histogram.summary: The private working set used by each Pepper Flash plugin process. Each plugin process provides one sample. Recorded once per process per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.PepperPlugin
histogram.summary: The private working set used by each Pepper plugin process. Each plugin process provides one sample. Recorded once per process per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.PepperPluginBroker
histogram.summary: The private working set used by each Pepper plugin broker process. Each process provides one sample. Recorded once per process per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.PepperPluginBrokerProcessCount
histogram.summary: The count of Pepper plugin broker processes, recorded once per metrics services (UMA) update. See MetricsReportingScheduler for details.

histogram.name: Memory.PepperPluginProcessCount
histogram.summary: The count of active Pepper plugin processes. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.Plugin
histogram.summary: The private working set used by each plugin process. Each plugin process provides one sample. Recorded once per process per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.PluginProcessCount
histogram.summary: The count of active plugin processes. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.PressureLevel
histogram.summary: The memory pressure level, which is recorded periodically. This shows the cumulative number of seconds that systems spend in each of the memory pressure states.

histogram.name: Memory.PressureLevelChanges
histogram.summary: The number of pressure level state changes for each possible pairwise state change.

histogram.name: Memory.PressureWindowDuration
histogram.summary: The time spent in each of the memory pressure state. Recorded when transitioning from a memory pressure state to the other and when exiting memory pressure.

histogram.name: Memory.ProcessCount
histogram.summary: The count of all active processes. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.ProcessLimit
histogram.summary: The current process limit. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.Renderer
histogram.summary: The private working set used by each renderer process. Each renderer process provides one sample. Recorded once per UMA ping. TODO(rkaplow): This should be replaced with Memory.Renderer.Large2 in M54.

histogram.name: Memory.Renderer.BlinkCachedMetadataGetResult
histogram.summary: The state of CachedMetadata on every attempt to use it for a script resource. Used for experiment in http://crbug.com/1045052.

histogram.name: Memory.Renderer.Committed
histogram.summary: The total committed memory used by each renderer process. Each renderer process provides one sample. Recorded once per process per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.Renderer.EstimatedDroppableEncodedSize
histogram.summary: The esitimated memory size which would be reduced when reusing disk cache is implemented. If it is 100% sure that the encoded data is in disk cache, the same data in memory can be dropped to reduce memory usage. This is rencorded when an ImageResource is pruned. See crbug/664437.

histogram.name: Memory.Renderer.Large2
histogram.summary: The private working set used by each renderer process. Each renderer process provides one sample. Recorded once per process per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.Renderer.PrivateMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary:
A rough estimate of the private memory footprint of the renderer process. Recorded once per process per UMA ping.
Do not modify this metric in any way without contacting speed-metrics-dev@chromium.org AND chrome-analysis-team@google.com.

histogram.name: Memory.Renderer.ResidentSet
histogram.summary: The size of the resident memory in a renderer process. This is influenced by factors we control (e.g. memory that is not accessed can be swapped) and factors we don't control (e.g. an unrelated process using a lot of memory can force memory in our process to be swapped). Recorded once per UMA ping on Windows/Linux/ChromeOS/Android.

histogram.name: Memory.Renderer.SharedMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary: A rough estimate of the shared memory footprint of the renderer process. Recorded once per process per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.RendererAll
histogram.obsolete: Removed 11/2017. No direct replacement, although Memory.Total.PrivateMemoryFootprint is similar.
histogram.summary: The private working set used by each renderer process, including all renderer types, i.e. this includes Chrome renderer, extensions renderer, as well as regular renderer processes. Each renderer process provides one sample. Recorded once per process per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.RendererAll.Committed
histogram.obsolete: Removed 01/2018. No direct replacement, although Memory.Total.PrivateMemoryFootprint is similar.
histogram.summary: The total committed memory used by each renderer process, including all renderer types, i.e. this includes Chrome renderer, extensions renderer, as well as regular renderer processes. Each renderer process provides one sample. Recorded once per process per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.RendererGrowthIn30Min
histogram.summary: Growth speed of the private working set used by each renderer process per 30 minutes. The usage and growth speed is recorded at most every 30 minutes, not every exact 30 minutes. If the interval is longer than 30 minutes, it is normalized to a speed KB per 30 minutes. Each renderer process provides one sample. Recorded once per UMA log unless this is the first time the UMA log is recorded after startup of the renderer, 30 minutes have not passed from the last recording of the renderer or the usage goes down. If the usage goes down, the amount of the shrink will be recorded in the Memory.RendererShrinkIn30Min histogram.

histogram.name: Memory.RendererProcessCount
histogram.summary:
The count of active renderer processes. Recorded once per UMA ping. Excludes renderers hosting chrome-extension:// and/or chrome:// URLs.
Computed based on OS data.
WARNINGS: Not reliable on Android, see https://crbug.com/875400. Ignores processes that do not host a widget, see https://crbug.com/949977#c36. Consider using Memory.RenderProcessHost.Count.* instead.

histogram.name: Memory.RendererShrinkIn30Min
histogram.summary: Shrink speed of the private working set used by each renderer process per 30 minutes. The usage and shrink speed is recorded at most every 30 minutes, not every exact 30 minutes. If the interval is longer than 30 minutes, it is normalized to a speed KB per 30 minutes. Each renderer process provides one sample. Recorded once per UMA log unless this is the first time the UMA log is recorded after startup of the renderer, 30 minutes have not passed from the last recording of the renderer or the usage goes up. If the usage goes up, the amount of the growth will be recorded in the Memory.RendererGrowthIn30Min histogram.

histogram.name: Memory.RenderProcessHost.Count.All
histogram.summary:
The count of all renderer processes. Recorded once per UMA ping. Covers all renderer processes, including ones hosting web content (i.e. http(s):// and/or file://), extensions (i.e. chrome-extension://) and WebUI (i.e. chrome://).
Computed based on count of RenderProcessHost objects, even if 1) they have not been launched yet (e.g. during session restore) or 2) they are dead (e.g. killed by the browser process or the OS).

histogram.name: Memory.RenderProcessHost.Count.InitializedAndNotDead
histogram.summary:
The count of active renderer processes. Recorded once per UMA ping. Covers all renderer processes, including ones hosting web content (i.e. http(s):// and/or file://), extensions (i.e. chrome-extension://) and WebUI (i.e. chrome://).
Computed based on count of RenderProcessHost objects that are IsInitializedAndNotDead (i.e. have been launched and not terminated yet).

histogram.name: Memory.SandboxHelper
histogram.summary: The private working set used by each sandbox helper process. Each sandbox helper process provides one sample. Recorded once per process per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.ShmemDir.AmountOfFreeSpace
histogram.summary: The amount of free space in temporary directory for shared memory files. Recorded each time a new discardable memory manager instance is created.

histogram.name: Memory.Stats.Win.AvailPageFile
histogram.obsolete: Removed in 595320 and replaced with Memory.Stats.Win.AvailPageFile2.
histogram.summary: Windows-only metric that represents the maximum amount of memory the current process can commit. This value is equal to or smaller than the system-wide available commit value.

histogram.name: Memory.Stats.Win.AvailPageFile2
histogram.summary: Windows-only metric that represents the maximum amount of memory the current process can commit. This value is equal to or smaller than the system-wide available commit value.

histogram.name: Memory.Stats.Win.AvailPhys
histogram.obsolete: Removed in 595320 and replaced with Memory.Stats.Win.AvailPhys2.
histogram.summary: Windows-only metric that represents the amount of physical memory currently available. This is the amount of physical memory that can be immediately reused without having to write its contents to disk first. It is the sum of the size of the standby, free, and zero lists.

histogram.name: Memory.Stats.Win.AvailPhys2
histogram.summary: Windows-only metric that represents the amount of physical memory currently available. This is the amount of physical memory that can be immediately reused without having to write its contents to disk first. It is the sum of the size of the standby, free, and zero lists.

histogram.name: Memory.Stats.Win.AvailVirtual
histogram.obsolete: Removed in 595320 and replaced with Memory.Stats.Win.AvailVirtual2.
histogram.summary: Windows-only metric that represents the amount of unreserved and uncommitted memory currently in the user-mode portion of the virtual address space of the calling process.

histogram.name: Memory.Stats.Win.AvailVirtual2
histogram.summary: Windows-only metric that represents the amount of unreserved and uncommitted memory currently in the user-mode portion of the virtual address space of the calling process.

histogram.name: Memory.Stats.Win.MemoryLoad
histogram.summary: Windows-only metric that represents the approximate percentage of physical memory that was in use (0 indicates no memory use and 100 indicates full memory use).

histogram.name: Memory.Stats.Win.TotalPageFile
histogram.obsolete: Removed in 595320 and replaced with Memory.Stats.Win.TotalPageFile2.
histogram.summary: Windows-only metric that represents the current committed memory limit for the system or the current process, whichever is smaller.

histogram.name: Memory.Stats.Win.TotalPageFile2
histogram.summary: Windows-only metric that represents the current committed memory limit for the system or the current process, whichever is smaller.

histogram.name: Memory.Stats.Win.TotalPhys
histogram.obsolete: Removed in 595320 and replaced with Memory.Stats.Win.TotalPhys2.
histogram.summary: Windows-only metric that represents the amount of actual physical memory.

histogram.name: Memory.Stats.Win.TotalPhys2
histogram.summary: Windows-only metric that represents the amount of actual physical memory.

histogram.name: Memory.Stats.Win.TotalVirtual
histogram.obsolete: Removed in 595320 and replaced with Memory.Stats.Win.TotalVirtual2.
histogram.summary: Windows-only metric that represents the size of the user-mode portion of the virtual address space of the calling process. This value depends on the type of process, the type of processor, and the configuration of the operating system. For example, this value is approximately 2 GB for most 32-bit processes on an x86 processor and approximately 3 GB for 32-bit processes that are large address aware running on a system with 4-gigabyte tuning enabled.

histogram.name: Memory.Stats.Win.TotalVirtual2
histogram.summary: Windows-only metric that represents the size of the user-mode portion of the virtual address space of the calling process. This value depends on the type of process, the type of processor, and the configuration of the operating system. For example, this value is approximately 2 GB for most 32-bit processes on an x86 processor and approximately 3 GB for 32-bit processes that are large address aware running on a system with 4-gigabyte tuning enabled.

histogram.name: Memory.Swap.Browser
histogram.summary: The swap used by the browser process. Recorded once per UMA ping if the system has swapped.

histogram.name: Memory.Swap.Chrome
histogram.summary: The swap used by each chrome:// renderer process. Each process provides one sample. Recorded once per process per UMA ping if the system has swapped.

histogram.name: Memory.Swap.CompressedDataSize
histogram.summary: The amount of memory that swap was compressed into. Recorded once per UMA ping if the system has swapped.

histogram.name: Memory.Swap.CompressionRatio
histogram.summary: The ratio of swapped data original size to compressed size. Recorded once per UMA ping if the system has swapped.

histogram.name: Memory.Swap.Extension
histogram.summary: The swap used by each extension process. Each process provides one sample. Recorded once per process per UMA ping if the system has swapped.

histogram.name: Memory.Swap.Gpu
histogram.summary: The swap used by the GPU process. Recorded once per UMA ping if the system has swapped.

histogram.name: Memory.Swap.HaveSwapped
histogram.summary: Indicates that the system has swapped memory out at least once since boot. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.Swap.MemUsedTotal
histogram.summary: The amount of memory that is used by swap, including bookkeeping. Recorded once per UMA ping if the system has swapped.

histogram.name: Memory.Swap.NativeClient
histogram.summary: The swap used by each Native Client loader process. Each process provides one sample. Recorded once per process per UMA ping if the system has swapped.

histogram.name: Memory.Swap.NativeClientBroker
histogram.summary: The swap used by each Native Client broker process. Each process provides one sample. Recorded once per process per UMA ping if the system has swapped.

histogram.name: Memory.Swap.NumReads
histogram.summary: The number of reads from swap. Recorded once per UMA ping if the system has swapped.

histogram.name: Memory.Swap.NumWrites
histogram.summary: The number of writes to swap. Recorded once per UMA ping if the system has swapped.

histogram.name: Memory.Swap.OriginalDataSize
histogram.summary: The amount of memory that was swapped out. Recorded once per UMA ping if the system has swapped.

histogram.name: Memory.Swap.PepperPlugin
histogram.summary: The swap used by each Pepper plugin process. Each plugin process provides one sample. Recorded once per process per UMA ping if the system has swapped.

histogram.name: Memory.Swap.PepperPluginBroker
histogram.summary: The swap used by each Pepper plugin broker process. Each process provides one sample. Recorded once per process per UMA ping if the system has swapped.

histogram.name: Memory.Swap.Plugin
histogram.summary: The swap used by each plugin process. Each plugin process provides one sample. Recorded once per process per UMA ping if the system has swapped.

histogram.name: Memory.Swap.Renderer
histogram.summary: The swap used by each renderer process. Each renderer process provides one sample. Recorded once per process per UMA ping if the system has swapped.

histogram.name: Memory.Swap.SandboxHelper
histogram.summary: The swap used by each sandbox helper process. Each sandbox helper process provides one sample. Recorded once per process per UMA ping if the system has swapped.

histogram.name: Memory.Swap.Total
histogram.summary: The sum of all processes' swap. Recorded once per UMA ping if the system has swapped. See Memory.Swap.Total2 for the same metric with higher precision bucketing.

histogram.name: Memory.Swap.Total2
histogram.summary: The sum of all processes' swap. Recorded once per UMA ping if the system has swapped.

histogram.name: Memory.Swap.Utility
histogram.summary: The swap used by each utility process. Each utility process provides one sample. Recorded once per process per UMA ping if the system has swapped.

histogram.name: Memory.Swap.Worker
histogram.summary: The swap used by each worker process. Each worker process provides one sample. Recorded once per process per UMA ping if the system has swapped.

histogram.name: Memory.Swap.Zygote
histogram.summary: The swap used by the zygote process. Recorded once per UMA ping if the system has swapped.

histogram.name: Memory.Total
histogram.summary: The sum of all processes. This is not aware of shared memory so it is just a rough estimate. Recorded once per UMA ping. See Memory.Total2 for the same metric with higher precision bucketing.

histogram.name: Memory.Total.PrivateMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary:
A rough estimate of the private memory footprint of all processes. Recorded once per UMA ping.
This histogram is of special interest to the chrome-analysis-team@. Do not change its semantics or retire it without talking to them first.

histogram.name: Memory.Total.RendererPrivateMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary: A rough estimate of the private memory footprint of all renderer processes. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.Total.ResidentSet
histogram.summary:
The size of the resident memory in all processes. This is influenced by factors we control (e.g. memory that is not accessed can be swapped) and factors we don't control (e.g. an unrelated process using a lot of memory can force memory in our process to be swapped). Recorded once per UMA ping on Windows/Linux/ChromeOS/Android.
Note: This metric is a simple sum of the resident set of all processes and this cause some double accounting for the shared portion of the working set. As a result this metric doesn't give an exact representation of how much physical memory is used by Chrome.

histogram.name: Memory.Total.SharedMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary: A rough estimate of the shared memory footprint of all processes. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.Total.SystemCommitLimit
histogram.summary: Records the system commit limit (number of total pages) x page size, collected in the info collection GPU process, once per UMA ping. This is Windows platform only.

histogram.name: Memory.Total2
histogram.summary: The sum of all processes. This is not aware of shared memory so it is just a rough estimate. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.Utility
histogram.summary: The private working set used by each utility process. Each utility process provides one sample. Recorded once per process per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.Utility.PrivateMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary: A rough estimate of the private memory footprint of the utility process. Recorded once per process per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.Utility.PrivateSwapFootprint
histogram.summary: An amount of private memory of the utility process placed in swap (VmSwap). Available only on Android and Linux.

histogram.name: Memory.Utility.ResidentSet
histogram.summary: The size of the resident memory in a utility process. This is influenced by factors we control (e.g. memory that is not accessed can be swapped) and factors we don't control (e.g. an unrelated process using a lot of memory can force memory in our process to be swapped). Recorded once per UMA ping on Windows/Linux/ChromeOS/Android.

histogram.name: Memory.Utility.SharedMemoryFootprint
histogram.summary: A rough estimate of the shared memory footprint of the utility process. Recorded once per process per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.Worker
histogram.summary: The private working set used by each worker process. Each worker process provides one sample. Recorded once per process per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.WorkerProcessCount
histogram.summary: The count of active worker processes. Recorded once per UMA ping.

histogram.name: Memory.Zygote
histogram.summary: The private working set used by the zygote process. Each zygote process provides one sample. Recorded once per process per UMA ping.

histogram.name: MemoryAndroid.DeviceMemoryClass
histogram.summary: Value of getMemoryClass() recorded once upon startup. This is an integer, device-specific constant correlated with the amount of memory available on Android device.

histogram.name: MemoryAndroid.EvictionReason
histogram.summary: Reasons behind evictions of individual tabs, recorded upon each tab eviction.

histogram.name: MemoryAndroid.LowMemoryLoadedTabCount
histogram.summary: Number of loaded (memory-resident) tabs when LowMemory notification is delivered.

histogram.name: MemoryAndroid.LowMemoryTimeBetween
histogram.summary: Time between two consecutive LowMemory notification in one foreground session.

histogram.name: MemoryAndroid.LowRamDevice
histogram.summary:
Whether Chrome is running in low ram mode. This histogram is reported on every upload by Android devices. A device is considered low-RAM if it has 512MiB of RAM or lower (see SysUtils.detectLowEndDevice()).
Note: The low-RAM determination has changed in M-49, it used to be given directly by the Android OS.

histogram.name: MemoryAndroid.NotificationBackground
histogram.summary: Memory notifications delivered through system callbacks to Chrome while in the background.

histogram.name: MemoryAndroid.NotificationForeground
histogram.summary: Memory notifications delivered through system callbacks to Chrome while in the foreground - we count LowMemory notification vs particular levels of TrimMemory foreground notification.

histogram.name: MemoryPurgeController.ReclaimedPartitionAllocInactiveTab
histogram.summary: The amount of reclaimed memory after a tab became inactive.

histogram.name: MemoryWarning.EvictedTabTimeSinceActive
histogram.summary: [iOS] When the OS sends a memory warning and the app evicts a tab, this histogram records the time since the evicted tab was active.

histogram.name: MemoryWarning.OccurrencesPerSession
histogram.summary: The number of memory warnings during a given foreground session.

histogram.name: MemoryWarning.ProtectedTabTimeSinceActive
histogram.summary: [iOS] When the OS sends a memory warning and the app protects a tab, this histogram records the time since the protected tab was active.

histogram.name: MessageLoop.DelayedTaskQueue.PendingTasksCountOnIdle
histogram.summary: The size of the delayed task queue when the loop becomes idle. Diagnosis metric for https://crbug.com/850450#c4.

histogram.name: MessageLoop.DelayedTaskQueue.PostedDelay
histogram.summary: Reports the delay of a delayed task posted to a MessageLoop. Reported once per delayed task. Diagnosis metric for https://crbug.com/850450#c4.

histogram.name: MessageLoop.DelayedTaskQueueForUI.PendingTasksCountOnIdle
histogram.summary: The size of the delayed task queue when the loop becomes idle on a UI thread. Diagnosis metric for https://crbug.com/850450#c4. Note: this metric is a bit broken on Mac OSX as CFRunLoop doesn't deterministically invoke MessageLoop::DoIdleWork().

histogram.name: MessageLoop.ScheduledSleep.Completed
histogram.summary: Reports the delay for which the MessageLoop successfully slept until an upcoming delayed task. Reported each time a MessageLoop successfully sleeps until the next delayed task.

histogram.name: MessageLoop.ScheduledSleep.Interrupted
histogram.summary: Reports the delay for which the MessageLoop had planned to sleep (next delayed task) before it was woken up early. Reported each time a MessageLoop is woken up early.

histogram.name: Mist.SwitchResult
histogram.summary: The result (e.g. success or the type of failure) of a modem interface switch operation performed by mist on Chrome OS.

histogram.name: MixedAutoupgrade.Navigation.OptedOut
histogram.summary: Whether a navigation opted out or not of Mixed Content autoupgrades. Recorded every navigation.

histogram.name: MixedAutoupgrade.ResourceRequest.ErrorOrResponseCode
histogram.summary: The net error or HTTP response code of a mixed content resource request that was autoupgraded to HTTPS

histogram.name: MixedAutoupgrade.ResourceRequest.Failure.IsAd
histogram.summary: Whether or not an autoupgrade mixed content request was for a resource we know to be an ad

histogram.name: MixedAutoupgrade.ResourceRequest.Failure.Type
histogram.summary: Logs the type of each resource request that is autoupgraded to HTTPS and fails to load.

histogram.name: MixedAutoupgrade.ResourceRequest.Response.Type
histogram.summary: Logs the type of each resource request that was autoupgraded to HTTPS and resulted in a response.

histogram.name: MixedAutoupgrade.ResourceRequest.Start.Type
histogram.summary: Logs the type of each resource request that is autoupgraded to HTTPS. This is logged when the request is started.

histogram.name: MixedAutoupgrade.ResourceRequest.Status
histogram.summary: The status of a mixed content resource request that was autoupgraded to HTTPS

histogram.name: MixedAutoupgrade.Websocket.Status
histogram.summary: The status of a mixed content websocket that was autoupgraded to WSS.

histogram.name: Mobile.CanonicalURLResult
histogram.summary: The result of the operation to retrieve the page's canonical URL.

histogram.name: Mobile.DefaultBrowser.BrowserCount
histogram.summary: Android: The number of browsers installed on the device. Logged when a new session is started.

histogram.name: Mobile.DefaultBrowser.State
histogram.summary: Android: Whether or not the device has a default browser and whether or not it is a system installed browser. Logged when a new session is started.

histogram.name: Mobile.DefaultBrowser.SystemBrowserCount
histogram.summary: Android: The number of system installed browsers. Logged when a new session is started.

histogram.name: Mobile.Legacy.Translate.Toggle.Delay
histogram.summary: Records the time between tapping the target and source language in a legacy infobar. If only one of either is tapped, nothing is logged.

histogram.name: Mobile.Legacy.Translate.Unused.Duration
histogram.summary: Records the time an unused legacy infobar persists on screen. This is logged when the infobar is closed.

histogram.name: Mobile.Messages.Badge.Tapped
histogram.summary: Records the state of a badge when it was tapped.

histogram.name: Mobile.Messages.Banner.Dismiss
histogram.summary: Records the reason why a Banner was dismissed.

histogram.name: Mobile.Messages.Banner.Event
histogram.summary: Records Infobar Banner events.

histogram.name: Mobile.Messages.Banner.OnScreenTime
histogram.summary: Records the time an Infobar (Messages) Banner is visible on screen. Used to know how long it takes a user to act on the banner before it gets auto-dismissed.

histogram.name: Mobile.Messages.ConcurrentPresented
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of multiple Infobars being presented at the same time. This will be recorded everytime an Infobar is presented.

histogram.name: Mobile.Messages.Confirm.Accept.Time
histogram.summary: Records the time between the Infobar Delegate is created, and the user accepts a Confirm Infobar action.

histogram.name: Mobile.Messages.Confirm.Event
histogram.summary: Records the events related to Confirm Infobars (e.g. Presented, Accepted, Dismissed,etc.)

histogram.name: Mobile.Messages.Modal.Event
histogram.summary: Records generic Infobar Modal events.

histogram.name: Mobile.Messages.OverflowRow.Tapped
histogram.summary: Records a tap on an Infobar overflow menu row.

histogram.name: Mobile.Messages.Passwords.Modal.Dismiss
histogram.summary: Records the reason why a Password Modal was dismissed.

histogram.name: Mobile.Messages.Passwords.Modal.Event
histogram.summary: Records Password specific Infobar Modal events.

histogram.name: Mobile.Messages.Passwords.Modal.Present
histogram.summary: Records the context when a Password Modal was presented.

histogram.name: Mobile.Messages.Save.Card.Modal.Event
histogram.summary: Records Save Card specific Infobar Modal events.

histogram.name: Mobile.Messages.Translate.Banner.Event
histogram.summary: Records the context when an iOS user taps on a Translate Banner Action Button.

histogram.name: Mobile.Messages.Translate.Modal.Event
histogram.summary: Records when an iOS user initiates an Translate specific Infobar Modal event.

histogram.name: Mobile.Messages.Translate.Modal.Present
histogram.summary: Records the context when an iOS user initiates a Translate Modal presentation.

histogram.name: Mobile.RecentTabsManager.TotalTabsFromOtherDevicesOpenAll
histogram.summary: Records the total number of tabs opened when Open all was selected from other devices in Recent Tabs.

histogram.name: Mobile.Splash.TranslucencyRemovalFailed
histogram.summary: Records the cases when removing a Trusted Web Activity's or WebAPK's translucency via a reflective call fails. Only "true" is recorded.

histogram.name: Mobile.SystemNotification.Action.Click
histogram.summary: Records which Android notifications users interact with. Recorded when an user taps the button in a notification, for any Chrome-generated notification on Android that includes a button tap-target.

histogram.name: Mobile.SystemNotification.Action.Click.Age
histogram.summary: Records the duration in minutes from the time when the system notification is shown to the time that the user clicked on an action button on the Android notification.

histogram.name: Mobile.SystemNotification.Blocked
histogram.summary: Android: Represents the number of system notifications that were blocked and could not be shown by type.

histogram.name: Mobile.SystemNotification.BlockedAfterShown
histogram.summary: Android: Represents the type of notification that was last shown before the user disabled notification permissions on Chrome. This is only logged the first time we attempt to show a notification and fail.

histogram.name: Mobile.SystemNotification.ChannelBlocked
histogram.summary: Android: Represents the number of system notifications by type that we attempted to show but were blocked, due to their notification channel being blocked. Only applies to devices on Android O and above.

histogram.name: Mobile.SystemNotification.Content.Click
histogram.summary:
Records the type of notification when the user clicks the body of Android notification. This does not include clicks on notification action buttons.
This histogram is of special interest to the chrome-analysis-team@. Do not change its semantics or retire it without talking to them first.

histogram.name: Mobile.SystemNotification.Content.Click.Age
histogram.summary: Records the duration in minutes from the time when the system notification is shown to the time that the user clicked the notification on Android.

histogram.name: Mobile.SystemNotification.CreationFailure
histogram.summary: Android: Represents the number of system notifications failed to be created by the Android API.

histogram.name: Mobile.SystemNotification.Dismiss
histogram.summary: Records the type of notification when the user dismisses the Android notification.

histogram.name: Mobile.SystemNotification.Dismiss.Age
histogram.summary: Records the duration in minutes from the time when the system notification is shown to the time that the user dismissed the notification on Android.

histogram.name: Mobile.SystemNotification.Shown
histogram.summary: Android: Represents the number of system notifications that will be successfully shown to the user by type.

histogram.name: Mobile.Translate.Unused.Count
histogram.summary: Records a histogram for an infobar (both legacy and Messages) that the user did not interact with throughout its lifetime.

histogram.name: MobileDownload.Background
histogram.summary: Android: Records events for downloads started from the background, that is, while the browser process is not running. Used to measure completion rate of these downloads.

histogram.name: MobileDownload.Background.TargetDeterminationResult
histogram.summary: Android: Records the target determination result for downloads started in the background, that is, while the browser process is not running.

histogram.name: MobileDownload.BytesDownloaded
histogram.summary: Android: Records the total data downloaded by completion status.

histogram.name: MobileDownload.BytesWasted.ChromeNetworkStack
histogram.summary: Android: Records the total data wasted during download due to resumptions.

histogram.name: MobileDownload.CancelledDownloadRemovedFromHistory
histogram.summary: Android: Records the number of cancelled download that are cleaned up from the history, after loading all the downloads from the history DB on startup.

histogram.name: MobileDownload.CancelReason
histogram.summary: Android: Records the reason that a download is canceled.

histogram.name: MobileDownload.ContextMenu.SaveImage
histogram.summary: Android: Records various counts related to the save image context menu option.

histogram.name: MobileDownload.DownloadPromptStatus
histogram.summary: Android: Records whether download dialog will be shown to the user. Recorded when the download preference is initialized in native.

histogram.name: MobileDownload.DownloadResumption
histogram.summary: Android: Records user interaction on the download resumption button.

histogram.name: MobileDownload.DownloadTime
histogram.summary: Android: Records the total time for a download by completion status.

histogram.name: MobileDownload.DuplicateInfobar
histogram.summary: Android: Records various counts related to the duplicate download infobar.

histogram.name: MobileDownload.FirstBackground.InterruptionCount
histogram.summary: Android: Records the number of interruptions for the first download started in background after it reaches a terminal state.

histogram.name: MobileDownload.FirstBackground.Reason
histogram.summary: Android: Records all the interrupt reasons for the first download started in background after every browser process launch. We only record the first download so that we can compare the difference between service manager only mode and full browser process mode.

histogram.name: MobileDownload.FirstBackground.StartedReason
histogram.summary: Android: After every browser process launch, records the interrupt reason of the first background download when it is started. This interrupt reason allows us to know whether download was interrupted due to browser process crash in an earlier attempt.

histogram.name: MobileDownload.InterceptFailureReason
histogram.obsolete: Removed 09/2016 in Issue 647755 with all downloads going through Chrome.
histogram.summary: Android: Records the reason that Chrome fails to intercept a download and pass it to the Android DownloadManager.

histogram.name: MobileDownload.InterruptedDownloadsRemovedFromHistory
histogram.summary: Android: Records the number of interrupted download that are cleaned up from the history, after loading all the downloads from the history DB on startup.

histogram.name: MobileDownload.InterruptionsCount
histogram.summary: Android: Records the total interruptions for a download by completion status.

histogram.name: MobileDownload.Location.Dialog.DirectoryType
histogram.summary: Records the directory type when the user selects the download directory through download location dialog. May be recorded even when the user didn't download anything.

histogram.name: MobileDownload.Location.Dialog.Result
histogram.summary: Records whether the user accepted or dismissed the dialog to select a download location.

histogram.name: MobileDownload.Location.Dialog.Type
histogram.summary: Records the download location dialog type when the dialog is shown to the user.

histogram.name: MobileDownload.Location.DirectoryType
histogram.summary: Records the directory type shown when a user opens download location dialog or download location setting. This is recorded when the directory provider generates a list of available directories.

histogram.name: MobileDownload.Location.Download.DirectoryType
histogram.summary: Records the directory type when download is completed.

histogram.name: MobileDownload.Location.Setting.DirectoryType
histogram.summary: Records the directory type when the user selects the download directory through download preference. May be recorded even when the user didn't download anything.

histogram.name: MobileDownload.Notification.FixingSummaryLeak
histogram.obsolete: Removed 10/2017 in Issue 722320 with the removal of DownloadNotificationService.hideDanglingSummaryNotification.
histogram.summary: Android: Records the situation where we try to fix a standalone downloads summary notification, which shouldn't be visible to the user. True if the notification was a foreground notification, in which case we can't dismiss it and need to attempt a more drastic workaround. False if it was a normal notification and we can dismiss it easily (this is okay and expected behavior in some scenarios).

histogram.name: MobileDownload.ResumptionsCount
histogram.summary: Android: Records the number of resumptions for a download by resumption type. For all resumption types, this is recorded when a download is finished, completed or cancelled. For automatic resumptions, it is also recorded when user manually resumes the download to clear the auto retry count.

histogram.name: MobileDownload.StoragePermission
histogram.summary: Android: Records various counts when requesting the storage permission.

histogram.name: MobileFre.PrivacyLinkTappedStatus
histogram.obsolete: Removed 04/2020 because the Privacy Notice is removed from FRE. (See crbug.com/1065912)
histogram.summary: iOS: Records the status of the privacy page after user taps on the privacy link on the first run welcome view. This metric is only recorded if and after the TOS are accepted. This metric is specific to iOS.

histogram.name: MobileFre.Progress
histogram.summary: Android: Records which states of the "first run experience" have been reached. Each bucket represents a state and recorded everytime the state changed. Pages can be double counted if the user goes backwards in the flow.

histogram.name: MobileFre.SignInChoice
histogram.summary: Android: How the user left the sign-in part of the "first run experience" activity (M-38+).

histogram.name: MobileFullscreenVideo.DurationAfterPotraitRotation
histogram.summary: Android: Records the duration that a fullscreen video is played after device rotates from portrait to landscape mode for the first time, and before it exits fullscreen. If there is no device rotation or if the video starts with landscape mode, it is not recorded. If there are mutiple rotations between portrait and landscape mode, only one record is emitted and it is equal to the time period from the first rotation to the moment when the video exits fullscreen.

histogram.name: MobileFullscreenVideo.LandscapeDuration
histogram.summary: Android: Records the duration that a fullscreen video is played in landscape mode. If a video starts playing in landscape mode, and then it is switched back and forth between landscape and portrait mode, only the time period before the first switch is accounted. If a video starts playing in portrait mode, it is not recorded.

histogram.name: MobileFullscreenVideo.LandscapeRotation
histogram.summary: Android: Records whether a fullscreen video is switched from landscape to portrait mode at any point during playback.

histogram.name: MobileFullscreenVideo.OrientationPortrait
histogram.summary: Android: Records the device orientation when a video enters fullscreen. The value is true if device orientation is portrait, or false otherwise. The video doesn't necessarily needs to be in a playing state.

histogram.name: MobileFullscreenVideo.PortraitDuration
histogram.summary: Android: Records the duration that a fullscreen video is played in portrait mode. If a video starts playing in portrait mode, and then it is switched back and forth between landscape and portrait mode, only the time period before the first switch is accounted. If a video starts playing in landscape mode, it is not recorded.

histogram.name: MobileFullscreenVideo.PortraitRotation
histogram.summary: Android: Records whether a fullscreen video is switched from portrait to landscape mode at any point during playback.

histogram.name: MobileFullscreenVideo.VideoPortrait
histogram.summary: Android: Records whether a video has a larger height than width when it enters the fullscreen mode.

histogram.name: MobileIntent.PageLoadDueToExternalApp
histogram.summary: Android: Count of page loads started by intents from external apps.

histogram.name: MobileOmnibox.LongPressPasteAge
histogram.summary:
Recorded when user long presses and pastes clipboard content into omnibox. The value indicates the estimated age of the clipboard.
Intended to be compared to Omnibox.ClipboardSuggestionShownAge.

histogram.name: MobileOmnibox.PopupOpenDuration
histogram.summary: Recorded when the omnibox popup is closed. Indicates the duration it was open.

histogram.name: MobileOmnibox.PressedClipboardSuggestionAge
histogram.summary:
When a user presses an omnibox suggestion based on the content of the clipboard, this histograms records how long ago Chrome detected a change in the clipboard.
Intended to be compared to Omnibox.ClipboardSuggestionShownAge.
If Chrome never detected a change to the clipboard, no clipboard suggestion is shown, meaning this histogram will never be emitted to.

histogram.name: MobileSignInPromo.BookmarkManager.ImpressionsTilDismiss
histogram.summary: Counts how many times the signin promo is implicitly dismissed (by closing the bookmark manager) per impression.

histogram.name: MobileSignInPromo.BookmarkManager.ImpressionsTilSigninButtons
histogram.summary: Counts how many times one of the "sign in" buttons (any of the signed-out "Sign in to Chrome" button, the "Continue as |name|" button, or the "Not |email|?" button) is clicked per impression.

histogram.name: MobileSignInPromo.BookmarkManager.ImpressionsTilXButton
histogram.summary: Counts how many times the explicit "X"-to-close button is clicked per impression.

histogram.name: MobileSignInPromo.SettingsManager.ImpressionsTilDismiss
histogram.summary: Counts how many times the signin promo is implicitly dismissed (by closing the settings view) per impression.

histogram.name: MobileSignInPromo.SettingsManager.ImpressionsTilSigninButtons
histogram.summary: Counts how many times one of the "sign in" buttons (any of the signed-out "Sign in to Chrome" button, the "Continue as |name|" button, or the "Not |email|?" button) is clicked per impression.

histogram.name: MobileSignInPromo.SettingsManager.ImpressionsTilXButton
histogram.summary: Counts how many times the explicit "X"-to-close button is clicked per impression.

histogram.name: MobileStartup.ColdStartupIntent
histogram.summary: For cold starts of Chrome (native not initialized at the time of intent), record in ChromeTabbedActivity whether the intent had the effect of opening a new page or was a restore of last session.

histogram.name: MobileStartup.DailyLaunchCount
histogram.summary: The count of launching Chrome mobile app within a day.

histogram.name: MobileStartup.IntentToCreationTime
histogram.summary: Android: The time it takes from creating an intent for ChromeActivity to activity creation. This includes activity creation time spent in the framework.

histogram.name: MobileStartup.LaunchType
histogram.summary: The type of launching Chrome mobile app, e.g launch by hitting icon.

histogram.name: MobileStartup.LoadedHomepageOnColdStart
histogram.obsolete: Deprecated and removed from the code 02/2020
histogram.summary: Android: Tracks whether a homepage was loaded on startup of ChromeTabbedActivity. Launching a homepage is conditional on a homepage being configured on the device and the absence of any existing tabs, so it will only happen on a subsets of cold starts. The metric splits out whether the homepage was the NTP or some other URL.

histogram.name: MobileStartup.MainIntentAction
histogram.obsolete: Removed and removed from the code 05/2018
histogram.summary: Android: Records the first action the user does within 10s of starting Chrome. Recorded in DocumentActivity and ChromeTabbedActivity only when the starting intent is of type MAIN.

histogram.name: MobileStartup.MobileMultiWindowInstances
histogram.summary:
Android: Number of instances of Chrome currently open during a MultiWindow session. Emitted every time Chrome is paused. Only emitted on Android MultiWindow devices.
A MultiWindow session is any period of time that Chrome was not used in a full screen mode but together with another Activity that is visible at the same time. This is only supported in a few Android models.

histogram.name: MobileStartup.MobileMultiWindowSession
histogram.summary:
Android: percent of the screen available for Chrome during a multi-window session. Emitted every time chrome is paused. Only emitted on Android MultiWindow devices.
A multiwindow session is any period of time that Chrome was not used in full screen mode but together with some other application that is visible at the same time. This is only supported in a few Android models.

histogram.name: MobileStartup.NonMainIntentAction
histogram.obsolete: Removed and removed from the code 05/2018
histogram.summary: Android: Records the first action the user does within 10s of starting Chrome. Recorded in DocumentActivity and ChromeTabbedActivity only when the starting intent is not of type MAIN, e.g. VIEW, etc.

histogram.name: MobileStartup.TimeSinceLastUse
histogram.obsolete: Removed and removed from the code 05/2018
histogram.summary:
Android: The time since last use until Chrome is launched from the home screen. This is measured from the time the last tab is closed until a Main intent is received. Has a minute level precision for first 10 minutes increasing exponentially till 30 days.
Note: this metric is broken in M63 and below and only reliable in M64+.

histogram.name: MobileStartup.ToolbarFirstDrawTime
histogram.obsolete: Removed and renamed to MobileStartup.ToolbarFirstDrawTime2 due to double reporting bug (see https://crbug.com/857508).
histogram.summary: Android: The time it takes from launch to the completion of first draw for Toolbar. This excludes activity creation time spent in framework.

histogram.name: MobileStartup.ToolbarFirstDrawTime2
histogram.summary: Android: The time it takes from launch to the completion of first draw for Toolbar. This excludes activity creation time spent in framework.

histogram.name: MobileStartup.ToolbarFirstFocusStartupState
histogram.summary: Android: The state of chrome startup at the first time the user focuses the omnibox, if the user does so within the first 30s of the launch (excluding activity creation time spent in the framework).

histogram.name: MobileStartup.ToolbarFirstFocusTime
histogram.obsolete: Removed and renamed to MobileStartup.ToolbarFirstFocusTime2 due to double reporting bug (see https://crbug.com/857508).
histogram.summary: Android: The time it takes from launch to the first time the user focuses the omnibox if the user does so within first 30s of the launch. This excludes activity creation time spent in framework.

histogram.name: MobileStartup.ToolbarFirstFocusTime2
histogram.summary: Android: The time it takes from launch to the first time the user focuses the omnibox if the user does so within first 30s of the launch. This excludes activity creation time spent in framework.

histogram.name: MobileStartup.ToolbarInflationTime
histogram.summary: Android: The time spent performing toolbar layout inflation.

histogram.name: ModuleBlacklistCache.BlacklistPathUpdated
histogram.summary: Records the result of writing the path of the module blacklist cache to the registry so that chrome_elf.dll can use it. A value is emitted every time the cache is updated because another instance of Chrome with a different User Data directory could have overwritten the path to point to their own version of the cache.

histogram.name: ModuleBlacklistCache.BlacklistStatus
histogram.summary: The status of a blacklisted module in the process. Recorded when the cache is updated.

histogram.name: ModuleBlacklistCache.DeleteResult
histogram.summary: The result of deleting the module blacklist cache when third-party module blocking is disabled. Attempting to delete an empty file is considered a success.

histogram.name: ModuleBlacklistCache.ExpectedMD5Digest
histogram.summary: Records whether the MD5 digest of the module blacklist cache matched the one recorded in the Local State file.

histogram.name: ModuleBlacklistCache.ModuleCount
histogram.summary: Records the number of modules in the module blacklist cache at the time it was written to disk.

histogram.name: ModuleBlacklistCache.ReadResult
histogram.summary: The result of reading the current module blacklist cache while updating it.

histogram.name: ModuleBlacklistCache.WriteResult
histogram.summary: The result of writing the updated module blacklist cache.

histogram.name: ModuleIntegrityVerification.BytesModified
histogram.summary: Represents the amount of bytes in a module that are different on disk and in memory. Logged when the ModuleIntegrityVerifier determines that a module is different on disk and in memory after being triggered by an incident.

histogram.name: ModuleIntegrityVerification.Difference
histogram.obsolete: Removed and removed from code as of 05/2015.
histogram.summary: Represents the difference in bytes deemed modified by the two ModuleIntegrityVerifier code paths. The suffix indicates which path had a higher value. Logged when the ModuleIntegrityVerifier determines that a module is different on disk and in memory after being triggered by an incident and the reported byte count for both methods is different.

histogram.name: ModuleIntegrityVerification.RelocationsUnordered
histogram.summary: Logged when the relocations in a module are not ordered causing the module verification to abort.

histogram.name: ModuleIntegrityVerification.RelocationsUnorderedModuleIndex
histogram.summary: Logged when the relocations in a module are not ordered causing the module verification to abort. The value is the index of the affected module.

histogram.name: Mojo.Connector.MaxUnreadMessageQuotaUsed
histogram.summary: The maximal unread message quota used for the lifetime of a Connector. This is sampled for a configurable percentage of Connectors only when the feature MojoRecordUnreadMessageCount is enabled. By default 1% of Connectors are sampled, as there's some overhead involved in enabling the unread message quota on a MessagePipe. See //mojo/public/cpp/bindings/lib/connector.cc.

histogram.name: Mojo.MachPortRelay.BrokerError
histogram.obsolete: The MachPortRelay was deleted in July 2019.
histogram.summary: Errors that the broker process encounters while trying to send or receive Mach ports from a child process.

histogram.name: Mojo.MachPortRelay.ChildError
histogram.obsolete: The MachPortRelay was deleted in July 2019.
histogram.summary: Errors that a child process encounters while trying to receive Mach ports from the broker process.

histogram.name: Mojo.Shell.ChildConnectionTime
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2/2018. Has not actually been collected for some time.
histogram.summary: Measures the time it takes for a new child process to receive an initialization request from the Mojo shell. This is precisely the duration of the MojoShellConnectionImpl::BindToMessagePipe() call in ChildThreadImpl.

histogram.name: Mojo.System.GetParentPlatformHandleSyncTime
