If the clipboard content was the same during a previous run of Chrome and this clipboard content was suggested during that run, those impressions are not counted. Also, if the clipboard content changes during a particular run of Chrome to other content, the omnibox is focused and that clipboard content is suggested, then content changes back and Chrome starts suggesting the older clipboard content again, the counts start again from scratch. Chrome only remembers the number of times the clipboard content was shown consecutively.
This value is useful to compare with the number of times a clipboard suggestion has been shown when it is clicked. This value can be obtained from OmniboxEvent records in which the selected suggestion is from Clipboard provider. In those cases, look in the Clipboard provider's ProviderInfo field for |times_returned_results_in_session|. Note that at the time of this writing that OmniboxEvent logs aren't recorded in incognito whereas histograms are. Thus, the total counts will not be comparable, though the distributions should be.

histogram.name: Omnibox.ClipboardSuggestionShownWithCurrentURL
histogram.summary:
Recorded every time the omnibox is focussed and a recent content from the user's clipboard is suggested. The value indicates whether the current URL was shown (which would appear above the clipboard suggestion) or was absent (which ought to only happen when the omnibox is empty / the user is on the NTP).
On Android, the total count for this histogram can be usefully compared with the count of the user action FocusLocation in order to determine the rate at which a clipboard suggestion is shown in the omnibox. To determine the same rate on iOS, one needs to compare this histogram's count with the sum of the user actions MobileFocusedOmniboxOnNtp, MobileFocusedFakeboxOnNtp, and MobileFocusedOmniboxNotOnNtp.
Furthermore, on either platform, this histogram's count can be usefully compared with the count in the clipboard bucket of the Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.Provider histogram to determine the clickthrough rate on these suggestions.

histogram.name: Omnibox.CutOrCopyAllText
histogram.summary: The number of cut or copy commands on all selected text in the omnibox. Gathered on desktop platforms (Win, Mac, Linux, Chrome OS).

histogram.name: Omnibox.DocumentSuggest.Requests
histogram.summary: Counts the number of document suggest requests the omnibox sent, were invalidated, and were completed successfully.

histogram.name: Omnibox.DocumentSuggest.ResultCount
histogram.summary: Number of results returned in each document suggestion reply. Logged for successful requests where the provider returned a parseable result set.

histogram.name: Omnibox.EditUrlSuggestionAction
histogram.summary: The action performed on the edit-URL omnibox suggestion that contains the "what you typed"/current URL. If visible, this is the first suggestion.

histogram.name: Omnibox.ElisionConfig
histogram.summary: The state of URL elision in the omnibox, e.g. URL default behavior, prevented by context menu option, or prevented by Chrome extension. This histogram is recorded the first time ShouldPreventElision is called on ChromeLocationBarModelDelegate with an existing profile. This occurs every time a browser window is open, including when opening a new profile or moving a tab to a new window. This metric measures the percentage of browser windows that use each type of elision behavior.

histogram.name: Omnibox.EnteredKeywordMode
histogram.summary: The number of times users enter keyword hint mode "Search ___ for:" and how.

histogram.name: Omnibox.EnteredKeywordMode2
histogram.summary: The new enumeration of how many times users enter keyword hint mode "Search ___ for:" and how. Note that we don't recognize the changing of the keyword itself as entering keyword mode, if the user never left keyword mode e.g. if the user was arrowing around the suggestions, so we'll preserve the original keyword mode entry method in this case.

histogram.name: Omnibox.FocusToEditTime
histogram.summary: The length of time between when a user focused on the omnibox and first modifies the omnibox.

histogram.name: Omnibox.FocusToOpenTime
histogram.summary: The length of time between when a user focused on the omnibox and opened an omnibox match (which could be what they typed or a suggestion).

histogram.name: Omnibox.FocusToOpenTimeAnyPopupState
histogram.summary: The length of time between when a user focused on the omnibox and opened an omnibox match (which could be what they typed or a suggestion). This is recorded regardless of whether the omnibox dropdown (a.k.a. popup) is open.

histogram.name: Omnibox.FocusToOpenTimeAnyPopupState2
histogram.obsolete: Replaced with Omnibox.FocusToOpenTimeAnyPopupState3 in January, 2017, which only differs in the bucketing and range.
histogram.summary:
The length of time between when a user focused on the omnibox and opened an omnibox match (which could be what they typed or a suggestion). This is recorded regardless of whether the omnibox dropdown (a.k.a. popup) is open. It is not recorded if a match is opened without triggering a focus event, e.g., when a user drags a URL to the omnibox to navigate.
To know how common this last condition is, compare the total count of this histogram to the total number of omnibox events.
Android started logging this in M76.

histogram.name: Omnibox.FocusToOpenTimeAnyPopupState3
histogram.summary:
The length of time between when a user focused on the omnibox and opened an omnibox match (which could be what they typed or a suggestion). This is recorded regardless of whether the omnibox dropdown (a.k.a. popup) is open. It is not recorded if a match is opened without triggering a focus event, e.g., when a user drags a URL to the omnibox to navigate.
To know how common this last condition is, compare the total count of this histogram to the total number of omnibox events.

histogram.name: Omnibox.HardwareKeyboardModeEnabled
histogram.summary: iOS: Records whether a hardware keyboard is used to input text. This is recorded each time a UIKeyboardWillChangeFrameNotification is sent while editing the omnibox text.

histogram.name: Omnibox.HasLegalDefaultMatchWithoutCompletion
histogram.summary: Whether there was at least one legal default match without an |inline_autocompletion|. Recorded every time AutocompleteResult::SortAndCull() is called, which could happen multiple times on each keystroke.

histogram.name: Omnibox.HistoryQuickHistoryIDSetFromWords
histogram.summary: Times URLIndexPrivateData::HistoryIDSetFromWords(), which is called by the omnibox's HistoryQuick provider. URLIndexPrivateData::HistoryIDSetFromWords() can be called multiple times per keystroke due to, for example, the cursor being in the middle of the input string or SearchProvider's calls to Classify().

histogram.name: Omnibox.IconOrFaviconShown
histogram.summary:
Records suggestions decorated with specific icon or favicon at the time the user exited the omnibox. Exiting the omnibox includes navigating (to entered text or any suggestion), pressing the system back key, clearing omnibox, blanking screen / locking the phone (whether intentionally or due to inactivity), or closing the Chrome app. This metric is logged every time the omnibox is exited, including when no answer is present in the list of suggestions.
This histogram is related to Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.IconOrFaviconType.

histogram.name: Omnibox.InputType
histogram.summary:
The kind of input the user provided when using the omnibox to go somewhere. The type can be misleading. For example if the user typed 'http:/', it gets marked as a query because it cannot be opened as a URL even though the user probably wanted and selected a URL from the list of suggestions.
In M-74 and earlier, this was only recorded if the user had no incognito windows open. In M-75 and later, it records all omnibox interactions regardless, in order to be consistent with how user actions and histograms are handled.

histogram.name: Omnibox.IsPasteAndGo
histogram.summary:
Whether an omnibox interaction is a paste-and-search/paste-and-go action. (This histogram records both of these in the "True" bucket for this histogram because both of these are referred to as paste-and-go in the code.) These typically involve right-clicking in the omnibox and selecting that option from the dropdown.
In M-74 and earlier, this was only recorded if the user had no incognito windows open. In M-75 and later, it records all omnibox interactions regardless, in order to be consistent with how user actions and histograms are handled.

histogram.name: Omnibox.IsPopupOpen
histogram.summary:
Whether the omnibox popup (a.k.a. dropdown) is open at the time the user used the omnibox to go somewhere. It can be closed if, for instance, the user clicked in the omnibox and hit return to reload the same page. Also, because paste-and-search/paste-and-go actions ignore the current content of the omnibox dropdown (if it is open) when they happen, we pretend the dropdown is closed when logging these.
In M-74 and earlier, this was only recorded if the user had no incognito windows open. In M-75 and later, it records all omnibox interactions regardless, in order to be consistent with how user actions and histograms are handled.

histogram.name: Omnibox.JustDeletedText
histogram.summary:
Whether the user deleted text immediately before selecting an omnibox suggestion. This is usually the result of pressing backspace or delete.
In M-74 and earlier, this was only recorded if the user had no incognito windows open. In M-75 and later, it records all omnibox interactions regardless, in order to be consistent with how user actions and histograms are handled.

histogram.name: Omnibox.LocalHistoryZeroSuggest.AsyncDeleteTime
histogram.summary: The length of time it takes for the corresponding URLs that would produce a deleted match to be queried asynchronously and deleted. Triggered when user deletes a local history zero-prefix suggestion.

histogram.name: Omnibox.LocalHistoryZeroSuggest.MaxMatchesCount
histogram.summary: The maximum number of matches returned by the provider. Triggered when zero-prefix suggestions are enabled and requested as a result of user focus into the Omnibox or the Realbox on the NTP.

histogram.name: Omnibox.LocalHistoryZeroSuggest.SearchTermsSeenCount
histogram.summary: The number of search terms examined until the maximum possible matches are fulfilled. Used to determine the upper bound of search terms to query from the keyword search terms database. Triggered when zero-prefix suggestions are enabled and requested as a result of user focus into the Omnibox or the Realbox on the NTP.

histogram.name: Omnibox.LocalHistoryZeroSuggest.SyncDeleteTime
histogram.summary: The length of time it takes to handle the deletion request for a match and to update the list of provider matches synchronously. Triggered when user requests to delete a local history zero-prefix suggestion.

histogram.name: Omnibox.MatchStability.AsyncMatchChange
histogram.summary:
This histogram was buggy and the data should be disregarded.
The original intent was to count asynchronous match updates for each position. The implementation had a bug, and it overcounted.

histogram.name: Omnibox.MatchStability.AsyncMatchChange2
histogram.summary:
This tracks how unstable our matches are due to asynchronous updates. Whenever a dropdown position gets asynchronously set to a new match that's different from the old match, this histogram logs the index.
We primarily care the default match, which is logged at index 0. All indices matter though, so we track the other indices too. When the number of matches changes asynchronously, matches that are removed are considered a "change" and logged here. Matches that are appended are less disruptive to the user, and are not logged.
This is because this metric is primarily interested in how many times the user could be looking at a match with the intention of selecting it, and then it surprisingly goes away.
Note that merely adding a new asynchronous provider will increase these counts. That's intentional. Swapping matches out annoys users, and to avoid this, we should do some things like: caching, update coaslescing, update deferral until next keystroke, provider timeouts, etc.
This metric is designed to be normalized by the True count of Omnibox.Start.WantAsyncMatches, which will yield the number of match changes asynchronously per keystroke or other user gesture.

histogram.name: Omnibox.MatchStability.AsyncMatchChangedInAnyPosition
histogram.summary:
This tracks how unstable our matches are due to asynchronous updates.
For each asynchronous autocomplete update, this histogram logs whether any match in any position has changed. Note: if the only change is new matches appended to the end of the list, that does not count as a change, since it's not very disruptive to the user.
This metric is designed to be normalized by the True count of Omnibox.Start.WantAsyncMatches, which will yield the number of times any match changes asynchronously per keystroke or other user gesture.
This histogram can be considered a boolean analogue to Omnibox.MatchStability.AsyncMatchChange2.

histogram.name: Omnibox.NumEvents
histogram.summary:
The number of times users used the omnibox to go somewhere.
In M-74 and earlier, this was only recorded if the user had no incognito windows open. In M-75 and later, it records all omnibox interactions regardless, in order to be consistent with how user actions and histograms are handled.

histogram.name: Omnibox.NumTypedTerms
histogram.summary:
The number of terms in the text the user entered in the omnibox when they used the omnibox to go somewhere. Terms are defined by splitting on whitespace. All values larger than 6 are recorded in bucket 6.
In M-74 and earlier, this was only recorded if the user had no incognito windows open. In M-75 and later, it records all omnibox interactions regardless, in order to be consistent with how user actions and histograms are handled.

histogram.name: Omnibox.OnDeviceHeadSuggest.AsyncQueryTime
histogram.summary: Time it takes for omnibox on device head provider to fulfill the asynchronous suggest request.

histogram.name: Omnibox.OnDeviceHeadSuggest.ResultCount
histogram.summary: The number of results returned by the on device head model which matches the user input. Note all numbers greater than 5 are recorded in bucket 5.

histogram.name: Omnibox.PaintTime
histogram.summary: Records the time to paint the omnibox contents. This is a subcomponent of Omnibox.CharTypedToRepaintLatency. Implemented on desktop platforms.

histogram.name: Omnibox.Paste
histogram.summary: The number of paste commands on the text in the omnibox. Reported every time a paste command is done.

histogram.name: Omnibox.PasteAndGo
histogram.summary: The number of paste-and-go commands on the text in the omnibox. Reported every time a paste-and-go command is done.

histogram.name: Omnibox.PhysicalWebProvider.SuggestionUsedWithoutOmniboxFocus
histogram.summary: Records a boolean value indicating whether the Physical Web provider was invoked by focusing the omnibox during the current omnibox session. This is unexpected; it's probably a bug somwhere. Recorded when the user selects an omnibox suggestion. Does not record when the Physical Web omnibox provider is disabled.

histogram.name: Omnibox.PhysicalWebProviderMatches
histogram.summary: The number of matches returned by PhysicalWebProvider. Emitted when the omnibox is first focused, unless the user is in incognito mode. Capped at 10.

histogram.name: Omnibox.ProgressBarBreakPointUpdateCount
histogram.summary: The number of progress bar break point updates from page load started to page load finished.

histogram.name: Omnibox.ProgressBarUpdateCount
histogram.summary: The number of progress bar frame updates from page load started to page load finished. If there is no animation, this matches Omnibox.ProgressBarBreakPointUpdateCount. Note that there can be additional updates after page load finished, animating to 100%, but this histogram doesn't include them.

histogram.name: Omnibox.ProviderTime
histogram.summary: The length of time taken by the named provider"s synchronous pass.

histogram.name: Omnibox.ProviderTime2
histogram.summary: The length of time taken by the named provider"s synchronous pass.

histogram.name: Omnibox.QueryBookmarksTime
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Time the HistoryContentProvider takes to perform a bookmark search.

histogram.name: Omnibox.QueryGeolocationAcquisitionTime
histogram.summary: The elapsed time to acquire the location sent in the X-Geo header for an Omnibox query.

histogram.name: Omnibox.QueryGeolocationHorizontalAccuracy
histogram.summary: The estimated horizontal accuracy of the location sent in the X-Geo header for an Omnibox query.

histogram.name: Omnibox.QueryIosLocationAuthorizationStatus
histogram.summary: For iOS, whether the application is authorized to use location services when the user enters a search query into the Omnibox.

histogram.name: Omnibox.QueryTime
histogram.summary: Time it takes for the omnibox to become responsive to user input after the user has typed N characters. This measures the time it takes to start all the asynchronous autocomplete providers (but not wait for them to finish).

histogram.name: Omnibox.QueryTime2
histogram.summary: Time it takes for the omnibox to become responsive to user input after the user has typed N characters. This measures the time it takes to start all autocomplete providers (but not wait for the asynchronous ones to finish).

histogram.name: Omnibox.RichEntity.DecorationType
histogram.summary:
Records, for each entity suggestion (people, music, ...) whether it was decorated with image / color / icon at the time the user exited the omnibox. Exiting the omnibox includes navigation (url or query), pressing back key, locking the phone, clearing the omnibox, closing Chrome, or just screen blacking out due to prolonged inactivity.
This metric is currently only recorded on Android, though it could be added to the other platforms.

histogram.name: Omnibox.RichEntityShown
histogram.summary:
Records number of presented Rich Entity suggestions at the time the user exited the omnibox. Exiting the omnibox includes navigating (to entered text or any suggestion), pressing the system back key, clearing omnibox, blanking screen / locking the phone (whether intentionally or due to inactivity), or closing the Chrome app. This metric is logged every time the omnibox is exited, including when no entities are present in the list of suggestions.
Nota bene: in some circumstances this histogram is double counting on purpose. Scenarios cover repetitive actions that expose same entity suggestions, ie. leave-enter-leave application, or leave-enter-clear where no new suggestion fetches are done.
This histogram is related to Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.RichEntity.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SaveStateForTabSwitch.UserInputInProgress
histogram.summary: When a user switches tabs, whether the omnibox had an edit in progress.

histogram.name: Omnibox.Search.OffTheRecord
histogram.summary: This histogram records the number of searches done from omnibox using default search engine on desktop and android devices, sliced based on being in regular mode or private modes.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SearchEngine
histogram.obsolete: Made obsolete around Chrome 32. Use Omnibox.SearchEngineType instead.
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The id of search engine that was used for search in omnibox. See src/chrome/browser/search_engines/template_url_prepopulate_data.cc for more info.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SearchEngineType
histogram.summary: The type of search engine associated with a match opened from the omnibox.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SearchProvider.AddHistoryResultsTime
histogram.summary: Time it takes to add all the raw history results to the list of matches.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SearchProvider.ConvertResultsTime
histogram.summary: Time it takes to convert all the results to matches and add them to a map, to keep the most relevant match for each result.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SearchProvider.GetMostRecentKeywordTermsDefaultProviderTime
histogram.summary: Time it takes for the omnibox to search the previous query history database for queries that start with the omnibox text.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SearchProviderMatches
histogram.summary: The number of matches returned by SearchProvider. Emitted on every call to SearchProvider::Start(), which effectively means every key stroke in the omnibox.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SelectedPosition
histogram.summary:
The index of the item that the user selected in the omnibox popup (a.k.a. dropdown) list. 0 means the inline suggestion shown within the omnibox. This is also the same suggestion shown as the top item in the dropdown. The second item in the dropdown will be recorded as bucket 1. The selected position is always set to 0 when the popup is closed at the time of navigation or if the user did a paste-and-search or paste-and-go action.
In M-74 and earlier, this was only recorded if the user had no incognito windows open. In M-75 and later, it records all omnibox interactions regardless, in order to be consistent with how user actions and histograms are handled.

histogram.name: Omnibox.ShowFullUrlsEnabled
histogram.summary: Tracks whether the context menu option to prevent URL elisions in the omnibox is enabled. Recorded when the pref is toggled.

histogram.name: Omnibox.Start.WantAsyncMatches
histogram.summary:
Whether asynchronous matches are requested. Recorded every time AutocompleteController::Start() is called.
False counts roughly correspond to non-interactive invocations of AutocompleteController - like when we are classifying user text as a Search or URL.
True counts roughly correspond to keystrokes or on-focus events, and can be used as a normalizer for per-user-gesture metrics.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.AnswerInSuggest
histogram.summary:
Counts how often omnibox suggestions are used, and in the case an Answer suggestion was selected - records the type of an answer.
This histogram is related to Omnibox.AnswerInSuggestShown.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.IconOrFaviconType
histogram.summary:
Counts how often omnibox suggestions are used, and in the case a regular search / url (not an Answer, Entity or any other specialized) suggestion was selected - records how the suggestion was decorated.
This histogram is related to Omnibox.IconOrFaviconShown.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.NearbyURLCount
histogram.obsolete: Removed in M58, replaced with Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.NearbyURLCount.AtFocus and Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.NearbyURLCount.AtMatchCreation.
histogram.summary:
The number of nearby Physical Web URLs when the user focused the omnibox. Recorded when the user accepts an omnibox suggestion, regardless of whether the suggestion came from PhysicalWebProvider. Capped at 50.
In M57, the values recorded by this histogram were determined to be bugged. It's believed that it was recording uninitialized values due to a corner case on Android that allows an omnibox suggestion to be selected without first focusing the omnibox. In addition to the new histograms, Omnibox.PhysicalWebProvider.SuggestionUsedWithoutOmniboxFocus was added to detect this case. https://crbug.com/691059

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.NearbyURLCount.AtFocus
histogram.summary:
The number of nearby Physical Web URLs when Physical Web Provider was last focused (i.e., the start of the current session). Recorded when the user accepts an omnibox suggestion, regardless of whether the suggestion came from PhysicalWebProvider. Capped at 50.
Does not record if the omnibox was not focused during the current session. Omnibox.PhysicalWebProvider.SuggestionUsedWithoutOmniboxFocus records true when we hit this case.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.NearbyURLCount.AtMatchCreation
histogram.summary: The number of nearby Physical Web URLs when Physical Web Provider last constructed matches. Recorded when the user accepts an omnibox suggestion, regardless of whether the suggestion came from PhysicalWebProvider. Capped at 50.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.OfferedTabMatch
histogram.summary:
Measures whether the suggestion that was selected by the user offered a tab match. A tab match occurs when the URL specified by the suggestion matches the URL navigated to by another tab in the browser, within the margin of our URL stripping scheme.
This metric typically only makes sense with respect to Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.OfferedTabMatch. Only recent versions of the browser send the metric. Therefore, other metrics may include datapoints not covered by this metric (unless one narrows the data by version.)
In M-74 and earlier, this was only recorded if the user had no incognito windows open. In M-75 and later, it records all omnibox interactions regardless, in order to be consistent with how user actions and histograms are handled.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.RichEntity
histogram.summary:
Whether a Rich Entity omnibox suggestion was selected when the user used the omnibox to go somewhere.
This histogram is related to Omnibox.RichEntityShown.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.Search.Experimental.ForegroundToFirstMeaningfulPaint.Prerender
histogram.summary: Measures the time from the page first appearing in the foreground to its first meaningful paint. Only recorded on navigations that use a prerender that is to a search query suggestion selected from the omnibox.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.Search.Experimental.NavigationToFirstMeaningfulPaint
histogram.summary: Measures the time from navigation start to first meaningful paint. Only recorded for a search query suggestion selected from the omnibox.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.Search.ForegroundToFirstContentfulPaint.Prerender
histogram.summary: Measures the time from the page first appearing in the foreground to its first contentful paint. Only recorded on navigations that use a prerender that is to a search query suggestion selected from the omnibox.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.Search.NavigationToFirstContentfulPaint
histogram.summary: Measures the time from navigation start to first contentful paint. Only recorded for a search query suggestion selected from the omnibox.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.Search.NavigationToFirstForeground.Prerender
histogram.summary: Measures the time from a page being navigated to in prerender to it first showing up in foreground. Only recorded on navigations that used a prerender that was to a search query suggestion selected from the omnibox. This is only recorded on pages that experience a first contentful paint.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.SelectedTabMatch
histogram.summary:
Measures whether the suggestion that was selected by the user offered a tab match and specifically that the user chose the tab match over normal navigation. A tab match occurs when the URL specified by the suggestion matches the URL navigated to by another tab in the browser, within the margin of our URL stripping scheme. By selecting the tab match, the browser simply switches focus to the matching tab.
This metric typically only makes sense with respect to Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.OfferedTabMatch. Only recent versions of the browser send the metric. Therefore, other metrics may include datapoints not covered by this metric (unless one narrows the data by version.)
This metric should be stricly less than Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.OfferedTabMatch, and is typically used to discover the percentage of offered tab matches that the user accepts.
In M-74 and earlier, this was only recorded if the user had no incognito windows open. In M-75 and later, it records all omnibox interactions regardless, in order to be consistent with how user actions and histograms are handled.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.URL.Experimental.ForegroundToFirstMeaningfulPaint.Prerender
histogram.summary: Measures the time from the page first appearing in the foreground to its first meaningful paint. Only recorded on navigations that use a prerender that is to a URL suggestion selected from the omnibox.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.URL.Experimental.NavigationToFirstMeaningfulPaint
histogram.summary: Measures the time from navigation start to first meaningful paint. Only recorded for a URL suggestion selected from the omnibox.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.URL.ForegroundToFirstContentfulPaint.Prerender
histogram.summary: Measures the time from the page first appearing in the foreground to its first contentful paint. Only recorded on navigations that use a prerender that is to a URL suggestion selected from the omnibox.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.URL.NavigationToFirstContentfulPaint
histogram.summary: Measures the time from navigation start to first contentful paint. Only recorded for a URL suggestion selected from the omnibox.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestionUsed.URL.NavigationToFirstForeground.Prerender
histogram.summary: Measures the time from a page being navigated to in prerender to it first showing up in foreground. Only recorded on navigations that used a prerender that was to a URL suggestion selected from the omnibox. This is only recorded on pages that experience a first contentful paint.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestRequest.Failure.GoogleResponseTime
histogram.summary: The time elapsed between the sending of a suggest request to Google until the time the request was returned with status==failed. Ignores requests that were canceled before being returned.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestRequest.Success.GoogleResponseTime
histogram.summary: The time elapsed between the sending of a suggest request to Google until the time the request was returned with status==success. Ignores requests that were canceled before being returned.

histogram.name: Omnibox.SuggestRequests
histogram.summary: Counts about the number of suggest requests the omnibox sent, invalidated, and replies received.

histogram.name: Omnibox.TimeUntilFirst
histogram.summary: The amount of time that passes in ms between the user focusing the omnibox and performing some action on Android. This is only recorded the first time the user performs this action per omnibox focus event. Other Omnibox.TimeUntilFirst.* events are not recorded past the first event.

histogram.name: Omnibox.ToggleSuggestionGroupId.Off
histogram.summary: Counts the number of times a suggestion group ID is prevented form appearing in the results. This is recorded when the user hides a set of grouped suggestions, such as proactive zero-suggest, in the omnibox UI using the control in the grouped suggestions' header.

histogram.name: Omnibox.ToggleSuggestionGroupId.On
histogram.summary: Counts the number of times a suggestion group ID is permitted to appear in the results. This is recorded when the user makes a set of previously hidden grouped suggestions, such as proactive zero-suggest, visible in the omnibox UI using the control in the grouped suggestions' header.

histogram.name: Omnibox.URLNavigationScheme
histogram.summary: The scheme of the destination URL for the selected omnibox suggestion. This could be a what-you-typed suggestion (if the user fully typed a URL), an inline autocompletion, or something in the dropdown, anything as long as it's a URL navigation, not a search query. Excludes omnibox URL navigations that are effectively reloads as well as cut-and-pastes of URLs. Also does not take into account any redirects that destination URL may do before finally serving a page. In other words, it's the scheme of the first request in a possible chain.

histogram.name: Omnibox.URLNavigationTimeToRedirectToHTTPS
histogram.summary:
The amount of time, in milliseconds, between the start of a typed URL navigation in the omnibox (the user typing a URL to completion, or selecting a URL from the inline autocomplete or dropdown) and the start of a redirect upgrading the URL to HTTPS. This is only recorded when the upgraded URL is the same except for the scheme and the addition/removal of a "www." prefix.
To calculate the percentage of HTTP URL navigations that have been upgraded in this way, divide the count of this histogram by the count for HTTP in Omnibox.URLNavigationScheme.

histogram.name: Omnibox.UserTextCleared
histogram.summary:
Counts the number of times that the user text is cleared. IME users are sometimes in the situation that IME was unintentionally turned on and failed to input latin alphabets (ASCII characters) or the opposite case. In that case, users may delete all the text and the user text gets cleared. This histogram helps us estimate how often this scenario happens.
Note that since we don't currently correlate "text cleared" events with IME usage, this also captures many other cases where users clear the text; though it explicitly doesn't log deleting all the permanent text as the first action of an editing sequence (see comments in OnAfterPossibleChange()).

histogram.name: Omnibox.WarmupTime
histogram.summary: Time it takes for the omnibox to process the first user interaction after startup. This measures the time it takes to start all autocomplete providers and their initialisation steps. This metric may be recorded twice as there is the normal omnibox path and also the "classify text" path to the autocomplete system.

histogram.name: Omnibox.ZeroSuggest.Eligible.OnFocus
histogram.obsolete: Removed 05/2019 because histogram was not recorded in every circumstance. Superseded by Omnibox.ZeroSuggest.Eligible.OnFocusV2.
histogram.summary:
Whether the user has settings configured so that the current page URL can be sent to the suggest server to request contextual suggestions. For example, this is only supported for users who have Google as their default search engine (unmodified version of Google), have search suggest enabled, are signed-in and syncing without a custom passphrase, and don't have an incognito window open. There are other criteria too. Recorded on focus in the omnibox if there is default search provider and we've constructed a suggest URL.
Some additional guidelines: if an incognito window is open, all focus events will go into the "generally ineligible" bucket. Likewise, if the current page is a search results page, we don't allow contextual suggestions either so focus events on those pages go in the "generally ineligible" bucket. The difference between "eligible" and "generally eligible but not this time" depends only the properties of the current URL.
Recorded regardless of whether contextual or non-contextual zero suggest is currently enabled on the user's platform. However, if zero suggest (in all forms) is entirely disabled, the user will be perpetually ineligible.

histogram.name: Omnibox.ZeroSuggest.Eligible.OnFocusV2
histogram.summary:
Whether the user has settings configured so that the current page URL can be sent to the suggest server to request contextual suggestions. For example, this is only supported for users who have Google as their default search engine (unmodified version of Google), have search suggest enabled, are signed-in and syncing without a custom passphrase, and don't have an incognito window open. There are other criteria too. Recorded on focus in the omnibox if there is default search provider and we've constructed a suggest URL.
Some additional guidelines: if an incognito window is open, all focus events will go into the "generally ineligible" bucket. Likewise, if the current page is a search results page, we don't allow contextual suggestions either so focus events on those pages go in the "generally ineligible" bucket. The difference between "eligible" and "generally eligible but not this time" depends only the properties of the current URL.
Recorded regardless of whether contextual or non-contextual zero suggest is currently enabled on the user's platform. However, if zero suggest (in all forms) is entirely disabled, the user will be perpetually ineligible.
This histogram captures all scenarios that are generally ineligible for zero suggest suggestions (including incognito mode), and reports eligibility for suggestions even if kOmniboxPopupShortcutIconsInZeroState is enabled (meaning: suggestions are not shown, but current URL is eligible).

histogram.name: Omnibox.ZeroSuggest.Eligible.OnProfileOpen
histogram.summary:
Whether the user has settings configured so that the current page URL could be sent to the suggest server to request contextual suggestions. For example, this is only supported for users who have Google as their default search engine (unmodified version of Google), have search suggest enabled, and are signed-in and syncing without a custom passphrase. There are other criteria too. Recorded on profile open. Note that opening an incognito window (if none are currently open under the given profile) counts as opening a new profile.
Some additional guidelines: unlike Omnibox.ZeroSuggest.Eligible.OnFocus, because this is recorded on profile open, users cannot be declared ineligible because they have an incognito window open (it's impossible to have an incognito window open for a given profile at the time of profile open) and also cannot be declared ineligible because the user is viewing a search results page. (We test this on-profile-open using an arbitrary URL.)
Recorded regardless of whether contextual or non-contextual zero suggest is currently enabled on the user's platform. However, if zero suggest (in all forms) is entirely disabled, the user will be perpetually ineligible.

histogram.name: Omnibox.ZeroSuggest.Eligible.RemoteNoUrl.OnNTP.OnFocus
histogram.summary:
Whether the user is eligible to receive personalized zero suggestions on the NTP. This histogram is updated only when the user focuses the omnibox on the NTP and is emitted only once even if multiple criteria make a user ineligible. Consult the source code to learn more about the precedence of these values.
This metric is recorded for all contexts (including users non-syncing, in incognito mode, or using different search engines) and targets specifically eligibility in the current situation.

histogram.name: Omnibox.ZeroSuggest.Eligible.RemoteNoUrl.OnNTP.OnProfileOpen
histogram.summary:
Whether the user is eligible to receive personalized zero suggestions on the NTP. This histogram is updated every time Chrome registers a profile change.
This metric is recorded for all contexts (including users non-syncing, in incognito mode, or using different search engines) and targets overall profile eligibility to receive zero-prefix query suggestions on a New Tab Page.

histogram.name: Omnibox.ZeroSuggest.MostVisitedResultsCounterfactual
histogram.summary: The number of most visited suggestions returned when ZeroSuggest would have triggered. The suggestions appear when the user has focused but not modified the omnibox.

histogram.name: Omnibox.ZeroSuggestRequests
histogram.summary: Counts about the number of zero suggest requests (requests for suggestions when the user has focused but not modified the omnibox) the omnibox sent, invalidated, and replies received.

histogram.name: OmniboxViewViews.SendTabToSelf
histogram.summary: Whether the user has clicked the item when it is shown" Logged when the share option is shown in the omnibox context menu"

histogram.name: OOBE.ArcTermsOfServiceScreen.ReviewFollowingSetup
histogram.summary: Whether the review Google Play following setup box is checked. Recorded when user leaves ArcTermsOfService screeen.

histogram.name: OOBE.BootToSignInCompleted
histogram.summary: Time from the login screen start to user session start.

histogram.name: OOBE.ErrorScreensTime.Enrollment
histogram.summary: Time spent on error screens during enrollment or autoenrollment.

histogram.name: OOBE.ErrorScreensTime.Signin
histogram.summary: Time spent on error screens during signin.

histogram.name: OOBE.ErrorScreensTime.Supervised
histogram.summary: Time spent on error screens during supervised user creation.

histogram.name: OOBE.ErrorScreensTime.Update
histogram.summary: Time spent on error screens during update.

histogram.name: OOBE.EulaScreen.UserActions
histogram.summary: Tracks all user actions on the EULA screen. Recorded when user clicks on buttons on the EULA screen.

histogram.name: OOBE.EULAToSignInTime
histogram.summary: Time from acceptance of the EULA until the login screen is first displayed.

histogram.name: OOBE.FingerprintSetupScreen.UserActions
histogram.summary: Tracks all user actions on the fingerprint screen. Recorded when user clicks on buttons on the fingerprint screen.

histogram.name: OOBE.GestureNavigationScreen.PageShownTime
histogram.summary: Records the amount of time that each page within the gesture navigation OOBE screen is shown. Recorded when the gesture navigation oobe screen is exiting.

histogram.name: OOBE.MarketingOptInScreen.BackendConnector
histogram.summary: The result of a request performed by the MarketingBackendConnector used in the OOBE MarketingOptInScreen when subscribing the user to Chromebook emails. This data is used to determine the success rate of the requests being performed.

histogram.name: OOBE.MarketingOptInScreen.Event
histogram.summary: Records the choice a user takes when prompted with a toggle on the OOBE MarketingOptInScreen to subscribe to Chromebook emails.

histogram.name: OOBE.MarketingOptInScreen.GeolocationResolve
histogram.summary: Records whether the user's geolocation was resolved successfully.

histogram.name: OOBE.MarketingOptInScreen.GeolocationResolveLength
histogram.summary: Records the length of the country code that was returned when determining the user's geolocation.

histogram.name: OOBE.NetworkErrorShown.Enrollment
histogram.summary: Number of times error screen has appeared during enrollment or autoenrollment.

histogram.name: OOBE.NetworkErrorShown.Signin
histogram.summary: Number of times error screen has appeared during signin.

histogram.name: OOBE.NetworkErrorShown.Supervised
histogram.summary: Number of times error screen has appeared during supervised user creation.

histogram.name: OOBE.NetworkErrorShown.Update
histogram.summary: Number of times error screen has appeared during update.

histogram.name: OOBE.RecommendApps.Fetcher.AppCount
histogram.summary: The number of recommended apps.

histogram.name: OOBE.RecommendApps.Fetcher.DownloadTime
histogram.summary: The time it takes to fetch the recommended apps.

histogram.name: OOBE.RecommendApps.Fetcher.ResponseCode
histogram.summary: The response code of fetching the recommended apps.

histogram.name: OOBE.RecommendApps.Fetcher.ResponseParseResult
histogram.summary: The result of parsing the recommend-apps response.

histogram.name: OOBE.RecommendApps.Fetcher.ResponseSize
histogram.summary: The size of the recommend-apps JSON response.

histogram.name: OOBE.RecommendApps.Screen.Action
histogram.summary: The user action on the recommend apps screen.

histogram.name: OOBE.RecommendApps.Screen.SelectedAppCount
histogram.summary: The number of apps user selected.

histogram.name: OOBE.RecommendApps.Screen.SelectedRecommendedPercentage
histogram.summary: The ratio of selected and recommended apps.

histogram.name: OOBE.RecommendApps.Screen.State
histogram.summary: Whether the reocmmend apps screen is shown.

histogram.name: OOBE.StepCompletionTime
histogram.summary: Time spent on specific OOBE screen.

histogram.name: OOBE.StepCompletionTimeByExitReason
histogram.summary: Time spent on specific OOBE screen grouped by exit reason.

histogram.name: OOBE.SyncConsentScreen.Behavior
histogram.summary: Whether the sync consent screen was shown, skipped with sync disabled, or skipped with sync enabled. Recorded when the screen is closed. Metrics Sync.InitialState and OOBE.SyncConsentScreen.SyncEnabled provide more detail on the state of the sync engine.

histogram.name: OOBE.SyncConsentScreen.ReviewFollowingSetup
histogram.summary: Whether the review following setup box is checked. Recorded when user leaves SyncConsent screen.

histogram.name: OOBE.SyncConsentScreen.SyncEnabled
histogram.summary: Final state of the sync service at the end of the sync consent OOBE step. The dialog may have been shown or skipped (e.g. by enterprise policy). See OOBE.SyncConsentScreen.Behavior for skip reasons.

histogram.name: OOBE.SyncConsentScreen.UserChoice
histogram.summary: Which button the user clicked on the out-of-box sync consent screen. Recorded when the user clicks the button, which also closes the screen.

histogram.name: OOBE.UpdateScreen.UpdateDownloadingTime
histogram.summary: Time spent on downloading ChromeOS update when critical updated is found during OOBE. Recorded when update is uploaded and ChromeOS device is about to restart to apply the update.

histogram.name: OOBE.WebUIToViewsSwitch.Duration
histogram.summary: Measures time spent by switch between WebUI and Views-based display hosts.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.ApplyDecision
histogram.summary: The decision made for whether the optimization type can be applied on the page load.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.ApplyDecisionAsync
histogram.summary: Records the decision made for whether the optimization type can be applied on a page load when all information for that page load has been sufficiently gathered. This will be recorded once per callback registered, so if two callbacks were registered for the same optimization type and page load, this will be recorded twice for that case.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.ClearFetchedHints.StoreAvailable
histogram.summary: Records whether the optimization guide store was available when fetched hints are cleared from the store after browser history is cleared.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.ClearHostModelFeatures.StoreAvailable
histogram.summary: Records whether the optimization guide store was available when host model features are cleared from the store after browser history is cleared.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.HintCache.FetchedHint.TimeToExpiration
histogram.summary: The remaining time a fetched hint that was loaded for use has before it expires and is removed from the hint cache store.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.HintCache.HasHint.AtCommit
histogram.obsolete: Obsolete as of 04/2020 since this histogram no longer makes sense with the current request flow.
histogram.summary: Records when the optimization guide hint cache has a hint entry for a URL's host at commit time.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.HintCache.HasHint.BeforeCommit
histogram.obsolete: Obsolete as of 04/2020 since this histogram no longer makes sense with the current request flow.
histogram.summary: Records when the optimization guide hint cache has a hint entry for a URL's host before commit time (e.g., at original navigation time or redirected navigation time).

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.HintCache.HintType.Loaded
histogram.summary: Records the store entry type of a hint when it is loaded from the hint cache store.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.HintCache.HostMatch.AtCommit
histogram.obsolete: Obsolete as of 04/2020 since this histogram no longer makes sense with the current request flow.
histogram.summary: Records when the optimization guide hint cache has a loaded hint entry matching a URL's host at commit time. This is recorded regardless of whether an associated preview type is allowed for the navigation or not. If no associated preview type is allowed, the hint will not be loaded from a backing store, so this will only capture matches for in-memory hints.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.HintCache.PageMatch.AtCommit
histogram.obsolete: Obsolete as of 04/2020 since this histogram no longer makes sense with the current request flow.
histogram.summary: Records when the optimization guide hint cache has a loaded page hint for a URL at commit time. This is recorded regardless of whether an associated preview type is allowed for the navigation or not. If no associated preview type is allowed, the hint will not be loaded from a backing store, so this will only capture matches for in-memory hints.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.HintCacheLevelDBStore.LoadMetadataResult
histogram.summary: Records the result of loading the metadata while initializing the HintCacheLevelDBStore.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.HintCacheLevelDBStore.Status
histogram.summary: Records each status change within the HintCacheLevelDBStore.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.HintCacheStore.OnLoadHint.FetchedHintExpired
histogram.summary: Records that a fetched hint loaded from the store has expired. If expired, the hint is not provided.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.Hints.NavigationHostCoverage.AtCommit
histogram.obsolete: Obsolete as of 04/2020 since this histogram no longer makes sense with the current request flow.
histogram.summary: Records once per navigation if the host that was navigated to was included in a hints fetch request in the last 7 days, even if no hints were returned, or had a hint served via the Optimization Hints component at commit.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.Hints.NavigationHostCoverage.BeforeCommit
histogram.obsolete: Obsolete as of 04/2020 since this histogram no longer makes sense with the current request flow.
histogram.summary: Records once per navigation if the host that was navigated to was included in a hints fetch request in the last 7 days, even if no hints were returned, or had a hint served via the Optimization Hints component before commit.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.HintsFetcher.GetHintsRequest.ActiveRequestCanceled
histogram.summary: Records when an in-flight hints request was canceled before it finished.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.HintsFetcher.GetHintsRequest.FetchLatency
histogram.summary: The duration of a request to fetch hints from the remote Optimization Guide Service starts until it completes. Recorded every time hints are fetched and parsed successfully.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.HintsFetcher.GetHintsRequest.HintCount
histogram.summary: Records the number of hints received from the remote Optimization Guide Service for every successful HintsFetch request.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.HintsFetcher.GetHintsRequest.HostCount
histogram.summary: Records the number of hosts selected for sending a hint request. This will be captured when any hint request is initiated.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.HintsFetcher.GetHintsRequest.NetErrorCode
histogram.summary: Net error codes for HintsFetch requests to the Optimization Guide Service on success and failure.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.HintsFetcher.GetHintsRequest.Status
histogram.summary: For each HintsFetch request to the Optimization Guide Service, log the HTTP response code on success and failure.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.HintsFetcher.GetHintsRequest.UrlCount
histogram.summary: Records the number of URLs selected for sending a hint request. This will be captured when any hint request is initiated.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.HintsFetcher.NavigationHostCoveredByFetch
histogram.obsolete: Obsolete as of 04/2020 since this histogram no longer makes sense with the current request flow.
histogram.summary: Records when hints fetching is enabled whether the HTTPS host being navigated to was included in a hints fetch request and any hints returned have not since expired. Captured at navigation start so it will not include hints fetched based on the current navigation.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.HintsFetcher.NavigationHostCoveredByFetch.AtCommit
histogram.obsolete: Obsolete as of 04/2020 since this histogram no longer makes sense with the current request flow.
histogram.summary: Records whether the host being navigated to was included in a hints fetch request and any hints returned have not since expired. Captured after a navigation is committed in order to determine if the fetch attempt made at navigation start succeeded or not.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.HintsFetcher.NavigationHostCoveredByFetch.BeforeCommit
histogram.obsolete: Obsolete as of 04/2020 since this histogram no longer makes sense with the current request flow.
histogram.summary: Records whether the host being navigated to was included in a hints fetch request and any hints returned have not since expired. Captured at navigation start so will not include any newly fetched hints for this navigation.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.HintsFetcher.RequestStatus.BatchUpdate
histogram.summary: The status of making a Batch Update context request of the OptmizationGuide HintsFetcher. This includes whether a network request was actually sent or not.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.HintsFetcher.RequestStatus.PageNavigation
histogram.summary: The status of making a Page Navigation context request of the OptmizationGuide HintsFetcher. This includes whether a network request was actually sent or not.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.HintsFetcher.TopHostProvider.BlacklistSize.OnInitialize
histogram.summary: Records the number of hosts placed on the HintsFetcherTopHostBlacklist when it is initialized.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.HintsFetcher.TopHostProvider.BlacklistSize.OnRequest
histogram.summary: Records the number of hosts on the HintsFetcherTopHostBlacklist when top hosts are requested.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.HintsFetcher.WasHostCoveredByFetch
histogram.summary: Records whether the host being navigated to was included in a hints fetch and any hints returned have not since expired.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.HintsLoadedPercentage
histogram.summary: The percentage (0-100) of hints loaded over all attempted page loads between component updates. Recorded when the OptimizationGuideService receives a component with a new version.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.HintsManager.ConcurrentPageNavigationFetches
histogram.summary: The number of active hints fetches initiated by the hints manager. Recorded when a fetch is initiated to the remote Optimization Guide Service at navigation start.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.HintsManager.RaceNavigationFetchAttemptStatus
histogram.summary: Status of a fetch attempt being raced against the current navigation start and whether it includes hosts and urls. Recorded on navigation start (including client redirects) by the hints manager.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.IsPredictionModelValid
histogram.summary: Records whether the prediction model being constructed for an optimization target is valid or not. Recorded when prediction models are loaded into memory by the prediction manager.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.OptimizationFilterStatus
histogram.summary: Status of processing OptimizationFilter configurations for server-provided blacklists. Recorded when the OptimizationGuideService receives a notification to process hints.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.OptimizationHintsComponent.MajorVersion
histogram.summary: Records the major version of the Optimization Hints component that was pushed to the client. This will be recorded at startup and when a new component is received.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.PredictionManager.HasHostModelFeaturesForHost
histogram.summary: Whether the PredictionManager had HostModelFeatures for the host of the navigation. Recorded when ShouldTargetNavigation is called.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.PredictionManager.HostModelFeaturesMapSize
histogram.summary: Records the number of host model features loaded into the map used by the PredictionManager. This is recorded when the host model features are loaded from the store.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.PredictionModelEvaluationLatency
histogram.summary: The duration of evaluating a prediction model for a registered optimization target. Recorded every time a prediction model is successfuly evaluated by the prediction manager. Not recorded if a cached decision is used or if the evaluation fails.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.PredictionModelFetcher.GetModelsRequest.HostCount
histogram.summary: Records the number of hosts included in a remote Optimization Guide Service client model request. This will be captured when any GetModelsRequest is initiated.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.PredictionModelFetcher.GetModelsResponse.HostModelFeatureCount
histogram.summary: Records the number of host model features received from the remote Optimization Guide Service for every successful GetModelsRequest.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.PredictionModelFetcher.GetModelsResponse.NetErrorCode
histogram.summary: Net error codes for GetModelsRequest to the Optimization Guide Service on success and failure.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.PredictionModelFetcher.GetModelsResponse.Status
histogram.summary: For each GetModelsRequest to the Optimization Guide Service, log the HTTP response code on success and failure.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.PredictionModelLoadedVersion
histogram.summary: Records the version of the prediction model that was loaded from the store and used until updated by a fetch. Recorded only if the loaded model was valid.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.PredictionModelStore.HostModelFeaturesLoadMetadataResult
histogram.summary: Records the result of loading the host model features metadata while initializing the OptimizationGuideStore.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.PredictionModelStore.OnLoadCollided
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 01/2020 since it rarely ever happens that there is a load collision.
histogram.summary: For each load of a prediction model from the OptimizationGuideStore, reports whether the load collided with an update being made to the store.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.PredictionModelUpdateVersion
histogram.summary: Records the version of the prediction model that was fetched as an update. Recorded only if a model returned was valid and is available for use.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.PredictionModelValidationLatency
histogram.summary: Records the duration of construction and validation of a prediction model when the prediction manager loads it into memory for a given optimization target. Recorded only if the model is valid and successfully loaded into memory for use.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.PredictionModelVersion
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 05/2020 to in favor of recording the version on update and on load.
histogram.summary: Records the version of the prediction model being updated and stored in the prediction model store and made available for use. Recorded only if the model is valid and successfully loaded into memory for use.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.ProcessHintsResult
histogram.summary: Whether processing the hints succeeded or failed at a particular step. Recorded when the OptimizationGuideService receives a notification to process hints.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.RemoteFetchingEnabled
histogram.summary: Whether the session is enabled for fetching information from the remote Optimization Guide Service. Recorded when the profile is initialized for the session if Optimization Hints are enabled and the profile is a non-incognito profile.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.ShouldTargetNavigation.PredictionModelStatus
histogram.summary: The status of the prediction model for an optimization target within the prediction manager. Recorded when the manager is asked if a navigation should be targeted and the decision for the navigation has not already been made.

histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.TargetDecision
histogram.summary: The decision made for whether the page load matches the optimization target.

histogram.name: OriginChip.Pressed
histogram.obsolete: Removed with CL 731423002. OriginChip has been removed.
histogram.summary: The number of clicks on the origin chip.

histogram.name: OriginTrials.FeatureEnabled
histogram.obsolete: Obsolete as of Chrome 54. Sort of replaced by OriginTrials.ValidationResult.
histogram.summary: Counts the results of origin trial checks to enable experimental features. The result for each feature check is counted at most once per execution context (e.g. page, worker).

histogram.name: OriginTrials.FeatureEnabled.MessageGenerated
histogram.summary: Counts how often an error message is generated for each origin trial check to enable an experimental feature.

histogram.name: OriginTrials.ValidationResult
histogram.summary: Counts the results of token validation checks to enable experimental features. The result for each token validation check is counted once per token per execution context (e.g. page, worker).

histogram.name: OSCrypt.BackendUsage
histogram.summary: The backend selected for this user and whether it was initialised successfully.

histogram.name: OSCrypt.EncryptionKeyLookupError
histogram.summary: Error code returned by SecKeychainFindGenericPassword when the encryption key is retrieved.

histogram.name: OSCrypt.EncryptionKeyOverwritingPreventions
histogram.summary: The number of times that Chrome prevented overwriting the encryption key in a row. This metric is logged once on startup.

histogram.name: OSCrypt.FindPasswordAgain
histogram.summary: Result of the second call to SecKeychainFindGenericPassword when the encryption key was not found but was expected to be there.

histogram.name: OSCrypt.GetEncryptionKeyAction
histogram.summary: Action taken when retrieving the encryption key from the Keychain. This metric is logged once on startup.

histogram.name: OSCrypt.Win.KeyDecryptionError
histogram.summary: The Windows GetLastError after a failure from calling into DPAPI to decrypt the master key for the os_crypt library on Windows. This is recorded during the OSCrypt::Init each time the decryption fails.

histogram.name: OSX.BluetoothAvailability
histogram.obsolete: Obsolete as of Chrome 73. This has been replaced by Bluetooth.Availability.
histogram.summary: The availability and capabilities of the Bluetooth driver on OSX devices. This metric is logged on startup.

histogram.name: OSX.CatSixtyFour
histogram.obsolete: Obsolete as of Chrome 43. See OmahaProxy for more relevant statistics.
histogram.summary: The cat's flavor and how many bits there are in it.

histogram.name: OSX.ExceptionHandlerEvents
histogram.summary: Events seen by the OSX NSException swizzle.

histogram.name: OSX.FastUserSwitch
histogram.summary: Records the Fast User Switching events that occur.

histogram.name: OSX.Fullscreen.Enter
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of Chrome 40. See OSX.Fullscreen.Enter.Style, OSX.Fullscreen.Enter.WindowLocation and OSX.Settings.ScreensHaveSeparateSpaces.
histogram.summary: This event is recorded each time a user triggers fullscreen for a browser window. The value's bits reflect different parameters. Bit 0: Fullscreen entry mechanism (AppKit vs Immersive). Bit 1: Whether the window was on the primary screen (Primary vs. Secondary). Bit 2: Whether displays have separate spaces options is enabled (Seperate vs Shared). Bit 3: Whether there are multiple screens.

histogram.name: OSX.Fullscreen.Enter.Source
histogram.summary: This event is recorded each time a user triggers fullscreen for a browser window. It indicates if the fullscreen is triggered for the browser or the tab content.

histogram.name: OSX.Fullscreen.Enter.Style
histogram.summary: This event is recorded each time a user triggers fullscreen for a browser window. It indicates the mechanism (immersive vs. AppKit) and the type of AppKit Fullscreen (Presentation Mode vs. Canonical Fullscreen).

histogram.name: OSX.Fullscreen.Enter.WindowLocation
histogram.summary: This event is recorded each time a user triggers fullscreen for a browser window. It indicates the screen in which the window was fullscreened, and the number of screens available.

histogram.name: OSX.Fullscreen.ToolbarStyle
histogram.summary: This event is recorded each time a user triggers fullscreen and when the fullscreen toolbar is updated with a new style.

histogram.name: OSX.Handoff.Origin
histogram.summary: This event is recorded each time a Handoff is received by Chrome on OSX. The enumeration indicates the source of the Handoff.

histogram.name: OSX.Installation.OtherChromeInstances
histogram.summary: The instances of other copies of Google Chrome on the user's Mac. Logged once on startup, and only for Chrome Beta and Chrome Dev. Will be used to determine if/how home directory migration will be done for side-by-side.

histogram.name: OSX.InstallationFilesystem
histogram.summary: Records the filesystem type that the app lives on.

histogram.name: OSX.KeychainReauthorizeIfNeeded
histogram.summary: The number of previous times that the keychain reauthorization did not complete, when run during the main browser launch.

histogram.name: OSX.KeychainReauthorizeIfNeededAtUpdate
histogram.summary: The number of previous times that the keychain reauthorization did not complete, when run at update time.

histogram.name: OSX.KeychainReauthorizeIfNeededAtUpdateSuccess
histogram.summary: How many times the keychain reauthorization ran before finally succeeding, when run at update time.

histogram.name: OSX.KeychainReauthorizeIfNeededSuccess
histogram.summary: How many times the keychain reauthorization ran before finally succeeding, when run during the main browser launch.

histogram.name: OSX.NativeShare
histogram.summary: Whether the user successfully shared via a native sharing extensions after selecting it from the share menu. False can indicate failure or user-initiated cancellation.

histogram.name: OSX.OtherInstances
histogram.summary: Records how many other instances of this binary are running at startup.

histogram.name: OSX.OtherInstancesCheckResult
histogram.summary: The result of the update process's check for other instances of Chrome.

histogram.name: OSX.RendererHost.SurfaceWaitTime
histogram.summary: This event records the amount of time that the browser process main thread blocks, waiting for a frame with the right dimensions to arrive from the gpu process.

histogram.name: OSX.Settings.ScreensHaveSeparateSpaces
histogram.summary: The configuration of the setting "Screens Have Separate Spaces" available in OSX 10.9+.

histogram.name: OSX.SharedMemory.Mechanism
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of Chrome 51 since Mach has become the default mechanism.
histogram.summary: A histogram entry is emitted each time a base::SharedMemory object is constructed. The value of the entry indicates the mechanism used to back the shared memory region.

histogram.name: OSX.StagingDirectoryLocation
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 02/2019 in favor of StagingDirectoryLocation2.
histogram.summary: Records first staging directory location that works.

histogram.name: OSX.StagingDirectoryLocation2
histogram.summary: Records, during Chrome startup, the first staging directory location that works. This is for the change to the updating system.

histogram.name: OSX.StartupUpdateState
histogram.summary: Records, during Chrome startup, the state of any staged updates.

histogram.name: OSX.SystemHotkeyMap.LoadSuccess
histogram.summary: Whether an attempt to load the system hotkeys on a Mac was successful.

histogram.name: OutdatedUpgradeBubble.NumLaterPerEnableAU
histogram.summary: Counts the number of times the user clicked on the later button of the outdated upgrade bubble, before clicking on the enable updates button in the same Chrome session.

histogram.name: OutdatedUpgradeBubble.NumLaterPerReinstall
histogram.summary: Counts the number of times the user clicked on the later button of the outdated upgrade bubble, before clicking on the reinstall button in the same Chrome session.

histogram.name: OutOfProcessHeapProfiling.ProfiledProcess.Type
histogram.summary: One metric is emitted every 24-hours after Chrome is launched for every process that is being profiled. The timer is reset if Chrome exits.

histogram.name: OutOfProcessHeapProfiling.ProfilingMode
histogram.summary: One metric is emitted every 24-hours after Chrome is launched for every Chrome instance that is using out of process heap profiling. The timer is reset if Chrome exits.

histogram.name: OutOfProcessHeapProfiling.RecordTrace.Success
histogram.summary: The metric is emitted each time Chrome attempts to record a memory-infra trace to upload an out-of-process heap-profiling memory dump.

histogram.name: OutOfProcessHeapProfiling.UploadTrace.Size
histogram.summary: The metric is emitted each time Chrome uploads a trace. It reflects the uncompressed size of the trace.

histogram.name: OutOfProcessHeapProfiling.UploadTrace.Success
histogram.summary: The metric is emitted each time Chrome uploads a trace. It reflects whether the upload was successful.

histogram.name: Overscroll.Cancelled
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of Chrome 59 in favour of Overscroll.Cancelled3.
histogram.summary: Overscroll gestures that were aborted before they were completed.

histogram.name: Overscroll.Cancelled3
histogram.summary: Overscroll gestures that were aborted before they were completed.

histogram.name: Overscroll.Completed
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of Chrome 44 in favour of Overscroll.Cancelled and Overscroll.Navigated2.
histogram.summary: Completed overscroll gestures.
histogram.details: An overscroll gesture starts when user scrolls past the edge of the web page and continues scrolling in the same direction. An overscroll gesture is completed when user stops scrolling (e.g. by lifting the fingers from the touchscreen or touchpad).

histogram.name: Overscroll.Navigated
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of Chrome 44 to switch to NavigationDirection enum.
histogram.summary: Navigations that were triggered due to completed overscroll gesture. Note that not all completed overscroll gestures trigger a navigation.

histogram.name: Overscroll.Navigated2
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of Chrome 59 in favour of Overscroll.Navigated3.
histogram.summary: Navigations that were triggered due to completed overscroll gesture. Note that not all completed overscroll gestures trigger a navigation.

histogram.name: Overscroll.Navigated3
histogram.summary: Navigations that were triggered due to completed overscroll gesture. Note that not all completed overscroll gestures trigger a navigation.

histogram.name: Overscroll.Started
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of Chrome 44 to switch to NavigationDirection enum.
histogram.summary: Overscroll gestures initiated by the user. Note that not all overcroll gestures started are completed (e.g. the overscroll gesture is aborted if user clicks or presses a key during the gesture).

histogram.name: Overscroll.Started2
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of Chrome 59 in favour of Overscroll.Started3.
histogram.summary: Overscroll gestures initiated by the user. Note that not all overscroll gestures started are completed (e.g. the overscroll gesture is aborted if user clicks or presses a key during the gesture).

histogram.name: Overscroll.Started3
histogram.summary: Overscroll gestures initiated by the user. Note that not all overscroll gestures started are completed (e.g. the overscroll gesture is aborted if user clicks or presses a key during the gesture).

histogram.name: Ozone.NeuralStylusReport.ActivePalmTouchCount
histogram.summary: When a stylus is touched to screen, the number of palms on the device at that time.

histogram.name: Ozone.NeuralStylusReport.FingerAgeBeforeStylus
histogram.summary: The time between a finger and a stylus touch. The finger may or may not still be on the touchscreen.

histogram.name: Ozone.NeuralStylusReport.PalmAgeBeforeStylus
histogram.summary: The time between a palm and a stylus touch. The palm may or may not still be on the touchscreen.

histogram.name: Ozone.TouchEventConverterEvdev.HoldCountAtCancel
histogram.summary: The count of the number of held events cancelled when we decide to finally cancel a stroke. Only reported if events have been held, and will be positive. Events are only held from PalmDetectionFilter.

histogram.name: Ozone.TouchEventConverterEvdev.HoldCountAtRelease
histogram.summary: The count of the number of held events released at once for a stroke when we decide to release. Only reported if events have been held, and will be positive. Events are only held from PalmDetectionFilter.

histogram.name: Ozone.TouchNoiseFilter.FarApartTapDistance
histogram.summary: The squared distance between taps which occur in quick succession. Only reported when the taps are far apart and touch noise filtering is enabled.

histogram.name: Ozone.TouchNoiseFilter.HorizontallyAlignedDistance
histogram.summary: The horizontal distance from a "touch press" to the closest other touch. Only reported if there are two or more fingers onscreen and touch noise filtering is enabled.

histogram.name: Ozone.TouchNoiseFilter.TimeSinceLastNoiseOccurrence
histogram.summary: The amount of time between noisy touches. The amount of time since Chrome startup is recorded for the first noisy touch after startup.

histogram.name: Ozone.TouchNoiseFilter.TouchesAtSinglePositionDuration
histogram.summary: The time between taps which occur in quick succession at the same screen location. Only reported when touch noise filtering is enabled.

histogram.name: P2P.Client.Canceled.WaitingTimeSeconds
histogram.summary: The wall-clock time spent until a lookup was canceled. This is reported every time p2p is used to find a candidate but the request was canceled.

histogram.name: P2P.Client.Found.CandidateCount
histogram.summary: The number of candidates on the LAN, i.e. the number of peers on the LAN offering at least N bytes of the requested file X. This is reported after examining responses from all peers on the LAN and picking a candidate.

histogram.name: P2P.Client.Found.ConnectionCount
histogram.summary: The number of p2p downloads of the peer that the returned URL points to. This is reported after examining responses from all peers on the LAN and picking a candidate.

histogram.name: P2P.Client.Found.WaitingTimeSeconds
histogram.summary: The wall-clock time spent waiting for the LAN-wide number of p2p downloads (i.e. the sum of p2p downloads from each peer on the LAN) to drop below the threshold. This is reported after examining responses from all peers on the LAN and picking a candidate.

histogram.name: P2P.Client.LookupResult
histogram.summary: The result of the lookup. Possible values include "Found" (if a candidate - i.e. a peer offering at least N bytes of file X - was chosen), "Not Found" (if no candidate could be found), "Vanished" (if a candidate was found but vanished while waiting in line), "Canceled" (if a candidate was found but the request was canceled while waiting in line), and "Filtered" (if it was detected that mDNS was filtered). This is reported after examining responses from all peers on the LAN when p2p is used to find a candidate.

histogram.name: P2P.Client.NumPeers
histogram.summary: The number of peers implementing p2p file sharing on the network. This is reported every time p2p is used to look up a resource on a network where mDNS is not filtered.

histogram.name: P2P.Client.Vanished.WaitingTimeSeconds
histogram.summary: The wall-clock time spent waiting for one or more candidates (i.e. peers offering at least N bytes of file X) that all vanished before the LAN-wide number of p2p downloads dropped below the threshold. This is reported every time candidates were found using p2p but then vanished.

histogram.name: P2P.Server.ClientCount
histogram.summary: The number of currently connected HTTP clients. This is reported every time a HTTP client connects.

histogram.name: P2P.Server.ContentServedInterruptedMB
histogram.summary: Number of megabytes (1,000,000 bytes) served from the device (via HTTP) where the client disconnects prematurely. This is reported every time a file is served and the client disconnects before receiving all data.

histogram.name: P2P.Server.ContentServedSuccessfullyMB
histogram.summary: Number of megabytes (1,000,000 bytes) served from the device (via HTTP). This is reported every time a file have been served successfully.

histogram.name: P2P.Server.DownloadSpeedKBps
histogram.summary: The average speed at which the download was served at, in kB/s. This is reported every time a file have been served successfully.

histogram.name: P2P.Server.FileCount
histogram.summary: The number of files available via p2p. This is reported every time a file is added or removed to the /var/cache/p2p directory.

histogram.name: P2P.Server.RangeBeginPercentage
histogram.summary: When a client resumes a download, the HTTP request includes range specifier to skip the bytes it already has. This metric conveys this as a percentage of the file size. This is reported every time a file is served, even if the request does not include a range specifier (in which case 0 is reported).

histogram.name: P2P.Server.RequestResult
histogram.summary: The result of the HTTP request. Possible values include "Response Sent" (the resource was found and the response was successfully sent), "Response Interrupted" (the resource was found but the client disconnected), "Malformed" (the request was malformed), "Not Found" (the request was for a resource that was not found), and "Index" (the request was for the '/' or '/index.html' resource). This is reported for every HTTP request handled.

histogram.name: PageActionController.ExtensionsWithPageActions
histogram.summary: The number of Extensions that have Page Actions. Measured once per startup per profile.

histogram.name: PageImportanceSignals.HadFormInteraction.OnCommitLoad
histogram.summary: Number of pages that had any HTML form interaction before next page load.

histogram.name: PageLoad.AbortTiming.Background
histogram.obsolete: Removed in favor of PageLoad.Experimental.AbortTiming equivalent.
histogram.summary: This metric is still experimental and not yet ready to be relied upon. Measures the time from navigation start to the time the page load was aborted due to being backgrounded.

histogram.name: PageLoad.AbortTiming.ClientRedirect
histogram.obsolete: Removed in favor of PageLoad.Experimental.AbortTiming equivalent.
histogram.summary: This metric is still experimental and not yet ready to be relied upon. Measures the time from navigation start to the time the page load was aborted by a client side redirect (Javascript navigation).

histogram.name: PageLoad.AbortTiming.Close
histogram.obsolete: Removed in favor of PageLoad.Experimental.AbortTiming equivalent.
histogram.summary: This metric is still experimental and not yet ready to be relied upon. Measures the time from navigation start to the time the page load was aborted by the user closing the tab or browser.

histogram.name: PageLoad.AbortTiming.ForwardBackNavigation
histogram.obsolete: Removed in favor of PageLoad.Experimental.AbortTiming equivalent.
histogram.summary: This metric is still experimental and not yet ready to be relied upon. Measures the time from navigation start to the time the page load was aborted by a forward or back navigation.

histogram.name: PageLoad.AbortTiming.NewNavigation
histogram.obsolete: Removed in favor of PageLoad.Experimental.AbortTiming equivalent.
histogram.summary: This metric is still experimental and not yet ready to be relied upon. Measures the time from navigation start to the time the page load was aborted by a new navigation.

histogram.name: PageLoad.AbortTiming.Other
histogram.obsolete: Removed in favor of PageLoad.Experimental.AbortTiming equivalent.
histogram.summary: This metric is still experimental and not yet ready to be relied upon. Measures the time from navigation start to the time the page load was aborted. The abort cause is unknown.

histogram.name: PageLoad.AbortTiming.Reload
