histogram.obsolete: Removed on January 2019 in favor of PageLoad.InteractiveTiming.InputDelay2 which correctly excludes some scrolling cases that were previously not excluded from this metric.
histogram.summary:
The duration between the hardware timestamp and the start of event processing on the main thread for a meaningful input. In ms.
Do not modify this metric in any way without contacting speed-metrics-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: PageLoad.InteractiveTiming.InputDelay2
histogram.obsolete: Removed on February 2019 in favor of PageLoad.InteractiveTiming.InputDelay3 which brings the metric more in line with the EventTiming API.
histogram.summary:
The duration between the hardware timestamp and the start of event processing on the main thread for a meaningful input. Excludes scrolls.
Do not modify this metric in any way without contacting speed-metrics-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: PageLoad.InteractiveTiming.InputDelay3
histogram.summary:
The duration between the hardware timestamp and the start of event processing on the main thread for a meaningful input. Excludes scrolls.
Do not modify this metric in any way without contacting speed-metrics-dev@chromium.org AND chrome-analysis-team@google.com.
WARNING: The long tail of this metric is buggy on Mac (overflows for a subset of users) which can lead to surprising false regressions or improvements.

histogram.name: PageLoad.InteractiveTiming.InputTimestamp
histogram.obsolete: Removed on January 2019 in favor of PageLoad.InteractiveTiming.InputTimestamp2 which correctly excludes some scrolling cases that were previously not excluded from this metric.
histogram.summary: The duration between navigation start and the hardware timestamp of a meaningful input. In ms.

histogram.name: PageLoad.InteractiveTiming.InputTimestamp2
histogram.obsolete: Removed on February 2019 in favor of PageLoad.InteractiveTiming.InputTimestamp3 which brings the metric more in line with the EventTiming API.
histogram.summary: The duration between navigation start and the hardware timestamp of a meaningful input. Excludes scrolls.

histogram.name: PageLoad.InteractiveTiming.InputTimestamp3
histogram.summary: The duration between navigation start and the hardware timestamp of a meaningful input. Excludes scrolls.

histogram.name: PageLoad.InteractiveTiming.LongestInputDelay
histogram.obsolete: Removed on January 2019 in favor of PageLoad.InteractiveTiming.LongestInputDelay2 which correctly excludes some scrolling cases that were previously not excluded from this metric.
histogram.summary: Measures longest Input Delay, the longest duration between the hardware timestamp and the start of event processing on the main thread for the meaningful input per navigation. In ms.

histogram.name: PageLoad.InteractiveTiming.LongestInputDelay2
histogram.obsolete: Removed on February 2019 in favor of PageLoad.InteractiveTiming.LongestInputDelay3 which brings the metric more in line with the EventTiming API.
histogram.summary: Measures longest Input Delay, the longest duration between the hardware timestamp and the start of event processing on the main thread for the meaningful input per navigation. Excludes scrolls.

histogram.name: PageLoad.InteractiveTiming.LongestInputDelay3
histogram.obsolete: Removed in October 2019 in favor of LongestInputDelay4, which updates naming to be consistent with LongestInput UKM.
histogram.summary: Measures longest Input Delay, the longest duration between the hardware timestamp and the start of event processing on the main thread for the meaningful input per navigation. Excludes scrolls.

histogram.name: PageLoad.InteractiveTiming.LongestInputDelay4
histogram.summary: Measures longest Input Delay, the longest duration between the hardware timestamp and the start of event processing on the main thread for the meaningful input per navigation. Excludes scrolls.

histogram.name: PageLoad.InteractiveTiming.LongestInputTimestamp
histogram.obsolete: Removed on January 2019 in favor of PageLoad.InteractiveTiming.LongestInputTimestamp2 which correctly excludes some scrolling cases that were previously not excluded from this metric.
histogram.summary: The duration between navigation start and the hardware timestamp of the meaningful input with longest queuing delay per navigation. In ms.

histogram.name: PageLoad.InteractiveTiming.LongestInputTimestamp2
histogram.obsolete: Removed on February 2019 in favor of PageLoad.InteractiveTiming.LongestInputTimestamp3 which brings the metric more in line with the EventTiming API.
histogram.summary: The duration between navigation start and the hardware timestamp of the meaningful input with longest queuing delay per navigation. Excludes scrolls.

histogram.name: PageLoad.InteractiveTiming.LongestInputTimestamp3
histogram.obsolete: Removed in October 2019 in favor of LongestInputTimestamp4, which updates naming to be consistend with LongestInput UKMs.
histogram.summary: The duration between navigation start and the hardware timestamp of the meaningful input with longest queuing delay per navigation. Excludes scrolls.

histogram.name: PageLoad.InteractiveTiming.LongestInputTimestamp4
histogram.summary: The duration between navigation start and the hardware timestamp of the meaningful input with longest queuing delay per navigation. Excludes scrolls.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Internal.ClientRedirect.FirstPaintToNavigation
histogram.summary: The delay between first paint and a client-side redirect navigation, for pages that reported a first paint. This metric is only intended to help in choosing a heuristic delay to distinguish between client-side redirects and other client initiated navigations, and will be deprecated in M54. Please contact bmcquade@chromium.org before using this metric.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Internal.ClientRedirect.NavigationWithoutPaint
histogram.summary: Counts how often a client-side redirect was initiated from a page that did not report a first paint. This metric is only intended to help in choosing a heuristic delay to distinguish between client-side redirects and other client initiated navigations, and will be deprecated in M54. Please contact bmcquade@chromium.org before using this metric.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Internal.ClientRedirectDelayAfterPaint
histogram.summary: The delay between first paint and a client-side redirection. This metric is only intended to help in choosing a heuristic delay to distinguish between client-side redirects and other client initiated navigations, and will be deprecated in M54. Please contact bmcquade@chromium.org before using this metric.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Internal.CommitToComplete.NoTimingIPCs
histogram.summary: The delay between commit and completion, for page loads that did not receive any timing IPCs. This metric is only intended to help understand the cases where committed page loads don't receive timing IPCs. Please contact bmcquade@chromium.org before using this metric.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Internal.ErrorCode
histogram.summary: Counts of various internal error conditions in the page_load_metrics system.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Internal.InteractiveToInteractiveDetection
histogram.summary: Measures time delta between Time to Interactive and when we can detect TTI. TTI cannot be detected in real time - we have to wait sufficiently long to determine main thread and network quiescence. See https://goo.gl/TFw6xz for detailed explanation of Time to Interactive. This histogram uses First Meaningful Paint as the lower bound for quiescent windows.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Internal.NavigationStartedInForeground
histogram.summary: Whether a navigation started in the foreground.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Internal.OutOfOrderInterFrameTiming
histogram.summary: The difference in magnitude between existing and updated timing, for inter-frame timings that are received out of order. Page load metrics observes timing updates in all frames on a page. It's possible for timings in different frames to arrive out of order. This metric tracks how often this happens, along with the magnitude of the difference.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Internal.PageLoadCompleted.AfterAppBackground
histogram.summary: Whether a page load completed after the app entered the background. We log a 'Started' value for each PageLoadTracker that is active when the app enters the background, and a 'Completed' value for each of those PageLoadTrackers that successfully runs its destructor. This helps to understand how many trackers are destroyed while the app is in the background before they are able to run their destructor. This metric is only intended to help in understanding how often app background leads to data loss, and will be deprecated in a subsequent release. Please contact bmcquade@chromium.org before using this metric.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Internal.PageLoadTimingStatus
histogram.summary: The status of PageLoadTiming structs received from the render process over IPC.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Internal.PaintTiming.HadUserInputBeforeFirstMeaningfulPaint
histogram.summary: Whether the user had any interaction on the page before FirstMeaningfulPaint. Recorded when the page load reaches network idle.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Internal.PaintTiming.LargestContentfulPaint.ContentType
histogram.summary: Measures whether the largest contentful paint, whose timestamp is measured by PageLoad.PaintTiming.NavigationToLargestContentfulPaint, comes from text or image. This value is recorded whenever PageLoad.PaintTiming.NavigationToLargestContentfulPaint is recorded.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Internal.PaintTiming.LargestContentfulPaint.MainFrame.ContentType
histogram.summary: Measures whether the largest content paint in the main frame, whose timestamp is measured by PageLoad.PaintTiming.NavigationToLargestContentPaint.MainFrame, comes from text or image. This value is recorded whenever PageLoad.PaintTiming.NavigationToLargestContentfulPaint.MainFrame is recorded.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Internal.PaintTiming.NavigationToFirstContentfulPaint.InitiatingProcess
histogram.summary: Breakdown of NavigationToFirstContentfulPaint counts by process that initiated the navigation (this will only be renderer or browser).

histogram.name: PageLoad.Internal.Prerender
histogram.summary: Counts the number of prerender navigations. Only logs true values.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Internal.ProvisionalAbortChainSize.ForwardBack
histogram.summary: This histogram counts the number of provisional loads aborted by other navigations, until a final forward back navigation commits.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Internal.ProvisionalAbortChainSize.NewNavigation
histogram.summary: This histogram counts the number of provisional loads aborted by new navigations, until a final new navigation commits.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Internal.ProvisionalAbortChainSize.NoCommit
histogram.summary: This histogram counts the number of provisional loads aborted by new navigations. In this case the chain never ends with a commit. This is logged on the next abort signal comes in (e.g. tab close or new navigation).

histogram.name: PageLoad.Internal.ProvisionalAbortChainSize.Reload
histogram.summary: This histogram counts the number of provisional loads aborted by other navigations, until a final reload navigation commits.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Internal.ProvisionalAbortChainSize.SameURL
histogram.summary: This histogram counts the number of consecutive provisional loads aborted by other navigations with the same url (before redirects). It's logged when a provisional abort does not share a URL, or when a load finally commits. Note that we don't log chains of length 0.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Internal.Renderer.FirstMeaningfulPaintDetector.FirstMeaningfulPaintOrdering
histogram.summary: Whether the two variants of First Meaningful Paint reported different values, and if so, which one was reported first.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Internal.Renderer.FirstMeaningfulPaintDetector.HadNetworkQuiet
histogram.summary: Recorded when the page load reached network 0-quiet (no active network connection for 0.5 seconds), or network 2-quiet (no more than 2 active network connections for 2 seconds). PageLoad.Experimental.PaintTiming.FirstMeaningfulPaintSignalStatus2 histogram gives the fraction of page loads that had network 2-quiet, so it can be used as a baseline.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Internal.Renderer.PaintTiming.SwapResult
histogram.summary: For first paint, first contentful paint, first meaningful paint, first text paint, and first image paint, we use the swap timestamp of the corresponding paint, which is the timestamp the CC SwapPromise was positively fulfilled (i.e. DidSwap() was invoked). If the swap did not occur (DidNotSwap() was invoked), we use the timestamp when the swap promise fails. However, in the future we may stop reporting timestamps for certain failure reasons. This metric records whether or not the swap occurred, and the reason for failure if it failed. The distribution of this metric will help determine the effect of not reporting timestamps in certain cases.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Internal.Renderer.PaintTiming.SwapTimeDelta
histogram.obsolete: deprecated May 2018: The swap-timestamp comes from a different process, and comparing that with the paint-time (which is from the local process) does not produce a reliable metric.
histogram.summary: Records the delta between the renderer timestamp and swap timestamp for first paint, first contentful paint, first meaningful paint, first text paint, and first image paint. The renderer timestamp is recorded on the Paint path, while the swap timestamp is the result of a CC SwapPromise (queued on the Paint path) either being successfully fulfilled, or broken in cases where the swap did not occur. As we switch to using the swap timestamps in place of their renderer counterparts, this metric allows us to see the discrepancies between the two timestamps.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Internal.Renderer.PresentationTime.DeltaFromSwapTime
histogram.summary: 'Swap time' is the timestamp of the renderer submitting a CompositorFrame, and 'Presentation time' is the timestamp of the frame becoming visible on screen. This metric measures the duration between the swap-time and the presentation-time. This is recorded only if a valid presentation-timestamp is available. See PageLoad.Internal.Renderer.PresentationTime.Valid for how often the presentation-timestamp is valid. This is reported only for compositor-frames that report a paint-timing metric (e.g. FirstContentfulPaint etc.), and after that frame has been displayed on screen.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Internal.Renderer.PresentationTime.Valid
histogram.summary: This boolean keeps track of whether a valid presentation-timestamp was received or not. This is reported only for compositor-frames that report a paint-timing metric (e.g. FirstContentfulPaint etc.), and after that frame has been displayed on screen.

histogram.name: PageLoad.LayoutInstability.CumulativeShiftScore
histogram.summary:
Measures the cumulative layout shift score (bit.ly/lsm-explainer) that has occurred on the page (including all subframes). Recorded at the end of the page lifetime, such as when the page is being navigated away from or when a tab is being closed. Stable since M79; previous versions are expermental and subject to fluctuation between releases.
Log of major changes: http://bit.ly/chrome-speed-metrics-changelog

histogram.name: PageLoad.LayoutInstability.CumulativeShiftScore.MainFrame
histogram.summary: Measures the cumulative layout shift score (bit.ly/lsm-explainer) that has occurred in the main frame. Recorded at the end of the page lifetime, such as when the page is being navigated away from or when a tab is being closed.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Navigation.RedirectChainLength
histogram.summary: The number of URLs in the redirect chain for this navigation. This excludes the first URL.

histogram.name: PageLoad.PageTiming.ForegroundDuration
histogram.summary: For page loads that start in the foreground, measures the duration of time until one of the following events occurs: the load of the main resource fails, the page load is stopped, the tab hosting the page is closed, the render process hosting the page goes away, a new navigation which later commits is initiated in the same tab, or the tab hosting the page is backgrounded. This metric has a very long tail, for pages that spend a long period of time in the foreground. As such, use of the mean or tail values are not recommended.

histogram.name: PageLoad.PageTiming.ForegroundDuration.PageEndReason.ForwardBackNavigation.UserGesture
histogram.summary: The time since navigation start before the user presses the back button on a page. Only measures page loads that remained in foreground and were created by user gesture inside the renderer (as opposed to browser UX gestures).

histogram.name: PageLoad.PageTiming.NavigationToFailedProvisionalLoad
histogram.summary: Measures the time from navigation timing's navigation start to the time the provisional load failed. Only measures provisional loads that failed in the foreground.

histogram.name: PageLoad.PageTiming.NavigationToFirstForeground
histogram.summary: Measures the time from navigation timing's navigation start to the time the user first foregrounds an initially backgrounded tab. Only measures navigations that started in the background.

histogram.name: PageLoad.PaintTiming.ForegroundToFirstContentfulPaint
histogram.summary: Measures the time from a background tab being switched to the foreground to the time the first contentful paint is performed, for main frame documents.

histogram.name: PageLoad.PaintTiming.ForegroundToFirstPaint
histogram.summary: Measures the time from a background tab being switched to the foreground to the time the first paint is performed, for main frame documents.

histogram.name: PageLoad.PaintTiming.NavigationToFirstContentfulPaint
histogram.summary:
The time from navigation start to first "contentful" paint.
Do not modify this metric in any way without contacting speed-metrics-dev@chromium.org AND chrome-analysis-team@google.com.
Log of major changes: http://bit.ly/chrome-speed-metrics-changelog

histogram.name: PageLoad.PaintTiming.NavigationToFirstImagePaint
histogram.summary: Measures the time from navigation timing's navigation start to the time the first image is painted, for main frame documents. For images that render progressively, this is recorded as soon as any image pixels have been drawn.

histogram.name: PageLoad.PaintTiming.NavigationToFirstPaint
histogram.summary:
Measures the time from navigation timing's navigation start to the time the first paint is performed, for main frame documents.
Do not modify this metric in any way without contacting speed-metrics-dev@chromium.org.

histogram.name: PageLoad.PaintTiming.NavigationToFirstTextPaint
histogram.obsolete: Discontinued Nov 2018 because data is no longer being used.
histogram.summary: Measures the time from navigation timing's navigation start to the time the first non-blank text is painted, for main frame documents.

histogram.name: PageLoad.PaintTiming.NavigationToLargestContentfulPaint
histogram.summary:
Measures the time from navigation timing's navigation start to the time the largest content (text or image) is first painted, across all frames. Excludes any content painted after user input. The value is recorded at the end of each page load unless there is an abort or user input before text or image paint. See http://bit.ly/fcp_plus_plus for details. Stable since M79; previous versions are experimental and subject to fluctuation between releases.
Log of major changes: http://bit.ly/chrome-speed-metrics-changelog

histogram.name: PageLoad.PaintTiming.NavigationToLargestContentfulPaint.MainFrame
histogram.summary: Measures the time from navigation timing's navigation start to the time the largest content (text or image) is first painted, for main frame documents. Excludes any content painted after user input. The value is recorded at the end of each page load unless there is an abort or user input before text or image paint. See http://bit.ly/fcp_plus_plus for details.

histogram.name: PageLoad.PaintTiming.ParseStartToFirstContentfulPaint
histogram.summary: Measures the time from when the HTML parser started, to when the page first paints content.

histogram.name: PageLoad.ParseTiming.NavigationToParseStart
histogram.summary: Measures the time from navigation timing's navigation start to the time the parser started, for main frame documents.

histogram.name: PageLoad.ParseTiming.ParseBlockedOnScriptExecution
histogram.summary: Measures the time that the HTML parser spent blocked on the execution of scripts, for main frame documents that finished parsing.

histogram.name: PageLoad.ParseTiming.ParseBlockedOnScriptExecutionFromDocumentWrite
histogram.summary: Measures the time that the HTML parser spent blocked on the execution of scripts inserted from document.write, for main frame documents that finished parsing.

histogram.name: PageLoad.ParseTiming.ParseBlockedOnScriptLoad
histogram.summary: Measures the time that the HTML parser spent blocked on the load of scripts, for main frame documents that finished parsing.

histogram.name: PageLoad.ParseTiming.ParseBlockedOnScriptLoadFromDocumentWrite
histogram.summary: Measures the time that the HTML parser spent blocked on the load of scripts inserted from document.write, for main frame documents that finished parsing.

histogram.name: PageLoad.ParseTiming.ParseDuration
histogram.summary: Measures the time that the HTML parser was active, for main frame documents that finished parsing.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Timing.NavigationToDOMContentLoadedEventFired
histogram.summary: Measures the time from navigation timing's navigation start to the time the DOMContentLoaded event is fired, for main frame documents.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Timing.NavigationToFirstLayout
histogram.summary: Measures the time from navigation timing's navigation start to the time the first layout is performed, for main frame documents.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Timing.NavigationToLoadEventFired
histogram.summary: Measures the time from navigation timing's navigation start to the time the load event is fired, for main frame documents.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Timing2.DOMLoadingToDOMContentLoadedEventFired
histogram.summary: Measures the time from when DOM loading occurs (the time that HTML parsing begins) to the time the DOMContentLoaded event is fired, for main frame documents. This is equivalent to the time that the HTML document resource was being parsed.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Timing2.DOMLoadingToFirstContentfulPaint
histogram.summary: Measures the time from when DOM loading occurs (the time that HTML parsing begins) to first "contentful" paint.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Timing2.ForegroundToFirstPaint
histogram.summary: Measures the time from a background tab being switched to the foreground to the time the first paint is performed, for main frame documents.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Timing2.NavigationToCommit
histogram.summary: Measures the time from navigation timing's navigation start to the time the navigation committed, for main frame documents.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Timing2.NavigationToDOMContentLoadedEventFired
histogram.summary: Measures the time from navigation timing's navigation start to the time the DOMContentLoaded event is fired, for main frame documents. This metric is being phased out in favor of the PageLoad.DocumentTiming equivalent and will be deprecated in M54.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Timing2.NavigationToFailedProvisionalLoad
histogram.summary: Measures the time from navigation timing's navigation start to the time the provisional load failed. Only measures provisional loads that failed in the foreground.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Timing2.NavigationToFirstBackground
histogram.summary: Measures the time from navigation timing's navigation start to the time the user first backgrounds the tab. Only measures navigations that started in the foreground.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Timing2.NavigationToFirstContentfulPaint
histogram.summary: The time from navigation start to first "contentful" paint. This metric is being phased out in favor of the PageLoad.PaintTiming equivalent and will be deprecated in M54.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Timing2.NavigationToFirstForeground
histogram.summary: Measures the time from navigation timing's navigation start to the time the user first foregrounds an initially backgrounded tab. Only measures navigations that started in the background.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Timing2.NavigationToFirstImagePaint
histogram.summary: Measures the time from navigation timing's navigation start to the time the first image is painted, for main frame documents. For images that render progressively, this is recorded as soon as any image pixels have been drawn. This metric is being phased out in favor of the PageLoad.PaintTiming equivalent and will be deprecated in M54.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Timing2.NavigationToFirstLayout
histogram.summary: Measures the time from navigation timing's navigation start to the time the first layout is performed, for main frame documents. This metric is being phased out in favor of the PageLoad.DocumentTiming equivalent and will be deprecated in M54.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Timing2.NavigationToFirstPaint
histogram.summary: Measures the time from navigation timing's navigation start to the time the first paint is performed, for main frame documents. This metric is being phased out in favor of the PageLoad.PaintTiming equivalent and will be deprecated in M54.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Timing2.NavigationToFirstTextPaint
histogram.summary: Measures the time from navigation timing's navigation start to the time the first non-blank text is painted, for main frame documents. This metric is being phased out in favor of the PageLoad.PaintTiming equivalent and will be deprecated in M54.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Timing2.NavigationToLoadEventFired
histogram.summary: Measures the time from navigation timing's navigation start to the time the load event is fired, for main frame documents. This metric is being phased out in favor of the PageLoad.DocumentTiming equivalent and will be deprecated in M54.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Timing2.ParseBlockedOnScriptLoad
histogram.summary: Measures the time that the HTML parser spent blocked on the load of scripts, for main frame documents that started parsing.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Timing2.ParseBlockedOnScriptLoad.ParseComplete
histogram.summary: Measures the time that the HTML parser spent blocked on the load of scripts, for main frame documents that finished parsing. This metric is being phased out in favor of the PageLoad.ParseTiming equivalent and will be deprecated in M54.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Timing2.ParseBlockedOnScriptLoadFromDocumentWrite
histogram.summary: Measures the time that the HTML parser spent blocked on the load of scripts inserted from document.write, for main frame documents that started parsing.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Timing2.ParseBlockedOnScriptLoadFromDocumentWrite.ParseComplete
histogram.summary: Measures the time that the HTML parser spent blocked on the load of scripts inserted from document.write, for main frame documents that finished parsing. This metric is being phased out in favor of the PageLoad.ParseTiming equivalent and will be deprecated in M54.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Timing2.ParseDuration
histogram.summary: Measures the time that the HTML parser was active, for main frame documents that finished parsing. This metric is being phased out in favor of the PageLoad.ParseTiming equivalent and will be deprecated in M54.

histogram.name: PageLoad.Timing2.ParseStartToFirstContentfulPaint
histogram.summary: Measures the time from when the HTML parser started, to when the page first paints content. This metric is being phased out in favor of the PageLoad.PaintTiming equivalent and will be deprecated in M54.

histogram.name: PageScheduler.PageLifecycleStateTransition
histogram.summary: Tracks the transitions of the page between the various PageLifecycleStates: active, hidden/backgrounded, hidden/foregrounded, and frozen. A page is considered active if is visible. A page is considered backgrounded if not visible and not currently providing a useful service, such as playing audio. A page is considered foregrounded if it is not backgrounded. A page can be frozen due to being hidden/backgrounded for a specified amount of time, or frozen due to critical resources.

histogram.name: PageSerialization.MhtmlGeneration.BrowserWaitForRendererTime.FrameTree
histogram.summary: Total time the browser process waited for all render processes to save their respective frames while saving a page into MHTML.

histogram.name: PageSerialization.MhtmlGeneration.BrowserWaitForRendererTime.SingleFrame
histogram.summary: Time the browser process waited on a single frame to be saved by a render processes while saving a page into MHTML.

histogram.name: PageSerialization.MhtmlGeneration.EncodingTime.SingleFrame
histogram.summary:
Time taken to encode into MTHML the fully serialized contents of a frame.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: PageSerialization.MhtmlGeneration.FinalSaveStatus
histogram.summary: Final status of the MHTML save operation for a page.

histogram.name: PageSerialization.MhtmlGeneration.FullPageSavingTime
histogram.summary: Time taken to save a page into an MHTML file.

histogram.name: PageSerialization.MhtmlGeneration.PopupOverlaySkipped
histogram.summary: Recorded when the popup overlays are removed from MHTML serialization.

histogram.name: PageSerialization.MhtmlGeneration.RendererMainThreadTime.FrameTree
histogram.summary: Time spent by the main threads of all involved render processes while saving the frame tree of a page to MHTML.

histogram.name: PageSerialization.MhtmlGeneration.RendererMainThreadTime.SingleFrame
histogram.summary: Time spent by the renderer main thread while saving one frame of a page to MHTML.

histogram.name: PageSerialization.MhtmlGeneration.RendererMainThreadTime.SlowestFrame
histogram.summary: The longest time taken by the main thread of a render processes to save one frame of a page being saved to MHTML. In other words this is the maximum value reported in PageSerialization.MhtmlGeneration.RendererMainThreadTime.SingleFrame for one page save operation.

histogram.name: PageSerialization.MhtmlGeneration.SerializationTime.SingleFrame
histogram.summary: Time taken to fully serialize the contents of a frame, including HTML and CSS and image resources.

histogram.name: PageSerialization.MhtmlGeneration.WriteToDiskTime.SingleFrame
histogram.summary: Time spent writing a frame's encoded MHTML data to the file or data pipe handle passed to the Renderer, recorded when the writing is done and the handle is closed. Note that when writing to a data pipe, this metric does not precisely reflect the time taken to write the data to disk.

histogram.name: PageSerialization.MhtmlLoading.LoadResult
histogram.summary: Reports the result of an attempt to load an MHTML archive.

histogram.name: PageSerialization.ProblemDetection.LoadedCSSPercentage
histogram.summary: Percentage of loaded CSS elements in the main frame at the time of serialization.

histogram.name: PageSerialization.ProblemDetection.LoadedImagePercentage
histogram.summary: Percentage of loaded images in the main frame at the time of serialization.

histogram.name: PageSerialization.ProblemDetection.TotalCSSCount
histogram.summary: Total number of CSS elements in the main frame for serialization.

histogram.name: PageSerialization.ProblemDetection.TotalImageCount
histogram.summary: Total number of images in the main frame for serialization.

histogram.name: PageSerialization.SerializationTime.CSSElement
histogram.summary:
Time spent serializing a CSS element (including embedded "sub"-CSS and images).
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: PageSerialization.SerializationTime.Html
histogram.summary:
Time taken to generate HTML data from a frame's DOM and serialize it (without sub-resources like CSS and images).
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: PageSerialization.SerializationTime.ImageElement
histogram.summary:
Time spent serializing an image element.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: PaintHolding.CommitTrigger
histogram.summary: Records the reason that a commit was triggered, to enable research into which event (non-same-origin, FCP, timeout, etc) led to the first commit.

histogram.name: PaintHolding.CommitTrigger2
histogram.summary: Records the reason that a commit was triggered, to enable research into which event (non-same-origin, FCP, timeout, etc) led to the first commit.

histogram.name: PaintHolding.InputTiming
histogram.summary: Records whether or not input arrived before the first commit.

histogram.name: PaintHolding.InputTiming2
histogram.summary: Records whether or not input arrived before the first commit.

histogram.name: Parser.AppendBytesDelay
histogram.summary: The delay from when bytes are received on the main thread to when the BackgroundHTMLParser starts tokenizing them. Always a shorter time than the time emitted to Parser.PreloadTokenizeDelay.

histogram.name: Parser.ChunkEnqueueTime
histogram.summary: The time it takes to enqueue a ParsedChunk onto the shared parser queue.

histogram.name: Parser.DiscardedTokenCount
histogram.summary: The number of speculative tokens discarded by the parser when a rewind is needed.

histogram.name: Parser.PeakPendingChunkCount
histogram.summary: The maximum number of pending Chunks in the ParsedChunkQueue after the parser is detached.

histogram.name: Parser.PeakPendingTokenCount
histogram.summary: The maximum number of pending tokens in the ParsedChunkQueue after the parser is detached.

histogram.name: Parser.PreloadTokenizeDelay
histogram.summary: The delay from when bytes are received on the main thread to when they are tokenized and preloads are sent back to the main parser. Always a greater time than the time emitted to Parser.AppendBytesDelay.

histogram.name: PartitionAlloc.CommittedSize
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 07/2019. Use Memory.Renderer.* and Memory.Experimental.Renderer2.* instead.
histogram.summary: The committed memory size in PartitionAlloc. The value is reported when we see the highest memory usage we've ever seen in the renderer process.

histogram.name: PartnerBookmark.Count
histogram.summary: The number of partner bookmark entries. Note that zero would be under-represented because reading is throttled if the last result is zero. Logged when using the bookmark, not skipped (PartnerBookmark.Skipped == False), and at most once per cold start. Note that the distribution is weighted by bookmark usage.

histogram.name: PartnerBookmark.Count2
histogram.summary: The number of partner bookmark entries. Logged when using the bookmark, and at most once per cold start. Note that the distribution is weighted by bookmark usage.

histogram.name: PartnerBookmark.FaviconThrottleFetchResult
histogram.summary: The response we got back from our favicon fetching throttler, once for each bookmark at each cold start. Only recorded on Android.

histogram.name: PartnerBookmark.LoadingTime
histogram.summary: The time spent on loading partner bookmarks, from kickOffReading() to bookmarkModelLoaded() callback.

histogram.name: PartnerBookmark.Null
histogram.obsolete: No longer recorded as of Apr 2018
histogram.summary: Whether there's no partner bookmark provider. By default, the system image contains a dummy partner bookmarks provider that provides zero entries. Logged when using the bookmark, not skipped (PartnerBookmark.Skipped == False), and at most once per cold start.

histogram.name: PartnerBookmark.Skipped
histogram.obsolete: No longer recorded as of Apr 2018
histogram.summary: Whether reading of partner bookmark is skipped. Logged when using the bookmark, and at most once per cold start.

histogram.name: PartnerBookmark.TimeSinceLastEmptyRead
histogram.summary: When trying to load the partner bookmarks, if no partner bookmark is read last time, record the time elapsed since then. It is recorded no matter whether loading would be skipped.

histogram.name: PartnerBookmark.TimeSinceLastEmptyRead2
histogram.obsolete: No longer recorded as of Apr 2018
histogram.summary: When trying to load the partner bookmarks, if no partner bookmark is read last time, record the time elapsed since then. It is recorded no matter whether loading would be skipped.

histogram.name: PartnerBookmarksFaviconThrottle.NumEntries
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019-07. See crbug.com/975263 for wrap-up analysis.
histogram.summary: Number of elements read from the partner bookmarks favicon cache, recorded once per cold start when reading partner bookmarks. Only recorded on Android.

histogram.name: PasswordBubble.DisplayDisposition
histogram.summary: When the password management bubble opened, what state was it in?

histogram.name: PasswordGeneration.Event
histogram.summary:
Measures the frequency of various password generation events.
Note that this histogram is logged from the renderer process, and consequently the numbers should not be directly compared to the other PasswordGeneration.* histograms, which are logged from the browser process. Histograms logged in different processes are lost at different rates, which introduces systematic bias between histograms logged in the renderer process vs. those logged in the browser process.

histogram.name: PasswordGeneration.GeneratedPasswordWasEdited
histogram.summary: Measures the frequency of user editing of generated passwords. Uploaded once per generated password save. Applicable to automatic and manual generations.

histogram.name: PasswordGeneration.IsTriggeredManually
histogram.summary: Measures the frequency of manually triggered password generations.

histogram.name: PasswordGeneration.PresaveConflict
histogram.summary: Records whether there is a username conflict with existing credentials when the user clicks 'Generate Password'.

histogram.name: PasswordGeneration.SubmissionAvailableEvent
histogram.summary: Measures the frequency of submission events for passwords that could have been generated, but the user didn't choose to use the feature. This is to compare with PasswordGeneration.SubmssionEvent.

histogram.name: PasswordGeneration.SubmissionEvent
histogram.summary: Measures the frequency of submission events for generated passwords. This is similar to PasswordManager.ActionsTakenWithPsl but only tracks events which are interesting for generated passwords.

histogram.name: PasswordGeneration.UploadStarted
histogram.summary: The number of times that we try to upload a form that we believe should trigger password generation. False means that something about the form would not allow us to try upload (not an Autofillable field, uploading disabled, Autofill servers in backoff, etc.). True does not mean that the upload actually completed successfully, just that it was started.

histogram.name: PasswordGeneration.UserDecision
histogram.summary: Records user-triggered events related to a generated password. Only the last occurring event is logged. The recording takes place when the password form is destroyed.

histogram.name: PasswordGeneration.UserEvent
histogram.summary: Records user-triggered events related to a generated password. Each event is logged at most once per lifetime of the generated password.

histogram.name: PasswordHash.CreateTime
histogram.summary: Time required to create the local hash of the user's GAIA password.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AbleToSavePasswordsOnSuccessfulLogin
histogram.summary: Records attempts to prompt user to save a password when password store is not ready for saving passwords due to an initialization error. Recorded once per form submission.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AboutBlankPasswordSubmission
histogram.summary: Records attempts to submit a password on a form in an about:blank frame, indicating whether this attempt is for a main frame or subframe. Recorded once per form submission.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AcceptedSaveUpdateSubmissionIndicatorEvent
histogram.summary: The type of event that the Password Manager used for detecting a password form submission when saving or updating a password. Recorded each time a user accepts a password save/update prompt.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AccessPasswordInSettings
histogram.summary: Recorded whenever the user accesses the passwords in the settings page.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AccountChooserDialog
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of Chrome 56. See the histograms for one and more accounts.
histogram.summary: The dismissal reason of the account chooser.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AccountChooserDialogAccounts
histogram.summary: The number of accounts displayed in the account chooser.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AccountChooserDialogEmptyAvatars
histogram.summary: The count of the placeholder avatars shown in the account chooser.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AccountChooserDialogMultipleAccounts
histogram.summary: The dismissal reason of the account chooser with multiple accounts.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AccountChooserDialogOneAccount
histogram.summary: The dismissal reason of the account chooser with one account.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AccountChooserDialogUsability
histogram.summary: Whether the account chooser includes an empty username or any duplicates

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AccountsPerSite
histogram.obsolete: Removed in M79 in favor of PasswordManager.AccountsPerSiteHiRes.
histogram.summary: The number of accounts stored per site in the password manager (one event per site)

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AccountsPerSiteHiRes
histogram.summary: The number of accounts stored per site in the password manager's profile-scoped store. Records one event per site, split by whether created by the user or generated by Chrome, and further by whether the user used sync with custom passphrase or not.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AccountsReusingPassword
histogram.summary:
Records, for each account, the number of other accounts (across all sites and usernames) that reuse the same password. Only non-blacklisted, HTML-form-based accounts are considered.
For each account, samples will only be recorded into the histograms that have the first suffix corresponding to whether that account was saved on a secure website or not. The second suffix is used to further categorize the other accounts that reuse the same password based on how they relate to the account in question, and whether they are secure. The number of other accounts in each category will be reported into histogram with the respective second suffixes.
Metrics are collected once per browser start-up. In case of multiple profiles, the counts are for the profile for which a WebContents is first created.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AccountStorage.SignedInAccountFoundDuringOptOut
histogram.summary: Records whether a signed-in account was still found by the time Chrome attempted to opt out the user of passwords account storage and clear the associated settings. If no signed-in account is found, the opt-out and clean up won't happen. Recorded only in Win/Mac/Linux, when opting out of passwords account storage. One example would be when the user clicks the opt-out button in chrome://settings/passwords.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AccountStorageUserStateDuration
histogram.summary:
The amount of active browsing time that was spent in different user states related to the PasswordManager's account-scoped storage.
This time is measured from when the user starts interacting with the browser until either they stopped interacting with the browser (as determined by DesktopSessionDurationTracker) or their user state changes. These end-points are when the metric is emitted.
Because a duration can end either because the user became inactive or because their status changed, the distribution of individual durations measured are unlikely to be helpful. Instead, please compare total durations.
This metric is only recorded on Win/Mac/Linux.
One caveat with this metric is that if the user has multiple profiles open at the same time, their session activity will be logged from all the profiles, instead of only from the profile that the user is using at that moment.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AccountStore.AccountsPerSiteHiRes
histogram.summary: The number of accounts stored per site in the password manager's account-scoped store. Records one event per site, split by whether created by the user or generated by Chrome, and further by whether the user used sync with custom passphrase or not.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AccountStore.BlacklistedSitesHiRes
histogram.summary: The total number of sites that the user has blacklisted in the password manager's account-scoped store. Recorded by iterating over stored passwords once per run of Chrome.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AccountStore.EmptyUsernames.CountInDatabase
histogram.summary: Number of password forms with empty username in the account-scoped Login Database. This is recorded on startup.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AccountStore.InaccessiblePasswords
histogram.summary: The number of saved passwords in the account-scoped store that could not be decrypted. Recorded once for the profile on startup.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AccountStore.TimesPasswordUsed
histogram.summary: The number of times each saved password from the account-scoped storage has been used to log in. Split by whether created by the user or generated by Chrome, and further by whether the user used sync with custom passphrase or not. Recorded by iterating over stored passwords once per browser run, soon after startup.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AccountStore.TotalAccountsHiRes.ByType
histogram.summary: The number of accounts stored in the password manager's account-scoped store (across all sites), split by whether created by the user or generated by Chrome, and further by whether the user used sync with custom passphrase or not.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AccountStore.TotalAccountsHiRes.WithScheme
histogram.summary: The number of accounts stored in the password manager's account-scoped store (across all sites) for origins with the scheme (e.g., HTTP, HTTPS, FTP) as given in the histogram suffix. For each scheme, the count is recorded once per browser start-up. (In case of multiple profiles, the counts are for the profile that first has a WebContents created.)

histogram.name: PasswordManager.ActionsTaken
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of Chrome 32. See PasswordManagerActionsTakenWithPsl
histogram.summary: Stats documenting how we handle every form containing a password, bucketed by the actions taken.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.ActionsTakenOnNonSecureForm
histogram.summary: Stats documenting how we handle every form containing a password on a non-secure page, bucketed by the actions taken.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.ActionsTakenV3
histogram.summary: Stats documenting how we handle every form containing a password, bucketed by the actions taken.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.ActionsTakenWithPsl
histogram.summary: Stats documenting how we handle every form containing a password, bucketed by the actions taken.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AffiliationBackend.FetchSize
histogram.summary: The number of facets for which affiliation information was requested in a network fetch. Recorded for each network fetch.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AffiliationBackend.FirstFetchDelay
histogram.summary: The time elapsed between creation of the AffiliationBackend and the first time it needed to issue a network fetch.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AffiliationBackend.SubsequentFetchDelay
histogram.summary: The elapsed time between subsequent network fetches. Recorded whenever the AffiliationBackend initiated a network fetch, regardless of success or failure.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AffiliationDummyData.RequestResultCount
histogram.obsolete: No longer recorded as of May 2015.
histogram.summary: The number of facets affiliated with a dummy Web facet, according to the affiliation information retrieved from the cache. Recorded for each dummy Web facet, once shortly after start-up, and then periodically every hour; but only if getting affiliations succeeded for the Web facet.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AffiliationDummyData.RequestSuccess
histogram.obsolete: No longer recorded as of May 2015.
histogram.summary: Whether or not affiliations of a dummy Web facet could be successfully retrieved from the cache. Recorded for each dummy Web facet, once shortly after start-up, and then periodically every hour.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AffiliationFetcher.FetchErrorCode
histogram.summary: The network error code, as reported by the underlying URLFetcher. Recorded only for each network fetch that failed due to network/server errors.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AffiliationFetcher.FetchHttpResponseCode
histogram.summary: The HTTP response code, as reported by the underlying URLFetcher. Recorded only for each network fetch that failed due to network/server errors.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AffiliationFetcher.FetchResult
histogram.summary: Whether the network fetch succeeded, failed due to network/server errors, or contained malformed data. Recorded for each network fetch.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AllowToCollectURLBubble.UIDismissalReason
histogram.obsolete: The bubble isn't shown anymore. Become obsolete in Feb. 2015.
histogram.summary: Why was "Allow to collect URL?" bubble closed?

histogram.name: PasswordManager.Android.ExportPasswordsProgressBarUsage
histogram.obsolete: No longer recorded as of June 2019.
histogram.summary: Records what happened to the progress bar during exporting passwords when its dismissal was requested.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.Android.PasswordCredentialEntry
histogram.summary: Records the action taken with a password credential entry in the preferences on Android.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.Android.PasswordCredentialEntry.Password
histogram.summary: Records the action taken with a password of a password credential entry in the preferences on Android.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.Android.PasswordCredentialEntry.Username
histogram.summary: Records the action taken with a username of a password credential entry in the preferences on Android.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.Android.PasswordCredentialEntry.Website
histogram.summary: Records the action taken with a website of a password credential entry on Android.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.Android.PasswordExceptionEntry
histogram.summary: Records the action taken with a password exception entry in the preferences on Android.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.Android.PasswordExceptionEntry.Website
histogram.summary: Records the action taken with a website of a password exception entry in the preferences on Android.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.Android.PasswordSearchTriggered
histogram.obsolete: No longer recorded as of June 2019.
histogram.summary: Records whether a user triggered the search function while visiting the password preferences page on Android.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.ApplySyncChanges.AddLoginSyncError
histogram.summary: Records different results upon adding a remote password to the password manager. It is recorded every time after receiving remote password incremental updates from the server.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.ApplySyncChanges.UpdateLoginSyncError
histogram.summary: Records different results upon updating a remote password to the password manager. It is recorded every time after receiving remote password incremental updates from the server.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.ApplySyncChangesState
histogram.summary: Records different states upon applying remote sync changes to the password manager. It recorded every time after receiving remote password incremental updates from the server.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AutocompletePopupSuppressedByGeneration
histogram.summary: If the password manager UI was suppressed because generation UI was already being displayed for the same field.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.AutoSigninFirstRunDialog
histogram.summary: The dismissal reason of the auto-signin first run experience.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.BlacklistedDuplicates
histogram.obsolete: Corresponding clean-up logic got introduced in M70 and has run its course.
histogram.summary: The number of duplicates forms from the password store. Recorded once for the profile on startup.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.BlacklistedSites
histogram.obsolete: Removed in M76 in favor of PasswordManager.BlacklistedSitesHiRes.
histogram.summary: The total number of sites that the user has blacklisted in the password manager's profile-scoped store. Recorded by iterating over stored passwords once per run of Chrome.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.BlacklistedSites.NeedNormalization
histogram.obsolete: Corresponding clean-up logic got introduced in M76 and has run its course.
histogram.summary: Records once on startup whether the blacklisted sites in the password store need to be normalized.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.BlacklistedSites.NeedRemoveBlacklistDuplicates
histogram.obsolete: Corresponding clean-up logic got introduced in M70 and has run its course.
histogram.summary: Records once on startup whether the blacklisted sites in the password store need to be cleared of duplications.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.BlacklistedSites.NeedToBeCleaned
histogram.summary: Records once on startup whether the blacklisted sites in the password store need to be updated to remove username/password. Can be removed around Chrome M69 (https://crbug.com/817754).

histogram.name: PasswordManager.BlacklistedSites.PreventedAddingDuplicates
histogram.summary: Boolean indicating whether adding a blacklist entry was prevented due to an already existing entry. Recorded after every blacklist site submission.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.BlacklistedSitesHiRes
histogram.summary: The total number of sites that the user has blacklisted. Recorded by iterating over stored passwords once per run of Chrome.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.BubbleSuppression.AccountsInStatisticsTable
histogram.summary: The number of accounts stored in password_manager::StatisticsTable. These are accounts for which the user ignored the save bubble at least once. The count is recorded once per browser start-up. (In case of multiple profiles, the counts are for the profile that first has a WebContents created.)

histogram.name: PasswordManager.BubbleSuppression.AccountsWithSuppressedBubble
histogram.summary: The number of accounts that do not trigger password save prompts anymore. The count is recorded once per browser start-up. (In case of multiple profiles, the counts are for the profile that first has a WebContents created.)

histogram.name: PasswordManager.BubbleSuppression.DomainsWithSuppressedBubble
histogram.summary: The number of domains for which at least one account exists that does not trigger password save prompts anymore. The count is recorded once per browser start-up. (In case of multiple profiles, the counts are for the profile that first has a WebContents created.)

histogram.name: PasswordManager.BulkCheck.CanceledCredentials
histogram.summary: Count of credentials initially to be checked when the password bulk check was canceled.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.BulkCheck.CanceledTime
histogram.summary: The time the password bulk check was running until canceled by the user.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.BulkCheck.CheckedCredentials
histogram.summary: Count of credentials analyzed when the password bulk check completed successfully.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.BulkCheck.Error
histogram.summary: Error encountered during the password bulk check.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.BulkCheck.LeaksFound
histogram.summary: Count of leaked credentials found when the password bulk check completed.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.BulkCheck.PasswordCheckReferrer
histogram.summary: Referrer of a navigation to the Password Check page. Recorded when the user visits the Password Check settings page, either via native or Web UI.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.BulkCheck.Time
histogram.summary: The time it took for the password bulk check to complete successfully.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.BulkCheck.TimePerCredential
histogram.summary: The average time per credential when the password bulk check completed successfully.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.BulkCheck.UserAction
histogram.summary: User actions performed on the Password Check settings page.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.ButtonTitlePerformance.HasFormTag
histogram.summary: The time (ms) it takes to infer button titles within a form tag.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.ButtonTitlePerformance.NoFormTag
histogram.summary: The time (ms) it takes to infer button titles outside of form tags.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.CertificateErrorsWhileSeeingForms
histogram.summary: When the password manager sees new forms on the page, it records in this histogram whether there were any SSL certificate errors. The presence of SSL errors likely means that the password manger will stop working, so the reporting is done at the last point when password manager is still guaranteed to be active. Some particular errors are distinguished, with the rest being reported in a catch-all bucket.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.ChromeSignInPageHashSaved
histogram.summary: Records whether a password hash was saved or not on Chrome sign-in page. Recorded whenever the user types a password on Chrome sign-in page.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.CleanedUpPasswords
histogram.summary: Collects positive number of undecryptable passwords that were successfully deleted when syncing was enabled. Called for sync user for each profile if there is at least one password deleted when merging logins from Sync and local database.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.CompromisedCredentials
histogram.summary: Collects the type of compromised credential when it is added, updated, or removed from the table. Compromised credentials are saved passwords that are added when they are leaked or phished, updated when signon_realm and/or username is changed, or removed when the password is dropped from the password_manager or the password has changed.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.CompromisedCredentials.CountLeaked
histogram.summary: Count of known leaked credentials in the database on start-up.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.CompromisedCredentials.CountLeakedAfterBulkCheck
histogram.summary: Count of known leaked credentials in the database on start-up if the password bulk check was executed at least once.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.CompromisedCredentials.CountPhished
histogram.summary: Count of known phished credentials in the database on start-up.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.CredentialsWithDuplicates
histogram.summary: The number of stored (non-blacklisted) credentials for which "duplicates" exist. A duplicate is a credential with the same signon realm, username, and password - i.e. indistinguishable from the user's point of view. This records the number of *sets* of duplicated credentials; the number of credentials in each set of duplicates is not recorded. Recorded once per browser start-up. (In case of multiple profiles, the counts are for the profile that first has a WebContents created.)

histogram.name: PasswordManager.CredentialsWithMismatchedDuplicates
histogram.summary: The number of stored (non-blacklisted) credentials for which "mismatched duplicates" exist. A mismatched duplicate is a credential with the same signon realm and username, but different password. This records the number of *sets* of duplicated credentials; the number of credentials in each set of duplicates is not recorded. Recorded once per browser start-up. (In case of multiple profiles, the counts are for the profile that first has a WebContents created.)

histogram.name: PasswordManager.DeleteCorruptedPasswordsResult
histogram.summary: Records the result of removing passwords that cannot be decrypted when retrieving passwords from LoginDatabase. Called for Mac users for each profile.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.DeleteUndecryptableLoginsReturnValue
histogram.summary: Records the return value of the DeleteUndecryptableLogins method in the LoginDatabase class. Called for sync user and for each profile when merging logins from Sync and local database.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.DropdownShown.OffTheRecord
histogram.summary: Records availability of password manager suggestions in regular and off-the-record modes.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.DynamicFormChanges
histogram.summary: Records a bitmask of password form changes after the first time this form is seen by Password Manager. Recorded once per a form when the form is removed from DOM. Bit 0 - changes in number of fields, bit 1 - changes in fields renderer ids, bit 2 - changes in autocomplete attributes, bit 3 - changes in form control types.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.EditsInSaveBubble
histogram.summary: Indication whether users change the credentials (username and password) in the save bubble of the password manager, considering only cases where the user saves a new credential or updates a credential.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.EmptyUsernames.CountInDatabase
histogram.summary: Number of password forms with empty username in the profile-scoped Login Database. This is recorded on startup.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.EmptyUsernames.FormWithoutUsernameFieldIsPasswordChangeForm
histogram.summary: When parsing a password form, and no username field is detected, whether this was a password change form.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.EmptyUsernames.OfferedToSave
histogram.summary: When offering to save a password, whether the username is empty.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.EmptyUsernames.ParsedUsernameField
histogram.summary: When parsing a password form, whether a username field is detected.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.EmptyUsernames.PasswordFieldCount
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 09/2018. The investigation this metric was supporting has been concluded in 2016.
histogram.summary: The number of password fields for password forms that do not have a username field. This is recorded every time such a password form is successfully parsed. Note that the parsing is attempted when the form is encountered (i.e when the document is loaded) and also when it is submitted.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.EmptyUsernames.TextAndPasswordFieldCount
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 09/2018. The investigation this metric was supporting has been concluded in 2016.
histogram.summary: The total number of text and password fields for password forms that do not have a username field. This is recorded every time such a password form is successfully parsed. Note that the parsing is attempted when the form is encountered (i.e when the document is loaded) and also when it is submitted.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.EmptyUsernames.WithoutCorrespondingNonempty
histogram.summary: Number of password forms with empty username in the Login Database for which there is not another password form from the same realm with a nonempty username. In other words, number of password forms with empty username which we do not suspect to be reauthentication forms.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.Enabled
histogram.summary: Indicates whether the password manager is enabled when a tab is opened. This includes prerendered tabs.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.ExportedPasswordsPerUserInCSV
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 2019/07. Password exporting has been rolled out.
histogram.summary: The number of passwords exported in CSV format per user.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.ExportPasswordsToCSVResult
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 2019/07. Password exporting has been rolled out.
histogram.summary: The success or error type of exporting passwords into CSV.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.FieldInfoTableRows
histogram.summary: Records number of rows in FieldInfoTable, which contains locally learned predictions about field types. Recorded once for the profile on startup.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.FilledCredentialWasFromAndroidApp
histogram.summary: When a credential is filled, whether it comes from an Android app.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.FillingAssistance
histogram.summary:
This metric records the user experience with regards to passwords filling. It is only recorded on password form submissions that are considered to be successful.
The first 4 buckets (0..3) are ranging from the best filling behavior (automatic filling on page load without user interaction necessary) to the worst (the user had to manually type a password that was already saved).
The following buckets (4..5) indicate cases when it was impossible to fill credentials because unknown credentials were submitted, meaning that the submitted password was not saved before.
The last bucket (6) corresponds to the strange cases, where the submitted form has neither user input nor autofilled data in password fields. This might indicate third-party password manager use, or might be a measurement/submission classification error.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.FillingSource
histogram.summary: This metric records the source of filled passwords: The profile-scoped store, the account-scoped store, both of them, or none of them (if the user typed the password manually). It is only recorded on password form submissions that are considered to be successful.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.FillSuggestionsIncludeAndroidAppCredentials
histogram.summary: When offering to fill the username and password, whether at least one of the credentials in the dropdown comes from an Android app.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.FirstRendererFillingResult
histogram.summary:
Records whether the PasswordAutofillAgent in the renderer manages to fill credentials as instructed by the browser or records a failure reason otherwise. Only the outcome of the first attempt to fill is recorded.
This is replicated as PasswordForm.Fill.FirstFillingResultInRenderer in UKM.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.FirstWaitForUsernameReason
histogram.summary:
This metric records why the browser instructs the renderer not to fill the credentials on page load but to wait for the user to confirm the credential to be filled. Only the value of the first decision to fill is recorded.
This is replicated as PasswordForm.Fill.FirstWaitForUsernameReason in UKM.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.FormDataDeserializationStatus
histogram.summary: The success or failure of deserializing saved FormData. Failure either indicates corrupted data or the presence of bugs in the serialization or deserialization code.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.GeneratedFormHasNoFormManager
histogram.summary: When the generation state of a password was changed, records whether an existing form corresponding to the password was found.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.GetMediated
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 05/2017. This metric has been replaced by PasswordManager.MediationOptional.
histogram.summary: Tracks result of navigator.credentials.get() with unmediated=false. That is the result of account chooser.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.GetUnmediated
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 05/2017. This metric has been replaced by PasswordManager.MediationSilent.
histogram.summary: Tracks result of navigator.credentials.get() with unmediated=true. That is the result of auto sign-in.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.GooglePasswordManager.NavigationResult
histogram.summary: The result of an attempted navigation to the Google Password Manager. Recorded when a signed-in and password syncing user navigates to https://passwords.google.com by clicking a link.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.GooglePasswordManager.TimeToFailure
histogram.summary: The time it took for a failed navigation to the Google Password Manager. Recorded when a signed-in and password syncing user navigates to https://passwords.google.com by clicking a link and the navigation fails before timing out.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.GooglePasswordManager.TimeToSuccess
histogram.summary: The time it took for a successful navigation to the Google Password Manager. Recorded when a signed-in and password syncing user navigates to https://passwords.google.com by clicking a link and the navigation succeeds before timing out.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.HttpCredentials
histogram.summary: Breakdown of HTTP credentials with regard to the existence of a HTTPS credential with the same username and whether the corresponding website has HSTS enabled. Recorded once for each HTTP credential of the profile on startup.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.HttpCredentialsWithConflictingHttpsCredential
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of M72. Superseded by PasswordManager.HttpCredentials.
histogram.summary: Number of HTTP credentials, with HSTS enabled as given in the histogram suffix, for which a conflicting (i.e.same host and username, but different password) HTTPS credential exists. Recorded once for the profile on startup.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.HttpCredentialsWithEquivalentHttpsCredential
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of M72. Superseded by PasswordManager.HttpCredentials.
histogram.summary: Number of HTTP credentials, with HSTS enabled as given in the histogram suffix, for which an equivalent (i.e. same host, username and password) HTTPS credential exists. Recorded once for the profile on startup.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.HttpCredentialsWithoutMatchingHttpsCredential
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of M72. Superseded by PasswordManager.HttpCredentials.
histogram.summary: Number of HTTP credential, with HSTS enabled as given in the histogram suffix, for which no HTTPS credential for the same username exists. Recorded once for the profile on startup.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.HttpPasswordMigrationCount
histogram.summary: Number of HTTP saved passwords that were migrated to HTTPS. Recorded on HTTPS password form load when there are no credentials saved.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.HttpPasswordMigrationMode
histogram.summary: The mode of migration applied to HTTP passwords migrating to HTTPS. Recorded on HTTPS password form load when there are no credentials saved.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.IE7LookupResult
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 07/2018. This metric was deleted at https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/chromium/src/+/1131495.
histogram.summary: The result of importing passwords stored in IE7 into Chrome's password store.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.ImportedPasswordsPerUserInCSV
histogram.summary: The number of passwords imported in CSV format per user

histogram.name: PasswordManager.ImportPasswordFromCSVResult
histogram.summary: The response code of successful or failed password import attempt from a CSV file.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.InaccessiblePasswords
histogram.summary: The number of saved passwords in the profile-scoped store that could not be decrypted. Recorded once for the profile on startup.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.InfoBarResponse
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 03/2016. This metric has been replaced by PasswordManager.UIDismissalReason.
histogram.summary: The distribution of responses to the "Do you want Chrome to remember this password"? info bar prompt.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.InvalidtHttpsCredentialsNeedToBeCleared
histogram.obsolete: Corresponding clean-up logic got introduced in M71 and has run its course.
histogram.summary: Records once on startup whether forms with wrong signon_realm created by HTTP to HTTPS migration need to be removed. See https://crbug.com/881731 for more details.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.IsSyncPasswordHashSaved
histogram.summary: This metric reports whether a sync password hash is saved. Recorded shortly after Chrome startup.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.IsSyncPasswordHashSavedForAdvancedProtectionUser
histogram.summary: This metric is recorded shortly after Chrome Startup, only for Advanced Protection users. This is a subset of PasswordManager.IsSyncPasswordHashSaved.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.ItemSelected.OffTheRecord
histogram.summary: Records selection of password manager suggestions in regular and off-the-record modes.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.JavaScriptOnlyValueInSubmittedForm
histogram.summary: Records whether a successfully submitted password form has only values that came from JavaScript.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.KeychainMigration.NumChromeOwnedInaccessiblePasswords
histogram.summary: After migration from the Mac Keychain fails, records the number of passwords in the Keychain owned by Chrome but not accessible by it.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.KeychainMigration.NumFailedPasswords
histogram.summary: After migration from the Mac Keychain fails, records the number of passwords in the Chrome Password Manager database for which the corresponding values in the Keychain are not accessible by Chrome.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.KeychainMigration.NumPasswordsOnFailure
histogram.summary: After migration from the Mac Keychain fails, records the number of passwords in the Chrome Password Manager database to be migrated. As there is no partial migration, it's the total number of passwords in the DB.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.KeychainMigration.Status
histogram.summary: The status of passwords migration from the Keychain. It's recorded shortly after startup.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.KWalletDeserializationStatus
histogram.summary: The success or failure of deserializing PasswordForms in KWallet. Failure either indicates corrupted data or the presense of bugs in the deserialization code.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.LeakDetection.AccessTokenFetchStatus
histogram.summary: Status of an attempted fetch of an access token. Recorded when a token fetch completes.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.LeakDetection.AccessTokenNetErrorCode
histogram.summary: Net Error Code of a failed attempt to fetch an access token. Recorded when a token fetch completes with an error state.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.LeakDetection.AnalyzeSingleLeakResponseResult
histogram.summary: Result of analyzing a single leak response.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.LeakDetection.AnalyzeSingleLeakResponseTime
histogram.summary: The time it took to analyze a single leak lookup response.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.LeakDetection.DialogDismissalReason
histogram.summary: Reason of the dismissal of the leak detection dialog. Recorded when a leaked credential is detected and the dialog is shown.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.LeakDetection.Enabled
histogram.summary: Indicates whether the password manager leak detection feature is enabled. Recorded for each profile on browser start-up.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.LeakDetection.Error
histogram.summary: Shows the error that happened in password leak detection on sign-in.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.LeakDetection.HttpResponseCode
histogram.summary: HTTP response code of a leak lookup request. Only recoded for each network fetch that failed due to network/server errors.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.LeakDetection.IsPasswordReused
histogram.summary: When a password is determined as leaked, records if it's saved for other sites.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.LeakDetection.IsPasswordSaved
histogram.summary: When a password is determined as leaked, records if it's saved in Chrome.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.LeakDetection.IsSyncing
histogram.summary: When a password is determined as leaked, records if the user is syncing passwords without passphrase.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.LeakDetection.LookupSingleLeakResponseResult
histogram.summary: Whether the network fetch succeeded, failed due to network/server errors, or contained malformed data. Recorded for each leak lookup.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.LeakDetection.NetErrorCode
histogram.summary: Network error code of a leak lookup request. Only recoded for each network fetch that failed due to network/server errors.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.LeakDetection.NotifyIsLeakedTime
histogram.summary: The time it took from starting a leak check to notifying the user about a leak.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.LeakDetection.ObtainAccessTokenTime
histogram.summary: The time it took to obtain an access token for an authenticated leak lookup request.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.LeakDetection.PrepareSingleLeakRequestTime
histogram.summary: The time it took to prepare the payload for a single leak lookup request.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.LeakDetection.ReceiveSingleLeakResponseTime
histogram.summary: The time it took to receive a server response after issuing a request for a single leak lookup.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.LeakDetection.SingleLeakResponsePrefixes
histogram.summary: The number of matched prefixes in a single leak lookup response. Only recorded if the network response was wellformed.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.LeakDetection.SingleLeakResponseSize
histogram.summary: The size of a single leak lookup response in bytes. Only recorded if there was no network or server error.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.LinuxBackendMigration.Adoption
histogram.summary: Whether the password store has been migrated to an encrypted login database. This is recorded on startup, before any migration attempts.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.LinuxBackendMigration.AttemptResult
histogram.summary: Whether the password store has been migrated to an encrypted login database. This is recorded at the end of a migration attempt.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.LinuxBackendMigration.TimeIntoEncrypted
histogram.summary: The time it took to read credentials from the native backend and write them into the encrypted login database. This is logged once per migration (normally once per client), at the end of copying into the database. The migration is triggered on the first operation to the password store.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.LinuxBackendMigration.TimeIntoNative
histogram.summary: The time it took to read credentials from the unencrypted login database and insert them into the native backend. This is logged once per migration (normally once per client), at the end of copying into the backend. The migration is triggered on the first operation to the password store.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.LinuxBackendStatistics
