histogram.summary: Information about usage of password storage backends on Linux. It also includes whether a command line flag for a specific backend is given.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.LoginDatabaseFailedVersion
histogram.summary: The version of LoginDatabase if the migration to a new one failed.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.LoginDatabaseInit
histogram.summary: An error on LoginDatabase initialization.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.ManagePasswordsReferrer
histogram.summary: Referrer of a navigation to the "Manage Passwords" page. Recorded when the user attempts to see their list of passwords, either via native or Web UI.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.ManagePasswordsReferrerSignedInAndSyncing
histogram.summary: Referrer of a navigation to the "Manage Passwords" page. Recorded only for signed in users syncing passwords without a custom passprase, when the user attempts to see their list of passwords.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.MediationOptional
histogram.summary: Tracks result of navigator.credentials.get() with mediation="optional". That is the result of account chooser.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.MediationRequired
histogram.summary: Tracks result of navigator.credentials.get() with mediation="required". That is the result of forced account chooser.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.MediationSilent
histogram.summary: Tracks result of navigator.credentials.get() with mediation="silent". That is the result of auto sign-in.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.MergeSyncData.AddLoginSyncError
histogram.summary: Records different results upon adding a remote password to the password manager. It is recorded during the initial sync when merging remote and local data.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.MergeSyncData.UpdateLoginSyncError
histogram.summary: Records different results upon updating a remote password in the password manager. It is recorded during the initial sync when merging remote and local data.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.MultiAccountPasswordUpdateAction
histogram.summary: A user action when a password update bubble with multiple accounts is shown.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.NewlySavedPasswordIsGenerated
histogram.summary: For a newly saved password, whether the password was generated by Chrome or typed manually by the user.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.NonSyncPasswordHashChange
histogram.summary: This metric reports a type of a password hash change event where the associated account is not syncing. Recorded when a non sync password hash is saved or cleared.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.NumFormManagersCleared
histogram.summary: Records how many FormManagers are deleted on the main frame navigation. This metric is useful for estimation of memory footprint.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.NumFormsExtractedIOS
histogram.summary: Records the number of password forms extracted by PasswordFormHelper at the moment of submission. This metric is needed to check the assumption that received and parsed message contains only one form at a a time.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.NumPasswordsDeletedByBulkDelete
histogram.summary: Number of passwords deleted when the user chooses to clear passwords via the clear browsing data UI.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.NumPasswordsDeletedDuringRollback
histogram.summary: Number of passwords deleted when browsing data is cleared during rollback.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.NumPasswordsDeletedWhenBlacklisting
histogram.summary: When the user chooses to never remember passwords for a form, we remove all previously saved credentials for that form. This is the count of those credentials.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.NumPasswordsNotShown
histogram.summary: The password manager only shows those credentials that are considered the best match for a particular form. This stat keep track of the credentials that were not as good of a match and were suppressed.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.Onboarding.ResultOfSavingFlow
histogram.summary: Result of the combined saving flow consisting of the potentially shown onboarding dialog and the save infobar. Recorded when the user is prompted to save their password.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.Onboarding.ResultOfSavingFlowAfterOnboarding
histogram.summary: Result of the combined saving flow consisting of the shown onboarding dialog and the save infobar. Recorded when the user is propmpted to save their password only if the onboarding was actually shown.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.Onboarding.State
histogram.summary: The current state of showing the password manager onboarding to the user. Recorded on startup.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.Onboarding.UIDismissalReason
histogram.summary: Reason of dismissal of the password manager onboarding dialog. Recorded when the user is propmpted to save their password and the dialog is shown.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.OsPasswordStatus
histogram.summary: Indicates whether the user's OS password is blank or not at browser startup.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.OtherPossibleUsernamesUsage
histogram.summary: Breakdown of how other possible usernames are displayed. Recorded every time we autofill a password form.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.PasswordDropdownItemSelected
histogram.summary: Logs the type of the suggestion in the password dropdown when selected.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.PasswordDropdownShown
histogram.summary: Logs the state of the password dropdown when it's shown.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.PasswordReuse.MainFrameMatchCsdWhitelist
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of May 17 2017. Replaced by PasswordProtection.RequestOutcome.ProtectedPasswordEntry.
histogram.summary:
A password reuse is an event when the user typed a string that is equal to a saved password on another domain, and this saved password is called reused password.
This metric shows whether a password reuse happens on a page whose main frame url matches CSD whitelist.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.PasswordReuse.NumberOfMatches
histogram.summary:
This metric shows how many saved credentials (num unique registry-controlled domains) have password that's equal to the reused password.
Before M63 this logged a "0" for sync-reuse.
A "password reuse" is when the user typed a string that is equal to a saved password on another domain or is equal to the sync password on a non google-login domain.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.PasswordReuse.PasswordFieldDetected
histogram.summary:
This metric reports whether a password field had been detected on a page when a password reuse happened.
A "password reuse" is when the user typed a string that is equal to a saved password on another domain or is equal to the sync password on a non google-login domain.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.PasswordReuse.PasswordLength
histogram.summary:
This metric shows the length of the reused password.
Change in M63: If multiple passwords are matched simultaneously, this logs the length of the longest password. Also, before M63, a "0" was logged for sync-reuse.
A "password reuse" is when the user typed a string that is equal to a saved password on another domain or is equal to the sync password on a non google-login domain.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.PasswordReuse.TotalPasswords
histogram.summary:
This metric shows how many credentials are stored in Password Manager when a password reuse happened. This counts unique registry-controlled domains with passwords saved.
A "password reuse" is when the user typed a string that is equal to a saved password on another domain or is equal to the sync password on a non google-login domain.
During M61/M62 this metric logged a "1" for every sync-reuse. It was fixed in M63 such that it logs actual Password Manager credential count.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.PasswordSavedWithManualFallback
histogram.summary: Measures whether users save passwords with automatic prompt or manual fallback.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.PasswordStoreInitResult
histogram.summary: Success rate of initialization of password store. Recorded for every user once on the Chrome profile startup.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.PasswordSyncState
histogram.summary: Whether the passwords are syncing and, if not, then what's causing sync failures. Recorded after the first sync attempt either succeeds or fails.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.PasswordUpdatedWithManualFallback
histogram.summary: Measures whether users update passwords with automatic prompt or manual fallback.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.PrefilledUsernameFillOutcome
histogram.summary: Records successful fills of prefilled username values known as placeholders and unsuccessful fills that were blocked because the prefilled value was not identified as a placeholder. Recorded once per PasswordAutofillAgent instance, when attempting to fill a password form that contains a username value which was prepopulated by the website.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.PresavedUpdateUIDismissalReason
histogram.summary: Why was the update password UI closed when a generated password caused a conflict?

histogram.name: PasswordManager.ProvisionalSaveFailure
histogram.summary: Breakdown of cases where a password is submitted, but we don't even try and save it. Recorded for every password form submit.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.PslDomainMatchTriggering
histogram.summary: Breakdown on trigger rate of providing a password form autofill entry based on matching stored information using the public suffix list (PSL) for possible matches. In addition, this metric also counts cases where a existing federated entry was successfully matched via PSL. For example, this includes cases where an existing federated credential for https://example.com was used for https://subdomain.example.com.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.QueryingSuppressedAccountsFinished
histogram.summary: Records, for each password form seen by the password manager, whether the PasswordStore responded quickly enough so that PasswordManager.SuppressedAccount histogram samples could be computed.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.ReauthToAccessPasswordInSettings
histogram.summary: Recorded whenever the user interacts with reauthentication in order to access the passwords in settings, or when such reauthentication is skipped for a well-defined reason.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.RemoveCompromisedCredentials
histogram.summary: Collects the cause of the change in the Password Store login which causes a call of a remove SQL statement on the compromised credentials table.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.RemoveCompromisedCredentials.RemoveReason
histogram.summary: Collects the cause of why remove SQL statement is called on the compromised credentials table.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.RemovedCorruptedPasswords
histogram.summary: Collects positive number of inaccessible passwords that were successfully deleted when retrieving them from the database. Recorded for Mac users.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.RequirementsSpecFetcher.HttpResponseCode
histogram.summary: HTTP response code of fetching a password requirements file.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.RequirementsSpecFetcher.NetErrorCode
histogram.summary: Network error code of fetching a password requirements file.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.RequirementsSpecFetcher.NetworkDuration
histogram.summary: Time until the lookup for a password requirements file completed (or was aborted).

histogram.name: PasswordManager.RequirementsSpecFetcher.Result
histogram.summary: Result of fetching requirements for password during password generation.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.ReusedPasswordType
histogram.summary: Records the type of password (e.g saved password, sync password, enterprise password etc) reused by the user. This is logged when a password reuse happens.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SavedEnterprisePasswordHashCount
histogram.summary: Records the number of enterprise password hashes that have been saved for password reuse detection. This is logged during the initialization of PasswordStore class.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SavedGaiaPasswordHashCount
histogram.summary: Records the number of Gaia password hashes that have been saved for password reuse detection. This is logged during the initialization of PasswordStore class.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SavePasswordPromptDisappearedQuickly
histogram.summary: Indicates whether the save password prompt disappeared in less than one second. This most likely indicates that the prompt was dismissed automatically, e.g. due to a page navigation, before the user was able to respond to the infobar.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SavePasswordPromptDisplayed
histogram.summary: Indicates whether the save password prompt was displayed.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SavePasswordPromptResponse
histogram.summary: Breakdown of which response the user selected from the save password prompt.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SaveUIDismissalReason
histogram.summary: Why was the save password UI (bubble or infobar) closed?

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SaveUIDismissalReasonAferUnblacklisting
histogram.summary: Why was the save password UI (bubble or infobar) closed? This is currently recorded only for Android and only after the user has unblacklisted an origin for saving from the accessory sheet.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SavingOnUsernameFirstFlow
histogram.summary: Records user actions when Chrome offers to save a username on a page which is considered to be a username first flow. Recorded on a successful submission.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SendPasswordFormToBrowserProcess
histogram.summary: Measures sending/not sending password forms to the browser process by PasswordAutofillAgent::SendPasswordForms.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SettingsReconciliation.InitialAndFinalValues
histogram.summary: Tracks the pair of initial values and pair of final values for the legacy preference for controlling the Chrome Password Manager and the new preference for controlling Smart Lock on Android. A single sample is recorded after sync has merged last snapshot and finished initialization for sync users, and on profile initialization for non-sync users.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SettingsReconciliation.InitialValues
histogram.summary: Tracks the pair of initial values for both for the legacy preference for controlling the Chrome Password Manager and new preference for controlling Smart Lock on Android. Sample is recorded on every profile initialization before reconciliation logic is taken place, e.g. when user logs in to browser, on a startup of a browser.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.ShouldBlockPasswordForSameOriginButDifferentScheme
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of M78. PasswordManager.ProvisionalSaveFailure contains strictly more information.
histogram.summary: This metric is recorded every time Chrome detects a password form submission. The credential might be proposed to be saved, or it might be blocked. It is blocked if the previously detected password form submission was successful, and the current submission is from a URL with the same origin, but a different and insecure scheme. That is, if the previously provisionally saved credential is for https://example.com, a new credential for http://example.com would be blocked. More details can be found in the doc associated with http://crbug.com/571580.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.ShouldShowAutoSignInFirstRunExperience
histogram.summary: Records if the user still has to click through the first run experience to benefit from auto sign-in behavior. Recorded on every start-up.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.ShouldShowPromptComparison
histogram.obsolete: Removed 08/2018 because the decision this should have helped with is now moot. See https://crbug.com/871509#c3.
histogram.summary: Records results of the new and the old algorithm for detecting whether to show a password prompt to the user. Recorded on each password form successful submission.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.ShowAllSavedPasswordsAcceptedContext
histogram.summary: Recorded when the "Show all saved passwords" fallback is accepted.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.ShowAllSavedPasswordsShownContext
histogram.summary: Recorded when the "Show all saved passwords" fallback is shown.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.ShowedFormNotSecureWarningOnCurrentNavigation
histogram.obsolete: Removed 05/2018 because the feature never launched.
histogram.summary: Recorded (at most once per main-frame navigation) when a password form shows a warning that the form is not secure.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SignInPromo
histogram.summary: User action on the Chrome Sync promo that appears in the password bubble. The promo offers to sign in to Chrome.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SignInPromoCountTilClick
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 12/16/16. New statistic is PasswordManager.SignInPromoCountTil*.
histogram.summary: The number of times the Sign In promo in the password bubble was shown before user clicked on it.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SignInPromoCountTilNoThanks
histogram.summary: The number of times the Sign In promo in the password bubble was shown before user clicked on "No thanks".

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SignInPromoCountTilSignIn
histogram.summary: The number of times the Sign In promo in the password bubble was shown before user clicked on "Sign in".

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SignInPromoDismissalCount
histogram.summary: The number of times the Sign In promo in the password bubble was implcitly dismissed. Recorded each time the promo is implicitly dismissed.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.StoreDecryptionResult
histogram.summary: Describes the result of decrypting a password value from the LoginDatabase.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.StorePasswordImportedFromCSVResult
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 14 September 2017, due to being no longer useful.
histogram.summary: Whether password manager stored passwords imported from CSV file successfully.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.StorePerformance.AddLogin
histogram.summary: The time (ms) it takes for the synchronous part of the AddLogin on the PasswordStore to complete.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.StorePerformance.GetLogin
histogram.summary: The time (ms) it takes for the synchronous part of the GetLogin on the PasswordStore to complete.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.StorePerformance.GetLogins
histogram.summary: The time (ms) it takes for the synchronous part of the GetLogins on the PasswordStore to complete.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.StorePerformance.RemoveLogin
histogram.summary: The time (ms) it takes for the synchronous part of the RemoveLogin on the PasswordStore to complete.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.StorePerformance.UpdateLogin
histogram.summary: The time (ms) it takes for the synchronous part of the UpdateLogin on the PasswordStore to complete.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.StoreReadyWhenWiping
histogram.obsolete: Removed since August 28, 2018, due to removing the corresponding feature.
histogram.summary: When the user logs in with their sync credential, PasswordManager checks if there are any outdated copies of it in the password store, in order to purge them. This histogram records whether password store data are ready during this check.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.StoresUsedForFillingInLast28Days
histogram.summary: Which of the password manager's stores (the profile-scoped one and/or the account-scoped one) were used within the last 28 days for filling passwords. Recorded whenever a password form is successfully submitted. This should typically only be used with "Count unique clients".

histogram.name: PasswordManager.StoresUsedForFillingInLast7Days
histogram.summary: Which of the password manager's stores (the profile-scoped one and/or the account-scoped one) were used within the last 7 days for filling passwords. Recorded whenever a password form is successfully submitted. This should typically only be used with "Count unique clients".

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SubmitNavigatesToDifferentDomain
histogram.summary: Indicates whether submitting a password login form changes the registry controlled domain of the main frame.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SubmittedFormFrame
histogram.summary: Records a frame type (eg. main frame, iframe with the same url as the main frame) of a submitted password form.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SubmittedFormType
histogram.summary: The type (e.g. signup, login, change password) of all submitted password forms. This is logged on form submission, but doesn't require that the password manager saves.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SubmittedNonSecureFormType
histogram.summary: The type (e.g. signup, login, change password) of all submitted password forms on non-secure pages. This is logged on form submission, but doesn't require that the password manager saves.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SuccessfulLoginHappened
histogram.summary: For a successful login, whether the origin is HTTPS or HTTP. A succesful login is detected by local heuristics. The heuristics can misclassify whether a login succeeded, so this is an approximation.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SuccessfulSubmissionIndicatorEvent
histogram.summary: The type of event that the Password Manager used for detecting a password form submission. Recorded for each successful password form submission.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SuggestionClicked
histogram.summary: The type of password suggestion on iOS displayed in the keyboard accessory. Recorded for each tap on the suggestion.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SuggestionShown
histogram.summary: The type of password suggestions on iOS displayed in the keyboard accessory. Recorded each time the password suggestion is shown.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SuppressedAccount
histogram.summary: Records, for each password form seen by the password manager, whether there were `suppressed` credentials, meaning stored credentials that were not filled because they were for an origin that was similar to, but not exactly (or PSL) matching the origin of the observed form (see the suffix description for the possible classes of such near-matches). If there were such credentials, the histogram also records whether the username and password of such suppressed credentials matched those submitted.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SyncCredentialFiltered
histogram.obsolete: Password manager no longer supports this behavior and the code that could generate values in this histogram is gone as of M74.
histogram.summary: This histogram is reported for those forms on accounts.google.com, on which Chrome is forced by Finch/flags during autofilling to remove sync credentials from password store results. It reports true if sync credentials were indeed removed from the store results, and false if there were no sync credentials in the results to begin with.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SyncingAccountState
histogram.summary: Information about the user's current sync status crossed with whether their synced password is saved.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SyncMetadataReadError
histogram.summary: This metric reports the error observed when trying to read the Sync metadata from the password store during the start of Password sync.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.SyncPasswordHashChange
histogram.summary: This metric reports a type of a sync password hash change event. Recorded when a sync password hash is saved or cleared.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.TimeBetweenStoreAndServer
histogram.summary: The time between receiving saved credentials from the password store and predictions from the Autofill server. Recorded each time when predictions for a form are received.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.TimeReadingExportedPasswords
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 2019/07. Password exporting has been rolled out.
histogram.summary: The time it takes to fetch and serialise the passwords for export.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.TimesGeneratedPasswordUsed
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 11/11/14. New statistic is PasswordManager.TimesPasswordUsed.AutoGenerated.
histogram.summary: The number of times each generated password has been used to log in. Recorded by iterating over stored passwords once per run. This information is persisted and synced.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.TimesPasswordUsed
histogram.summary: The number of times each saved password from the profile-scoped storage has been used to log in. Split by whether created by the user or generated by Chrome, and further by whether the user used sync with custom passphrase or not. Recorded by iterating over stored passwords once per run. This information is persisted and synced.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.TotalAccounts
histogram.obsolete: Removed in M76 in favor of PasswordManager.TotalAccountsHiRes.ByType.
histogram.summary: The number of accounts stored in the password manager (across all sites), split by whether created by the user or generated by Chrome, and further by whether the user used sync with custom passphrase or not.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.TotalAccountsHiRes.ByType
histogram.summary: The number of accounts stored in the password manager's profile-scoped store (across all sites), split by whether created by the user or generated by Chrome, and further by whether the user used sync with custom passphrase or not.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.TotalAccountsHiRes.WithScheme
histogram.summary: The number of accounts stored in the password manager's profile-scoped store (across all sites) for origins with the scheme (e.g., HTTP, HTTPS, FTP) as given in the histogram suffix. For each scheme, the count is recorded once per browser start-up. (In case of multiple profiles, the counts are for the profile that first has a WebContents created.)

histogram.name: PasswordManager.TouchToFill.CredentialIndex
histogram.summary: The index of a selected credential in the Touch To Fill sheet. Only recorded when the sheet showed at least two entries.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.TouchToFill.DismissalReason
histogram.summary: The reason why a user dismissed the Touch To Fill sheet. Recorded once for each dismissal.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.TouchToFill.NumCredentialsShown
histogram.summary: The number of credentials shown in the Touch To Fill sheet.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.TouchToFill.UserAction
histogram.summary: The action a user took when interacting with the Touch To Fill sheet.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.UIDismissalReason
histogram.summary: Why was the password manager's UI (bubble or infobar) closed? Save and update UI are tracked separately.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.UpdatePasswordSubmissionEvent
histogram.summary: The password submission event happened when the user changes their password on a website, say foo.com or types new password on a sign-in form for saved credential (which might mean that the password was changed in a different browser). The later case called password overriding. This histogram measures whether the user has any passwords saved in the Chrome password manager for foo.com or the password was overriden on sign-in form, and what action the user performed on the offered bubble. It's recorded when the bubble is closed.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.UpdateUIDismissalReason
histogram.summary: Why was the update password UI (bubble or infobar) closed?

histogram.name: PasswordManager.UsernameCorrectionFound
histogram.summary: Chrome saves locally all fields a user typed on a sign-up form. If Chrome saved an incorrect username and the user changes the username on a sign-in form to a value of another field from the sign-up form, then Chrome uploads a username correction vote which points to the correct username field. This histogram measures how many correction votes are uploaded.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.UsernameDetectionMethod
histogram.summary: Measures what method was used for username field detection in the renderer code. The metric may be recorded several times for page visit because it is recorded at every PasswordForm creation. If a site changes HTML attributes of fields or server-side predictions is received, different values can be recorded for the same form. If an outcome of HTML classifier or a server-side prediction coincides with the outcome of base heuristic, the metric points to base heuristic method.

histogram.name: PasswordManager.UserStoredPasswordWithInvalidSSLCert
histogram.obsolete: Removed 07/2016 because this information is no longer stored in PasswordForm. See also http://crbug.com/413020.
histogram.summary: Whether saved password for HTTPS site had a valid SSL cert when the password was saved.

histogram.name: PasswordProtection.CacheVerdictDuration
histogram.obsolete: Removed since caching is not the root cause of time out issue.
histogram.summary: The time it takes for PasswordProtectionService to cache a verdict.

histogram.name: PasswordProtection.ChromeSettingsAction
histogram.obsolete: Removed since 03/2020 due to no usage.
histogram.summary: Records how a user interacts with the chrome://settings page that displays the password protection warning. Logged when the page is shown and when the user chooses to change their password on the page.

histogram.name: PasswordProtection.DomFeatureExtractionDuration
histogram.summary: The time it takes to extract the DOM features of a login page before sending a PasswordProtectionRequest. Logged on every password reuse PhishGuard ping.

histogram.name: PasswordProtection.DomFeatureParsing
histogram.obsolete: Removed in M78. The DOM features always parsed successfully, so this histogram wasn't useful.
histogram.summary: Records whether the DOM features were parsed successfully when returned from the renderer. This is logged on every PhishGuard ping.

histogram.name: PasswordProtection.GaiaPasswordReusesBeforeGaiaPasswordChanged
histogram.summary: The number of Gaia password reuse warnings shown to user before user changes their password. Logged when we see that user has changed their Gaia password. The number of reuses will be reset to zero if current session of browser process ends. This count doesn't include the reuses that user marked as legitimate.

histogram.name: PasswordProtection.GetCachedVerdictDuration
histogram.obsolete: Removed since caching is not the root cause of time out issue.
histogram.summary: The time it takes for PasswordProtectionService to get a cached verdict.

histogram.name: PasswordProtection.InterstitialAction
histogram.summary: Records how a user interacts with the password protection interstitial (a.k.a chrome://reset-password page). Logged when the interstitial is shown and when the user chooses to change their password.

histogram.name: PasswordProtection.InterstitialActionByUserNavigation
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2019-01 due to lack of usage. https://crbug.com/915894
histogram.summary: When user manually navigates to chrome://reset-password page, records how this user interacts with this page. Logged when the interstitial is shown and when the user chooses to change their password.

histogram.name: PasswordProtection.InterstitialString
histogram.summary: Records the type of strings shown to the user on chrome://reset-password page. Logged when the interstitial in initialized.

histogram.name: PasswordProtection.ModalWarningDialogAction
histogram.summary: Records how a user interacts with the password protection modal warning dialog. Logged when the dialog is shown and when the user chooses to change their password, ignore warning, or navigate away.

histogram.name: PasswordProtection.ModalWarningDialogLifetime
histogram.summary: Records the interval between when the password protection modal warning dialog is constructed and when the dialog is destructed. The dialog can be destructed when the user takes action or the web content is destroyed.

histogram.name: PasswordProtection.NumberOfCachedVerdictBeforeShutdown
histogram.obsolete: Removed since 07/2018 due to lack of usage.
histogram.summary: Number of password protection verdicts stored in content settings of a profile before this profile is destructed.

histogram.name: PasswordProtection.NumberOfVerdictsMigratedDuringInitialization
histogram.summary: We start changing the way of caching password protection verdicts in M69. This histogram counts the number of verdicts migrated to the new way during initialization of PasswordProtectionService. It helps determine when we can remove migration code.

histogram.name: PasswordProtection.PageInfoAction
histogram.summary: Records how a user interacts with page info bubble that displays the password protection warning. Logged when such page info bubble is shown and when the user chooses to change their password, ignore warning, or mark site as legitimate via buttons on the page info bubble.

histogram.name: PasswordProtection.PageZoomFactor
histogram.summary: Reports the zoom factor for a login page, when the protected password is entered.

histogram.name: PasswordProtection.PasswordAlertModeOutcome
histogram.summary: Records the outcome of the password alert mode, indicating if password alert warning is shown successfully or if it is skipped for some reason.

histogram.name: PasswordProtection.PasswordProtectionResponseOrErrorCode
histogram.summary: Response or error codes for PasswordProtectionRequest. Logged after chrome receives response of PasswordProtectionRequest from Safe Browsing service.

histogram.name: PasswordProtection.PasswordReuseEventVerdict
histogram.summary: Verdict types returned by Safe Browsing server for password reuse events.

histogram.name: PasswordProtection.PasswordReuseSyncAccountType
PasswordProtection.ReferrerChainSize
histogram.obsolete: Removed September 18 2018. This wasn't being used, and can be recreated on the backend if needed.
histogram.summary: The referrer chain size of a password protection request. This is recorded when Chrome receives a verdict for this request.

histogram.name: PasswordProtection.RendererDomFeatureResult
histogram.summary: The result from the renderer after DOM feature extraction. This is collected when Chrome password protection detects password reuse, or (for SBER users), when the user focuses on a password field.

histogram.name: PasswordProtection.RequestNetworkDuration
histogram.summary: The time it takes for PasswordProtectionService request. It is not recorded for requests that were canceled.

histogram.name: PasswordProtection.RequestOutcome
histogram.summary: Records the outcome of the password protection request, indicating if request is sent out successfully or if it is skipped or canceled for some reason.

histogram.name: PasswordProtection.SampleReportSent
histogram.summary: Logs when an event where users reuse their password or focus on a password field is sent as a sample ping when the URL in the allowlist to Safe Browsing. Events only happen 1% of the time when URLs match the allowlist.

histogram.name: PasswordProtection.UnfamiliarLoginPageVerdict
histogram.summary: Verdict types returned by Safe Browsing server for unfamiliar login pages.

histogram.name: PasswordProtection.UserClickedThroughSafeBrowsingInterstitial
histogram.obsolete: Removed since 07/2018 due to lack of usage.
histogram.summary: Whether the user has clicked through the Safe Browsing interstitial before the password protection ping is triggered on the same page. Logged right before password protection ping is sent.

histogram.name: PasswordProtection.Verdict
histogram.summary: Verdict types returned by Safe Browsing server for a password protection request. Request can be triggered when user focuses on a password field or enters a protected password (syncing, signed-in, or saved).

histogram.name: PasswordProtection.VisualFeatureExtractionDuration
histogram.summary: The time it takes to extract the visual features of a login page before sending a PasswordProtectionRequest. Logged every time visual features are extracted (when an SBER user sends an On Focus ping).

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.CanMakePayment.NotUsed.WithShowEffectOnCompletion
histogram.summary: Whether the flow was completed when CanMakePayment was not called by the merchant.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.CanMakePayment.Usage
histogram.summary: Whether the merchant used the CanMakePayment method during a Payment Request.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.CanMakePayment.Used.EffectOnShow
histogram.summary: The effect of the CanMakePayment return value on whether Show was called.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.CanMakePayment.Used.FalseWithShowEffectOnCompletion
histogram.summary: Whether the flow was completed when CanMakePayment was called by the merchant and returned false.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.CanMakePayment.Used.TrueWithShowEffectOnCompletion
histogram.summary: Whether the flow was completed when CanMakePayment was called by the merchant and returned true.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.CheckoutFunnel.Aborted
histogram.summary: The reason that lead to an abort of the Payment Request.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.CheckoutFunnel.Completed
histogram.summary: When the merchant has processed the user's Payment Request.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.CheckoutFunnel.Initiated
histogram.summary: When a Payment Request gets initiated by the user.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.CheckoutFunnel.NoShow
histogram.summary: The reason that leads to the Payment Request not being shown to the user.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.CheckoutFunnel.PayClicked
histogram.summary: When the user clicks the "pay" button in the Payment Request UI.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.CheckoutFunnel.ReceivedInstrumentDetails
histogram.summary: When the browser retrieves the instrument details from the payment app to complete a purchase.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.CheckoutFunnel.Shown
histogram.summary: When the Payment Request UI gets shown after initialization.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.CheckoutFunnel.SkippedShow
histogram.summary: When the Payment Request UI gets skipped to go directly to the payment app.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.Events
histogram.summary: A bitfield representing the different events that happened during the Payment Request.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.JourneyLoggerHasRecorded
histogram.summary: Whether a journey logger has recorded an events bit field or not.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.JourneyLoggerHasRecordedMultipleTimes
histogram.summary: When a journey logger attempts to record multiple events bit fields.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.MissingContactFields
histogram.summary: A bitfield representing different missing fields of the contact section in payment sheet. This only gets recorded when no complete contact profile is available. In case of multiple partially complete profiles, this is only recorded for the most complete one which is also the first profile in the suggestion list.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.MissingPaymentFields
histogram.summary: A bitfield representing different missing fields of the payment info section in the payment sheet. This only gets recorded when no complete payment instrument is available (SW based instruments are always considered as complete). In case of multiple partially complete cards this is only recorded for the first card in the suggestion list.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.MissingShippingFields
histogram.summary: A bitfield representing different missing fields of the shipping section in the payment sheet. This only gets recorded when no complete shipping profile is available. In case of multiple partially complete profiles, this is only recorded for the most complete one which is also the first profile in the suggestion list.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.NumberOfSelectionAdds
histogram.summary: The number of times the user added an entry during a Payment Request.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.NumberOfSelectionChanges
histogram.summary: The number of times the user changed an entry during a Payment Request.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.NumberOfSelectionEdits
histogram.summary: The number of times the user edited an entry during a Payment Request.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.NumberOfSuggestionsShown
histogram.summary: The number of suggestions shown to the user during a payment request.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.RequestedInformation
histogram.summary: Tracks what user information is required by merchants to complete a Payment Request.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.SelectedPaymentMethod
histogram.summary: Tracks what payment method was used to complete a transaction in Payment Request.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.ServiceWorkerStatusCodeTimeout
histogram.summary: True when a service worker times out 5 mins after request.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.TimeToCheckout.Completed
histogram.summary: Records the time between a payment request .show() and its completion.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.TimeToCheckout.Completed.Shown
histogram.summary: Records the time between a payment request .show() and its completion when payment sheet is shown.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.TimeToCheckout.Completed.SkippedShow
histogram.summary: Records the time between a payment request .show() and its completion when payment sheet is skipped.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.TimeToCheckout.OtherAborted
histogram.summary: Records the time between a payment request .show() and its termination by any factors other than the user (e.g. merchant).

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.TimeToCheckout.UserAborted
histogram.summary: Records the time between a payment request .show() and its termination by user.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.TransactionAmount.Completed
histogram.summary: Records the transaction amounts completed using payment request API after converting to USD.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.TransactionAmount.Triggered
histogram.summary: Records the transaction amounts triggered using payment request API after converting to USD.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.UserDidNotHaveCompleteSuggestionsForEverything.EffectOnCompletion
histogram.summary: Whether the flow was completed when the user did not have a complete suggestion offered for each requested information.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.UserDidNotHaveInitialFormOfPayment.EffectOnCompletion
histogram.summary: Whether the flow was completed when the user did not have a a form of payment on file when the Payment Request was shown.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.UserDidNotHaveSuggestionsForEverything.EffectOnCompletion
histogram.summary: Whether the flow was completed when the user did not have suggestions offered for each requested information.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.UserHadCompleteSuggestionsForEverything.EffectOnCompletion
histogram.summary: Whether the flow was completed when the user had at least one complete suggestion offered for each requested information.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.UserHadInitialFormOfPayment.EffectOnCompletion
histogram.summary: Whether the flow was completed when the user had a form of payment on file when the Payment Request was shown.

histogram.name: PaymentRequest.UserHadSuggestionsForEverything.EffectOnCompletion
histogram.summary: Whether the flow was completed when the user had suggestions offered for each requested information.

histogram.name: PDF.Actions
histogram.summary: Tracks user actions in the PDF viewer. Logged when the document is opened and when one of the relevant user actions is taken, such as opening the bookmarks bar or setting the zoom mode to fit-to-page. The enum values that are named "First" are logged only once per document, so that we can measure in what percentage of documents a given action was performed at least once.

histogram.name: PDF.AnnotationType
histogram.summary: Tracks documents opened in the PDF viewer that displayed one or more pages with the given annotation type.

histogram.name: PDF.DocumentFeature
histogram.summary: Tracks which features are used by documents opened in the PDF viewer, logged when the document finishes loading.

histogram.name: PDF.FileSizeInKB
histogram.summary: The size of PDF documents opened in the PDF viewer.

histogram.name: PDF.FormType
histogram.summary: Tracks what types of forms are present in PDF document, logged when the document finishes loading.

histogram.name: PDF.HasAttachment
histogram.summary: Measures if PDFs opened in the PDF viewer have attachments. This is logged whenever a document is opened in the PDF viewer.

histogram.name: PDF.IsFontSubstituted
histogram.summary: Measures if PDFs opened in the PDF viewer require fonts to be substituted. This is logged whenever a document is opened in the PDF viewer.

histogram.name: PDF.IsLinearized
histogram.summary: Measures if PDFs opened in the PDF viewer are Linearized PDFs. This is logged whenever a document is opened in the PDF viewer.

histogram.name: PDF.IsTagged
histogram.summary: Tracks documents opened in the PDF viewer that are Tagged PDFs.

histogram.name: PDF.LinkHighlightOverlapsInPage
histogram.summary: Records the number of overlapping link and highlight annotations in a PDF page. This is logged once per page whenever accessibility tree creation is triggered for that page. It will not be logged on render or scroll operations.

histogram.name: PDF.LoadStatus
histogram.summary: Tracks what happens when Chrome tries to load a PDF. This metric tracks all cases, but is focused on measuring failed PDF embeds, which occur if the user is on Android, or if PDF plugin is disabled.

histogram.name: PDF.PageCount
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of pages in PDF documents opened in the PDF viewer.

histogram.name: PDF.Version
histogram.summary: Tracks versions of documents opened in the PDF viewer.

histogram.name: Pepper.BrokerAction
histogram.summary: Counts various actions related to the Pepper Broker interface.

histogram.name: Pepper.Graphics3DHasShareGroup
histogram.summary: True if a non-flash Pepper Graphics3D context has a share group.

histogram.name: Pepper.InterfaceUsed
histogram.summary: The number of out-of-process plugin processes that have loaded a particular PPB interface version.

histogram.name: Pepper.PluginContextSecurity.TCPConnect
histogram.summary: Whether a Pepper TCP connect attempt comes from a plugin in a secure or an insecure origin.

histogram.name: Pepper.PluginContextSecurity.UDPBind
histogram.summary: Whether a Pepper UDP bind attempt comes from a plugin in a secure or an insecure origin.

histogram.name: Pepper.SecureOrigin.MediaStreamRequest
histogram.summary: Whether a Pepper media stream request (mic/cam access) comes from a plugin in a secure or an insecure origin.

histogram.name: Performance.MeasureParameter.EndMark
histogram.summary: The count of possible incoming values to the endMark parameter in performance.measure(). The purpose is to investigate how much the change of API of User Timing L3 will impact the real world use cases. This histogram was deprecated in 05/2018 and re-reenabled in 11/2018. The re-enabled histogram adds the counting of undefined, null, number and unprovided, to the original navigation-timing names, object and string. This value is recorded at each call to performance.measure().

histogram.name: Performance.MeasureParameter.StartMark
histogram.summary: The count of possible incoming values to the startMark parameter in performance.measure(). The purpose is to investigate how much the change of API of User Timing L3 will impact the real world use cases. This histogram was deprecated in 05/2018 and re-reenabled in 11/2018. The re-enabled histogram adds the counting of undefined, null, number and unprovided, to the original navigation-timing names, object and string. This value is recorded at each call to performance.measure().

histogram.name: PerformanceHints.Observer.HintForURLResult
histogram.summary: Records the result of querying PerformanceHintsObserver for a URL-specific performance hint. This enables tracking how often the OptimizationGuideDecider provides a hint in time for a HintForURL request.

histogram.name: PerformanceHints.Observer.PerformanceClassForURL
histogram.summary: Records the PerformanceClass determined for a given URL. This value represents the expected loading performance for the URL. This is recorded before any overrides are performed by the PerformanceHintsObserver.

histogram.name: PerformanceHints.Observer.SourceLookupStatus
histogram.summary: Records the result of a hint lookup in a single source (e.g. link hints of the current page, the page's OptimizationMetadata, etc). See the PerformanceHintsSource suffix for name completions. All source suffix histograms should have the same total count.

histogram.name: PerformanceManager.AgentsByTime
histogram.summary: Tracks the total number of agents hosted by the browser by time. An entry in bucket N corresponds to N agents being hosted across all live renderer process for 1 second. Recorded on state changes and every 5 minutes.

histogram.name: PerformanceManager.AgentsPerRendererByTime
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of agents hosted per renderer by time. An entry in bucket N corresponds to N agents being hosted by a renderer process for 1 second. Recorded on state changes and every 5 minutes.

histogram.name: PerformanceManager.AgentsUniqueByTime
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of unique agents hosted by the browser by time. An entry in bucket N corresponds to N unique agents being hosted across all live renderer process for 1 second. Unique means that the agent count would remain the same if they were in one process (i.e. they have different protocol+site). Recorded on state changes and every 5 minutes.

histogram.name: PerformanceManager.BrowsingInstancePluralityVisibilityState.ByPageTime
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2020-01-06 as this was only added for a brief exploration.
histogram.summary: Tracks the plurality and visibility state of all BrowsingInstances over time. An entry in each bucket corresponds to a page in a BrowsingInstance being in that state for one second. This is effectively "page seconds" of time spent in each state. Recorded on state changes and every 5 minutes.

histogram.name: PerformanceManager.BrowsingInstancePluralityVisibilityState.ByTime
histogram.obsolete: Removed 2020-01-06 as this was only added for a brief exploration.
histogram.summary: Tracks the plurality and visibility state of all BrowsingInstances over time. An entry in each bucket corresponds to a BrowsingInstance being in that state for one second. Recorded on state changes and every 5 minutes.

histogram.name: PerformanceManager.FrameSiteInstanceProcessRelationship.ByProcess
histogram.summary: Tracks the types of frames that are being hosted by a process over its entire lifetime. An entry in the "AllFramesHaveDistinctSiteInstances" means that the process only ever hosted frames with distinct site instances over its entire lifetime. Recorded on state changes and every 5 minutes.

histogram.name: PerformanceManager.FrameSiteInstanceProcessRelationship.ByProcess2
histogram.summary: Tracks the types of frames that are being hosted by a process over its entire lifetime. An entry in the "AllFramesHaveDistinctSiteInstances" means that the process only ever hosted frames with distinct site instances over its entire lifetime. Recorded on state changes and every 5 minutes.

histogram.name: PerformanceManager.FrameSiteInstanceProcessRelationship.ByTime
histogram.summary: Tracks the types of frames that are being hosted by a process, aggregated over time. Each entry in each bucket corresponds to a process being in that state for one second. Recorded on state changes and every 5 minutes.

histogram.name: PerformanceManager.FrameSiteInstanceProcessRelationship.ByTime2
histogram.summary: Tracks the types of frames that are being hosted by a process, aggregated over time. Each entry in each bucket corresponds to a process being in that state for one second. Recorded on state changes and every 5 minutes.

histogram.name: PerformanceManager.FramesPerRendererByTime
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of frames hosted per renderer by time. An entry in bucket N corresponds to N frames being hosted by a renderer process for 1 second. Recorded on state changes and every 5 minutes.

histogram.name: PerformanceManager.SiteInstancesPerRendererByTime
histogram.summary: Tracks the number of site instances hosted per renderer by time. An entry in bucket N corresponds to N site instances being hosted by a renderer process for 1 second. Recorded on state changes and every 5 minutes.

histogram.name: PerformanceManager.WorkerClientFound
histogram.summary: Records a boolean indicating if a worker's client frame node could be found when trying to connect the worker to a client frame, given its ID. This is supposed to be always true if the performance manager has perfect coverage of WebContents objects.

histogram.name: PerformanceMonitor.AverageCPU
histogram.summary: Average CPU utilization of a process, read out at each two-minute interval. The utilization is in the 0-100% range per CPU, which is then summed up. I.e. a quadcore system fully loaded would read as 400%.

histogram.name: PerformanceMonitor.AverageDisk
histogram.summary: Average disk utilization of a process, recorded at every two-minute interval. The amount of data transferred (Total I/O bytes per second) for a given process.

histogram.name: PerformanceMonitor.EnergyImpact
histogram.summary: (Mac only) A synthetic power use estimate, as displayed in macOS Activity Monitor and the battery menu. This incorporates CPU utilization, idle wakeups, IO, and task QoS level using per-machine-model weights. Divide by 100 to match Activity Monitor's scale. Recorded every two minutes.

histogram.name: PerformanceMonitor.HighCPU
histogram.summary: The number of times a process has continuously stayed above a certain threshold of CPU utilization over a certain time period (currently set to two minutes).

histogram.name: PerformanceMonitor.IdleWakeups
histogram.summary: The average CPU idle wakeups per second, sampled every two minutes.

histogram.name: PerformanceMonitor.PackageExitIdleWakeups
histogram.summary: (Mac only) The average package exit idle wakeups per second, sampled every two minutes. This is a subset of wakeups that indicate that the processor complex was taken out of low-power state. For more info, see the powermetrics man page on macOS.

histogram.name: PerformanceMonitor.SystemMonitor.DiskIdleTime
histogram.summary: Average percentage of time during which the disk has been idle, logged at regular intervals. If the system has multiple disks then this is the average value for all of them. Windows only.

histogram.name: PerformanceMonitor.SystemMonitor.FreePhysMemory
histogram.summary: The amount of free physical memory available, logged at regular intervals. Windows only.

histogram.name: PeriodicBackgroundSync.Event.BatchSize
histogram.summary: Records the number of `periodicsync` events which were fired in a batch. A batch is defined as the set of `periodicsync` events dispatched at the same time by the BackgroundSyncManager. Periodic syncs usually run in a batch.

histogram.name: PeriodicBackgroundSync.Event.FromWakeupTask
histogram.summary: Records whether the fired `periodicsync` events upon completion orginated from a background wakeup task or the foreground.

histogram.name: PeriodicBackgroundSync.Event.Time
histogram.summary: Time taken to execute a batch of periodicsync events. A batch is defined as the set of `periodicsync` events dispatched at the same time by the BackgroundSyncManager. Periodic syncs often run in a batch.

histogram.name: Permissions.Action
histogram.summary: Tracks whether a permission was granted, rejected, etc. The suffix of the histogram indicates which particular permission.

histogram.name: Permissions.Action.InsecureOrigin
histogram.summary: Tracks whether a permission was granted, rejected, etc on an insecure origin. The suffix of the histogram indicates which particular permission.

histogram.name: Permissions.Action.SecureOrigin
histogram.summary: Tracks whether a permission was granted, rejected, etc on a secure origin. The suffix of the histogram indicates which particular permission.

histogram.name: Permissions.Action.WithDisposition
histogram.summary: Tracks the action that a user had taken per permission prompt UI disposition. Suffixed by the UI disposition that the prompt used. Recorded when a permission prompt has been resolved.

histogram.name: Permissions.AutoBlocker.EmbargoPromptSuppression
histogram.summary:
For every permission request that would show a prompt to the user, this metric tracks whether the request is suppressed by embargo (and the source of that embargo), or whether there is no embargo and the prompt is shown. This is the proportion of permission prompts that the user would have seen that have been blocked due to embargo.
If the (origin, permission) pair was previously placed under embargo, no prompt is displayed and the reason for embargo is recorded. Otherwise, the prompt is shown and a no embargo reason is recorded.

histogram.name: Permissions.AutoBlocker.EmbargoStatus
histogram.summary: Tracks the reason that an (origin, permission) pair has been placed under embargo (blocked from making requests for that permission for a period of time). This is triggered when a permission is placed under embargo for blacklisting (once Safe Browsing has returned a result), when a permission prompt has been shown and it is placed under embargo for repeated dismissals, or when a permission prompt is shown and not embargoed.

histogram.name: Permissions.AutoBlocker.SafeBrowsingResponse
histogram.obsolete: Permission blacklisting was removed in M67.
histogram.summary: Tracks the response (if received) from Safe Browsing when the API blacklist is queried for an (origin, permission) pair. The response could be that the origin was blacklisted, not blacklisted, or Safe Browsing timed out before a response was received.

histogram.name: Permissions.AutoBlocker.SafeBrowsingResponseTime
histogram.obsolete: Permission blacklisting was removed in M67.
histogram.summary: Records the elapsed time between the client sending a request to Safe Browsing and receiving a result, or the maximum wait time is exceeded and Safe Browsing is deemed to have timed out.

histogram.name: Permissions.BatteryLevel.Accepted.Geolocation
histogram.summary: Battery percentage when a geolocation permission prompt was accepted on Android.

histogram.name: Permissions.BatteryLevel.Accepted.LocationSettingsDialog
histogram.summary: Battery percentage when a location settings dialog was accepted.

histogram.name: Permissions.BatteryLevel.Denied.Geolocation
histogram.summary: Battery percentage when a geolocation permission prompt was denied on Android.

histogram.name: Permissions.BatteryLevel.Denied.LocationSettingsDialog
histogram.summary: Battery percentage when a location settings dialog was denied.

histogram.name: Permissions.BatteryLevel.Dismissed.Geolocation
histogram.summary: Battery percentage when a geolocation permission prompt was dismissed on Android.

histogram.name: Permissions.Chip.TimeToInteraction
histogram.summary: Records how long it takes for the user to click on the permission chip after it was shown.

histogram.name: Permissions.CrowdDeny.DidHoldbackQuietUi
histogram.summary: Whenever a notification permission prompt is to be displayed, crowd deny might trigger based on site reputation which will result in a quiet permission prompt UI to be used. Whenever this happens, there is a small chance that the normal UI will still be used. This records, when a quiet UI is supposed to be shown, whether it has been randomly selected into the hold-back group. In this case the normal UI will be displayed unless overriden by the user Prefences.

histogram.name: Permissions.CrowdDeny.PreloadData.NotificationUxQuality
histogram.summary: Records, each time a notification permission prompt is about to be shown, the NotificationUxQuality result for the requesting origin.

histogram.name: Permissions.CrowdDeny.PreloadData.WarningOnly
histogram.summary: Records, each time a notification permission prompt is about to be shown, the whether there is preload data that indicates the origin being on a warning list.

histogram.name: Permissions.CrowdDeny.SafeBrowsing.RequestDuration
histogram.summary: Records, each time a Safe Browsing request is made in response to a website requesting the notification permission, how long that Safe Browsing request took. This also includes all delays introduced by thread hopping.

histogram.name: Permissions.CrowdDeny.SafeBrowsing.Verdict
histogram.summary: Records, each time a Safe Browsing request is made in response to a website requesting the notification permission, the Safe Browsing verdict regarding the notification prompt experience on that site.

histogram.name: Permissions.Engagement.Accepted
histogram.summary: Tracks the engagement score for sites where permission prompts are accepted.

histogram.name: Permissions.Engagement.Denied
histogram.summary: Tracks the engagement score for sites where permission prompts are denied.

histogram.name: Permissions.Engagement.Dismissed
histogram.summary: Tracks the engagement score for sites where permission prompts are dismissed.

histogram.name: Permissions.Engagement.Ignored
histogram.summary: Tracks the engagement score for sites where permission prompts are ignored.

histogram.name: Permissions.MissingOSLevelPermission.Action
histogram.summary: Recorded when the "Chrome needs permission..." infobar is resolved. Records the resulting action that was taken. Suffixed by `PermissionTypes` to provide information per permission type.

histogram.name: Permissions.MissingOSLevelPermission.ShouldShow
histogram.summary: Recorded when the "Chrome needs permission..." infobar might be displayed to the user because a site already has the permission but Chrome does not. Records whether the infobar should be displayed or not. Ignores situations when the infobar cannot be displayed (for example because there is no appropriate web contents) as in those cases no decision is actually made. Suffixed by `PermissionTypes` to provide information per permission type.

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.Accepted
histogram.summary: Tracks the permission bubbles (merged and non-merged) that are accepted. Merged bubbles are considered accepted if all permissions are allowed.

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.Accepted.Persisted
histogram.obsolete: Persist toggle experiments ran from M56 to M59.
histogram.summary: For each granted permission prompt displayed with a persistence toggle (remember my decision), records whether the persistence toggle was enabled (persist) or not enabled (don't persist).

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.Accepted.PriorDismissCount
histogram.summary: This metric, recorded at the time of a permission prompt accept, records the total number of prompt dismissal events for this origin since the last time the user cleared their history or site data and prior to the accept. The suffix of the histogram indicates which particular permission.

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.Accepted.PriorDismissCount2
histogram.summary: This metric, recorded at the time of a permission prompt accept, records the total number of prompt dismissal events for this origin since the last time the user explicitly set the permission state or cleared history / site data and prior to the accept. The suffix of the histogram indicates which particular permission.

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.Accepted.PriorIgnoreCount
histogram.summary: This metric, recorded at the time of a permission prompt accept, records the total number of prompt ignore events for this origin since the last time the user cleared their history or site data and prior to the accept. The suffix of the histogram indicates which particular permission.

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.Accepted.PriorIgnoreCount2
histogram.summary: This metric, recorded at the time of a permission prompt accept, records the total number of prompt ignore events for this origin since the last time the user explicitly set the permission state or cleared history / site data and prior to the accept. The suffix of the histogram indicates which particular permission.

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.Denied
histogram.summary: Tracks the permission bubbles (merged and non-merged) that are denied. Merged bubbles are considered denied if any permission is denied.

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.Denied.Persisted
histogram.obsolete: Persist toggle experiments ran from M56 to M59.
histogram.summary: For each denied permission prompt displayed with a persistence toggle (remember my decision), records whether the persistence toggle was enabled (persist) or not enabled (don't persist).

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.Denied.PriorDismissCount
histogram.summary: This metric, recorded at the time of a permission prompt deny, records the total number of prompt dismissal events for this origin since the last time the user cleared their history or site data and prior to the accept. The suffix of the histogram indicates which particular permission.

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.Denied.PriorDismissCount2
histogram.summary: This metric, recorded at the time of a permission prompt deny, records the total number of prompt dismissal events for this origin since the last time the user explicitly set the permission state or cleared history / site data and prior to the accept. The suffix of the histogram indicates which particular permission.

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.Denied.PriorIgnoreCount
histogram.summary: This metric, recorded at the time of a permission prompt deny, records the total number of prompt ignore events for this origin since the last time the user cleared their history or site data and prior to the accept. The suffix of the histogram indicates which particular permission.

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.Denied.PriorIgnoreCount2
histogram.summary: This metric, recorded at the time of a permission prompt deny, records the total number of prompt ignore events for this origin since the last time the user explicitly set the permission state or cleared history / site data and prior to the accept. The suffix of the histogram indicates which particular permission.

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.DismissCount
histogram.summary: This metric, recorded at the time of a permission prompt dismissal, records the total number of prompt dismissal events for this origin since the last time the user cleared their history or site data, inclusive of the current dismissal. Every event in a bucket larger than 1 in this histogram will also have an event in each smaller bucket. The suffix of the histogram indicates which particular permission.

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.Dismissed.PriorDismissCount
histogram.summary: This metric, recorded at the time of a permission prompt dismiss, records the total number of prompt dismissal events for this origin since the last time the user cleared their history or site data and prior to the dismiss. The suffix of the histogram indicates which particular permission.

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.Dismissed.PriorDismissCount2
histogram.summary: This metric, recorded at the time of a permission prompt dismiss, records the total number of prompt dismissal events for this origin since the last time the user explicitly set the permission state or cleared history / site data and prior to the dismiss. The suffix of the histogram indicates which particular permission.

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.Dismissed.PriorIgnoreCount
histogram.summary: This metric, recorded at the time of a permission prompt dismiss, records the total number of prompt ignore events for this origin since the last time the user cleared their history or site data and prior to the dismiss. The suffix of the histogram indicates which particular permission.

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.Dismissed.PriorIgnoreCount2
histogram.summary: This metric, recorded at the time of a permission prompt dismiss, records the total number of prompt ignore events for this origin since the last time the user explicitly set the permission state or cleared history / site data and prior to the dismiss. The suffix of the histogram indicates which particular permission.

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.IgnoreCount
histogram.summary: This metric, recorded at the time of a permission prompt ignore, records the total number of prompt ignore events for this origin since the last time the user cleared their history or site data, inclusive of the current ignore. Every event in a bucket larger than 1 in this histogram will also have an event in each smaller bucket. The suffix of the histogram indicates which particular permission.

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.Ignored.PriorDismissCount
histogram.summary: This metric, recorded at the time of a permission prompt ignore, records the total number of prompt dismissal events for this origin since the last time the user cleared their history or site data and prior to the ignore. The suffix of the histogram indicates which particular permission.

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.Ignored.PriorDismissCount2
histogram.summary: This metric, recorded at the time of a permission prompt ignore, records the total number of prompt dismissal events for this origin since the last time the user explicitly set the permission state or cleared history / site data and prior to the ignore. The suffix of the histogram indicates which particular permission.

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.Ignored.PriorIgnoreCount
histogram.summary: This metric, recorded at the time of a permission prompt ignore, records the total number of prompt ignore events for this origin since the last time the user cleared their history or site data and prior to the ignore. The suffix of the histogram indicates which particular permission.

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.Ignored.PriorIgnoreCount2
histogram.summary: This metric, recorded at the time of a permission prompt ignore, records the total number of prompt ignore events for this origin since the last time the user explicitly set the permission state or cleared history / site data and prior to the ignore. The suffix of the histogram indicates which particular permission.

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.Infobar.DetailsExpanded
histogram.summary: Tracks whether the user has expanded the infobar notification permission request.

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.MergedBubbleAccepted
histogram.obsolete: Redundant since M61 (bug 728483) as we now only merge Mic+Camera. The same counts are available in Permissions.Prompt.Accepted.
histogram.summary: Tracks acceptance of permission bubble request types that have been merged into coalesced bubbles.

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.MergedBubbleDenied
histogram.obsolete: Redundant since M61 (bug 728483) as we now only merge Mic+Camera. The same counts are available in Permissions.Prompt.Denied.
histogram.summary: Tracks denial of permission bubble request types that have been merged into coalesced bubbles.

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.MergedBubbleTypes
histogram.obsolete: Redundant since M61 (bug 728483) as we now only merge Mic+Camera. The same counts are available in Permissions.Prompt.Shown.
histogram.summary: Tracks the permission bubble request types that are being merged into coalesced bubbles.

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.RequestsPerPrompt
histogram.obsolete: Redundant since M61 (bug 728483) as we now only merge Mic+Camera. The same counts are available in Permissions.Prompt.Shown.
histogram.summary: How many permission requests each permissions prompt shown to the user contains.

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.Shown
histogram.summary: Tracks how many times permission prompts are shown to users.

histogram.name: Permissions.Prompt.TimeToDecision
histogram.summary: Records how long it takes for the user to make a decision about permission request, from when permission prompt is shown until when the user clicks on one of the buttons in the prompt (accept, block or close).

histogram.name: Permissions.QuietNotificationPrompts.DidEnableAdapativelyInPrefs
histogram.summary: Records, each time the quiet UI gets enabled in preferences, whether it was enabled by the adaptive activation logic.

histogram.name: Permissions.QuietNotificationPrompts.EnabledStateInPrefsChangedTo
histogram.summary: Records when the quiet notification permission UI state changes, that is, it either gets enabled or disabled, in preferences.

histogram.name: Permissions.QuietNotificationPrompts.IsEnabledInPrefs
histogram.summary: Records whether the quiet notification permission UI is enabled in preferences at profile creation. No sample recorded when notifications are set to be blocked completely.

histogram.name: Permissions.Requested.CrossOrigin
histogram.summary:
The embedder's permission setting at the time of a cross-origin iframe permission request for a given permission type. See the corresponding histogram suffixes.
A request is when a website makes a permission request and the user has the permission set to prompt (i.e. not blocked or allowed).
Note this is probably not the metric you want - it does not correspond to the total number of times websites request a permission. Also, because specific permissions have code that can automatically block or grant permissions based on things like incognito, installed extensions etc., this does also not correspond to the number of times users are prompted to allow permissions.
See https://crbug.com/638076 for more details.

histogram.name: Permissions.Requested.SameOrigin
histogram.summary:
The permission type (geolocation, and such) of a same-origin permission request.
A request is when a website makes a permission request and the user has the permission set to prompt (i.e. not blocked or allowed).
Note this is probably not the metric you want - it does not correspond to the total number of times websites request a permission. Also, because specific permissions have code that can automatically block or grant permissions based on things like incognito, installed extensions etc., this does also not correspond to the number of times users are prompted to allow permissions.
See https://crbug.com/638076 for more details.

histogram.name: PhysicalWeb.ActivityReferral
histogram.summary: Referer used by user to reach the main Physical Web Activity.

histogram.name: PhysicalWeb.InitialState.IosChrome
histogram.summary: Initial state of the Physical Web in Chrome for iOS.

histogram.name: PhysicalWeb.ReferralDelay.OptInNotification
histogram.summary: Time between the most recent opt in notification update and when the user arrives at the ListUrlsActivity.

histogram.name: PhysicalWeb.ReferralDelay.StandardNotification
histogram.summary: Time between the most recent standard notification update and when the user arrives at the ListUrlsActivity.

histogram.name: PhysicalWeb.ResolveTime.Background
histogram.summary: Round trip time to the metadata server for a Physical Web URL resolution which the client requests during a background scan.

histogram.name: PhysicalWeb.ResolveTime.Foreground
histogram.summary: Round trip time to the metadata server for a Physical Web URL resolution which the client requests during a foreground scan, that is not explicitly requested via a refresh.

histogram.name: PhysicalWeb.ResolveTime.Refresh
histogram.summary: Round trip time to the metadata server for a Physical Web URL resolution which the client requests during a foreground scan, that is explicitly requested via a refresh.

histogram.name: PhysicalWeb.RoundTripTimeMilliseconds
histogram.summary: Round trip time to the metadata server for Physical web.

histogram.name: PhysicalWeb.State.Bluetooth
histogram.summary: Whether bluetooth is on, off, or unknown.

histogram.name: PhysicalWeb.State.DataConnectionActive
histogram.summary: Whether the data connection is active.

histogram.name: PhysicalWeb.State.LocationPermission
histogram.summary: Whether location permission has been accepted.

histogram.name: PhysicalWeb.State.LocationServices
histogram.summary: Whether location services are enabled.

histogram.name: PhysicalWeb.State.Preference
histogram.summary: Whether the preference is on, off, or if the user is in an onboarding state when Chrome first starts up.

histogram.name: PhysicalWeb.TotalUrls.OnInitialDisplay
histogram.summary: The number of URLs displayed to a user when a list of nearby URLs is first displayed.

histogram.name: PhysicalWeb.TotalUrls.OnRefresh
histogram.summary: The number of URLs displayed to a user when a list of nearby URLs is displayed after refreshing.

histogram.name: PhysicalWeb.WebUI.ListViewUrlPosition
histogram.summary: Count of how many times the user selected a nearby URL at the specified index in the Physical Web WebUI list view (zero-based). Capped at 50.

histogram.name: PictureInPictureWindow.ButtonPressed
histogram.summary: Records Picture-in-Picture window control buttons pressed by user (basically everything except user tap gestures).

histogram.name: PictureInPictureWindow.TapGesture
histogram.summary: Records Picture-in-Picture window control user tap gestures.

histogram.name: Platform.AnyCrashesDaily
histogram.summary: Count of crashes (user, kernel, and unclean shutdowns) during the previous day, or the most recent day the device was in use. Reported at most once a day.

histogram.name: Platform.AnyCrashesWeekly
histogram.summary: Count of crashes (user, kernel, and unclean shutdowns) during the previous epoch-week, or the most recent epoch-week the device was in use. Reported at most once a week. Epoch-weeks divide the time in 7-day intervals starting at the UNIX epoch.

histogram.name: Platform.AsvGroup
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Chrome OS (ARM Chromebooks using Exynos 5250 only) Adaptive Support Voltage Group, recorded once per bootup. Indicates which "bin" the SoC is part of, which sets the voltage that different rails on the system will run at. The values 0-11 are valid. A value of 12 indicates an error parsing dmesg and should be investigated. See also Platform.LotIdEnum.

histogram.name: Platform.BatteryAbsent
histogram.summary: Indicates whether the kernel's ACPI Smart Battery System driver logged an error trying to find the battery on boot.

histogram.name: Platform.BootMode.DevSwitch
histogram.summary: Indicates the status of the developer switch on Chrome OS hardware. The developer switch allows users to run their own code on the device.

histogram.name: Platform.BootMode.FirmwareWriteProtect
histogram.summary: On Chrome OS hardware, indicates whether flash write protection is enabled for the firmware indicated by the histogram suffix.

histogram.name: Platform.BootMode.WriteProtectSwitch
histogram.summary: Indicates the status of the hardware write protect switch on Chrome OS hardware. The write protect switch protects firmware from being rewritten.

histogram.name: Platform.BootSectorsRead
histogram.summary: Chrome OS number of disk sectors read at boot from kernel start to login-prompt-ready.

histogram.name: Platform.BootSectorsWritten
histogram.summary: Chrome OS number of disk sectors written at boot from kernel start to login-prompt-ready.

histogram.name: Platform.Cast.MeminfoMemAvailable
histogram.obsolete: Removed 11/2018 in favor of Platform.Cast.MeminfoMemAvailable2 with a larger range.
histogram.summary: /proc/meminfo's 'MemAvailable' in Mbytes. Collected on Cast devices with kernel version 3.14 and above and capped at 500.

histogram.name: Platform.Cast.MeminfoMemAvailable2
