histogram.summary: Cumulative number of times a tab was killed with a "He's dead, Jim!" page and the user saw the page, recorded once per tab kill event. For example, a user who loses 3 tabs will record a count in the 1 bin, 2 bin, and 3 bin. Thus each bin N is the number of sessions where users experienced N or more kill events. Compare to Tabs.SadTab.CrashCreated.

histogram.name: Tabs.SadTab.KillDisplayed.OOM
histogram.summary: Cumulative number of times a tab was killed with a "He's dead, Jim!" page due to the kernel out-of-memory killer and the user saw the page, recorded once per tab kill event. For example, a user who loses 3 tabs will record a count in the 1 bin, 2 bin, and 3 bin. Thus each bin N is the number of sessions where users experienced N or more kill events. Compare to Tabs.SadTab.CrashCreated.

histogram.name: Tabs.SadTab.OomCreated
histogram.summary: Cumulative number of times a tab crashed with "Aw, Snap!", recorded once per tab oom event. For example, a user who ran out of memory in 3 tabs will record a count in the 1 bin, 2 bin, and 3 bin. Thus each bin N is the number of sessions where users experienced N or more oom events. The user may not have actually seen the sad tab page, as it might have been an inactive tab. Compare to Tabs.SadTab.OomDisplayed.

histogram.name: Tabs.SadTab.OomDisplayed
histogram.summary: Cumulative number of times a tab ran out of memory with "Aw, Snap!" and the user saw the page, recorded once per tab oom event. For example, a user who ran out of memory in 3 tabs will record a count in the 1 bin, 2 bin, and 3 bin. Thus each bin N is the number of sessions where users experienced N or more oom events. Compare to Tabs.SadTab.OomCreated.

histogram.name: Tabs.SadTab.Reload.Event
histogram.summary: Counts of events from the style of sad tab which has a reload button as its primary action. Compare to Tabs.SadTab.Feedback.Event.

histogram.name: Tabs.SadTab.ReloadCount
histogram.summary: The number of times a tab was reloaded because it was killed (usually by the kernel OOM killer) and the user clicked on it later, recorded once per reload event. For example, a user who clicks on 3 discarded tabs will record a count in the 1 bin, 2 bin, and 3 bin. Thus each bin N is the number of sessions where users experienced N or more reload events. Compare to Tabs.Discard.DiscardCount.

histogram.name: Tabs.ScrubbedInInterval.KeyPress
histogram.summary: This histogram records the number of tabs that are 'scrubbed' by key press during a given interval of time (i.e. ctrl+tab navigation). For this metric, a tab is considered 'scrubbed' if it was active for less than or equal to a given amount of time.

histogram.name: Tabs.ScrubbedInInterval.MousePress
histogram.summary: This histogram records the number of tabs that are 'scrubbed' by mouse press during a given interval of time. For this metric, a tab is considered 'scrubbed' if it was active for less than or equal to a given amount of time.

histogram.name: Tabs.ScrubDistance
histogram.summary: The distance a user multi-finger scrubbed to change tabs. Always positive (ignores left / right).

histogram.name: Tabs.ScrubDuration
histogram.summary: The duration a user spent doing multi-finger scrubbing to change tabs before a different tab than the currently active one was actually activated.

histogram.name: Tabs.SpeculativeRestoreApplicability
histogram.summary: Applicability of speculative tab restore, recorded every time a tab is switched. This allows to estimate the fraction of tab restores experienced on mobile that can be mitigated using speculative restore. Options higher in the enum take precedence over the lower ones (i.e. low-memory tablet will be accounted as tablet).

histogram.name: Tabs.SpeculativeRestorePredictionAccuracy.SideSwipe
histogram.summary: Accuracy of the tab switch predictions made when the user begins the side swipe gesture.

histogram.name: Tabs.SpeculativeRestorePredictionAccuracy.TabSwitcher
histogram.summary: Accuracy of the tab switch predictions made when the user enters the tab switcher.

histogram.name: Tabs.SpeculativeRestoreTargetStatus
histogram.summary: Status of a tab recorded when the tab is targeted with speculative restore.

histogram.name: Tabs.SpeculativeRestoreTimeAhead.SideSwipe
histogram.summary: Time between starting the speculative load and actual tab switch for correct speculative load predictions made when the user begins the side swipe gesture.

histogram.name: Tabs.SpeculativeRestoreTimeAhead.TabSwitcher
histogram.summary: Time between starting the speculative load and actual tab switch for correct speculative load predictions made when the user enters the tab switcher.

histogram.name: Tabs.StateTransfer.NumberOfOtherTabsActivatedBeforeMadeActive
histogram.summary: Recorded once per tab activation. The number of tabs that were activated while the tab was inactive.
histogram.details: This metric is to be removed after M46 hits stable. We don't expect this metric to change over release, so we will remove this once we have the numbers from stable.

histogram.name: Tabs.StateTransfer.TabDistanceInactiveToActive
histogram.summary: Number of tabs between the previously active tab and the new active tab.
histogram.details: This metric is to be removed after M46 hits stable. We don't expect this metric to change over release, so we will remove this once we have the numbers from stable.

histogram.name: Tabs.StateTransfer.Target
histogram.summary: The state to which a tab transitioned. Recorded when a tab transitions from the state in the histogram name to a new state.
histogram.details: kouhei@ and tzik@ will remove some variation of these once M46 hits stable.

histogram.name: Tabs.StateTransfer.Time
histogram.summary: Tabs.StateTransfer.TimeA_B measures the time a tab was in state A before it transferred to state B.

histogram.name: Tabs.Suggestions.Close.NumSuggestionsChanged
histogram.summary:
The user is presented with recommendations on which Tabs they could close. The user has the option to: 1) Remove a Tab from the recommendation 2) Add a Tab which wasn't in the recommendation to the recommendation
This metric records the sum of the number of times the user does 1) or 2) and is a measure of how useful the recommendation is. This metric is recorded after the user accepts a recommendation - potentially with edits by performing 1) or 2).

histogram.name: Tabs.SwitchFromCloseLatency
histogram.summary: [Android] Time between the event that closes a tab and the start of rendering.

histogram.name: Tabs.SwitchFromExitLatency
histogram.summary: [Android] Time between the event that exits an app and the start of rendering.

histogram.name: Tabs.SwitchFromNewLatency
histogram.summary: [Android] Time between the event that creates a tab and the start of rendering.

histogram.name: Tabs.SwitchFromUserLatency
histogram.summary: [Android] Time between the event that selects a tab and the start of rendering.

histogram.name: Tabs.TabCount
histogram.summary: Counts the number of tabs across all windows, reported every 5 minutes. Desktop only.

histogram.name: Tabs.TabCountActiveWindow
histogram.summary: The number of tabs open in the active window when a load completes.

histogram.name: Tabs.TabCountInGroupPerLoad
histogram.summary: The number of tabs in the current active group when a load completes. Records 0 if the active tab is not in a group.

histogram.name: Tabs.TabCountInSwitcher
histogram.summary: The number of tabs open when the grid tab switcher is shown.
histogram.details: When the user is shown the tab switcher, logs the total number of individual open tabs. Android only.

histogram.name: Tabs.TabCountOnStartScreenShown
histogram.summary: The number of tabs open when the user is returned to the tab switcher on start.
histogram.details: When the user is returned to the tab switcher on starting Chrome due to inactivity, logs the overall number of tabs open. Android only.

histogram.name: Tabs.TabCountPerLoad
histogram.summary: The number of tabs open in all browsers (counting app-mode windows) when a load completes.
histogram.details:
This is basically the average number of tabs over time.
See also MPArch.RPHCountPerLoad for the number of processes used by these tabs.

histogram.name: Tabs.TabCountPerWindow
histogram.summary: The number of tabs open per window (counting app-mode windows) when a load completes.
histogram.details: This value will be recorded multiple times per load if more than one window is open.

histogram.name: Tabs.TabOffsetOfSwitch
histogram.summary: How many tabs a user moved forward or backward in the Android tab switcher.
histogram.details: Logged by the Android tab switcher when a user switches tabs (other than by opening a new tab). If the user switches to an earlier tab in the stack, we log a positive offset value indicating how many tabs back they moved. If the user switches to a later tab, we log a negative offset value. If the user stays on the same tab, we log 0. If the user switches to a tab in a different stack (i.e. they switch from normal to incognito or vice-versa), we don't log a value.

histogram.name: Tabs.TabsStatsDailyEventInterval
histogram.summary: Counts how often tab stats daily interval events were fired.

histogram.name: Tabs.Tasks.AverageTabGroupSize
histogram.summary: The average tab group size, average number of tabs in a group, on a cold start after all tabs are restored.

histogram.name: Tabs.Tasks.DuplicatedTab.DuplicatedTabCount
histogram.summary:
During tab restoration, a tab is considered as a duplicated tab when it has an identical url with another restored tab.
This histogram records the number of duplicated tabs on a cold start after all tabs are restored. The possible range for this number is from 0 to total number of restored tabs minus 1. In other words, this number is equals to total number of restored tabs minus the number of unique URLs.

histogram.name: Tabs.Tasks.DuplicatedTab.DuplicatedTabRatio
histogram.summary:
During tab restoration, a tab is considered as a duplicated tab when it has an identical url with another restored tab.
This histogram records the ratio of duplicated tabs to the total number of tabs on a cold start after all tabs are restored. This ratio will never reach 100%, since the highest possible number of duplicated tabs is total number of restored tabs minus 1.

histogram.name: Tabs.Tasks.HubAndSpokeNavigationUsage
histogram.summary: The cumulative number of "spokes" from any "hub" page in every tab. A "hub" is any page that the user navigates away from, and a "spoke" is a page that's navigated to. The "spokes" count is incremented and recorded each time the user navigates back to a "hub" page via navigation back button and then navigates to a new "spoke".

histogram.name: Tabs.Tasks.TabCreated.Count
histogram.summary: This recorded value is the number of currently open tabs that were created from a given source. This is recorded when entering the tab switcher mode.

histogram.name: Tabs.Tasks.TabCreated.Percent
histogram.summary: The recorded value is the percentage of the number of currently open tabs that are created from a given source, relative to the total number of currently open tabs. This is recorded when entering the tab switcher mode.

histogram.name: Tabs.Tasks.TabGroupCount
histogram.summary: The number of tab groups on a cold start after all tabs are restored.

histogram.name: Tabs.Tasks.TabGroupDensity
histogram.summary: The tab group density, the ratio of the number of tab groups to the total number of tabs, on a cold start after all tabs are restored.

histogram.name: Tabs.Tasks.TabsInGroupCount
histogram.summary: The number of tabs is belonging to a tab group on a cold start after all tabs are restored.

histogram.name: Tabs.Tasks.TabsInGroupRatio
histogram.summary: The ratio of all tabs in tab groups to the total number of tabs on a cold start after all tabs are restored.

histogram.name: Tabs.TimeSinceLastInteraction
histogram.summary: Reports the delta between the last user interaction time with a WebContents and the time browser code wants to verify a recent user interaction with that WebContents. Used to tune the threshhold for timing out a user interaction.

histogram.name: Tabs.TimeSinceLastView.OnTabClobber
histogram.summary: When a tab is revisited and subsequently the omnibox is focused within a certain threshold of time, this reports the amount of time in seconds that has passed since the last user engagement with the tab. Currently Android-only.

histogram.name: Tabs.TimeSinceLastView.OnTabClose
histogram.summary: When a tab is closed, this reports the amount of time in seconds that has passed since the last user engagement with the tab. Currently Android-only.

histogram.name: Tabs.TimeSinceLastView.OnTabView
histogram.summary: When a tab is revisited, this reports the amount of time in seconds that has passed since the last user engagement with the tab. Currently Android-only.

histogram.name: Tabs.UnusedAndClosedInInterval.Count
histogram.summary: The number of tabs that haven't been interacted with, visible or audible over a given interval time, but which no longer exist at all at the end of this interval.

histogram.name: Tabs.UnusedInInterval.Count
histogram.summary: The number of tabs that haven't been interacted with, visible or audible or visible during a given interval of time and still exist at the end of this interval.

histogram.name: Tabs.UsedAndClosedInInterval.Count
histogram.summary: The number of tabs that have been interacted with, visible or audible over a given interval of time, but which no longer exist at all at the end of this interval.

histogram.name: Tabs.UsedInInterval.Count
histogram.summary: The number of tabs that have been interacted with, visible or audible during a given interval of time, includes tabs that don't exist at the end of this interval.

histogram.name: Tabs.WindowCount
histogram.summary: Counts the number of Chrome windows, reported every 5 minutes. Desktop only.

histogram.name: TabsApi.RequestedWindowState
histogram.summary: The requested window creation state from the tabs extensions API when it's used to create a new window or update the state of an existing one.

histogram.name: TabStrip.PaintChildrenDuration
histogram.summary:
Total time spent in each call of TabStrip::PaintChildren(). This comprises the paint time for all parts of the TabStrip that do not paint to their own layers. This is recorded at the end of TabStrip::PaintChildren(), when all child views are finished painting.
As of writing (May 17, 2019), this always includes the tab backgrounds, strokes, labels, close buttons. The tab icons and new tab button sometimes paint to their own layer; in frames where this is so, their times won't be included here.

histogram.name: TabStrip.ReasonToShow
histogram.summary: When conditional tab strip shows, records which kind of intentional tab-related action triggered it.

histogram.name: TabStrip.Tab.Icon.PaintDuration
histogram.summary: Time spent painting a tab icon. This includes a page's favicon and loading animation (when loading). This is recorded at the end of TabIcon::OnPaint().

histogram.name: TabStrip.TabCountOnPageLoad
histogram.summary:
This histogram records the number of tabs within the group during page load.
If the loading happens in a tab that does not belong to a group, we log 0. Else, we log a positive number >= 2.

histogram.name: TabStrip.TimeToSwitch
histogram.summary: The amount of time from cursor entering the tabstrip to first click on a tab to switch.
histogram.details: This will exclude some kinds of switching (hotkeys, between windows) where it would be difficult to define a start time. Entering the tabstrip is an approximation which will vary between users, but should still be useful for A/B comparison.

histogram.name: TabStrip.UserStatus
histogram.summary: A tab strip session starts when the last time Chrome comes to the foreground was more than an hour ago. At the beginning of a tab strip session, records the user status of conditional tab strip feature in the last tab strip session.

histogram.name: TaskQueueManager.ActiveQueuesCount
histogram.summary: Used to track the number of active task queues in the task queue manager. Reported every time a task is selected for execution.

histogram.name: TaskQueueSelector.TaskServicedPerSelectorLogic
histogram.summary: Used to track the number of tasks serviced due to starvation versus the number of tasks serviced due to priroity. Incremented whenever a task queue is selected to service.

histogram.name: TaskScheduler.BlockShutdownTasksPostedDuringShutdown
histogram.obsolete: Removed 10/2018. Instead of recording a histogram with the number of BLOCK_SHUTDOWN tasks posted during shutdown, we generate a crash when too many BLOCK_SHUTDOWN tasks are posted during shutdown. A crash dump is more actionnable than a histogram.
histogram.summary: Number of BLOCK_SHUTDOWN tasks that were posted to a base::TaskScheduler after its Shutdown() method was called but before it returned.

histogram.name: TaskScheduler.DetachDuration
histogram.summary: Time elapsed between when the thread managed by a SchedulerWorker is detached and when the main function of a new thread managed by the same SchedulerWorker is entered (following a wake up). Recorded each time that a thread is recreated for a given SchedulerWorker.

histogram.name: TaskScheduler.HeartbeatLatencyMicroseconds
histogram.summary:
Latency of dummy "heartbeat" tasks posted with specific traits (see suffix). The heartbeat recording avoids dependencies between this report and other work in the system. See TaskScheduler.TaskLatencyMicroseconds.* for a metric that is closer to the real workload.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: TaskScheduler.NumActiveWorkers
histogram.summary: Number of workers running a task in a given SchedulerWorkerPool. Recorded every 59 minutes (sampling rate is not expected to affect the distribution).

histogram.name: TaskScheduler.NumCancelledDelayedTasks
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 03/2019. The number of cancelled delayed tasks found by the lazy approach is 0 more than 99% of the time.
histogram.summary: Lower-bound number of cancelled tasks that remain in the DelayedTaskManager. An exact number is not recorded, as it could potentially be expensive to calculate. Instead, a lazy count is maintained by noting cancelled tasks as new delayed tasks are added. Recorded every time the ServiceThread performs a heartbeat latency report.

histogram.name: TaskScheduler.NumTasksBeforeDetach
histogram.summary: Number of tasks executed by a SchedulerWorker before it detached. Recorded when a SchedulerWorker detaches.

histogram.name: TaskScheduler.NumTasksBetweenWaits
histogram.summary: Number of tasks executed by a SchedulerWorker between two waits on its WaitableEvent. This should be maximized without affecting perceived browser performance.

histogram.name: TaskScheduler.NumTasksRunWhileQueuing
histogram.summary: Number of tasks run by TaskScheduler while task was queuing (from time task was posted until time it was run). Recorded for dummy "heartbeat" tasks posted with specific traits (see suffix). The heartbeat recording avoids dependencies between this report and other work in the system. Recorded every time the ServiceThread performs a heartbeat latency report.

histogram.name: TaskScheduler.NumWorkers
histogram.summary: Number of workers that live in a given SchedulerWorkerPool. Recorded every 59 minutes (sampling rate is not expected to affect the distribution).

histogram.name: TaskScheduler.PercentCancelledDelayedTasks
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 03/2019. The number of cancelled delayed tasks found by the lazy approach is 0 more than 99% of the time.
histogram.summary: Lower-bound percentage of tasks in the DelayedTaskManager that have been cancelled. An exact number is not recorded, as it could potentially be expensive to calculate. Instead, a lazy count is maintained by noting cancelled tasks as new delayed tasks are added. Recorded every time the ServiceThread performs a heartbeat latency report.

histogram.name: TaskScheduler.TaskLatency
histogram.obsolete: Removed 4/2017. Units changed from milliseconds to microseconds.
histogram.summary: Time elapsed between when a task is posted and when it starts to run. Recorded for each task that runs inside the TaskScheduler. This metric is useful to determine true latency of the average task in the average runtime scenario. Its total count can also be used as a proxy for the number of tasks posted to a given category. On the other hand, a fallout of this metric is that timing every task means each report is dependent on the many things that can cause an individual task to be delayed (e.g. tasks on a single bogged down sequence will be reported with high latency even if not stalling the scheduler itself). See TaskScheduler.HeartbeatLatencyMicroseconds.* for a metric that is independent of such factors.

histogram.name: TaskScheduler.TaskLatencyMicroseconds
histogram.summary: Time elapsed between when a task is posted and when it starts to run. Recorded for each task that runs inside the TaskScheduler.

histogram.name: TerminalSystemAppFrame.WrenchMenu.MenuAction
histogram.summary: Number of times that each menu item is clicked from the terminal system app menu button.

histogram.name: TextFragmentAnchor.AmbiguousMatch
histogram.summary: Whether we found multiple matches for a selector. Recorded when TextFragmentAnchor finishes searching once the page is loaded.

histogram.name: TextFragmentAnchor.DidScrollIntoView
histogram.summary: Whether the page did a non-zero scroll in order to scroll the match into view. Only recorded if we found a match and scrolled it into view. Recorded when TextFragmentAnchor finishes searching once the page is loaded.

histogram.name: TextFragmentAnchor.DirectiveLength
histogram.summary: The raw length of the directive in the URL (e.g. spaces count as the three characters '%20'), regardless of how many text= terms there are. Recorded if there was a text directive, regardless of whether a match was found. Recorded when TextFragmentAnchor finishes searching once the page is loaded.

histogram.name: TextFragmentAnchor.ElementIdFragmentFound
histogram.summary: Whether an element-id based fragment was found in the document. Counted only when a fragment was specified and it wasn't parsed into a |targetText| style anchor.

histogram.name: TextFragmentAnchor.EndTextLength
histogram.summary: The length of the end parameter, in the case of a range match. Only recorded if a match was found. Recorded when TextFragmentAnchor finishes searching once the page is loaded.

histogram.name: TextFragmentAnchor.ExactTextLength
histogram.summary: The length of the matched text, in the case of an exact match. Only recorded if a match was found. Recorded when TextFragmentAnchor finishes searching once the page is loaded.

histogram.name: TextFragmentAnchor.ListItemMatch
histogram.summary: Whether the match was within a list item. Only recorded for exact text fragments and only if a match was found. Recorded when TextFragmentAnchor finishes searching once the page is loaded.

histogram.name: TextFragmentAnchor.MatchRate
histogram.summary: The percentage of selectors for which a match was found in the document. Recorded when TextFragmentAnchor finishes searching once the page is loaded.

histogram.name: TextFragmentAnchor.Parameters
histogram.summary: An enum indicating which parameters were specified in the text fragment anchor. Only recorded if a match was found. Recorded when TextFragmentAnchor finishes searching once the page is loaded.

histogram.name: TextFragmentAnchor.RangeMatchLength
histogram.summary: The length of the matched text, in the case of a range match. Only recorded if a match was found. Recorded when TextFragmentAnchor finishes searching once the page is loaded.

histogram.name: TextFragmentAnchor.ScrollCancelled
histogram.summary: Whether the scroll into view was cancelled by a user gesture or programmatic scroll. Recorded when TextFragmentAnchor finishes searching once the page is loaded.

histogram.name: TextFragmentAnchor.SelectorCount
histogram.summary: The number of selectors in the text fragment anchor if it is present. Recorded when TextFragmentAnchor finishes searching once the page is loaded.

histogram.name: TextFragmentAnchor.StartTextLength
histogram.summary: The length of the start parameter, in the case of a range match. Only recorded if a match was found. Recorded when TextFragmentAnchor finishes searching once the page is loaded.

histogram.name: TextFragmentAnchor.TableCellMatch
histogram.summary: Whether the match was within a table cell. Only recorded for exact text fragments and only if a match was found. Recorded when TextFragmentAnchor finishes searching once the page is loaded.

histogram.name: TextFragmentAnchor.TimeToScrollIntoView
histogram.summary: The time between creating the TextFragmentAnchor (i.e. when it is parsed) and scrolling the target into view. Only recorded if there is a match and we scroll it into view. Recorded when TextFragmentAnchor finishes searching once the page is loaded.

histogram.name: TextFragmentAnchor.TimeToScrollToTop
histogram.summary: The time between scrolling a match into view and the user scrolling back to the top of the page. Used as a signal for whether a match was useful. Only recorded if a match is scrolled into view and the user subsequently scrolled back to the top of the page. Recorded when TextFragmentAnchor finishes searching once the page is loaded.

histogram.name: TextToSpeech.Event
histogram.summary: Events fired by the text-to-speech engine when speaking an utterance.

histogram.name: TextToSpeech.Settings.DefaultVoicePicked
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2020 as it is no longer needed for analysis.
histogram.summary: The user picked a default voice of the specified voice ID, which is hashed from a JSON string of the voice name and extension ID.

histogram.name: TextToSpeech.Settings.PreviewVoiceClicked
histogram.obsolete: Removed 06/2020 as it is no longer needed for analysis.
histogram.summary: When the user requests a preview of a text-to-speech voice, records which voice was previewed.

histogram.name: TextToSpeech.Utterance.FromExtensionAPI
histogram.summary: Indicates if an utterance spoken via synthesized text-to-speech was triggered by the Chrome TTS extension API or the web speech API.

histogram.name: TextToSpeech.Utterance.HasGender
histogram.summary: True if an utterance spoken via synthesized text-to-speech requested a specific gender.

histogram.name: TextToSpeech.Utterance.HasLang
histogram.summary: True if an utterance spoken via synthesized text-to-speech specified a language code.

histogram.name: TextToSpeech.Utterance.HasPitch
histogram.summary: True if an utterance spoken via synthesized text-to-speech requested a specific pitch other than the default.

histogram.name: TextToSpeech.Utterance.HasRate
histogram.summary: True if an utterance spoken via synthesized text-to-speech requested a specific speech rate other than the default.

histogram.name: TextToSpeech.Utterance.HasVoiceName
histogram.summary: True if an utterance spoken via synthesized text-to-speech requested a specific voice by name.

histogram.name: TextToSpeech.Utterance.HasVolume
histogram.summary: True if an utterance spoken via synthesized text-to-speech requested a specific volume other than the default.

histogram.name: TextToSpeech.Utterance.Native
histogram.summary: True if an utterance is spoken with native speech provided by the operating system, otherwise it's spoken via a Chrome extension that implements text-to-speech support.

histogram.name: TextToSpeech.Utterance.TextLength
histogram.summary: The length of an utterance to be spoken via synthesized text-to-speech.

histogram.name: TextToSpeechEngine.ParseVoice.HasGender
histogram.summary: True if a voice parsed from a ttsEngine extension manifest has a specific gender. This is recorded each time a TTS extension is parsed, which may occur multiple times per extension, in tts_engine_manifest_handler: TtsVoices::Parse.

histogram.name: TextToSpeechEngine.UpdateVoice.HasGender
histogram.summary: True if a voice specified in chrome.ttsEngine.updateVoices by a ttsEngine extension has a specific gender.

histogram.name: ThirdPartyModules.Certificates.Microsoft
histogram.summary: The total number of certificates for modules loaded (or potentially loaded) into the browser process that are signed by Microsoft. A catalog counts as a single certificate, and may refer to many modules. Measured shortly after startup. Windows only.

histogram.name: ThirdPartyModules.Certificates.Total
histogram.summary: The total number of certificates for modules loaded (or potentially loaded) into the browser process. A catalog counts as a single certificate, and may refer to many modules. Measured shortly after startup. Windows only.

histogram.name: ThirdPartyModules.GetDriveLetterPathFound
histogram.summary: Records whether an equivalent driver letter path was found for a device path.

histogram.name: ThirdPartyModules.Heartbeat.BlockedModulesCount
histogram.summary: Records the number of modules that were blocked from loading into the browser process by the third-party DLL blocking feature. This is recorded every 5 minutes.

histogram.name: ThirdPartyModules.Heartbeat.PrintingWorkaround.BlockingEnabled
histogram.summary: Records whether or not Chrome is still blocking third-party DLLs. This is a bit that turns to false when the in-process printing is invoked. Recorded every 5 minutes.

histogram.name: ThirdPartyModules.Heartbeat.UniqueBlockedModulesCount
histogram.summary: Records the number of unique modules that were blocked from loading into the browser process by the third-party DLL blocking feature. This is recorded every 5 minutes.

histogram.name: ThirdPartyModules.InputMethodEditorsCount
histogram.summary: The number of registered input method editors found on the user's machine. This is emitted shortly after startup when the IME enumeration takes place.

histogram.name: ThirdPartyModules.InstalledApplications.GetDataTime
histogram.summary: Records the time it took to gather the list of installed third-party programs and their install location or components.

histogram.name: ThirdPartyModules.InstalledApplications.GotUserSidString
histogram.summary: Records whether retrieving the current user SID was successful.

histogram.name: ThirdPartyModules.InstalledPrograms.DataSize
histogram.summary: Records the size of the in-memory representation of the installed third-party programs on the user's machine.

histogram.name: ThirdPartyModules.InstalledPrograms.GetDataTime
histogram.summary: Records the time it took to gather the list of installed third-party programs and their install location or components.

histogram.name: ThirdPartyModules.Modules.Loaded
histogram.summary: The total number of third-party modules (neither Microsoft nor Google) that are loaded in the browser process. Measured shortly after startup. Windows only.

histogram.name: ThirdPartyModules.Modules.NotLoaded
histogram.summary: The total number of third-party modules (neither Microsoft nor Google) that are not yet loaded in the browser process, but may potentially be (shell extensions, for example). Measured shortly after startup. Windows only.

histogram.name: ThirdPartyModules.Modules.Signed
histogram.summary: The total number of signed modules loaded (or potentially loaded) into the browser process. Measured shortly after startup. Windows only.

histogram.name: ThirdPartyModules.Modules.Signed.Catalog
histogram.summary: The total number of modules loaded (or potentially loaded) into the browser process that are signed via a catalog. Measured shortly after startup. Windows only.

histogram.name: ThirdPartyModules.Modules.Signed.Microsoft
histogram.summary: The total number of modules loaded (or potentially loaded) into the browser process that are signed by Microsoft. Measured shortly after startup. Windows only.

histogram.name: ThirdPartyModules.Modules.Total
histogram.summary: The total number of modules loaded (or potentially loaded) into the browser process. Measured shortly after startup. Windows only.

histogram.name: ThirdPartyModules.Modules.Unsigned
histogram.summary: The total number of unsigned modules loaded (or potentially loaded) into the browser process. Measured shortly after startup. Windows only.

histogram.name: ThirdPartyModules.ShellExtensionsCount
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 14 Aug 2018 as reporting to this metric was incorrect since its inception. Replaced by ThirdPartyModules.ShellExtensionsCount2.
histogram.summary: The number of registered shell extensions found on the user's machine. This is emitted shortly after startup when the shell extensions enumeration takes place.

histogram.name: ThirdPartyModules.ShellExtensionsCount2
histogram.obsolete: Removed on 30/08/2018 now that the enumeration removes duplicate entries.
histogram.summary: The number of registered shell extensions found on the user's machine. This is emitted shortly after startup when the shell extensions enumeration takes place.

histogram.name: ThirdPartyModules.ShellExtensionsCount3
histogram.summary: The number of registered shell extensions found on the user's machine. This is emitted shortly after startup when the shell extensions enumeration is done. Doesn't count duplicates.

histogram.name: ThirdPartyModules.TimeDateStampObtained
histogram.summary: Indicates whether the TimeDateStamp for a loaded module was successfully obtained.

histogram.name: ThirdPartyModules.Uninstallable
histogram.summary: Records whether or not a loaded third party module could be uninstalled using the Windows Apps & Features page.

histogram.name: ThreadPool.DetachDuration
histogram.summary: Time elapsed between when the thread managed by a SchedulerWorker is detached and when the main function of a new thread managed by the same SchedulerWorker is entered (following a wake up). Recorded each time that a thread is recreated for a given SchedulerWorker.

histogram.name: ThreadPool.HeartbeatLatencyMicroseconds
histogram.summary:
Latency of dummy "heartbeat" tasks posted with specific traits (see suffix). The heartbeat recording avoids dependencies between this report and other work in the system. See ThreadPool.TaskLatencyMicroseconds.* for a metric that is closer to the real workload.
Warning: This metric may include reports from clients with low-resolution clocks (i.e. on Windows, ref. |TimeTicks::IsHighResolution()|). Such reports will cause this metric to have an abnormal distribution. When considering revising this histogram, see UMA_HISTOGRAM_CUSTOM_MICROSECONDS_TIMES for the solution.

histogram.name: ThreadPool.NumActiveWorkers
histogram.summary: Number of workers running a task in a given SchedulerWorkerPool. Recorded every 59 minutes (sampling rate is not expected to affect the distribution).

histogram.name: ThreadPool.NumTasksBeforeDetach
histogram.summary: Number of tasks executed by a SchedulerWorker before it detached. Recorded when a SchedulerWorker detaches.

histogram.name: ThreadPool.NumTasksBetweenWaits
histogram.obsolete: Removed 8/2019. Not used in active investigations.
histogram.summary: Number of tasks executed by a SchedulerWorker between two waits on its WaitableEvent. This should be maximized without affecting perceived browser performance.

histogram.name: ThreadPool.NumTasksInSequenceOnPush
histogram.obsolete: Stopped recording on 21/4/2020 due to a bug.
histogram.summary: Number of tasks in a ThreadPool sequence, recorded every time a task is pushed to a sequence. This is recorded to determine how often the size of a sequence grows above 10000 tasks, and whether it is reasonable to upload a process dump when that happens.

histogram.name: ThreadPool.NumTasksRunWhileQueuing
histogram.summary: Number of tasks run by ThreadPool while task was queuing (from time task was posted until time it was run). Recorded for dummy "heartbeat" tasks posted with specific traits (see suffix). The heartbeat recording avoids dependencies between this report and other work in the system. Recorded every time the ServiceThread performs a heartbeat latency report.

histogram.name: ThreadPool.NumWorkers
histogram.summary: Number of workers that live in a given SchedulerWorkerPool. Recorded every 59 minutes (sampling rate is not expected to affect the distribution).

histogram.name: ThreadPool.TaskLatencyMicroseconds
histogram.summary: Time elapsed between when a task is posted and when it starts to run. Recorded for each task that runs inside the ThreadPool.

histogram.name: ThreadWatcher.ResponseTime
histogram.summary: The time it takes indicated thread to respond with a pong message for a ping message from WatchDog thread.

histogram.name: ThreadWatcher.ResponsiveThreads
histogram.summary: This is the total number of watched threads that are responding when we got no response from the watched thread.

histogram.name: ThreadWatcher.Unresponsive
histogram.summary: This is the total unresponsive time since last pong message for the indicated thread when we got no response from the watched thread.

histogram.name: ThreadWatcher.UnresponsiveThreads
histogram.summary: This is the total number of watched threads that are not responding when we got no response from the watched thread.

histogram.name: Thumbnails.AddedToTopSites
histogram.summary: A page thumbnail (for use on the New Tab page) was added to TopSites.

histogram.name: Thumbnails.CaptureOutcome
histogram.summary: The result of trying to capture a thumbnail of the current page.

histogram.name: Thumbnails.CopyFromSurfaceTime
histogram.summary: While taking a screenshot of the current tab for use as a thumbnail, the time it took to copy the tab's contents into a bitmap.

histogram.name: Thumbnails.Gradient.ImageDetectionTime
histogram.summary: The time it takes to detect whether a thumbnail requires a gradient overlay. This is run when thumbnails are downloaded for content suggestions, as they load and the result is cached for future use. It is Android specific.

histogram.name: Thumbnails.Gradient.ImageRequiresGradient
histogram.summary: Whether a thumbnail used for a suggestions image requires a gradient to be overlaid to not fade into the background. It applies to the thumbnails used for content suggestions and is Android specific.

histogram.name: Thumbnails.ProcessBitmapTime
histogram.summary: While taking a screenshot of the current tab for use as a thumbnail, the time it took to post-process the captured bitmap.

histogram.name: TileManager.ExceededMemoryBudget
histogram.summary: Measures whether the tile manager exceeded the hard GPU memory budget (OOMed). Recorded each time the tile manager assigns GPU memory to tiles.

histogram.name: TileManager.TilesGPUMemoryUsage
histogram.obsolete: Replaced by Compositing.Renderer.GPUMemoryForTilingsInKb which is an existing metric tracking the same value.
histogram.summary: The amount of GPU memory, in kilobytes. Recorded on devices with less than or equal to 512MB of memory and when tile manager had sufficient memory to schedule all visible tiles.

histogram.name: TileManager.TilesGPUMemoryUsage2
histogram.obsolete: Replaced by Compositing.Renderer.GPUMemoryForTilingsInKb which is an existing metric tracking the same value.
histogram.summary: The amount of GPU memory used for tiles, in kilobytes. Recorded when the tile manager had sufficient memory to schedule all visible tiles.

histogram.name: TimeZone.TimeZoneRequest.Event
histogram.summary: Events in TimeZoneRequest.

histogram.name: TimeZone.TimeZoneRequest.ResponseCode
histogram.summary: Http response codes in TimeZoneRequest.

histogram.name: TimeZone.TimeZoneRequest.ResponseFailureTime
histogram.summary: The time elapsed between the sending of the first API request and the time the final (failed) response was recorded. Includes all retries.

histogram.name: TimeZone.TimeZoneRequest.ResponseSuccessTime
histogram.summary: The time elapsed between the sending of the first API request and the time the final (successfull) response was recorded. Includes all retries.

histogram.name: TimeZone.TimeZoneRequest.Result
histogram.summary: Result of TimeZoneRequest.

histogram.name: TimeZone.TimeZoneRequest.Retries
histogram.summary: Number of retries until the final response was recorded.

histogram.name: Toolbar.ActionsModel.ComponentActionsCount
histogram.obsolete: We no longer have component actions.
histogram.summary: The number of component action icons the Browser Actions Container knows about (visible or in the overflow bucket). Does not count icons that have been permanently hidden by the user. Measured once per startup per (non-incognito) profile.

histogram.name: Toolbar.ActionsModel.OverallActionsCount
histogram.obsolete: After the removal of component actions, this histogram is equivalent to ExtensionToolbarModel.BrowserActionsCount. See that histogram instead.
histogram.summary: The total number of action icons the Browser Actions Container knows about (visible or in the overflow bucket). Does not count icons that have been permanently hidden by the user. Measured once per startup per (non-incognito) profile.

histogram.name: Toolbar.ActionsModel.ToolbarActionsVisible
histogram.summary: The number of visible toolbar icons in the Browser Actions Container (visible as in number of icons not in the overflow bucket). 0 means all icons are in the overflow bucket. MAX_INT means the toolbar is always showing all icons. Measured once per startup per (non-incognito) profile but only for those profiles that have one or more browser actions showing in the toolbar.

histogram.name: Toolbar.AppMenuTimeToAction
histogram.summary: The number of millseconds between when the user opens the app menu and when the app menu is closed. Logged once per app menu run when the menu closes. Not recorded for menu runs that are initiated by a drag-and-drop sequence. Note that the code paths for Views-platforms vs Mac are different, so cross- platform comparison may not always be reasonable.

histogram.name: Toolbar.Menu.NewIncognitoTabPresentationDuration
histogram.summary: The number of millseconds between the user requesting a new incognito tab, e.g. by tapping the New Incognito Tab entry in the main tools menu, and it completing its animation on screen.

histogram.name: Toolbar.Menu.NewTabPresentationDuration
histogram.summary: The number of millseconds between the user requesting a new tab, e.g. by tapping the New Tab entry in the main tools menu, and it completing its animation on screen.

histogram.name: Toolbar.ShowToolsMenuResponsiveness
histogram.summary: Number of milliseconds passed between the event that triggers the presentation of the tools menu (the main menu of actions accessed from the toolbar), and the actual appearance of the menu on screen. Includes the animation duration.

histogram.name: Toolbar.TabSwitcher.NewIncognitoTabPresentationDuration
histogram.summary: The number of millseconds between the user requesting a new incognito tab, from within the tab switcher, e.g. by tapping the New Tab button from the tab switcher UI, and it completing its animation on screen.

histogram.name: Toolbar.TabSwitcher.NewTabPresentationDuration
histogram.summary: The number of millseconds between the user requesting a new tab, from within the tab switcher UI, e.g. by tapping the New Tab button from the tab switcher UI, and it completing its animation on screen.

histogram.name: TopSites.NumberOfApplyBlacklist
histogram.summary: The number of times TopSitesImpl::ApplyBlacklist is called.

histogram.name: TopSites.NumberOfBlacklistedItems
histogram.summary: The number of items in the user Most Visited blacklist every time TopSitesImpl::ApplyBlacklist is called.

histogram.name: TouchAction.EffectiveTouchAction
histogram.summary: Tracks how often each touch action is allowed (AKA the frequency of each effective touch action) at the end of each touch sequence.

histogram.name: TouchAction.EquivalentEffectiveAndWhiteListed
histogram.summary: Tracks how often the effective touch action computed by blink is or is not equivalent to the whitelisted touch action computed by the compositor at the end of each touch sequence.

histogram.name: TouchAction.GestureEventFiltered
histogram.summary: Tracks how often a gesture event is or is not dropped due to the current allowed touch action state not matching the gesture event.

histogram.name: TouchAction.GestureEventFilterResults
histogram.summary: Track whether a gesture event is allowed or filtered or deferred.

histogram.name: TouchBar.Default.Metrics
histogram.summary: Tracks the usage of the default touch bar buttons.

histogram.name: Touchpad.Acceleration.Changed
histogram.summary: Tracks touchpad acceleration setting changes by the user.

histogram.name: Touchpad.Acceleration.Started
histogram.summary: Tracks touchpad acceleration setting on startup.

histogram.name: Touchpad.Device
histogram.summary: Tracks touchpad device state.

histogram.name: Touchpad.Metrics
histogram.summary: Tracks unusual CrOS touchpad operational states (e.g. running into the noisy ground issue). This is sampled at every touchpad event.

histogram.name: Touchpad.NaturalScroll.Changed
histogram.summary: Tracks touchpad natural scroll setting changes by the user.

histogram.name: Touchpad.NaturalScroll.Started
histogram.summary: Tracks touchpad natural scroll setting on startup.

histogram.name: Touchpad.PointerSensitivity.Changed
histogram.summary: Tracks touchpad sensitivity setting changes by the user. This replaces the old Touchpad.Sensitivity.Changed metric.

histogram.name: Touchpad.PointerSensitivity.Started
histogram.summary: Tracks touchpad sensitivity setting on startup. This replaces the old Touchpad.Sensitivity.Started metric.

histogram.name: Touchpad.ScrollAcceleration
histogram.summary: Whether touchpad scroll acceleration is enabled.

histogram.name: Touchpad.ScrollSensitivity
histogram.summary: Touchpad scroll sensitivity value.

histogram.name: Touchpad.Sensitivity.Changed
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 6/2013, replaced by Touchpad.PointerSensitivity.Changed.
histogram.summary: Tracks touchpad sensitivity setting changes by the user.

histogram.name: Touchpad.Sensitivity.Started
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 6/2013, replaced by Touchpad.PointerSensitivity.Started.
histogram.summary: Tracks touchpad sensitivity setting on startup.

histogram.name: Touchpad.TapDragging.Changed
histogram.summary: Tracks touchpad TapDragging setting changes by the user.

histogram.name: Touchpad.TapDragging.Started
histogram.summary: Tracks touchpad TapDragging setting on startup.

histogram.name: Touchpad.TapToClick.Changed
histogram.summary: Tracks touchpad TapToClick setting changes by the user.

histogram.name: Touchpad.TapToClick.Started
histogram.summary: Tracks touchpad TapToClick setting on startup.

histogram.name: TouchScreen.MissedTOUCHEVENTF_UP
histogram.summary:
The touch device driver failed to send a TOUCHEVENTF_UP, and chromium generated a simulated event to maintain consistency/correctness.
This workaround will be removed once usage drops off. See https://crbug.com/811273 for details.

histogram.name: Touchscreen.TapDisambiguation
histogram.summary: How the user interacted with the tap disambiguation feature on Android.

histogram.name: Touchscreen.TouchEventsEnabled
histogram.summary:
Tracks whether Touch Event Feature Detection is enabled. This is based on the touch events enabled flag and the presence of a touchscreen.
Team: input-dev@chromium.org.
histogram.details: NOTE: This metric was incorrectly recorded on Chrome OS for versions 42 through 45, see http://crbug.com/499476 for more details. As of http://crbug.com/644318, we changed the internal name to TouchEventFeatureDetection, but we still keep the old histogram name here to keep consistensy.

histogram.name: TPM.EarlyResetDuringCommand
histogram.summary: Reported at boot if the previous boot session was interrupted in the middle of a TPM command during the first 30 seconds of uptime.

histogram.name: Tracing.Background.FinalizationDisallowedReason
histogram.summary: Reason why background tracing finalization was not allowed. Also see "Tracing.Background.ScenarioState" metric, which records the total number of times finalization was allowed and not allowed.

histogram.name: Tracing.Background.FinalizingTraceSizeInKB
histogram.summary: The size, in kilobytes, of a finalized trace ready to be uploaded.

histogram.name: Tracing.Background.NetworkConnectionTypeWhenStarted
histogram.summary: Connection type of the network used to determine the trace buffer size, when background tracing was triggered. Recorded only on Android.

histogram.name: Tracing.Background.NetworkConnectionTypeWhenUploaded
histogram.summary: Connection type of the network used to determine the trace upload limit, when background tracing upload was started. Recorded only on Android.

histogram.name: Tracing.Background.ScenarioState
histogram.summary: Records state of the Background Tracing system, from when scenarios are attempted to be activated until they're completed (successfully or failed)

histogram.name: Tracing.SharedBufferIsValid
histogram.summary: Records success or failure of creating the shared buffer in ProducerClient::InitSharedMemoryIfNeeded()

histogram.name: TrackedObjects.GetRetiredOrCreateThreadData
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 09/2017. Code measured by this histogram no longer exists.
histogram.summary: Time spent in base::ThreadData::GetRetiredOrCreateThreadData(). This method is called at most once per thread, when its name is set, when it posts a task or when it runs a task (whichever comes first).

histogram.name: TrafficStatsAmortizer.AmortizationDelay
histogram.summary: The amount of time spent waiting to perform an amortization run. This is logged at the end of each amortization run, before passing any DataUse objects to their respective callbacks.

histogram.name: TrafficStatsAmortizer.BufferSizeOnFlush
histogram.summary: The number of DataUse objects processed in an amortization run.

histogram.name: TrafficStatsAmortizer.ConcurrentTabs
histogram.summary: The number of unique tabs seen across all DataUse objects buffered for a single amortization run by the TrafficStatsAmortizer. This is recorded even if TrafficStats byte counts are unavailable, but not recorded if no DataUse objects have been buffered.

histogram.name: TrafficStatsAmortizer.PostAmortizationRunDataUseBytes
histogram.summary: The total bytes after performing amortization across all DataUse objects buffered for a single amortization run by the TrafficStatsAmortizer. This is recorded even if TrafficStats byte counts are unavailable, but not recorded if no DataUse objects have been buffered.

histogram.name: TrafficStatsAmortizer.PreAmortizationRunDataUseBytes
histogram.summary: The total bytes before performing amortization across all DataUse objects buffered for a single amortization run by the TrafficStatsAmortizer. This is recorded even if TrafficStats byte counts are unavailable, but not recorded if no DataUse objects have been buffered.

histogram.name: Translate.AcceptLanguages.CanBeAcceptDuration
histogram.summary: Time taken for the TranslateAcceptLanguages to decide if a given language is available as Accept-Languages.

histogram.name: Translate.AlwaysTranslateLang
histogram.summary: The number of times the always translate option was selected in the translate infobar.

histogram.name: Translate.BubbleUiEvent
histogram.summary: Tracks UI events related to the translate bubble.

histogram.name: Translate.CaptureText
histogram.summary: The time spent capturing plain text from the DOM. Pre sub frame translation support (this includes before M83), this is reported by ChromeRenderViewObserver. If sub frame translation is enabled, this is captured in the browser process and reported by ContentTranslateDriver.

histogram.name: Translate.CLD2.LanguageAccuracy
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 11/2017, since cld2 is deprecated.
histogram.summary: Accuracy of the language detected by CLD2. Only recorded if the detection returned a "known" result.

histogram.name: Translate.CLD2.LanguageDetected
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 11/2017, since cld2 is deprecated.
histogram.summary: Language of page detected by CLD2.

histogram.name: Translate.CLD3.LanguageDetected
histogram.summary: Language of the input page detected by CLD3. This information is logged on every page load.

histogram.name: Translate.CLD3.LanguagePercentage
histogram.summary: Percentage of the bytes that are associated with the most popular language on the input page. Only recorded if the detection returned a "known" result.

histogram.name: Translate.CompactInfobar.Event
histogram.summary: Various user actions performed in the translate infobar.

histogram.name: Translate.CompactInfobar.Language.AlwaysTranslate
histogram.summary: Records the hashcode of the source language when always translate this language option is clicked in the menu.

histogram.name: Translate.CompactInfobar.Language.MoreLanguages
histogram.summary: Records the hashcode of the language clicked on the more languages menu.

histogram.name: Translate.CompactInfobar.Language.NeverTranslate
histogram.summary: Records the hashcode of the source language when never translate this language option is clicked in the menu.

histogram.name: Translate.CompactInfobar.Language.PageNotIn
histogram.summary: Records the hashcode of the language clicked on the menu to indicate the page is not in the selected language.

histogram.name: Translate.CompactInfobar.Language.Translate
histogram.summary: Records the hashcode of the language clicked on the infobar.

histogram.name: Translate.CompactInfobar.TranslationsPerPage
histogram.summary: Records the number of times a page is translated, every time the page is translated. For instance on a page a) translation from A to B, we record "1" b) from A to B - we record "1" and revert from B to A - we record "2" c) from A to B - we record "1", then translation to C - we record "2". d) from A to B - we record "1", then translation to C - we record "2" and then revert to A - we record "3". We increment the translation count: a) every time the target language is changed. b) every time we revert to the original language (user can only revert to original language and not intermediate target languages). This essentially means 1 denotes >= 1 translations, 2 is >=2 translations per page and so on. This will give us a sense of how often people try out several translates on a page, or flip back and forth.

histogram.name: Translate.ContentLanguage
histogram.summary: A page may provide a Content-Language HTTP header or a META tag. For each page load, measures whether the Content-Language header exists and is valid.

histogram.name: Translate.DeclineTranslate
histogram.summary: The number of times the "Nope" (don't translate) or the infobar's X button was clicked in the translate infobar.

histogram.name: Translate.DeclineTranslateCloseInfobar
histogram.summary: The number of times the translate infobar was closed by clicking the X button without the user translating the page.

histogram.name: Translate.DeclineTranslateDismissUI
histogram.summary: The number of times the translate UI was closed without translating in the way that the user doesn't deny translating explicityly, like pressing 'Nope' button. This is counted on both the infobar and the bubble UI. We are comparing this on infobar to that on bubble by A/B testing and expecting that the user will click 'Nope' button on bubble less times than infobar. We won't delete this histogram after the experiment.

histogram.name: Translate.ExplicitLanguageAsk.Event
histogram.summary: The events (shown, saved, cancelled) happening in the Explicit Language Ask prompt.

histogram.name: Translate.ExplicitLanguageAsk.LanguageAdded
histogram.summary: The languages that were added to the Accept Languages list from the Explicit Language Ask prompt.

histogram.name: Translate.ExplicitLanguageAsk.LanguageRemoved
histogram.summary: The languages that were removed from the Accept Languages list from the Explicit Language Ask prompt.

histogram.name: Translate.ForceTriggerBackoffStateReached
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 04/2020. No longer used for analysis.
histogram.summary: Whether or not the backoff threshold for triggering Translate on UI-language content is reached when the translation process is initiated.

histogram.name: Translate.HrefHint.Status
histogram.summary: For Google navigations, the hrefTranslate hint may trigger a translation automatically. If the hint is present on a Google navigation, record whether or not automatic translation happened. If automatic translation was already going to happen, then we use the auto translate target language. Also record if this caused the target language to be different than the hrefTranslate target language.

histogram.name: Translate.HtmlLang
histogram.summary: A page may provide a lang attribute in html tag. For each page load, measures whether the lang attribute exists and is valid.

histogram.name: Translate.InfobarShown
histogram.summary: The number of times the translate infobar was shown in the old translate UI on Android. Only true is recorded.

histogram.name: Translate.InitiationStatus
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 11/2013, and replaced by Translate.InitiationStatus.v2.
histogram.summary: The reason why Chrome decided to perform the next action (e.g., to show infobar, to translate a page without any prompting, and so on) when Chrome Translate is ready to translate a page.

histogram.name: Translate.InitiationStatus.v2
histogram.summary: The reason why Chrome decided to perform the next action (e.g., to show infobar, to translate a page without any prompting, and so on) when Chrome Translate is ready to translate a page.

histogram.name: Translate.LanguageDetection.ContentLength
histogram.summary: The number of characters of page content used for language detection.

histogram.name: Translate.LanguageDetectionConflict
histogram.summary:
This metric logs CLD3-detected languages for pages that specify a conflicting English language in their markup. In these cases, we must decide on a language source to favor.
Bucket names are of the form "x,y", where x is the common English language code specified in the page markup (or "other" if the English language code is uncommon), and y is the CLD3-detected language.
This histogram only logs detection conflicts. Hence, "matching" buckets (e.g. "en,en", "en-AU,en") will never be populated.

histogram.name: Translate.LanguageDetectionTiming
histogram.summary: For each page load, records whether language detection occurs on time or gets deferred. If deferred language detection later completes, this is also recorded. This allows measuring the UX impact of using a non-static CLD data source.

histogram.name: Translate.LanguageSettingsIsShown
histogram.summary: Log everytime the language settings page is shown. This can be either user visits chrome://settings/languages or user visits the advanced languages card in chrome://settings. With this stat, we will be able to understand how well the language model is doing by looking into how the number changes over time. In additiona to that, we are also interested in how many users ever visit the languages settings card.

histogram.name: Translate.LanguageVerification
histogram.summary: For each page load, measures whether the provided HTML language (i.e. the page lang attribute if it exists, otherwise the header Content-Language value) matches the language determined by CLD. Beyond directly matching or mismatching the HTML language, CLD can complement the HTML language. For example, suppose the HTML language is 'zh' (general Chinese), a language code that the Translate server does not support. In this case, CLD can detect a subcode like '-TW' or '-CN', resulting in language codes 'zh-TW' and 'zh-CN', which the Translate server supports. This is referred to as "complementing a language subcode".

histogram.name: Translate.LocalesOnDisabledByPrefs
histogram.summary: Logs the user locale when the Translate feature is disabled by the user. This is recorded each time a webpage is loaded and prefs for translation is checked. This allows us to investigate the correlation between the user locale and the usage rates of the Translate.

histogram.name: Translate.MobileMenuTranslate.Shown
histogram.summary: Whether the 'Translate' app menu entry is shown to the user. Emitted when the app menu (three dots) is shown and a translation could be performed.

histogram.name: Translate.ModifyOriginalLang
histogram.summary: The number of times the original language in the translate infobar has been changed.

histogram.name: Translate.ModifyTargetLang
histogram.summary: The number of times the target language in the translate infobar has been changed.

histogram.name: Translate.NeverTranslateLang
histogram.summary: The number of times the never translate option was selected in the translate infobar.

histogram.name: Translate.NeverTranslateSite
histogram.summary: The number of times the never translate site was selected in the translate infobar.

histogram.name: Translate.PageCaptured
histogram.summary: The time spent capturing plain text from the DOM. This is reported by ChromeRenderViewObserver when a page is loaded completely.

histogram.name: Translate.PageScheme
histogram.summary: Counts translation target page schemes.

histogram.name: Translate.Ranker.Model.Status
histogram.summary: Tracks the outcome of attempts to download a Translate Ranker Model.

histogram.name: Translate.Ranker.Model.Version
histogram.summary: The date tamp (e.g., 20160916 -> 15 Sept 2016) which denotes the TranslateRankerModel's version.

histogram.name: Translate.Ranker.QueryResult
histogram.summary: Whether the TranslateRanker accepts or denies to show the translation prompt.

histogram.name: Translate.Ranker.Timer.CalculateScore
histogram.summary: Time taken for the TranslateRanker to use the translate ranker model to calculate a score for the translation, in ms.

histogram.name: Translate.Ranker.Timer.DownloadModel
histogram.summary: Time taken for the Translate Ranker Model Loader to download its model from the configured URL, in ms.

histogram.name: Translate.Ranker.Timer.ParseModel
histogram.summary: Time taken for the Translate Ranker Model Loader to parse its model, in ms.

histogram.name: Translate.Ranker.Timer.ReadModel
histogram.summary: Time taken for the Translate Ranker Model Loader to read its model from local storage (cache), in ms.

histogram.name: Translate.Ranker.Timer.ShouldOfferTranslation
histogram.summary: Time taken for the TranslateRanker to decide if a given translation should be offered or not, in ms. This includes the time taken to extract the relevant features upon which to base the decision, as well as the time taken to calculate the result.

histogram.name: Translate.Ranker.Timer.WriteModel
histogram.summary: Time taken for the Translate Ranker Model Loader to write its model to local storage, in ms.

histogram.name: Translate.ReportLanguageDetectionError
histogram.summary: The number of times the "report this error" of options menu is selected in the translate infobar.

histogram.name: Translate.RevertTranslation
histogram.summary: The number of times the show original button was clicked in the translate infobar.

histogram.name: Translate.ServerReportedUnsupportedLanguage
histogram.summary: The number of times the detected language is not supported by Translate Element.

histogram.name: Translate.ShowBeforeTranslateInfobar
histogram.summary: The number of times an infobar proposing to translate a page has been shown.

histogram.name: Translate.ShowErrorInfobar
histogram.summary: Chrome Translate shows an error infobar when an error happens on translation and the infobar message depends on what kind of error happens. This metric counts how often each error message is shown.

histogram.name: Translate.ShowErrorUI
histogram.summary: Chrome Translate shows an error UI (infobar or bubble) when an error happens on translation and the UI message depends on what kind of error happens. This metric counts how often each error message is shown.

histogram.name: Translate.SimilarLanguageMatch
histogram.summary: This metrics is logged whenever a page is loaded. The logged value is "Mathced" when the CLD-detected language differs from the page language code , and the two languages are such similar languages. In that case, Chrome ignore the CLD-determined language and instead uses the page language code. The page language code is decided by Content-Language and HTML lang attribute.

histogram.name: Translate.SourceLanguage
histogram.summary: The number of requests sent to the Translate server, grouped by source language.

histogram.name: Translate.TargetLanguage
histogram.summary: The number of requests sent to the Translate server, grouped by target language.

histogram.name: Translate.TargetLanguage.Origin
histogram.summary: Where the target language was determined from. Can be the most recent target language, from the language model, the UI language, the user's accept languages, or default to English.

histogram.name: Translate.TimeToBeReady
histogram.summary: The time from injecting scripts for Chrome Translate to being ready to perform translation.

histogram.name: Translate.TimeToLoad
histogram.summary: The time from injecting scripts for Chrome Translate to the finishing loads of all depending libraries.

histogram.name: Translate.TimeToTranslate
histogram.summary: The time from starting translation to the completion.

histogram.name: Translate.Translate
histogram.summary: The number of times the translate button was clicked in the translate infobar.

histogram.name: Translate.Translate.AMPCacheURL
histogram.summary: The number of times the translate button was clicked in the translate infobar for a page that is likely an AMP Cache URL.

histogram.name: Translate.TranslateFrameCount
histogram.summary: The number of frames translated for a translated page (including the main frame). This is recorded when sub frame translation is enabled.

histogram.name: Translate.TranslateSubframe.ErrorType
histogram.summary: The error type for a failed sub frame translation (where the main frame was successfully translated). This is recorded when sub frame translation is enabled.

histogram.name: Translate.TranslateSubframe.SuccessPercentage
histogram.summary: The percentage of sub frames that translated successfully for a translated page (where the main frame was successfully translated). This is recorded when sub frame translation is enabled.

histogram.name: Translate.UndisplayableLanguage
histogram.summary: Logs an undisplayable language included in the language list sent by the Translate server. The Translate server sends the list each time the user runs Chrome. This metrics tells us that there is a language which UI should support but doesn't.

histogram.name: Translate.UnsupportedLanguageAtInitiation
histogram.summary: Logs an unsupported source language detected during initiation of the Translate feature. This is reported when the language detector successfully detects the language of the webpage, but the language is not supported by the translation server because it is too minor. This metric allows us to assess how important the unsupported language is for Google translate.

histogram.name: Translate.UserActionDuration
histogram.summary: The time from a page content language being determined to user requesting Chrome Translate.

histogram.name: TrustedWebActivity.ClearDataDialogOnClearAppDataAccepted
histogram.summary: Emits true if the user went to site settings from the dialog that is shown after a Trusted Web Activity client app has had its data cleared.

histogram.name: TrustedWebActivity.ClearDataDialogOnUninstallAccepted
histogram.summary: Emits true if the user went to site settings from the dialog that is shown after a Trusted Web Activity client app has been uninstalled.

histogram.name: TrustedWebActivity.DelegatedNotificationSmallIconFallback
histogram.summary: Logs which kind of fallback for notification small icon was used for Trusted Web Activity notification delegation.

histogram.name: TrustedWebActivity.LocationDelegationEnrolled
histogram.summary: When a site running in Truested Web Activity is accessing geolocation, records whether the last tracked focused Trusted Web Activity client app enrolled location delegation.

histogram.name: TrustedWebActivity.LocationPermissionChanged
histogram.summary: Records the new permission state when a Trusted Web Activity client app's location permission is changed.

histogram.name: TrustedWebActivity.LocationPermissionRequestIsGranted
histogram.summary: Records the boolean result (granted or not) from requesting a Trusted Web Activity client app's location permission.

histogram.name: TrustedWebActivity.ShareTargetRequest
histogram.summary: Recorded when data is shared via a Trusted Web Activity.

histogram.name: TrustedWebActivity.SplashScreenShown
histogram.summary: Records whether or not a splash screen has been shown when launching a Trusted Web Activity.

histogram.name: TrustedWebActivity.TimeInVerifiedOrigin
histogram.obsolete: Retired in M77 in favour of TrustedWebActivity.TimeInVerifiedOrigin.V2 which uses a more appropriate timescale.
histogram.summary: Time spent in a verified origin until navigating to an unverified one or pausing the Trusted Web Activity.

histogram.name: TrustedWebActivity.TimeInVerifiedOrigin.V2
histogram.summary: Time spent in a verified origin until navigating to an unverified one or pausing the Trusted Web Activity.

histogram.name: TrustedWebActivity.TimeOutOfVerifiedOrigin
histogram.obsolete: Retired in M77 in favour of TrustedWebActivity.TimeOutOfVerifiedOrigin.V2 which uses a more appropriate timescale.
histogram.summary: Time spent out of verified origins until navigating back to a verified one or pausing the Trusted Web Activity.

histogram.name: TrustedWebActivity.TimeOutOfVerifiedOrigin.V2
histogram.summary: Time spent out of verified origins until navigating back to a verified one or pausing the Trusted Web Activity.

histogram.name: TrustedWebActivity.TranslucencyRemovalFailed
histogram.summary: Records the cases when removing a Trusted Web Activity's translucency via a reflective call fails. Only "true" is recorded.

histogram.name: TryScroll.SlowScroll
histogram.obsolete: Removed 02/2016 in Issue 1741103002, and replaced by Renderer4.CompositorWheelScrollUpdateThread and Renderer4.CompositorTouchScrollUpdateThread.
histogram.summary: Whether a scroll is executed on main thread.

histogram.name: UI.CompositorResizeLock.Duration
histogram.summary: The amount of time the CompositorResizeLock was held in milliseconds. This is a measure of jank as UI will not generate new frames as long as the lock is held.

histogram.name: UI.CompositorResizeLock.TimedOut
histogram.summary: Tracks whether the CompositorResizeLock is being released due to timing out or not.

histogram.name: UI.DeviceScale
histogram.summary: The device scales available on the system at startup. A system may report more than one if it has multiple displays with varying device scales. Only logged on Windows.

histogram.name: UI.WindowTreeHost.SurfaceSynchronizationDuration
histogram.summary: The amount of time it took for UI's CompositorFrame to activate due to a surface synchronization event. This a measure of jank as UI will not generate new frames as long synchronization is in progress. This metric is similar to UI.CompositorResizeLock.Duration but measures the new synchronization code path.

histogram.name: UKM.ActualLogUploadInterval
histogram.summary: The actual interval between log upload start and previous log upload finished within the same process. A value of 0 indicates there was no previous upload.

histogram.name: UKM.BuildAndStoreLogIsEmpty
histogram.summary: Emitted when UKM Service attempts to build and store a log. Emits true if it was empty (no sources or entries) which means no log is written, false otherwise.

histogram.name: UKM.ConsentObserver.AllowedForAllProfiles
histogram.summary: Logged in UpdateUkmAllowedForAllProfiles from the UkmConsentStateObserver which is called when UkmConsentStateObserver starts observing a new profile and every time a URL-keyed anonymized data collection state changes or a sync service state changes. This records whether UKM is allowed for all profiles.

histogram.name: UKM.ConsentObserver.Purge
histogram.summary: Logged in the UpdateProfileState call from the UkmConsentStateObserver. This records if the UKM allowed change will trigger a purge of the local UKM data.

histogram.name: UKM.Entries.Dropped
histogram.summary: Number of UKM entries that are dropped. Categorized by the cause of it being dropped.

histogram.name: UKM.Entries.Dropped.ByEntryHash
histogram.summary: The hashed name of the UKM entry being dropped. The hash is the same hash sent in the UKM log but truncated to 31 bits with "event_hash & 0x7fffffff". Recorded when adding an entry and the UKM recorder decides it should be dropped.

histogram.name: UKM.Entries.SerializedCount
histogram.summary: Number of serialized UKM entries when storing a UKM log.

histogram.name: UKM.Entries.SerializedCount2
histogram.summary: Number of serialized UKM entries when storing a UKM log.

histogram.name: UKM.Entries.SerializedCountFCP
histogram.summary: Number of serialized UKM entries that are of type PageLoad that have the metric PaintTiming.NavigationToFirstContentfulPaint. This is recorded every time a UKM log is stored.

histogram.name: UKM.InitSequence
histogram.summary: Logged during UkmService initialization whether the init task or the initial log timer completed first. The expectation is the vast majority of the time, the init task should complete first. If metrics show otherwise, then it may indicate there's a bug in the UkmService init sequence and that it should be investigated.

histogram.name: UKM.LogSize.OnSuccess
histogram.summary: Size in kilobytes (after compression) of an uploaded UKM log. Recorded after a successful UKM upload.

histogram.name: UKM.LogUpload.Canceled.CellularConstraint
histogram.summary: Logs whether a log was not uploaded due to cellular log throttling logic. Android only.

histogram.name: UKM.LogUpload.ResponseOrErrorCode
histogram.summary: HTTP response codes and network errors encountered by UKM when attempting to upload logs to the server.

histogram.name: UKM.LogUploader.UploadSize
histogram.summary: Reports total upload length in bytes when the UKM service type is used.

histogram.name: UKM.MigratedClientIdInt64ToUInt64
histogram.summary: The client id was migrated from an Int64 pref to a UInt64 pref. Not migrated is recorded when a valid UInt64 value is loaded from prefs. Migration started in M75.

histogram.name: UKM.PersistentLogRecall.Status
histogram.summary: The status when loading UKM PersistedLogs from Prefs.

histogram.name: UKM.ResetReason
histogram.summary: Recorded when a UKM client state (client_id, session_id) is reset. This emits the reason the resetting was triggered.

histogram.name: UKM.Sources.Dropped
histogram.summary: Number of UKM sources that are dropped. Categorized by the cause of it being dropped.

histogram.name: UKM.Sources.KeptSourcesCount
histogram.summary: Number of unsent UKM sources that were kept in-memory to be potentially transmitted in the next log.

histogram.name: UKM.Sources.SerializedCount
histogram.summary: Number of serialized UKM sources when storing a UKM log.

histogram.name: UKM.Sources.SerializedCount2
histogram.summary: Number of serialized UKM sources when storing a UKM log.

histogram.name: UKM.Sources.UnmatchedSourcesCount
histogram.summary: Number of UKM sources that were not sent because they did not match a navigation source URL.

histogram.name: UKM.Sources.UnsentSourcesCount
histogram.summary: Number of UKM sources that were not sent because they had no associated entries.

histogram.name: UKM.SyncDisable.Info
histogram.summary: Information about the determination by the UkmConsentStateObserver on why it enabled or disabled UKM.

histogram.name: UKM.SyncDisable.Purge
histogram.summary: Logged in the UpdateSyncState call from the UkmConsentStateObserver. This records if the UKM allowed change will trigger a purge of the local UKM data.

histogram.name: UKM.UnsentLogs.DroppedSize
histogram.summary: Number of bytes in an excessively large log that was discarded while persisting UKM logs to disk to retry during next chrome run.

histogram.name: UKM.UnsentLogs.NumDropped
histogram.summary: Counter for number of UKM unsent logs removed from persistent storage.

histogram.name: UKM.Upload.ResponseCode
histogram.summary: For each upload to the UKM server, log the response received from the server.

histogram.name: UKM.UserDemographics.Status
histogram.summary: The status of the retrieval of user demographics from sync that are reported to UKM. Emitted each time UKM metrics are reported.

histogram.name: UMA.ActualLogUploadInterval
histogram.summary: The actual interval between log upload start and previous log upload finished within the same process. A value of 0 indicates there was no previous upload. Prior to M71, logging for this metric was broken.

histogram.name: UMA.AntiVirusMetricsProvider.Result
histogram.summary: Records the result of the attempt to enumerate the installed AntiVirus on the machine. This is recorded once during metrics initialization.

histogram.name: UMA.CleanExitBeaconConsistency
histogram.summary: Reports the combined state of distinct clean exit beacons stored in Local State and the Windows registry. They are normally expected to be identical.

histogram.name: UMA.ClientIdBackupRecoveredWithAge
histogram.summary: Recorded when we are somehow missing the client ID stored in Local State yet are able to recover it from a backup location along with the backed up installation date. This report carries the age in hours of the recovered client id.

histogram.name: UMA.ClientIdMigrated
histogram.summary: Recorded when the one-time UMA client id reset was performed (and the client id of this user was migrated).

histogram.name: UMA.CollectExternalEventsTime
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of August 2015. The histogram showed no unexpected slowness, and a profiler is a better tool for identifying any future issues.
histogram.summary: The time to run the external metrics collection task (Chrome OS).

histogram.name: UMA.ComputeCurrentSigninStatus
histogram.obsolete: Removed as of Jun 2016. The histogram was added for debugging purpose and is not needed anymore.
histogram.summary: Records attempts to compute the current the signin status and error encountered when computing.

histogram.name: UMA.CreatePersistentHistogram.Result
histogram.obsolete: Removed April, 2018 as the information has never shown any problems.
histogram.summary: Records attempts to create histograms in presistent space and any errors encountered when doing so.

histogram.name: UMA.Debug.EnableCrashUpload.DeferredStartUpAsyncTaskDuration
histogram.obsolete: No longer useful or monitored, removed 2020-01-06.
histogram.summary: Measures how much time it took to complete all async deferred startup tasks on the background thread. Only logged on Android.

histogram.name: UMA.Debug.EnableCrashUpload.DeferredStartUpCompleteTime
