histogram.name: WinJumplist.RatioAddCategoryTime
histogram.obsolete: Obsolete 07/23/2017 as it's no longer needed.
histogram.summary: The ratio of the duration spent adding the most-visited category compared to the duration spent adding the recently-closed category, multiplied by 10.

histogram.name: WinJumplist.StartLoadingFaviconDuration
histogram.obsolete: Obsolete 06/22/2017 as it's no longer needed.
histogram.summary: Time spent in StartLoadingFavicon().

histogram.name: WinJumplist.UpdateJumpListDuration
histogram.obsolete: Obsolete 04/20/2017 as it's no longer accurate due to the code change.
histogram.summary: Time spent in UpdateJumpList(). This method is called whenever there is a jumplist update.

histogram.name: WinJumplistUpdater.AddCustomCategoryDuration
histogram.obsolete: Obsolete 07/26/2017 as it's no longer needed.
histogram.summary: Time spent in JumpListUpdater::AddCustomCategory.

histogram.name: WinJumplistUpdater.AddTasksDuration
histogram.obsolete: Obsolete 07/10/2017 as it's no longer needed.
histogram.summary: Time spent in JumpListUpdater::AddTasks.

histogram.name: WinJumplistUpdater.BeginUpdateDuration
histogram.obsolete: Obsolete 07/26/2017 as it's no longer needed.
histogram.summary: Time spent in JumpListUpdater::BeginUpdate.

histogram.name: WinJumplistUpdater.CommitUpdateDuration
histogram.obsolete: Obsolete 07/26/2017 as it's no longer needed.
histogram.summary: Time spent in JumpListUpdater::CommitUpdate.

histogram.name: WinTimeTicks.FailedToChangeCores
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: Incremented each time the TimeTicks field trial runs on a machine with multiple cores, but failed to change thread affinity. Broken down by Windows version.

histogram.name: WinTimeTicks.MinResolutionNanoseconds
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The smallest non-zero delta reported by subsequent calls to QueryPerformanceCounter.

histogram.name: WinTimeTicks.NonStopTsc
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: True if the CPU's timestamp counter ticks at a constant rate regardless of CPU frequency.

histogram.name: WinTimeTicks.TickedBackwards
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of times the TimeTicks field trial failed because QueryPerformanceCounter ticked backwards. Broken down by Windows version.

histogram.name: WinTimeTicks.VersionSuccessful
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of times the TimeTicks field trial succeeded. Broken down by Windows version.

histogram.name: WinTimeTicks.VersionTotal
histogram.owner: Please list the metric's owners. Add more owner tags as needed.
histogram.summary: The number of times the TimeTicks field trial ran for comparison with WinTimeTicks.VersionSuccess. Broken down by Windows version.

histogram.name: Worker.TopLevelScript.OriginType.RequestUrl.DedicatedWorker
histogram.summary: Type of worker top-level script's request URL's origin, relative to parent execution context's origin, recorded on each successful classic dedicated worker creation.

histogram.name: WorkerScheduler.WorkerThreadLoad
histogram.summary: Worker thread load, i.e. percentage of time spent on running tasks. This metric is emitted at most once per kWorkerThreadLoadTrackerReportingInterval sec per worker thread amortized. E.g. if a worker ran a single task for X seconds and then went to idle for Y seconds before it shuts down, and assuming the interval rate was 1 sec, we get X samples for 100% and Y samples for 0%.

histogram.name: WorkerThread.DebuggerTask.Time
histogram.summary: The time taken for running a debugger task on WorkerThread.

histogram.name: WorkerThread.ExitCode
histogram.summary: Records the exit code of WorkerThread.

histogram.name: WorkerThread.Runtime
histogram.summary: The amount of time a worker thread ran for. Starts recording when a worker scheduler for the thread is created, and stops when the scheduler is shut down.

histogram.name: WorkerThread.Task.Time
histogram.obsolete: Removed on 2018-02. The sample count was overflowing. crbug.com/809672.
histogram.summary: The time taken for running a worker task on WorkerThread.

histogram.name: WrenchMenu.MenuAction
histogram.summary: Number of times that each menu item is clicked.

histogram.name: WrenchMenu.OpeningAnimationFrameTimes
histogram.obsolete: Removed on 2020-04. Bucket sizes are not very useful for measuring jank. The only user of AnimationFrameTimeHistogram.
histogram.summary: Frame times of the Android wrench menu opening animation. For example, if the menu opening animation runs exactly at 60fps for a second, then each frame time is 16ms, and a total of 60 values of 16ms are recorded. If the animation is janky, we will see values greater than 16ms in the histogram.

histogram.name: WrenchMenu.RecentTabsSubMenu
histogram.summary: The number of times each tab or window restore option in the Recent Tabs submenu is clicked.

histogram.name: WrenchMenu.TimeToAction
histogram.summary: The time a user takes to select a menu item after opening the menu.

histogram.name: WrenchMenu.TouchDuration
histogram.summary: Time difference between touch down and touch up on Android wrench button.

histogram.name: X11.XInternAtomFailure
histogram.summary: Counts how often the X11 server fails to register an atom. Recorded when ::XInternAtom fails (returns None). Failure rates will inform whether we implement error handling. https://crbug.com/1000919

histogram.name: XHR.Async.PageDismissal
histogram.summary: Records occurence of async XHR during page dismissal state (unload, beforeunload, pagehide etc).

histogram.name: XHR.Sync.BlockingTime.MainThread
histogram.summary: Records the time spent in sync XHR requests on the main thread.

histogram.name: XHR.Sync.BlockingTime.WorkerThread
histogram.summary: Records the time spent in sync XHR requests in a worker thread.

histogram.name: XHR.Sync.PageDismissal
histogram.summary: Records occurence of sync XHR during page dismissal state (unload, beforeunload, pagehide etc).

histogram.name: XHR.Sync.PageDismissal_forbidden
histogram.summary: Records occurence of sync XHR requests during page dismissal state (unload, beforeunload, pagehide etc) that were forbidden.

histogram.name: XR.RuntimeAvailable
histogram.summary: Indicates which VR APIs are installed. Recorded shortly after startup.

histogram.name: XR.RuntimeUsed
histogram.summary: Indicates which XR runtime was used for a session. Recorded when the session was successfully created.

histogram.name: XR.VRSession.StartAction
histogram.summary: The action that triggered the transition into Chrome in VR, either from 2D or another VR app.

histogram.name: XR.WebXR.ConsentFlow
histogram.summary: Records the actions of a user consent dialog which is displayed on requesting an XR session.

histogram.name: XR.WebXR.ConsentFlowDuration.ConsentFlowAborted
histogram.summary: Time the user takes to abort the consent flow by clicking on the window-close system button of a session request consent dialog.

histogram.name: XR.WebXR.ConsentFlowDuration.ConsentGranted
histogram.summary: Time the user takes to click on the allow-and-enter-VR button on a session request consent dialog.

histogram.name: XR.WebXR.ConsentFlowDuration.ConsentNotGranted
histogram.summary: Time the user takes to click on dont-allow button on a session request consent dialog.

histogram.name: XR.WebXR.PresentationSession
histogram.summary: The action that triggered VR presentation, which is the display of web content on a VR device.

histogram.name: XR.WebXR.ReferenceSpace.Requested
histogram.summary: Records which reference space was requested when a site calls XRSession.requestReferenceSpace().

histogram.name: XR.WebXR.ReferenceSpace.Succeeded
histogram.summary: Records which reference spaces are successfully created when the promise returned by XRSession.requestReferenceSpace() is resolved.

histogram.name: XR.WebXR.RenderPath.Used
histogram.obsolete: Removed July 2019. This was being tracked in case the assumptions about feature availability based on platform version turned out to be incorrect, but it appears that this is working as intended. The webxr-render-path flag that could be used to override this at runtime was previously removed in https://crrev.com/c/1487074.
histogram.summary: Records the WebXR/WebVR render path used for presentation on presentation start. The render path is affected by overrides and device capabilities.

histogram.name: ZeroSuggest.AllResults
histogram.summary: The number of results (either query or URL) from ZeroSuggest. This is set every time a successful response from ZeroSuggest is received, which can be every time the user focuses on the omnibox.

histogram.name: ZeroSuggest.QueryResults
histogram.summary: The number of query results returned from ZeroSuggest. This is set every time a successful response from ZeroSuggest is received, which can be every time the user focuses on the omnibox.

histogram.name: ZeroSuggest.URLResults
histogram.summary: The number of URL results returned from ZeroSuggest. This is set every time a successful response from ZeroSuggest is received, which can be every time the user focuses on the omnibox.

suffix.name: EveryReport
suffix.label: sent with every metrics report, and preferred for computing uniques

suffix.name: OnChange
suffix.label: Driven from a change in signin status or change in content area GAIA accounts.

histogram_suffixes.name: AccountReconcilorExecution
histogram_suffixes.obsolete: Removed M80. Only affects one histogram, which is deprecated.

suffix.name: Offline

suffix.name: Online

suffix.name: RestrictedPool

suffix.name: Tabbed
suffix.label: Chrome starts up with intent to ChromeTabbedActivity

suffix.name: WebApk
suffix.label: Chrome starts up with intent to WebApkActivity

suffix.name: Image
suffix.label: Resources identified by any supported image mime type.

suffix.name: JS
suffix.label: Resources identified by any supported javascript mime type.

suffix.name: Other
suffix.label: Resources identified by any mime type that did match any supported css, html, image, javascript, or video mime types.

suffix.name: Mainframe.VanillaResource
suffix.label: Mainframe resources not tagged as ads.

suffix.name: Mime
suffix.label: Size of ad resources identified by the response header mime type.

suffix.name: Subframe.VanillaResource
suffix.label: Subframe resources not tagged as ads.

suffix.name: Clients.Ads.All
suffix.label: Includes all discovered ads.
suffix.obsolete: Deprecated January 2019 as we're transitioning to only using SubresourceFilter data.

suffix.name: Clients.Ads.Google
suffix.label: Includes only Google ads.
suffix.obsolete: Deprecated January 2019 as we're transitioning to only using SubresourceFilter data.

suffix.name: Clients.Ads.SubresourceFilter
suffix.label: Includes only ads discovered by the SubresourceFilter.
suffix.obsolete: Deprecated January 2019, as these metrics only use subresource filter data and metrics are being split out by visibility.

suffix.label: Usage before and after user activation.

suffix.name: AdFrames.Aggregate
suffix.label: Includes resources loaded across all ad frames on a page.

suffix.name: AdFrames.PerFrame
suffix.label: Includes resources loaded for a single ad frame.

suffix.name: FullPage
suffix.label: Includes all resources loaded by the page.

suffix.name: NonAdFrames.Aggregate.Total
suffix.label: The size (in KB) of all of the page's resources except for those loaded in ad frames.

suffix.name: NonAdFrames.Aggregate.Total2
suffix.label: The size (in KB) of all of the page's resources except for those loaded in ad frames.

suffix.name: AdFrames.PerFrame.PeakWindowedPercent
suffix.label: The peak load over all 30 second windows while the page is unactivated of an ad creative. This is measured as percentage of a cpu core used and capped at 100% for multicore systems.

suffix.name: AdFrames.PerFrame.PeakWindowStartTime
suffix.label: The start time of the 30 second window associated with the PeakWindowedPercent of the frame, relative to navigation start of the page. Measured in milliseconds. Frames with no CPU usage do not record a start time. This is recorded only when the page keeps foregrounded.

suffix.name: AdFrames.PerFrame.PercentUsage
suffix.label: The average load of an ad creative across the life of the page. This is measured as percentage of a cpu core used and capped at 100% for multicore systems.

suffix.name: AdFrames.PerFrame.PercentUsage2
suffix.label: The average load of an ad creative across the life of the page, up until the creative is removed from the page. This is measured as percentage of a cpu core used and capped at 100% for multicore systems.

suffix.name: AdFrames.PerFrame.TotalUsage
suffix.label: The total load of an ad creative across the life of the page. This is measured in milliseconds and capped at 10 seconds.

suffix.name: Activated
suffix.label: Includes only creatives that have had a user gesture.

suffix.name: Unactivated
suffix.label: Includes only creatives without a user gesture.

suffix.name: AdFrames.Aggregate.TotalUsage
suffix.label: The total CPU usage across all ad frames on a page in its lifetime. This is measured in milliseconds.

suffix.name: FullPage.PeakWindowedPercent
suffix.label: The peak load over all 30 second windows during the lifetime of the page. This is measured as percentage of a cpu core used and capped at 100% for multicore systems.

suffix.name: FullPage.PeakWindowStartTime
suffix.label: The start time of the 30 second window associated with the PeakWindowedPercent of the page, relative to navigation start of the page. Measured in milliseconds. Pages with no CPU usage do not record a start time. This is recorded only when the page keeps foregrounded.

suffix.name: FullPage.PercentUsage
suffix.label: The average load of the page across its lifetime. This is measured as percentage of a cpu core used and capped at 100% for multicore systems.

suffix.name: FullPage.TotalUsage
suffix.label: The total load of the page across its lifetime. This is measured in milliseconds and capped at 10 minutes.
Usage before and after the page is interactive.

suffix.name: PostInteractive
suffix.label: Usage after the page is interactive.
suffix.obsolete: Removed 01/2020, did not provide useful information on ad CPU performance.

suffix.name: PreInteractive
suffix.label: Usage before the page is interactive.
suffix.obsolete: Removed 01/2020, did not provide useful information on ad CPU performance.

suffix.name: MainFrame
suffix.label: Only includes resources loaded directly in the main frame.

suffix.name: MainFrame.Ads
suffix.label: Only includes resources loaded directly in the main frame that were identified to be ad resources.

suffix.name: Network
suffix.label: The size (in KB) of all of resources that loaded over the network.

suffix.name: Total
suffix.label: The size (in KB) of the resources loaded.

suffix.name: Total2
suffix.label: The size (in KB) of the resources loaded.

suffix.name: PercentAdFrames
suffix.label: The percentage of bytes loaded from within ad frames.

suffix.name: PercentAds
suffix.label: The percentage of bytes loaded from within ad frames.

suffix.name: PercentAds2
suffix.label: The percentage of bytes loaded from within ad frames.

suffix.name: PercentNetwork
suffix.label: The percentage of bytes that were loaded over the network.

suffix.name: PercentNetwork2
suffix.label: The percentage of bytes that were loaded over the network.

suffix.name: PercentSameOrigin
suffix.label: The percentage of bytes loaded from the same origin as the root frame of the page or ad.

suffix.name: PercentSameOrigin2
suffix.label: The percentage of bytes loaded from the same origin as the root frame of the page or ad.

suffix.name: SameOrigin
suffix.label: The number of bytes loaded from the same origin as the root frame of the page or ad.

suffix.name: SameOrigin2
suffix.label: The number of bytes (including those from both the HTTP cache and memory cache) loaded from the same origin as the root frame of the page or ad.

suffix.name: Clients.Ads.NonVisible
suffix.label: Only includes ad frames that have a display: none style, or have an area less than or equal to 16 pixels.

suffix.name: Clients.Ads.Visible
suffix.label: Only includes ad frames that have a display != none style and have an area greater than 16 pixels.

suffix.name: OnStartup

suffix.name: Periodic

suffix.name: AlternateProtocol_spdy
suffix.label: with alternate protocol available but http is used

suffix.name: Clicked

suffix.name: Visible

suffix.name: FromIntent
suffix.label: User navigation through the bottom navigation bar

suffix.name: NavBar
suffix.label: User navigation in response to an Intent

suffix.name: FirstBoot

suffix.name: FirstBootAfterUpdate

suffix.name: Downloading
suffix.label: Duration between download start and download end

suffix.name: Installing

histogram_suffixes.name: CertificateTypeBRValidity
histogram_suffixes.obsolete: Removed as of 8/2013. This histogram only considered the leaf certificate expiry date as a proxy for whether a certificate was in-scope for the BRs, but did not consider the issuance date. As some CAs have issued long-lived certs prior to the BRs, this disproportionately reported those certs as being subject to the BRs, but non-compliant, when in reality they're not subject.

suffix.name: BR
suffix.label: The *leaf* certificate of the chain expires after 2013-12-31, meaning that it should be in scope for the Baseline Requirement's key size requirements

suffix.name: NonBR
suffix.label: The *leaf* certificate of the chain expires on or before 2013-12-31

suffix.name: BR
suffix.label: The *leaf* certificate of the chain expires after 2013-12-31 and was issued on or after 2012-07-01, as judged by the notBefore, meaning that it should be in scope for the Baseline Requirement's key size requirements

suffix.name: NonBR
suffix.label: The *leaf* certificate of the chain expires on or before 2013-12-31 or was issued before 2012-07-01

histogram_suffixes.name: CertIo

histogram_suffixes.name: ClockResolution
histogram_suffixes.obsolete: These were temporary metrics intended to understand whether high/low resolution clocks adversely impact page load metrics. After collecting a few months of data it was determined that clock resolution doesn't adversely impact our metrics, and that these histograms were no longer needed.

suffix.name: HighResolutionClock
suffix.label: base::TimeTicks::IsHighResolution() is true for this measurement

suffix.name: LowResolutionClock

suffix.name: LessThan10

suffix.name: MoreThan30
suffix.label: Percentage when number of picture layers with text is greater than 30

suffix.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.LayoutUpdate
suffix.label: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on layout update.

suffix.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.Paint
suffix.label: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on paint.

suffix.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.Prepaint
suffix.label: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on prepaint.

suffix.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.ScrollingCoordinator
suffix.label: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on scrolling coordinator.

suffix.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.StyleUpdate
suffix.label: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on style update.

suffix.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.UpdateLayers
suffix.label: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on updating layers.

suffix.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame
suffix.label: The time from when the compositor frame is submitted to the display compositor to when it is presented.

suffix.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.ReceivedCompositorFrameToStartDraw
suffix.label: The time from when the compositor frame is received to when it starts to draw.

suffix.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.StartDrawToSwapEnd
suffix.label: The time from when the compositor frame is started to draw to when it ends swap. (obsolete)

suffix.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.StartDrawToSwapStart
suffix.label: The time from when the compositor frame is started to draw to when it starts swap.

suffix.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.SubmitToReceiveCompositorFrame
suffix.label: The time from when the compositor frame is submitted to when it is received.

suffix.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.SwapEndToPresentationCompositorFrame
suffix.label: The time from when the compositor frame ends swap to when it is presented.

suffix.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.SwapStartToSwapEnd
suffix.label: The time from when the compositor frame starts to swap to when it ends swap.

suffix.name: TotalLatency
suffix.label: The total time starting from BeginImplFrame to when CompositorFramePresentation is done with updates from the MainThread (i.e. the time it takes for BeginImplFrame, BeginMainFrame, Commit, Activate, SubmitCompositorFrame and PresentCompositorFrame).

suffix.name: BeginImplFrameToFinishImpl
suffix.label: The time from when the Impl frame is started to when Impl fram is ready.

suffix.name: ImplFrameDoneToSubmitCompositorFrame
suffix.label: The time from when the Impl frame is ready to the the time compositor frame is submitted.

suffix.name: SendBeginMainFrameToBeginMainAbort
suffix.label: The time from when the begin main frame is sent to when begin main is aborted.

suffix.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame
suffix.label: The time from when the a compositor frame is submitted to the display compositor to when it is presented.

suffix.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.ReceivedCompositorFrameToStartDraw
suffix.label: The time from when a compositor frame is received to when it starts to draw.

suffix.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.StartDrawToSwapStart
suffix.label: The time from when a compositor frame is started to draw to when it starts swap.

suffix.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.SubmitToReceiveCompositorFrame
suffix.label: The time from when the a compositor frame is submitted to when it is received.

suffix.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.SwapEndToPresentationCompositorFrame
suffix.label: The time from when the a compositor frame ends swap to when it is presented.

suffix.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.SwapStartToSwapEnd
suffix.label: The time from when a compositor frame starts to swap to when it ends swap.

suffix.name: TotalLatency
suffix.label: The total time starting from BeginImplFrame to when CompositorFramePresentation is done.

suffix.name: Impl
suffix.label: Impl side invalidation initiated pending tree

histogram_suffixes.name: CompositorTimingHistoryProcessObsolete

suffix.name: Underestimate
suffix.label: Tracks when the compositor's estimates were too low and by how much.

suffix.name: BlockedAfterSniffingWithRangeSupport

suffix.label: Here the CORB protection decision was kBlock.

suffix.name: CrOSVM
suffix.label: A client created by ChromeOS VM.

suffix.name: Legacy
suffix.label: A client using an old CRAS lib.

suffix.name: ServerStream
suffix.label: A client created by CRAS server.

suffix.name: NotificationAdded
suffix.label: A notification was created (regardless if it was shown or not)

suffix.name: Shown
suffix.label: A message was displayed to the user.

suffix.name: Triggered
suffix.label: A user did an unsupported thing. May or may not have resulted in a message to the user.

suffix.name: Active
suffix.label: For an active tab which is shown foreground in a browser window.

suffix.name: Detached
suffix.label: For a tab that is being dragged by user to outside of the browser window.

suffix.name: Inactive
suffix.label: For tabs in background and not shown to user.

suffix.name: Initial
suffix.label: For a tab that is just being created.

suffix.name: TWA
suffix.label: CustomTab opened by Trusted Web Activity. (obsolete)

suffix.name: DataReductionProxy
suffix.label: Only page loads through the data reduction proxy are considered.

suffix.name: HTTPS_DataReductionProxy
suffix.label: Only page loads through the data reduction proxy through an HTTP tunnel for HTTPS origins are considered.
Recorded approximately 15 seconds after navigation start
Recorded approximately 30 seconds after navigation start
Recorded approximately 60 seconds after navigation start

suffix.name: HTTPS_DataReductionProxy_AutoLoFiOn
suffix.label: Only page loads through the data reduction proxy through an HTTP tunnel for HTTPS origins with auto LoFi enabled are considered.

suffix.name: disabled_prefetch
suffix.label: DNS pre-resolving is disabled in these clients

suffix.name: disabled_prefetch_4_connections
suffix.label: DNS pre-resolving is disabled in these clients, and a maximum of 4 connections per host was allowed

suffix.name: enabled_prefetch_4_connections
suffix.label: a maximum of 4 connections per host was allowed in these clients

suffix.name: parallel_4_prefetch
suffix.label: DNS pre-resolving was only doing 4 concurrent speculative resolutions in this test

suffix.name: parallel_4_prefetch
suffix.label: with only 4 concurrent speculative resolutions done in parallel

suffix.name: parallel_6
suffix.label: with only 6 concurrent resolutions done in parallel

suffix.name: parallel_7
suffix.label: with only 7 concurrent resolutions done in parallel

suffix.name: parallel_8
suffix.label: with only 8 concurrent resolutions done in parallel

suffix.name: parallel_9
suffix.label: with only 9 concurrent resolutions done in parallel

suffix.name: parallel_10
suffix.label: with only 10 concurrent resolutions done in parallel

suffix.name: parallel_14
suffix.label: with only 14 concurrent resolutions done in parallel

suffix.name: parallel_20
suffix.label: with only 20 concurrent resolutions done in parallel

suffix.name: parallel_default
suffix.label: with the default number of concurrent resolutions done in parallel

suffix.name: AsyncPrivateSupportsDoh
suffix.label: using Chromium's resolver, with a DNS server that supports DNS-over-HTTPS

suffix.name: SystemPrivate
suffix.label: using the system resolver using private DNS

suffix.name: SystemSupportsDoh
suffix.label: using the system resolver, with a DNS server that supports DNS-over-HTTPS. Note that on Android the DNS servers are not passed to native code when private DNS is used so we cannot detect when private DNS is used with servers that support DNS-over-HTTPS, so this bucket will fall into the SystemPrivate bucket.

suffix.name: Other
suffix.label: No possible upgrade to a DoH provider.

suffix.name: AggressivePurgeTriggered
suffix.label: Aggressive purge was triggered on memory pressure.

suffix.name: InactiveOnLowEndDevice
suffix.label: Inactive areas found in low end device.

suffix.name: ModeratePurgeTriggered
suffix.label: Moderate purge was triggered on memory pressure.

suffix.name: HardRecovery
suffix.label: File is deleted in hard recovery.

suffix.name: Orphaned
suffix.label: No client associated with the file.

suffix.name: Unknown
suffix.label: No database entry associated with the file.

suffix.name: Proceed
suffix.label: The user clicked through and recovered the download.

suffix.name: Shown
suffix.label: The user was shown the prompt.

suffix.name: WSOrWSSScheme
suffix.label: downloads with ws or wss scheme

suffix.name: Removed
suffix.label: Captures only draw quads that have removed by draw occlusion.
suffix.obsolete: Deprecated as of 03/2018. We'd learned enough from this metric and it made no sense to keep it around.

suffix.name: Resized
suffix.label: Captures only draw quads that have changed size by draw occlusion.
suffix.obsolete: Deprecated as of 03/2018. We'd learned enough from this metric and it made no sense to keep it around.

suffix.name: Skipped
suffix.label: Captures only draw quads that are too small to apply draw occlusion.
suffix.obsolete: Deprecated as of 03/2018. We'd learned enough from this metric and it made no sense to keep it around.

suffix.name: Smaller
suffix.label: Captures only draw quads that are smaller than the layer occlusion minimum size (160x160).
suffix.obsolete: Deprecated as of 03/2018. We'd learned enough from this metric and it made no sense to keep it around.

suffix.name: With.Complex.Transform
suffix.label: Captures only draw quads that need to go through no scale and no translation transforms.
suffix.obsolete: Deprecated as of 03/2018. We'd learned enough from this metric and it made no sense to keep it around.

suffix.name: ForwardSecure
suffix.label: Packets sent at Forward Secure encryption only.

suffix.name: Handshake
suffix.label: Packets sent at Handshake encryption only.

suffix.name: Initial
suffix.label: Packets sent at Initial encryption only.

suffix.name: FadeInOverview
suffix.label: Triggered by entering overview by fading in from home

suffix.name: FadeOutOverview
suffix.label: Triggered by exiting overview by fading into home

affected-histogram.name: Apps.StateTransition.AnimationSmoothness
affected-histogram.obsolete: Removed in 2019-11. Equivalent values are tracked by Apps.HomeLauncherTransition.AnimationSmoothness.* histograms.

histogram_suffixes.name: Enterprise.ResourceCacheTiming
histogram_suffixes.obsolete: Removed 01/2020 since the histogram indicators were stable.

suffix.name: Clear
suffix.label: The time to perform the 'clear' operation on a ResourceCache, which deletes all subkeys of given key.

suffix.name: Delete
suffix.label: The time to perform the 'delete' operation on a ResourceCache, which deletes given subkey.

suffix.name: FilterSubkeys
suffix.label: The time to perform the 'filterSubkeys' operation on a ResourceCache, which deletes filtered subkeys of given key.

suffix.name: Init
suffix.label: The time to perform the initialization of a ResourceCache.

suffix.name: Load
suffix.label: The time to perform the 'load' operation on a ResourceCache, which loads the content of given key.

suffix.name: LoadAllSubkeys
suffix.label: The time to perform the 'loadAllSubkeys' operation on a ResourceCache, which loads the content of all subkeys of given key.

suffix.name: PurgeOtherKeys
suffix.label: The time to perform the 'purgeOtherKeys' operation on a ResourceCache, which deletes all keys except given.

suffix.name: PurgeOtherSubkeys
suffix.label: The time to perform the 'purgeOtherSubkeys' operation on a ResourceCache, which deletes all subkeys except given.

suffix.name: Store
suffix.label: The time to perform the 'store' operation on a ResourceCache, which stores data under given key.

suffix.name: InitialStore
suffix.label: the initial device policy stored during enrollment

suffix.name: Update
suffix.label: the updated device policy that overwrites the previous one

suffix.name: UserDuringLogin
suffix.label: User policy fetch response during login.

suffix.name: Activation
suffix.label: The duration of the activation stage.

suffix.name: BeginImplFrameToSendBeginMainFrame
suffix.label: The time from when the compositor impl frame is started to when BeginMainFrame is sent.

suffix.name: BrowserToRendererCompositor
suffix.label: The time from when the event is generated until the first compositor stage after the event arrives in the renderer.

suffix.name: Commit
suffix.label: The duration of the commit stage.

suffix.name: EndActivateToSubmitCompositorFrame
suffix.label: The time from when an activation is complete to the next SubmitCompositorFrame.

suffix.name: EndCommitToActivation
suffix.label: The time from when a commit is complete to the beginning of the next activation.

suffix.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit
suffix.label: The time from when the BeginMainFrame is sent to the beginning of the commit.

suffix.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.Animate
suffix.label: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on animations.

suffix.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.BeginMainSentToStarted
suffix.label: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent before starting main thread work.

suffix.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.Composite
suffix.label: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on compositing.

suffix.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.CompositeCommit
suffix.label: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on composite commit.

suffix.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.HandleInputEvents
suffix.label: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on handling imput events.

suffix.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.LayoutUpdate
suffix.label: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on layout update.

suffix.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.Paint
suffix.label: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on paint.

suffix.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.Prepaint
suffix.label: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on prepaint.

suffix.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.ScrollingCoordinator
suffix.label: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on scrolling coordinator.

suffix.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.StyleUpdate
suffix.label: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on style update.

suffix.name: SendBeginMainFrameToCommit.UpdateLayers
suffix.label: The time portion of SendBeginMainFrameToCommit spent on updating layers.

suffix.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame
suffix.label: The time from when the compositor frame is submitted to the display compositor to when it is presented.

suffix.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.ReceivedCompositorFrameToStartDraw
suffix.label: The time from when the compositor frame is received to when it starts to draw.

suffix.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.StartDrawToSwapStart
suffix.label: The time from when the compositor frame is started to draw to when it starts swap.

suffix.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.SubmitToReceiveCompositorFrame
suffix.label: The time from when the compositor frame is submitted to when it is received.

suffix.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.SwapEndToPresentationCompositorFrame
suffix.label: The time from when the compositor frame ends swap to when it is presented.

suffix.name: SubmitCompositorFrameToPresentationCompositorFrame.SwapStartToSwapEnd
suffix.label: The time from when the compositor frame starts to swap to when it ends swap.

suffix.name: TotalLatency
suffix.label: Total latency from when the event is generated until the frame is presented on screen.

suffix.name: TotalLatencyToSwapEnd
suffix.label: Total latency from when the event is generated until the gpu-swap is completed.

suffix.name: Scrollbar
suffix.label: Event latency for scrollbar scroll events.

suffix.name: Touchscreen
suffix.label: Event latency for touchscreen scroll events.

suffix.name: Wheel
suffix.label: Event latency for mousewheel scroll events.

histogram_suffixes.name: ExpectedQueueingDurationThreshold
histogram_suffixes.obsolete: Removed as of 08/2017. Replaced with ExpectedQueueingDurationThreshold.

suffix.name: InBeforeUnload
suffix.label: Created during an event handler for the 'beforeunload' event.
suffix.obsolete: Deprecated and removed from code as of 05/2015.

suffix.name: InUnload
suffix.label: Created during an event handler for the 'unload' event.
suffix.obsolete: Deprecated and removed from code as of 05/2015.

suffix.name: Normal
suffix.label: Created during any time other than the 'unload' or 'beforeunload' handlers.
suffix.obsolete: Deprecated and removed from code as of 05/2015.

suffix.name: Total
suffix.label: The total number of ports created.

suffix.name: Extension
suffix.label: A port opened to an extension context.

suffix.name: NativeApp
suffix.label: A port opened to a native application.

suffix.name: Tab
suffix.label: A port opened to a tab context.

suffix.name: WebSocket
suffix.label: sec-websocket-*, origin, cache-control, pragma, host, upgrade, connection

suffix.name: WebSocketExceptProtocol
suffix.label: sec-websocket-* (except for sec-websocket-protocol), origin, cache-control, pragma, host, upgrade, connection

suffix.name: OffStore
suffix.label: Extension hosted off the Chrome Web Store

suffix.name: WebStore
suffix.label: Extension from the Chrome Web Store

suffix.name: Uber
suffix.label: The Uber chrome://extensions page.
suffix.obsolete: Deprecated and removed from code as of 04/2018.

suffix.name: NonWebstore
suffix.label: sideloaded extensions that don't update from the webstore

suffix.name: Webstore
suffix.label: sideloaded extensions that update from the webstore

suffix.name: Drive
suffix.label: The message was sent with the Drive sender ID

suffix.name: Policy
suffix.label: The message was sent with the Policy sender ID

suffix.name: Sync
suffix.label: The message was sent with the Sync sender ID

suffix.name: NotSynthetic
suffix.label: Continuations that require making remote requests to fetch more articles.

suffix.name: Synthetic
suffix.label: Continuations generated by the Feed library.

suffix.name: FeedQuery
suffix.label: Requests to fetch new feed content

suffix.name: UploadActions
suffix.label: Requests to upload user action data

suffix.label: Do not make request and show current content.

suffix.label: Do not make request until timeout.

suffix.name: NoRequestWithWait
suffix.label: Do not make request and wait for existing request.

suffix.name: BasedOnAutocomplete
suffix.label: Quality metrics based on autocomplete attributes.

suffix.name: DriveComputers

suffix.name: MyDrive
suffix.label: Shared path in My Drive volume.

suffix.name: Other
suffix.label: Shared path in any other volume.

suffix.name: Removable
suffix.label: Shared path in removable USB volume.

suffix.name: TeamDrive
suffix.label: Shared path in Team Drive volume.

suffix.name: BackgroundLaunch
suffix.label: Time from onLaunched event is called to the window is created.

suffix.name: BackgroundRestart
suffix.label: Time to create a window on restart.

suffix.name: BackgroundScript
suffix.label: Time to initialize backend script to wait for events.

suffix.name: Construct
suffix.label: Time spent constructing the main Javascript object.

suffix.name: FileSystem
suffix.label: Time to get access to the local file system.

suffix.name: ImportElements
suffix.label: Time to initialize the polymer elements.

suffix.name: InitBackgroundPage
suffix.label: Time to initialize the background page.

suffix.name: InitDocuments
suffix.label: Time to initialize the background page and the elements.

suffix.name: InitUI
suffix.label: Time to initialize all UI after all elements are initialized.

suffix.name: Parse
suffix.label: Time to parse Javascript and CSS (Deprecated).

suffix.name: Roots
suffix.label: Time to enumerate file system roots.

suffix.name: Script
suffix.label: Time to parse Javascript and CSS.

suffix.name: Total
suffix.label: Total load time from the moment the Javascript started parsing till the moment the empty file list is displayed.

suffix.name: Aborted
suffix.label: The request got aborted before it went through every text in the document.

suffix.name: Finished
suffix.label: The request finished going through every text in the document.

suffix.name: first_packet_intact
suffix.label: with GET/POST headers often using only 1 packet

suffix.name: first_packet_split
suffix.label: with all GET/POST requests using at least 2 packets

suffix.name: Continuation
suffix.label: and performed an user action indicating that they were continuing a task from the last time they used the app

suffix.name: Expiration
suffix.label: and did not perform any user actions that indicate either a new task or a continued task before some timeout expired

suffix.name: NewTask
suffix.label: and performed an user action indicating that they were beginning a new task from the last time they used the app

suffix.name: Backgrounded
suffix.label: The user sent Chrome to the background

suffix.name: Continuation
suffix.label: The user continued the task from the last time they used the app

suffix.name: NtpCreated
suffix.label: The user action open a NTP.

suffix.name: Omnibox
suffix.label: The user interacted with the omnibox.

suffix.label: In product help for long press a link to open in a tab group.

suffix.name: IPH_TabGroupsTapToSeeAnotherTab
suffix.label: In product help for tab strip.

suffix.name: IPH_TabGroupsYourTabsTogether
suffix.label: In product help for tab switcher cards with multiple tab thumbnails.

suffix.name: IPH_TranslateMenuButton
suffix.label: In product help translate menu button.

suffix.name: IPH_WebUITabStrip
suffix.label: In product help for opening the WebUI tab strip.

suffix.name: IPv6_probe_done
suffix.label: with IPv6 probed for and possibly disabled

suffix.name: IPv6_probe_skipped
suffix.label: with IPv6 not probed, and default OS settings used

histogram_suffixes.name: JankyIntervalsPerThirtySeconds
histogram_suffixes.obsolete: This metric is now only recorded after the main message loop starts and no longer distinguishes the first 30 seconds as "startup". Startup.BrowserMessageLoopStartTime can be used for the startup phase (before the loop is running) and Startup.FirstWebContents.NonEmptyPaint2 can be used for the user-visible startup performance.

suffix.name: NonStartup
suffix.label: Does not include jank in the first 30 seconds [startup].

suffix.name: NoSuspend
suffix.label: Only counts jank without power suspend/resume.

suffix.name: Startup
suffix.label: Only counts jank in the first 30 seconds [startup].

suffix.name: MainFrame
suffix.label: dialog spawned by the main frame

suffix.name: Leave
suffix.label: user opted to leave the page

suffix.name: Stay
suffix.label: user opted to stay on the page

suffix.name: HighDPI
suffix.label: In web pages where device scale factor is greater than or equal to 1.5.

suffix.name: LowDPI
suffix.label: In web pages where device scale factor is less than 1.5.

suffix.name: Change
suffix.label: The user is asked to change their password

suffix.name: Checkup
suffix.label: The user is asked to visit the Password Checkup

suffix.name: CheckupAndChange
suffix.label: The user is asked to visit the Password Checkup and change their password

suffix.name: LegacyTLS_NotTriggered
suffix.label: Page didn't trigger legacy TLS warning

suffix.name: BudgetManager
suffix.label: Database for storing budget information for origins.

suffix.name: FeedStreamDatabase
suffix.label: Database for Feed v2 stream and content storage.

suffix.name: UsageReportsBufferBackend
suffix.label: The result of the first attempt to open the usage reports buffer backend database.

suffix.name: OtherError
suffix.label: This histogram shows the limit when open failed for reasons other than exceeding the limit.

suffix.name: Success
suffix.label: This histogram shows the limit when open succeeded.

suffix.name: TooManyOpened
suffix.label: This histogram shows the limit when open failed because the limit had been reached.

suffix.name: Browser
suffix.label: Cache shared databases whose access pattern is dictated by browser code.

suffix.name: Unified
suffix.label: Cache shared by both web and browser.

suffix.name: Web
suffix.label: whose access pattern is directly influenced by Web APIs, like Indexed DB, etc.

suffix.name: ByLiveTabCount.0Tabs
suffix.label: Recorded with 0 live tabs open in the browser.

suffix.name: ByLiveTabCount.1Tab
suffix.label: Recorded with 1 live tab open in the browser.

suffix.name: ByLiveTabCount.2Tabs
suffix.label: Recorded with 2 live tabs open in the browser.

suffix.name: ByLiveTabCount.3To4Tabs
suffix.label: Recorded with between 3 and 4 live tabs open in the browser.

suffix.name: ByLiveTabCount.5To7Tabs
suffix.label: Recorded with between 5 and 7 live tabs open in the browser.

suffix.name: ByLiveTabCount.8To19Tabs
suffix.label: Recorded with between 8 and 19 live tabs open in the browser.

suffix.name: ByLiveTabCount.8To19Tabs
suffix.label: Recorded with between 20 and 39 live tabs open in the browser.

suffix.name: ByLiveTabCount.40OrMoreTabs
suffix.label: Recorded with 40 or more live tabs open in the browser.

affected-histogram.name: PageLoad.InteractiveTiming.FirstInputDelay3
affected-histogram.obsolete: Deprecated 12/2019. Recording suffixed version of PageLoad.InteractiveTiming.FirstInputDelay4 instead.

suffix.name: Navigation
suffix.label: Prediction trigged at start of navigation based on what was learned locally from previous navigations to the same origin.

suffix.name: Localhost.DbRequests
suffix.label: Requests made to localhost on a database server port.

suffix.name: Localhost.DevRequests
suffix.label: Requests made to localhost on a development server port.

suffix.name: Localhost.OtherRequests
suffix.label: Requests made to localhost on any port not otherwise monitored by the other local network request metrics.

suffix.name: Localhost.PrinterRequests
suffix.label: Requests made to localhost on a printer server port.

suffix.name: Localhost.WebRequests
suffix.label: Requests made to localhost on a web server port.

suffix.name: DifferentSubnetRequests
suffix.label: Requests made to local resources on a different subnet.

suffix.name: SameSubnetRequests
suffix.label: Requests made to local resources on the same reserved IP space as the page.

suffix.name: RouterRequests
suffix.label: Requests made to resources likely to be routers.

suffix.name: AudioUsageInBackground
suffix.label: A tab played some audio while it was in background.

suffix.name: FaviconUpdateInBackground
suffix.label: A tab changed its favicon while it was in background.

suffix.name: NotificationsUsageInBackground
suffix.label: A tab triggered a non-persistent notification while it was in background.

suffix.name: TitleUpdateInBackground
suffix.label: A tab changed its title while it was in background.

suffix.name: InvalidVersion
suffix.label: After an invalid version was read.

suffix.name: ReadVersionError
suffix.label: After reading the schema version failed.

suffix.name: LocationAttached
suffix.label: This histogram is for when the location was successfully attached to the request.

suffix.name: LocationNotAttached
suffix.label: This histogram is for when the location was not attached to the request. A location may not be attached if it is too old or if the permissions do not allow it.

suffix.name: BatterySaving
suffix.label: This histogram is for location mode Battery Saving.

suffix.name: GpsOnly
suffix.label: This histogram is for location mode GPS Only.

suffix.name: HighAccuracy
suffix.label: This histogram is for location mode High Accuracy.

histogram_suffixes.name: LockExistingCachedImage
histogram_suffixes.obsolete: Removes as of 6/2020. We now get enough cache hit information from Renderer4.SoftwareImageDecodeState* and don't need to divide it into tile bins.

suffix.name: Background
suffix.label: The app window is in background (behind the lock screen)

suffix.name: Foreground
suffix.label: The app window is in foreground (on top of the lock screen)

suffix.name: TotalActive
suffix.label: The total amount of time an app window was active on lock screen

histogram_suffixes.name: LowMemoryMargin
histogram_suffixes.obsolete: Removed 12/2017. The low memory margin is a per-platform constant.

suffix.name: default
suffix.label: Low memory margin set to the system default

suffix.name: HighLatency
suffix.label: Measures the standard Mac audio driver (i.e. not low-latency).

suffix.name: smart_dim_model_20190521_v3
suffix.label: Predict whether to cancel screen dims, temporarily added for downloadable smart dim models, will remove after M84.

suffix.name: SmartDimModel
suffix.label: Predict whether to cancel screen dims.

suffix.name: TestModel
suffix.label: A simple model for testing purpose.

suffix.name: TopCatModel
suffix.label: Predict the next app to be launched.

affected-histogram.name: MachineLearningService.LoadModelResult.ElapsedTimeMicrosec

affected-histogram.name: MachineLearningService.PrivateMemoryDeltaKb

suffix.name: OtherMimeType
suffix.label: A MIME type that doesn't fall into either of the other two categories.

suffix.name: ProtectedMimeType
suffix.label: A MIME type we expect CORB to protect (i.e. JSON, HTML or XML).

suffix.name: PublicMimeType
suffix.label: A MIME type we consider public (i.e. javascript or an image type).

suffix.name: ChromeNotSystem
suffix.label: Chrome is not a system installed browser.

suffix.name: ChromeSystem
suffix.label: Chrome is a system installed browser.

suffix.name: OtherDefault
suffix.label: Another browser is the default browser.

suffix.name: ConfirmInfobarTypeBlockPopups
suffix.label: Recorded for Block Popups Confirm Messages.

suffix.name: ConfirmInfobarTypeRestore
suffix.label: Recorded for Restore Tabs Confirm Messages.

suffix.name: WithByteSet
suffix.label: The version of the Module Integrity Verifier that uses a hash set to track relocations.
suffix.obsolete: Deprecated and removed from code as of 05/2015.

suffix.name: WithoutByteSet
suffix.label: The version of the Module Integrity Verifier that doesn't use a hash set to track relocations.

suffix.name: Hidden
suffix.label: The native window is not visible because it is in a minimized window

suffix.name: Occluded
suffix.label: The native window is fully covered by other windows

suffix.name: Visible
suffix.label: The native window is visible or partially visible.

suffix.name: ExistingRenderer_BeforeUnloadDiscounted
suffix.label: Navigation reused an existing renderer process. Time spent in beforeunload subtracted.

suffix.name: NewRenderer_BeforeUnloadDiscounted
suffix.label: Navigation spawned a new renderer process. Time spent in beforeunload subtracted.

suffix.name: SessionRestored
suffix.label: Navigation caused by restoring a tab from a previous session (whether from a crash or a continued session) either spawning or reusing a renderer. Time spent in beforeunload subtracted.
suffix.obsolete: Replaced by the likely named entry. It had a misleading name as it also has the before-unload time discounted.

suffix.name: SessionRestored_BeforeUnloadDiscounted
suffix.label: Navigation caused by restoring a tab from a previous session (whether from a crash or a continued session) either spawning or reusing a renderer. Time spent in beforeunload subtracted.

suffix.name: SerializedDisplayStateSize
suffix.label: Estimated size of DisplayState in serialized session item used to persist single NavigationItem DisplayState in session history.

suffix.name: SerializedPostDataSize
suffix.label: Estimated size of PostData in serialized session item used to persist single NavigationItem PostData in session history.

suffix.name: SerializedReferrerURLSize
suffix.label: Estimated size of referrer's URL in serialized session item used to persist single NavigationItem referrer's URL in session history.

suffix.name: SerializedRequestHeadersSize
suffix.label: Estimated size of HTTP request headers in serialized session item used to persist single NavigationItem headers in session history.

suffix.name: SerializedSize
suffix.label: Estimated size of serialized session item used to persist single NavigationItem in session history.

suffix.name: SerializedTitleSize
suffix.label: Estimated size of title in serialized session item used to persist single NavigationItem title in session history.

suffix.name: SerializedURLSize
suffix.label: Estimated size of URL in serialized session item used to persist single NavigationItem URL in session history.

suffix.name: SerializedVirtualURLSize
suffix.label: Estimated size of virtual URL in serialized session item used to persist single NavigationItem virtual URL in session history.

suffix.name: OnDSE
suffix.label: Recorded only for pages whose URL matches the URL of the search results page of the user's default search engine.

suffix.name: OnNonDSE
suffix.label: Recorded only for pages whose URL does not match the URL of the search results page of the user's default search engine.

suffix.name: NavPreloadFirst

suffix.name: HTTP2

suffix.name: QUIC

suffix.name: Insecure

suffix.name: TooMany
suffix.label: for secure QUIC when there were too many rejects.

suffix.name: First21
suffix.label: Only the first group of 21 packets in a connection via

suffix.name: Some21s
suffix.label: After the first 21, this records data for some groups of 21 consecutive sequence nmubers, arriving via.

suffix.name: First6
suffix.label: Only the first group of 6 packets in a connection via

suffix.name: Some6s
suffix.label: After the first 6, this records patterns for some groups of 6 consecutive sequence numbers, arriving via.

suffix.name: Ack
suffix.label: Only packets that were received by Chrome as well being part of connections via

suffix.name: IsAnAck
suffix.label: Only packets that were probably solo ACK packets when received by Chrome as well being part of connections via

suffix.name: IsNotAck
suffix.label: Only packets that were probably NOT solo ACK packets when received by Chrome as well being part of connections via

suffix.name: Nack
suffix.label: Only packets that were missed by Chrome as well being part of connections via

suffix.name: CONNECTION_ETHERNET
suffix.label: ethernet are tallied, but this may include connections to a WiFi bridge.

suffix.name: CONNECTION_NONE

suffix.name: CONNECTION_WIFI_ANCIENT

suffix.name: NonPacedPacket
suffix.label: In this histogram results are only shown if at least two packets were ACKed in the Startup Test. Packets were sent as rapidly as possible.

suffix.name: PacedPacket
suffix.label: In this histogram results are only shown if at least two packets were ACKed in the Startup Test. Packets are sent at equal intervals. The interval is selected to match the bandwidth discovered during the StartPacket test.

suffix.name: StartPacket
suffix.label: Packets are sent as rapidly as possible, just after successfully sending an UMA upload. Each packet was numbered, as was its ACK sent back by Google. If no packets (of the 21) were ever ACKed, then the port is assumed to be blocked, and no data is recorded in this histogram.

suffix.name: Sent21
suffix.label: This histogram shows the number of echo responses received from the first

suffix.name: Sent21.AckReceivedForNthPacket
suffix.label: Each packet was numbered, as was its ACK sent back by Google. This histogram records, for each packet number, how often we received an ACK for that packet.

suffix.name: Sent21.GotAnAck
suffix.label: The histogram shows if we ever got an ACK for a packet in our series of 21.

suffix.name: Sent21.443
suffix.label: This histogram shows the difference between the time when we have received 1st byte from the server and the last time when we have received data from the server on port 443.

suffix.name: Sent21.6121
suffix.label: This histogram shows the difference between the time when we have received 1st byte from the server and the last time when we have received data from the server on port 6121.

suffix.name: 443.100B.PacketDelay
suffix.label: 100 bytes of data is sent on port 443.

suffix.name: 443.500B.PacketDelay
suffix.label: 500 bytes of data is sent on port 443.

suffix.name: 443.1200B.PacketDelay
suffix.label: 1200 bytes of data is sent on port 443.

suffix.name: 6121.100B.PacketDelay
suffix.label: 100 bytes of data is sent on port 6121.

suffix.name: 6121.500B.PacketDelay
suffix.label: 500 bytes of data is sent on port 6121.

suffix.name: 6121.1200B.PacketDelay
suffix.label: 1200 bytes of data is sent on port 6121.

suffix.name: Sent21.Success.RTT
suffix.label: The histogram shows the RTT for

suffix.name: Send6.SeriesAcked
suffix.label: Chrome sends 6 UDP packets in a row to test to see if there is a probabalistic dependency in packet loss for consecutive packets. We record a bit vector of packets received, where the least significant bit is a 1 if the first packet was received, etc. For example, if all packets other than packet 2 and 4 are responded to, then we'd have a sample (in binary) of 110101B, or 53.

suffix.name: Sent21.PacketsSent
suffix.label: This histogram records how many packets (out of 21 attempted) were sent to the server via UDP.

suffix.name: 443.100B
suffix.label: 100 bytes of data is sent on port 443.

suffix.name: 443.500B
suffix.label: 500 bytes of data is sent on port 443.

suffix.name: 443.1200B
suffix.label: 1200 bytes of data is sent on port 443.

suffix.name: 6121.100B
suffix.label: 100 bytes of data is sent on port 6121.

suffix.name: 6121.500B
suffix.label: 500 bytes of data is sent on port 6121.

suffix.name: 6121.1200B
suffix.label: 1200 bytes of data is sent on port 6121.

suffix.name: 443.100B
suffix.label: 100 bytes of data is sent on port 443.

suffix.name: 443.100B.NoProxy
suffix.label: 100 bytes of data is sent on port 443 with no proxy.

suffix.name: 443.500B
suffix.label: 500 bytes of data is sent on port 443.

suffix.name: 443.500B.NoProxy
suffix.label: 500 bytes of data is sent on port 443 with no proxy.

suffix.name: 443.1200B
suffix.label: 1200 bytes of data is sent on port 443.

suffix.name: 443.1200B.NoProxy
suffix.label: 1200 bytes of data is sent on port 443 with no proxy.

suffix.name: 6121.100B
suffix.label: 100 bytes of data is sent on port 6121.

suffix.name: 6121.100B.NoProxy
suffix.label: 100 bytes of data is sent on port 6121 with no proxy.

suffix.name: 6121.500B
suffix.label: 500 bytes of data is sent on port 6121.

suffix.name: 6121.500B.NoProxy
suffix.label: 500 bytes of data is sent on port 6121 with no proxy.

suffix.name: 6121.1200B
suffix.label: 1200 bytes of data is sent on port 6121.

suffix.name: 6121.1200B.NoProxy
suffix.label: 1200 bytes of data is sent on port 6121 with no proxy.

suffix.name: NATBind.Sent2
suffix.label: Two packets were sent spreading over a random period, to test if the NAT dropped the binding. Afterwords, an extra (short) packet was sent with renewed NAT binding to test whether the network that was used to deliver the first packet is still connected. Results are only shown in this histogram if at least ten packets were received in the StartPacket test.

suffix.name: NonPacedPacket
suffix.label: 21 Packets were sent as rapidly as possible. Results are only shown in this histogram if at least two packets were received in the StartPacket Test.

suffix.name: PacedPacket
suffix.label: 21 Packets were sent at equal intervals, which were selected to match the bandwidth discovered during the StartPacket test. Results are only shown in this histogram if at least two packets were received in the StartPacket Test.

suffix.name: StartPacket
suffix.label: 21 Packets were sent as rapidly as possible, just after the client successfully sent a UMA upload. Each packet was numbered when it was sent by Google.

suffix.name: Bind.Failure
suffix.label: Only when the second packet never arrived (we wait for 10 extra seconds) and the first and the extra (short) packets arrived did we record the duration in seconds between the sendings of the first two packets in this histogram.

suffix.name: Bind.Success
suffix.label: Only when all three packets including the extra (short) packet arrived did we record the duration in seconds between the sendings of the first two packets in this histogram.

suffix.name: Connectivity.Failure
suffix.label: Only when the extra (short) packet (with renewed NAT binding) never arrived (we wait for 10 extra seconds) did we record the duration in seconds between the sendings of the first two packets in this histogram.

suffix.name: Connectivity.Success
suffix.label: Only when the extra (short) packet arrived did we record the duration in seconds between the sendings of the first two packets in this histogram.

suffix.name: SendToLastRecvDelay
suffix.label: This histogram records the time duration (in milliseconds) between the client sending the request and the receiving of the second packet sent from the server, excluding the idle time between sendings of the first two packets. Results are only shown if the first two packets are both received.

suffix.name: First6.SeriesRecv
suffix.label: This histogram records a bit vector of the first 6 packets sent, where the least significant bit is a 1 if the first packet was received, etc. For example, if all packets other than packet 2 and 4 are received, then we'd have a sample (in binary) of 110101B, or 53.

suffix.name: Sent21
suffix.label: This histogram shows the number of packets received from the first

suffix.name: Sent21.GotAPacket
suffix.label: The histogram shows if we ever got at least one packet in our series of 21.

suffix.name: Sent21.PacketDelay
suffix.label: The histogram shows the average inter-arrival time between every two consecutive packets we receive in our series of 21 multiplied by 20 (so this is essentially the time duration between the first and the last received packets).

suffix.name: Sent21.PacketsRecv
suffix.label: The histogram shows how many packets we receive in our series of 21.

suffix.name: Sent21.RecvNthPacket
suffix.label: Each packet was numbered when it was sent by Google. This histogram records, for each packet number, how often we received that packet.

suffix.name: Sent21.SendToLastRecvDelay
suffix.label: This histogram records the time duration between the client sending the request and the receiving of the last packet sent from the server, excluding the total pacing time requested by the client. Results are only shown if at least two packets are received.

suffix.name: Sent21.Success.RTT
suffix.label: The histogram shows the RTT for the

suffix.name: NTP.ThirdParty
suffix.label: The NTP served by a third party (e.g. Bing)

suffix.name: BypassedDRP
suffix.label: Bytes of traffic that bypassed the Data Reduction Proxy

suffix.name: Direct
suffix.label: Bytes of traffic that were ineligible for the Data Reduction Proxy

suffix.name: Other
suffix.label: Bytes of traffic that don't fit into the other categories of Net.HttpContentLength

suffix.name: ViaDRP
suffix.label: Bytes of traffic that passed through the Data Reduction Proxy

suffix.name: Error
suffix.label: Connection to proxy resulted in an error

suffix.name: DiskCache
suffix.label: DiskCache backend is using disk cache.

suffix.name: MemoryCache
suffix.label: DiskCache backend is using memory cache.

suffix.name: WaitForDataReady
suffix.label: Tracks the last failure reason until WaitForDataReady or its callback is executed. This is recorded when data is ready in WaitForDataReady or when the callback is executed

suffix.name: Failure
suffix.label: Failed to fetch data from background service

suffix.name: Failure
suffix.label: Failed to fetch middle-slot Promo data

suffix.name: SuccessWithoutPromo
suffix.label: Successfully fetched middle-slot Promo data which did not contain a promo

suffix.name: SuccessWithPromo
suffix.label: Successfully fetched middle-slot Promo data which contained a promo

suffix.name: Failure
suffix.label: Failed to fetch search suggestion data

suffix.name: SuccessWithoutSuggestions
suffix.label: Successfully fetched search suggestion data which did not contain suggestions

suffix.name: SuccessWithSuggestions
suffix.label: Successfully fetched search suggestion data which contained suggestions

suffix.name: IconsColor
suffix.label: Icons using a fallback color (favicon resolution too low).

suffix.name: IconsGray
suffix.label: Icons using a gray color (no favicon available).

suffix.name: IconsReal
suffix.label: Icons using an actual icon published by the site.

suffix.name: Thumbnail
suffix.label: Not an icon but a thumbnail/screenshot of the page.

suffix.name: ThumbnailFailed
suffix.label: Default gray box in place of a thumbnail/screenshot.

suffix.name: client0
suffix.obsolete: Hasn't been used for a while, as of 2016-07.

suffix.name: popular
suffix.label: Deprecated with M-62! Non-personalized, popular suggestions.

suffix.name: popular_fetched
suffix.label: Non-personalized, popular suggestions (fetched from the server).

suffix.name: search_page
suffix.label: Google Search page
suffix.obsolete: Used for experiment but was never launched, as of 2019-07.

suffix.name: server0
suffix.obsolete: Hasn't been used for a while, as of 2016-07.

suffix.name: NewTab
suffix.label: NTP loaded on a new tab.

suffix.name: Other
suffix.label: Default search provider is not Google.

suffix.name: Active
suffix.label: For a tab active which is shown foreground in a browser window.

suffix.name: Closed
suffix.label: For a tab that is about to be closed.

suffix.name: Detached
suffix.label: For a tab that is being dragged by user to outside of the browser window.

suffix.name: Inactive
suffix.label: For tabs in background and not shown to user.

suffix.name: NoAcceptRangesHeader
suffix.label: For parallel requests created without accept-ranges header.

suffix.name: Fullscreen.Shown
suffix.label: A notification sent by a fullscreen app or webpage that is displayed.

suffix.name: Fullscreen.Suppressed
suffix.label: A notification sent by a fullscreen app or webpage that is not displayed.

suffix.name: Windowed
suffix.label: A notification sent by a non-fullscreen app or webpage.

suffix.name: Negative
suffix.label: Estimate of the metric was lower than observed metric

suffix.name: Positive
suffix.label: Estimate of the metric was higher than observed metric
Recorded approximately 15 seconds after navigation start
Recorded approximately 30 seconds after navigation start
Recorded approximately 60 seconds after navigation start

suffix.name: Broadband
suffix.label: Observed effective connection type was Broadband

suffix.name: Offline
suffix.label: Observed effective connection type was Offline

suffix.name: Slow2G
suffix.label: Observed effective connection type was Slow2G

suffix.name: Unknown
suffix.label: Observed effective connection type was Unknown
Observed metric was between 0 and 20 (inclusive) units
Observed metric was between 20 and 60 (inclusive) units
Observed metric was between 60 and 140 (inclusive) units
Observed metric was between 140 and 300 (inclusive) units
Observed metric was between 300 and 620 (inclusive) units
Observed metric was between 620 and 1260 (inclusive) units
Observed metric was between 1260 and 2540 (inclusive) units
Observed metric was between 2540 and 51000 (inclusive) units

suffix.name: 5100_Infinity
suffix.label: Observed metric was greater than 5100 (inclusive) units

suffix.name: Kbps
suffix.label: Records availability of the downstream throughput estimate.

suffix.name: RTT
suffix.label: Records availability of the HTTP RTT estimate.

suffix.name: TransportRTT
suffix.label: Records availability of the transport RTT estimate.

histogram_suffixes.name: NumLayersBucket
histogram_suffixes.obsolete: Removed 2018-07 as the non bucketed metrics are enough for future reference.

histogram_suffixes.name: OffDomainInclusionAbortReason
histogram_suffixes.obsolete: Removed 2016-05 as the OffDomainInclusionDetector was removed.

suffix.name: EmptyMainFrameURL
suffix.label: The main frame URL was empty.

suffix.name: HistoryLookupFailed
suffix.label: The lookup to the HistoryService failed.

suffix.name: Incognito
suffix.label: The profile associated with the frame under analysis is an incognito profile.

suffix.name: NoHistoryService
suffix.label: There was no HistoryService associated with the profile of the frame under analysis (this can happen seldomly on startup for early net requests, or on shutdown by the asynchronous nature of the analysis, but shouldn't be common).

suffix.name: NoProfile
suffix.label: The profile associated with the frame under analysis couldn't be resolved (expected to happen at a low frequency by the asynchronous nature of the analysis).

suffix.name: ntp_suggestions
suffix.label: NTP suggestions offlined by the user

suffix.name: Failed
suffix.label: For items that failed being prefetched for any reason.

suffix.name: Successful
suffix.label: For items that were completed sucessfully.

affected-histogram.name: OptimizationGuide.PredictionModelVersion
affected-histogram.obsolete: Removed as of 05/2020 in favor of recording on update and load of the prediction model.

suffix.name: LitePage
suffix.label: HTTP server preview (served from the Data Reduction Proxy)

suffix.name: LitePageRedirect
suffix.label: HTTPS server preview (served from the litepages service)

suffix.name: LoadingPredictor
suffix.label: Provides information about subresources predicted to be on the page

suffix.name: NoScript
suffix.label: Disables the fetching and execution of JavaScript

suffix.name: PerformanceHints
suffix.label: Provides aggregated performance information about the page

suffix.name: ResourceLoading
suffix.label: Applies a set of resource loading hints to load the page

suffix.name: ComponentHint
suffix.label: Holds hint received from the Chrome Optimization Hints Component

suffix.name: FetchedHint
suffix.label: Holds hint fetched from the Chrome Optimization Guide Service

suffix.name: FirstChange
suffix.label: First setting change since the user has focused the page (may include gaps of under one minute during which the page is not focused).

suffix.name: SubsequentChange
suffix.label: Subsequent setting changeafter the user has already changed a setting on the page.

suffix.name: OutOfRaster
suffix.label: Task was not performed as part of a raster task.

suffix.name: ClamshellMode
suffix.label: Normal clamshell mode with multiple windows animates

suffix.name: MinimizedTabletMode
suffix.label: Animates in Tablet mode w/o split view and all windows are minimized

suffix.name: SingleClamshellMode
suffix.label: In Clamshell mode with only top maximized/fullscren window animates

suffix.name: TabletMode
suffix.label: Animates in Tablet mode w/o split view

suffix.name: BG
suffix.label: Event occurred at least partially in the background

suffix.name: Background
suffix.label: The page was backgrounded at least once from navigation start to this event.

affected-histogram.name: PageLoad.Clients.ServiceWorker.Timing2.NavigationToFirstContentfulPaint
affected-histogram.obsolete: Deprecated in favor of PaintTiming equivalent.

suffix.name: AfterBuffering
suffix.label: Recorded after buffering of timing updates in the browser process has completed.

suffix.name: AfterPaint
suffix.label: Limited to the duration of time starting after first paint, for page loads that reached first paint.

suffix.name: RedirectToNonAmpPage
suffix.label: Limited to redirects to non-AMP pages.

suffix.name: AtTimingCallbackDispatch
suffix.label: Evaluated at the time timing update calls are dispatched to observers.

suffix.name: Clients.AMP
suffix.label: AMP page load. Same page navigations are not tracked.

suffix.name: Clients.AMP.AmpCache
suffix.label: AMP page load from the AMP cache CDN.

suffix.name: Clients.AMP.GoogleNews
suffix.label: AMP page load in the Google News AMP viewer. Same page navigations are not tracked.

suffix.name: Clients.AMP.GoogleSearch
suffix.label: AMP page load in the Google Search AMP viewer. Same page navigations are not tracked.

suffix.name: Clients.AMPCache
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics that are a result of a navigations to an AMP cache page. Same page navigations are not tracked.

suffix.name: Clients.AMPCache2
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics that are a result of a navigations to an AMP cache page. Same page navigations are not tracked.

suffix.name: Clients.CssScanner
suffix.label: Metrics from pages scanned by the preload scanner for @import CSS rules

suffix.name: Clients.DataReductionProxy
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics that are a result of a navigation through the data reduction proxy.

suffix.name: Clients.DataReductionProxy.LoFiOn
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics that are a result of a navigation through the data reduction proxy with session in LoFi enabled or control field trial, and when the connection was slow.

suffix.name: Clients.Previews.LitePages
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics that are a result of a navigation through the data reduction proxy where a lite page response was received.

suffix.name: NonProxied
suffix.label: Resources not loaded through data reduction proxy.

suffix.name: PercentProxied
suffix.label: Percent of resources loaded through data reduction proxy.

suffix.name: Proxied
suffix.label: Resources loaded through data reduction proxy.

suffix.name: Clients.DocWrite.Block
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics from a page that has synchronous, cross-origin document.written scripts that could be blocked if the document.write script blocking feature is enabled.

suffix.name: Clients.FontPreload
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics when the first rendering cycle has been delayed in favor of font preloading

suffix.name: Clients.FromGoogleSearch
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics that are a result of a navigation from a Google web search

suffix.name: Clients.FromGWS
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics that are a result of a navigation from a Google web search

suffix.name: Clients.FromGWS2
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics that are a result of a navigation from a Google web search

suffix.name: Preconnectable
suffix.label: The predictor database contained origins for preconnecting for a page

suffix.name: Clients.LoFi
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics that are a result of a navigation that displayed a LoFi image.

suffix.name: Clients.MediaPageLoad
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics for page loads that involved playing a media element.

suffix.name: Clients.MultiTabLoading
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics for page loads that started while there are other loading tabs.

suffix.name: Clients.MultiTabLoading.2OrMore
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics for page loads that started while there are 2 or more other loading tabs.

suffix.name: Clients.MultiTabLoading.5OrMore
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics for page loads that started while there are 5 or more other loading tabs.

suffix.name: Clients.NoScriptPreview
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics that are a result of a navigation that tirggered the NoScript Preview intervention.

suffix.name: search
suffix.label: Custom histogram for the Google Search results page.

suffix.name: Clients.Previews.OfflinePages
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics that are a result of a navigation that shows users an offline page preview. Offline page previews are shown when a user's effective connection type is prohibitively slow.

suffix.name: Clients.Protocol.H1
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics that are a result of a navigation to main resource over HTTP/1.1

suffix.name: Clients.Protocol.H2
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics that are a result of a navigation to main resource over HTTP/2

suffix.name: Clients.Protocol.H11
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics that are a result of a navigation to main resource over HTTP/1.1

suffix.name: Clients.Protocol.QUIC
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics that are a result of a navigation to main resource over QUIC

histogram_suffixes.name: PageLoadMetricsClientsReload
histogram_suffixes.obsolete: Removed at M53 in favor of LoadType.Reload.

suffix.name: Clients.Reload
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics from a page that is reloaded

suffix.name: Clients.ResourceLoadingHintsPreview
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics that are a result of a navigation that used ResourceLoadingHints to show a Preview version of the page.

histogram_suffixes.name: PageLoadMetricsClientsResourcePrefetchPredictor
histogram_suffixes.obsolete: Removed at M66. No longer recorded.

histogram_suffixes.name: PageLoadMetricsClientsResourcePrefetchPredictorPrefetchingType
histogram_suffixes.obsolete: Removed at M66. No longer recorded.

suffix.name: Prefetchable
suffix.label: The predictor database contained subresources for prediction for a page

suffix.name: Clients.Scheme.HTTP
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics that are a result of a navigation to a main resource where the committed URL is HTTP.

suffix.name: Clients.Scheme.HTTPS
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics that are a result of a navigation to a main resource where the committed URL is HTTPS.

suffix.name: Clients.NoServiceWorker
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics from a page that is not controlled by a Service Worker. This is intended to understand the performance impact of service workers on special apps using Clients.ServiceWorker
suffix.obsolete: Deprecated on June 2018 (M69) in favor of Clients.NoServiceWorker2.

suffix.name: Clients.NoServiceWorker2
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics from a page that is either not controlled by a service worker or is controlled by a service worker without a fetch event handler. This should NOT be compared to Clients.ServiceWorker2, see https://goo.gl/EdaGQn.

suffix.name: Clients.ServiceWorker
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics from a page that is controlled by a Service Worker.
suffix.obsolete: Deprecated on June 2018 (M69) in favor of Clients.ServiceWorker2.

suffix.name: Clients.ServiceWorker2
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics from a page that is controlled by a service worker that has a fetch event handler.

affected-histogram.name: PageLoad.Timing2.NavigationToFirstContentfulPaint
affected-histogram.obsolete: Deprecated in favor of PaintTiming equivalent.

suffix.name: docs
suffix.label: Custom histogram for the Google Docs page

suffix.name: search
suffix.label: Custom histogram for the Google Search results page

suffix.name: Clients.SubresourceFilter
suffix.label: For pages with filtered subresources. Includes dryrun matches.

suffix.name: Clients.TabRestore
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics that are a result of a navigation caused by a tab restore.

suffix.name: LoadType.NewNavigation
suffix.label: Restricted to new navigations (link clicks, URLs typed into the URL box, etc).

suffix.name: MediaPlayed
suffix.label: Limited to pages where media was played.

suffix.name: NoCommit
suffix.label: Limited to page loads that did not commit.

suffix.name: NoEndTime
suffix.label: The page load had no recorded end time, so an end time was synthesized at the time the page end notification was processed.

suffix.name: NoMediaPlayed
suffix.label: Limited to pages where no media was played.

suffix.name: WithoutPaint
suffix.label: The foreground duration for page loads did not reach first paint.

suffix.name: WithPaint
suffix.label: The foreground duration for page loads reached first paint.

suffix.name: Clients.SignedExchange
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics for page loads for which the document was served through a signed exchange.

suffix.name: Clients.SignedExchange.AltSubSXG
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics for page loads for which the document was served with prefetched alternative subresource signed exchange.

suffix.name: Clients.SignedExchange.Cached
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics for page loads for which the document was served through a cached signed exchange.

suffix.name: Clients.SignedExchange.NotCached
suffix.label: PageLoadMetrics for page loads for which the document was served through a signed exchange directly from network.

suffix.name: UserGesture
suffix.label: Restricted to pages loaded via a user gesture.

suffix.name: UserInitiated
suffix.label: (experimental) Page load metric that is approximately user initiated

suffix.name: BrowserInitiated
suffix.label: (experimental) Page load that was initiated from the browser process

suffix.name: UserGesture
suffix.label: (experimental) Page load that has a user gesture

suffix.name: HistoryLoad
suffix.label: but only for user pressing back or forward

suffix.name: LinkLoad
suffix.label: deprecated - see LinkLoadReload, LinkLoadNormal, LinkLoadStaleOk, LinkLoadCacheOnly; content initiated, commonly back to a posted page

suffix.name: LinkLoadCacheOnly
suffix.label: content initiated, commonly back to a posted page, where browser must ONLY use cache
